reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Total depreciation charged against the asset from the capitalization date to the current valuation date. Represents the cumulative reduction in asset value due to wear, obsolescence, and usage over its useful life.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The SAP General Ledger contra-asset account code used to record accumulated depreciation for this asset. Supports balance sheet presentation of gross asset value less accumulated depreciation.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IFRS; GAAP ASC 360

description: The original historical cost of acquiring the asset, including purchase price, import duties, installation costs, and any directly attributable costs to bring the asset to its intended use. Recorded in the transaction currency per IAS 16.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP ASC 360

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost transaction currency (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency asset accounting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP FI-AA; IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The acquisition cost translated into the group reporting currency (e.g., USD for a US-headquartered multinational). Used for consolidated financial reporting and CAPEX tracking at the enterprise level.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; IFRS 10

description: The date on which the asset was acquired or purchased. May differ from the capitalization date if the asset was under construction or in transit before being placed in service.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: SAP FI-AA sub-asset number used to track individual components or additions to a main asset (e.g., a capital improvement added to an existing machine). Enables component-level depreciation tracking.
reference: SAP FI-AA Fixed Asset Accounting; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Indicates whether the asset is currently classified as an Asset Under Construction (AUC) in SAP FI-AA. AUC assets are not yet depreciated and are transferred to a final asset class upon completion and capitalization.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The SAP Project System (PS) or internal project number associated with the capital expenditure that funded this asset acquisition. Links the asset to the originating CAPEX budget and project for investment tracking and ROI analysis.
reference: SAP PS Project System; SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The date on which the asset was capitalized and placed into service for depreciation purposes in SAP FI-AA. This is the start date for depreciation calculation and is critical for CAPEX accounting under IAS 16 and GAAP ASC 360.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP ASC 360

description: SAP company code representing the legal entity or subsidiary that owns the asset for financial accounting purposes. Supports multi-entity IFRS/GAAP reporting across the multinational enterprise.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 360

description: SAP Controlling (CO) area code that groups company codes for internal cost accounting and CAPEX/OPEX reporting. Enables cross-company cost center analysis and asset cost allocation.
reference: SAP CO Controlling; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: The SAP Controlling cost center to which depreciation and asset-related costs are allocated. Enables OPEX cost allocation to manufacturing departments, plants, or business units for internal reporting.
reference: SAP CO Controlling; IFRS 8; GAAP

description: The depreciation expense charged for the current fiscal period (month or year) as calculated by the SAP FI-AA depreciation run. Used for P&L reporting and OPEX cost allocation to cost centers.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The date as of which the asset valuation figures (net book value, accumulated depreciation) are reported. Typically corresponds to the fiscal period-end or year-end closing date for balance sheet reporting.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS 13

description: The SAP FI-AA depreciation key code that defines the specific depreciation calculation method, period control, and base value for the asset (e.g., LINR for straight-line, DGRW for declining balance). Drives automated depreciation posting.
reference: SAP FI-AA Fixed Asset Accounting

description: The accounting method used to allocate the depreciable cost of the asset over its useful life (e.g., straight-line, declining balance, units of production). Determined by asset class and must comply with IFRS/GAAP policies.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS for SMEs Section 17

description: The date on which the asset was retired, sold, scrapped, or otherwise disposed of. Triggers derecognition of the asset from the balance sheet and recognition of any gain or loss on disposal per IAS 16.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The net proceeds received from the sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate the gain or loss on disposal (disposal proceeds minus net book value at disposal date) for P&L reporting.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The fiscal period (posting period) within the fiscal year to which this valuation record applies (e.g., '01' through '12' for monthly periods, or '13'-'16' for special closing periods in SAP). Enables period-level depreciation reporting.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the group/consolidated reporting currency used for enterprise-level CAPEX and asset valuation reporting (e.g., USD, EUR).
reference: ISO 4217; SAP FI-AA; IFRS 10; IAS 21

description: The date on which an impairment loss was recognized for the asset. Required for IAS 36 impairment assessment documentation and audit trail purposes.
reference: IAS 36 Impairment of Assets; GAAP ASC 360

description: The amount by which the carrying amount of the asset exceeds its recoverable amount, recognized as an impairment loss in the income statement. Recorded when an asset's value has declined beyond normal depreciation per IAS 36.
reference: IAS 36 Impairment of Assets; GAAP ASC 360; SAP FI-AA

description: The timestamp of the most recent update to the asset valuation record in the source SAP FI-AA system. Used for incremental data loading, audit trail, and change tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IFRS; GAAP

description: The accounting policy model applied to the asset class for subsequent measurement: 'cost_model' (cost less accumulated depreciation and impairment) or 'revaluation_model' (fair value less subsequent depreciation and impairment) per IAS 16.
reference: IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; IFRS 13

description: The current carrying amount of the asset on the balance sheet, calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation and any accumulated impairment losses. Key metric for IFRS/GAAP fixed asset reporting and balance sheet valuation.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement

description: The SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the asset is physically located. Supports plant-level CAPEX tracking and asset register reporting.
reference: SAP FI-AA; SAP MM Plant

description: The estimated amount the organization expects to recover from the asset at the end of its useful life, net of disposal costs. The depreciable amount is acquisition cost minus residual value. Reviewed at each reporting date per IAS 16.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The residual (salvage) value expressed as a percentage of the original acquisition cost. Used in SAP FI-AA to automatically calculate the residual value amount when the acquisition cost changes.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16

description: The amount by which the asset's carrying value was adjusted upward or downward under the revaluation model permitted by IAS 16. Positive values indicate upward revaluation; negative values indicate downward revaluation to revaluation reserve.
reference: IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; SAP FI-AA

description: The date on which the asset was formally revalued to fair value under the IAS 16 revaluation model. Revaluations must be performed with sufficient regularity to ensure the carrying amount does not differ materially from fair value.
reference: IAS 16; IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement; SAP FI-AA

description: Current lifecycle status of the asset valuation record. Drives financial reporting inclusion/exclusion and depreciation run eligibility. 'fully_depreciated' indicates net book value has reached residual value.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The estimated useful life of the asset expressed in months, providing finer granularity for depreciation calculation in SAP FI-AA. Used when the useful life does not align to whole years.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: The estimated number of years the asset is expected to be economically useful to the organization. Used to calculate the annual depreciation charge under straight-line and other time-based methods. Reviewed periodically per IAS 16.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each asset classification record within the enterprise asset taxonomy.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset Classification; ISO 14224 Equipment Taxonomy

description: Foreign key linking to product.classification. Business justification: Asset classes are aligned to product classification schemes (e.g., equipment taxonomy). Asset management and product engineering teams share classification structures to ensure consistent categorizati
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Primary industry or regulatory standard governing the design, operation, or maintenance of assets in this class (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 61131, ANSI/NFPA 70, CE Marking, UL 508A).
reference: ISO; IEC; ANSI; OSHA; CE Marking; UL

description: Indicates whether the asset class represents a fixed asset, movable asset, leased asset, or tooling, driving accounting treatment and depreciation rules under IFRS/GAAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; IFRS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: Minimum acquisition cost (in base currency) above which an asset of this class must be capitalized as a fixed asset rather than expensed as OPEX, per company CAPEX policy and GAAP/IFRS guidelines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; IFRS IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the capitalization threshold amount, supporting multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 16

description: High-level grouping of the asset class into a major category (e.g., production equipment, facility infrastructure, IT/OT systems) for portfolio-level reporting and CAPEX/OPEX planning.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset Classification; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 14224

description: Indicates whether assets of this class require CE Marking for operation within the European Union, ensuring conformity with applicable EU directives (Machinery Directive, Low Voltage Directive, EMC Directive).
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; EU Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the asset class, used for cross-system referencing between Maximo EAM and SAP S/4HANA PM module.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset Classification; SAP PM Asset Class Code

description: Default SAP cost center code for assets of this class, used for OPEX maintenance cost allocation and management accounting reporting in SAP S/4HANA CO module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS; GAAP

description: Timestamp when this asset class record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity)

description: Standardized criticality rating for assets of this class based on impact to production throughput, safety, and quality. Drives maintenance prioritization, spare parts stocking strategy, and OEE reporting.
reference: Maximo EAM - Criticality Analysis; ISO 55000; IEC 60812 FMEA

description: Accounting depreciation method applied to assets of this class, governing how asset value is expensed over its useful life in compliance with IFRS/GAAP financial reporting standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; IFRS IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: Detailed narrative description of the asset class, including its typical function, application context, and distinguishing characteristics within the manufacturing environment.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset Classification; ISO 14224

description: Date from which this asset class definition becomes active and valid for use in asset creation and classification across EAM and ERP systems.
reference: Maximo EAM; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 55000

description: Energy efficiency classification for assets of this class, supporting ISO 50001 energy management compliance and OPEX energy cost reporting across manufacturing facilities.
reference: ISO 50001 Energy Management; EU Energy Efficiency Directive

description: Environmental risk classification for assets of this class, indicating potential environmental impact (e.g., refrigerant leaks, chemical containment, emissions) for compliance with ISO 14001 and EPA regulations.
reference: ISO 14001; EPA; REACH Regulation; RoHS Directive

description: Date after which this asset class definition is no longer valid for new asset creation, supporting controlled deprecation of obsolete classifications in the enterprise taxonomy.
reference: Maximo EAM; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 55000

description: SAP General Ledger account code associated with this asset class for CAPEX capitalization and OPEX expense posting, ensuring correct financial accounting treatment in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.

description: Numeric depth of this asset class within the classification hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents the root category and higher numbers represent more granular sub-classifications.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset Classification Hierarchy; ISO 14224

description: Indicates whether assets of this class are configured for real-time condition monitoring via the Siemens MindSphere IIoT platform, enabling predictive maintenance and digital performance analytics.

description: Mandatory interval in calendar days between regulatory compliance inspections for assets of this class, as prescribed by applicable governing bodies (OSHA, CE, UL, local regulations).

reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; CE Marking Directive; UL Standards; ISO 45001

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this asset class record, used for change tracking, data synchronization between Maximo EAM and SAP S/4HANA, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity)

description: Standard interval in calendar days between scheduled preventive maintenance activities for assets of this class, used to auto-generate work orders in Maximo EAM.
reference: Maximo EAM - Preventive Maintenance; ISO 55000; TPM

description: Default maintenance strategy class assigned to assets of this type, aligning with Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) and Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) frameworks configured in Maximo EAM.
reference: Maximo EAM - Maintenance Strategy; ISO 55000 Asset Management; TPM

description: Industry-standard or OEM-specified Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in hours for assets of this class, used as a benchmark for reliability analysis, maintenance planning, and OEE calculations.
reference: ISO 14224; IEC 60050-191; Maximo EAM - Reliability; MTBF

description: Industry-standard or OEM-specified Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) in hours for assets of this class, used to estimate downtime impact, plan maintenance labor resources, and set SLA targets for work orders.

description: Full descriptive name of the asset class (e.g., CNC Machining Center, Industrial Robot, PLC Controller, HVAC System, Power Transformer).

description: Indicates whether assets of this class contain substances subject to REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) or RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) compliance requirements.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Estimated residual (salvage) value of assets in this class expressed as a percentage of original acquisition cost, used in depreciation base calculations per IFRS IAS 16.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; IFRS IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: Indicates whether assets of this class are designated as safety-critical, requiring enhanced maintenance protocols, mandatory inspection records, and compliance with ISO 45001 occupational health and safety standards.

description: Corresponding asset class code in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA module, used for financial accounting, depreciation area assignment, and CAPEX tracking. Ensures EAM-ERP synchronization.

description: Current lifecycle status of the asset class record, controlling whether new assets can be created under this classification in EAM and ERP systems.

description: Secondary classification level within the asset category, providing finer granularity (e.g., CNC Machining, Robotic Welding, SCADA Systems, HVAC Chillers) for maintenance strategy alignment.

description: Indicates whether assets of this class require UL (Underwriters Laboratories) product safety certification for operation in North American markets.
reference: UL Standards; ANSI; NFPA

description: Standard expected useful life in years for assets belonging to this class, used to calculate depreciation schedules and plan asset replacement cycles under IFRS IAS 16.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; IFRS IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: Standard manufacturer warranty duration in months applicable to assets of this class, used to configure warranty tracking and claim management in Maximo EAM and Salesforce Service Cloud.
reference: Maximo EAM - Warranty Management; Salesforce Service Cloud

description: Unique system-generated identifier for a measurement point or counter position defined on a piece of equipment or technical object. Serves as the primary key for the measurement point entity.
reference: SAP PM Measurement Point (IMPTT); Maximo EAM Meter/Measurement Point

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: Measurement points on assets are defined against product specifications from engineering, which set the nominal values and tolerance limits. Condition monitoring teams use this link to evaluate whethe
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Indicates whether automated alerts or notifications are configured for this measurement point when readings breach the defined upper or lower limit thresholds. Drives real-time alarm management in SCADA and MindSphere.
reference: IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity Alarm Management; Siemens MindSphere Alert Configuration; SCADA Alarm Management

description: Number of days between required calibration events for the sensor or instrument at this measurement point. Used to schedule preventive calibration work orders and ensure measurement traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025 Calibration Intervals; SAP PM Maintenance Plan

description: Indicates whether the sensor or instrument associated with this measurement point requires periodic calibration to maintain measurement accuracy. Drives calibration work order generation and compliance with metrology standards.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5 Monitoring and Measuring Resources; ISO/IEC 17025 Calibration; SAP PM Calibration Management

description: Classifies the measurement point as either a 'counter' (accumulating numeric value such as operating hours or cycle count used for counter-based maintenance triggering) or a 'characteristic' (instantaneous measurement such as temperature, pressure, or vibration used for condition monitoring).
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-MPTYP; ISO 13374-1 Condition Monitoring and Diagnostics of Machines

description: Reference code linking this measurement point to a classification characteristic in the EAM/PLM system (e.g., SAP classification characteristic or Teamcenter attribute). Enables structured querying and grouping of measurement points by engineering characteristic.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-ATINN; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Attribute Classification

description: Indicates whether this measurement point is actively used for condition-based maintenance (CBM) monitoring. When true, readings are evaluated against thresholds to trigger predictive maintenance work orders via MindSphere or SAP PM.
reference: SAP PM Condition Monitoring; Siemens MindSphere Predictive Analytics; ISO 13379 Condition Monitoring Diagnostics

description: Maximum value a counter-type measurement point can reach before rolling over to zero. Critical for correctly computing cumulative counter readings across overflow events (e.g., an odometer rolling over at 999,999 km). Applicable only to category='counter'.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-UEBGW; Maximo EAM Meter Rollover Value

description: Indicates whether the counter increments upward (ascending, e.g., operating hours accumulating) or decrements downward (descending, e.g., remaining service life countdown). Applicable only to category='counter'.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-ZAEHLRICHT; Maximo EAM Meter Direction

description: Date and time when this measurement point record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and lifecycle tracking for the measurement point.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management Records; SAP PM IMPTT-ERDAT/ERZET; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Number of decimal places to which measurement readings are recorded and displayed for this measurement point. Controls precision of data capture and reporting.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-ANZST; ISO 80000-1 Measurement Precision

description: Date on which the most recent calibration of the sensor or instrument at this measurement point was performed. Used to calculate next calibration due date and assess current measurement validity.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025; SAP PM Calibration Record

description: Date and time when this measurement point record was most recently updated in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental data loading in the lakehouse Silver layer, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management Records; SAP PM IMPTT-AEDAT/AEZET; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that recorded the most recent measurement reading. Supports audit trail and data quality validation for manual readings.
reference: SAP PM IMRG-ERNAM; Maximo EAM Reading Entered By; ISO 9001 Quality Management Audit Trail

description: Date and time when the most recent measurement reading was recorded at this point. Used to assess data freshness, identify stale sensors, and support maintenance scheduling based on elapsed time since last reading.
reference: SAP PM IMRG-IDATE/ITIME Last Reading Date/Time; Maximo EAM Last Reading Date; ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring

description: Most recent measurement reading recorded at this measurement point. Provides the current state of the monitored parameter for real-time dashboards, maintenance scheduling, and condition assessment without querying the full reading history.
reference: SAP PM IMRG-MESSWERT Last Measurement; Maximo EAM Last Reading; ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring

description: Minimum acceptable value for readings at this measurement point. Readings below this threshold trigger alerts, condition-based maintenance work orders, or SCADA alarms. Used in Statistical Process Control (SPC) and predictive maintenance analytics.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-UNTGW; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity Alarm Management; ISO 13379 Condition Monitoring Diagnostics

description: Code of the maintenance strategy (e.g., time-based, counter-based, condition-based) associated with this measurement point. Determines how maintenance plans and task lists are triggered based on readings from this point.
reference: SAP PM Maintenance Strategy (T353); TPM (Total Productive Maintenance); ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Name of the MindSphere aspect (logical grouping of related time-series variables) to which this measurement point's sensor variable belongs. Aspects organize sensor data streams for analytics and Digital Twin modeling in MindSphere.
reference: Siemens MindSphere Aspect Model; IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things) Platform Integration

description: Identifier of the digital asset in Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform to which this measurement point is bound. Enables real-time sensor data ingestion, edge computing, and predictive analytics via MindSphere.
reference: Siemens MindSphere Asset Management API; IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things) Integration

description: Specific time-series variable name within the MindSphere aspect that maps to this measurement point. Provides the exact binding between the physical sensor signal and the MindSphere data model for real-time streaming and predictive analytics.
reference: Siemens MindSphere Time Series API; IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things) Sensor Binding

description: Descriptive name of the measurement point that identifies its physical or logical location and purpose on the equipment (e.g., 'Spindle Vibration Sensor', 'Operating Hours Counter', 'Coolant Temperature').
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-MPTXT; Maximo EAM Meter Description; ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring

description: Code of the manufacturing plant or facility where the equipment bearing this measurement point is located. Supports multi-site reporting and plant-level condition monitoring dashboards.
reference: SAP PM T001W Plant Master; ISO 14224 Facility Classification

description: Memory address or register reference in the PLC that provides the raw signal for this measurement point. Used for IT/OT convergence data pipelines to pull readings directly from PLC registers into the lakehouse.
reference: IEC 61131-3 PLC Programming Standard; PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) Integration; IT/OT Convergence

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the measurement point as defined in the source EAM/CMMS system (e.g., SAP PM measurement point number). Used for cross-system referencing and shop-floor identification.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-MPOBJ; Maximo EAM Meter Number

description: Physical or engineering type of the measurement being captured at this point. Drives sensor configuration, alarm thresholds, and analytics models in Siemens MindSphere and SAP PM.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-MPTYP; IEC 61131-3 PLC Signal Types; ISO 13373 Vibration Condition Monitoring

description: Indicates how measurement readings are captured: 'automatic' (via IIoT sensor/SCADA integration), 'manual' (technician enters reading during inspection or maintenance), or 'semi_automatic' (sensor-assisted but requires manual confirmation).
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-ABLESART; Maximo EAM Meter Reading Method; ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring

description: Frequency in seconds at which sensor readings are captured and transmitted for this measurement point. Drives data volume planning, edge computing configuration, and real-time alerting latency in MindSphere and SCADA systems.
reference: Siemens MindSphere Edge Configuration; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity; IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things) Data Acquisition

business_glossary_term: SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) Tag Name
description: Tag name or address in the SCADA or DCS system that corresponds to this measurement point. Enables mapping between the EAM measurement point and the operational technology (OT) data source for automated reading ingestion.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this measurement point record originates (e.g., SAP PM, Maximo EAM, Siemens MindSphere). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Silver layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; NIST Cybersecurity Framework Data Provenance; ISO 9001 Data Traceability

description: Natural key or primary identifier of this measurement point record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP PM measurement point number, Maximo meter ID). Enables reverse lookup and reconciliation with the system of record.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; SAP PM IMPTT-POINT; Maximo EAM Meter ID

description: Current operational status of the measurement point. 'active' indicates readings are being collected; 'inactive' means temporarily disabled; 'decommissioned' means permanently retired; 'under_calibration' means the point is offline for calibration; 'suspended' means paused due to equipment downtime.

description: Engineering unit in which measurement readings are recorded at this point (e.g., 'h' for hours, 'rpm' for revolutions per minute, '°C' for Celsius, 'bar' for pressure, 'mm/s' for vibration velocity). Aligns with SAP PM unit of measure configuration.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-MSEHI; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; ANSI/ISA-5.1 Instrumentation Symbols

description: Maximum acceptable value for readings at this measurement point. Readings exceeding this threshold trigger alerts, condition-based maintenance work orders, or SCADA alarms. Used in SPC and predictive maintenance analytics.
reference: SAP PM IMPTT-OBGW; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity Alarm Management; ISO 13379 Condition Monitoring Diagnostics

description: Code of the work center or production cell associated with the equipment on which this measurement point resides. Enables OEE and condition monitoring analysis at the work center level.

description: Date from which this measurement point definition is effective and readings should be collected. Supports temporal validity management for measurement point configurations that change over the asset lifecycle.

description: Date until which this measurement point definition is effective. After this date, the measurement point is considered expired or superseded. Used for lifecycle management and historical data segmentation.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Assessment of the potential impact on product quality when an instrument is found out of tolerance. Supports CAPA initiation and product disposition decisions per ISO 9001 requirements.

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the instrument is installed and calibrated. Supports multi-site calibration program management.

description: Free-text field for additional observations, anomalies, or notes recorded by the calibration technician during the calibration event. Supports root cause analysis and audit documentation.

description: Overall outcome of the calibration event indicating whether the instrument met the required accuracy specifications. Drives disposition decisions and QMS non-conformance workflows.

description: Maximum acceptable deviation from the nominal value for the instrument to be considered within calibration tolerance. Used to determine pass/fail result.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.task_list. Business justification: maintenance_item currently stores task_list_number (STRING) and task_list_type (STRING) as denormalized references to the task_list product. task_list has a `number` and `type` attribute that are the
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Cost accounting activity type used to allocate internal labor costs for this maintenance task to the appropriate cost center. Links maintenance execution to financial controlling (CO) in SAP.

description: Reference to the applicable calibration standard or specification that governs this calibration task item (e.g., 'ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.5', 'NIST Traceable', 'IEC 61315'). Applicable only when task_type is 'calibration'. Supports quality management system compliance.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.5 Monitoring and Measuring Resources; NIST Handbook 150; IEC 61315; SAP QM

description: SAP PM control key that determines how the maintenance operation is processed, including whether it is internally performed, externally serviced, or a milestone operation. Controls costing, confirmation, and scheduling behavior.

description: Financial cost center code to which the costs of this maintenance task are allocated. Enables maintenance cost tracking by organizational unit, department, or production area for management accounting purposes.

description: Financial classification of the maintenance task cost as either Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) — improving or extending asset life — or Operational Expenditure (OPEX) — routine maintenance to sustain current performance. Critical for financial reporting and asset accounting.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP ASC 360; SAP FI-AA; ISO 55001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated and actual cost values associated with this maintenance item (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations in a multinational enterprise.

description: Date and time when this maintenance item record was created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.

description: Planned total cost estimate for executing this maintenance task item, including labor, materials, and external services. Used for maintenance budget planning, CAPEX/OPEX classification, and cost variance analysis.

description: Planned time in hours required to complete this maintenance task item. Used for work order scheduling, capacity planning, and labor cost estimation. Basis for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) benchmarking.
reference: SAP PM - Normal Duration (PLPO-DAUNO); Maximo EAM - Estimated Duration; ISO 55001; MTTR KPI

description: Target or nominal measurement value expected at the measurement point during inspection or calibration. Used to assess whether the equipment is operating within acceptable parameters and to trigger corrective actions when deviations occur.

description: Flag indicating whether this maintenance task involves handling, disposal, or exposure to hazardous materials (chemicals, lubricants, refrigerants, etc.). Triggers REACH/RoHS compliance checks and hazardous waste disposal procedures.

description: Unit of measure for the maintenance interval, defining whether the interval is time-based (days, weeks, months), usage-based (operating hours, cycles, kilometers), or event-based. Determines the scheduling trigger type.

description: Numeric value of the maintenance interval at which this task should be performed (e.g., 90 for every 90 days, 500 for every 500 operating hours). Combined with interval_unit to define the full maintenance cycle.

description: Sequential counter number identifying the position of this task item within its parent maintenance plan or task list. Used for ordering and referencing individual steps (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030 in SAP PM convention).

description: Short descriptive text summarizing the specific maintenance task to be performed, such as 'Inspect hydraulic seals', 'Lubricate conveyor bearings', or 'Calibrate pressure sensor'. Used for display and search.
reference: SAP PM - Maintenance Item Text (MPOS-LTXA1); Maximo EAM - Job Plan Task Description

description: Date and time when this maintenance item record was last updated. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring in the Databricks Silver layer.

description: Number of days in advance that preparation activities (material procurement, permit acquisition, resource booking) must begin before the scheduled execution date of this maintenance task. Supports proactive maintenance scheduling.

description: Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures must be applied before performing this maintenance task. Mandatory for tasks involving electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, or thermal energy sources to prevent accidental energization.

description: Detailed narrative description of the maintenance task including step-by-step instructions, technical specifications, acceptance criteria, and any special notes for the technician. Supports Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) documentation.

description: Broad category of maintenance strategy this item belongs to, distinguishing between preventive (scheduled), predictive (condition-triggered), corrective (reactive), statutory (regulatory-mandated), and other maintenance types. Supports TPM and reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis.

description: Flag indicating whether this maintenance task item has an associated list of required spare parts, consumables, or materials (Bill of Materials for maintenance). When true, material components are defined in the task list component assignment.

description: Identifier or description of the specific measurement point on the equipment where readings are to be taken during inspection or condition monitoring tasks (e.g., 'Bearing Temperature - Drive End', 'Vibration - Motor NDE'). Supports IIoT and condition-based maintenance.

description: Unit of measure for the expected and actual measurement values recorded during this maintenance task (e.g., °C, bar, mm/s, kPa, RPM, mA). Ensures consistent data capture and comparison against specifications.

description: Number of maintenance technicians or workers required to perform this task item simultaneously. Used for crew sizing, capacity planning, and safety compliance (e.g., confined space entry requiring minimum two persons).

description: Indicates whether a formal Permit to Work (PTW) is required before executing this maintenance task. Applies to high-risk activities such as hot work, confined space entry, electrical isolation, or working at height. Mandatory for safety compliance.

description: Type of work permit required for this maintenance task when permit_required is true. Determines the safety authorization workflow, hazard controls, and regulatory compliance requirements that must be satisfied before work begins.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146 (Confined Space); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 (LOTO); ISO 45001; NFPA 70E

description: Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where this maintenance item is applicable. Supports multi-plant operations and plant-level maintenance reporting in a multinational enterprise.
reference: SAP PM - Plant (MPOS-WERKS); ISO 55001; GAAP Plant Asset Classification

description: Description of the Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) required for safe execution of this maintenance task (e.g., 'Safety glasses, chemical-resistant gloves, steel-toed boots, hearing protection'). Supports safety compliance and technician briefing.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.132 PPE; ISO 45001 Clause 8.1.2; SAP PM - Safety Instructions; Maximo EAM - Safety Plan

description: Required skill, trade certification, or qualification level that the assigned technician must possess to perform this maintenance task (e.g., 'Certified Electrician', 'Hydraulics Level 2', 'Confined Space Entry Certified'). Supports workforce compliance.
reference: SAP PM - Qualification (HR); Maximo EAM - Craft; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 45001 Clause 7.2

description: Version or revision identifier of this maintenance item definition, tracking changes made to task instructions, intervals, or requirements over time. Supports document control and change management in compliance with ISO 9001.

description: Document number or identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that governs the execution of this maintenance task. Ensures technicians follow approved procedures and supports ISO 9001 document control requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; SAP DMS - Document Management; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; Maximo EAM

description: Current lifecycle status of the maintenance item within the maintenance plan. Controls whether the item is actively scheduled and executed. Inactive or obsolete items are retained for audit history.

description: Classification of the specific maintenance activity to be performed. Drives material requirements, skill requirements, and safety protocols. Supports Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) task decomposition and reporting.

description: Minimum acceptable measurement value for this maintenance task. Readings below this threshold indicate a non-conformance condition requiring corrective action or Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) initiation.

description: Maximum acceptable measurement value for this maintenance task. Readings above this threshold indicate a non-conformance condition requiring corrective action or Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) initiation.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: System timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance plan record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.

description: Date on which the most recent maintenance call was scheduled under this plan. Used to calculate the next due date and track scheduling cadence compliance.

description: The plant responsible for executing the maintenance activities, which may differ from the plant where the asset is located in multi-site or centralized maintenance organizations.

description: Business category of the maintenance plan aligned with Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) disciplines: preventive, predictive, lubrication, calibration, regulatory inspection, or major overhaul.

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the maintenance plan, used for cross-system referencing between SAP PM and Maximo EAM.

description: Indicates whether this maintenance plan is mandated by a regulatory body (e.g., OSHA, EPA, CE Marking, ISO 45001, pressure vessel inspection regulations). Regulatory plans receive elevated priority and compliance tracking.

description: Version or revision identifier for the maintenance plan, incremented when significant changes are made to cycle parameters, scope, or strategy. Supports change management and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP PM Maintenance Plan; ISO 9001 Quality Management; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management

description: Indicates whether the maintenance plan covers safety-critical equipment or activities where failure could result in personnel injury, environmental incident, or production safety hazard. Triggers mandatory completion tracking under ISO 45001.

description: Forward-looking planning window in days within which the maintenance plan will generate scheduled work orders. Defines how far ahead the system creates maintenance calls for resource and capacity planning.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this maintenance plan record originated: SAP S/4HANA PM module, Maximo EAM, or manually entered. Supports data lineage and reconciliation between systems.

description: Current lifecycle status of the maintenance plan indicating whether it is being drafted, actively scheduling work orders, suspended, or retired.

description: Classification of the maintenance strategy governing this plan. Time-based triggers on calendar intervals, counter-based on usage meters (hours, cycles), condition-based on sensor thresholds, and predictive leverages IIoT/MindSphere analytics.
reference: SAP PM Maintenance Plan; Maximo EAM PM Program; ISO 55001; TPM (Total Productive Maintenance)

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where the measurement was taken. Supports multinational regulatory compliance reporting (e.g., OSHA, EPA, EU directives) and regional analytics.

description: Date and time when the measurement reading record was created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.

description: Hierarchical plant structure identifier representing the physical location of the asset (e.g., plant, production line, work center). Aligns with SAP PM Functional Location (IL01) and Maximo Location hierarchy for spatial analytics.

description: Indicates whether the reading value was estimated (e.g., interpolated due to sensor gap, manually estimated by technician) rather than directly measured. Estimated readings are flagged for analytics quality control and excluded from certain SPC calculations.

description: Date on which the sensor or measurement instrument was last calibrated. Used to assess the validity and reliability of the reading value and to support ISO 9001 measurement system compliance.

description: Date and time when the measurement reading record was last updated or corrected. Supports change tracking, data quality audits, and Silver Layer incremental processing.

description: The actual date on which the engineering change was physically implemented for this affected item in the production environment. Distinguishes the planned effectivity date from the actual execution date for change tracking and audit purposes.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Implementation Record; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Current status of the engineering change implementation for this specific affected item. Tracks the lifecycle from initial identification through verification, enabling program managers and engineers to monitor change execution progress across all affected items.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Status Workflow; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: The descriptive name or title of the affected item (e.g., part description, drawing title, document name) as recorded in the PLM/PDM system. Provides human-readable identification alongside the item number.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Item Master; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Description

description: The unique part number, document number, or identifier of the specific item affected by the engineering change, as maintained in the PLM/PDM or ERP system (SAP S/4HANA MM or Teamcenter). This is the primary business key for the affected object.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Item Master; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master

description: Classification of the item affected by the engineering change. Indicates whether the affected object is a component, assembly, Bill of Materials (BOM), engineering drawing, manufacturing routing, controlled document, tooling, specification, software artifact, or firmware image.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Object Type Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: The date and time when this change affected item record was most recently updated in the PLM/PDM system. Supports change audit trails, data lineage tracking, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Audit Trail; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) associated with this affected item change record. Links the engineering change to the specific quality non-conformance event that necessitated the design or process modification.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Control of Nonconforming Outputs; SAP S/4HANA QM - Quality Notification

description: The revision identifier assigned to the affected item after the engineering change is implemented. Represents the updated configuration baseline and is used for release control and downstream BOM/routing updates.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Revision Management; ANSI/ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

description: The revision identifier (e.g., A, B, 01, REV-C) of the affected item prior to the engineering change being applied. Establishes the baseline from which the change is made and supports full revision history traceability.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Revision Management; ANSI/ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

description: The SAP plant code or facility identifier of the manufacturing plant responsible for implementing the change on this affected item. Supports multi-plant change management and ensures the correct facility executes the change in global manufacturing operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP - Plant Code; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: The part number or identifier of the parent assembly or document that directly contains or references the affected item. Supports where-used analysis and impact assessment across the product structure when evaluating the scope of an engineering change.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Where-Used Analysis; SAP S/4HANA MM - Where-Used List

description: The originally scheduled date for implementing the engineering change on this affected item. Used for program planning, resource scheduling, and on-time delivery tracking. Compared against the actual implementation date to measure change execution performance.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Schedule; SAP S/4HANA PP - Production Planning

description: Indicates whether the engineering change to this affected item requires formal approval from an external regulatory body (e.g., CE Marking re-assessment, UL re-certification, RoHS/REACH compliance re-evaluation) before the change can be released to production.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Certification; IEC 62443

description: Current status of the regulatory approval process for the engineering change on this affected item. Tracks whether external certification or compliance re-evaluation has been initiated, submitted to the authority, approved, or rejected.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Certification

description: The name or employee identifier of the design or process engineer accountable for implementing and verifying the engineering change on this specific affected item. Establishes clear ownership for change execution and audit trail purposes.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Ownership; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 Roles and Responsibilities

description: Indicates whether the affected item is classified as safety-critical, requiring mandatory safety review, DFMEA/PFMEA update, and regulatory authority notification before the change can be implemented. Applies to items subject to CE Marking, UL certification, ISO 45001, or OSHA requirements.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; CE Marking Directive; UL Product Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; IEC 61131

description: Numeric sequence number defining the order in which affected items within the same ECN/ECO should be processed or implemented. Ensures correct sequencing of interdependent changes across multiple affected items within a single engineering change.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Item Sequencing; SAP S/4HANA PP - Change Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this change affected item record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA; Siemens Opcenter MES; NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Data Provenance

description: The date on which verification of the engineering change was completed for this affected item. Used to confirm that the change has been validated before the item is released for full production use.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Verification Record

description: The engineering verification method required to confirm that the change has been correctly implemented and meets design intent for this affected item. First Article Inspection (FAI) is commonly required for safety-critical or form-fit-function changes.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Verification Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4 Design Verification; AS9102 First Article Inspection

description: Current status of the verification activity for this affected item following change implementation. Indicates whether the required verification (test, inspection, FAI) has been completed successfully, is in progress, has failed, or has been formally waived.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Verification Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4; SAP S/4HANA QM

business_glossary_term: Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) ID
description: Unique system-generated identifier for the DFMEA record within the enterprise data platform. Serves as the primary key for all downstream references and linkages.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3; AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: Design FMEA analyzes failure modes of specific components and assemblies whose geometry is defined in CAD models. Linking dfmea to cad_model provides direct traceability from the design risk analysis
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: dfmea.ecn_reference is a STRING reference to the ECN that triggered or is associated with this DFMEA update. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for design ri
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: DFMEA is conducted at the assembly/sub-assembly level defined in the engineering BOM. Linking dfmea to engineering_bom identifies which product assembly the design risk analysis covers, enabling BOM-l
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: Design FMEA is a specialized type of FMEA managed in quality systems. Engineering references the quality FMEA framework for risk assessment methodology and tracking.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: DFMEA failure modes are directly tied to product specifications — a failure mode represents the inability to meet a specific functional or performance specification. Linking dfmea to product_specifica
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual date on which the recommended action was completed and verified. Used to measure APQP schedule adherence and close open DFMEA action items.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6; APQP 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Action Priority classification (H=High, M=Medium, L=Low) per the AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook, used alongside RPN to determine urgency of recommended actions. High priority requires immediate action regardless of RPN value.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 5 – Action Priority Table; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Description of the actual corrective or preventive action implemented to address the failure mode. Recorded after completion to close the DFMEA action loop and support CAPA documentation.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6; CAPA; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Name or employee identifier of the engineering manager or quality authority who formally approved the DFMEA document for release.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: The APQP phase during which this DFMEA was created or last updated. Phase 1=Plan and Define, Phase 2=Product Design and Development, Phase 3=Process Design and Development, Phase 4=Product and Process Validation, Phase 5=Feedback Assessment and Corrective Action.
reference: APQP 2nd Edition; AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the engineering site or manufacturing location where the design is being developed. Supports multinational regulatory compliance tracking (e.g., CE Marking for EU, UL for USA).
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; CE Marking Directive; UL Standards; REACH/RoHS

description: Name of the external customer or internal program for which this DFMEA is being prepared. Relevant for PPAP submissions and customer-specific requirements (CSR).
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Current design controls in place to detect the failure mode or cause before the product reaches the customer (e.g., 'prototype testing', 'FEA simulation', 'design review checklist', 'First Article Inspection (FAI)'). Addresses detection-type controls per AIAG-VDA Step 5.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 5; FAI; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4

description: Current design controls in place to prevent the failure cause from occurring (e.g., 'design standard XYZ applied', 'material specification review', 'DFM analysis completed'). Addresses prevention-type controls per AIAG-VDA Step 5.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 5; DFM/DFA analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the ability of current design controls to detect the failure cause or failure mode before product release. A rating of 10 indicates no detection capability; 1 indicates near-certain detection.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: The specific design deficiency or root cause mechanism that leads to the failure mode (e.g., 'insufficient wall thickness', 'incorrect material specification', 'inadequate clearance between mating parts'). Identified through DFMEA root cause analysis.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 5; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; CAPA process

description: The consequence of the failure mode on the next higher-level assembly, end customer, or end user. Describes what the customer experiences if the failure mode occurs (e.g., 'loss of motor control', 'vehicle does not start', 'operator injury').
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 4 – Function Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: The specific manner in which the design element could potentially fail to meet its intended function or design intent (e.g., 'fracture under load', 'electrical short circuit', 'corrosion of contact surface'). Core DFMEA field per AIAG-VDA Step 3.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 3 – Structure Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Date of the most recent revision to the DFMEA document. Tracks the currency of the risk assessment and supports document control requirements.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: The model year or product generation to which this DFMEA applies. Used for product lifecycle tracking and APQP milestone alignment.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; APQP 2nd Edition

business_glossary_term: Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) Number
description: Business-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the DFMEA document, used for cross-referencing in APQP deliverables, PPAP packages, and Teamcenter PLM. Unique within the organization.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook 1st Edition; APQP 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Numeric rating (1–10) estimating the likelihood that the failure cause will occur and result in the failure mode. A rating of 10 indicates near-certain occurrence; 1 indicates extremely unlikely. Based on historical data, design similarity, and engineering judgment.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or engineering site responsible for the product design covered by this DFMEA. Supports multi-site and multinational operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Submission Required
description: Indicates whether this DFMEA is required as part of a PPAP submission package to the customer. When true, the DFMEA must meet customer-specific requirements (CSR) for PPAP level compliance.
reference: PPAP 4th Edition; AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Date on which the DFMEA was initially prepared and documented. Used for APQP timeline tracking and document control.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; APQP 2nd Edition

description: Name or employee identifier of the engineer or analyst who originally prepared and authored the DFMEA document.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

description: Specific corrective or preventive action recommended to reduce severity, occurrence, or detection ratings for high-priority failure modes. May include design changes, additional testing, material substitution, or tolerance revisions.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6 – Optimization; CAPA process; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Indicates whether this DFMEA addresses failure modes related to regulatory compliance requirements such as RoHS, REACH, CE Marking, UL certification, or OSHA/EPA mandates. Triggers mandatory compliance review workflow.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; CE Marking Directive; UL Standards; OSHA; EPA

description: Name or employee identifier of the design engineer responsible for implementing the recommended action and updating the DFMEA. Assigned per AIAG-VDA Step 6 accountability requirements.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Updated detection rating after implementation of recommended actions. Reflects improved detection capability post-mitigation.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Updated occurrence rating after implementation of recommended actions. Reflects the reduced likelihood of the failure cause occurring post-mitigation.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Recalculated Risk Priority Number (Revised S × Revised O × Revised D) after corrective actions have been implemented. Used to confirm risk reduction and validate DFMEA closure.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; APQP 2nd Edition

description: Updated severity rating after implementation of recommended actions. Reflects the residual risk level post-mitigation. Compared against original severity to demonstrate risk reduction effectiveness.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the current version of the DFMEA document (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02). Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or design update.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Risk Priority Number calculated as the product of Severity × Occurrence × Detection ratings (S × O × D). Ranges from 1 to 1000. Used to prioritize failure modes requiring corrective action. Note: AIAG-VDA also uses Action Priority (AP) as a complementary risk metric.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 5; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; APQP 2nd Edition

description: Indicates whether any failure mode in this DFMEA has a severity rating of 9 or 10, classifying it as safety-critical or regulatory-critical. Safety-critical DFMEAs require mandatory escalation and additional design validation per ISO 26262, IEC 61508, or applicable product safety standards.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; IEC 61508; UL Product Safety Certification; CE Marking Directive

description: Narrative description of the specific component, assembly, or system covered by this DFMEA, including functional boundaries and interfaces considered in the analysis.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Defines the hierarchical level of the design analysis scope: component (individual part), subsystem (grouped components), assembly (full assembly), or system (complete product system). Determines the depth and breadth of the FMEA analysis.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 1 – Planning and Preparation; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Numeric rating (1–10) indicating the seriousness of the failure effect on the customer or end user. A rating of 10 indicates a safety-critical failure with no warning; 1 indicates no discernible effect. Defined per AIAG-VDA severity criteria.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Current lifecycle status of the DFMEA record: draft (initial creation), in_review (under engineering review), approved (formally approved and baselined), closed (all actions completed), obsolete (superseded by a newer revision).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook

description: Planned date by which the recommended action must be completed and verified. Used for APQP milestone tracking and PPAP readiness assessment.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook Step 6; APQP 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Unique document identifier assigned by Siemens Teamcenter PLM for the DFMEA document object. Enables direct traceability to the authoritative source document in the PLM/PDM system.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Descriptive title of the DFMEA document, typically referencing the product, component, or assembly being analyzed (e.g., 'Motor Drive Assembly DFMEA – Electrification Module').
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Engineering Change Order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Management Object ID

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: eco.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the parent ECN that this ECO implements. The ECO is the formal authorization child of the ECN (ECN proposes, ECO authorizes implementation). Replacing with FK e
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Actual Implementation Cost
description: Actual total cost incurred to implement the engineering change, captured upon completion. Compared against estimated_cost for variance analysis and continuous improvement of change cost estimation accuracy.
reference: IFRS; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA CO – Cost Object Controlling; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.3

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Affected Bill of Materials (BOM) Revisions
description: Comma-separated list or description of the Bill of Materials (BOM) revision identifiers that are created or updated as a result of this ECO. Provides traceability between the change order and specific BOM versions in SAP PP and Teamcenter.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Change Number; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – BOM Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Affected Drawing Revisions
description: Comma-separated list or description of the engineering drawing revision identifiers (CAD/CAM drawings) that are updated as part of this ECO. Ensures drawing version control traceability in Teamcenter PDM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Document Management; ANSI Y14.35 – Revision of Engineering Drawings; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Affected Part Numbers
description: Comma-separated list of material/part numbers (SAP material numbers or Teamcenter item IDs) directly affected by this engineering change. Used for impact analysis and to identify all downstream BOM assemblies requiring update.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Master; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Item Revision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Affected Routing Revisions
description: Comma-separated list or description of the production routing (Bill of Process/BoP) revision identifiers updated by this ECO. Routing changes affect work center assignments, operation sequences, and standard times in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Routing Change Number; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – BoP Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: The date on which the ECO received final authorization to proceed with implementation. Marks the transition from approval workflow to release for manufacturing execution.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Approval Date

description: Severity classification of the engineering change. Class 1 (Major) changes affect form, fit, or function and require full PPAP re-submission; Class 2 (Minor) changes require limited validation; Class 3 (Administrative) changes are documentation-only.
reference: PPAP 4th Edition – Change Level Classification; APQP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; Customer-specific requirements

description: Classification of the type of engineering change being implemented. Determines which downstream systems (SAP PP BOM, Teamcenter drawings, routing) must be updated and which approval workflows are triggered.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; APQP/PPAP requirements

description: The actual date on which all implementation tasks for the ECO were confirmed complete, including BOM updates, drawing revisions, routing changes, and shop floor verification. Used for cycle time analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Closure Date

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country where this engineering change is being implemented. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance tracking and regional reporting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 – Country Codes; EU RoHS/REACH; CE Marking; GDPR Article 4

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated and actual cost fields (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Customer Approval Required Flag
description: Indicates whether formal written customer approval must be obtained before the engineering change can be implemented. Applicable for changes to customer-specific designs or regulated products under customer quality agreements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; PPAP 4th Edition; Customer-specific quality requirements

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Customer Notification Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the customer must be formally notified of this engineering change prior to or upon implementation. Required for changes affecting form, fit, or function on customer-specific products or under contractual SLA obligations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; Customer-specific quality requirements; SLA agreements; PPAP 4th Edition

description: Detailed narrative describing the technical change being implemented, including what is changing, why it is changing, and the expected outcome. Supports traceability and regulatory audit requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Order Description

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Disposition of Existing Stock
description: Defines how existing finished goods and raw material inventory affected by the change are to be handled. Determines whether pre-change stock can be consumed, must be reworked, or must be scrapped.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Inventory Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Internal ECO SOP

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Disposition of Work in Progress (WIP)
description: Defines how existing Work in Progress (WIP) inventory and in-process assemblies are to be handled at the time the engineering change becomes effective. Drives shop floor instructions and material disposition in SAP MM/PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM – Material Disposition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 – Control of Nonconforming Outputs; Internal ECO SOP

description: The calendar date on which the engineering change becomes effective in production, applicable when effectivity_type is 'date_based'. Used to schedule BOM and routing updates in SAP PP and Teamcenter.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Valid-From Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Effectivity Lot Number From
description: The starting production lot number from which the engineering change becomes effective, applicable when effectivity_type is 'lot_based'. All lots at or above this number incorporate the change.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Lot Effectivity; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Effectivity Management

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Effectivity Serial Number From
description: The starting serial number from which the engineering change becomes effective, applicable when effectivity_type is 'serial_number_based'. All units with serial numbers at or above this value incorporate the change.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Serial Number Effectivity; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Effectivity Management

description: Defines the method by which the engineering change becomes effective in production. Date-based effectivity triggers on a calendar date; serial-number-based triggers at a specific unit; lot-based triggers at a specific production lot. Drives SAP PP BOM and routing effectivity configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Effectivity; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Effectivity Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Estimated Implementation Cost
description: Estimated total cost to implement the engineering change, including engineering labor, tooling, material scrap/rework, and validation costs. Used for change impact assessment, CAPEX/OPEX planning, and ROI analysis.
reference: IFRS; GAAP; Internal Engineering Change Management SOP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: The mandatory completion date by which all BOM revisions, drawing revisions, routing revisions, and shop floor updates must be fully implemented. Used for scheduling, escalation management, and compliance tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; Internal Engineering Change Management SOP; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Human-readable, business-facing identifier for the Engineering Change Order as assigned by the PLM/PDM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for cross-system referencing in SAP PP, shop floor communications, and audit trails.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Order Object; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6 – Control of Changes

description: The organizational department that initiated the engineering change request (e.g., Product Engineering, Quality, Manufacturing, Customer Service). Used for change volume analytics and departmental accountability reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3; Internal Engineering Change Management SOP

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or facilities where the engineering change must be implemented. Supports multi-plant rollout coordination and plant-specific BOM/routing updates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Plant (Werk); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Submission Level
description: The PPAP submission level required for this engineering change, ranging from Level 1 (Part Submission Warrant only) to Level 5 (full documentation retained at customer location). Determined by customer requirements and change classification.
reference: PPAP 4th Edition – AIAG; Customer-specific quality requirements

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) submission is required for this engineering change, typically mandated for Class 1 (Major) changes affecting form, fit, or function for automotive or regulated customers.
reference: PPAP 4th Edition – AIAG; APQP; Customer-specific quality requirements; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Business priority assigned to the ECO, reflecting urgency of implementation. Critical priority ECOs (e.g., safety or regulatory-driven) are fast-tracked through approval and implementation workflows.
reference: Internal Engineering Change Management SOP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Standardized reason code indicating the primary business driver for the engineering change. Used for root cause analysis, change trend reporting, and CAPA linkage.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 – Nonconformity and Corrective Action; CAPA process; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Regulatory Driven Flag
description: Indicates whether this engineering change is mandated by a regulatory requirement (e.g., RoHS/REACH compliance, CE Marking, UL certification, OSHA safety requirement). Regulatory-driven ECOs receive elevated priority and mandatory completion tracking.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation 1907/2006; CE Marking Directive; UL Standards; OSHA 29 CFR; ISO 14001:2015

description: Specific regulatory standard, directive, or requirement number that mandates this engineering change (e.g., 'RoHS 2011/65/EU', 'REACH SVHC List Update', 'CE Marking EN 60204-1'). Populated only when regulatory_driven is true.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation 1907/2006; CE Marking; UL Standards; ISO 14001:2015; ISO 45001:2018

description: The date on which the ECO was formally released to manufacturing and the updated BOM/routing/drawings were published in SAP PP and Teamcenter for shop floor execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Change Release Date; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Release Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: The date on which the ECO was formally requested or initiated. Used for lead time analysis and change management performance reporting.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Order Creation Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Name or employee identifier of the lead engineer accountable for coordinating and completing the ECO implementation. This is the primary point of contact for all technical questions related to the change.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 – Organizational Roles; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Responsible Person
