description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this ECO record was sourced (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Policy; NIST Cybersecurity Framework – Data Provenance

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) Source System Identifier
description: The native identifier of this ECO record in the originating operational system (e.g., Teamcenter Change Order UID or SAP Change Number). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse and the system of record.
reference: Internal Data Governance Policy; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Object UID; SAP S/4HANA – Change Number

description: Current lifecycle status of the ECO, tracking its progression from initial drafting through approval, release to manufacturing, implementation, and closure. Drives workflow routing in Teamcenter and SAP PP.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Order Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Short, descriptive title summarizing the nature and scope of the engineering change. Used in dashboards, approval workflows, and stakeholder communications.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Order Title Field; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying an individual operation step within a Bill of Process. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Operation; Siemens Teamcenter Manufacturing Process Step

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: bop_operation is a child record of bop — each operation step belongs to a Bill of Process header. Without this FK, bop_operation is siloed from its parent. This is the fundamental parent-child relatio
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.pfmea. Business justification: bop_operation.pfmea_reference_number is a STRING reference to the PFMEA that analyzes failure modes for this operation. Replacing with FK pfmea_id → pfmea.pfmea_id enforces the PFMEA-to-operation trac
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.tooling_equipment. Business justification: BOP operations specify required tooling and equipment. Production scheduling and maintenance systems use this to ensure correct tools are available, calibrated, and assigned to workstations before ope
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference quantity to which the standard time values (machine time, labor time) apply. For example, a machine time of 5 minutes per base quantity of 100 pieces. Corresponds to SAP PP BMSCH field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (BMSCH)

description: Unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., EA for each, PC for pieces, KG for kilograms). Aligns with SAP unit of measure configuration and ISO 80000 measurement standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module (MEINS); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: SAP PP control key (e.g., PP01, PP03, QM01) that governs the scheduling, costing, confirmation, and quality inspection behavior of the operation. Determines whether the operation is relevant for capacity planning, external processing, or quality inspection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (STEUS); SAP PP Routing Configuration

description: SAP cost center code associated with the work center performing this operation. Used for cost allocation, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation, and financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module (KOSTL); GAAP; IFRS IAS 2

description: Timestamp when this BoP operation record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the external processing price (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency operations in multinational manufacturing environments.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Reference number of the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that introduced or last modified this operation. Provides full traceability of process changes to engineering change management records.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; SAP S/4HANA PP ECN; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Date from which this operation version becomes valid and can be used in production planning and execution. Supports date-effective routing management and Engineering Change Notice (ECN) implementation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (DATUV); ECN/ECO Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Date after which this operation version is no longer valid. Used to manage operation version supersession during Engineering Change Order (ECO) implementation and routing revision cycles.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (DATUB); ECN/ECO Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Standard price per unit for externally processed operations performed by a subcontractor. Used in product costing and purchase order creation for subcontracting. Corresponds to SAP PP PEINH/PREIS fields.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories); GAAP

description: Flag indicating whether this operation involves handling of hazardous materials, chemicals, or substances subject to REACH, RoHS, or OSHA regulations. Triggers mandatory safety documentation and PPE requirements on the shop floor.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 (Hazard Communication); EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; ISO 45001:2018

description: Code or identifier of the inspection characteristic group (master inspection characteristic catalog) assigned to this operation for in-process quality control. Defines the measurement parameters and tolerances to be checked.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SPC (Statistical Process Control)

description: Flag indicating whether this operation requires a quality inspection step (in-process inspection). When true, inspection characteristics and AQL (Acceptable Quality Level) parameters must be defined for this operation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 (Release of Products); AQL Standards

description: Standard wait and move time in minutes between this operation and the next operation in the routing. Includes queue time, move time, and wait time. Used in lead time calculation and scheduling. Corresponds to SAP PP interoperation times.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; ISO 22400-2 Lead Time KPIs

description: Standard labor time in minutes per base quantity unit required for this operation. Used for workforce planning, labor cost calculation, and takt time analysis. Corresponds to SAP PP ARBZE field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (ARBZE); ISO 22400-2 KPI Framework; Kronos Workforce Central

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this BoP operation record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); GDPR Article 5

description: Detailed narrative description of the operation including step-by-step instructions, safety notes, and quality requirements. Supports shop floor instruction generation and Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1 (Controlled Production)

description: Standard machine processing time in minutes per base quantity unit for this operation. Used for capacity planning, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and production scheduling. Corresponds to SAP PP BEAZE field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (BEAZE); ISO 22400-2 OEE KPI; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Maximum production quantity that can be processed in a single run of this operation. Constrains batch sizes for capacity and quality management purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; MRP II Planning Standards

description: Minimum production quantity that can be processed in a single run of this operation. Used in production planning and scheduling to enforce batch size constraints.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; MRP II Planning Standards

description: Identifier of the CNC (Computer Numerical Control) or NC program used to execute this operation on automated machinery. Links to the CAM (Computer-Aided Manufacturing) system for program retrieval.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM (CAM Integration); ISO 6983 (NC Programming); CAM Standards

description: Number of parallel machines or labor resources required to execute this operation simultaneously. Used for capacity planning and scheduling. Corresponds to SAP PP ANZZL field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (ANZZL); ISO 22400-2 Capacity KPIs

description: Short descriptive text for the operation step, summarizing the manufacturing activity performed (e.g., 'Weld Frame Assembly', 'CNC Milling – Housing', 'Final Inspection'). Corresponds to SAP PP LTXA1 field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (LTXA1); Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Sequential numeric identifier for the operation step within the routing or Bill of Process, typically in increments of 10 (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Corresponds to the SAP PP routing operation number (VORNR) and Teamcenter process step sequence number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (VORNR); Siemens Teamcenter PLM Manufacturing Process

description: Classification of the operation indicating whether it is performed in-house, by an external subcontractor, or as a quality inspection step. Drives procurement and costing logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 (External Provision)

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this operation is performed. Supports multi-plant BoP management for multinational operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (WERKS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Determines the scheduling direction for this operation in production planning — forward scheduling (from start date), backward scheduling (from required finish date), or current date. Corresponds to SAP PP scheduling parameters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; MRP II Scheduling Standards

description: Standard setup time in minutes required to prepare the work center for this operation, including tooling changeover and machine configuration. Used in capacity planning, scheduling, and SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Dies) analysis. Corresponds to SAP PP RUSTE field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (RUSTE); SMED Methodology; ISO 22400-2 KPI Framework

description: Document number or identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the execution of this operation. Links to the document management system for shop floor instruction retrieval.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this BoP operation was sourced (e.g., SAP_PP for SAP S/4HANA PP routing, TEAMCENTER for Siemens Teamcenter manufacturing process steps). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 (Data Provenance); Enterprise Data Governance Standards

description: SAP CO activity type code used to assign standard costs to this operation (e.g., machine hours, labor hours). Links the operation to cost center accounting and product costing. Corresponds to SAP CO LSTAR field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module (LSTAR); GAAP; IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories)

description: Current lifecycle status of the BoP operation record. 'Active' operations are available for production order assignment; 'obsolete' operations are retained for historical traceability but cannot be used in new routings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Document Control)

description: Vendor or supplier code for externally processed operations where the operation is performed by a subcontractor. Applicable when operation_type is 'external' or 'subcontracting'. Corresponds to SAP PP external processing vendor.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (External Processing); SAP Ariba Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Standard teardown or post-processing time in minutes required after the operation is completed, including cleaning, tool removal, and work center reset. Corresponds to SAP PP ABRUE field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (ABRUE); ISO 22400-2 KPI Framework

description: Reference identifier for the tooling list or production resource/tool (PRT) assignment associated with this operation. Specifies jigs, fixtures, cutting tools, and gauges required. Corresponds to SAP PP PRT assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (Production Resource/Tool); Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Version identifier for this operation step, incremented when the operation is revised through the engineering change process. Enables version-controlled routing management and historical comparison.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA PP Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the work center (machine, production line, or labor group) where this operation is performed. Corresponds to SAP PP ARBPL field and links to the work center master data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (ARBPL); ISO 22400 Manufacturing Operations Management KPIs

description: Human-readable name of the work center where the operation is executed (e.g., 'CNC Cell 3', 'Welding Station A', 'Final Assembly Line 2'). Supports shop floor reporting and scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Opcenter MES

business_glossary_term: Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) ID
description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each PFMEA record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 6.1

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: Process FMEA is conducted for a specific Bill of Process (manufacturing routing). Linking pfmea to bop at the header level provides traceability from the process risk analysis to the complete process
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: pfmea.ecn_reference is a STRING reference to the ECN associated with this PFMEA update. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for process risk analysis. ecn_ref
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: Process FMEA is a specialized FMEA type managed in quality systems. Manufacturing engineering uses quality FMEA structure for process risk analysis and mitigation tracking.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual date on which the recommended action was completed and evidence of implementation was recorded. Used to close out action items in CAPA workflows and update the PFMEA with revised ratings.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: AIAG-VDA 2019 Action Priority classification (High/Medium/Low) derived from the combination of Severity, Occurrence, and Detection ratings using the AP lookup table. Supersedes sole reliance on RPN for prioritization in the updated PFMEA methodology.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.5.6

description: Name or role of the person or team responsible for implementing the recommended action. Used for accountability tracking in CAPA workflows and PFMEA review meetings.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Target date by which the recommended action must be completed and verified. Monitored in SAP QM CAPA tracking and PFMEA review cycles to ensure timely risk mitigation.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949:2016

description: Date on which the PFMEA document was formally approved and released for production use. Establishes the effective date for quality control plan alignment and PPAP submission records.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

description: Name or employee identifier of the quality or engineering authority who formally approved the PFMEA document for release. Required for PPAP Level 3/4 submission and customer-facing quality documentation.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; IATF 16949:2016

description: Reference number of the linked Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record in SAP QM or the Quality Management System (QMS). Establishes traceability between the PFMEA risk item and the formal corrective action workflow.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 10.2.3

description: Reference number of the associated SPC Control Plan that incorporates the detection controls and inspection frequencies derived from this PFMEA. Links PFMEA outputs to Siemens Opcenter quality inspection plans.
reference: AIAG Control Plan Reference Manual; APQP Reference Manual; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.6.2

description: Timestamp when the PFMEA record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and PFMEA lifecycle reporting in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: Description of existing process controls designed to detect the failure mode or failure cause before the product reaches the next operation or customer (e.g., 'In-process CMM inspection', 'Vision system check', 'SPC control chart monitoring').
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Description of existing process controls designed to prevent the failure cause or failure mode from occurring (e.g., 'Poka-Yoke fixture', 'Operator SOP training', 'Incoming material inspection'). Supports Poka-Yoke and error-proofing documentation.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the ability of current detection controls to identify the failure cause or failure mode before the product leaves the process. A rating of 1 indicates near-certain detection; 10 indicates no detection capability.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; IATF 16949:2016

description: Root cause or mechanism by which the failure mode could occur in the process. Describes the specific process deficiency, variation source, or error condition (e.g., 'Worn electrode tip', 'Operator setup error', 'Material hardness variation').
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4 – Failure Analysis; ANSI/ASQ Q9001

description: Description of the consequence or impact of the failure mode on the next operation, end customer, or product quality. Used to justify the severity rating and drive prioritization of corrective actions.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4 – Failure Analysis; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.5.6

description: Description of the specific way in which the process could potentially fail to meet its intended function or process requirement. Core element of the PFMEA risk assessment (e.g., 'Insufficient weld penetration', 'Incorrect torque applied').
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4 – Failure Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 6.1

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the PFMEA record. Supports change detection in ETL pipelines, audit compliance, and version control within the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5; IATF 16949:2016

description: Name or employee identifier of the lead process engineer responsible for authoring, maintaining, and updating the PFMEA document. Primary point of contact for PFMEA-related quality inquiries.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1 – Planning and Preparation; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Model year or program year associated with the product or platform for which this PFMEA was developed. Used for APQP program tracking and PPAP submission documentation.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); APQP Reference Manual; PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the PFMEA to ensure it remains current with process changes, quality data trends, and engineering change orders. Supports proactive risk management per IATF 16949 requirements.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.3

business_glossary_term: Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) Number
description: Business-assigned document control number for the PFMEA, used for cross-referencing in quality management systems, Siemens Teamcenter PLM, and SAP QM. Follows the enterprise document numbering convention.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; IATF 16949:2016

description: Numeric rating (1–10) estimating the likelihood or frequency of the failure cause occurring during production. Based on historical process data, Statistical Process Control (SPC) data, or engineering judgment per AIAG-VDA criteria.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the process being analyzed is executed. Supports multi-site PFMEA management and plant-level quality reporting across global operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Plant Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 4.3.1

description: The intended function or purpose of the process step being analyzed, describing what the operation is designed to achieve (e.g., 'Join two steel components with specified weld penetration depth').
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 3 – Function Analysis

description: Detailed description of the specific process step or sub-operation within the BoP operation being analyzed in this PFMEA record. Provides granular context for the failure mode analysis.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Specific corrective or preventive action recommended to reduce the severity, occurrence, or detection rating of the identified failure mode. Drives CAPA initiation in SAP QM and Siemens Opcenter quality workflows.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949:2016

description: Updated detection rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects improved detection capability resulting from new inspection methods, SPC controls, or automated detection systems.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; IATF 16949:2016

description: Updated occurrence rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects the reduced likelihood of the failure cause occurring following process improvements or error-proofing measures.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; IATF 16949:2016

description: Recalculated Risk Priority Number (Revised S × Revised O × Revised D) after completion of recommended actions. Demonstrates risk reduction effectiveness and supports PFMEA closure criteria.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Updated severity rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects the residual risk level post-mitigation and is used to recalculate the revised RPN for the PFMEA record.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; IATF 16949:2016

description: Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the current version of the PFMEA document. Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or process change that impacts the risk assessment.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management

description: Risk Priority Number calculated as the product of Severity × Occurrence × Detection ratings (range 1–1000). Used to prioritize failure modes requiring corrective action. High RPN values trigger mandatory CAPA initiation per quality management policy.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 6.1; IATF 16949:2016

description: Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the seriousness of the failure effect on the customer or next process. A rating of 10 indicates a safety or regulatory compliance impact. Defined per AIAG-VDA severity evaluation criteria.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 6.1; IATF 16949:2016

description: Current lifecycle status of the PFMEA document. Controls workflow routing in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and determines whether the PFMEA is active for production use in Siemens Opcenter MES quality inspection plans.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3

description: Comma-separated list of names or employee IDs of cross-functional team members who participated in the PFMEA development session. Documents team composition for APQP and PPAP submission requirements.
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1 – Planning and Preparation; APQP Reference Manual; IATF 16949:2016

description: Descriptive title of the PFMEA document, typically referencing the process, product family, or manufacturing operation being analyzed (e.g., 'Welding Process PFMEA – Automation Frame Assembly').
reference: AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: SAP work center or Opcenter resource identifier where the manufacturing operation is performed. Provides traceability from the PFMEA risk item to the physical production resource on the shop floor.
reference: SAP PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES Resource Management; AIAG-VDA PFMEA Handbook (2019)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual BOM line item record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the bom_line data product.
reference: SAP PP BOM Item; Siemens Teamcenter BOM Line

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: Each BOM line item (component) corresponds to a CAD model in the PLM/PDM system. Linking bom_line to cad_model provides direct traceability from the product structure to the 3D geometry definition, en
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that authorized the creation or modification of this BOM line item. Supports full change traceability and audit compliance.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter Change Management; SAP PP ECN (AENR)

description: Reference to the parent BOM header to which this line item belongs. Enables multi-level BOM explosion and parent-child component hierarchy traversal for MRP and production planning.
reference: SAP PP CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2; Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: BOM line items for raw material or semi-finished components must reference the applicable material specification defining chemical composition, mechanical properties, and compliance requirements. This
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Every BOM line specifies a component quantity in a unit of measure (each, kg, meter). Referencing the shared UOM table ensures consistency across engineering, procurement, and production quantity calc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Groups multiple BOM line items that are interchangeable alternatives for the same functional requirement. During MRP and production order creation, the system selects the preferred or available alternative based on priority and stock availability. Supports substitute component management and supply chain flexibility.
reference: SAP PP STPO-EWAHR Alternative Item; Siemens Teamcenter Substitute Parts

description: Numeric priority rank within an alternative item group, where lower numbers indicate higher preference (1 = first choice). Used by MRP and production planning to determine which substitute component to consume first when multiple alternatives are defined for the same functional position.
reference: SAP PP STPO-PRIOK; APICS Substitute Component Priority

description: The probability percentage (0–100) that this alternative component will be selected over other alternatives in the same group during production planning. Used by MRP to distribute planned requirements across alternative components proportionally, supporting multi-source procurement strategies.
reference: SAP PP STPO-EWAHR; APICS Probabilistic BOM

description: Sequential item number assigned to this BOM line within the parent BOM header, typically in increments of 10 (e.g., 0010, 0020). Used for ordering and referencing line items within the BOM structure in SAP PP.
reference: SAP PP STPO-POSNR; ISO 10303-44 Product Structure

description: The hierarchical depth level of this component within the multi-level BOM structure, where level 0 is the finished good, level 1 is the first-tier sub-assembly, and so on. Used for BOM explosion reporting, MRP low-level code assignment, and production scheduling sequencing.
reference: SAP PP Low-Level Code (MARA-LLGPF); APICS BOM Level; ISO 10303-44

description: Defines the business purpose for which this BOM is used, corresponding to SAP BOM usage keys (e.g., 1=Production, 2=Engineering/Design, 3=Universal, 4=Plant Maintenance, 5=Sales, 6=Costing). Determines which BOM is selected during MRP, costing, sales order processing, and maintenance planning.
reference: SAP PP STKO-STLAN; Siemens Teamcenter BOM View; APICS BOM Types

description: The manufacturer or internal part number of the child component as defined in the material master or PLM system. Used for procurement, inventory lookup, and cross-referencing with supplier catalogs and engineering drawings.
reference: SAP MM MARA-MATNR; Siemens Teamcenter Part Number; ISO 10303-44

description: The timestamp when this BOM line record was originally created in the source system. Used for audit trail, change history analysis, and data lineage tracking in compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.
reference: SAP PP STPO-ERDAT/ERZET; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5

description: The date after which this BOM line item is no longer valid for production planning and MRP explosion. Used to phase out obsolete components following an Engineering Change Notice (ECN) while maintaining historical BOM traceability for previously manufactured products.
reference: SAP PP STPO-DATUB; Siemens Teamcenter Effectivity Management; ISO 10303-44

description: The date from which this BOM line item becomes valid and active for use in production planning and MRP explosion. Supports date-effective BOM management for engineering change implementation, ensuring new component versions are introduced at the correct production date.
reference: SAP PP STPO-DATUV; Siemens Teamcenter Effectivity Management; ISO 10303-44

description: Balloon or find number referencing the component's callout position on the engineering drawing or assembly diagram. Used by manufacturing engineers and quality inspectors to locate the component on the drawing during First Article Inspection (FAI) and assembly.
reference: ANSI Y14.5; Siemens Teamcenter Drawing Reference; ASME Y14.100

description: Indicates whether the component quantity on this BOM line is fixed regardless of the production order quantity (true) or variable and proportional to the order quantity (false). Fixed quantities are used for setup materials, tooling, or components consumed once per batch regardless of batch size.
reference: SAP PP STPO-FMENG; APICS Fixed vs. Variable BOM Quantity

description: Indicates whether this BOM line item is a bulk material (e.g., adhesives, lubricants, solder paste, wire) that is consumed in variable quantities and typically not individually issued to production orders. Bulk materials are often backflushed or managed via replenishment rather than discrete picking.
reference: SAP PP STPO-SCHGT; Siemens Opcenter MES Material Management

description: Indicates whether this BOM line item is a co-product or by-product produced alongside the primary output of the manufacturing process. Co-products have positive quantity and receive cost allocation; by-products may have negative cost impact. Relevant for process manufacturing and chemical/electrification solution production.
reference: SAP PP Co-Product (STPO-KZKUP); ISO 14040 Life Cycle Assessment

description: Indicates whether this BOM line represents a phantom assembly — a logical grouping of components that is not physically stocked or produced as a discrete item but is exploded through during MRP to directly consume its child components. Common in modular product structures and configurable BOMs.
reference: SAP PP Phantom Assembly (STPO-POSTP=P); APICS Phantom BOM; Siemens Teamcenter Phantom

description: The warehouse storage location from which this component is issued to the production order or work order. Drives automatic goods issue (backflush) and pick list generation in SAP WM/WMS and Infor WMS for shop floor material replenishment.
reference: SAP PP STPO-LGORT; Infor WMS Storage Location; SAP WM

description: Classification of the BOM line item type as defined in SAP PP. Stock items (L) are warehouse-managed materials; non-stock items (N) are procured directly to the order; document items (D) reference engineering documents; phantom assemblies (P) are logical groupings not physically stocked; variable-size items (R) have variable dimensions.
reference: SAP PP STPO-POSTP; ISO 10303-44 BOM Item Type

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this BOM line record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements for engineering change management and quality management system (QMS) compliance.
reference: SAP PP STPO-AEDAT/AEZET; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter Change History

description: Extended descriptive text or engineering notes associated with this BOM line item. May include assembly instructions, special handling requirements, quality inspection notes, or engineering change justifications visible to production planners and shop floor operators.
reference: SAP PP BOM Item Long Text; Siemens Teamcenter BOM Note; ISO 9001:2015 Documented Information

description: The routing operation or activity number to which this BOM component is assigned for consumption. Links the component to the specific manufacturing step in the Bill of Process (BoP) where it is consumed, enabling accurate work-in-progress (WIP) tracking and operation-level material staging.
reference: SAP PP STPO-VORNR; Siemens Opcenter MES Operation; BoP (Bill of Process)

description: The number of days (positive or negative) by which the component requirement date is offset from the production order start date. Used in multi-level scheduling to stage component availability at the correct operation step, supporting JIT (Just-In-Time) material staging on the shop floor.
reference: SAP PP STPO-EWAHR; APICS Lead Time Offset; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: The SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or production site for which this BOM line is valid. BOMs can be plant-specific to accommodate local sourcing, manufacturing processes, or regional regulatory requirements across the multinational enterprise.
reference: SAP PP STKO-WERKS; ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; SAP Organizational Structure

description: Indicates whether the component on this BOM line is manufactured in-house (make), externally procured (buy), or can be sourced either way. Drives MRP planning logic to generate either planned production orders or purchase requisitions for the component.
reference: SAP MM MARC-BESKZ; APICS Make vs. Buy Decision; SAP Ariba Procurement

description: The quantity of the child component required to produce one unit of the parent assembly as specified on this BOM line. Used directly by MRP (Material Requirements Planning) to calculate gross requirements and by production planning for work order component staging.
reference: SAP PP STPO-MENGE; ISO 10303-44 Quantity; APICS MRP Standard

description: Alphanumeric label identifying the specific physical location(s) of a component on a printed circuit board (PCB) or assembly drawing (e.g., R1, C12, U5). Critical for electronics manufacturing, PCB assembly, and automated optical inspection (AOI) processes.
reference: IEC 61346; ANSI/IEEE 315; SAP PP BOM Reference Designator (STPO-EWAHR)

description: The anticipated scrap or yield loss percentage for this component during the manufacturing process, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%). MRP inflates the gross requirement by this factor to account for expected material loss, ensuring sufficient components are procured or issued to production orders.
reference: SAP PP STPO-AUSSS; APICS MRP Scrap Factor; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this BOM line record was sourced (e.g., SAP PP for production BOMs, Siemens Teamcenter for engineering/design BOMs). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between PLM and ERP BOM structures in the silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; Data Governance Best Practice

description: The natural or composite key of this BOM line record as it exists in the originating source system (e.g., SAP BOM number + item number + plant, or Teamcenter BOM line UID). Enables traceability back to the system of record and supports delta load reconciliation in the lakehouse ingestion pipeline.
reference: SAP PP STKO-STLNR + STPO-POSNR; Siemens Teamcenter BOM Line UID; Data Lineage Standard

description: Specifies a special procurement strategy for this component, such as subcontracting (component sent to external vendor for processing), consignment stock, inter-plant stock transfer, or direct production. Overrides the standard procurement type for specific supply chain scenarios.
reference: SAP MM MARC-SOBSL; SAP Ariba Subcontracting; APICS Special Procurement

description: Current lifecycle status of this BOM line item. Active lines are included in MRP explosion and production planning. Pending approval lines are awaiting ECN authorization. Obsolete lines are retained for historical traceability but excluded from planning runs.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter BOM Status; SAP PP BOM Item Status; ISO 9001:2015 Document Control

description: The production supply area or supermarket location on the shop floor where this component is staged for consumption at the work center. Supports Kanban and JIT (Just-In-Time) material flow management in Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP.
reference: SAP PP Supply Area (LGPLA); Siemens Opcenter MES Material Staging; Lean Manufacturing Kanban

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a CAD/CAM design model record within the PLM/PDM system. Serves as the primary key for the cad_model data product in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management Object ID

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: cad_model.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized the CAD model revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for design geometry. ecn_num
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: cad_model.lifecycle_state is a STRING that should reference the authoritative PLM lifecycle state reference table. Replacing with FK plm_lifecycle_state_id → plm_lifecycle_state.plm_lifecycle_state_id
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Maximum extent of the CAD model geometry along the X-axis in millimeters, representing the width of the component's bounding envelope. Used for packaging analysis, fixture design, and logistics planning (e.g., pallet/container sizing).
reference: ISO 10303 (STEP); DFM/DFA Analysis Standards

description: Maximum extent of the CAD model geometry along the Y-axis in millimeters, representing the depth of the component's bounding envelope. Used for packaging analysis, fixture design, and logistics planning.
reference: ISO 10303 (STEP); DFM/DFA Analysis Standards

description: Maximum extent of the CAD model geometry along the Z-axis in millimeters, representing the height of the component's bounding envelope. Used for packaging analysis, fixture design, and logistics planning.
reference: ISO 10303 (STEP); DFM/DFA Analysis Standards

description: Name of the CAD/CAM software application used to create and maintain the model (e.g., Siemens NX, CATIA V5/V6, SolidWorks). Determines file format compatibility, interoperability requirements, and licensing needs for downstream engineering and manufacturing processes.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Application Reference; ISO 10303 (STEP) Interoperability Standard

description: Version or release number of the CAD/CAM software used to create or last modify the model (e.g., 'NX 2206', 'CATIA V5 R31'). Critical for file compatibility management, migration planning, and ensuring models can be opened by the correct software version in the engineering environment.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Application Version; ISO 10303 (STEP)

description: Username or employee ID of the engineer who currently has the CAD model checked out for editing in the PLM/PDM system. A checked-out model is locked for exclusive editing. Null if the model is not currently checked out. Corresponds to Teamcenter 'Checked Out By'.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Check-Out Management

description: Date and time when the CAD model was checked out from the PLM/PDM vault for editing. Used to track concurrent engineering activities, identify long-running checkouts that may block other engineers, and support audit trails.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Check-Out Timestamp

description: Standardized classification code assigned to the CAD model based on the company's engineering parts classification system (e.g., eCl@ss, UNSPSC, or internal taxonomy). Enables design reuse, variant management, and supplier sourcing by grouping similar components.
reference: eCl@ss Standard; UNSPSC Classification; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Classification; ISO 22745

description: Classification of the engineering component or assembly represented by the CAD model. Determines BOM structure, procurement strategy (make vs. buy), and applicable design standards. 'assembly' models link to child part models via the BOM hierarchy in PLM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Item Type; ISO 10303; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Type

description: Type of coordinate system used to define the geometry of the CAD model (e.g., Cartesian, Cylindrical). Ensures correct spatial orientation when integrating models into assemblies, simulation environments, or digital twin platforms.
reference: ISO 10303 (STEP); ANSI/ASME Y14.5

description: Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where the component represented by this CAD model is designed or manufactured (e.g., DEU, USA, CHN). Used for export control compliance (ITAR/EAR), customs declarations, and regional regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; ITAR (22 CFR Parts 120-130); EAR (15 CFR Parts 730-774)

description: Date and time when the CAD model record was first created in the PLM/PDM system. Provides the starting point for the model's audit trail and lifecycle history. Corresponds to Teamcenter 'Creation Date' metadata.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Creation Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Name or employee identifier of the responsible design engineer who owns and maintains the CAD model. Accountable for model accuracy, revision control, and release approvals. Corresponds to the Teamcenter 'Owning User' field.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Owning User; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3 Design and Development

description: Current status of the Design for Assembly (DFA) analysis for this CAD model. Evaluates the ease of assembly, part count reduction opportunities, and assembly process compatibility. Relevant for assembly-type models and sub-assemblies.
reference: DFA Methodology (Boothroyd-Dewhurst); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3; APQP Standard

description: Current status of the Design for Manufacturability (DFM) analysis for this CAD model. Indicates whether the design has been reviewed for manufacturing feasibility, cost optimization, and process compatibility. A 'passed' status is typically required before release to production.
reference: DFM/DFA Methodology; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3 Design and Development; APQP Standard

description: Identifier linking this CAD model to its corresponding digital twin instance on the Siemens MindSphere IIoT platform. Enables real-time correlation between the design geometry and operational asset behavior for predictive maintenance and performance optimization.
reference: Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform - Asset Model; ISO 23247 Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework

description: Export control classification code assigned to the CAD model, indicating whether the design data is subject to export regulations (e.g., ITAR USML category, EAR ECCN code, or 'EAR99' for unrestricted). Critical for controlling access to design data shared with international suppliers or subsidiaries.
reference: ITAR (22 CFR Parts 120-130); EAR (15 CFR Parts 730-774); EU Dual-Use Regulation (2021/821)

description: Native or neutral file format of the CAD model dataset (e.g., STEP, IGES, JT, NX native). Determines interoperability with downstream systems including CAM, FEA simulation, and supplier collaboration. STEP (ISO 10303) is the preferred neutral exchange format for cross-system sharing.
reference: ISO 10303 (STEP); IGES Standard; Siemens JT Open Standard; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Dataset Format

description: Full file name of the CAD model dataset as stored in the PLM/PDM vault, including file extension (e.g., 'motor_housing_v2.prt', 'drive_shaft_asm.CATProduct'). Used for file retrieval, version tracking, and integration with CAM and simulation tools.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Dataset File Name

description: Size of the CAD model file in megabytes as stored in the PLM vault. Used for storage capacity planning, data migration scoping, and identifying oversized models that may impact system performance or collaboration efficiency.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Dataset File Size

description: Date on which the CAD model was frozen, preventing further modifications. Typically aligned with production launch milestones (e.g., PPAP approval, SOP - Start of Production). A frozen model can only be changed via a formal ECO process.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Freeze Date; APQP/PPAP Standard; ANSI/EIA-649

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this CAD model is designated as the master geometry model for the component or assembly. The master model is the authoritative source for all downstream derivations (drawings, simulations, CAM programs). Only one master model should exist per component revision.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Master Model Concept; ISO 10303 (STEP)

description: Date and time of the most recent modification to the CAD model record in the PLM/PDM system. Used for change tracking, synchronization with downstream systems (SAP, MES), and audit compliance. Corresponds to Teamcenter 'Last Modified Date'.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Last Modified Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Calculated mass of the component or assembly as derived from the CAD model geometry and assigned material properties, expressed in kilograms. Used for structural analysis, logistics weight calculations, BOM costing, and compliance with weight-sensitive design requirements.
reference: ISO 10303 (STEP); ANSI/ASME Y14.5; DFM Analysis

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the CAD/CAM model as defined by the design engineer. Typically reflects the component or assembly name (e.g., 'Motor Housing Assembly', 'Drive Shaft Bracket'). Corresponds to Teamcenter Item Name.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Item Name

description: Business-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the CAD/CAM model as registered in the PLM/PDM system. Used for cross-referencing with BOM, ECN/ECO, and engineering documentation. Corresponds to the Teamcenter Item ID.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Item ID; ISO 10303 (STEP) Product Data Standard

description: Classification of the CAD model by its geometric or representational type. Determines the applicable CAD tools, downstream processes (e.g., CAM toolpath generation, FEA simulation), and BOM linkage. '3d_solid' is the primary type for CNC machining and DFM analysis.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Dataset Type; ISO 10303 (STEP); CAD/CAM Industry Standard

description: Date on which the CAD model revision was marked as obsolete, indicating it has been superseded by a newer revision or the component has been discontinued. Used for lifecycle management, archive planning, and BOM cleanup.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Obsolescence Date; ISO 10007 Configuration Management

description: Name of the engineering team, department, or organizational group responsible for the CAD model (e.g., 'Mechanical Design', 'Electrical Engineering', 'Automation Systems'). Used for workload distribution, access control, and organizational reporting in PLM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Owning Group

description: Name or code of the primary engineering material assigned to the CAD model (e.g., 'Aluminum 6061-T6', 'Stainless Steel 316L', 'ABS Plastic'). Drives material requirements planning (MRP), cost estimation, and compliance checks for REACH/RoHS regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Classification; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Name of the product line or product family to which the designed component or assembly belongs (e.g., 'Automation Systems', 'Electrification Solutions', 'Smart Infrastructure'). Supports portfolio-level reporting and PLM product structure navigation.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Product Line Classification; SAP S/4HANA MM - Product Hierarchy

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the engineering project or program under which the CAD model was created. Links the model to project planning, resource allocation, and CAPEX tracking in SAP PS (Project System). Supports APQP phase tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS - Project Code; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Project; APQP Standard

description: Date on which the CAD model revision was formally released (approved and promoted to 'released' lifecycle state) in the PLM system. Marks the point at which the design is authorized for use in manufacturing, procurement, and BOM structures.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Release Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4 Design and Development Controls

description: Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the version of the CAD model (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02). Incremented upon each approved engineering change. Corresponds to Teamcenter Item Revision ID and is critical for configuration management and BOM accuracy.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Item Revision; ISO 10303-21; ANSI/EIA-649 Configuration Management Standard

description: Required surface finish or roughness specification for the component as defined in the CAD model (e.g., 'Ra 1.6 µm', 'Anodized', 'Powder Coated'). Drives manufacturing process selection, quality inspection criteria, and supplier specifications.
reference: ISO 1302 Surface Texture Indication; ANSI/ASME B46.1 Surface Texture

description: General tolerance class applied to the CAD model for dimensions not individually toleranced (e.g., Fine, Medium, Coarse per ISO 2768). Determines manufacturing precision requirements, process selection, and inspection rigor. Critical for DFM analysis.
reference: ISO 2768 General Tolerances; ANSI/ASME Y14.5 Dimensioning and Tolerancing

description: Base unit of measurement used in the CAD model geometry (e.g., MM for millimeters, IN for inches). Critical for dimensional accuracy, interoperability with CAM systems, and compliance with regional engineering standards. Must be consistent across all models in an assembly.
reference: ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; ANSI/ASME Y14.5 Dimensioning and Tolerancing

business_glossary_term: Component Tooling Assignment - Component Tooling Assignment Id
description: Primary key for the component_tooling_assignment association
reference: Internal - M:N Association PK

business_glossary_term: Component Tooling Assignment - Bom Line Id
description: Foreign key linking to the BOM line item (component) that requires this tooling assignment.
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

business_glossary_term: Component Tooling Assignment - Tooling Equipment Id
description: Foreign key linking to the tooling or equipment master record assigned to this component.
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: The date from which this component-tooling assignment is valid for production use. Aligns with engineering change effectivity and PPAP approval windows. Belongs to the assignment, not to the BOM line or tool master independently.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: The date after which this component-tooling assignment is no longer valid. Used to manage tool retirement, design changes, and supersession. Belongs to the assignment relationship.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this specific component-tooling assignment applies. The same component may require different tools at different plants. Belongs to the assignment because it scopes the pairing to a specific production site.
reference: SAP Plant Code (T001W)

description: Numeric order in which this tool must be set up relative to other tools required for the same BOM component. Belongs to the assignment because sequence is specific to the component-tool pairing, not to the tool or component alone.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: Classifies the functional role of the tool in manufacturing this component (e.g., FIXTURE for holding, CUTTING_TOOL for machining, GAUGE for inspection). Belongs to the assignment because the same tool may serve different roles for different components.
reference: PPAP Element 4 — Process Flow Diagram

business_glossary_term: Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) Lifecycle State ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each authorized lifecycle state record within the Teamcenter PLM governance framework.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management

description: Foreign key linking to product.lifecycle. Business justification: PLM lifecycle states in engineering must map to the commercial product lifecycle (introduction, growth, maturity, end-of-life). This synchronization drives release gates, obsolescence planning, and af
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Pipe-delimited list of target state codes to which a PLM object may transition from this state (e.g., 'IN_REVIEW|RELEASED|OBSOLETE'). Defines the authorized state machine edges for workflow enforcement.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Workflow and Lifecycle Management

description: Pipe-delimited list of PLM-managed object types to which this lifecycle state applies (e.g., 'Component|BOM|Drawing|CAD_Model|Document'). Governs which object classes can be assigned this state.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Object Type Management

business_glossary_term: Advanced Product Quality Planning (APQP) Phase Alignment
description: Maps this lifecycle state to the corresponding APQP phase, enabling traceability between PLM object maturity and the formal product quality planning process used for new product introduction.
reference: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) - AIAG Standard; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Hexadecimal color code used to visually represent this lifecycle state in PLM dashboards, engineering portals, and reporting tools. Supports intuitive visual differentiation of product data maturity stages.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - UI Configuration

description: Timestamp when this lifecycle state configuration record was first created in the data product. Provides audit trail for governance and compliance purposes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Identifies downstream operational systems that are triggered upon a PLM object entering this lifecycle state (e.g., 'SAP_MM_Material_Master|SAP_PP_BOM|Opcenter_MES'). Supports IT/OT convergence and PLM-ERP integration governance.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - SAP Integration; IT/OT Convergence; SAP S/4HANA MM/PP

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Notice (ECN) Required for Entry
description: Indicates whether a valid Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) must be referenced before a PLM object can be transitioned into this lifecycle state. Enforces formal change control governance.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Notice (ECN) Required for Exit
description: Indicates whether a valid Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) must be referenced to transition a PLM object out of this lifecycle state. Typically applies to released or frozen states.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6

description: Date from which this lifecycle state configuration is valid and available for use in PLM workflows. Supports versioned governance of lifecycle state definitions over time.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Date after which this lifecycle state configuration is no longer valid for new PLM object assignments. Null indicates the state is currently active with no planned expiry.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Indicates whether an export control classification review (e.g., EAR, ITAR, dual-use goods assessment) must be completed before a PLM object can be transitioned into this lifecycle state.
reference: U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ITAR; EU Dual-Use Regulation (EU) 2021/821

description: Reference identifier or file path for the icon image associated with this lifecycle state in the PLM user interface. Used for visual representation in Teamcenter and integrated engineering portals.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - UI Configuration

description: Indicates whether PLM objects in this state are frozen (read-only) and cannot be directly modified. Changes must be initiated via an Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) process.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6 Design and Development Changes

description: Indicates whether this lifecycle state is the default starting state assigned to newly created PLM objects. Only one state per applicable object type should be flagged as the initial state.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management

description: Indicates whether this lifecycle state constitutes a formal release milestone, meaning the PLM object is authorized for use in production, procurement, or downstream processes such as BOM activation or MES execution.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Release Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3 Design and Development

description: Indicates whether this lifecycle state is a terminal (end) state from which no further forward transitions are permitted (e.g., Obsolete, Archived). Terminal states represent the end of the active product data lifecycle.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management

description: ISO 639-1 language code for the locale in which the state_name and state_description are expressed (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'zh-CN'). Supports multinational PLM deployments with multi-language state label requirements.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; GDPR Article 12 (Multilingual Communication)

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this lifecycle state configuration record. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental data pipeline processing.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Standardized maturity level classification associated with this lifecycle state, indicating the degree of completeness and validation of the product data. Used for portfolio-level maturity reporting and APQP phase alignment.
reference: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Maximum number of calendar days a PLM object is permitted to remain in this lifecycle state before an escalation or alert is triggered. Supports governance of stale or stuck product data records.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Governance

description: Minimum number of distinct approvers required to authorize a transition into this lifecycle state. Supports multi-signatory approval requirements for safety-critical or regulatory-relevant product data.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Workflow Approval; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4

description: Pipe-delimited list of organizational roles that receive automated notifications upon a PLM object entering this lifecycle state (e.g., 'Procurement|Manufacturing_Engineering|Quality_Assurance').
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Workflow Notification

description: Indicates whether automated notifications must be sent to stakeholders (e.g., engineering team, quality, procurement) when a PLM object transitions into this lifecycle state.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Workflow Notification

description: Name of the Teamcenter PLM workflow template associated with transitions into this lifecycle state. Identifies the formal approval and review workflow process definition used to govern state entry.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Workflow Designer

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Required Indicator
description: Indicates whether a Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) submission is required as a prerequisite for transitioning a PLM object into this lifecycle state. Relevant for states representing production readiness.
reference: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) - AIAG Standard; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Indicates whether a formal quality gate review (e.g., APQP phase gate, design review, DFMEA completion check) must be passed before a PLM object can enter this lifecycle state.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Quality Management; APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4

description: Indicates whether regulatory compliance review (e.g., CE Marking, RoHS/REACH, UL certification, export control classification) must be completed before a PLM object can enter this lifecycle state.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; UL Certification Standards

description: Pipe-delimited list of organizational roles or job functions that must provide approval before a PLM object can enter this lifecycle state (e.g., 'Design_Engineer|Quality_Manager|Chief_Engineer'). Enforces the approval governance matrix.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Workflow Approval; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4 Design and Development Controls

description: Numeric ordering value that defines the canonical progression sequence of lifecycle states from concept through obsolescence. Used for sorting and maturity progression reporting.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management

business_glossary_term: State Transition Service Level Agreement (SLA) Days
description: Target number of business days within which a PLM object should complete the review and approval process to transition out of this lifecycle state. Used for engineering workflow performance monitoring against SLA targets.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Workflow Management; SLA (Service Level Agreement)

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this lifecycle state configuration was sourced (e.g., 'Teamcenter'). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage

description: High-level category grouping lifecycle states for reporting and analytics. Enables aggregation of states into broader maturity phases (e.g., all review-type states grouped under 'review').
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management; ISO 10303 Product Lifecycle

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lifecycle state within the PLM system (e.g., CONCEPT, IN_REVIEW, RELEASED, OBSOLETE). Used as the business key for state lookups and workflow routing.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management; ISO 10303 (STEP) Product Data Representation

description: Detailed narrative description of the lifecycle state, including its business meaning, entry criteria, and the maturity level it represents within the product data governance framework.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Human-readable name of the lifecycle state as displayed in the Teamcenter PLM user interface and engineering reports (e.g., 'Concept', 'Under Review', 'Released for Production', 'Obsolete').
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management

description: Operational status of this lifecycle state configuration record. 'Active' states are available for assignment to PLM objects; 'Inactive' states are suspended; 'Deprecated' states are retained for historical reference only.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management

description: Name of the Teamcenter PLM lifecycle policy (policy object) under which this state is defined. A lifecycle policy groups related states and transition rules for a specific object type family.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Policy Management

description: Native Siemens Teamcenter PLM system unique identifier (UID) for this lifecycle state object. Used for cross-system traceability and integration with Teamcenter APIs and downstream PLM-ERP interfaces.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Object Management

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the substitute component association record in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Alternate/Substitute Material Record

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: A substitute component is defined as an approved alternate for a specific primary component in the BOM. Linking substitute_component to bom_line identifies the exact BOM line item (primary component p
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: substitute_component.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized this substitution. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for approved substitut
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: substitute_component.applicable_bom_number is a STRING reference to the BOM where this substitution is applicable. Replacing with FK bom_id → bom.bom_id establishes the authoritative substitution-to-B
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Substitute components must meet the same or equivalent material specifications as the primary component they replace. Linking substitute_component to material_specification provides traceability from
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.substitution. Business justification: Engineering-approved substitute components must align with commercial substitution rules managed in the product domain. Procurement and production use this link to validate that engineering substitute
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Substitute components are typically sourced from alternative suppliers. Engineering's substitute component record must reference the procurement supplier master so buyers can immediately source the su
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or employee identifier of the authorized engineer or manager who approved this substitute component association for use in production.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4 - Design and Development Controls; SAP S/4HANA Workflow Approval

description: Date and time when the substitute component association was formally approved, providing an audit trail for quality management and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: Specifies the BOM usage context in which this substitution is applicable, aligning with SAP BOM usage categories (e.g., production BOM, engineering BOM, costing BOM).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - BOM Usage; ISO 10303-44 Product Structure

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost delta value, supporting multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Indicates the directional cost impact of using the substitute component compared to the primary component, supporting cost management and COGS analysis without storing calculated values.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO - Cost Object Controlling; IFRS/GAAP Cost Accounting Standards

description: Date and time when this substitute component association record was first created in the system, providing a full audit trail for data governance and compliance purposes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity and Accountability

description: Indicates whether the substitute component is subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR), which may restrict its use in certain geographies or customer applications and require additional compliance review.
reference: US Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ITAR; EU Dual-Use Regulation (EC) No 428/2009

description: Indicates whether the substitute component is a true form, fit, and function equivalent to the primary component, meaning it can be used without any design or process modification. Critical for engineering change and quality management decisions.
reference: ANSI/IPC-7711/7721 - Rework and Repair; MIL-STD-961 - Defense Specifications

description: Date and time when this substitute component association record was most recently updated, supporting change tracking, delta processing, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity and Accountability

description: The difference in procurement or manufacturing lead time in days between the substitute component and the primary component (substitute lead time minus primary lead time). Negative values indicate faster availability. Used in MRP scheduling and supply chain resilience planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Lead Time; MRP II Planning Parameters

description: Indicates whether this substitute component record is active and visible to the MRP engine for automatic exception handling and alternate material selection during planning runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MRP - Substitute Material in Planning; MRP II Logic

description: The business department that originated the substitute component request, used for workflow routing, accountability tracking, and cross-functional reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 - Organizational Roles; SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where this substitute component association is valid. Substitutions may be plant-specific due to local supplier availability or process constraints.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Plant-Level Material Data; ISO 3166-1

description: Numeric rank indicating the preference order when multiple substitutes exist for the same primary component. Lower values indicate higher preference (1 = most preferred). Used by MRP to sequence substitute selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Substitute Priority; MRP II Planning Logic

description: Multiplier applied to the original BOM quantity when using the substitute component. A value of 1.0 means the substitute is used in the same quantity; values other than 1.0 indicate a quantity adjustment is required (e.g., 2.0 means twice as many substitutes are needed).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Substitute Quantity Factor; MRP Material Requirements Calculation

description: Business-facing unique identifier for the substitute component association record, used for cross-system referencing and audit trails.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Substitute Material Record; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Alternate Part Management

description: Indicates whether using this substitute component triggers a regulatory compliance review, such as RoHS, REACH, CE Marking, or UL certification re-assessment. Ensures regulatory obligations are not inadvertently violated during substitution.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EU CE Marking Directive; UL Certification Requirements

description: Name or employee identifier of the person who initiated the request to establish this substitute component association, typically a supply chain planner, procurement specialist, or design engineer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 - Control of Externally Provided Processes; SAP S/4HANA Change Request Management

description: Indicates whether use of this substitute component requires explicit engineering sign-off prior to production use, beyond the standard approval captured in the status field.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3 - Design and Development; APQP Phase Gate Requirements

description: Indicates whether use of this substitute component triggers a quality revalidation activity such as First Article Inspection (FAI), updated control plan, or re-qualification testing before production release.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; PPAP Requirements; AIAG APQP Manual

description: Detailed notes describing any restrictions, limitations, or special handling instructions associated with using this substitute component, including regulatory, safety, or performance constraints.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this substitute component association record was sourced, supporting data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer - Data Lineage; NIST SP 800-53 - Information System Documentation

description: The natural or surrogate key of this record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP substitute material record key), enabling reverse lookup and delta processing in the Silver Layer ingestion pipeline.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer - Data Integration; SAP S/4HANA MM - Substitute Material Record

description: The difference in standard cost per unit between the substitute component and the primary component (substitute cost minus primary cost). Negative values indicate cost savings. Used for financial impact assessment and BOM costing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC - Product Cost Planning; IFRS IAS 2 - Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Current lifecycle and approval status of the substitute component record, governing whether it may be used in production planning and Material Requirements Planning (MRP) runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 - Control of Externally Provided Processes

description: Standardized reason code explaining why this substitute component was identified and approved. Supports root cause analysis, supply chain resilience reporting, and procurement strategy decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Change Reason; APQP Phase 2 - Product Design and Development

description: Classification of the substitution relationship: 'full' indicates the substitute is interchangeable in all contexts; 'conditional' requires specific conditions to be met; 'temporary' is time-bounded; 'emergency' is approved only for urgent supply disruptions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Substitute Material Strategy; MRP Exception Handling

description: Reference identifier for this substitute component association record in Siemens Teamcenter PLM, enabling cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the PLM system of record.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Item and Item Revision Management

description: Unit of measure applicable to the substitute component quantity, which may differ from the primary component's unit of measure when a conversion is required.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Base Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Free-text or structured description of the specific conditions under which this substitute may be used, such as 'only when primary component stock is zero', 'applicable for customer orders above 100 units', or 'valid only for non-safety-critical assemblies'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Substitute Usage Condition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The date from which this substitute component association becomes effective and may be used in production planning and BOM resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Effectivity; ISO 8601 Date Format

description: The date after which this substitute component association expires and is no longer valid for use. Null indicates no expiry. Used to manage temporary substitutions and time-bounded approvals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Effectivity; ISO 8601 Date Format

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory valuation record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the inventory_valuation data product.
