reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW Table

description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_valuation. Business justification: Inventory valuation for spare parts and MRO items is reconciled against asset valuation during financial close. Finance teams link these to ensure capitalized spare parts are correctly reflected in as
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Inventory valuation is scoped to a controlling area in manufacturing finance (e.g., plant-level vs. company-wide). Controlling area determines the costing variant and currency used for valuation runs.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Inventory valuations are calculated and frozen per fiscal period for financial reporting. Period-end close process requires accurate period assignment for audit trails.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.price_list_item. Business justification: Inventory valuation references the authoritative price list item for standard cost or transfer price. Finance uses this to ensure valuation aligns with approved pricing, eliminating duplicated price d
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: inventory_valuation currently has only a cross-domain FK (fiscal_period_id → finance) and no in-domain FK, making it siloed within the inventory domain. Adding storage_location_id connects inventory_v
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_invoice. Business justification: Inventory valuation (especially for moving average or standard cost updates) is directly impacted by supplier invoice prices. Finance and inventory accounting teams link valuations to invoices to reco
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The ABC inventory classification of the material based on consumption value or strategic importance. A = High value/high priority, B = Medium value/medium priority, C = Low value/low priority. Used for inventory control prioritization, cycle counting frequency, and safety stock policy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MARC.ABCIN; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4; Lean Manufacturing / Pareto Principle

description: The base unit of measure in which the material stock quantity is managed and the valuation price is expressed (e.g., EA, KG, L, M, PC). Corresponds to SAP MARA.MEINS. Required for accurate stock value calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MARA.MEINS; ISO 80000 (Quantities and Units)

description: SAP valuation class (BKLAS) that determines the general ledger accounts updated during inventory movements. Groups materials with similar accounting treatment (e.g., raw materials, finished goods, WIP, MRO). Critical for COGS and balance sheet posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.BKLAS; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: The SAP company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit responsible for the inventory valuation. Used for financial consolidation, intercompany reconciliation, and statutory reporting across multinational operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 27; GAAP ASC 810

description: The SAP product cost estimate number (from CO-PC) that was used to calculate or release the current standard price. Links the inventory valuation to the underlying cost estimate for traceability and variance analysis. Corresponds to SAP KEKO.KALNR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC (Product Cost Planning) - KEKO.KALNR; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inventory valuation amounts (standard price, moving average price, total stock value) are expressed. Corresponds to the local currency of the company code or valuation area (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
reference: ISO 4217 (Currency Codes); SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21

description: The business date as of which the inventory valuation snapshot is captured. For period-end closing, this is the last day of the fiscal period. For on-demand valuations, this is the date the valuation was requested or extracted.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: The foreign exchange rate applied to translate the local currency inventory value to the group currency as of the valuation date. Used for multi-currency financial consolidation and reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this valuation record belongs (01–12 for standard periods, up to 16 for special periods). Corresponds to SAP MBEW.LFMON. Used for period-end inventory closing and financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - MBEW.LFMON; IFRS IAS 34; GAAP ASC 270

description: The fiscal year to which this inventory valuation record belongs. Used for period-end closing, financial reporting, and year-over-year inventory analysis. Aligns with the company's fiscal calendar.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - MBEW.LFGJA; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 270

description: The planned standard price for the next cost estimate or future period, staged for release. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.ZKPRS (future price field). Used in standard cost roll and cost estimate release planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW; SAP Product Cost Planning (CO-PC); IFRS IAS 2

description: The date from which the future planned price becomes effective. Used to schedule standard cost releases at the start of a new fiscal period or cost estimate period. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.ZKDAT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.ZKDAT; SAP CO-PC; IFRS IAS 2

description: The general ledger account code to which the inventory value is posted on the balance sheet. Determined by the valuation class and account determination configuration. Used for financial reconciliation between MM and FI modules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Account Determination (OBYC); IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 210

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the corporate group reporting currency used in consolidated financial statements (e.g., USD, EUR). Enables multi-currency inventory valuation reporting across global operations.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The category of inventory being valued, classifying the material by its stage in the production lifecycle. Raw Material (RM), Work In Progress (WIP), Finished Goods (FG), Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO), Semi-Finished, Trading Goods, or Spare Parts. Used for balance sheet sub-classification and COGS analysis.
reference: IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories); GAAP ASC 330; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The date on which the standard price or moving average price was last changed for this material at the valuation area. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.LAEPR. Used for price variance analysis, audit trails, and identifying stale standard costs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.LAEPR; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: The percentage devaluation applied to the inventory under the lowest value principle (LVP) for balance sheet reporting. Reflects write-downs for slow-moving, obsolete, or damaged stock. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.ABWKZ / devaluation indicator logic. Required for IFRS IAS 2 net realizable value (NRV) assessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW; IFRS IAS 2 (Net Realizable Value); GAAP ASC 330-10

description: The accounting valuation method applied to determine the cost of inventory. Standard Cost uses a predetermined cost; Moving Average Price recalculates with each receipt; FIFO (First-In First-Out) and LIFO (Last-In First-Out) are layer-based methods. Determines COGS and balance sheet inventory value.
reference: IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories); GAAP ASC 330; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The current weighted moving average cost per unit of measure, recalculated after each goods receipt or invoice verification when price control is 'V'. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.VERPR. Reflects the most current average procurement cost.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.VERPR; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: The estimated selling price of the inventory in the ordinary course of business less the estimated costs of completion and the estimated costs necessary to make the sale. Required under IFRS IAS 2 to test whether inventory should be written down below cost. Used for period-end impairment assessment.
reference: IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories) Paragraph 6; GAAP ASC 330-10-35

description: The SAP plant code representing the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the inventory is held and valued. Corresponds to MBEW.BWKEY (valuation area) when valuation is at plant level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.BWKEY; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: The standard price that was in effect before the most recent price change. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.ZKPRS. Used for price variance analysis, standard cost roll comparison, and audit of cost changes during period-end closing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.ZKPRS; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Indicates the inventory valuation method applied to the material. 'S' = Standard Price (fixed standard cost used for all movements); 'V' = Moving Average Price (price recalculated with each goods receipt). Corresponds to SAP MBEW.VPRSV. Drives how inventory is valued on the balance sheet.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.VPRSV; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: The timestamp at which this inventory valuation record was extracted from the source system and loaded into the silver layer lakehouse. Used for data freshness tracking, incremental load processing, and audit trail.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer; Data Governance Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this valuation record was captured as part of a scheduled period-end closing, year-end closing, or an on-demand/ad-hoc valuation request. Used to distinguish official financial closing snapshots from operational queries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI Period-End Closing; IFRS IAS 34; GAAP ASC 270

description: The operational system of record from which this inventory valuation record was sourced. Primarily SAP S/4HANA MM (MBEW table), but may include Infor WMS for warehouse-level stock positions or manual adjustments. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor WMS; Data Governance Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the inventory belongs to a special stock category. SAP special stock types include: E = Sales Order Stock, K = Consignment Stock at Customer, O = Parts Provided to Vendor, Q = Project Stock, V = Consignment Stock from Vendor, W = Vendor Managed Inventory. Empty for standard unrestricted stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.SOBKZ; IFRS IAS 2

description: The total quantity of valuated stock on hand for the material at the plant/valuation area as of the valuation date. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.LBKUM. Used to calculate total stock value (total_stock_quantity × price).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.LBKUM; IFRS IAS 2

description: The total financial value of the valuated stock at the plant level, calculated as total_stock_quantity × (standard_price or moving_average_price) / price_unit. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.SALK3 (standard price) or MBEW.VERPR-based value. Used for balance sheet inventory reporting and period-end closing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.SALK3; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

business_glossary_term: Total Stock Value in Group Currency
description: The total inventory value translated into the group (corporate) reporting currency for consolidated financial statements. Supports multinational consolidation and group-level balance sheet reporting. Derived from local currency value using the applicable exchange rate.
reference: IFRS IAS 21 (Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates); GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI

business_glossary_term: Total Stock Value in Local Currency
description: The total inventory value expressed in the local (company code) currency. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.SALK3 for standard price or the MAP-based value. Used for local statutory financial reporting and balance sheet preparation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/MM - MBEW.SALK3; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: SAP split valuation type (BWTAR) used when a single material is valuated differently based on origin, quality, or batch. For example, externally procured vs. internally produced stock may carry different values. Empty if split valuation is not active.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBEW.BWTAR; IFRS IAS 2

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each reorder policy record in the inventory replenishment system. Serves as the primary key for the reorder_policy data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - MRP Planning Data

description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_bom. Business justification: Reorder policies for spare parts are defined per asset BOM component. Maintenance planners set min/max stock levels based on which BOM positions are critical to keep assets running — a standard MRO in
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: reorder_policy.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Reorder policies are defined at the storage location level. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links the policy to the granular
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: ABC inventory classification of the SKU based on consumption value or demand frequency. A = high-value/high-frequency, B = medium, C = low-value/low-frequency, D = dormant/obsolete. Drives differentiated replenishment strategies and cycle count frequencies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - ABC Indicator (Field: ABCIN); APICS CPIM ABC Analysis

description: The average quantity of the SKU consumed or issued per calendar day at this location, based on historical consumption data. Used as the baseline for reorder point and safety stock calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Consumption-Based Planning; APICS CPIM Demand Forecasting

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values associated with this reorder policy (e.g., ordering cost, holding cost rate). Supports multi-currency operations across global plants.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: The standard deviation of demand during the replenishment lead time period, used in statistical safety stock calculations. Reflects the variability of consumption patterns for this SKU at this location.
reference: APICS CPIM Statistical Safety Stock; SAP S/4HANA MM Forecast-Based Planning

description: The Economic Order Quantity (EOQ) — the mathematically optimal order quantity that minimizes total inventory holding costs and ordering costs. Used as the target replenishment quantity in cost-optimized procurement strategies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data; APICS CPIM EOQ Model; GAAP Inventory Costing

description: The date from which this reorder policy becomes active and drives replenishment decisions. Enables future-dated policy changes and seasonal parameter adjustments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: The date on which this reorder policy expires and is no longer applicable. Used for time-bound policies such as seasonal replenishment strategies or temporary safety stock adjustments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: The fixed quantity used for replenishment orders when the lot sizing procedure is set to fixed lot size. Overrides EOQ calculations and is typically aligned with supplier standard pack sizes or production batch sizes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: LOSGR)

description: The number of working days required to process a Goods Receipt Note (GRN) after physical delivery, including inspection, quality checks, and putaway. Contributes to total replenishment lead time calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: WEBAZ); ISO 9001 Clause 8.6 - Inspection

description: The annual inventory holding cost expressed as a percentage of the unit value (e.g., 20.00 = 20% per year). Includes storage, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost of inventory. Used in EOQ and total cost of ownership calculations.
reference: APICS CPIM Holding Cost; GAAP Inventory Valuation; IFRS IAS 2

description: The date on which the reorder policy parameters were last formally reviewed and validated by the inventory planning team. Supports governance and audit requirements for replenishment parameter maintenance.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.4 - Control of Externally Provided Processes; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Defines the lot sizing method used by MRP to calculate replenishment order quantities. Options include exact quantity, fixed lot size, Economic Order Quantity (EOQ), period lot sizing, lot-for-lot, and min/max replenishment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: DISMM/DISLS); APICS CPIM Lot Sizing

description: Classification of the SKU by material category: raw material, Work In Progress (WIP), finished good, Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO), semi-finished, trading good, or packaging. Determines applicable replenishment rules and valuation methods.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (Field: MTART); GAAP Inventory Classification

description: The maximum quantity that can be ordered in a single replenishment order. Used to prevent over-ordering, manage storage capacity constraints, and control cash flow exposure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: MAXLG)

description: The maximum allowable inventory quantity for the SKU at this location. Replenishment orders are sized to bring stock up to but not exceed this level. Used in min/max replenishment strategies in Infor WMS.
reference: Infor WMS - Min/Max Replenishment; SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: MABST)

description: The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) — the smallest quantity that can be ordered from a supplier or produced in a single replenishment run. Reflects supplier contract terms or production batch constraints.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: MINLG); SAP Ariba Supplier Contract Terms

description: Defines the replenishment planning method for the SKU at this location. Determines how SAP MRP or Infor WMS triggers replenishment: reorder point (ROP), MRP-driven, consumption-based, Kanban, manual, or vendor-managed inventory (VMI).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Type (Field: DISMM); ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: The scheduled date for the next formal review of this reorder policy. Drives workflow alerts and ensures parameters remain aligned with current demand patterns and supply conditions.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.4; SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Parameter Review

description: The rounding value applied to calculated replenishment quantities to align with supplier pack sizes, pallet quantities, or production batch increments. Ensures order quantities are practical multiples.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: UMREZ)

description: The fixed cost incurred each time a replenishment order is placed, including administrative, processing, and supplier transaction costs. Used as an input to Economic Order Quantity (EOQ) calculations.
reference: APICS CPIM EOQ Model; GAAP Cost Accounting; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: The planned number of working days required for a supplier to deliver the material after purchase order creation. Distinct from total replenishment lead time as it excludes internal processing time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: WEBAZ)

description: SAP plant or facility code identifying the manufacturing plant, distribution center, or warehouse location where this reorder policy is in effect. Enables location-specific replenishment rules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Plant/Storage Location Master

description: Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the reorder policy, used for cross-system reference and reporting. Typically sourced from SAP MRP planning data or Infor WMS min/max configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (MRP1/MRP2 views)

description: Descriptive name for the reorder policy, providing a human-readable label for reporting, dashboards, and business communication.
reference: Infor WMS - Min/Max Replenishment Configuration

description: Current operational status of the reorder policy. Active policies drive automated replenishment triggers. Inactive or suspended policies are excluded from MRP runs. Obsolete policies are retained for audit history.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data; Infor WMS Replenishment Configuration

description: Indicates whether the SKU is replenished via external procurement (purchase from supplier), in-house production (manufacturing order), or both. Drives MRP planning logic in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: BESKZ)

description: The timestamp when this reorder policy record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: The timestamp when this reorder policy record was most recently modified. Enables change detection, incremental data loads, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: The Reorder Point (ROP) quantity — the inventory level at which a replenishment order is automatically triggered. When on-hand stock falls to or below this level, a procurement or production order is initiated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: MINBE); APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: The total number of calendar days from placement of a replenishment order to receipt of goods at the storage location. Includes supplier lead time, transit time, and goods receipt processing time. Used to calculate the reorder point.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: PLIFZ); SAP Ariba Supplier Lead Time; APICS CPIM

description: The frequency in calendar days at which inventory levels are reviewed and replenishment decisions are made. Applicable to periodic review replenishment policies (P-system). A value of 0 indicates continuous review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data; APICS CPIM Periodic Review System

description: The minimum buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty. Represents the floor inventory level below which stock should not fall under normal operating conditions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: EISBE); APICS CPIM; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Indicates whether this reorder policy incorporates seasonal demand adjustments. When true, reorder point and safety stock parameters may vary by season or planning period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Seasonal MRP; APICS CPIM Demand Management

description: The target inventory service level expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 = 95%). Represents the desired probability of fulfilling demand from available stock without a stockout. Used to statistically calculate safety stock requirements.
reference: APICS CPIM Service Level; SAP S/4HANA MM Safety Stock Calculation; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this reorder policy was sourced: SAP S/4HANA MRP planning data, Infor WMS min/max configuration, or manually entered. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor WMS

description: Specifies a special procurement strategy such as consignment stock, subcontracting, inter-plant stock transfer, direct production, phantom assembly, or vendor-managed inventory (VMI). Extends the standard procurement type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Planning Data (Field: SOBSL)

description: The base unit of measure in which all quantity fields (reorder point, safety stock, MOQ, EOQ, etc.) are expressed for this SKU. Examples: EA (each), KG, L, M, PC, PAL.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (Field: MEINS); ISO 80000 International System of Units

description: XYZ demand variability classification of the SKU. X = stable/predictable demand, Y = variable demand, Z = highly irregular/sporadic demand. Used in conjunction with ABC classification to optimize safety stock and replenishment strategy.
reference: APICS CPIM Demand Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Inventory Controlling

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each WIP stock record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the wip_stock data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO WIP Calculation

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bop_operation. Business justification: WIP stock is staged at a specific BOP operation step (e.g., 'after welding', 'before painting'). Production control uses this to locate WIP on the shop floor and calculate operation-level throughput a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Free-text field for capturing operational notes, exceptions, special circumstances, or instructions related to the physical inventory count event (e.g., system outage during count, partial area exclusions).

description: The scheduled calendar date on which the physical inventory count is planned to be executed. Used for resource planning and production freeze scheduling.

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the physical inventory count is being conducted.

description: The accounting posting date on which inventory variance adjustments are financially posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period impact of inventory differences.

description: Indicates whether production activities in the plant or warehouse must be frozen (halted) during the physical inventory count to ensure accurate stock snapshot. Critical for wall-to-wall full counts.

description: Full name of the inventory controller or warehouse manager responsible for the physical inventory count event. Retained for reporting and audit trail purposes.

description: Current lifecycle status of the physical inventory count event: created (document created, counting not started), counting (active count in progress), recounting (recount triggered by variance), completed (all counts recorded), posted (variance adjustments financially posted), cancelled (event cancelled before completion).

description: Number of SKUs where the physically counted quantity differs from the book quantity beyond the defined tolerance threshold. Drives recount and adjustment decisions.

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each stock adjustment record in the inventory management system. Serves as the primary key for the stock_adjustment data product.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Full name of the manager or authorized personnel who approved the inventory adjustment. Captured for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance. Not a direct personal identifier in the operational context.

description: The system-recorded (book) inventory quantity for the SKU at the storage location immediately after the adjustment was posted. Confirms the resulting inventory position and supports reconciliation.

description: The system-recorded (book) inventory quantity for the SKU at the storage location immediately before the adjustment was posted. Provides the baseline for variance calculation and audit trail.

description: SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for the inventory adjustment. Used for legal entity-level financial reporting, intercompany reconciliation, and statutory compliance.

description: The controlling cost center responsible for absorbing the financial impact of the inventory adjustment. Used for internal cost allocation and management reporting.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or distribution center where the adjustment occurred. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting and regional inventory analytics.

description: The date and time when the stock adjustment record was initially created in the system. Provides a precise audit trail timestamp for compliance and data lineage purposes.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the financial impact amount and unit cost are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The date the adjustment document was created or the physical event occurred (e.g., date of cycle count, date damage was discovered). May differ from the posting date when adjustments are processed with a delay.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Document Date (BLDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: The monetary value of the inventory adjustment calculated as adjusted quantity multiplied by unit cost. Represents the debit or credit to the inventory account and corresponding expense or income account. Negative values indicate a write-down.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Inventory Difference Posting; GAAP ASC 330; IFRS IAS 2; IFRS 15

description: The fiscal year to which the inventory adjustment posting belongs. Used for period-end financial reporting, inventory reconciliation, and year-end closing activities.

description: The General Ledger (GL) account code to which the financial impact of the inventory adjustment is posted. Determined by SAP automatic account determination based on movement type and valuation class.

description: Classification of the material by its role in the production process: raw material, Work In Progress (WIP), finished goods, Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO), or semi-finished goods. Supports domain-specific inventory reporting.

description: SAP MM three-digit movement type code governing the inventory posting logic (e.g., 551 for scrapping, 701 for inventory difference posting). Determines the general ledger account determination and stock category impact.

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the inventory adjustment is being made. Supports multi-plant inventory management across global operations.

description: The SAP profit center associated with the inventory adjustment for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Enables P&L impact attribution at the business unit level.

description: The document number of the source business document that triggered the adjustment (e.g., physical inventory document number, cycle count document number, NCR number, quality inspection lot number). Enables cross-system traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Physical Inventory Document (MI01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Type of the source business document that triggered or authorized the inventory adjustment (e.g., cycle count document, physical inventory document, Non-Conformance Report (NCR), quality inspection lot). Provides traceability to the originating business event.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reference Document; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reversal Material Document; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 CAPA

description: Flag indicating whether this stock adjustment has been reversed (cancelled after posting). A value of true means the adjustment has been negated by a subsequent reversal document, and the net inventory impact is zero.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reversal Document (STGRD); GAAP Correction Entries; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which the stock adjustment originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, Infor WMS, Siemens Opcenter MES). Supports data lineage tracking in the lakehouse silver layer.

description: Current workflow status of the stock adjustment record, tracking its lifecycle from initial creation through approval, financial posting, and potential reversal. Drives process control and reporting filters.

description: Category of stock being adjusted, indicating the inventory valuation and availability status. Determines whether the adjustment affects unrestricted-use stock, quality inspection stock, or blocked stock.

description: The standard or moving average cost per unit of the material at the time of the adjustment, used to calculate the financial impact. Sourced from the SAP material valuation record.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: ABC inventory classification of the SKU being replenished, indicating its relative consumption value and replenishment priority. A-class items are high-value/high-velocity; C-class items are low-value/low-velocity. Used for prioritization and cycle count scheduling.
reference: Infor WMS ABC Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; Inventory Management Best Practices

description: The actual quantity of the SKU physically moved and confirmed at the destination location upon completion of the replenishment task. Used for variance analysis against requested and confirmed quantities.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Reason code explaining why the replenishment order was cancelled. Used for exception analysis, process improvement, and identifying systemic issues in replenishment triggering logic.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 (CAPA)

description: The date and time when the replenishment order was cancelled, if applicable. Populated only for orders with status 'cancelled'. Used for exception reporting and root cause analysis.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module

description: The date and time when the replenishment order was fully completed and stock confirmed at the destination location. Used for cycle time measurement, SLA compliance, and inventory availability updates.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: The quantity confirmed as available and allocated for replenishment at the source location. May differ from requested quantity if source stock is insufficient.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module

description: Warehouse location code identifying the target location where replenished stock must be delivered (e.g., forward pick face, kanban lane, production staging area). Corresponds to the Infor WMS location master.
reference: Infor WMS Location Master

description: Classification of the destination warehouse location to which stock is being moved. Supports analytics on replenishment demand by location type and operational zone.
reference: Infor WMS Location Master

description: The on-hand stock quantity at the destination location at the time the replenishment order was created. Provides context for the replenishment trigger and supports inventory position reporting.
reference: Infor WMS Inventory Module; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Indicates whether a partial fulfillment of the replenishment order is acceptable when the full requested quantity is not available at the source location. When false, the order must be fulfilled in full or held.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the replenishment order has been escalated as urgent, typically due to an imminent or active stockout at the destination pick location. Triggers expedited handling by warehouse supervisors.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module

description: The kanban card or signal identifier that triggered this replenishment order, applicable when trigger_type is 'kanban'. Enables traceability between physical kanban signals and digital replenishment records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Kanban; Lean Manufacturing / Kanban Methodology

description: The License Plate Number (LPN) or handling unit identifier associated with the stock being replenished. Used in Infor WMS for unit-level tracking of pallets, totes, or containers during replenishment movements.
reference: Infor WMS Handling Unit Management; GS1 SSCC Standards

description: The maximum stock level threshold at the destination location for min/max replenishment. The replenishment order quantity is typically calculated to bring stock up to this level.
reference: Infor WMS Min/Max Replenishment; SAP S/4HANA MM MRP

description: The minimum stock level threshold at the destination location that triggered the replenishment order (applicable for min/max trigger type). When on-hand quantity falls below this level, a replenishment order is generated.
reference: Infor WMS Min/Max Replenishment; SAP S/4HANA MM MRP

description: Free-text field for warehouse operators or supervisors to record additional instructions, exceptions, or observations related to the replenishment order execution.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the replenishment order, used for tracking and communication across warehouse operations teams. Formatted as REP-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution plant associated with the replenishment order. Enables cross-plant inventory reporting and MRP alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Business priority level assigned to the replenishment order, used by warehouse management to sequence and expedite fulfillment. Critical priority indicates immediate action required to prevent stockout at pick location.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module

description: Numeric priority score (1–100) assigned by the WMS to enable fine-grained sequencing of replenishment tasks when multiple orders share the same priority tier. Higher scores indicate greater urgency.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module

description: The inventory replenishment strategy applied to this order. Fixed quantity replenishes a set amount each time; fixed cycle replenishes on a schedule; two-bin and kanban are signal-driven; wave-pick is batch-driven by outbound order waves.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; Lean Manufacturing / Kanban Methodology; MRP II

description: The quantity of the SKU requested to be moved from the source location to the destination location as specified when the replenishment order was created.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: The date and time when the replenishment order was created or triggered in the WMS. Serves as the start of the replenishment lifecycle and is used for lead time and aging analysis.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The date and time by which the replenishment must be completed to prevent a stockout at the destination pick location. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and escalation triggers.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; SLA Management

description: The safety stock level maintained at the destination location to buffer against demand variability and replenishment lead time uncertainty. Informs replenishment urgency and priority assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Safety Stock; Infor WMS; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Classification of the source warehouse location from which stock is being moved. Used for operational routing and analytics on replenishment flow patterns.
reference: Infor WMS Location Master

description: The on-hand stock quantity available at the source location at the time the replenishment order was created. Used to assess feasibility of fulfilling the requested quantity and for inventory position analysis.
reference: Infor WMS Inventory Module; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The date and time when the warehouse operator began executing the replenishment task (first scan or task acceptance). Used to measure task execution time and operator productivity.
reference: Infor WMS Task Management Module

description: Current lifecycle status of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from open (created, not yet started) through in-progress (movement underway) to completed (stock moved to destination) or cancelled/on-hold states.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Infor WMS storage type code defining the physical storage method (e.g., pallet rack, floor storage, carousel, automated storage and retrieval system) applicable to the replenishment movement.
reference: Infor WMS Storage Type Master

description: The Infor WMS warehouse task identifier assigned to the physical replenishment movement instruction. Used by warehouse operators on handheld devices or voice-directed systems to execute the replenishment.
reference: Infor WMS Task Management Module

description: Indicates the mechanism that initiated the replenishment order. Min/max triggers fire when stock falls below minimum threshold; kanban triggers are signal-card driven; manual orders are created by warehouse staff; demand-driven triggers are based on forecasted pick demand.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment Module; Lean Manufacturing / Kanban Methodology

description: The unit of measure in which the replenishment quantities (requested, confirmed, actual) are expressed. Aligned with the SKU's base unit of measure in the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; GS1 Standards; ISO 80000

description: The outbound picking wave number associated with this replenishment order when triggered by a wave-pick process. Links replenishment activity to the outbound order fulfillment wave for operational coordination.
reference: Infor WMS Wave Management Module

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each stock transfer record in the inventory domain. Serves as the primary key for the stock_transfer data product.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transport Order (STO); Infor WMS Transfer Order

description: FK to inventory.storage_location

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: stock_transfer.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK enables batch-level traceability for inter-location stock transfers.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: stock_transfer.serial_number is a denormalized serial number reference. Normalizing to serialized_unit_id FK links serialized item transfers to the serialized unit master.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: stock_transfer.source_storage_location is a denormalized location code for the origin bin/rack. Normalizing to source_storage_location_id FK with a descriptive prefix distinguishes it from the destina
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The actual calendar date on which the goods issue was posted at the source location, confirming physical departure of stock. Triggers inventory reduction at source and initiates in-transit stock accounting.
reference: SAP MM Goods Issue Posting Date (MIGO); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4 Preservation

description: The actual calendar date on which the goods receipt was posted at the destination location, confirming physical arrival and acceptance of transferred stock. Triggers inventory increase at destination and closes in-transit stock.
reference: SAP MM Goods Receipt Posting Date (MIGO); Infor WMS Receipt Confirmation

description: SAP company code representing the legal entity initiating the stock transfer. Critical for intercompany transfer identification, financial accounting segregation, and regulatory reporting across multinational operations.
reference: SAP FI Company Code (BUKRS); IFRS IAS 27 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810

description: Date and time when the stock transfer order was created in the source system (SAP MM or Infor WMS). Provides the starting point for transfer lifecycle duration analysis and audit trail compliance.
reference: SAP MM Document Creation Timestamp; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transfer value and valuation price are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing plants and distribution centers.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP FI Currency Configuration

description: Official customs declaration or export/import document reference number for cross-border stock transfers. Required for regulatory compliance with customs authorities and trade compliance reporting in multinational operations.
reference: EU Customs Union Regulation; US CBP Import/Export Regulations; REACH Regulation; RoHS Directive

description: SAP outbound delivery document number (VL10) or Infor WMS shipment document number associated with the stock transfer. Links the inventory transfer to the logistics delivery process for goods issue posting and shipment tracking.
reference: SAP MM/SD Outbound Delivery (VL10); Infor WMS Shipment Document

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination plant or warehouse location. Used for cross-border transfer identification, import duty determination, customs compliance, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; EU Customs Union; US CBP Trade Regulations

description: Code identifying the receiving manufacturing plant or facility to which the stock is being transferred. Corresponds to the SAP receiving plant code. Used for inter-plant inventory reconciliation and cost center allocation.
reference: SAP MM Plant (WERKS); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5 Production and Service Provision

description: Identifier of the receiving warehouse within the destination plant. Corresponds to the SAP warehouse number or Infor WMS warehouse code at the destination. Used for inbound planning and putaway operations.
reference: SAP WM Warehouse Number (LGNUM); Infor WMS Warehouse Master

description: Precise date and time when the goods issue was posted at the source location. Provides sub-day precision for operational tracking, shift-level reporting, and audit trail requirements beyond the goods issue date.
reference: SAP MM Goods Issue Timestamp (MIGO); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5 Production and Service Provision

description: Precise date and time when the goods receipt was posted at the destination location. Enables transit time calculation at sub-day precision and supports SLA compliance monitoring for inter-plant replenishment.
reference: SAP MM Goods Receipt Timestamp (MIGO); Infor WMS Receipt Timestamp

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the stock transfer crosses international borders, requiring customs documentation, export/import declarations, and compliance with trade regulations (e.g., CE Marking, REACH, RoHS, export control).
reference: EU CE Marking; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; US Export Administration Regulations (EAR)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the stock transfer crosses legal entity boundaries (intercompany transfer). When true, the transfer requires intercompany billing, transfer pricing compliance, and consolidated financial elimination entries.
reference: SAP MM Intercompany Stock Transport Order; IFRS IAS 27; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines

description: Actual quantity of the SKU physically issued or picked from the source location and dispatched for transfer. May differ from the planned transfer quantity due to partial shipments or stock availability constraints.
reference: SAP MM Goods Issue (MIGO); Infor WMS Transfer Order Issued Quantity

description: Date and time of the most recent update to the stock transfer record in the source system. Used for change detection in data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance.
reference: SAP MM Document Change Timestamp; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: SAP MM movement type code that classifies the inventory transaction (e.g., 641 for goods issue for STO, 101 for goods receipt against STO). Determines the accounting entries and inventory update logic in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP MM Movement Type (BWART); SAP S/4HANA MM Configuration

description: The scheduled calendar date on which the transferred stock is expected to arrive and be received at the destination location. Used for inbound planning, safety stock calculations, and Available to Promise (ATP) at the destination.
reference: SAP MM Planned Delivery Date; MRP II Planning Standards; ATP/CTP Calculations

description: The scheduled calendar date on which the stock transfer is planned to be executed (goods issue from source). Used for production planning, MRP scheduling, and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transport Order Delivery Date; MRP II Planning Standards

description: Business priority level assigned to the stock transfer order, used by warehouse and logistics teams to sequence picking, packing, and dispatch activities. Urgent transfers may be triggered by production line stoppages or critical customer orders.
reference: Infor WMS Transfer Order Priority; SAP MM STO Priority; Lean Manufacturing - Andon / Production Urgency

description: Actual quantity of the SKU confirmed as received at the destination location. Used to calculate transfer variance and update destination inventory positions. Triggers goods receipt posting in SAP MM.
reference: SAP MM Goods Receipt (MIGO); Infor WMS Transfer Order Receipt Confirmation

description: SAP company code of the receiving legal entity in an intercompany stock transfer. When this differs from the issuing company code, the transfer triggers intercompany billing and cross-company financial postings.
reference: SAP FI Company Code (BUKRS); IFRS IAS 27 Consolidated Financial Statements; SAP MM Intercompany STO

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the source plant or warehouse location. Used for cross-border transfer identification, customs compliance, trade statistics, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; EU Customs Union; US CBP Trade Regulations

description: Code identifying the originating manufacturing plant or facility from which the stock is being transferred. Corresponds to the SAP plant code (WERKS). Essential for inter-plant inventory movement tracking and cost allocation.
reference: SAP MM Plant (WERKS); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5 Production and Service Provision

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which the stock transfer record originated (SAP S/4HANA MM, Infor WMS, or manual entry). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between systems in the Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor WMS; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage

description: Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer order. Tracks progression from creation through in-transit movement to receipt confirmation or cancellation. Used for operational monitoring and inventory position accuracy.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transport Order (STO); Infor WMS Transfer Order Lifecycle

description: Inventory stock category of the material being transferred, indicating its usability status: unrestricted (available for use), quality inspection (under QM hold), blocked (not available), restricted, in-transit, or consignment. Drives inventory availability calculations and MRP planning.
reference: SAP MM Stock Type (SOBKZ); ISO 9001 Clause 8.6 Release of Products and Services

description: Business-facing document number assigned to the stock transfer order by the source system (SAP MM STO number or Infor WMS transfer order number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with logistics and warehouse teams.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transport Order (STO); Infor WMS Transfer Order

description: Ordered or planned quantity of the SKU to be transferred, expressed in the base unit of measure. This is the quantity requested on the transfer order document, which may differ from the actual received quantity.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transport Order Item Quantity; Infor WMS Transfer Order Quantity

description: Business reason code explaining why the stock transfer was initiated. Used for demand classification, cost allocation, and operational analytics to understand the drivers of inter-plant and inter-warehouse stock movements.
reference: SAP MM Movement Reason; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5 Production and Service Provision

description: Classification of the stock transfer indicating the scope and nature of the movement: plant-to-plant (inter-plant), warehouse-to-warehouse, storage location transfer within a plant, or a formal Stock Transport Order (STO) involving procurement and logistics. Drives process routing and document flow.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transport Order (STO); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5 Production and Service Provision

description: Total monetary value of the stock transfer, calculated as transfer quantity multiplied by the valuation price. Used for financial reporting, intercompany billing, and inventory cost accounting. Stored as a raw business figure; aggregations are performed in the analytics layer.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transport Order Value; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory

description: Reference number linking the stock transfer to the associated transport or shipment document (e.g., SAP shipment number, carrier waybill, or TMS reference). Enables traceability between inventory movement and physical logistics execution.
reference: SAP SD Shipment Document; TMS (Transportation Management System) Reference; Infor WMS Shipment Reference

description: Base unit of measure in which the transfer quantity is expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for litre, PAL for pallet). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure and ISO 80000 measurement standards.
reference: SAP MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; GS1 Unit of Measure Codes

description: Unit price used to value the transferred stock at the time of transfer, based on the material's standard price or moving average price (MAP) in SAP. Used for inventory valuation, intercompany pricing, and financial accounting of stock movements.
reference: SAP MM Material Valuation (MBEW); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each inventory movement event. Serves as the primary key for the inventory_transaction data product and enables precise traceability of every stock movement across the enterprise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Document; Infor WMS Transaction History

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Inventory transactions must be assigned to a fiscal period to ensure goods movements are reflected in the correct financial period. Period-end closing procedures validate all transactions are period-s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Inventory transactions generated by inbound goods receipts must reference the originating goods receipt document. This is a standard SAP/ERP pattern — goods receipt posts an inventory transaction that
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Every goods movement transaction (goods receipt, goods issue, transfer) creates a financial accounting document. The inventory-to-GL link is a core integration point in any manufacturing ERP system.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: inventory_transaction.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK enables full batch traceability for every inventory movement event.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: inventory_transaction.serial_number is a denormalized serial number reference. Normalizing to serialized_unit_id FK links serialized inventory movements to the serialized unit master for full unit-lev
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_transfer. Business justification: An inventory transaction record is frequently generated as the result of a stock transfer execution (goods issue at source, goods receipt at destination). Adding a typed FK from transaction to stock_t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: inventory_transaction.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links each transaction to the granular bin/rack location master.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The SAP company code representing the legal entity for which this inventory transaction is posted. Enables multi-entity financial reporting and intercompany inventory reconciliation for multinational operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code (T001-BUKRS); IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or warehouse location where the inventory transaction occurred. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting, customs documentation, and geographic inventory analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS/REACH Compliance; GDPR

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the valuation amount is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency inventory valuation for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-WAERS); IFRS IAS 21

description: The external delivery note or packing slip number from the supplier or logistics provider associated with this goods receipt or transfer transaction. Used for three-way matching and inbound logistics reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MKPF-XBLNR); Infor WMS; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4.3

description: For stock transfer transactions, the receiving plant code to which the material is being transferred. Populated only for inter-plant transfer movements (e.g., SAP movement types 301, 303, 351).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-UMWRK); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: For stock transfer transactions, the receiving storage location within the destination plant. Populated for intra-plant and inter-plant transfer postings to identify the exact destination of the transferred stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-UMLGO)

description: The date on the source document (e.g., delivery note, goods receipt slip, transfer order) that triggered the inventory transaction. May differ from the posting date when documents are processed with a delay.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MKPF-BLDAT); IFRS IAS 2

description: The Goods Receipt Note (GRN) number associated with this transaction when the movement represents an inbound goods receipt. Cross-references the procurement GRN document for three-way matching (PO, GRN, invoice).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM GRN; procurement.goods_receipt; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4.3

description: The line item number within the SAP material document corresponding to this inventory transaction. A single material document may contain multiple line items representing different materials or storage locations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Document (MSEG-ZEILE)

description: The SAP MM material document number assigned to this inventory transaction at the time of posting. Serves as the primary cross-reference to the source system of record for goods movements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Document (MSEG/MKPF)

description: The fiscal year associated with the SAP material document number. Required in combination with material_document_number to uniquely identify a document in SAP MM, as document numbers are reset annually.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Document (MKPF-MJAHR)

description: High-level business classification of the inventory movement event. Groups SAP movement types into business-meaningful categories for simplified reporting and analytics (e.g., goods_receipt covers GRN against PO, production, and returns from customer).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor WMS; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4 Preservation

description: SAP MM movement type code that classifies the nature of the inventory transaction (e.g., 101=Goods Receipt against PO, 201=Goods Issue to Cost Center, 261=Goods Issue to Production Order, 301=Transfer Posting, 551=Scrapping). Drives account determination and stock update logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Movement Type (T156); ISO 9001 Inventory Control

description: Human-readable description of the inventory movement type corresponding to the movement_type_code. Provides business context for reporting and analytics without requiring a lookup join.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Movement Type (T156T)

description: The unit of measure used in the reference order document (PO, production order, sales order) for this transaction. May differ from the base unit of measure when conversion factors apply.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-ERFME)

description: The SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility, distribution center, or warehouse where the inventory transaction occurred. Plant is the primary organizational unit for inventory management and production planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant (T001W-WERKS); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: The accounting date on which the inventory transaction is posted to the general ledger and stock ledger. Determines the fiscal period for inventory valuation and financial reporting. May differ from the document date or actual physical movement date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MKPF-BUDAT); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: The precise date and time at which the inventory transaction was posted in the system, including timezone offset. Used for audit trails, sequencing of concurrent transactions, and intraday inventory position reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MKPF-CPUDT / MKPF-CPUTM); ISO 8601

description: The SAP controlling profit center associated with the inventory transaction. Enables profit center-level inventory valuation and profitability analysis across business segments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center (CEPC-PRCTR); IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: The quantity of material moved in this inventory transaction, expressed in the base unit of measure. Positive values indicate stock increases (receipts); negative values indicate stock decreases (issues, scrapping). Precision of 4 decimal places supports materials measured in fractional units.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-MENGE); Infor WMS; ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.5

business_glossary_term: Quantity in Order Unit of Measure
description: The transaction quantity expressed in the order unit of measure (e.g., purchase order UoM or sales order UoM), which may differ from the base unit of measure. Supports reconciliation with reference documents such as POs and SOs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-ERFMG / MSEG-ERFME)

description: Business reason code explaining why the inventory transaction was performed, particularly for adjustments, scrapping, and returns. Supports root cause analysis, quality management reporting, and inventory loss investigation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-GRUND); ISO 9001 Clause 10.2 CAPA; ISO 14001

description: The line item number within the reference document (PO, production order, sales order) associated with this inventory transaction. Required for precise reconciliation between inventory movements and order line items.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-EBELP / MSEG-POSNR)

description: The document number of the business document that triggered this inventory transaction (e.g., PO number for goods receipt, production order number for goods issue, sales order number for outbound delivery). Enables end-to-end process traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-EBELN / MSEG-AUFNR / MSEG-VBELN)

description: The type of business document that triggered or is referenced by this inventory transaction (e.g., purchase_order for GRN, production_order for goods issue to manufacturing, sales_order for outbound delivery). Enables cross-domain traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-KZBEW / MSEG-AUFNR); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether this inventory transaction is a reversal (cancellation) of a previously posted transaction. Reversed transactions negate the original stock and accounting impact. Critical for audit trail integrity and inventory reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-SHKZG); IFRS; GAAP; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: The material document number of the original transaction that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true. Enables bidirectional linkage between original and reversal transactions for audit and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-LFBNR); ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: SAP special stock indicator identifying whether the material belongs to a special stock category such as sales order stock (E), vendor consignment (K), project stock (Q), or returnable packaging (V). Blank indicates standard own stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Special Stock (MSEG-SOBKZ)

description: Current processing status of the inventory transaction record. Indicates whether the transaction has been fully posted, is pending approval, has been reversed, or is blocked for further processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor WMS; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: The stock category or special stock indicator defining the usability and ownership status of the inventory at the time of the transaction (e.g., unrestricted=available for use, quality_inspection=under QM hold, blocked=not available, in_transit=inter-plant transfer in progress).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Type (MSEG-SOBKZ / MSEG-INSMK); ISO 9001 Clause 8.6

description: The specific bin, rack, or slot within the warehouse where the material was placed or picked during this transaction. Enables precise bin-level inventory tracking and supports warehouse slotting optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM (LGPLA); Infor WMS Bin Master

description: The base unit of measure in which the transaction quantity is expressed (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, M=Meter). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure and ISO 80000 international units.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-MEINS); ISO 80000; ANSI X12 Unit of Measure Codes

description: The monetary value of the inventory quantity moved in this transaction, calculated using the applicable valuation method (standard price, moving average price, or FIFO). Used for inventory accounting, COGS calculation, and financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-DMBTR); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330; IFRS

description: The unit price used to calculate the valuation amount for this transaction, expressed in the transaction currency. Reflects the standard price or moving average price at the time of posting. Higher precision (6 decimal places) supports materials with very low unit costs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG-VERPR / MSEG-STPRS); IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: The inventory valuation method applied to calculate the monetary value of this transaction (e.g., standard_price for manufactured goods, moving_average_price for purchased materials). Determines how cost variances are handled in financial accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Valuation (MBEW-VPRSV); IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: The transaction reference number from Infor WMS corresponding to this inventory movement. Used to cross-reference warehouse-level execution details with the ERP material document.
reference: Infor WMS Transaction History

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each approved freight carrier or logistics service provider (LSP) record in the master data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM (Transportation Management) Carrier Master

description: Type of digital integration method used to exchange shipment data with the carrier's systems. Determines TMS connectivity configuration and automation capability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Carrier Integration; Infor WMS Carrier Interface

description: Date on which the carrier was formally approved and added to the approved carrier list (ACL) following qualification review, insurance verification, and compliance checks.
reference: ISO 9001 Supplier Qualification; SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle Management

description: Maximum monetary value (in contract currency) the carrier is liable for in the event of cargo loss or damage, as specified in the insurance certificate and transportation contract.
reference: Carmack Amendment (49 U.S.C. § 14706); IATA Warsaw/Montreal Convention; CMR Convention

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
