description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment destination location. Used for trade lane analysis, import duty applicability, and freight cost allocation by geography.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; Incoterms 2020; EU Customs Code

description: Contractual discount or rebate applied by the carrier on this invoice (e.g., volume discount, early payment discount). Reduces the net payable amount and must be validated against the carrier contract terms.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; Carrier Rate Agreement; GAAP ASC 606

description: Free-text or coded description of the reason for disputing the freight invoice (e.g., rate overcharge, duplicate billing, service failure, unauthorized accessorial). Populated when audit_status is 'failed' or status is 'disputed'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Dispute Management; Freight Audit and Payment (FAP) Process

description: Date on which the freight invoice dispute was resolved between the company and the carrier. Used to measure dispute cycle time and carrier responsiveness KPIs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Dispute Management; Carrier SLA Agreement

description: Date by which payment must be made to the carrier to comply with contracted payment terms and avoid late payment penalties or interest charges.
reference: GAAP ASC 210; IFRS IAS 37; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

description: Exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency to the company's functional reporting currency at the time of invoice processing. Required for multi-currency financial consolidation and IFRS/GAAP compliance.
reference: IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Valuation

description: Fuel surcharge billed by the carrier, typically calculated as a percentage of the base freight charge based on the applicable fuel index (e.g., DOE diesel index). Subject to contracted fuel surcharge table validation.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI 210; DOE Fuel Index; Carrier Fuel Surcharge Schedule

description: ISO 4217 currency code of the company's functional reporting currency (e.g., USD) to which the invoice amount is converted for financial consolidation and IFRS/GAAP reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: General Ledger (GL) account code to which the freight cost is posted in the financial system. Enables freight cost allocation to the correct P&L line (e.g., inbound freight, outbound freight, intercompany freight).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP ASC 330; IFRS IAS 2

description: Classification of the freight invoice by billing type. Distinguishes standard per-shipment invoices from consolidated periodic invoices, credit/debit adjustments, and corrected re-bills.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI 210; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Invoice Types

description: Total gross amount billed by the carrier on this invoice in the invoice currency, inclusive of all charges (base freight, fuel surcharge, accessorials, taxes). The primary amount subject to freight audit and payment approval.
reference: GAAP ASC 210; IFRS IAS 1; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; ANSI X12 EDI 210

business_glossary_term: Total Invoiced Amount in Functional Currency
description: Total invoiced amount converted to the company's functional reporting currency using the exchange_rate. Used for financial consolidation, cost center allocation, and management reporting.
reference: IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Indicates whether this freight invoice represents an intercompany charge between legal entities within the same corporate group. Drives intercompany elimination in financial consolidation and transfer pricing compliance.
reference: IFRS IAS 27; GAAP ASC 810; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA FI-IC

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment origin location. Used for trade lane analysis, customs compliance validation, and freight cost allocation by geography.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; Incoterms 2020; EU Customs Code

description: Actual date the invoice was paid to the carrier. Populated upon payment confirmation from the accounts payable system. Used for cash flow reporting and on-time payment KPI tracking.
reference: GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

description: Method used or designated for payment of this freight invoice (e.g., ACH, wire transfer, check, virtual card). Drives accounts payable processing workflow and bank reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; NACHA ACH Rules; GAAP ASC 230

description: Payment transaction reference number (e.g., ACH trace number, check number, wire reference) generated upon payment execution. Used for bank reconciliation and payment confirmation to the carrier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; NACHA ACH Rules; GAAP ASC 230

description: Contracted payment terms code applicable to this invoice (e.g., NET30, NET45, 2/10NET30). Determines the payment due date and any early payment discount eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Payment Terms; GAAP ASC 210

description: Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number used to track the freight shipment through the carrier's network. Serves as the carrier's primary shipment identifier and is referenced on the freight invoice for audit matching.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI 210; NMFTA Freight Bill Standard

description: Date the freight invoice was received by the accounts payable or freight audit team. Used to calculate days-to-pay and to track invoice processing cycle time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; GAAP ASC 210

description: Contracted service level or transit time commitment under which the freight was shipped. Used to validate that the billed service level matches the contracted rate and shipment requirements.
reference: SAP Transportation Management (TM); Carrier Service Level Agreements (SLA)

description: Current processing status of the freight invoice through the freight audit and payment lifecycle. Drives workflow routing, approval actions, and financial accrual decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Invoice Lifecycle; Freight Audit and Payment (FAP) Process

description: Total tax charges included in the freight invoice (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax on transportation services). Required for tax input credit recovery and compliance with local tax regulations.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; GAAP ASC 740; IFRS IAS 12; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX

description: Mode of transportation used for the freight movement being billed. Drives rate validation logic, accessorial applicability, and freight cost allocation by mode in financial reporting.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI 210; Incoterms 2020; SAP Transportation Management (TM)

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each freight rate record in the Transportation Management System (TMS). Serves as the primary key for all freight rate lookups, cost estimation, and freight audit processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; Infor TMS

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Freight rates are negotiated with specific carriers. freight_rate.carrier_code and carrier_name are denormalized references to the carrier master. Adding carrier_id FK enforces referential integrity a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Freight rates are defined for specific carrier service offerings (LTL, FTL, express, etc.). Adding carrier_service_id FK normalizes the service reference to the carrier_service master, enabling servic
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: Freight rates vary by product category due to handling requirements, density, and risk. Logistics pricing teams use this to calculate accurate shipping costs.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.currency_exchange_rate. Business justification: International freight rates are quoted in foreign currencies. Finance-controlled exchange rates must be applied consistently to freight cost calculations for accurate cost reporting and cross-border s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_contract. Business justification: Freight rates are defined within freight contracts. freight_rate.contract_number is a denormalized reference to the freight_contract master. Adding freight_contract_id FK normalizes this relationship.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: freight_rate defines contracted and spot rates for specific transportation lanes. route is the master definition of a transportation lane between origin and destination. Linking freight_rate to route
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or identifier of the authorized approver who validated and approved this freight rate for use in TMS freight cost estimation. Supports internal controls, audit trail requirements, and procurement governance.
reference: SAP Ariba; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: The date on which this freight rate was formally approved for operational use. Provides audit trail for rate governance and supports freight audit processes requiring proof of rate authorization.
reference: SAP Ariba; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; ISO 9001

description: Standardized commodity classification code (NMFC for domestic road freight or HS/HTS code for international shipments) associated with this rate. Used for customs/trade compliance documentation, rate lookup, and freight classification audits.
reference: NMFC - NMFTA; HS Codes - WCO (World Customs Organization); HTS - USITC; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM

description: Classification of the goods or commodity type to which this rate applies. Freight rates often vary by commodity due to density, handling requirements, or hazardous material classification. Aligns with NMFC commodity codes for road freight or HS codes for international shipments.
reference: NMFC (National Motor Freight Classification); HS (Harmonized System) Codes - WCO; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the freight rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Essential for multi-currency freight cost management across global distribution networks and financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the shipment destination location of the freight lane. Defines the geographic endpoint of the rate lane for global distribution network management and customs/trade compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS

description: Geographic region, zone, or postal code range for the shipment destination. Used in zone-based rate structures for TMS freight cost estimation and delivery performance analysis across global distribution networks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; USPS Zone Chart; UPS/FedEx Zone Definitions

description: The date from which this freight rate becomes valid and applicable for shipment cost estimation and carrier invoicing. Used in TMS rate selection to ensure only currently valid rates are applied to shipments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: The date after which this freight rate is no longer valid. Used in TMS rate selection to exclude expired rates and trigger rate renewal workflows. Supports contract lifecycle management and freight audit date-range validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: Name or identifier of the fuel price index used to calculate or adjust the fuel surcharge component of this rate (e.g., DOE Weekly Retail On-Highway Diesel, IATA Jet Fuel Monitor, Platts Bunker Index). Ensures transparent and auditable fuel cost pass-through in carrier contracts.
reference: DOE Fuel Index; IATA Fuel Surcharge Index; Platts; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM

description: Fuel surcharge rate expressed as a percentage of the base freight rate. Applied dynamically based on fuel index benchmarks (e.g., DOE diesel index). Used in TMS freight cost estimation when rate_type is base_rate to calculate the total all-in freight cost.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; DOE (U.S. Department of Energy) Fuel Index; IATA Fuel Surcharge Index

description: Indicates whether this freight rate applies to shipments containing hazardous materials (Hazmat). Hazmat shipments require special handling, documentation, and regulatory compliance under DOT, IATA DGR, and IMDG regulations. Used in TMS carrier selection and compliance screening.
reference: USDOT 49 CFR (Hazardous Materials Regulations); IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR); IMDG Code; OSHA

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and seller for the shipment covered by this rate. Determines which party bears the freight cost and at what point risk transfers. Critical for customs/trade compliance documentation.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD; Customs/Trade Compliance

description: The maximum freight charge cap applicable for a shipment on this lane. Protects shippers from excessive charges on large shipments. Used in TMS freight cost estimation to apply ceiling pricing when calculated charges exceed this threshold.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; Carrier Contract Terms

description: The minimum freight charge applicable for a shipment on this lane regardless of weight, volume, or quantity. Ensures carrier cost recovery for small shipments. Used in TMS freight cost estimation to apply floor pricing when calculated charges fall below this threshold.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; Carrier Tariff Standards

description: Name or identifier of the procurement/logistics team member or department responsible for negotiating this freight rate with the carrier. Used for accountability tracking in carrier management and contract lifecycle management.
reference: SAP Ariba Procurement; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the shipment origin location of the freight lane. Defines the geographic starting point of the rate lane for global distribution network management and customs/trade compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS

description: Geographic region, zone, or postal code range for the shipment origin. Used in zone-based rate structures where rates vary by geographic zone rather than specific point-to-point lanes. Supports TMS freight cost estimation for regional distribution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; USPS Zone Chart; UPS/FedEx Zone Definitions

description: The contracted or spot freight rate value expressed per the defined rate basis (e.g., rate per kg, per pallet, per container, per mile). Core financial field used in TMS freight cost estimation, carrier invoice validation, and freight audit. Stored with 4 decimal precision to support per-unit micro-rates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: The unit of measure upon which the freight rate is applied. Determines how the rate is multiplied against shipment characteristics to calculate total freight cost. Critical for TMS freight cost estimation and carrier invoice validation during freight audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; IATA Rate Construction Manual; NMFC

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the freight rate record, used in TMS freight cost estimation, freight audit, and carrier negotiations. Typically references the rate tariff or contract schedule number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS Rate Management

description: Indicates the origin or basis of the freight rate. Contract rates are pre-negotiated with carriers; spot rates are obtained for individual shipments; benchmark rates are used for market comparison; tariff rates are published carrier schedules. Drives rate selection logic in TMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; IATA; FMC (Federal Maritime Commission)

description: Classification of the freight rate component. Base rate is the primary transportation charge; fuel surcharge adjusts for fuel cost fluctuations; accessorial covers special handling services; other types cover specific operational scenarios. Used in TMS freight cost estimation and freight audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; NMFC (National Motor Freight Classification); Infor TMS

description: Timestamp indicating when this freight rate record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA; Infor TMS; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this freight rate record. Used for change tracking, incremental data loading in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer, and freight audit trail maintenance.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA; Infor TMS; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: The carrier service level or transit time commitment associated with this rate. Differentiates between speed-of-delivery tiers (e.g., express vs. economy) and shipment consolidation types (e.g., Full Truck Load vs. Less-than-Truck Load). Used in TMS carrier selection and SLA compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; NMFC; IATA

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this freight rate record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD-TM, Infor TMS, SAP Ariba, carrier portal). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer and freight audit traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; SAP Ariba; Data Governance Best Practices

description: Current lifecycle status of the freight rate record. Active rates are available for TMS freight cost estimation; expired rates are retained for historical freight audit; superseded rates have been replaced by newer versions; pending_approval rates are awaiting authorization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS

description: Published carrier tariff or rate schedule number from which this rate is derived. Used for tariff-based rate validation during freight audit and regulatory compliance with published rate requirements (e.g., FMC tariff filing for ocean freight).
reference: FMC (Federal Maritime Commission) Tariff Filing; NMFTA; SAP S/4HANA SD-TM

description: Indicates whether this freight rate applies to temperature-controlled (cold chain) shipments requiring refrigerated or heated transport equipment. Relevant for industrial manufacturing products with temperature-sensitive components or chemicals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; GDP (Good Distribution Practice) Guidelines

description: Contracted or standard number of calendar or business days for transit from origin to destination under this rate and service level. Used in TMS delivery date calculation, SLA compliance monitoring, and customer order promising (Available to Promise / ATP).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; SLA Agreement Standards

description: The mode of transportation to which this freight rate applies. Determines the applicable carrier pool, regulatory requirements, and rate structure. Used in TMS route optimization and carrier selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; IATA; IMO (International Maritime Organization); USDOT

description: Maximum shipment weight in kilograms for which this rate applies. Defines the upper bound of the weight break tier in tiered rate structures. Used in TMS rate selection logic to match shipment weight to the correct rate tier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; IATA Rate Construction Manual

description: Minimum shipment weight in kilograms for which this rate applies. Defines the lower bound of the weight break tier in tiered rate structures. Used in TMS rate selection logic to match shipment weight to the correct rate tier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD-TM; Infor TMS; IATA Rate Construction Manual

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each outbound delivery record in the logistics domain. Serves as the primary key for the delivery data product and is used for cross-system linkage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Outbound Delivery (VL01N/VL02N)

description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Deliveries require precise customer address for routing, proof of delivery, and customer location validation. Warehouse management systems use this for every outbound shipment.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Outbound deliveries to customers trigger AR invoices. Finance and logistics teams link delivery confirmation to the AR invoice to validate goods-issue-based billing and support revenue recognition pro
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to logistics.carrier

Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Deliveries need receiving contact for appointment scheduling, delivery confirmation, and issue escalation. Last-mile delivery teams use this for every customer delivery.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order. Business justification: delivery is the outbound delivery document confirming physical dispatch of goods. freight_order is the operational carrier execution order. A delivery is executed under a freight order — linking them
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Deliveries are executed against a fulfillment plan that coordinates multi-line, multi-shipment orders. Logistics planners reference this link daily to ensure each delivery aligns with the committed fu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.production_confirmation. Business justification: Outbound deliveries of finished goods are initiated only after production confirmation. Warehouse and logistics teams reference the production confirmation to validate goods are ready for shipment — a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to logistics.route

Foreign key linking to order.schedule_line. Business justification: In manufacturing, deliveries are executed against confirmed schedule lines (ATP-confirmed delivery dates and quantities). Order fulfillment teams use this link to confirm which schedule line commitmen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to service.spare_parts_request. Business justification: A delivery is the physical fulfillment of a spare parts request raised by the service team. Logistics coordinators track which delivery closes which spare parts request to confirm parts reached the fi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.warranty_policy. Business justification: Outbound delivery documents for industrial equipment must reference the applicable warranty policy to include warranty certificates in shipment documentation. Customer service and logistics teams use
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The date on which goods were physically delivered to and received by the recipient. Used to calculate on-time delivery (OTD) performance against the planned delivery date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Proof of Delivery; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.5 Post-Delivery Activities

description: The actual date on which goods were physically dispatched from the shipping point. Captured at goods issue posting and used for carrier performance and lead time analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Goods Issue Posting; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.4 Preservation

description: Date and time when the outbound delivery document was created in the source system. Used for lead time analysis, process cycle time measurement, and audit trail compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Creation; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Reference number of the export license issued by the relevant government authority for controlled goods or dual-use items. Required for compliance with export control regulations for industrial automation and electrification products.
reference: US EAR (Export Administration Regulations); EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations)

description: Terms defining who bears the freight cost for this delivery — whether the shipper prepays, the consignee pays on collection, or a third party is billed. Impacts accounts payable and customer invoicing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Freight Terms; ANSI X12 EDI 210 Motor Carrier Freight Details

description: The accounting date on which the goods issue was posted in the ERP system, triggering inventory reduction and revenue recognition. Critical for financial period-end closing and COGS recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Goods Issue Posting; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP ASC 606

description: Precise date and time when the goods issue was posted in the ERP system. Provides sub-day precision for operational reporting, shift-level analysis, and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Goods Issue; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5 Production and Service Provision

description: Flag indicating whether the delivery contains hazardous materials subject to special handling, labeling, and transport regulations. Triggers hazmat documentation requirements and carrier restrictions.
reference: IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR); IMDG Code (International Maritime Dangerous Goods); ADR (European Agreement on Dangerous Goods by Road); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, risk transfer, insurance, and freight costs. Governs liability and cost allocation for the shipment.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD Incoterms; WCO Trade Documentation

description: Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact location where risk and cost transfer between seller and buyer (e.g., 'Port of Hamburg' for FOB, 'Customer Warehouse' for DDP).
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD Incoterms Location

description: Date and time of the most recent modification to the delivery document in the source system. Used for change detection in data integration pipelines and audit trail maintenance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Change Log; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the outbound delivery document as assigned by the source system (SAP SD). Used for tracking, communication with carriers, and customer-facing references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Outbound Delivery; ISO 6422 Layout Key for Trade Documents

description: Quantity of goods shipped in excess of the ordered quantity. Requires customer approval or return authorization and may trigger credit memo processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Variance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs

description: Aggregate goods movement status across all line items in the delivery, indicating whether goods issue has been fully posted, partially posted, or not yet started. Derived from SAP SD header-level goods movement status.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Outbound Delivery Header Status

description: Reference number of the packing list document associated with this delivery, detailing the contents, quantities, and packaging of shipped goods. Required for customs clearance and receiving verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Packing List; ISO 6422; WCO (World Customs Organization) Trade Documentation Standards

description: Status of the packing process for this delivery, indicating whether goods have been fully, partially, or not yet packed into handling units or shipping containers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Packing Status; Infor WMS Packing Operations

description: Status of the warehouse picking process for this delivery, indicating whether warehouse staff have fully, partially, or not yet picked the goods from storage locations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM/EWM Picking Status; Infor WMS Picking Operations

description: The originally scheduled date on which goods are expected to be delivered to the recipient. Used for on-time delivery performance measurement and customer commitment tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Scheduling; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3 Review of Requirements

description: The scheduled date on which goods are planned to be dispatched from the shipping point. Used for transportation planning, carrier booking, and warehouse workload scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Scheduling; TMS Route Planning

description: The date on which proof of delivery was confirmed by the recipient or carrier, indicating that goods were received at the destination. Triggers billing and revenue recognition in POD-based billing scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Proof of Delivery (POD); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Reference number or identifier for the proof-of-delivery document, such as a carrier-issued POD number, electronic signature reference, or consignment note number. Used for dispute resolution and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD POD Processing; CMR Convention (Convention on the Contract for the International Carriage of Goods by Road)

description: Business priority classification assigned to the delivery, influencing warehouse picking sequence, carrier selection, and transportation mode. Critical deliveries may be linked to production line stoppage prevention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Priority; SLA Agreement Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the origin location from which goods are shipped. Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, and export control screening.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WCO Customs Data Model; EU Customs Code

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country to which goods are being delivered. Used for import duty calculation, trade compliance, and export control classification.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WCO Customs Data Model; EU Customs Code; US Export Administration Regulations (EAR)

description: SAP organizational unit representing the physical location (plant, warehouse, or dock) from which the delivery is dispatched. Determines the shipping calendar, transportation zone, and warehouse management assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Shipping Point Configuration

description: Quantity of goods that was ordered but not shipped due to stock shortages, picking errors, or capacity constraints. A non-zero value triggers back-order processing and customer notification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Variance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs

description: Identifier of the operational source system from which this delivery record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA system ID or client). Supports multi-system landscapes and data lineage traceability in the lakehouse.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; NIST SP 800-53 Data Provenance

description: Current processing status of the outbound delivery document, tracking the lifecycle from creation through goods issue posting, transit, and final proof-of-delivery confirmation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Processing Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5 Production and Service Provision

description: Total quantity of all line items included in this delivery document, expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for fulfillment rate calculation and short-ship/over-ship variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Quantity

description: Total gross weight of all items in the delivery including packaging, expressed in kilograms. Used for freight cost calculation, carrier capacity planning, and customs declarations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Weight; WCO Customs Data Model; IATA Air Cargo Standards

description: Total net weight of all items in the delivery excluding packaging, expressed in kilograms. Used for customs valuation, product compliance declarations, and REACH/RoHS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Weight; WCO Customs Data Model; EU REACH Regulation

description: Total volumetric measurement of all items in the delivery expressed in cubic meters. Used for truck load planning, container utilization, and freight cost calculation based on dimensional weight.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Volume; IATA Dimensional Weight Standards

description: Carrier-issued tracking number (e.g., waybill number, bill of lading number, or parcel tracking ID) used to monitor shipment progress through the carrier's tracking system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD External Tracking; TMS Shipment Tracking

description: Mode of transportation used for the delivery, such as road (truck), rail, air freight, sea freight, or multimodal. Drives freight cost calculation, transit time estimation, and carbon emissions reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Transportation Mode; UN/CEFACT Transport Mode Codes; GHG Protocol Scope 3 Emissions

description: Classification of the delivery indicating whether it is an outbound shipment to a customer, an interplant transfer between manufacturing sites, a replenishment to a distribution center, a return delivery, or a subcontractor delivery.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Type Configuration

description: United Nations four-digit number identifying the hazardous substance or article in the delivery, as defined by the UN Committee of Experts on the Transport of Dangerous Goods. Required when hazmat_indicator is true.
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods (Orange Book); IATA DGR; IMDG Code; ADR

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the inbound delivery record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: Internal Data Architecture Standard

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Three-way matching in manufacturing requires linking inbound goods receipt to the supplier AP invoice. AP teams use this daily to confirm delivery before releasing payment to suppliers.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Inbound deliveries are executed by a carrier. inbound_delivery.carrier_name is a denormalized reference to the carrier master. Adding carrier_id FK enforces referential integrity and enables carrier p
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order. Business justification: logistics_inbound_delivery tracks receipt of purchased materials from suppliers. The inbound delivery is physically executed under a freight order issued to the inbound carrier. Linking logistics_inbo
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: An inbound delivery in logistics is the physical execution of a goods receipt in procurement. Warehouse teams reconcile the two daily — the inbound delivery record must reference the procurement goods
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.planned_order. Business justification: Inbound deliveries of raw materials or components are triggered by planned production orders. Supply chain planners link inbound deliveries to planned orders daily to confirm material availability and
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Inbound deliveries are received at a specific plant where the product is stocked or manufactured. Goods receipt teams use the product-plant assignment to validate correct receiving location, trigger i
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Current customs clearance status of the shipment at the time of this event. Critical for cross-border trade compliance, import/export documentation management, and REACH/RoHS regulatory adherence.

description: Duration in minutes the shipment remained stationary at the event location before the next movement. Used for terminal efficiency analysis, detention charge management, and bottleneck identification.
reference: TMS Industry Standard; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Carrier-provided or TMS-calculated estimated delivery date updated at the time of this tracking event. Used for OTIF measurement, customer promise management, and ATP/CTP calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ANSI X12 EDI 214; OTIF KPI Framework

description: Identifies the originating system or feed that generated the tracking event, such as carrier EDI, TMS, GPS telematics, IIoT sensor, or manual entry. Supports data lineage and source reliability analysis.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI 214; Siemens MindSphere IIoT; Infor WMS; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: The exact date and time at which the tracking event occurred, as reported by the carrier, IIoT telematics, GPS device, or TMS integration. Used for transit time analysis and OTIF measurement.
reference: ISO 8601; ANSI X12 EDI 214; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: IANA timezone identifier for the location where the event occurred (e.g., America/Chicago, Europe/Berlin). Enables accurate local-time reporting across global distribution networks.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; ISO 8601

description: Categorizes the nature of the shipment milestone or status change event, such as departed, in-transit, customs-cleared, out-for-delivery, delivered, or exception. Drives OTIF performance measurement and shipment visibility dashboards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; TMS Industry Standard; ANSI X12 EDI 214

description: Standardized code classifying the type of shipment exception when is_exception is true. Used for root cause analysis, CAPA processes, and carrier performance management.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI 214; SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 CAPA

description: Free-text description of the shipment exception providing additional context beyond the exception code, such as specific damage details, delay reason, or customs hold explanation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this tracking event represents a shipment exception (e.g., delay, damage, lost, refused delivery). Triggers exception management workflows and customer notifications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; TMS Industry Standard; SLA Management

description: Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the event location as captured by GPS telematics or IIoT device. Enables real-time map visualization and geofence-based alerting.
reference: WGS84 (World Geodetic System 1984); ISO 6709; Siemens MindSphere IIoT

description: Standardized location identifier where the event occurred, such as a UN/LOCODE port code, facility code, or carrier hub code (e.g., USORD for Chicago O'Hare). Used for network analysis and lane performance reporting.
reference: UN/LOCODE; IATA Airport Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Classifies the type of location where the event occurred, enabling network topology analysis and identification of bottlenecks in the distribution network.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; TMS Industry Standard

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the event location as captured by GPS telematics or IIoT device. Enables real-time map visualization and geofence-based alerting.
reference: WGS84 (World Geodetic System 1984); ISO 6709; Siemens MindSphere IIoT

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the tracking event record in the Silver layer, such as exception enrichment or status correction. Supports change data capture and audit trail requirements.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Architecture; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Reference number or document identifier for the proof-of-delivery (POD) record associated with a delivered event. Used for freight audit, claims processing, and customer dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Infor WMS

description: Progressive (PRO) number assigned by the carrier for LTL (Less-Than-Truckload) shipments. Used for freight bill matching, claims processing, and carrier reconciliation.
reference: NMFTA; ANSI X12 EDI 214

description: Reference key or identifier pointing to the original raw EDI, API, or telematics message stored in the Bronze layer. Supports data lineage, reprocessing, and audit trail requirements.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Architecture; ANSI X12 EDI 214

description: Ordinal sequence number of this tracking event within the shipment's event history, starting from 1. Enables chronological ordering of events and detection of out-of-sequence EDI transmissions.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI 214; EDIFACT IFTSTA

description: Name or identifier of the person who signed for the delivery at the destination, as captured in the proof-of-delivery (POD) record. Used for delivery confirmation and dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Infor WMS; Proof of Delivery (POD) Standard

description: Lifecycle status of the tracking event record in the Silver layer. 'Superseded' indicates a later event has replaced this one; 'duplicate' flags EDI retransmissions. Supports data quality management and deduplication.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Convention; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Ambient or cargo temperature in degrees Celsius recorded by IIoT telematics or cold-chain sensors at the time of the event. Used for cold-chain compliance monitoring and quality assurance of temperature-sensitive goods.
reference: Siemens MindSphere IIoT; ISO 9001 Quality Management; GDP (Good Distribution Practice)

description: Carrier-assigned tracking number or waybill number associated with the shipment at the time of this event. Used for carrier portal lookups and customer-facing shipment visibility.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI 214; EDIFACT IFTSTA; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Mode of transportation active at the time of this tracking event (e.g., road, rail, air, ocean, intermodal). Drives freight cost analysis, carbon footprint reporting, and modal performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; INCOTERMS 2020; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Identifier of the vehicle, vessel, aircraft, or rail car transporting the shipment at the time of this event (e.g., truck license plate, IMO vessel number, flight number). Supports asset tracking and incident investigation.
reference: IMO Ship Identification; IATA Flight Numbering; Siemens MindSphere IIoT

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the freight order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order (TRKM_FO)

description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Freight orders for industrial equipment shipments must reference the customer delivery address to coordinate carrier pickup and drop-off. Freight coordinators use this to validate destination before d
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to logistics.carrier

Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Freight orders are placed for a specific carrier service (LTL, FTL, express, etc.). Adding carrier_service_id FK normalizes the service reference to the carrier_service master, enabling service-level
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Freight orders must be assigned to a controlling area to enable cost tracking and internal reporting. Controlling teams use this to aggregate logistics spend within defined organizational boundaries.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Freight order costs must be posted to the correct fiscal period for period-end closing. Finance controllers use this link to ensure logistics costs are accrued and reported in the right accounting per
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Freight orders are created to execute the transportation leg of a fulfillment plan. Logistics coordinators tie freight orders to fulfillment plans to ensure transport capacity is aligned with committe
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: freight_order currently stores origin_location_code as a denormalized STRING. The logistics.location master table is the authoritative source for all physical locations in the network. Adding origin_l
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: A freight order is created to transport goods procured via a purchase order. Logistics coordinators reference the originating PO when booking freight to ensure correct quantities, destinations, and su
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: Freight orders execute along a defined transportation route/lane. Adding route_id FK normalizes the lane reference. origin_location_code and destination_location_code are retained as execution-specifi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.tooling_equipment. Business justification: Industrial manufacturers frequently ship tooling and equipment between plants or to customer sites. The freight order references the specific tooling equipment record to track asset movement, ensure p
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.transport_plan. Business justification: Freight orders are generated from TMS transport plans. Adding transport_plan_id to freight_order establishes the plan→execution relationship, enabling plan fulfillment tracking and variance analysis b
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual date and time at which the freight was delivered to the destination. Used to calculate on-time delivery (OTD) performance, transit time actuals, and SLA compliance against planned delivery windows.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 8601 Timestamp; ISO 9001 Delivery Performance

description: Actual date and time at which the carrier collected the freight from the origin location. Used to calculate pickup punctuality, carrier on-time performance, and transit time actuals.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 8601 Timestamp; Carrier Performance KPI

description: Unique identifier of the Bill of Lading (BOL) or Air Waybill (AWB) issued for the freight order. Serves as the primary transport document, title of goods, and carrier receipt. Required for customs, insurance, and payment terms.
reference: IMO Bill of Lading; IATA Air Waybill; UCP 600 (ICC); SAP TM Freight Document

description: Carrier-assigned booking or reservation reference number confirming the carrier's acceptance of the freight order. Used for shipment tracking, carrier portal access, and proof of booking.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; IATA Cargo Booking Reference; IMO Bill of Lading Standards

description: Legal or trade name of the transportation carrier assigned to execute the freight order. Used for carrier performance reporting, invoice reconciliation, and compliance documentation.
reference: SAP TM Carrier Master; FMCSA Carrier Registry; EU Regulation 1071/2009

description: Carrier-confirmed or invoiced freight cost for the order after execution. Used for freight invoice reconciliation, actual vs. estimated variance analysis, and COGS allocation.
reference: SAP TM Freight Settlement; IFRS 15; GAAP COGS; SAP FI/CO

description: Date and time at which the freight order was initially created in the TMS. Used for order aging analysis, SLA compliance measurement, and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 8601; ISO 9001 Record Control

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which freight costs are denominated. Supports multi-currency operations for global freight procurement and financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP; SAP FI Multi-Currency

description: Official customs declaration or entry number assigned by customs authorities for cross-border freight movements. Required for import/export compliance, duty payment tracking, and trade audit trails.
reference: WCO Customs Data Model; EU Customs Code (UCC) Regulation 952/2013; US CBP Entry Number; SAP GTS

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the freight order destination. Required for import customs declarations, duty calculation, trade compliance screening, and restricted party checks.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WCO Customs Data Model; EU Customs Code; OFAC Screening

description: Standardized code identifying the delivery or destination location for the freight order, such as a customer site, distribution center, or port. Used for route planning, delivery scheduling, and carrier assignment.
reference: UN/LOCODE; SAP TM Location Master; Infor WMS Location

description: Pre-execution estimated freight cost for the order as calculated by the TMS rate engine or carrier quote. Used for freight budget planning, cost allocation, and purchase order approval workflows.
reference: SAP TM Rate Management; IFRS 15; GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Government-issued export license number authorizing the shipment of controlled goods across international borders. Required for dual-use goods, defense articles, and technology subject to export control regulations.
reference: US EAR (Export Administration Regulations); ITAR; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; SAP GTS Export Control

description: Total gross weight of all freight items included in the freight order, expressed in kilograms. Used for carrier rate calculation, vehicle load planning, weight compliance checks, and customs declarations.
reference: SAP TM Load Planning; IATA Weight Standards; EU Road Transport Regulation (EC) 561/2006

description: Indicates whether the freight order contains hazardous materials (Hazmat) requiring special handling, documentation, and regulatory compliance. Triggers ADR/IATA DGR/IMDG documentation requirements and carrier hazmat certification checks.
reference: ADR (European Agreement on Dangerous Goods by Road); IATA DGR; IMDG Code; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; EPA RCRA; REACH; RoHS

description: United Nations (UN) four-digit number identifying the specific hazardous substance or article in the freight order. Required on shipping documents, labels, and placards for regulatory compliance when hazmat_flag is true.
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods; ADR; IATA DGR; IMDG Code; OSHA

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the allocation of costs, risks, and responsibilities between shipper and consignee for the freight movement. Governs insurance, customs clearance, and freight cost ownership.
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC); SAP SD/TM Incoterms; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition

description: Named place, port, or location associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact point of risk and cost transfer between shipper and consignee as required by Incoterms 2020.
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC); SAP TM/SD Incoterms Location

description: Date and time of the most recent update to the freight order record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 8601; ISO 9001 Record Control; GDPR Article 5 (Data Accuracy)

description: Linear loading meters (LDM) required on a trailer or vehicle for the freight order. Standard European road freight capacity unit used for LTL/FTL determination, carrier rate calculation, and load planning.
reference: SAP TM Load Planning; European Road Freight LDM Standard; IRU

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the freight order as assigned by the Transportation Management System (TMS). Used for carrier communication, shipment tracking, and cross-system reference.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 6346 (Container/Unit Identification)

description: Classification of the freight order by direction and operational purpose. Distinguishes inbound supplier deliveries, outbound customer shipments, interplant transfers, customer returns, cross-dock movements, and direct drop shipments.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order Type; Infor WMS Shipment Classification

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the freight order origin location. Required for customs declarations, export licensing, trade compliance screening, and freight cost determination.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WCO Customs Data Model; EU Customs Code

description: Total number of individual packages, pallets, or handling units included in the freight order. Used for carrier booking, loading verification, proof of delivery reconciliation, and customs packing list.
reference: SAP TM Handling Unit Management; WCO Customs Packing List; IATA ULD Standards

description: Scheduled calendar date on which the freight is expected to arrive at the destination. Used for customer delivery commitments, Available to Promise (ATP) calculations, and delivery performance measurement.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 8601; ATP/CTP Standards

description: Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the freight is scheduled to arrive at the destination. Supports time-window delivery commitments, receiving dock scheduling, and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 8601 Timestamp; SLA Agreement Standards

description: Scheduled calendar date on which the carrier is expected to collect the freight from the origin location. Used for production scheduling, warehouse outbound planning, and carrier appointment management.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 8601 Date Format

description: Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the carrier is scheduled to collect the freight. Enables dock scheduling, labor planning, and carrier appointment window management at the origin facility.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order; ISO 8601 Timestamp

description: Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number used to track LTL (Less-than-Truckload) shipments through the carrier's network. Standard identifier for freight tracking, claims, and invoice matching in North American road freight.
reference: NMFTA PRO Number Standard; ANSI X12 EDI 214; SAP TM Shipment Tracking

description: Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled freight during transport. Used to specify cold chain upper limits for carrier compliance and monitoring alert thresholds.
reference: GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 68/01); ISO 21898; SAP TM Temperature Range

description: Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled freight during transport. Used to specify cold chain requirements for carrier compliance and monitoring alert thresholds.
reference: GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 68/01); ISO 21898; SAP TM Temperature Range

description: Contracted service level defining the speed and priority of freight execution. Determines carrier selection, cost tier, and delivery commitment windows aligned with customer SLA agreements.
reference: SAP TM Service Level; SLA Agreement Standards; ISO 9001

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which the freight order originated. Supports data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and multi-system integration governance in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: SAP TM; Infor WMS; ANSI X12 EDI; Data Governance Best Practices

description: Free-text field capturing specific handling requirements for the freight order, such as fragile goods, top-load only, no stacking, liftgate required, or white-glove delivery. Communicated to the carrier for execution compliance.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order Notes; IATA Special Handling Codes; ISO 780 Packaging Marking

description: Current lifecycle status of the freight order, tracking progression from initial creation through carrier confirmation, active transport execution, and final delivery or cancellation.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order Status; ISO 9001 Process Control

description: Indicates whether the freight order requires temperature-controlled transport (cold chain or heated). Triggers selection of refrigerated or temperature-controlled vehicles and monitoring requirements.
reference: SAP TM Special Requirements; GDP Guidelines (EU); ISO 21898 Packaging for Temperature-Sensitive Goods

description: Primary mode of transportation used for executing the freight order. Drives carrier selection, rate determination, transit time estimation, and customs documentation requirements.
reference: IATA; IMO; ANSI X12 EDI 204; Incoterms 2020 (ICC)

description: Total cubic volume of all freight items in the freight order, expressed in cubic meters. Used for vehicle capacity planning, dimensional weight calculation, and container/trailer utilization optimization.
reference: SAP TM Load Planning; IATA Volumetric Weight; ISO 668 Container Dimensions

description: Unique system-generated identifier for a transportation route or lane definition within the Transportation Management System (TMS). Serves as the primary key for all route-related data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM (Transportation Management); Infor WMS

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_location. Business justification: Route origin is a physical logistics network location. Adding origin_logistics_location_id FK normalizes the origin reference to the logistics_location master. origin_location_code is retained as a co
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Number of international border crossings on this route. Used to assess customs complexity, transit documentation requirements, and potential delay risk.
reference: WCO (World Customs Organization); EU CE Marking; REACH/RoHS

description: Carbon dioxide (CO2) emission factor for this route expressed in kilograms of CO2 per kilometer, based on transport mode and carrier fleet data. Used for environmental reporting, carbon footprint calculation, and ISO 14001 / ESG compliance.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management; EPA; EU CE Marking; GHG Protocol (Greenhouse Gas Protocol)

description: Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the transportation route or lane, used in TMS, ERP, and operational communications (e.g., 'USCHI-DEHAM-SEA').
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM; ISO 6346 (freight container identification)

description: Timestamp when the route record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); SAP S/4HANA

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the standard freight cost and rate agreements for this route are denominated (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'CNY').
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Indicates whether customs clearance documentation and procedures are required for shipments on this route (e.g., cross-border international routes). Drives trade compliance workflow activation.
reference: EU CE Marking; REACH/RoHS; WCO (World Customs Organization); GDPR

description: Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) by which a shipment must be tendered to the carrier to meet the scheduled departure on this route. Critical for order fulfillment and warehouse operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM; Infor WMS

description: Comma-separated list of days of the week on which departures are scheduled on this route (e.g., 'MON,WED,FRI'). Used for shipment scheduling and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; Infor WMS

description: Detailed narrative description of the route, including any special handling requirements, routing constraints, or operational notes relevant to logistics planners.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country where the route terminates. Used for import customs declarations, trade compliance, and duty calculations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; EU CE Marking; REACH/RoHS Compliance; GDPR

description: Standardized code identifying the arrival point of the route (e.g., customer delivery point, distribution center, port code, or UN/LOCODE). Represents where freight is delivered.
reference: UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations); SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Total distance of the route measured in kilometers. Used for freight cost calculation, carbon emission estimation, and TMS route optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; ISO 80000-3 (Quantities and Units)

description: Total distance of the route measured in miles. Provided for regions using imperial measurement (e.g., USA, UK) and for carrier rate agreements denominated in miles.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; ANSI

description: Date from which this route definition becomes valid and available for shipment assignment. Used for time-bound route management and seasonal lane activation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; IFRS; GAAP

description: Date after which this route definition is no longer valid for shipment assignment. Used for contract-bound lanes, seasonal routes, and regulatory compliance periods.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; IFRS; GAAP

description: Operational frequency at which shipments are dispatched on this route. Used for production planning, inventory replenishment scheduling, and carrier capacity booking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/TM; Infor WMS

description: Indicates whether the transport of hazardous materials (HAZMAT) is permitted on this route. Governs compliance with dangerous goods regulations and carrier restrictions.
reference: OSHA; EPA; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR); ADR (European Agreement on Dangerous Goods by Road); REACH/RoHS

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the responsibilities of buyers and sellers for delivery of goods on this route, including risk transfer and cost allocation points.
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC – International Chamber of Commerce); SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the route record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5; SAP S/4HANA

description: Maximum allowable shipment volume in cubic meters for a single consignment on this route, based on carrier capacity and vehicle/vessel constraints.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; ISO 80000-3

description: Maximum allowable shipment weight in kilograms for a single consignment on this route, based on carrier capacity, regulatory limits, and infrastructure constraints.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; ISO 80000-4 (Mechanics); ANSI

description: Maximum number of calendar days allowed for transit on this route before a shipment is considered delayed. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach monitoring and carrier performance management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Minimum number of calendar days achievable for transit on this route under optimal conditions. Used for express service planning and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM

description: Descriptive human-readable name for the transportation route, typically combining origin and destination location names and transport mode (e.g., 'Chicago to Hamburg – Ocean Freight').
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; Infor WMS

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country where the route originates. Used for customs, trade compliance, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; EU CE Marking; REACH/RoHS Compliance

description: Standardized code identifying the departure point of the route (e.g., plant code, warehouse code, port code, or UN/LOCODE). Represents where freight originates.
reference: UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations); SAP S/4HANA MM/SD

description: Code identifying the primary/preferred carrier assigned to this route based on carrier selection criteria such as cost, reliability, and service level. Used by TMS for automatic carrier assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; SAP Ariba (Supplier Management)

description: Code identifying the geographic logistics region or trade lane cluster to which this route belongs (e.g., 'EMEA', 'APAC', 'AMER'). Used for regional freight management and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM; UN/LOCODE

description: Level of service associated with this route, defining speed and priority of delivery. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) assignment and freight rate differentiation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; IATA; Incoterms 2020 (ICC)

description: Standard or benchmark freight cost for a full shipment on this route, expressed in the route's base currency. Used for freight budget planning, cost allocation, and carrier rate benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS 15; GAAP

description: Standard number of calendar days expected for freight to travel from origin to destination on this route under normal operating conditions. Used for delivery date calculation and SLA (Service Level Agreement) commitments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM; Incoterms 2020 (ICC)

description: Current operational status of the route. 'Active' routes are available for shipment assignment; 'inactive' routes are not currently in use; 'suspended' routes are temporarily halted; 'under_review' routes are being evaluated for modification; 'discontinued' routes have been permanently retired.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Indicates whether this route requires temperature-controlled (cold chain) transportation. Relevant for chemical, pharmaceutical, or sensitive industrial components requiring specific temperature ranges.
reference: ISO 9001; ISO 14001; OSHA

description: External route identifier as recorded in the source Transportation Management System (TMS), used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; Infor WMS

description: Indicates whether road tolls, port fees, canal dues, or other infrastructure usage charges apply on this route. Used for accurate freight cost estimation and carrier invoice validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; ANSI

description: Code identifying the intermediate consolidation or transshipment hub used on hub-and-spoke or relay routes. Applicable when route_type is 'hub_and_spoke' or 'relay'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; UN/LOCODE

description: Primary mode of transportation used on this route. Drives carrier selection, freight rate assignment, transit time estimation, and TMS routing logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; Incoterms 2020 (ICC); IATA; IMO

description: Classification of the route's structural pattern. 'Direct' indicates point-to-point movement; 'hub_and_spoke' involves a consolidation hub; 'multimodal' uses multiple transport modes; 'milk_run' involves multiple pickup/delivery stops; 'cross_dock' involves transshipment without storage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; Infor WMS; CSCMP (Council of Supply Chain Management Professionals)

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each individual stop or waypoint within a transportation route. Serves as the primary key for the route_stop entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM (Transportation Management) - Stop Node Identifier

description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Route stops reference customer addresses for GPS routing, delivery sequencing, and geofencing. Route optimization systems use this for daily route planning and execution.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_location. Business justification: Each route stop corresponds to a physical logistics location. Adding logistics_location_id FK normalizes the stop location reference. location_name, address_line_1, city, state_province, postal_code,
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: route_stop is a child record of route, representing individual waypoints within a multi-leg route. Adding route_id FK establishes the parent→child relationship. Stop-specific address and timing fields
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual date and time the carrier arrived at this stop, captured via TMS event, driver mobile app, or geofence trigger. Used for on-time arrival performance tracking and carrier SLA measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Actual Arrival; Infor WMS; ISO 8601

description: Actual date and time the carrier departed from this stop. Used to calculate actual dwell time, identify detention events, and measure stop-level execution performance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Actual Departure; Infor WMS; ISO 8601

description: Actual duration in minutes the carrier remained at this stop, derived from actual arrival and departure timestamps. Used to identify detention, inefficiencies, and carrier performance deviations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Dwell Time; ANSI ASC X12 EDI 214

description: Actual quantity of goods loaded onto the vehicle at this stop, as confirmed by the driver, warehouse staff, or WMS. Compared against planned load quantity to identify discrepancies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; Infor WMS - Goods Receipt Note (GRN)

description: Actual quantity of goods unloaded from the vehicle at this stop, as confirmed by the receiver or WMS. Used for proof-of-delivery validation and discrepancy management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; Infor WMS - Goods Receipt Note (GRN)

description: Reference number for the dock or facility appointment scheduled for this stop. Links the route stop to the appointment management system for dock scheduling and carrier check-in.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Appointment Management; Infor WMS

description: Difference in minutes between actual and planned arrival timestamps. Positive values indicate late arrival; negative values indicate early arrival. Key metric for on-time delivery (OTD) performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - On-Time Performance; SLA Agreement

description: Indicates whether customs clearance activities are required at this stop. True for border crossings, customs offices, or bonded warehouse stops. Triggers customs documentation workflow.
reference: EU Customs Code; REACH/RoHS; WCO (World Customs Organization) Data Model

description: Official customs declaration or entry number issued by the customs authority for goods processed at this stop. Required for cross-border shipments and trade compliance audit trails.
reference: WCO Data Model; EU Customs Code; US CBP (Customs and Border Protection)

description: Identifier of the specific dock door or bay assigned to this stop at the facility. Used for dock scheduling, yard management, and operational coordination between TMS and WMS.
reference: Infor WMS - Dock Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Timestamp when the vehicle entered the geofenced boundary of the stop location, as detected by the telematics or IIoT platform. Provides an objective, system-generated arrival signal independent of driver input.
reference: Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform - Asset Monitoring; SAP S/4HANA TM

description: Timestamp when the vehicle exited the geofenced boundary of the stop location, as detected by the telematics or IIoT platform. Used to validate actual dwell time and departure confirmation.
reference: Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform - Asset Monitoring; SAP S/4HANA TM

description: Indicates whether this stop is the origin or first stop of the route. Used to distinguish route origin from intermediate stops in multi-leg route analysis and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Route Structure

description: Indicates whether this stop is the final destination or last stop of the route. Used to identify route completion events and trigger final delivery confirmation workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Route Structure

description: Indicates whether the carrier arrived within the agreed delivery or pickup time window at this stop. True if actual arrival falls within the time window; False if outside. Used for SLA compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - On-Time Performance; SLA Agreement; ISO 9001

description: Standardized business identifier for the physical location of this stop, such as a warehouse, plant, customer site, port, or customs facility. Aligns with the enterprise location master in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Location Master; GS1 GLN (Global Location Number)

description: Classification of the stop location by facility type. Drives operational rules, dwell time expectations, and compliance requirements applicable at the stop.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Location Type; ISO 28219

description: Scheduled date and time the carrier is expected to arrive at this stop, as defined in the Transportation Management System (TMS) route plan. Baseline for on-time performance measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Planned Arrival; ISO 8601

description: Scheduled date and time the carrier is expected to depart from this stop, as defined in the TMS route plan. Used to calculate planned dwell time and downstream stop arrival windows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Planned Departure; ISO 8601

description: Planned duration in minutes the carrier is expected to remain at this stop for loading, unloading, or other activities. Used for route scheduling and carrier appointment management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Dwell Time; ANSI ASC X12 EDI 214

description: Planned quantity of goods to be loaded onto the vehicle at this stop, expressed in the unit of measure defined at the shipment level. Applicable for pickup and cross-dock stop types.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Load Planning; Infor WMS

description: Planned quantity of goods to be unloaded from the vehicle at this stop. Applicable for delivery and cross-dock stop types. Used for dock resource planning and shipment verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Unload Planning; Infor WMS

description: Reference number for the proof of delivery document captured at this stop upon successful goods handover. Used for invoice verification, dispute resolution, and customer confirmation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Proof of Delivery; ANSI ASC X12 EDI 214

description: Unit of measure applicable to planned and actual load/unload quantities at this stop (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, PLT for pallets). Ensures consistent quantity interpretation across stops.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Unit of Measure; ISO 80000; GS1

description: Name of the individual or entity who received or handed over goods at this stop, as recorded on the proof of delivery. Used for delivery confirmation and dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Confirmation; GDPR Article 4

description: Identifier of the originating operational system from which this route stop record was sourced (e.g., SAP TM, Infor WMS). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Data Provenance; SAP S/4HANA TM

description: Code indicating any special handling requirements applicable to goods at this stop, such as hazardous materials (HAZMAT), temperature-controlled, or high-value cargo. Drives compliance and operational procedures.
reference: IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; ADR (European Agreement on Dangerous Goods); OSHA; EPA

description: Distance in kilometers from the previous stop (or route origin for the first stop) to this stop. Used for freight cost allocation, carrier rate calculation, and route optimization analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Distance Calculation; ANSI ASC X12 EDI 204

description: Standardized code identifying any exception or deviation that occurred at this stop. Used for root cause analysis, carrier performance management, and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Exception Management; ISO 9001 CAPA

description: Free-text description providing additional context for any exception or deviation recorded at this stop. Supplements the exception code with operational details for investigation and resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Exception Notes; ISO 9001

description: Ordinal position of this stop within the overall route, starting from 1. Determines the order in which the carrier visits locations along the route. Used for milk-run and multi-leg route planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Stop Sequence; ISO 28219 Supply Chain Traceability

description: Current operational status of the stop within the route execution lifecycle. Tracks progression from planned through arrival, completion, or exception states such as delay, skip, or cancellation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Stop Status; Infor WMS Shipment Tracking

description: Classification of the purpose of this stop within the route. Determines operational activities performed at the stop, such as loading (pickup), unloading (delivery), cross-docking, customs clearance, or driver rest.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Stop Category; IATA Cargo Handling Manual

description: Latest acceptable arrival time at this stop as agreed with the customer or facility. Defines the closing of the delivery or pickup appointment window. Arrivals after this time are flagged as SLA breaches.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Time Window; ISO 8601; SLA Agreement

description: Earliest acceptable arrival time at this stop as agreed with the customer or facility. Defines the opening of the delivery or pickup appointment window for dock scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Time Window; ISO 8601; SLA Agreement

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the Transportation Management System (TMS) transport plan record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP TM (Transportation Management) / Internal Data Architecture Standard

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: transport_plan is the TMS-generated plan consolidating shipment demands. It currently stores origin_region and origin_country_code as STRING fields for planning purposes. Adding origin_location_id FK
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to logistics.carrier

Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: Transport plans are built around specific transportation lanes/routes. Adding route_id FK links the TMS plan to the route master, enabling route-level planning analytics and optimization tracking. ori
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Business reason provided when a transport plan is cancelled or placed on hold (e.g., 'demand_cancelled', 'carrier_unavailable', 'route_blocked', 'regulatory_hold'). Populated only when status is 'cancelled' or 'on_hold'.
reference: SAP TM / ISO 9001 Non-Conformance Management
