description: Date and time when the transport plan was formally confirmed by the logistics planner, transitioning from 'draft' to 'confirmed' status. Used for planning cycle time analysis.
reference: SAP TM / ISO 9001 Process Audit Trail

description: Date and time when the transport plan record was initially created in the TMS. Used for audit trail, planning cycle analysis, and data lineage tracking.
reference: SAP TM / ISO 9001 Record Control

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the planned freight spend and cost values in this transport plan (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes / IFRS / GAAP

description: Indicates whether customs clearance documentation and procedures are required for freight movements in this transport plan. True for cross-border international shipments.
reference: EU CE Marking / REACH / RoHS / Customs and Border Protection

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of freight destination for this transport plan. Used for import/export compliance and duty calculation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 / EU CE Marking / REACH / RoHS Compliance

description: Geographic region or logistics zone to which freight is destined in this transport plan. Used for delivery performance reporting and freight cost allocation by region.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD / Infor WMS Logistics Zone Configuration

description: Reference identifier of the MRP II Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP) run that generated the shipment demands consolidated into this transport plan. Provides traceability from demand signal to transport execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MRP II / Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP)

description: Reference code linking this transport plan to the corresponding freight budget line item in the financial planning system. Enables freight spend tracking against approved OPEX budgets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center / IFRS / GAAP

description: Indicates whether any freight loads in this transport plan contain hazardous materials (HAZMAT) requiring special handling, documentation, and regulatory compliance.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 / IATA DGR / ADR / REACH / RoHS

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the transfer of risk, cost responsibility, and delivery obligations between shipper and receiver for freight in this plan.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020 / SAP S/4HANA SD Trade Terms

description: Date and time of the most recent modification to the transport plan record. Used for change tracking, version control, and incremental data pipeline processing.
reference: SAP TM / ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Free-text field for logistics planners to capture additional context, special instructions, or exceptions related to the transport plan (e.g., peak season constraints, customer-specific requirements).
reference: SAP TM / Operational Logistics Standard

description: Primary optimization criterion used by the TMS route optimization engine when generating the transport plan. Drives carrier selection, load consolidation, and route assignment decisions.
reference: SAP TM Freight Optimization / ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of freight origin for this transport plan. Used for customs and trade compliance documentation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 / Customs and Trade Compliance

description: Geographic region or logistics zone from which freight originates in this transport plan (e.g., 'EMEA', 'APAC', 'AMER'). Used for regional freight budget allocation and carrier lane management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD / Infor WMS Logistics Zone Configuration

description: Descriptive name or label assigned to the transport plan for easy identification in planning dashboards and reporting (e.g., 'Q3-EMEA-Outbound-Week42').
reference: SAP TM / Infor WMS Operational Standard

description: Human-readable business reference number for the transport plan, used for cross-system communication, carrier coordination, and freight audit. Typically generated by the TMS (e.g., SAP TM or equivalent).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD / TMS Internal Numbering Standard

description: Classification of the transport plan by freight direction and operational mode. Supports Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP) and MRP II-aligned logistics execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD / MRP II Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP)

description: Incremental version number of the transport plan, incremented each time the plan is revised or re-optimized. Supports plan change tracking and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Document Control / SAP TM Change Management

description: Number of distinct carriers (logistics service providers) included in the planned carrier mix for this transport plan. Supports carrier diversification analysis and risk management.
reference: SAP TM Carrier Selection / SAP Ariba Supplier Management

description: Estimated CO2 equivalent emissions in kilograms for all freight movements in this transport plan, calculated by the TMS optimization engine. Supports sustainability reporting and carbon footprint management.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management / EPA GHG Reporting / EU Green Deal

description: Planned date on which freight loads in this transport plan are scheduled to arrive at the destination. Used for customer delivery commitments, SLA compliance, and order fulfillment tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD / Customer SLA Agreement / ISO 9001

description: Planned date on which freight loads in this transport plan are scheduled to depart from the origin location. Used for production scheduling alignment and Available-to-Promise (ATP) calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD ATP / MRP II Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP)

description: Total planned freight cost in the plan currency for all loads and carriers in this transport plan. Used for freight budget management, cost-to-serve analysis, and OPEX reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO / IFRS / GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Total number of freight loads (shipment units, trucks, containers, or pallets) consolidated and planned within this transport plan. Key metric for capacity planning and freight budget management.
reference: SAP TM Load Planning / Infor WMS Shipment Management

description: Total number of individual shipment demands (orders, deliveries, or transfer orders) consolidated into this transport plan. Distinct from load count as multiple shipments may be consolidated into a single load.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD / SAP TM Shipment Consolidation

description: Total planned freight volume in cubic meters for all loads in this transport plan. Used for container/truck utilization optimization and freight cost estimation.
reference: SAP TM Freight Unit / ISO 668 Container Dimensions

description: Total planned gross weight in kilograms of all freight included in this transport plan. Used for carrier capacity validation, freight cost calculation, and load optimization.
reference: SAP TM Freight Unit / ANSI MH10.8 Shipping Standards

description: End date of the planning window covered by this transport plan. Defines the latest shipment delivery date included in the plan.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MRP II / Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP)

description: Start date of the planning window covered by this transport plan. Defines the earliest shipment pickup or departure date included in the plan, aligned with MRP II Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP) cycles.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MRP II / Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP)

description: Organizational unit (e.g., logistics planning center, distribution hub, or plant code) responsible for creating and managing this transport plan. Supports multi-entity global operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure / SAP TM

description: Contracted or planned service level for the transport plan, defining the speed and priority of freight movement. Linked to Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments with customers or internal stakeholders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD SLA / Customer SLA Agreement

description: Current lifecycle status of the transport plan. 'draft' indicates initial TMS-generated plan pending review; 'confirmed' indicates planner approval; 'in_execution' indicates active shipment movement; 'executed' indicates all loads delivered; 'cancelled' indicates plan voided; 'on_hold' indicates temporarily suspended.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM / ISO 9001 Process Control

description: External reference identifier assigned by the source Transportation Management System (TMS) to this transport plan. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the TMS and the lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: SAP TM / Infor WMS Integration Standard

description: Primary mode of transportation used in this plan (e.g., road, rail, air, ocean, intermodal). Drives carrier selection, lead time estimation, and CO2 emissions calculation.
reference: SAP TM Freight Order / IATA / IMO / ANSI X12 Transportation Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each production order type record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this reference data entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Type Configuration

description: SAP checking rule code that governs how component and capacity availability is verified when creating or releasing production orders of this type. Determines which stock categories and supply elements are considered in the ATP (Available to Promise) check.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Availability Check (T-Code OPJJ); ATP/CTP Standards

description: Indicates whether batch classification (assignment of batch characteristics and values) is mandatory for production orders of this type. Relevant for regulated industries and traceability requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Batch Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; REACH/RoHS Compliance

description: SAP BOM usage key (e.g., 1 = Production, 5 = Sales, 6 = Costing) that determines which BOM alternative is selected during production order creation for this order type. Ensures the correct BOM is used for component determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP BOM (T-Code CS01); Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management

description: Indicates whether production orders of this type generate capacity requirements on work centers. When true, orders of this type are included in capacity planning and leveling processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Planning (T-Code CM01); MRP II Capacity Planning Standards

description: SAP PP order type key (e.g., PP01, PP02, PI01, RW01, PR01) that uniquely identifies the order type in the source ERP system. Used during production order creation to default processing parameters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Type (T-Code OPJH)

description: SAP confirmation control key that defines the confirmation behavior for operations in production orders of this type, including whether milestone confirmations, partial confirmations, or automatic final confirmations are permitted.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Confirmation (T-Code CO11N); Siemens Opcenter MES Integration

description: SAP controlling area code associated with this order type, defining the organizational unit within which cost accounting and internal reporting are performed for production orders of this type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Controlling Area; IFRS; GAAP

description: SAP overhead costing sheet assigned to this order type, defining the overhead calculation rules (overhead rates, base values, credit objects) applied to production orders for overhead absorption and cost allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Overhead Costing; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: SAP costing variant code (e.g., PPP1, PPP2) assigned to this order type. Determines the valuation approach, cost component structure, and overhead calculation method used for production order cost estimation and actual cost collection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Product Costing; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Timestamp when this production order type record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); NIST SP 800-53 AU-3

description: Full business description of the production order type, explaining its intended use, applicable scenarios, and any special processing rules associated with this type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Type Configuration

description: Defines the component goods issue (GI) method for production orders of this type. Manual requires explicit GI posting; backflush automatically posts GI at confirmation; automatic triggers GI at order release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Backflushing (Movement Type 261); Lean Manufacturing Kanban Standards

description: Indicates whether an automatic goods receipt (GR) posting is triggered upon final confirmation of a production order of this type. When true, the finished goods inventory is updated automatically without a separate GR transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Goods Receipt (Movement Type 101); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.4

description: SAP QM inspection type code (e.g., 03 for goods receipt from production, 89 for in-process inspection) automatically assigned to inspection lots created for production orders of this type. Drives quality inspection planning and execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; AQL Standards

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the language in which the order type name and description are maintained. Supports multilingual configuration for multinational manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA Multilingual Configuration

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this production order type record in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); NIST SP 800-53 AU-3

description: Indicates whether production orders of this type are considered in Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and MRP II runs. When true, open orders of this type reduce net requirements during planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MRP (T-Code MD01); MRP II Standards; APICS CPIM

description: Short descriptive name of the production order type (e.g., 'Standard Production Order', 'Rework Order', 'Prototype Order', 'Co-Product Order'). Used in UI dropdowns and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Type Configuration

description: SAP internal order category code that classifies the fundamental nature of the order type. Category 10 = PP Production Order, 20 = Process Order (PI), 30 = Inspection Order, 40 = Maintenance Order. Drives system behavior at the lowest level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Category (AUFART-AUTYP)

description: Controls when a production order of this type transitions to Released status. Manual requires explicit user action; automatic_on_creation releases immediately upon order creation; automatic_on_save releases when the order is saved.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Release (T-Code CO02); MES Shop Floor Control Standards

description: Business usage classification of the order type indicating the manufacturing scenario it supports: standard production, rework of non-conforming material, prototype/development builds, co-product manufacturing, disassembly, subcontracting, or repair orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: SAP plant code to which this production order type is applicable. Enables plant-specific configuration of order types, supporting multinational operations where different plants may use different order type configurations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: SAP print control key that determines which shop floor documents (traveler, pick list, routing sheet, label) are automatically printed when a production order of this type is created or released.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Shop Floor Papers; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Manufacturing production strategy associated with this order type. MTO (Make to Order) produces against a specific sales order; MTS (Make to Stock) produces for inventory; ETO (Engineer to Order) involves custom engineering; ATO (Assemble to Order) assembles from standard components on demand.
reference: SAP MRP II Planning Strategy; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1

description: SAP order type code used as the reference template when creating orders of this type. Enables inheritance of default parameters from a base order type, supporting standardized configuration management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Type Configuration

description: SAP results analysis key assigned to the order type, controlling how work-in-progress (WIP) and reserves for unrealized costs are calculated during period-end closing. Relevant for ETO and long-running production orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Results Analysis; IFRS IAS 11; GAAP ASC 606

description: SAP routing usage key (e.g., 1 = Production, 3 = Universal) that determines which task list types (routings, reference operation sets) are valid for production orders of this type. Controls routing selection during order creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing (T-Code CA01); BoP (Bill of Process) Standards

description: Determines the scheduling direction applied when creating production orders of this type. Forward scheduling calculates finish date from start; backward scheduling calculates start date from required finish; current_date uses today as the reference point.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Scheduling (T-Code OPUZ); MRP II Scheduling Standards

description: SAP serial number profile assigned to this order type, controlling whether and when serial numbers must be assigned to finished goods produced under orders of this type. Supports unit-level traceability for high-value or regulated products.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Serial Number Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: SAP settlement profile code that defines the rules for settling production order variances and costs to cost objects (e.g., material stock, cost center, profitability segment). Controls period-end cost settlement behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Settlement Profile; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this production order type configuration was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage Standards; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Current lifecycle status of the production order type configuration record. Active types are available for production order creation; inactive types are temporarily disabled; obsolete types are retired and no longer used.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Configuration Lifecycle Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: SAP user status profile assigned to this order type, defining the allowed user-defined statuses and permissible status transitions for production orders of this type (e.g., HOLD, QUAL, APPR). Enables differentiated status management beyond standard system statuses.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Order Status Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Date from which this production order type configuration is effective and available for use in production order creation. Supports time-dependent configuration management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Time-Dependent Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Date until which this production order type configuration remains effective. After this date, the order type is no longer available for new production order creation. Supports planned configuration transitions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Time-Dependent Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: SAP variance key assigned to this order type, controlling which variance categories (input price, quantity, resource-usage, output price, mixed-price, remaining) are calculated during period-end variance analysis for production orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Variance Calculation; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Defines the method used to calculate Work In Progress (WIP) value for production orders of this type during period-end closing. Target cost uses standard cost; actual cost uses confirmed actual costs; none excludes WIP calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC WIP Calculation; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a manufacturing routing record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all routing-related joins and references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing (CA01/CA02); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1 Control of Production

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: Manufacturing routings implement engineering Bill of Process specifications. Production engineering translates design intent into executable shop floor operations with this link.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference quantity for which all standard times (setup, machine, labor) in the routing operations are defined. Typically 1 or a standard batch size. Used to scale standard values proportionally for different production order quantities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Base Quantity (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Unit of measure corresponding to the base quantity (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, M=Meter). Aligns with the SAP material master base unit and ISO unit standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Reference identifier linking this routing to the corresponding Bill of Process (BoP) in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. The BoP defines the engineering-side process structure, while the routing is the ERP execution counterpart.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Bill of Process (BoP); SAP S/4HANA PP-PLM Integration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether this routing is used as the basis for standard cost calculation in SAP CO Product Costing. When true, the routing's standard times and work center activity rates are used to compute the manufacturing cost component of the product standard cost.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Product Costing (CK11N); IFRS; GAAP COGS Calculation

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where manufacturing under this routing takes place. Used for regulatory compliance (CE Marking, RoHS, REACH), customs declarations, and multinational production reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Date and time when the routing record was originally created in the source system (SAP PP). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and routing lifecycle tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Audit Trail; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 (Control of Documented Information)

description: Detailed textual description of the routing, explaining the overall manufacturing process, intended product, and any special processing notes for production planners and shop floor engineers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that authorized the creation or last modification of this routing. Provides traceability between process changes and engineering change management.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; SAP S/4HANA PP Engineering Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: SAP PP routing group key that, together with the routing group counter, uniquely identifies a task list. Groups related routing variants for the same material or product family.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Task List (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: SAP PP group counter that differentiates multiple routing variants (standard, rework, alternate) within the same routing group. Combined with routing_group to form the unique SAP task list key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Task List (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether the routing contains alternative operation sequences that can be selected based on work center availability, capacity constraints, or material characteristics. Supports flexible shop floor scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Alternative Sequences; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether the routing includes quality inspection operations (linked to SAP QM inspection characteristics). Supports integration with the Quality Management System (QMS) and First Article Inspection (FAI) processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Plan Integration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 (Release of Products); APQP

description: Indicates whether the routing contains parallel operation sequences (concurrent process branches) in addition to the standard sequence. Parallel sequences enable simultaneous execution of independent operations, reducing total lead time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Sequences (Parallel/Alternative); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the language in which the routing description and operation texts are maintained (e.g., EN, DE, FR, ZH). Supports multilingual routing documentation for global manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA Language Management; GDPR Article 12 (Multilingual Communication)

description: Date and time of the most recent modification to the routing record in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental data loading in the lakehouse Silver Layer, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Audit Trail; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Minimum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Used in SAP PP lot-size-dependent routing selection to ensure the correct process sequence is applied based on production quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Lot Size; MRP II Lot Sizing Rules; APICS CPIM

description: Maximum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Together with lot_size_from, defines the quantity range for routing selection during production order creation and MRP II planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Lot Size; MRP II Lot Sizing Rules; APICS CPIM

description: SAP material master number of the finished or semi-finished product that this routing is designed to manufacture. Links the routing to the product engineering domain and Bill of Materials (BOM).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Short descriptive name or title of the routing, providing a human-readable label for the manufacturing process sequence (e.g., 'Standard Assembly Routing – Motor Drive Unit').
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Bill of Process (BoP)

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the routing as defined in SAP PP (Routing Group / Task List Number). Used by production planners and shop floor personnel to reference the manufacturing process sequence.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Total number of individual operations (process steps) defined in this routing. Provides a quick indicator of routing complexity and is used for routing comparison and process simplification analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Operations; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Bill of Process (BoP)

description: SAP organizational unit code identifying the manufacturing plant where this routing is valid and executed. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations and plant-level capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.4

description: External identifier of the corresponding routing or process plan object in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Enables bidirectional traceability between the ERP routing (SAP PP) and the PLM process definition for Engineering Change Management.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA PP-PLM Integration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Manufacturing production strategy associated with this routing: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Determines how the routing is triggered and consumed in production order processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planning Strategy; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Date and time when the routing status was changed to 'released', indicating formal approval for production use. Key lifecycle event for quality management traceability and Engineering Change Order (ECO) implementation tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Release; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; APQP Production Part Approval Process (PPAP)

description: Alphanumeric revision level or version identifier of the routing (e.g., A, B, 01, 02). Incremented with each approved Engineering Change Order (ECO). Supports routing version control and audit traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Revision; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Version Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

description: Indicates whether this routing is used for production order scheduling and capacity requirements planning (CRP) in MRP II. When true, the routing drives work center load calculations and finite/infinite scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP II Capacity Requirements Planning (CRP); APICS CPIM

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this routing record was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for PP module, TEAMCENTER for PLM BoP, OPCENTER for MES routing). Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; NIST Cybersecurity Framework (Data Provenance)

description: Current lifecycle status of the routing record: 'draft' during initial creation, 'released' when approved for production use, 'frozen' when locked against changes, 'obsolete' when superseded, 'under_review' during Engineering Change Order (ECO) processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

description: Takt time in seconds for this routing, representing the rate at which products must be completed to meet customer demand. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Key Lean Manufacturing metric for line balancing and flow design.
reference: Lean Manufacturing Takt Time; SAP S/4HANA PP Rate Routing; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: SAP PP task list type code indicating the category of the routing: N=Standard Routing, R=Reference Operation Set, M=Rate Routing, S=Rough-Cut Planning Profile, E=Equipment Task List, Q=Inspection Plan. Drives system behavior in MRP II and shop floor scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Task List Type (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Aggregate standard labor time in minutes across all routing operations for the base quantity. Used for workforce capacity planning, standard cost calculation (labor cost component), and productivity analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Standard Values; SAP CO Product Costing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Total manufacturing lead time in days for the complete routing, representing the elapsed calendar time from start of first operation to completion of last operation. Used in MRP II scheduling, ATP (Available to Promise), and delivery date calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Lead Time Scheduling; APICS CPIM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Aggregate standard machine processing time in minutes across all routing operations for the base quantity. Core input for MRP II capacity requirements planning (CRP), takt time analysis, and production scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Standard Values; MRP II CRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Aggregate standard setup time in minutes across all operations in the routing for the base quantity. Used for capacity planning, costing, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis. Supports SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Dies) improvement tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Standard Values; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SEMI E10 OEE Standard

description: Classification of the routing variant indicating its intended use: 'standard' for normal production, 'rework' for non-conforming material reprocessing, 'alternate' for secondary process paths, 'inspection' for quality inspection routings, 'reference' for reference operation sets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Task List Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 (Nonconforming Outputs)

description: SAP PP usage key defining the application context of the routing (e.g., 1=Production, 2=Engineering, 3=Universal, 5=Sales and Distribution). Controls which business processes can reference this routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Task List Usage (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Date from which this routing version is effective and valid for use in production order creation and MRP II planning. Supports date-effective routing management and Engineering Change Order (ECO) implementation scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Validity Period (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Date until which this routing version remains valid for production use. After this date, the routing is superseded by a newer version or marked obsolete. Used for routing version lifecycle management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Validity Period (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single operation step within a manufacturing routing. Serves as the primary key for the routing_operation entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Routing Operation (CA02/CA03)

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bop_operation. Business justification: Routing operations execute specific BOP operations defined by engineering. Work instructions and quality checkpoints reference this to ensure design compliance during manufacturing.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: routing_operation is a child step within a routing. The FK routing_id creates the proper parent-child relationship between individual operation steps and their parent routing, enabling routing structu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.task_list. Business justification: Routing operations for production equipment setup or changeover reference asset task lists that define the exact maintenance or inspection steps required. Process engineers link these to standardize o
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: routing_operation is assigned to a specific work_center for execution. The FK work_center_id normalizes the work_center_code reference and enables joins to work_center master data for routing analysis
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: SAP CO activity type code (e.g., MACH for machine hours, LABOR for labor hours) used to calculate the standard cost of this operation. Links operation time values to cost rates defined in the controlling module for product cost calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Module - Activity Type (KL01); Standard Cost Calculation; GAAP COGS

description: Reference quantity to which the standard time values apply. For example, if standard machine time is 5 minutes per 100 pieces, the base quantity is 100. Enables accurate time scaling for variable production lot sizes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Base Quantity; MRP II Standard Costing

description: Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) number that authorized the creation or modification of this routing operation. Provides full traceability of routing changes back to the originating engineering change request.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Engineering Change Management; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - ECN/ECO; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: SAP routing control key that governs operation behavior — determines whether the operation is scheduled, costed, printed on shop floor papers, requires confirmation, or triggers quality inspection. Examples: PP01 (internal processing), PP03 (external processing), QM01 (inspection).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Control Key (OPJ8); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: SAP Controlling (CO) cost center code associated with this operation's work center. Enables operation-level cost rollup, activity-based costing, and COGS allocation to the correct organizational unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Cost Center Accounting; IFRS; GAAP - Cost Allocation

description: Timestamp when this routing operation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with ISO 9001 record control requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the external processing price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Supports multi-currency operations in multinational manufacturing environments.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Standard price agreed with the subcontractor for performing this operation externally. Used for purchase order creation, standard cost calculation, and make-vs-buy analysis. Applicable only when is_subcontracting is true.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module - External Processing Price; SAP Ariba Sourcing; IFRS 2

description: Indicates whether this operation is a quality inspection step that triggers an inspection lot in the Quality Management (QM) module. When true, the operation creates an inspection point and requires quality results recording before the production order can be confirmed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module - Inspection Point; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 Release of Products

description: Indicates whether this operation is a milestone (gate) in the production routing. Milestone operations must be confirmed before subsequent operations can begin, enabling stage-gate control of the production flow and partial goods receipt.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Milestone Confirmation; Siemens Opcenter MES - Gate Control

description: Indicates whether this operation is performed by an external subcontractor rather than internally. When true, the operation triggers a subcontracting purchase order in SAP MM and requires goods issue/receipt processing for the outsourced step.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module - External Processing (Control Key PP02); SAP Ariba Procurement

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this routing operation record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse pipeline.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Maximum production quantity that can be processed in a single run of this operation. Prevents overloading of work centers and ensures quality control limits are not exceeded per batch.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Lot Size Constraints; MRP II Lot Sizing; GMP Batch Size Limits

description: Minimum production quantity that can be processed in a single run of this operation. Constrains MRP II lot sizing and production order splitting decisions to ensure operational efficiency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Lot Size Constraints; MRP II Lot Sizing

description: Standard time required to transport materials or WIP from this operation to the next work center. Contributes to total lead time calculation and is used in scheduling to account for inter-operation material movement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Move Time; MRP II Lead Time Components; Value Stream Mapping (VSM)

description: Standard number of workers (labor resources) required to perform this operation simultaneously. Used in workforce capacity planning, labor cost calculation, and shop floor staffing models.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Number of Labor Resources; Kronos Workforce Central - Labor Planning; ISO 22400-2

description: Standard number of machines required to perform this operation simultaneously. Used in machine capacity planning and OEE-based scheduling to determine resource requirements per production order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Number of Machines; OEE Capacity Planning; ISO 22400-2

description: Detailed work instructions, standard operating procedure (SOP) narrative, or technical guidance for performing the operation. Includes tooling requirements, safety precautions, quality checkpoints, and step-by-step execution notes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Operation Long Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1 SOP; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Sequential numeric identifier for the operation within the routing, typically in increments of 10 (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Determines the execution sequence on the shop floor and drives MES scheduling order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Routing Operation Sequence; Siemens Opcenter MES - Operation Sequencing

description: Brief descriptive label for the operation step (e.g., 'CNC Milling', 'Spot Welding', 'Final Assembly', 'Visual Inspection', 'Powder Coating'). Displayed on shop floor traveler documents and work order printouts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Routing Operation Short Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Classification of the manufacturing operation by process type. Enables filtering and grouping of operations for capacity analysis, cost rollup by process category, and shop floor scheduling by operation family.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Operation Type Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Unit of measure for the operation quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Defines how the operation output is counted and reported on the shop floor and in MES confirmations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Operation Unit; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Defines whether and how this operation can overlap with the preceding operation in the routing sequence. 'no_overlap' requires full completion before the next starts; 'time_overlap' allows a fixed time offset; 'quantity_overlap' allows partial quantity transfer. Enables lead time compression in scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Scheduling: Overlap; Lean Manufacturing Lead Time Reduction

description: Numeric value defining the extent of overlap with the preceding operation. Interpreted based on overlap_type: for time_overlap, the number of time units (per time_unit); for quantity_overlap, the minimum quantity to transfer before the next operation starts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Scheduling Overlap Value; Lead Time Optimization

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this routing operation is executed. Supports multi-plant routing management and plant-specific capacity planning in multinational operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Assignment; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.3 Infrastructure

description: Standard waiting time (queue) before this operation begins, representing the time a work order waits in the queue at the work center. Included in lead time calculation and critical path scheduling. Expressed in the unit defined by time_unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Queue Time; MRP II Lead Time Components; ISO 22400-2

description: Expected percentage of material or components that will be scrapped during this operation under normal production conditions. Used in MRP II to inflate planned order quantities, in standard cost calculation, and in yield analysis. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Operation Scrap (%); ISO 22400-2 Manufacturing KPIs; Six Sigma Yield Analysis

description: Standard labor (person) time required per base quantity unit for this operation. Used for labor cost rollup, workforce capacity planning, and standard cost calculation. Expressed in the unit defined by time_unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Standard Values (Labor Time); Kronos Workforce Central - Labor Analytics; ISO 22400-2

description: Standard machine processing time per base quantity unit for this operation. Drives machine capacity loading in MRP II, OEE calculations, and takt time analysis. Expressed in the unit defined by time_unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Standard Values (Machine Time); OEE Calculation; ISO 22400-2

description: Standard time required to set up the work center before production begins for this operation (e.g., tooling changeover, fixture installation, machine calibration). Used in capacity planning, SMED analysis, and production scheduling. Expressed in the unit defined by time_unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Standard Values (Setup Time); SMED Methodology; ISO 22400-2 KPI for Manufacturing

description: Standard time required to dismantle tooling, clean the work center, and restore it to a ready state after the operation completes. Included in total operation duration for scheduling and capacity loading.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Standard Values (Teardown Time); ISO 22400-2

description: Current lifecycle status of the routing operation record. 'active' operations are available for production order assignment; 'inactive' are temporarily disabled; 'under_review' are pending engineering change approval; 'obsolete' are retired and no longer used.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Routing Status; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Unit of measure applied to all standard time values (setup, machine, labor, teardown) for this operation. Ensures consistent interpretation of time fields across scheduling, costing, and capacity planning systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Time Unit; ISO 80000-3 Quantities and Units

description: Date from which this routing operation version becomes effective and can be used in production order scheduling. Supports date-effective routing management and engineering change control (ECN/ECO) implementation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Validity Period; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - ECN Effectivity; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Date after which this routing operation version expires and is no longer valid for new production orders. Used in conjunction with valid_from_date to manage engineering change effectivity and routing version control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Validity Period; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - ECN Effectivity; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Standard time the material must wait after this operation completes before the next operation can begin (e.g., curing time, cooling time, drying time). Included in lead time scheduling and critical path analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Wait Time; MRP II Lead Time Components; Process Engineering Standards

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the work center (machine, production cell, or labor group) where this operation is performed. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and cost center assignment in MRP II resource planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Work Center (CR01/CR02); MRP II Resource Planning

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each production shift definition record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and OEE calculations.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Master

description: Total scheduled break time in minutes for the shift (sum of all meal breaks, rest breaks, and paid/unpaid breaks). Subtracted from gross duration to derive net available production time.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Break Schedule; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 (Rest Period Standards)

description: Structured text description of the break schedule within the shift, specifying break start times and durations (e.g., '10:00-10:15 rest; 12:00-12:30 meal; 15:00-15:15 rest'). Stored as a formatted string for display in MES and scheduling tools.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Break Schedule Configuration

description: Short alphanumeric business code uniquely identifying the shift definition within a plant (e.g., SH-DAY, SH-NIGHT, SH-SWING). Used as the natural key in MES and ERP integrations.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Master; SAP S/4HANA PP - Shift Definition

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant location where this shift is defined (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Enables country-specific labor law compliance, regulatory reporting, and multi-regional OEE benchmarking.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR; Local Labor Regulations

description: Timestamp when this shift definition record was first created in the source system or Silver Layer, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: ISO 8601; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information Control; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether the shift spans across midnight (i.e., end_time is earlier than start_time on the clock). Critical for correct date assignment of production orders and OEE period calculations for night shifts.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Schedule Logic

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to financial attributes of this shift record (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency reporting in multinational manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Detailed narrative description of the shift, including its purpose, applicable production lines, or special operational notes (e.g., reduced-capacity weekend shift, holiday skeleton crew).
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Master

description: Total gross duration of the shift in decimal hours (e.g., 8.00, 10.00, 12.00). Represents the full clock time from start to end before deducting breaks. Used as the denominator in OEE availability calculations.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - OEE Calculation; ISO 22400-2 KPI for Manufacturing

description: The calendar date from which this shift definition becomes valid and applicable for production scheduling. Supports time-variant shift configurations for seasonal or contractual changes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Validity Period; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: The calendar date on which this shift definition expires and is no longer valid for production scheduling. A null value indicates the shift is open-ended with no planned expiry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Validity Period; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Scheduled end time of the shift in HH:mm:ss 24-hour format (e.g., 14:00:00 for Day Shift). Combined with start_time to determine the gross shift window for capacity and OEE calculations.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Schedule; SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift

description: Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Fridays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Code referencing the collective bargaining agreement or labor contract governing this shift's working conditions, break entitlements, and overtime rules. Ensures compliance with labor regulations and union agreements.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ILO Working Time Standards; Local Collective Bargaining Agreements

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this shift definition record in the source system or Silver Layer. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information Control; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Mondays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Human-readable name of the production shift (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Swing Shift, Morning Shift, Afternoon Shift). Used in scheduling boards, OEE dashboards, and production reports.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.3

description: Planned available production time in decimal hours after deducting all scheduled breaks and planned downtime from the gross shift duration. This is the planned operating time used as the basis for OEE availability rate calculations per ISO 22400-2.
reference: ISO 22400-2:2014 KPI for Manufacturing Operations Management; Siemens Opcenter MES OEE

description: Count of distinct scheduled break periods within the shift (e.g., 1 meal break + 2 rest breaks = 3). Used in MES break scheduling and shop floor Andon system configuration.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Break Schedule; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage for this shift, representing the planned benchmark for availability, performance, and quality combined. Used to evaluate actual shift OEE against the planned target in production analytics.
reference: ISO 22400-2:2014 KPI for Manufacturing Operations Management; SEMI E10 OEE Standard

description: Indicates whether production hours worked in this shift beyond the standard duration qualify for overtime compensation under the applicable labor agreement. Used in labor cost calculations and payroll integration.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; FLSA (Fair Labor Standards Act); Local Labor Law

description: Code referencing the shift rotation pattern or schedule group to which this shift belongs (e.g., 3-SHIFT-ROTATING, 2-SHIFT-FIXED, CONTINENTAL). Links individual shift definitions to their broader scheduling pattern for MRP II capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Shift Pattern; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility to which this shift definition belongs. Enables multi-plant OEE segmentation and shift-based capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Plant Master (T001W); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1

description: Percentage premium applied to the base labor rate for hours worked in this shift (e.g., 25% night shift premium, 50% weekend premium). Used in production cost calculations and COGS reporting.
reference: IFRS; GAAP; Local Labor Law; Collective Bargaining Agreement

description: Manufacturing production strategy applicable to this shift (Make to Order, Make to Stock, Engineer to Order, Assemble to Order, or mixed). Determines how production orders are assigned and prioritized within the shift window.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Planning Strategy; MRP II Framework

description: Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Saturdays. Relevant for extended production weeks, overtime scheduling, and weekend capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Ordinal position of the shift within a 24-hour production day cycle (e.g., 1 = first shift, 2 = second shift, 3 = third shift). Used to order shifts in scheduling boards and rotation pattern definitions.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Sequence; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this shift definition was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, OPCENTER_MES). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer - Data Lineage; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Scheduled start time of the shift in HH:mm:ss 24-hour format (e.g., 06:00:00 for Day Shift). Used to calculate available production time and assign production orders to the correct shift window.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Schedule; SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift

description: Current lifecycle status of the shift definition. Only active shifts are available for production order assignment and OEE calculations. Archived shifts are retained for historical reporting.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Master

description: Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Sundays. Relevant for 24/7 continuous production environments and holiday/weekend capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Planned takt time in seconds for this shift, calculated as net available production time divided by customer demand rate. Represents the required production rhythm to meet demand within the shift window. Used in Lean Manufacturing and shop floor pacing.
reference: Lean Manufacturing - Takt Time; ISO 22400-2:2014; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Thursdays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: IANA timezone identifier for the plant location where this shift operates (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Shanghai). Critical for correct timestamp interpretation in global MES integrations and cross-plant OEE reporting.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; ISO 8601

description: Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Tuesdays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Classification of the shift by its operational time window or purpose (e.g., day, night, swing, weekend, holiday, overtime). Used to segment OEE metrics and apply differential labor cost rates.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Shift Type Classification; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Indicates whether this shift definition is scheduled to operate on Wednesdays. Used in weekly production scheduling and MRP II capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the production version record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module – Production Version Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: Production versions link to Bill of Process defining manufacturing operations sequence. Production engineering uses this to establish work center routings and cycle times.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: A production version (BOM/routing revision) is created or superseded by an Engineering Change Notice. Production control teams use this link to know which ECN triggered a version change and when to cu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: Production versions define valid BOM alternatives for manufacturing. Production planning uses this to select cost-optimal or capacity-optimal manufacturing configurations.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: A production version (SAP PP Production Version) defines the valid combination of a BOM and a routing for manufacturing a material. version currently references routing via denormalized string attribu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The APQP approval status for this production version, indicating whether the manufacturing process has been formally validated through the Advanced Product Quality Planning process. Required for automotive and regulated manufacturing.
reference: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning); AIAG APQP Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: The BOM alternative number (STLAL) assigned to this production version. Multiple BOM alternatives can exist for the same material, each representing a different component configuration or manufacturing method.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL.STLAL); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Defines how the BOM is exploded during MRP II planning and production order creation for this version: single-level (one level only), multi-level (full BOM tree), or phantom (virtual assembly, components passed through).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Explosion; MRP II; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: SAP BOM usage key (STLAN) indicating the application area for which the BOM is defined (e.g., 1=Production, 5=Sales, 6=Costing). Determines which BOM is selected during production order creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Usage (CS_BOM_USAGE); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The Engineering Change Number (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) reference that authorized the creation or last modification of this production version. Provides traceability to the change management process in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Management (ECN/ECO); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: The alphanumeric production version identifier (VERID) as defined in SAP PP. Uniquely identifies a specific BOM/routing combination for a material-plant combination. Enables multiple manufacturing methods for the same product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL.VERID); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The reference lot size used for product cost calculation (standard cost estimate) for this production version. Determines the per-unit cost allocation of fixed setup and overhead costs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Product Costing (CKMLMV); IFRS; GAAP – Inventory Costing

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where this production version is applicable. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance, reporting, and regional manufacturing strategy analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA – Country (T005)

description: The SAP user ID of the person who created this production version record. Supports accountability, audit trail, and change management traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA – User Audit Trail; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: The date and time when this production version record was first created in the source SAP system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – MKAL Creation Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Document Control)

description: Indicates whether this is the default (preferred) production version for the material-plant combination. When multiple versions are valid, MRP II and production planners use the default version unless overridden.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version Selection; MRP II Planning

description: Indicates whether this production version has been flagged for deletion in the source SAP system (MKAL.LOEKZ). A flagged version is excluded from MRP II planning and production order creation but retained for historical traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version Deletion Flag (MKAL.LOEKZ); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Short descriptive text explaining the purpose or distinguishing characteristics of this production version (e.g., 'Standard Line A Process', 'High-Volume Variant', 'Pilot Run Method').
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL.TEXT1)

description: The total in-house production lead time in working days for this production version. Used by MRP II to calculate planned order dates and available-to-promise (ATP) commitments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – In-House Production Time (MARC.DZEIT); MRP II; ATP/CTP

description: The SAP storage location (LGORT) from which components are issued to the production order for this version. Drives goods movement and WIP inventory tracking on the shop floor.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/WM – Storage Location (MKAL.LGORT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The SAP user ID of the person who last modified this production version record. Supports accountability, audit trail, and change management traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA – User Audit Trail; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: The date and time when this production version record was last modified in the source SAP system. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data loading in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – MKAL Change Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Document Control)

description: The maximum production lot size (MABST) for which this production version is valid. MRP II will not propose this version for planned orders exceeding this quantity. Enables capacity-constrained version selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL.MABST); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; MRP II Planning

description: The minimum production lot size (MTVFP/MABST) for which this production version is valid. MRP II will not propose this version for planned orders below this quantity. Supports lot-size-dependent manufacturing method selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL.MABST); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; MRP II Planning

description: The unit of measure (MEINS) applicable to the minimum and maximum lot size values for this production version (e.g., EA, KG, L, M). Aligns with the base unit of measure of the material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Unit of Measure (T006); ISO 80000-1

description: Short description of the material (MAKTX) for which this production version is defined. Provides human-readable context for reporting and analytics without requiring a join to the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Description (MAKT.MAKTX)

description: The SAP material number (MATNR) identifying the finished or semi-finished product for which this production version is defined. Aligns with the material master in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP – Material Master (MATNR); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Indicates whether this production version is considered by MRP II during planning runs. When false, the version is excluded from automatic planned order generation even if it is within its validity period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – MRP II Planning; MRP Relevance (MKAL); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: SAP plant code (WERKS) identifying the manufacturing facility where this production version is valid. A production version is always plant-specific.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The PPAP submission level required or achieved for this production version. Defines the extent of documentation and sample submission required to validate the manufacturing process for customer approval.
reference: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process); AIAG PPAP Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Numeric priority ranking used to determine the preferred production version when multiple versions are valid for the same lot size range and validity period. Lower number indicates higher priority.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version Selection; MRP II Planning

description: The specific production line or work center group (ARBPL) designated for this production version. Used in repetitive manufacturing and rate-based scheduling to assign the version to a physical line.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL.ARBPL); Siemens Opcenter MES – Line Assignment

description: The manufacturing strategy applicable to this production version: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Drives MRP II planning logic and order type selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Planning Strategy; MRP II; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The SAP storage location (LGORT) to which the finished goods produced under this version are received upon goods receipt. Supports inventory management and warehouse operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/WM – Storage Location; Infor WMS – Receiving; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Indicates whether this production version is enabled for repetitive manufacturing (SAP MKAL.SERKZ). When true, rate-based planning and backflushing are applicable. Relevant for high-volume, continuous production scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Repetitive Manufacturing (MKAL.SERKZ); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The type of task list (PLNTY) associated with this production version. 'routing' is standard discrete manufacturing; 'rate_routing' is used for repetitive manufacturing; 'master_recipe' is used for process manufacturing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Task List Type (PLNTY); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The scheduling direction used when calculating production dates for orders using this version: 'forward' (from start date), 'backward' (from required finish date), or 'current_date'. Drives lead time calculation in MRP II.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Scheduling (MKAL); MRP II Planning; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Current operational status of the production version. 'active' means it is available for MRP II planning and shop floor execution. 'inactive' means it is defined but not currently used. 'locked' means it is frozen for change control. 'obsolete' means it has been superseded.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The date from which this production version is valid and can be used for MRP II planning and production order creation (SAP MKAL.ADATU). Defines the start of the validity period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL.ADATU); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The date until which this production version is valid (SAP MKAL.BDATU). After this date, the version is no longer proposed by MRP II for new production orders. Supports time-phased manufacturing method transitions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Version (MKAL.BDATU); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the work center record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center (CR01/CR02); Siemens Opcenter MES Resource Master

description: Foreign key linking to asset.class. Business justification: Work centers are classified by asset class (e.g., CNC, press, conveyor) in manufacturing ERP systems. Production planners use this daily to filter capacity by equipment class and apply class-level mai
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Work centers are physically situated at specific plant or facility locations. This link is used by logistics planners for material routing, transport planning to the correct shop floor area, and capac
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: SAP Controlling activity type code (e.g., MACH, LABOR) used to allocate costs from this work center to production orders. Enables granular cost-of-goods-sold (COGS) analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Activity Type (LSTAR); COGS; GAAP; IFRS IAS 2

description: Total available production hours per day for this work center, calculated from active shifts and break schedules. Used as the baseline for capacity requirements planning (CRP) in MRP II.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity (KAPAZ); MRP II CRP; APICS CPIM

description: Defines the primary capacity resource type tracked for this work center (machine hours, labor hours, energy units, tooling). Used in MRP II capacity requirements planning (CRP).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Category (KAPID); MRP II; APICS CPIM

description: SAP work center category code (e.g., 0001 for machine, 0002 for labor) that controls which fields and formulas are active for capacity and costing. Drives MRP II planning behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Category (VERWE); MRP II Resource Planning

description: Alphanumeric business key identifying the work center within the plant, as defined in SAP PP (field ARBPL). Used in routing operations, production orders, and MRP II capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center ARBPL; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.3 Infrastructure

description: SAP PP control key assigned to operations at this work center, determining whether operations are confirmed, printed, scheduled externally, or subject to milestone confirmation. Governs MES integration behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Operation Control Key (STEUS); Siemens Opcenter MES Integration

description: Identifier of the formula used to calculate production costs (machine costs, labor costs, overhead) for operations performed at this work center. Links to SAP CO activity types and cost rates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Product Costing; GAAP; IFRS IAS 2

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this work center is physically located. Supports multinational regulatory compliance, labor law adherence, and regional capacity reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR; OSHA; CE Marking; RoHS/REACH

description: Timestamp when the work center master data record was first created in the source system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and master data governance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency used in cost rate calculations and activity pricing for this work center. Supports multi-currency costing in multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA CO Multi-Currency

description: Standard time in seconds to complete one unit of production at this work center under normal operating conditions. Compared against takt time to identify bottlenecks and balance production lines.
reference: ISO 22400-2 Manufacturing KPIs; Lean Manufacturing Cycle Time; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Ratio of standard time to actual time expressed as a percentage, representing how efficiently the work center converts available time into productive output. Used in capacity planning and OEE calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Efficiency (EFFIZIENZ); OEE; ISO 22400-2 KPIs for Manufacturing

description: Physical floor space occupied by the work center in square meters. Used in facility layout planning, 5S audits, and capacity expansion assessments.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.3 Infrastructure; Lean Manufacturing 5S

description: Hierarchical grouping path of the work center within the plant structure (e.g., 'Plant A > Production Area 2 > Assembly Line 1'). Used for aggregated capacity reporting and organizational drill-down in analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Hierarchy; MRP II Capacity Planning

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the work center master data record. Used for incremental data loading, change tracking, and master data governance in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Standard time in hours required to physically transport WIP from this work center to the next operation in the routing. Contributes to total manufacturing lead time in MRP II scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Move Time (MOVEZEIT); MRP II Lead Time

description: Descriptive name of the work center (e.g., 'CNC Machining Cell 3', 'Final Assembly Line A'). Used in shop floor scheduling, routing, and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center KTEXT; Siemens Opcenter MES Resource Description

description: Number of workers or operators assigned to this work center per shift. Used in labor capacity planning, workforce scheduling via Kronos, and standard cost calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Labor Capacity; Kronos Workforce Central; MRP II

description: Count of individual machines or equipment units grouped within this work center. Used to calculate aggregate capacity and determine parallel processing capability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Capacity (ANZMA); Siemens Opcenter MES Resource Pool

description: Number of production shifts per day scheduled at this work center. Directly determines available capacity hours and is used in shift-based scheduling and OEE calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Capacity (SCHICHT); Siemens Opcenter MES Shift Model

description: Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage set for this work center, representing the desired level of availability, performance, and quality combined. Used as the KPI benchmark in TPM and continuous improvement programs.
reference: OEE Framework (SEMI E10); ISO 22400-2; TPM; Siemens MindSphere IIoT Analytics

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this work center is physically located. Supports multi-plant and multinational capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Plant (WERKS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.3

description: Manufacturing execution strategy applicable to this work center: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Drives order type and scheduling behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planning Strategy; APICS CPIM; MRP II

description: Standard waiting time in hours that a production order or WIP lot is expected to wait in queue before processing begins at this work center. Used in lead time scheduling and order float calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Queue Time (WARTEZEIT); MRP II Lead Time Scheduling

description: Name or employee identifier of the shop floor supervisor or foreman accountable for operations at this work center. Used for escalation routing, shift handover, and accountability reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Responsible Person; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 Roles

description: Defines the permitted usage of this work center in routing operations: production routing, inspection routing, rework routing, maintenance routing, or all types. Controls which routing types can reference this work center.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Usage (VERWE); Bill of Process (BoP)

description: Standard time in minutes required to prepare (set up) the work center before production begins for a new operation or product. Used in routing time calculations and SMED improvement programs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Setup Time (RUSTE); SMED; ISO 22400-2

description: Standard duration in hours of a single production shift at this work center. Combined with number_of_shifts to derive total daily available capacity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Capacity; Siemens Opcenter MES Shift Definition

description: SAP PP standard value key defining which standard values (setup, machine, labor, teardown) are relevant for operations at this work center. Drives costing formula selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Standard Value Key (VGWTS); Activity-Based Costing

description: Current operational status of the work center. Active work centers are available for scheduling; under_maintenance are temporarily unavailable; decommissioned are retired from production.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.3; Siemens Opcenter MES Resource Status
