description: The rate at which products must be completed at this work center to meet customer demand, expressed in seconds per unit. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Core metric in Lean Manufacturing and line balancing.
reference: Lean Manufacturing Takt Time; ISO 22400-2; VSM

description: Standard time in minutes required to clean up and restore the work center after production of an operation is complete. Included in total operation lead time calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Teardown Time (ABRUE); ISO 22400-2

description: Classification of the work center by its primary operational mode: machine (CNC, robot), labor (manual assembly), process (chemical/batch), assembly (multi-component joining), inspection (QA station), or storage (buffer/WIP area).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Category (VERWE); Siemens Opcenter MES Resource Type

description: Percentage of available capacity time that is actually scheduled or consumed by production orders. A key input to capacity leveling and bottleneck analysis in MRP II.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Utilization; ISO 22400-2; OEE Framework

description: Date from which this work center record is valid and available for production scheduling and routing assignments. Supports time-dependent master data management in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Validity Period; ISO 9001:2015 Document Control

description: Date until which this work center record is valid. After this date, the work center is no longer available for new routing or scheduling assignments. Supports planned decommissioning and capacity transitions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Validity Period; ISO 9001:2015 Document Control

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the batch master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all batch-level traceability and genealogy operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Batch Master (MCHA/MCH1/MCHB)

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: In process manufacturing, production batches of raw materials are created at goods receipt. Traceability regulations require linking the production batch directly to the goods receipt for full upstrea
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_lifecycle. Business justification: Batches track which product lifecycle phase was active during manufacturing. This supports obsolescence management, warranty tracking, and determining which production standards/specifications were ap
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: A production batch directly corresponds to an inventory lot/batch record created upon goods receipt or production output. Pharmaceutical and discrete manufacturing require this link for end-to-end bat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Each production batch must be traceable to the engineering material specification it was produced against. Used in batch records, certificates of conformance, and regulatory traceability audits in ind
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_confirmation. Business justification: Production batches are created and traceable to specific confirmed customer orders, especially in make-to-order manufacturing. Quality and traceability teams link batches to order confirmations for en
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) document associated with this batch. The CoA documents the actual test results and quality characteristics confirming the batch meets specification. Linked to SAP DMS or external document management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Certificate of Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; GMP 21 CFR Part 211.192

description: SAP user ID (AENAM) of the person who last modified the batch master record. Supports change management audit trails and accountability in quality-regulated environments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master (MCHA-AENAM); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GMP 21 CFR Part 211.68

description: SAP classification class number (KLASSE) assigned to this batch for batch classification. Defines the set of characteristics (e.g., purity, viscosity, tensile strength) used to describe and search for batches.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Classification System (KLAH); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Serialized JSON string containing all batch classification characteristic values from SAP Classification System (AUSP) that do not have dedicated columns (e.g., color, hardness, moisture content, particle size). Enables flexible storage of material-specific quality parameters without schema changes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Classification System (AUSP/CABN); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the batch material was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, and preferential origin determination.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO Rules of Origin; EU Customs Code; US CBP Country of Origin Rules

description: SAP user ID (ERNAM) of the person who created the batch master record. Required for audit trail and accountability in regulated manufacturing environments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master (MCHA-ERNAM); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GMP 21 CFR Part 211.68

description: Timestamp when the batch master record was first created in the source system (SAP MCHA-ERSDA + MCHA-CRTM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and batch lifecycle tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master (MCHA-ERSDA); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Date on which the batch was received into inventory via goods receipt posting (SAP MSEG-BUDAT). For externally procured batches, this is the inbound logistics date used for aging, FIFO, and supplier lead time analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (MKPF-BUDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: SAP material document number (MBLNR) for the goods receipt posting that created or received this batch. Provides the inbound logistics and inventory posting reference for traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (MKPF/MSEG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Indicates whether the batch contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, labeling, and transport documentation (e.g., SDS, UN number). Drives HSE compliance workflows and warehouse segregation rules.
reference: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS/UN GHS; EPA RCRA; ADR/IATA/IMDG

description: SAP QM inspection lot number (PRUEFLOS) linked to this batch for quality inspection. Connects the batch to the inspection results, usage decision, and defect records in the Quality Management module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Lot (QALS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; AQL Sampling Standards

description: Indicates whether this batch has been verified as compliant with restricted substance regulations including EU RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) and REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals). Required for CE marking and export compliance.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; CE Marking Directive

description: Timestamp of the most recent change to the batch master record in the source system (SAP MCHA-LAEDA + MCHA-LAEZT). Supports change detection, delta loading, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master (MCHA-LAEDA); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5

description: The date on which the batch was manufactured or produced (SAP MCHA-HSDAT). Used for shelf life calculation, FIFO/FEFO inventory management, and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master (MCHA-HSDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; GMP 21 CFR Part 211

description: Short text description of the material associated with this batch, sourced from the SAP material master (MAKT-MAKTX). Supports human-readable identification in reports and quality documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master (MAKT)

description: SAP material number (MATNR) of the material to which this batch belongs. Identifies the specific product or raw material managed in lot/batch control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master (MARA-MATNR); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised against this batch if quality defects or deviations were identified. Links the batch to the CAPA process and corrective action tracking in the QMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Defect Recording (QMEL); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 CAPA; IATF 16949

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic quality inspection of this batch. Supports recurring inspection planning for long-shelf-life or regulated materials and triggers QM inspection lot creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Recurring Inspection; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; GMP 21 CFR Part 211

description: Indicates whether the batch was created through internal manufacturing (production order), external procurement (purchase order/GRN), subcontracting, or customer returns. Drives traceability and genealogy logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Batch Origin; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: SAP plant code (WERKS) where the batch was produced or received. Determines the organizational unit responsible for batch management, quality inspection, and inventory valuation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure (T001W); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: SAP production order number (AUFNR) that created this batch. Links the batch to the manufacturing execution context including routing, work center, BOM, and planned/actual quantities. Applicable for internally produced batches.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order (AUFK); Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Purity level of the batch expressed as a percentage, stored as a batch classification characteristic value. Critical quality parameter for chemical, pharmaceutical, and specialty material batches used in acceptance/rejection decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; AQL Standards

description: Total quantity of material in this batch at the time of creation or last stock posting (SAP MCHB-CLABS + CEINM + CSPEM + CEINM). Represents the lot size for production planning, quality sampling, and inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Stock (MCHB); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

business_glossary_term: REACH Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) Flag
description: Indicates whether this batch contains any Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) as defined under EU REACH regulation. Triggers mandatory disclosure obligations to customers and downstream users when SVHC concentration exceeds 0.1% w/w.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 59; ECHA SVHC Candidate List

description: Number of days remaining before the batch expires, calculated as the difference between expiry date and the current date. Used for FEFO prioritization, near-expiry alerts, and inventory disposition decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Shelf Life Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Date by which the batch must be retested to confirm it still meets quality specifications (SAP MCHA-RRDAT). Used for periodic quality re-evaluation of long shelf-life materials and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master (MCHA-RRDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; GMP ICH Q1A Stability

description: Total shelf life of the batch expressed in days, as defined in the material master shelf life data (SAP MRP4 view - MHDRZ). Used to calculate expiry date and manage FEFO-based inventory consumption.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master Shelf Life (MARA-MHDRZ); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Current stock/quality status of the batch as managed in SAP MM/QM (MCHB-EINME / Batch Classification). Determines whether the batch is available for use: unrestricted (available), quality_inspection (under QM hold), blocked (not available), restricted (conditionally available), or obsolete (expired/decommissioned).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Control of Nonconforming Outputs

description: SAP storage location code (LGORT) where the batch is physically stored within the plant. Used for inventory management, warehouse operations, and stock transfer processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM/MM Storage Location (T001L); Infor WMS

description: The batch or lot number assigned by the external supplier for procured materials (SAP LICHA). Required for supplier traceability, incoming quality inspection, and regulatory compliance (e.g., REACH, RoHS).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master (MCHA-LICHA); EU REACH Regulation; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: SAP vendor/supplier number (LIFNR) for externally procured batches. Enables supplier quality tracking, incoming inspection correlation, and supplier performance analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master (LFA1); SAP Ariba Supplier Management

description: Tensile strength of the batch material in megapascals (MPa), stored as a batch classification characteristic. Critical mechanical property for metal, composite, and structural material batches used in quality release decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Classification; ISO 6892-1 Metallic Materials Tensile Testing; ASTM E8

description: Base unit of measure for the batch quantity (SAP MEINH/MEINS), such as KG, L, EA, M, M2, M3. Aligns with SAP material master base UoM for consistent inventory and quality reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure (T006); ANSI X12; ISO 80000

description: SAP QM usage decision code (ENTSCHEIDUNG) for the inspection lot associated with this batch. Determines the quality disposition: accept (unrestricted use), reject (block/scrap), or conditional release. Drives automatic batch status change.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Usage Decision (QAVE); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO 2859 AQL

description: Viscosity measurement of the batch in millipascal-seconds (mPa·s), stored as a batch classification characteristic. Key quality parameter for fluid, adhesive, coating, and polymer material batches.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Classification; ISO 2555 Plastics Viscosity; ASTM D445

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each batch genealogy relationship record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the batch_genealogy data product.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2 - Identification and Traceability

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: batch_genealogy tracks the parent batch consumed during manufacturing. The FK parent_batch_id normalizes the parent_batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for upstream tr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line_item. Business justification: Batch genealogy must trace input materials back to the specific PO line item that sourced them. Regulatory compliance and recall management in industrial manufacturing require this upstream traceabili
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: batch_genealogy records the operation step at which batch transformation occurred. The FK routing_operation_id enables joins to routing_operation master data for process-level genealogy traceability a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: batch_genealogy.work_center_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the work_center master. Batch genealogy records capture the work center at which the parent-child batch transformation occurred
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Shelf life expiration date of the child (produced) batch as recorded in SAP batch master. Used for finished product shelf life management, customer delivery compliance, and regulatory traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master; EU GMP Annex 11; FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: Identifier of the child batch — the output material, assembly, or finished product batch that was produced or resulted from the manufacturing operation. In SAP PP, this corresponds to the 'To Batch' in the batch where-used list.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; SAP PP Batch Where-Used (MB56)

description: Quality/inventory status of the child (produced) batch at the time of production confirmation, as recorded in SAP QM. Indicates whether the produced batch passed quality inspection or requires further disposition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Human-readable description of the child batch material, denormalized for traceability reports, recall notifications, and regulatory submissions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: SAP material number (or equivalent ERP identifier) of the child batch material. Enables cross-referencing to the material master for finished product specifications and compliance data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Actual quantity of the parent batch that was consumed during the manufacturing operation. Used for material balance calculations, yield analysis, and traceability completeness verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Goods Movement (MIGO); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Unit of measure for the consumed quantity of the parent batch (e.g., KG, L, EA). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure for the parent material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the batch transformation occurred. Required for customs declarations, CE marking, RoHS/REACH compliance, and country-of-origin traceability in global supply chains.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006

description: Date and time when the batch genealogy record was first created in the source system of record (SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record retention compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5(2); FDA 21 CFR Part 820.180

description: Business-facing unique identifier for the batch genealogy relationship record, used in quality investigations, recall management, and regulatory submissions. Typically sourced from SAP PP Batch Where-Used or Siemens Opcenter MES.

SAP material document number (from MIGO/MB1A) recording the goods issue of the parent batch or goods receipt of the child batch. Provides the financial and inventory audit trail for the batch relationship.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement (MIGO); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: SAP QM inspection lot number associated with the child batch produced in this genealogy record. Links the batch genealogy to quality inspection results, usage decisions, and non-conformance reports (NCR).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; IATF 16949:2016

description: Date and time of the most recent update to the batch genealogy record in the source system. Supports change detection, incremental data loading in the Silver layer, and audit trail completeness.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5(2); SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Sequential number identifying the order of parent batches when multiple parent batches are merged into a single child batch (relationship_type = 'merge'). Enables reconstruction of the full merge genealogy and proportional quantity attribution.
reference: SAP PP Batch Merge; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised against the parent or child batch in this genealogy record. Enables direct linkage between batch traceability and quality defect management for CAPA investigations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.7; SAP QM

description: Actual end date and time of the manufacturing operation during which the batch relationship was established. Together with operation_start_timestamp, defines the production time window for traceability and cycle time analysis.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Production Confirmation

description: Routing operation number (e.g., 0010, 0020) within the production order at which the batch relationship was established — i.e., where the parent batch was consumed and the child batch was produced or transformed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Actual start date and time of the manufacturing operation during which the batch relationship was established. Used for time-based traceability, shift analysis, and process timeline reconstruction during quality investigations.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Production Confirmation

description: Shelf life expiration date of the parent batch as recorded in SAP batch master. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) compliance, regulatory submissions, and determining if expired materials were used in production.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master; EU GMP Annex 11; FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: Quality/inventory status of the parent batch at the time of consumption, as recorded in SAP QM batch status management. Indicates whether the consumed batch was in unrestricted use, under quality inspection, or blocked — critical for quality investigations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Human-readable description of the parent batch material, denormalized for reporting and traceability documents without requiring a join to the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: SAP material number (or equivalent ERP identifier) of the parent batch material. Enables cross-referencing to the material master for specification, classification, and compliance data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the batch genealogy relationship was established. Supports multi-plant traceability and regional regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Actual quantity of the child batch that was produced or resulted from the manufacturing operation. Used for yield calculations, material balance, and production confirmation reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Confirmation (CO11N); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Unit of measure for the produced quantity of the child batch (e.g., KG, EA, L). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure for the child material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Reference to the SAP PP production order or MES work order under which the parent-child batch relationship was created. Links genealogy records to the manufacturing execution context.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Indicates whether this batch genealogy record has been flagged as part of an active product recall or field action. When true, the record is included in recall scope reports and regulatory notifications. Critical for recall management and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; FDA 21 CFR Part 7; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 10.2

description: Reference number of the product recall or field action campaign to which this batch genealogy record belongs. Populated when recall_flag is true. Enables aggregation of all affected batches under a single recall event.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949:2016

description: Lifecycle status of the batch genealogy record. 'active' indicates a valid, confirmed relationship; 'reversed' indicates the goods movement was reversed in SAP; 'cancelled' indicates the production order was cancelled; 'superseded' indicates the record was replaced by a corrected entry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

description: Primary regulatory or quality standard under which this batch genealogy record must be maintained and reported. Drives retention period, traceability depth, and reporting format requirements. Multiple standards may apply; the most stringent is recorded.
reference: ISO 9001:2015; IATF 16949:2016; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; EU GMP; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006

description: Classifies the nature of the parent-child batch relationship. 'component_to_assembly' is the standard BOM consumption; 'split' occurs when one batch is divided into multiple child batches; 'merge' when multiple parent batches are combined; 'rework' when a non-conforming batch is reprocessed; 'co_product' and 'by_product' for simultaneous production outputs; 'reprocess' for quality-driven reprocessing.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.5.2; SAP PP Batch Classification

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this batch genealogy record was sourced (e.g., SAP PP Batch Where-Used, Siemens Opcenter MES). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer reconciliation.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Sequential number identifying the order of child batches when a parent batch is split into multiple child batches (relationship_type = 'split'). Enables reconstruction of the full split genealogy tree and quantity reconciliation.
reference: SAP PP Batch Split; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: SAP storage location from which the parent batch was issued or to which the child batch was received. Supports inventory traceability and warehouse management integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Location; Infor WMS

description: Indicates the direction of traceability this record supports. 'forward' traces from raw material/component to finished product (where-used); 'backward' traces from finished product to raw material/component (where-from); 'bidirectional' supports both directions. Critical for product recall scope determination.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; IATF 16949:2016

description: The precise date and time at which the batch genealogy relationship was recorded in the system of record (SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES). This is the authoritative timestamp for regulatory traceability and recall scope determination.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.65; IATF 16949:2016

description: Ratio of produced quantity to consumed quantity expressed as a percentage, representing the material conversion efficiency for this specific batch relationship. Supports yield analysis and process capability monitoring (SPC).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 9.1

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each downtime event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and OEE/TPM calculations.
reference: ISO 22400-2 Manufacturing Operations Management KPIs

description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_notification. Business justification: When a machine goes down on the shop floor, an asset notification is raised immediately. Production supervisors link downtime events to notifications to trigger maintenance response and track resoluti
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Unplanned downtime incurs idle capacity costs that must be allocated to cost centers or orders. Finance uses this link to distribute machine downtime costs across cost objects during period-end cost a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Production downtime caused by equipment failures must reference the formal failure record for root cause analysis, warranty claims, and reliability engineering. Links operational impact to technical f
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Downtime events are tied to a physical location on the shop floor or facility. Maintenance and operations managers use this to correlate downtime with logistics disruptions (e.g., blocked aisles, dela
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Unplanned downtime events often require emergency spare part procurement. Maintenance and production teams link the downtime event to the resulting purchase order to track response time, cost of downt
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: downtime_event occurs during a specific shift. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift_code reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level downtime analysis and MTTR/MTBF calculations.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: downtime_event occurs at a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id enables joins to work_center master data for downtime analysis, MTTR/MTBF calculations, and OEE availability loss attribution per
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference number of the formal CAPA record raised as a result of this downtime event. Links to the QMS CAPA module for systemic root cause elimination and recurrence prevention tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA Methodology; SAP QM; Siemens Opcenter Quality

description: High-level classification of the downtime event type. Drives OEE loss categorization (availability loss vs. planned stops), TPM pillar analysis, and MTBF/MTTR segmentation. Aligns with Opcenter MES downtime category taxonomy.
reference: ISO 22400-2; SEMI E10 Equipment Reliability Standard; Siemens Opcenter MES Downtime Category

description: Description of the immediate corrective action taken to restore production (e.g., replaced bearing, reset PLC, cleared material jam). Distinct from CAPA — this is the immediate fix, not the systemic preventive action.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 Corrective Action; CAPA Methodology; SAP PM Notification

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where the downtime event occurred. Supports multi-country OEE benchmarking, regulatory reporting, and regional maintenance performance analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes

description: Timestamp when the downtime event record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data freshness monitoring, and SLA compliance on downtime reporting timeliness.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Method by which the downtime event was detected and initiated. Distinguishes between human-reported events and automated detection via SCADA, PLC fault codes, or MindSphere predictive alerts. Supports IIoT maturity assessment.
reference: Siemens MindSphere IIoT Predictive Analytics; SCADA/DCS Alarm Management; IEC 62443

description: Total elapsed duration of the downtime event in minutes, calculated from start and end timestamps. Stored explicitly to support direct aggregation in OEE availability calculations and TPM dashboards without requiring timestamp arithmetic at query time.
reference: ISO 22400-2 KPI: Downtime Duration; OEE Availability Component

description: Exact date and time when the downtime event was resolved and production resumed. Used with start_timestamp to compute duration and MTTR. Null if the event is still open.
reference: ISO 22400-2 KPI: Mean Time To Repair (MTTR); Siemens Opcenter MES Downtime

description: Human-readable business identifier for the downtime event, typically generated by Siemens Opcenter MES or SAP PM. Used for cross-system referencing, shop floor communication, and audit trails.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Downtime Management; SAP PM Notification

description: Specific failure mode description identifying how the equipment or process failed, aligned with FMEA/PFMEA failure mode taxonomy. Supports root cause analysis and reliability engineering programs.
reference: FMEA Methodology; PFMEA; ISO 14224 Reliability and Maintenance Data

description: Fault or alarm code generated by the PLC, SCADA, or DCS system at the time of the downtime event. Provides precise machine-level diagnostic information for maintenance engineers and reliability analysis.
reference: IEC 61131 PLC Programming Standard; SCADA/DCS Alarm Management; Siemens MindSphere

description: Indicates whether the downtime event was planned (e.g., scheduled maintenance, changeover, trial run) or unplanned (e.g., breakdown, quality hold). Critical for correctly computing OEE availability — only unplanned downtime reduces OEE availability.
reference: ISO 22400-2 OEE Availability; SEMI E10; Lean Manufacturing OEE Framework

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the downtime event record. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline and change data capture (CDC) processing.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer CDC; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Quantity of production units lost or not produced as a direct result of the downtime event. Feeds OEE performance and availability loss calculations, and supports financial impact assessment of downtime.
reference: ISO 22400-2 OEE; Lean Manufacturing Throughput Analysis

description: Unit of measure for the lost production quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters). Ensures dimensional consistency when aggregating lost production across different product types.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Unit of Measure; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Unique identifier of the specific machine or equipment asset within the work center that experienced the downtime. Corresponds to the equipment number in SAP PM and Maximo EAM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Equipment Master; Maximo EAM Asset Record; ISO 14224

description: Descriptive name of the machine or equipment asset that experienced the downtime. Supports readability in OEE dashboards and TPM reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Equipment Master; Maximo EAM

description: Reference to the SAP PM or Maximo EAM maintenance work order created to address the downtime event. Links downtime records to maintenance execution, spare parts consumption, and labor cost tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Work Order; Maximo EAM Work Order Management

description: Native event identifier from Siemens Opcenter MES downtime module. Enables traceability back to the source system record for reconciliation and audit purposes.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Downtime Module

description: Asset identifier from Siemens MindSphere IIoT platform used to correlate the downtime event with real-time telemetry, predictive alerts, and condition monitoring data from the affected machine or work center.
reference: Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform Asset Monitoring

description: Classification of how this downtime event impacts the OEE calculation — specifically which OEE loss category it belongs to (availability loss for unplanned stops, planned stop for scheduled downtime). Enables automated OEE waterfall chart generation.
reference: ISO 22400-2 OEE; SEMI E10; Lean Manufacturing OEE Framework

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the downtime event occurred. Enables multi-plant OEE benchmarking and global downtime analysis across the multinational enterprise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 22400-2

description: SAP production order number that was active or impacted at the time of the downtime event. Links the downtime to the specific manufacturing order for cost impact and schedule deviation analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order; Siemens Opcenter MES Work Order

description: Standardized reason code from the MES or ERP downtime reason catalog that identifies the specific cause of the downtime event (e.g., mechanical failure, electrical fault, tooling wear). Enables Pareto analysis and FMEA correlation.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Reason Code Catalog; SAP PM Notification Cause Code; FMEA Methodology

description: Human-readable description of the downtime reason code. Provides context for operators and maintenance engineers when reviewing downtime records without requiring a lookup to the reason code catalog.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP PM Notification

description: Employee ID or username of the operator or technician who reported or initiated the downtime event record in the MES or maintenance system. Supports accountability tracking and operator performance analysis.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES User Management; SAP PM Notification Reporter

description: Employee ID or username of the maintenance technician or engineer who resolved the downtime event. Used for MTTR analysis by technician, skills gap identification, and TPM workforce planning.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; Maximo EAM Work Order Assignment; SAP PM

description: Department accountable for resolving or owning the downtime event (e.g., Maintenance, Production, Quality, Logistics). Used for departmental KPI reporting and escalation routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Identified root cause of the downtime event as determined through investigation (e.g., 5-Why analysis, fishbone diagram). Populated after CAPA investigation is completed. Supports TPM and continuous improvement programs.
reference: CAPA Methodology; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Kaizen / Lean Manufacturing

description: Production shift identifier during which the downtime event occurred (e.g., A-shift, B-shift, night shift). Enables shift-level downtime analysis and supports labor accountability in TPM programs.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Shift Management; Kronos Workforce Central Scheduling

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which the downtime event was originated or ingested (e.g., Siemens Opcenter MES, MindSphere IIoT, SAP PM, Maximo EAM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: Data Governance — Data Lineage; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Exact date and time when the downtime event began, as recorded by Opcenter MES or MindSphere IIoT. Critical for MTBF calculations, OEE availability component, and shift-level downtime reporting.
reference: ISO 22400-2 KPI: Availability; Siemens Opcenter MES Downtime

description: Current lifecycle status of the downtime event record. Drives workflow routing in MES and maintenance systems — open events trigger alerts, resolved events enable MTTR calculation, closed events are locked for reporting.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Downtime Workflow; SAP PM Notification Status

description: Code identifying the work center or production cell where the downtime event occurred. Aligns with SAP PP/PM work center master data and Opcenter MES resource definitions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; ISO 22400-2

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each scrap record event in the production domain. Serves as the primary key for the scrap_record data product in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Opcenter MES Scrap Reporting

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: scrap_record captures scrap generated from a specific batch. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-level scrap analysis and yiel
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.defect_code. Business justification: Production scrap must be categorized using standardized defect codes for Pareto analysis, trend reporting, and quality improvement initiatives. Used by production supervisors during scrap reporting.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Scrap caused by equipment malfunction must be traceable to the specific failure record for warranty claims, quality investigations, and cost allocation. Quality engineers use this link daily in non-co
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Scrap records must reference the specific inventory lot/batch being scrapped for full material traceability. Regulatory compliance and quality investigations require linking scrap events to the origin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.pfmea. Business justification: Scrap events are classified against PFMEA failure modes so quality engineers can track actual vs. predicted failure rates. This closed-loop feedback between production scrap and process FMEA is standa
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Scrap events generate financial write-offs posted against the production order cost object. Cost accountants use this link to capture scrap variances and report waste costs against the manufacturing o
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.returns_order. Business justification: Scrap records generated during rework of customer returns are linked to the originating returns order. Quality and production teams use this link to track disposition of returned goods and calculate r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: scrap_record references the routing under which scrap was generated. The FK routing_id enables joins to routing master data for process-level scrap analysis and routing optimization.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: scrap_record captures scrap generated at a specific routing_operation step. The FK routing_operation_id enables joins to routing_operation master data for operation-level scrap analysis and process im
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Scrapped materials move to designated scrap storage locations. Material handlers need exact locations for segregation and disposal processing.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: scrap_record captures scrap generated during a specific shift. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift_code reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level scrap analysis and quality tre
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment_item. Business justification: Scrap can originate from defective incoming shipment items. Quality and production teams trace scrap back to the specific shipment item to support supplier claims, incoming quality control, and root c
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Scrap caused by defective incoming materials is linked to the responsible supplier. Quality and procurement teams use this to raise supplier claims, update supplier performance scores, and drive corre
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: scrap_record captures scrap generated at a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id enables joins to work_center master data for scrap analysis, quality hotspot identification, and cost attribution
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference identifier of the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record initiated as a result of this scrap event. Enables traceability from scrap occurrence to quality improvement action.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA Methodology; FDA 21 CFR Part 820 (where applicable)

description: Reference to the production confirmation document in SAP PP under which the scrap quantity was confirmed. Provides direct linkage to the goods movement and actual cost posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Confirmation; ISO 9001:2015

description: SAP controlling cost center to which the scrap cost is charged. Enables cost center-level scrap cost accountability and management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; GAAP; IFRS

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the scrap cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency financial consolidation for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where the scrap event occurred. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting (EPA, EU environmental directives) and regional scrap benchmarking.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ISO 14001:2015; EPA; EU Environmental Directives

description: Timestamp when the scrap record was created in the source system or ingested into the Silver layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance for data freshness.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Specific defect classification code from the quality defect catalog (SAP QM or Opcenter Quality) identifying the type of defect that caused the scrap. Supports SPC analysis and defect Pareto reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Defect Catalog; Siemens Opcenter Quality; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SPC Methodology

description: Indicates whether the scrapped material is classified as hazardous under applicable regulations (REACH, RoHS, EPA). Triggers mandatory hazardous waste disposal procedures and regulatory reporting obligations.
reference: EU REACH Regulation; EU RoHS Directive; EPA Hazardous Waste Regulations; ISO 14001:2015

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the scrapped item has been designated for rework rather than disposal. When true, a rework order reference should be present. Distinguishes reworkable non-conformances from permanent scrap for cost and yield analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Rework Order; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the scrap record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Human-readable description of the scrapped material as maintained in the material master. Supports reporting and communication without requiring a material master lookup.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: SAP material number of the scrapped component or finished/semi-finished product. Used for material-level scrap rate analysis, COGS variance, and MRP II feedback.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Routing operation number at which the scrap was identified and reported. Enables pinpointing the specific manufacturing step responsible for the scrap event.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; Siemens Opcenter MES Shop Floor Execution

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the scrap event occurred. Supports multi-plant scrap benchmarking and regional compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015

description: Accounting posting date for the scrap event in SAP, which may differ from the reporting timestamp. Determines the financial period in which the scrap cost is recognized for COGS variance and period-end closing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date; GAAP; IFRS IAS 2

description: Reference to the production order under which the scrap event occurred. Links the scrap event to the originating manufacturing order for cost variance and OEE analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Manufacturing strategy under which the scrapped order was produced: Make to Order (MTO), Make to Stock (MTS), Engineer to Order (ETO), or Assemble to Order (ATO). Contextualizes scrap impact on customer commitments and inventory.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planning Strategy; MRP II Methodology

description: Date and time when the scrap event was reported in the MES or ERP system by the operator or automated process. Used for time-series scrap trend analysis and shift-level reporting.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Reference number of the rework production order created to remediate the scrapped item. Populated only when is_rework is true. Enables end-to-end rework cost tracking and yield recovery analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Rework Order; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Reference to the production routing used for the order in which the scrap occurred. Enables routing-level scrap analysis to identify process steps with systemic quality issues.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: High-level classification of the scrap event origin: process scrap (inherent process loss), machine scrap (equipment malfunction), material defect (incoming material issue), operator error, design defect, tooling defect, or incoming material. Drives root cause categorization for Six Sigma and CAPA programs.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Scrap Reporting; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; PFMEA Methodology

description: Monetary value of the scrapped material calculated using the standard or moving average cost from the material master. Key input for COGS variance analysis, scrap cost reporting, and financial impact assessment of quality failures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Product Costing; GAAP; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories

description: Business-facing document number assigned to the scrap event by the source system (SAP PP or Opcenter MES). Used for cross-system traceability and audit purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Quantity of material declared as scrap in the base unit of measure. Core metric for scrap rate KPI calculation, COGS variance analysis, and Six Sigma improvement programs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Confirmation; Siemens Opcenter MES Scrap Reporting; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Standardized reason code from the MES or ERP scrap reason catalog describing the specific cause of scrap (e.g., dimensional out-of-tolerance, surface defect, wrong material). Used for Pareto analysis and CAPA prioritization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Defect Catalog; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA Methodology

description: Free-text description providing additional context for the scrap reason beyond the standardized code. Captures operator observations and nuanced failure details for quality investigations.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Production shift identifier during which the scrap event occurred (e.g., Day, Afternoon, Night). Enables shift-level scrap rate benchmarking and operator performance analysis.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Identifies the originating operational system that generated the scrap record: SAP S/4HANA PP, Siemens Opcenter MES, or manual entry. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Unit of measure for the scrap quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure for the material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Code identifying the work center (machine, cell, or production line) where the scrap was generated. Used for work center-level scrap rate KPI reporting and capacity impact analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each capacity requirement record generated by MRP II capacity planning in SAP PP (CM01). Serves as the primary key for the capacity_requirement data product in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP PP Capacity Requirements Planning (CM01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1 Operational Planning

description: Foreign key linking to sales.forecast. Business justification: Capacity requirements planning (CRP) derives load from sales forecasts. Planners trace capacity needs back to the originating forecast to validate whether the plant can meet projected sales demand.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Capacity requirements are calculated from demand represented in fulfillment plans. Capacity planners trace capacity loads back to fulfillment plans to identify bottlenecks caused by specific customer
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_plan. Business justification: Capacity planners must account for planned maintenance windows when calculating available machine capacity. Linking capacity requirements to maintenance plans ensures production scheduling respects pr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.mrp_run. Business justification: Capacity requirements are calculated as a direct output of an MRP run. Production planners must trace which MRP run generated each capacity requirement to validate planning results and resolve overloa
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Capacity requirements carry planned activity costs (machine hours × cost rate) that feed into the production order cost object. Cost planners use this link to build the planned cost structure for manu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: capacity_requirement is generated by MRP II for specific routings. The FK routing_id normalizes the routing_number string reference and enables joins to routing master data for capacity planning analy
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: capacity_requirement is computed per routing_operation for detailed capacity planning. The FK routing_operation_id normalizes the routing_operation_number string reference and enables joins to routing
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: capacity_requirement is planned per shift for detailed capacity analysis. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift_code reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level capacity load analy
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to production.work_center

Total capacity (in hours) available at the work center during the planning period, derived from the work center's capacity definition and shift model in SAP PP. Used to compute load percentage and identify overloads.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Capacity (CR01); ISO 22400-2

description: SAP capacity category code distinguishing the type of capacity being planned (e.g., machine capacity, labor capacity, tool capacity). Corresponds to SAP PP capacity category (e.g., 001 = Machine, 002 = Labor).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Category; ISO 22400-2 Manufacturing Operations KPIs

description: Human-readable description of the capacity category (Machine, Labor, Tool, Other). Used for filtering and grouping in capacity leveling and bottleneck analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Category; ISO 22400-2

description: Unit of measure in which capacity requirements and availability are expressed (e.g., H = Hours, MIN = Minutes). Ensures consistent interpretation of capacity figures across work centers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity UoM; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: SAP planning version identifier (e.g., 000 for active version, simulation versions) under which this capacity requirement was calculated. Enables what-if scenario management and simulation-based capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planning Versions; APICS CPIM Capacity Planning

description: SAP Controlling (CO) cost center code associated with the work center. Enables financial allocation of capacity costs and integration with OPEX reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where the work center is located. Supports multi-country capacity reporting and compliance with regional labor regulations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; OSHA; EU Working Time Directive

description: Timestamp when the capacity requirement record was first created in the source system (SAP PP) or ingested into the Silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and incremental load processing.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5

description: Efficiency factor (as a percentage) applied to the work center when calculating available capacity, reflecting the expected productive utilization rate (e.g., 85% efficiency means 85% of nominal capacity is available). Sourced from SAP PP work center definition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Efficiency (CR01); ISO 22400-2 KPI: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)

description: Flag indicating whether the work center is overloaded during the planning period (required capacity exceeds available capacity). Triggers capacity leveling actions and what-if scheduling scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP CM01 Overload Indicator; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1 Operational Planning and Control

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the capacity requirement record in the source system or Silver layer. Used for change detection, incremental ETL, and audit trail compliance.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5

description: Action taken during capacity leveling to resolve an overload condition at the work center (e.g., Rescheduled, Split, Outsourced, Overtime, Deferred). Supports audit trail of capacity leveling decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Leveling (CM21); APICS CPIM Capacity Management

description: Ratio of required capacity to available capacity expressed as a percentage (required_capacity_hours / available_capacity_hours × 100). Key indicator for bottleneck identification and capacity leveling decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP CM01 Capacity Evaluation; ISO 22400-2 KPI: Capacity Utilization

description: Date on which the MRP II planning run was executed that generated this capacity requirement record. Enables time-series comparison of capacity plans across successive MRP runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP II (MD01/MD02); APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Unique identifier of the MRP II planning run that generated this capacity requirement. Supports audit traceability and comparison between planning runs for what-if scenario analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP II (MD01/MD02)

description: Free-text field for planner annotations, leveling justifications, or exception comments related to the capacity requirement record. Supports operational communication and audit documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Number of individual machines, operators, or tools at the work center contributing to the available capacity for this planning period. Used to calculate per-resource load and support headcount/machine planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Capacity (CR01); ISO 22400-2

description: Number of production orders and planned orders contributing to the capacity requirement for this work center and planning period. Indicates the complexity of the load and supports scheduling prioritization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Requirements (CM01); APICS CPIM

description: Number of hours by which required capacity exceeds available capacity during the planning period. Quantifies the severity of the overload for prioritization and leveling decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Leveling (CM21); APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Capacity hours contributed by MRP-generated planned orders (not yet converted to production orders) within the planning period. Distinguishes firm demand from planned demand for capacity analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP Planned Orders; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1

description: Granularity of the planning time bucket used for capacity aggregation (Daily, Weekly, Monthly). Determines the resolution of capacity load visibility for scheduling and leveling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP II Capacity Planning (CM01); APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: End date of the planning period for which the capacity requirement is calculated. Together with planning_period_start_date, defines the time bucket for capacity load aggregation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP II Capacity Planning; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1

description: Start date of the planning period (day, week, or shift bucket) for which the capacity requirement is calculated. Defines the time horizon of the capacity load record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP II Capacity Planning; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility for which the capacity requirement is calculated. Enables multi-plant capacity visibility across the enterprise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.3

description: Portion of the required capacity hours attributable to actual production/processing activities (machine run time or labor operation time) at the work center during the planning period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Processing Time; ISO 22400-2 KPI: Production Process Time

description: Capacity hours contributed by firm production orders (released or confirmed) within the planning period. Represents committed capacity load as opposed to planned/tentative demand.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Orders; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1

description: Residual available capacity hours after subtracting required capacity from available capacity (available_capacity_hours − required_capacity_hours). Negative values indicate overload conditions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Leveling (CM21); ISO 22400-2

description: Total capacity (in hours) required at the work center during the planning period, as calculated by MRP II from open production orders and planned orders. Core metric for capacity load analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Requirements (CM01); ISO 22400-2 KPI for Manufacturing Operations Management

description: Business-facing unique identifier for the capacity requirement record as assigned by SAP PP capacity planning (CM01). Used for cross-system traceability and operational reference.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Management (CM01)

description: Identifier of the production routing (Bill of Process) from which the work center operation and capacity requirement was derived. Links capacity requirements to the engineering routing definition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing (CA01); Siemens Teamcenter BOP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Specific operation number within the routing that generates the capacity requirement at this work center. Enables operation-level capacity traceability within a multi-step routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Operations (CA01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Portion of the required capacity hours attributable to setup/changeover activities at the work center during the planning period. Supports SMED analysis and setup time reduction initiatives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Setup Time; ISO 22400-2; SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Dies)

description: Identifier of the production shift (e.g., Day, Afternoon, Night) within the planning period. Enables shift-level capacity load analysis and scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Shift Planning; Kronos Workforce Central Scheduling

description: Current status of the capacity requirement record indicating its planning state: Active (current plan), Leveled (capacity leveling applied), Overloaded (exceeds available capacity), Cancelled (order cancelled), or Simulated (what-if scenario).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Management (CM01/CM21); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1

description: Portion of the required capacity hours attributable to teardown/cleanup activities at the work center during the planning period. Included in total capacity requirement calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Teardown Time; ISO 22400-2

description: Utilization factor (as a percentage) applied to the work center capacity, representing the proportion of available time the resource is scheduled for productive work. Sourced from SAP PP work center definition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center Utilization (CR01); ISO 22400-2 KPI: Utilization

description: Identifier of the work center (machine group, production cell, or labor pool) for which the capacity requirement is calculated. Corresponds to the SAP PP work center object (CR01/CR02).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center (CR01/CR02); ISO 22400-2 KPI for Manufacturing Operations

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each material staging and issuance record on the shop floor. Serves as the primary key for the material_staging data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Goods Issue / Material Staging

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: material_staging references the batch being issued to the shop floor. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-level material flow
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery. Business justification: Material staging is directly triggered by an inbound or outbound delivery document. Production planners and warehouse staff link staged materials to the delivery to confirm what has arrived and is rea
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Material staging in production is directly triggered by goods receipts from procurement. Warehouse and production teams use this link to confirm that received materials are staged to the correct work
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line_item. Business justification: Materials are staged for specific order line items in configure-to-order environments. Warehouse and production teams use this to ensure correct components are available for customer-specific configur
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_location. Business justification: Material staging occurs at specific logistics locations (warehouses, staging areas, docks) before production. Production planners coordinate with logistics to ensure materials are positioned correctly
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Production material staging references the originating purchase order to trace which procurement document authorized the material supply. Used by production planners to resolve material shortages and
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.schedule_line. Business justification: Material staging is driven by customer order schedule lines that specify required quantities and dates. Production logistics teams stage materials against specific schedule lines to meet confirmed del
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Material staging pulls from specific storage locations. Pick lists direct warehouse staff to exact bin locations for production material retrieval.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: material_staging.work_center_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the work_center master. Material staging records are associated with the work center to which materials are being staged (supp
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual date and time when the material was physically moved to the staging area or production supply area. Captured by MES or WMS system. Used for staging lead time analysis and JIT performance measurement.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; Infor WMS; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether the material is subject to backflushing (automatic goods issue upon production order confirmation) rather than manual goods issue. Backflushed components do not require explicit staging transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Backflush (RGEKZ); Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Batch or lot number (SAP CHARG) of the material being staged or issued. Critical for batch-managed materials, traceability, quality management, and regulatory compliance (e.g., RoHS, REACH).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Batch (CHARG); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2; EU RoHS; REACH

description: Position or item number of the component within the Bill of Materials (BOM) for the production order. Enables traceability from the staged material back to the BOM structure in PLM/ERP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - BOM Item (POSNR); Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant where the staging event occurred (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Supports multinational regulatory compliance, REACH/RoHS jurisdiction tracking, and regional reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU RoHS Directive; REACH Regulation; GDPR

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the material cost values (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing plants.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI - Currency (WAERS); IFRS

description: Date and time when the formal goods issue (SAP movement type 261) was posted in SAP S/4HANA, confirming material consumption against the production order. Triggers WIP cost update and inventory reduction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM - Goods Issue Posting Date (BUDAT); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether the staged material is classified as hazardous under applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, RoHS, OSHA HazCom). Triggers additional handling, documentation, and safety protocols on the shop floor.
reference: EU REACH Regulation; EU RoHS Directive; OSHA HazCom Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; ISO 14001

description: Actual quantity of material formally issued to the production order via SAP Goods Issue (movement type 261). Represents confirmed material consumption posted to the production order and used for WIP costing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM - Goods Issue Quantity (ENMNG); Movement Type 261; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: Identifier of the warehouse or warehouse complex (SAP WM Warehouse Number / Infor WMS Warehouse) from which the material is physically picked and issued to the shop floor. Supports WMS integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM - Warehouse Number (LGNUM); Infor WMS

description: Kanban card or container identifier associated with this material staging record when the replenishment_strategy is 'Kanban'. Links the staging event to the Kanban control cycle for pull-based replenishment tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Kanban (KANUM); Kanban; Lean Manufacturing

description: Standard or moving average cost per unit of the material at the time of goods issue, as valuated in SAP S/4HANA CO. Used for WIP costing, COGS calculation, and production order cost analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO - Material Valuation (VERPR/STPRS); IFRS; GAAP; COGS

description: SAP inventory movement type code (BWART) associated with the staging or issuance transaction. Common values: 261 (Goods Issue to Production Order), 262 (Reversal of GI), 311 (Transfer to Production Storage Location), 312 (Reversal of Transfer).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Movement Type (BWART)

description: Planned date on which the material should be staged or issued to the shop floor, as determined by MRP II scheduling and production order requirements. Used for material availability planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Requirements Date (BDTER); MRP II

description: SAP plant code (WERKS) identifying the manufacturing facility where the material staging and issuance takes place. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM - Plant (WERKS); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: Reference to the production order (SAP PP Production Order / Process Order) for which materials are being staged or issued. Links material consumption to the manufacturing execution context.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Production Order (AUFNR); Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Manufacturing production strategy under which the production order was created: MTO (Make to Order), MTS (Make to Stock), ETO (Engineer to Order), or ATO (Assemble to Order). Determines material planning and staging behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Planning Strategy; MTO; MTS; ETO; ATO; MRP II

description: Material replenishment strategy governing how this component is supplied to the shop floor. Supports JIT (Just-In-Time), Kanban pull-based replenishment, MRP-driven push replenishment, manual staging, or consignment stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM - Replenishment Strategy; JIT; Kanban; MRP II

description: Planned quantity of the material required for the production order as specified in the BOM or reservation. Represents the demand quantity before actual issuance. Used for shortage analysis and MRP II planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Requirement Quantity (BDMNG); MRP II

description: Line item within the material reservation corresponding to the specific component being staged. Used to match the staging record to the exact reservation item in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reservation Item (RSPOS)

description: SAP reservation number (RSNUM) created by MRP or production order release, representing the planned material requirement that this staging record fulfills. Supports JIT and MRP II material planning workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reservation (RSNUM); MRP II

description: Date and time when a goods issue reversal (SAP movement type 262) was posted, cancelling a previously issued quantity. Applicable only when staging_status is 'reversed'. Supports audit trail and WIP cost correction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reversal Document (STBLG); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: Serial number of the specific component unit being issued, applicable for serialized materials. Supports unit-level traceability and asset lifecycle management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Serial Number (SERNR); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2

description: Quantity of material that could not be staged or issued due to insufficient stock (shortage). Calculated as required_quantity minus issued_quantity when status is 'short'. Drives material shortage alerts and expediting actions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Material Shortage; MRP II; JIT

description: Boolean flag indicating whether a material shortage exists for this staging record (i.e., required_quantity cannot be fully fulfilled from available stock). Drives shortage alerts, expediting, and JIT replenishment triggers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Material Availability Check; MRP II; JIT

description: Operational system of record from which this material staging record originated. Identifies whether the record was created in SAP S/4HANA PP/MM, Siemens Opcenter MES, Infor WMS, or entered manually. Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Data Lineage Best Practice

description: Quantity of material physically moved to the staging area or production supply area (PSA) but not yet formally issued via goods issue posting. Represents the intermediate staging step in JIT and Kanban workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Staged Quantity; Siemens Opcenter MES; JIT; Kanban

description: Line item number within the staging document, identifying the specific component or material being staged or issued within a multi-line goods issue document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Document Item (ZEILE)

description: Business document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA (PP/MM) to the goods issue or material staging transaction. Corresponds to the SAP Material Document Number (MBLNR) generated upon goods issue posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Document (MBLNR); SAP PP Goods Issue

description: Fiscal year associated with the staging document number in SAP S/4HANA. Required to uniquely identify a material document in conjunction with the document number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Document Year (MJAHR)

description: Current lifecycle status of the material staging record. 'planned' = reservation created; 'staged' = material moved to staging area; 'issued' = goods issue posted; 'short' = insufficient stock; 'partially_issued' = partial goods issue; 'reversed' = goods issue reversed; 'cancelled' = staging cancelled.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Goods Issue Status; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: SAP storage location (LGORT) from which the material is staged or issued. Identifies the physical warehouse or shop floor storage area within the plant from which inventory is consumed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/WM - Storage Location (LGORT); Infor WMS

description: SAP Production Supply Area (PSA) or line-side staging location where the material is delivered for shop floor consumption. Represents the physical destination on the shop floor for staged materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Production Supply Area (PVBE); Lean Manufacturing; JIT
