description: Total cost of the issued material quantity (issued_quantity × material_cost_per_unit) in the transaction currency. Feeds WIP costing, production order actual cost, and COGS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO - Production Order Actual Costs; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP; COGS

description: Base unit of measure (SAP MEINS) for the material quantities (required, staged, issued, short). Examples: EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter), M2 (square meter). Ensures consistent quantity reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Base Unit of Measure (MEINS); ISO 80000; ANSI

description: Costing method used to value the issued material: standard cost (fixed standard price), moving average price (MAP), or actual cost. Determines how WIP and COGS are calculated for the production order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO/MM - Valuation Type (BWTAR); IFRS IAS 2; GAAP

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each production order status transition event record in the Silver layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Status; Siemens Opcenter MES Work Order Status History

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: order_status snapshots reference the batch associated with the production order at the time of status transition. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.order_type. Business justification: order_status.production_order_type (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the order_type master. Adding order_type_id FK normalizes this, allowing status lifecycle records to be filtered and analyzed
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: order_status.routing_number (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the routing master. Production order status lifecycle records are associated with a specific routing that defines the operations bei
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: order_status.work_center_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the work_center master. Status transitions on production orders are often associated with a specific work center (e.g., a HOLD sta
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The actual date and time when production execution was completed for this order, as recorded in Opcenter MES or SAP PP technical completion. Used to calculate actual cycle time and on-time delivery performance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Technical Completion; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: The actual date and time when production execution began for this order, as recorded in Opcenter MES or SAP PP confirmation. Used to calculate actual lead time and schedule variance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Confirmation; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: The production batch or lot number associated with the production order at the time of the status transition. Critical for batch traceability, recall management, and GMP compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Batch Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2 Traceability; FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for the production order. Supports multi-entity financial reporting, IFRS/GAAP compliance, and intercompany manufacturing scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code; IFRS; GAAP

description: The cumulative quantity of the production order that has been confirmed (goods receipt posted) at the time of the status transition. Enables partial completion tracking and yield analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Confirmation; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: SAP cost center associated with the production order at the time of the status transition. Supports COGS allocation, production cost tracking, and financial controlling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; GAAP COGS Reporting

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or manufacturing site where the production order status transition occurred. Supports multinational regulatory compliance and regional performance reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA Plant Configuration

description: The elapsed time in minutes that the production order spent in the from_status before transitioning to the to_status. Enables dwell time analysis, bottleneck identification, and SLA breach detection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES Analytics; Lean Manufacturing Lead Time Analysis

description: The production order status immediately before the transition event. Represents the prior lifecycle state as recorded in SAP PP or Opcenter MES (e.g., CREATED, RELEASED, IN_PROCESS).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Status; Siemens Opcenter MES Work Order Status

description: Indicates whether the production order exceeded its defined SLA target duration at the time of this status transition. Drives escalation workflows and production performance KPI reporting.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3; SAP S/4HANA SD SLA Management

description: Short description of the material or finished product being manufactured, as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable context for reporting without requiring a join.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: SAP material number (SKU) of the finished good or semi-finished product being manufactured by this production order. Links the status history to the product being produced.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2 Identification and Traceability

description: The specific MES operation or activity number within the production order routing that was active or completed at the time of the status transition. Provides granular shop floor execution context.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Work Order Operations; SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Operations

description: Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) that triggered a QM hold or rework status transition. Enables traceability between production order status events and quality non-conformances.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs; CAPA Process

description: SAP order category distinguishing production orders (discrete manufacturing), process orders (batch/process manufacturing), maintenance orders, rework orders, and inspection orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/PI Order Categories

description: The total planned production quantity for the order at the time of the status transition. Captures the order size context for throughput and capacity analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order; MRP II

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the production order is being executed. Supports multi-plant and multinational reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001 Clause 4.1

description: The business priority assigned to the production order, influencing scheduling and resource allocation decisions. EXPEDITE indicates customer-driven urgency or supply chain escalation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Priority; MRP II Scheduling

description: The business key of the production order (SAP PP order number or Opcenter work order number) whose status is being tracked. Serves as the primary cross-system reference for the order lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: SAP profit center assigned to the production order, enabling cost and revenue attribution by business unit or product line for management accounting and EBITDA reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center Accounting; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: The SAP QM inspection lot number associated with this status transition when the trigger is a quality hold, quality release, or inspection result. Links production order status to quality inspection outcomes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001 Clause 8.6 Release of Products

description: The unit of measure for the order quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, PC for piece). Ensures correct interpretation of quantity values across materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Unit of Measure; ISO 80000 International System of Units

description: The timestamp when this status transition record was created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and incremental load processing.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 Accountability

description: The timestamp when this status transition record was last updated in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and SCD Type 1 correction scenarios.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: The planned completion date of the production order as scheduled in SAP PP or Opcenter MES at the time of the status transition. Baseline for on-time delivery and SLA compliance measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Scheduling; MRP II; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3

description: The planned start date of the production order as scheduled in SAP PP or Opcenter MES at the time of the status transition. Used for schedule adherence and lead time SLA tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Scheduling; MRP II

description: The quantity of production output declared as scrap at the time of the status transition. Used for scrap rate calculation and quality performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Scrap; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs

description: The contracted or internally defined SLA target duration in hours for the production order to complete from release to technical completion. Used to assess whether the order met its lead time commitment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD SLA; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3 Customer Requirements

description: The originating operational system that generated or recorded this status transition event (e.g., SAP_PP for ERP-driven transitions, OPCENTER_MES for shop floor-driven transitions). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES; Data Governance Best Practices

description: The unique event or change document identifier from the originating source system (SAP change document number or Opcenter event ID) for this status transition. Enables traceability back to the system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Change Documents; Siemens Opcenter MES Audit Log

description: Standardized reason code explaining why the status transition occurred (e.g., MATERIAL_SHORTAGE, CAPACITY_CONSTRAINT, QM_INSPECTION_FAIL, CUSTOMER_CHANGE, PLANNED_MAINTENANCE). Supports root cause analysis and production performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Reason Codes; ISO 9001 Clause 10.2 CAPA

description: Free-text description providing additional context for the status transition reason, supplementing the structured reason code. Allows planners and supervisors to document specific circumstances.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: The production order status immediately after the transition event. Represents the new lifecycle state the order has entered (e.g., RELEASED, TECHNICALLY_COMPLETED, CLOSED).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Order Status; Siemens Opcenter MES Work Order Status

description: The exact date and time (with timezone offset) at which the status transition occurred. Critical for SLA tracking, lead time calculation, and audit trail compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GAAP Audit Trail Requirements

description: The business event or system action that caused the status transition (e.g., MRP run, manual release by planner, system confirmation from MES, QM hold from quality inspection). Enables root cause analysis of order lifecycle events.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: The SAP or Opcenter user ID of the person or system account that initiated the status transition. Used for accountability, audit trail, and change management compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Change Documents; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a planned order record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planned Order (PLAF table)

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
