reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Planned production orders are created in direct response to a fulfillment plan. Production planning teams link planned orders to fulfillment plans daily to ensure manufacturing output aligns with comm
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: In batch-managed manufacturing, planned orders are pre-assigned a lot/batch number before production starts. This enables early reservation and traceability linkage between planning and inventory lot
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.mrp_planned_order. Business justification: MRP generates planned orders that convert to production orders or purchase requisitions. Production planning uses MRP output daily to determine what to manufacture versus procure externally.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.order_type. Business justification: planned_order.order_type (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the order_type master. Adding order_type_id FK normalizes this relationship, enabling joins to retrieve order category, confirmation co
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.priority. Business justification: Planned orders inherit or align with customer order priority classifications. Production schedulers use order priority to sequence planned orders, ensuring high-priority customer orders drive manufact
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.product_cost_estimate. Business justification: Planned orders reference the product cost estimate to determine standard costs for materials and activities. Production planning and cost controlling use this link to compare planned vs. actual costs
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Planned orders are created for a specific product variant. Production planning must reference the exact engineering variant to pull the correct BOM and routing. Used daily in MRP/ERP order generation.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: planned_order references a routing for MRP planning purposes. The FK routing_id normalizes the routing_number string reference and enables joins to routing master data for lead time calculation and ca
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Planned orders specify the target storage location where produced goods will be received. MRP/MPS planning uses this to allocate storage capacity and direct goods receipt postings to the correct locat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.production_version. Business justification: planned_order references a production_version that defines the valid BOM and routing combination for manufacturing. The FK production_version_id normalizes the production_version string code and enabl
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: planned_order is assigned to a specific work_center for execution. The FK work_center_id normalizes the work_center_code reference and enables joins to work_center master data for MRP capacity plannin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the Bill of Materials assigned to this planned order, defining the component materials and quantities required for production. Sourced from the production version or material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - BOM (STKO-STLNR); Siemens Teamcenter PLM - BOM Management

description: The date and time at which the planned order was converted to a production order or purchase requisition. Supports lead time analysis and planning cycle performance measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planned Order Conversion

description: The production order number or purchase requisition number generated when this planned order was converted. Provides traceability from planned demand to shop floor execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Production Order (AUFNR)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant where this planned order is to be executed. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance, reporting, and regional production analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes

description: The date and time at which the planned order record was created in the source system (SAP PP). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and planning cycle analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planned Order Creation (PLAF-ERSDA)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the planned order cost valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency cost planning in multinational manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: The identifier of the originating demand element (e.g., sales order number, forecast record ID, project WBS element) that triggered this planned order. Enables end-to-end demand-to-production traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - MRP Element Number (MDEZ-MATNR)

description: Classification of the originating demand element that triggered the creation of this planned order by MRP. Supports demand traceability and planning analysis across MTO, MTS, and ETO strategies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - MRP Element (MDEZ-DELKZ)

description: SAP MRP exception message code indicating a planning anomaly requiring planner attention (e.g., 01=Reschedule in, 02=Reschedule out, 10=Cancel, 15=New planned order). Drives the MRP exception monitor workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - MRP Exception Messages (T460A-AUNAM)

description: The number of working days required to process the goods receipt after production completion before the material is available in stock. Included in MRP scheduling calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module - GR Processing Time (MARC-WEBAZ)

description: The planned in-house production lead time in calendar days for this planned order, as determined by the routing or material master. Used by MRP for backward and forward scheduling calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - In-House Production Time (MARC-DZEIT)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this planned order has been fully converted to a production order or purchase requisition. Once converted, the planned order is no longer an open planning element.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Conversion Indicator (PLAF-KZUMW)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the planned order has been manually firmed (fixed) by a planner, protecting it from automatic rescheduling or deletion by subsequent MRP runs. A firmed planned order represents a committed production plan.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Firming Indicator (PLAF-KZFIR)

description: The date and time at which the planned order record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, delta processing in the lakehouse pipeline, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planned Order Change (PLAF-AEDAT)

description: MRP lot-sizing procedure code used to calculate the planned order quantity (e.g., EX=Exact lot size, FX=Fixed lot size, HB=Replenishment up to maximum stock level, MB=Monthly lot size). Determines how demand quantities are grouped into planned orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Lot Size Procedure (T439A-DISLS)

description: Short descriptive text for the material to be produced, sourced from the SAP material master. Supports readability in reports and dashboards without requiring a join to the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master (MAKT-MAKTX)

description: SAP material master identifier for the finished good or semi-finished product to be produced under this planned order. Links to the material master record in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master (MARA-MATNR)

description: Three-character code identifying the MRP controller (planner) responsible for managing this planned order. Used for workload distribution and exception message routing in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - MRP Controller (T024D-DISPO)

description: The calendar date on which the MRP or MPS run was executed that generated this planned order. Enables traceability of planned orders back to specific planning cycles.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - MRP Run (MDKP-MDLDT)

description: Unique reference identifier for the MRP/MPS planning run that generated this planned order. Supports audit traceability and re-planning analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - MRP Run Reference

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the planned order as assigned by the MRP/MPS run in SAP PP. Used by planners and shop floor personnel to reference a specific planned production requirement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planned Order (PLAF-PLNUM)

description: The date on which the planned order should be converted to a production order or purchase requisition to allow sufficient lead time for execution. Calculated by MRP based on in-house production time and goods receipt processing time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Opening Date (PLAF-POEDT)

description: Estimated total production cost for this planned order in the local plant currency, including material costs, labor costs, and overhead. Used for cost planning, variance analysis, and COGS forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Cost Estimate; IFRS; GAAP

description: The SAP plant code of the planning plant responsible for MRP planning of this material, which may differ from the production plant in cross-plant planning scenarios. Supports multi-plant and central planning configurations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planning Plant (MARC-WERKS)

description: Four-character SAP plant identifier representing the manufacturing facility where the planned order is to be executed. Supports multi-plant production planning in a multinational environment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant (T001W-WERKS)

description: Manufacturing strategy governing how the planned order was generated: Make to Order (MTO), Make to Stock (MTS), Engineer to Order (ETO), or Assemble to Order (ATO). Determines demand traceability and inventory posting behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planning Strategy (T461A-STRAT)

description: The date by which the planned order quantity must be available to satisfy the originating demand requirement (e.g., sales order, dependent demand from a higher-level BOM). Aligns with the MRP requirements element date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Requirements Date (PLAF-BDTER)

description: Identifier of the production routing (sequence of operations) assigned to this planned order. Defines the manufacturing process steps, work centers, and standard times for producing the material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Routing (PLKO-PLNNR)

description: The planned calendar date on which production of this order is scheduled to be completed, as calculated by MRP forward scheduling. Drives delivery date commitments and ATP checks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planned Order Finish Date (PLAF-PEDTR)

description: The planned calendar date on which production of this order is scheduled to begin, as calculated by MRP backward scheduling. Used for capacity planning and material availability checks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planned Order Start Date (PLAF-PSTTR)

description: Assembly scrap percentage applied to the planned order quantity to account for expected production losses. The planned quantity is inflated by this percentage to ensure the net required quantity is met after scrap.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Assembly Scrap (MARC-AUSSS); ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: SAP special procurement type key indicating non-standard procurement scenarios such as subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or phantom assembly. Influences how MRP generates and processes the planned order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module - Special Procurement Type (MARC-SOBSL)

description: Current lifecycle status of the planned order. 'created' indicates MRP-generated and open; 'firmed' indicates manually fixed and protected from MRP rescheduling; 'converted' indicates fully converted to a production order or purchase requisition; 'partially_converted' indicates a split conversion; 'deleted' indicates cancelled.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Planned Order Status (PLAF-KZFIR, PLAF-KZUMW)

description: SAP storage location code where the finished goods produced under this planned order will be received into inventory upon completion. Supports warehouse and inventory management integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/WM Module - Storage Location (T001L-LGORT)

description: Base unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, PC for piece, L for liter). Aligned with the material master base UoM in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Unit of Measure (T006-MSEHI); ISO 80000

description: Identifier of the primary work center or production resource assigned to execute this planned order. Used for capacity requirements planning (CRP) and shop floor scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Work Center (CRHD-ARBPL)

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a production confirmation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module – Order Confirmation (CO11N/CO15)

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: production_confirmation references the batch produced during the confirmed operation. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-leve
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Finished Goods Storage Location Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Production confirmations specify where finished goods are received into inventory. Warehouse systems use this for automatic putaway task generation.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Production confirmations are assigned to fiscal periods for period-end closing, work-in-process valuation, and cost of goods manufactured reporting. Controllers use this for monthly financial close.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.goods_issue. Business justification: Production confirmations trigger or reference goods issue documents when finished goods are transferred to fulfillment. Warehouse and production teams reconcile production output against goods issue r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: When a production confirmation is posted, it triggers or references an inspection lot for goods-receipt or in-process quality check. This is a standard SAP PP-QM integration used in every manufacturin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.measurement_reading. Business justification: When confirming production output, operators record machine counter or meter readings (e.g., press strokes, spindle hours) that update asset measurement points. This link drives usage-based maintenanc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: Production confirmations record that output was verified against a specific product specification revision. Quality inspectors use this link to confirm which spec version governed the production run —
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: production_confirmation is posted for a specific routing_operation. The FK routing_operation_id enables joins to routing_operation master data for operation-level confirmation analysis and standard ti
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: production_confirmation is posted for a specific shift. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift string reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level confirmation analysis and labor tim
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.wip_stock. Business justification: Production confirmations update WIP stock levels as operations complete. Each confirmation reduces or transfers WIP inventory, requiring a direct reference to the WIP stock record being consumed or tr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: production_confirmation is posted at a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id enables joins to work_center master data for confirmation analysis and capacity utilization reporting. work_center_na
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual manufacturing cost settled to the production order upon confirmation, including material, labor, machine, and overhead components. Used for actual vs. standard cost variance analysis and COGS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Actual Cost Settlement (KSTAR); IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330; COGS

description: Actual date and time when the confirmed operation was completed on the shop floor. Combined with actual start timestamp to derive actual cycle time and compare against takt time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Actual Finish Date/Time (IEDD/IETD); Siemens Opcenter MES; OEE

description: Actual labor time in minutes consumed during the confirmed operation. Used for labor cost settlement, workforce productivity analysis, and comparison against standard labor time from the routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO – Actual Labor Time (ISARB); Kronos Workforce Central; GAAP ASC 330

description: Actual machine runtime in minutes consumed during the confirmed operation. Used for OEE performance rate calculation, machine utilization reporting, and actual vs. standard cost variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Actual Machine Time (ISMNW); Siemens Opcenter MES; OEE

description: Actual changeover/setup time in minutes recorded for the confirmed operation. Used for SMED analysis, OEE availability calculation, and identification of changeover reduction opportunities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Actual Setup Time (ISRST); Siemens Opcenter MES; SMED; OEE

description: Actual date and time when the confirmed operation began on the shop floor. Used to calculate actual cycle time, machine utilization, and OEE availability rate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Actual Start Date/Time (ISDD/ISTD); Siemens Opcenter MES; OEE

description: Manufacturing batch or lot number assigned to the confirmed production output. Critical for batch traceability, quality inspection lot linkage, and regulatory compliance (e.g., serialization, REACH/RoHS).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/QM – Batch (CHARG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; EU RoHS/REACH

description: Quantity of good units successfully produced and confirmed against the production order operation. Represents the yield posted to inventory. Used for OEE quality rate, throughput, and order completion percentage calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Confirmed Yield Quantity (LMNGA); Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant where the confirmation was posted. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting (e.g., CE Marking, RoHS, REACH, OSHA) and regional analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the actual cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency cost reporting for multinational manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI – Currency (WAERS)

description: SAP material document number generated by the inventory posting (goods receipt) triggered by this production confirmation. Provides the audit trail link between the confirmation event and the resulting stock and accounting entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Document (MBLNR); IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Indicates whether the goods receipt (inventory posting from WIP to finished goods) was triggered automatically upon confirmation posting, as configured in the production order control key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Auto GR Indicator (AUTOM); SAP PP Control Key

description: Indicates whether this confirmation is the final posting for the production order operation, triggering order status change to 'Technically Completed' (TECO) in SAP PP and closing WIP balances.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Final Confirmation Indicator (SCHLW); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Short description of the material being confirmed, as maintained in the SAP material master. Supports self-contained reporting without requiring a material master lookup.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Description (MAKTX)

description: SAP material master number of the finished or semi-finished product being confirmed. Links the confirmation to the product catalog, BOM, and costing records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Master (MATNR); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: SAP goods movement type code associated with the inventory posting triggered by this confirmation (e.g., 101 = GR for production order, 531 = GR for by-product). Determines the accounting entries and stock update logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Movement Type (BWART); SAP PP Goods Receipt

description: Reference to the Non-Conformance Report raised against this confirmation when scrap or rework quantities are posted. Triggers CAPA workflow and feeds the QMS for defect tracking and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM – NCR (QMEL); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA; PFMEA

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the production confirmation, as assigned by SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES. Used for cross-system traceability and operator reference on the shop floor.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Confirmation Number (AFRU-RUECK); Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Routing operation sequence number (e.g., 0010, 0020) within the production order for which this confirmation is posted. Identifies the specific manufacturing step confirmed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Routing Operation (VORNR); Siemens Opcenter MES

description: SAP plant identifier representing the manufacturing facility where the production confirmation was posted. Enables multi-plant production reporting and cross-site OEE benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Plant (WERKS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Accounting-relevant date on which the goods movement and cost settlement are posted to the general ledger. May differ from the confirmation timestamp when backdating is applied for period-end closing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO – Posting Date (BUDAT); IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Reference to the production order against which this confirmation is posted. Links shop floor execution output back to the originating manufacturing order in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Order (AUFNR); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Manufacturing strategy under which the confirmed production order was executed: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Drives demand linkage and inventory valuation logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Planning Strategy (STRAT); MRP II; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1

description: SAP QM inspection lot number automatically created upon posting of the production confirmation. Links the confirmed output to the quality inspection process for usage decision and AQL-based sampling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM – Inspection Lot (PRUEFLOS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; AQL

description: Quantity of units identified as requiring rework during the confirmed operation. Drives rework order creation and feeds cost-of-poor-quality (COPQ) reporting and CAPA processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/QM – Rework Quantity; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA

description: Quantity of units scrapped during the confirmed operation. Triggers scrap cost postings and feeds scrap rate KPI reporting. Used in Six Sigma and Lean Manufacturing waste analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Scrap Quantity (RMNGA); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Six Sigma

description: Manufacturing shift during which the production confirmation was recorded (e.g., Day, Afternoon, Night, Shift-A). Used for shift-level OEE analysis, throughput reporting, and labor cost allocation.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES – Shift Management; Kronos Workforce Central

description: Originating operational system from which the production confirmation record was extracted (e.g., SAP_PP, OPCENTER_MES, MANUAL). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements in the Silver Layer.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 – Audit and Accountability; GDPR Article 5(1)(f)

description: Current processing status of the production confirmation record. 'Posted' indicates successful goods movement; 'Reversed' indicates a corrective reversal has been applied; 'Blocked' indicates a hold pending quality or variance review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – System Status (JEST); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: SAP storage location to which confirmed finished goods or semi-finished goods are transferred upon posting. Drives inventory placement in WMS and supports ATP/CTP calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/WM – Storage Location (LGORT); Infor WMS

description: Date and time at which the production confirmation was posted in the MES or ERP system. Serves as the authoritative completion event timestamp for WIP-to-finished-goods inventory postings and actual cost settlement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Confirmation Date/Time (BUDAT/UZEIT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether this confirmation represents a partial quantity posting, a final completion posting, a reversal of a prior confirmation, or a milestone-based confirmation. Drives order status transitions in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Confirmation Type (AUTYP); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Base unit of measure for confirmed, scrap, and rework quantities (e.g., EA, KG, L, M). Aligns with SAP material master base UoM to ensure consistent inventory and cost postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Base Unit of Measure (MEINS); ISO 80000-1

description: Coded reason explaining deviations from planned quantities, times, or costs (e.g., machine breakdown, material shortage, quality rejection). Feeds variance analysis in SAP CO and supports CAPA and root cause analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO – Variance Reason (GRUND); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA

description: Free-text description providing additional context for the variance reason code. Captures operator or supervisor notes on deviations for root cause analysis and continuous improvement initiatives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO – Variance Reason Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Kaizen

description: Identifier of the work center (machine, production line, or cell) where the confirmed operation was executed. Used for capacity utilization, OEE calculation, and cost center allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Work Center (ARBPL); Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each production schedule record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for the production_schedule data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: production_schedule references a batch for batch-managed production planning. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-level schedu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_forecast. Business justification: Master production scheduling uses sales forecasts to plan capacity and material requirements for make-to-stock items. Production planners align manufacturing schedules with anticipated demand from sal
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Production schedules are built to satisfy fulfillment plans that aggregate customer order commitments. Master schedulers align production schedules directly to fulfillment plans to ensure on-time deli
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.planned_order. Business justification: production_schedule is built from MRP-generated planned_orders. The FK planned_order_id normalizes the planned_order_number string reference and enables joins to planned_order master data for schedule
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: production_schedule references a routing for scheduling purposes. The FK routing_id normalizes the routing_number string reference and enables joins to routing master data for capacity planning and sc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: Production schedules specify which shift executes the work. Shift supervisors use this for crew assignments and labor planning.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: production_schedule is planned for a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id normalizes the work_center_code reference and enables joins to work_center master data for capacity load analysis and s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Indicates whether this production schedule entry contributes to Available to Promise (ATP) stock calculations in SAP S/4HANA SD. When true, the planned production quantity is included in ATP/CTP commitment logic for the order domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD ATP; SAP PP; APICS CPIM

description: Batch or lot number assigned to the production run under this schedule entry, used for batch traceability in regulated or quality-controlled manufacturing environments. Aligns with SAP batch management and QM module requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice)

description: Percentage of the work center's available capacity consumed by this production schedule entry. A value of 100 indicates full utilization; values above 100 indicate overload conditions requiring rescheduling or capacity leveling in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Planning; MRP II; APICS CPIM

description: Quantity confirmed as feasible by capacity planning and resource availability checks in SAP PP or Siemens Opcenter MES. May differ from planned quantity when capacity constraints exist. Used for Capable to Promise (CTP) commitments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Planning; CTP; APICS CPIM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant location where this production schedule is executed. Supports multinational reporting, regulatory compliance tracking (CE, RoHS, REACH), and regional capacity analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS Directive; REACH Regulation

description: Date and time when this production schedule record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA PP or Siemens Opcenter MES). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP S/4HANA; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the plant's local currency, used when cost estimates or standard cost values are associated with this production schedule entry in SAP CO.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS; GAAP

description: Planned cycle time in seconds for producing one unit at the assigned work center under this schedule. Used to validate feasibility against takt time targets and to calculate total scheduled duration for capacity planning.
reference: Lean Manufacturing; Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; APICS CPIM

description: Date up to which the production schedule is frozen and protected from automatic MRP II rescheduling. Orders within the freeze horizon require manual authorization to change, ensuring shop floor stability and preventing nervousness in the plan.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP II Planning Time Fence; APICS CPIM

description: Date and time of the most recent update to this production schedule record in the source system. Used for change detection in the Databricks Silver Layer incremental load process and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP S/4HANA; GDPR Article 5

description: Short description of the material or finished good to be produced, as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable context for schedule review and shop floor communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: SAP S/4HANA material master number identifying the finished good or semi-finished material to be produced under this schedule. Used to link to BOM, routing, and MRP planning data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: SAP MRP controller code or name responsible for managing the material's planning parameters and reviewing the generated production schedule. Used for accountability and exception management in the planning process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP; APICS CPIM

description: Date on which the MRP II planning run was executed in SAP S/4HANA that generated or last updated this production schedule. Provides auditability of the planning cycle and enables comparison between planning runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP II; APICS CPIM

description: Descriptive human-readable name for the production schedule, used for identification in planning dashboards and MES scheduling boards (e.g., 'Week 32 Motor Assembly Plan - Plant DE01').
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the production schedule, used for cross-system referencing between SAP S/4HANA PP and Siemens Opcenter MES. Typically follows a plant-year-sequence format.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage set for the work center during this schedule period. OEE combines availability, performance, and quality rates. Used as a KPI baseline for shop floor performance monitoring via Siemens MindSphere and Opcenter Analytics.
reference: OEE Industry Standard; SEMI E10; Siemens MindSphere; ISO 22400-2 KPIs for Manufacturing

description: Estimated total production cost for this schedule entry in the plant's local currency, derived from SAP CO standard cost estimates. Includes material, labor, and overhead components. Used for budget planning and COGS analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Product Costing; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Reference to the MRP-generated planned order in SAP S/4HANA PP that this schedule entry is derived from. Enables traceability from the production schedule back to the originating MRP II planning run and demand signal.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planned Orders; MRP II

description: Total quantity of the material planned for production within this schedule entry's time bucket and work center. Expressed in the material's base unit of measure as defined in the SAP material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; MRP II; APICS CPIM

description: The last calendar date of the planning horizon covered by this production schedule. Defines the end boundary for MRP II capacity planning and scheduled production orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP II; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: The first calendar date of the planning horizon covered by this production schedule. Defines the start boundary for MRP II capacity planning and scheduled production orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP II; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: SAP S/4HANA plant identifier representing the manufacturing facility for which this production schedule is generated. Aligns with the organizational unit in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES plant configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015

description: Numeric priority ranking assigned to this production schedule entry for shop floor sequencing. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by Siemens Opcenter MES to sequence competing orders at shared work centers.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA PP; APICS CPIM

description: Manufacturing execution strategy governing how production orders are triggered. Make to Order (MTO) produces against customer orders; Make to Stock (MTS) produces to replenish inventory; Engineer to Order (ETO) requires custom engineering; Assemble to Order (ATO) assembles from standard components on demand.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge; ISO 9001:2015

description: Date and time when the production schedule status was changed to 'released', authorizing shop floor execution. Captured for audit trail and compliance with ISO 9001 documented information requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Reference to the SAP PP routing (Bill of Process) that defines the sequence of operations, work centers, and standard times for producing the scheduled material. Ensures the schedule is aligned with the approved manufacturing process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; Bill of Process (BoP); Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Planned calendar date on which production for this schedule entry is expected to be completed at the assigned work center. Drives delivery date commitments and ATP/CTP calculations for the order domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES; APICS CPIM

description: Precise date and time at which production is scheduled to complete, including shift and time-zone context. Used by Siemens Opcenter MES for detailed shop floor sequencing and completion tracking.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 8601

description: Planned calendar date on which production for this schedule entry is expected to begin at the assigned work center. Used for shop floor sequencing and ATP/CTP date commitments to the order domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES; APICS CPIM

description: Precise date and time at which production is scheduled to begin, including shift and time-zone context. Used by Siemens Opcenter MES for detailed shop floor sequencing and takt time alignment.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 8601

description: Scheduling direction applied during MRP II planning. Forward scheduling calculates the earliest possible completion date from a start date; backward scheduling calculates the latest start date from a required delivery date; mixed applies both depending on order priority.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Scheduling; APICS CPIM; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Shift configuration planned for this production schedule entry, defining the number of operating shifts per day. Drives available capacity calculation and Kronos Workforce Central labor scheduling alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Kronos Workforce Central; APICS CPIM

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this production schedule record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when multiple systems contribute schedule data to the Silver Layer.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Architecture; SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Current lifecycle status of the production schedule. Draft indicates initial planning; released means approved for shop floor execution; frozen means locked against changes; in_execution means active on the shop floor; completed means all planned orders fulfilled; cancelled means schedule voided.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015

description: Target takt time in seconds for this production schedule entry, representing the rate at which products must be completed to meet customer demand. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Used for line balancing and OEE target setting.
reference: Lean Manufacturing; APICS CPIM; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015

description: Granularity of the planning time bucket used in this schedule. Daily buckets are used for short-horizon detailed scheduling; weekly for medium-term MRP II planning; monthly for long-range capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP II; APICS CPIM; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Base unit of measure for the planned production quantity, aligned with the SAP material master UoM definition (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, PC for piece). Ensures consistent quantity interpretation across MES and ERP systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; ANSI

description: Incremental version counter for the production schedule. Each re-planning or revision cycle increments this value, enabling version history tracking and comparison of planning iterations under MRP II.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Identifier of the work center (machine group, production line, or cell) to which this schedule entry is assigned. Corresponds to the SAP PP work center and Siemens Opcenter MES resource definition used for capacity load calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES Resource Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each shop order operation execution record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP PP Operation Confirmation; Siemens Opcenter MES Operation Record

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: shop_order_operation processes a specific batch on the shop floor. The FK batch_id normalizes the batch_number string reference and enables joins to batch master data for batch-level operation traceab
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the specific machine or equipment asset assigned to execute this operation. Links to the equipment master in Maximo EAM and Siemens MindSphere for OEE tracking, predictive maintenance, and asset utilization reporting.
reference: Maximo EAM Asset Record; Siemens MindSphere Asset Model; ISO 22400-2 OEE KPIs

description: Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Each shop order operation incurs costs tracked in production_order_cost. Cost controllers link operational execution to financial cost objects daily to capture actual labor, machine, and overhead cost
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: shop_order_operation executes operations defined in a routing. The FK routing_id normalizes the routing_number string reference and enables joins to routing master data for operation sequence validati
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: shop_order_operation executes a specific routing_operation step on the shop floor. The FK routing_operation_id creates a direct relational link between execution records and their corresponding routin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: shop_order_operation is executed during a specific shift. The FK shift_id normalizes the shift_code reference and enables joins to shift master data for shift-level productivity analysis, labor cost a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.tooling_equipment. Business justification: Shop order operations specify which engineering-defined tooling or equipment must be used. Process engineers define tooling requirements; production dispatchers assign them to operations. Critical for
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.wip_stock. Business justification: Each shop order operation consumes or produces WIP inventory at intermediate routing steps. Production control links operations to WIP stock records to track in-process material quantities across the
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: shop_order_operation executes at a specific work_center. The FK work_center_id normalizes the work_center_code reference and enables joins to work_center master data for capacity analysis, OEE attribu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.work_order_operation. Business justification: Production operations on a shop order sometimes require a concurrent maintenance work order operation (e.g., tooling change, calibration mid-run). Planners link these to coordinate production and main
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual date and time when the operation execution was completed on the shop floor, as recorded by the MES or operator confirmation. Used for cycle time calculation, throughput analysis, and OEE performance measurement.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Operation Complete Event; SAP PP Confirmation (CO11N); ISO 22400-2

description: Actual direct labor time in minutes consumed by the operator(s) for this operation. Used for actual cost confirmation in SAP CO, labor efficiency variance analysis, and workforce productivity reporting.
reference: SAP PP Confirmation Labor Time (CO11N); Kronos Workforce Central; ISO 22400-2

description: Actual machine or process run time in minutes for this operation, excluding setup and teardown. Core input for OEE performance rate calculation and cycle time variance analysis against standard run time.
reference: SAP PP Confirmation Machine Time; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 22400-2 OEE Performance Rate

description: Actual time in minutes spent on machine/tooling setup before production run began. Used for SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Dies) analysis, changeover optimization, and OEE availability loss calculation.
reference: SAP PP Confirmation Setup Time; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 22400-2 OEE; SMED Methodology

description: Actual date and time when the operation execution began on the shop floor, as recorded by the MES or operator confirmation. Used for cycle time calculation, schedule adherence, and OEE availability analysis.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Operation Start Event; SAP PP Confirmation (CO11N); ISO 22400-2

description: Production batch or lot number associated with the material being processed in this operation. Critical for batch traceability, genealogy tracking, and regulatory compliance in industries requiring lot-level control (e.g., serialized components).
reference: SAP MM Batch Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2 Identification and Traceability; GS1 Traceability Standard

description: SAP PP operation confirmation document number (CO11N confirmation number) generated when the operation is confirmed in the ERP system. Provides the audit trail link between the MES execution record and the ERP financial posting.
reference: SAP PP Operation Confirmation (CO11N); SAP CO Actual Cost Posting

description: SAP PP routing control key that governs the scheduling, confirmation, and costing behavior of this operation (e.g., PP01 for internally processed, PP03 for milestone confirmation). Determines whether the operation is relevant for scheduling, capacity planning, and cost confirmation.
reference: SAP PP Routing Control Key (CA01); SAP PP Configuration

description: Timestamp when this shop order operation record was first created in the source system (MES or ERP). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Actual cycle time in seconds measured for this operation execution, calculated from actual start to actual finish divided by yield quantity. Compared against takt time and standard cycle time for OEE performance rate and lean analysis.
reference: ISO 22400-2 Cycle Time; Lean Manufacturing; OEE Performance Rate Calculation

description: Total unplanned downtime in minutes recorded during this operation execution (e.g., machine breakdown, material shortage, quality stop). Core input for OEE availability rate calculation and downtime root cause analysis.
reference: ISO 22400-2 OEE Availability; Siemens MindSphere Asset Monitoring; Maximo EAM Downtime Tracking

description: Coded classification of the primary downtime cause during this operation (e.g., MECH_FAILURE, TOOLING, MATERIAL, QUALITY_HOLD, CHANGEOVER, PLANNED_MAINT). Used for Pareto analysis of downtime losses and TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) improvement programs.
reference: ISO 22400-2 OEE Loss Categories; TPM Methodology; Siemens Opcenter MES Downtime Tracking; CMMS

description: Coded reason for placing this operation in 'held' status (e.g., QUALITY_HOLD, MATERIAL_SHORTAGE, SAFETY_STOP, ENGINEERING_REVIEW). Populated when status is 'held'. Supports root cause analysis and downtime categorization.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Hold Management; SAP PP Order Hold; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; ANDON System

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this shop order operation record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: SAP material number of the finished or semi-finished product being produced by this operation. Enables product-level OEE, yield, and scrap analysis across production orders.
reference: SAP MM Material Master (MM01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2 Identification and Traceability

description: Indicates whether a non-conformance (NC) was detected during this operation. When true, triggers NCR (Non-Conformance Report) creation in the QMS and may initiate a hold on the production order. Key quality gate indicator.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs; SAP QM; Siemens Opcenter MES Quality; NCR Process

description: Reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised against this operation when nc_flag is true. Provides direct traceability to the quality management system record for CAPA and disposition tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Notification; CAPA Process; APQP

description: Human-readable description of the manufacturing operation being performed (e.g., 'CNC Milling', 'Weld Assembly', 'Final Inspection'). Sourced from the routing master in SAP PP or Opcenter MES operation definition.
reference: SAP PP Routing; Siemens Opcenter MES Operation Definition

description: Sequential operation number within the production routing (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Corresponds to the routing operation sequence in SAP PP and Opcenter MES, defining the order of execution steps within a production order.
reference: SAP PP Routing Operation; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5 Production and Service Provision

description: Classification of the operation by its functional role in the manufacturing process. Distinguishes between value-adding processing steps, setup/teardown activities, quality inspection, rework, transport, and wait states for OEE and lean analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Lean Manufacturing Value Stream Mapping (VSM) Principles

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this operation is executed. Supports multi-plant, multinational production reporting and cost allocation by plant.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Plant Master (MM); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1 Organizational Context

description: Business-visible production order number (SAP PP order number or Opcenter MES work order number) to which this operation belongs. Provides the primary traceability link between the operation execution record and the parent production order.
reference: SAP PP Production Order (CO01); Siemens Opcenter MES Work Order

description: Manufacturing strategy under which this operation is executed: Make-to-Order (MTO), Make-to-Stock (MTS), Engineer-to-Order (ETO), or Assemble-to-Order (ATO). Drives scheduling priority, cost allocation, and customer order linkage rules.
reference: SAP PP Planning Strategy; MRP II; ISO 22400-1 Manufacturing Operations Management

description: Unit of measure for yield, scrap, and rework quantities (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter, L for liter). Sourced from the SAP MM material master base unit of measure.
reference: SAP MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; ANSI X12

description: Quantity of units requiring rework (reprocessing to meet specification) identified during this operation. Distinct from scrap as units are recoverable. Used for rework cost tracking, quality trend analysis, and CAPA initiation.
reference: SAP PP Rework Confirmation; Siemens Opcenter MES Quality; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA Process

description: Identifier of the manufacturing routing (Bill of Process / BoP) from which this operation was derived. Links the actual execution back to the planned routing master for variance analysis and process improvement.
reference: SAP PP Routing Master (CA01); Siemens Teamcenter Bill of Process (BoP)

description: Planned finish date and time for this operation as determined by the production scheduler. Used for on-time delivery tracking, schedule adherence KPIs, and downstream operation sequencing.
reference: SAP PP/DS Scheduling; Siemens Opcenter Advanced Planning and Scheduling (APS)

description: Planned start date and time for this operation as determined by the production scheduler (SAP PP/DS or Opcenter Scheduling). Used for schedule adherence measurement and capacity planning variance analysis.
reference: SAP PP/DS Scheduling; Siemens Opcenter Advanced Planning and Scheduling (APS)

description: Quantity of units scrapped (irreversibly rejected) during this operation. Feeds OEE quality rate calculation, scrap cost reporting in SAP CO, and quality management CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) processes.
reference: SAP PP Scrap Confirmation (CO11N); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs; ISO 22400-2

description: Serial number of the specific unit being processed in this operation, applicable for serialized production (e.g., ETO or high-value assemblies). Enables unit-level traceability from manufacturing through field service.
reference: SAP MM Serial Number Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; GS1 Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC)

description: Production shift during which this operation was executed (e.g., DAY, AFTERNOON, NIGHT, WEEKEND). Used for shift-level OEE analysis, labor cost allocation by shift, and production performance benchmarking across shifts.
reference: SAP PP Shift Planning; Kronos Workforce Central Shift Definition; ISO 22400-2

description: Identifies the originating operational system of record for this operation record (Siemens Opcenter MES, SAP PP, or manual entry). Supports data lineage, reconciliation between MES and ERP, and silver layer data quality management.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; SAP PP; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Indicates whether a Statistical Process Control (SPC) control chart violation (e.g., out-of-control signal) was detected during this operation. Triggers quality alert and potential hold in the MES quality module.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; SPC Methodology (Western Electric Rules); Siemens Opcenter MES Quality Analytics; Six Sigma

description: Planned or standard run time in minutes for this operation as defined in the routing master. Used as the baseline for cycle time variance, OEE performance rate, and manufacturing cost standard costing.
reference: SAP PP Routing Standard Values; ISO 22400-2 Planned Cycle Time

description: Current execution status of the shop order operation on the shop floor. Drives WIP tracking, MES workflow routing, and production scheduling visibility. 'Held' indicates a quality or safety stop; 'partially_confirmed' indicates a mid-operation confirmation in SAP PP.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Operation Status; SAP PP Order Confirmation (CO11N); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Planned takt time in seconds for this operation, representing the rate at which products must be completed to meet customer demand. Used for line balancing, capacity planning, and lean manufacturing analysis.
reference: Lean Manufacturing Takt Time Principle; ISO 22400-2; VSM (Value Stream Mapping)

description: Identifier of the work center (machine group, production cell, or cost center) where this operation is executed. Maps to the SAP PP Work Center master and Opcenter MES Resource. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and cost allocation.
reference: SAP PP Work Center (CR01); Siemens Opcenter MES Resource; ISO 22400 KPI for Manufacturing Operations

description: Quantity of conforming (good) units produced by this operation that pass to the next step or are completed. Core metric for OEE quality rate, first-pass yield (FPY), and production order confirmation in SAP PP.
reference: SAP PP Yield Confirmation (CO11N); Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 22400-2 OEE Quality Rate; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the CAPA record within the Quality Management System.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_hierarchy. Business justification: CAPAs are frequently initiated in response to customer complaints or audit findings. Linking CAPA to the customer account enables tracking of corrective actions per customer and supports 8D/customer-f
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: CAPA actions incur real costs (rework, containment, supplier audits). Finance allocates these costs to a cost center or project via cost_allocation. Controllers use this link to report quality-related
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.defect_code. Business justification: CAPAs are initiated for specific defect types and should reference the standardized defect code master. This FK links the CAPA to the authoritative defect classification, enabling PPM analysis and def
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.downtime_event. Business justification: CAPAs are frequently initiated in response to equipment downtime events that caused quality escapes. Quality engineers link CAPA records to the originating downtime event to drive root cause analysis
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: Corrective actions often require engineering changes to resolve quality issues. CAPA systems link to ECNs when design modifications are needed to prevent recurrence.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: CAPA corrective actions may trigger engineering change orders to implement design improvements. Quality teams track ECO implementation as part of CAPA closure verification.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: CAPAs are frequently triggered by asset failures. Quality engineers link a CAPA directly to the asset failure record that initiated the corrective action — standard in RCA-driven quality systems and r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: CAPAs are frequently initiated from failed inspection lots. This FK provides direct traceability from the corrective action record back to the triggering inspection event. The existing source_referenc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Corrective and preventive actions are frequently issued to suppliers for recurring defects. Procurement tracks open CAPAs per supplier as part of supplier development programs and qualification review
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.work_order_operation. Business justification: Corrective actions in a CAPA often result in or reference a specific maintenance work order operation performed on equipment. Linking these allows quality and maintenance teams to confirm physical cor
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or employee ID of the individual responsible for implementing the corrective or preventive action and ensuring timely closure of the CAPA.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Notification

description: Department or organizational unit of the CAPA action owner, used for workload distribution, escalation routing, and departmental quality performance reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Date on which the CAPA was formally closed after successful verification of effectiveness, used to measure cycle time and on-time closure performance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Human-readable description of the affected part, component, or material to provide context without requiring a lookup.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Part number or SKU of the product, component, or material directly affected by the nonconformance or quality issue.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP MM Module

description: Name or code of the manufacturing process, work center, or production stage where the nonconformance was identified or originated.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Bill of Process (BoP)

description: Name or employee ID of the quality manager or authorized approver who reviewed and formally approved the CAPA record and action plan.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3; SAP QM Workflow

description: Date on which the CAPA record and corrective action plan were formally approved by the authorized quality manager.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Detailed description of the corrective or preventive actions planned and implemented to eliminate the root cause and prevent recurrence of the nonconformance.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where the CAPA originated, supporting multinational regulatory compliance reporting.
Indicates whether the customer must be formally notified of the nonconformance and the corrective actions taken, as required by customer-specific requirements or SLA agreements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.1; IATF 16949 Customer-Specific Requirements

description: Date on which the customer was formally notified of the nonconformance and the corrective actions being taken.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.1; SLA Management

description: Formal assessment of whether the implemented corrective or preventive action successfully eliminated the root cause and prevented recurrence of the nonconformance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2.1(e); IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Description of the short-term containment actions taken to prevent further nonconforming product from reaching the customer or next process stage while the root cause is investigated.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; AIAG 8D Step D3

description: Name or employee ID of the person who initiated and raised the CAPA record in the Quality Management System.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Module

description: Date on which the CAPA was formally initiated and entered into the Quality Management System.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Module

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the CAPA record, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking for ISO 9001 compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5

description: Human-readable business reference number for the CAPA record, used in communications, reports, and audit trails. Follows the format CAPA-YYYY-NNNNNN.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Module

description: Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the CAPA originated, supporting multi-site quality management and regional reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1; SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure

description: Business priority level assigned to the CAPA based on risk severity, customer impact, regulatory exposure, or recurrence frequency.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; FMEA Risk Priority Number (RPN)

description: Detailed description of the quality problem, nonconformance, or potential risk being addressed, including what, where, when, and extent of the issue.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; 8D Problem Solving Methodology

description: Reference number of the new CAPA raised if the original nonconformance recurred, enabling traceability of recurring quality issues and escalation chains.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Indicates whether the same or similar nonconformance has recurred after the CAPA was closed, triggering escalation and re-opening of the quality action.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Name of the regulatory authority or certification body to which the nonconformance must be reported (e.g., OSHA, EPA, UL, CE, RoHS authority).
reference: ISO 9001:2015; OSHA; EPA; UL; CE Marking; RoHS; REACH

description: Indicates whether the nonconformance or corrective action requires formal notification to a regulatory body such as OSHA, EPA, or a product certification authority.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; OSHA; EPA; CE Marking; RoHS; REACH

description: Updated target closure date if the original target was extended due to complexity, resource constraints, or scope changes, with justification tracked separately.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Categorization of the root cause using the 6M Ishikawa framework: Man, Machine, Material, Method, Measurement, or Environment, enabling systemic trend analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Ishikawa Diagram; Six Sigma DMAIC

description: Detailed narrative of the identified root cause(s) of the nonconformance as determined through the root cause analysis methodology.
Methodology used to identify the root cause of the nonconformance, such as 5-Why analysis, Ishikawa (fishbone) diagram, Fault Tree Analysis, or 8D problem solving.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2.1; AIAG 8D; PFMEA

description: Reference number of the originating document or record that triggered the CAPA (e.g., NCR number, audit finding ID, customer complaint number).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Identifies the originating trigger for the CAPA, such as a Non-Conformance Report (NCR), customer complaint, audit finding, Statistical Process Control (SPC) alert, supplier defect, or field failure.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; APQP; PPAP

description: Current workflow status of the CAPA record, tracking progression from initiation through root cause analysis, action planning, implementation, verification, and closure.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Module

description: Planned date by which all corrective or preventive actions must be implemented and the CAPA formally closed, used for SLA tracking and escalation management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SLA Management

description: Short descriptive title summarizing the nonconformance or quality issue that triggered the CAPA.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Classifies the action as corrective (addressing an existing nonconformance), preventive (addressing a potential nonconformance), or improvement (proactive quality enhancement).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949

description: Date on which the effectiveness of the corrective or preventive action was formally verified and documented.
Description or reference to objective evidence (inspection records, audit results, SPC charts, production data) demonstrating the effectiveness of the implemented actions.
Method used to verify that the implemented corrective or preventive action was effective in eliminating the root cause and preventing recurrence.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2.1(e); IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Name or employee ID of the quality engineer or auditor who performed and approved the effectiveness verification of the CAPA.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each customer complaint record in the Quality Management System.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 – Nonconformity and Corrective Action

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
