reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date on which the complaint was formally closed after customer acceptance of the resolution, used for SLA compliance measurement and cycle time analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Case Closed Date

description: Production batch or lot number of the affected product, used to assess the scope of the quality issue and determine whether a batch recall or containment is required.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; SAP QM Batch Management

description: Human-readable business reference number assigned to the complaint, used for cross-system tracking and customer communication (e.g., CC-2024-000123).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Customer Complaint (QM-QN)

description: Classification of the complaint by its nature and origin, distinguishing field failures, warranty claims, OEM partner complaints, safety concerns, and regulatory issues.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2

description: Description of the immediate containment action taken to protect the customer from further exposure to the defect, corresponding to 8D Discipline 3 (D3).
reference: 8D Problem Solving D3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Date on which the containment action was fully implemented and verified, used to measure response speed and SLA compliance for immediate customer protection.
reference: 8D Problem Solving D3; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Description of the permanent corrective action implemented to eliminate the root cause and prevent recurrence, corresponding to 8D D5/D6.
reference: 8D Problem Solving D5/D6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA Process

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the customer's location where the failure was reported, used for regional quality trend analysis and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 4; REACH/RoHS Compliance

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the financial impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP), supporting multi-currency financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; GAAP; SAP FI Currency Configuration

description: Indicates whether the customer has formally accepted and approved the complaint resolution before the record is closed, ensuring customer sign-off is captured.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3; 8D D8

description: The complaint or claim reference number assigned by the customer or OEM partner in their own system, used for cross-referencing during resolution communications.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.1; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Customer's satisfaction rating provided at complaint closure, measuring the effectiveness of the resolution process and informing Net Promoter Score (NPS) and customer retention analytics.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2; NPS Framework; Salesforce Service Cloud CSAT

description: High-level category of the defect type, used for trend analysis, FMEA alignment, and quality reporting across product lines.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2

description: Current step in the 8 Disciplines (8D) structured problem-solving process, tracking progression from team formation through root cause analysis to preventive action and closure.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 10.2; Ford 8D Problem Solving Methodology; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Date on which the product failure or quality issue was first observed or experienced by the customer, used for warranty age calculation and field reliability analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Detailed narrative description of the failure or quality issue as reported by the customer, including observed symptoms, conditions of failure, and customer impact.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; 8D Problem Solving – D2 Problem Description

description: Estimated or actual financial cost associated with the complaint, including warranty claims, field returns, rework, scrap, and customer penalties. Used for Cost of Poor Quality (COPQ) reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IFRS; GAAP; Cost of Poor Quality (COPQ)

description: Engineering revision level of the affected part at the time of the complaint, critical for determining whether the issue is design-related and for ECN/ECO traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management

description: Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the affected product was produced, used for plant-level quality performance analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; SAP PP Plant Code

description: Business priority level assigned to the complaint based on safety impact, customer significance, regulatory exposure, and production disruption risk.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Number of units reported as defective or non-conforming in the customer complaint, used for PPM calculation and scope assessment.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 PPM Reporting

description: Unit of measure for the complained quantity (e.g., EA for each, PC for pieces, KG for kilograms), aligned with SAP UoM configuration.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP MM Unit of Measure

description: Date on which the complaint was formally received and registered in the Quality Management System, used for SLA tracking and response time measurement.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Case Created Date

description: Indicates whether the complaint involves a safety or regulatory issue that must be reported to a governing body (e.g., OSHA, EPA, CE/RoHS authority), triggering mandatory notification workflows.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; OSHA; EPA; CE Marking; RoHS/REACH; ISO 45001

description: Name or employee ID of the quality engineer assigned as the primary owner of the complaint investigation and resolution process.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Notification Responsible Person

description: Standardized classification of the root cause type, enabling trend analysis across complaints to identify systemic quality issues by category.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949; Ishikawa / 5-Why Root Cause Analysis

description: Detailed description of the verified root cause of the failure, as determined through structured root cause analysis (e.g., 5-Why, Ishikawa), corresponding to 8D D4.
reference: 8D Problem Solving D4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3

description: Indicates whether the complaint involves a potential safety hazard to end users or operators, triggering escalated priority handling and mandatory safety review.
reference: ISO 45001:2018; OSHA; UL Product Safety; CE Marking; IEC 62443

description: Channel through which the complaint was received, enabling analysis of complaint intake patterns and customer engagement touchpoints.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud Case Origin; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which the complaint record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Salesforce CRM), supporting data lineage and integration traceability.
reference: Data Governance; SAP S/4HANA QM; Salesforce Service Cloud

description: Current lifecycle status of the customer complaint, tracking progression from initial receipt through 8D problem-solving workflow to closure.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Notification Status

description: Committed date by which the complaint must be fully resolved and closed, as agreed with the customer or defined by internal SLA policy.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2.3; SLA Agreement

description: Short descriptive title summarizing the nature of the customer complaint, used for quick identification in lists and dashboards.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Case Subject

description: Indicates whether the complained product is within the warranty period, out of warranty, or subject to a goodwill claim, driving financial liability and resolution approach.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.1; SAP SD Warranty Management; IFRS 15

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each defect code record in the enterprise quality management system. Serves as the primary key for the defect code master reference table.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 - Control of Nonconforming Outputs

description: Industry sector or product line for which this defect code is relevant (e.g., Automation Systems, Electrification, Smart Infrastructure, Transportation). Supports multi-division enterprises where defect taxonomies may differ by business unit.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1 - Understanding the Organization

description: Type of material or product to which this defect code is applicable. Restricts defect code selection during inspection to relevant material types, reducing data entry errors and improving defect data quality.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP QM Material Type Assignment

description: Name or employee ID of the quality authority (e.g., Quality Manager, Quality Engineer) who approved this defect code for use in the enterprise defect taxonomy. Supports audit trail and change control requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 - Control of Documented Information; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Date on which this defect code was formally approved by the designated quality authority. Part of the document control and change management audit trail required by ISO 9001.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 - Control of Documented Information

description: Default Acceptable Quality Level associated with this defect code for sampling inspection purposes. Defines the maximum percent defective that can be considered satisfactory as a process average for this defect type.
reference: ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 2859-1 Sampling Procedures for Inspection by Attributes; SAP QM Sampling Procedure

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether detection of this defect type mandates the initiation of a CAPA record. Automates CAPA workflow triggering in the QMS for high-severity or recurring defect types.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 - Nonconformity and Corrective Action; AIAG APQP

description: SAP QM catalog code group to which this defect code belongs. Code groups organize related defect codes within a catalog for structured selection during inspection result recording and NCR creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module - Catalog Code Group Configuration

description: SAP QM catalog type classification indicating whether this code represents a defect type (what went wrong), defect cause (why it went wrong), defect location (where on the part), or defect action (what was done). Aligns with SAP QM catalog 1/2/3/5 structure.
reference: SAP QM Catalog Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: High-level classification of the defect type based on its nature. Dimensional defects relate to out-of-tolerance measurements; surface defects include scratches, porosity, and finish issues; functional defects affect product performance; material defects relate to wrong or substandard material; assembly defects arise from incorrect assembly operations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; AIAG APQP Reference Manual; SAP QM Defect Category

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Quality Change Order that authorized the creation or modification of this defect code. Provides traceability between defect taxonomy changes and formal change management records.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6 - Control of Changes; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management

description: Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying a specific defect type within the enterprise defect taxonomy. Used across NCRs, inspection results, scrap/rework transactions, and CAPA records to ensure consistent defect classification.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Defect Code (Catalog 1)

description: Timestamp when this defect code record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking in compliance with ISO 9001 documented information requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity and Accountability

description: Indicates whether the customer must be notified when this defect type is detected on product destined for or already delivered to them. Supports SLA compliance and customer quality agreement obligations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7.2; AIAG PPAP Customer Notification Requirements

description: Reference to a customer-specific quality standard, drawing note, or specification that defines acceptance criteria for this defect type. Enables customer-specific defect taxonomy mapping for OEM and key account quality reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3 - Review of Requirements; AIAG PPAP

description: Default material disposition action recommended for nonconforming product carrying this defect code. Provides a starting point for quality engineers during usage decision processing, though final disposition may be overridden case-by-case.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7.1; SAP QM Usage Decision Configuration

business_glossary_term: Default Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Severity Rating
description: Default FMEA severity rating (1–10 scale) associated with this defect type, as defined in the enterprise FMEA reference manual. Used to pre-populate FMEA records and to prioritize defect types in Risk Priority Number (RPN) calculations.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook 1st Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 6.1

description: Severity classification of the defect based on its impact on product safety, function, and customer satisfaction. Critical defects may cause safety hazards or regulatory non-compliance; major defects significantly impair function or appearance; minor defects are slight deviations with minimal impact; incidental defects are negligible.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 AQL Standard; MIL-STD-1916

description: Grouping of related defect codes into a logical family for Pareto analysis, trend reporting, and Six Sigma improvement projects. Examples: 'Weld Defects', 'Machining Defects', 'Surface Treatment Defects'. Enables roll-up reporting across defect codes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Six Sigma DMAIC Methodology; AIAG APQP

description: Primary inspection or detection method used to identify this defect type. Informs inspection plan design, gauge selection, and SPC control strategy. Aligns with FMEA detection controls.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; AIAG FMEA Reference Manual; AIAG MSA Reference Manual

description: Corresponding defect code or classification identifier used by a customer, supplier, or external standard body. Enables cross-reference between the enterprise defect taxonomy and external quality systems for EDI, customer portal reporting, and supplier scorecards.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 - Control of Externally Provided Processes; AIAG Customer-Specific Requirements

description: Reference to the applicable ISO standard clause or section that defines or governs this defect type. Examples: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7, ISO 1101 (Geometric Tolerancing), ISO 8062 (Casting Tolerances). Supports compliance traceability.
ISO 639-1 language code for the language in which the defect code descriptions are maintained (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'zh-CN'). Supports multilingual defect taxonomy for global manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; GDPR Article 12 - Multilingual Communication

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this defect code record. Supports change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance for the quality master data management process.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Enterprise Data Governance Policy

description: Detailed narrative description of the defect, including observable characteristics, typical manifestation, and distinguishing features. Used for inspector training, FMEA documentation, and CAPA root cause analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; AIAG FMEA Reference Manual

description: Indicates whether detection of this defect type during inspection should automatically trigger creation of a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) in the QMS without manual intervention. Supports automated quality workflow execution.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Defect Recording Configuration; Siemens Opcenter MES Quality

description: Comma-separated list or wildcard pattern of plant codes where this defect code is valid and available for use. Supports multi-plant enterprises where certain defect types are specific to particular manufacturing sites or technologies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Plant-Level Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Relevant Flag
description: Indicates whether this defect type is relevant to PPAP submission requirements. PPAP-relevant defects must be addressed in the control plan and may require customer approval before production release.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Maximum acceptable defect occurrence rate expressed in Parts Per Million (PPM) for this defect type. Used to set quality targets, trigger CAPA escalation, and evaluate supplier quality performance against contractual PPM commitments.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; AIAG Customer-Specific Requirements; Six Sigma Quality Targets

description: Manufacturing or supply chain process stage at which this defect type is typically detected or originates. Used to route inspection findings to the correct process owner and to focus PFMEA and SPC controls at the right stage.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; AIAG PFMEA Reference Manual; Siemens Opcenter MES Process Stage

description: Indicates whether occurrences of this defect type must be reported to a regulatory authority (e.g., CE Marking body, UL, EPA, OSHA). Drives automated regulatory notification workflows in the QMS.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; UL Product Safety Standards; OSHA 29 CFR 1904; EPA Reporting Requirements

description: Categorical classification of the typical rework cost associated with this defect type. Used for cost-of-quality (COQ) analysis, scrap/rework budget planning, and prioritization of defect prevention investments.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; ASQ Cost of Quality Framework; GAAP/IFRS Cost Accounting

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) / Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Relevant Flag
description: Indicates whether this defect type is relevant to RoHS or REACH regulatory compliance. When true, NCRs carrying this defect code may trigger mandatory regulatory reporting and customer notification workflows.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7.2

description: Indicates whether this defect type is classified as safety-critical, meaning its occurrence could result in personal injury, equipment damage, or regulatory non-compliance. Safety-critical defects trigger mandatory escalation, hold, and CAPA workflows.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; ISO 45001:2018 Clause 8.1; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; IEC 62443

description: Brief, standardized label for the defect code used in dropdown selections, reports, and shop floor displays. Typically 5–50 characters for quick identification.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Catalog Text

description: Operational system of record from which this defect code was originated or is mastered. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across SAP QM, Siemens Opcenter MES, and Teamcenter PLM systems.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Policy; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: Indicates whether Statistical Process Control monitoring should be applied to track the occurrence rate of this defect type over time. When true, SPC charts (e.g., p-chart, c-chart) are generated for this defect code in the quality analytics platform.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; Six Sigma DMAIC

description: Current lifecycle status of the defect code record. Active codes are available for use in inspections and NCRs; inactive codes are temporarily disabled; obsolete codes are retired and no longer assignable to new transactions; under_review codes are pending approval for activation or modification.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; SAP QM Catalog Management

description: Date from which this defect code is valid and available for use in quality transactions. Supports time-bounded defect taxonomy management and ensures historical records reference the correct code version.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP QM Validity Period Configuration

description: Date after which this defect code is no longer valid for use in new quality transactions. Enables planned obsolescence of defect codes while preserving historical data integrity.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP QM Validity Period Configuration

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the First Article Inspection (FAI) record. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.drawing. Business justification: FAI records are performed against a specific engineering drawing revision. Inspectors measure dimensions and features directly from the drawing — AS9102 FAI standard requires the drawing reference to
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: First Article Inspection records are created for specific products to verify conformance before production. Quality engineers conduct FAI per product; this is a standard aerospace and industrial requi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A First Article Inspection is executed as an inspection lot (inspection_lot has is_first_article_inspection flag). Linking fai_record to the inspection_lot that captured the measurements enables full
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_confirmation. Business justification: First Article Inspection records are tied to the first confirmed production order for a new part. Quality engineers reference the order confirmation to validate the FAI scope and customer requirements
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.ppap_submission. Business justification: FAI is a required element of PPAP (fai_complete flag exists in ppap_submission). The FAI record should reference the PPAP submission package it belongs to, enabling traceability from PPAP package to F
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: First Article Inspection records validate that the production routing produces conforming parts. FAI explicitly references the approved routing to confirm the manufacturing process matches the qualifi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: First Article Inspections are performed on parts from a specific supplier before series production begins. FAI approval is a procurement gate — purchase orders for production quantities are only relea
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date on which the FAI was formally approved by the designated approving authority (internal quality engineer, customer representative, or regulatory body).
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Full name of the authorized quality representative or customer delegate who reviewed and approved or rejected the FAI package.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Reference identifier or document number of the ballooned drawing used during the FAI. A ballooned drawing annotates each dimension and characteristic with a numbered balloon corresponding to the inspection data sheet entries.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Section 2.2; ANSI Y14.5

description: Number of inspected characteristics that were found to be within the specified engineering tolerances and fully conforming to the drawing requirements.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Number of inspected characteristics that were found to be outside the specified engineering tolerances. Non-conforming characteristics trigger Non-Conformance Report (NCR) and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) processes.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant or supplier facility where the first article was produced. Supports multinational regulatory compliance (CE Marking, RoHS, REACH) and export control tracking.
reference: ISO 3166-1; EU CE Marking; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006

description: Timestamp when the FAI record was initially created in the quality management system. Used for audit trail, record retention compliance, and lifecycle tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: Identifier of the customer for whom the FAI is being performed, when the inspection is driven by customer-specific requirements or a customer-specific form (e.g., PPAP customer-specific requirements). Blank for internal FAIs.
reference: AS9102 Rev B; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2

description: Reference number of any approved engineering deviation or waiver associated with this FAI, permitting use of a part that does not fully conform to the drawing specification under defined conditions.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7.1; AS9102 Rev B

description: Final disposition decision for the FAI. 'Approved' means the part fully conforms to all requirements. 'Approved with Deviation' allows limited production under documented deviation. 'Rejected' requires rework or re-inspection. 'Interim Approval' permits limited production pending resolution of open items.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Section 1.4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition

description: Date after which the FAI approval is no longer valid and a re-FAI may be required. Applicable when customer contracts or quality plans specify a validity period for FAI approvals.
reference: AS9102 Rev B; Customer-Specific Requirements; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Human-readable, business-assigned FAI reference number used for cross-referencing with engineering drawings, customer documentation, and quality records. Typically follows a structured naming convention tied to part number and revision.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Section 1.1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Business reason that triggered the FAI. Captures whether the inspection was initiated due to a new part introduction, engineering change, process change, supplier change, tooling change, production interruption, customer request, or Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA).
reference: AS9102 Rev B Section 1.3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Classification of the FAI scope. 'Full' indicates a complete FAI per AS9102. 'Partial' covers a subset of characteristics. 'Delta' is triggered by an Engineering Change Order (ECO) affecting specific features. 'Re-FAI' is required after a process or supplier change.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Section 1.3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether AS9102 Form 1 (Design Documentation) has been fully completed and all required design documentation (drawings, specifications, material certifications) has been collected and verified.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Indicates whether AS9102 Form 2 (Product Accountability, Materials, and Special Processes) has been fully completed, including raw material certifications, special process approvals (e.g., heat treatment, plating), and sub-tier supplier documentation.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; NADCAP

description: Indicates whether AS9102 Form 3 (Characteristic Accountability, Verification, and Compatibility Evaluation) has been fully completed, covering all dimensional, functional, and critical characteristics from the ballooned drawing.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; ANSI Y14.5

description: Status of functional performance testing conducted on the first article part to verify it meets operational and performance specifications beyond dimensional conformance.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Date on which the First Article Inspection was physically performed. Used for scheduling, compliance tracking, and quality record retention.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Quality standard or specification framework governing the FAI process. AS9102 is the aerospace standard; AIAG PPAP is the automotive standard; customer-specific standards may apply for key accounts.
reference: AS9102 Rev B; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015

description: Full name of the quality inspector or engineer who performed the First Article Inspection. Required for traceability and accountability per quality management system requirements.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the FAI record. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: Status of raw material certifications (mill certs, material test reports) required as part of the FAI package. Verifies that the material used in the first article conforms to the specified material standard.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3; ASTM Material Standards

description: Number of FAI action items or discrepancies that remain open and unresolved at the time of record capture. Open items may prevent full approval and require resolution before final disposition.
reference: AS9102 Rev B; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Engineering revision level of the part drawing at the time of the FAI. Ensures the inspection is performed against the correct and current drawing revision. Linked to Engineering Change Notice (ECN) / Engineering Change Order (ECO) history.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4

description: Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the first article part was produced and inspected. Supports multi-site quality management and regulatory compliance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Submission Level
description: PPAP submission level required by the customer, ranging from Level 1 (Part Submission Warrant only) to Level 5 (complete package reviewed at supplier's facility). Applicable when the FAI is part of a PPAP submission per AIAG standards.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Status of the Part Submission Warrant (PSW), the PPAP cover document that summarizes the FAI results and certifies that the part meets all customer requirements. Required for PPAP-based FAI submissions.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; AS9102 Rev B

description: Operational system of record from which the FAI record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Status of approvals for special manufacturing processes (e.g., welding, heat treatment, surface treatment, non-destructive testing) applied to the first article part. Special processes typically require NADCAP or customer-specific accreditation.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 2; NADCAP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1.2

description: Current workflow status of the FAI record throughout its lifecycle, from initial creation through inspection, submission, review, and final disposition.
reference: AS9102 Rev B; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Date on which the completed FAI package was formally submitted to the customer or internal approving authority for review and disposition.
reference: AS9102 Rev B; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Total number of dimensional, material, and functional characteristics identified on the ballooned drawing and required to be measured and verified during the FAI.
reference: AS9102 Rev B Form 3; ANSI Y14.5

description: Manufacturing work center or production cell where the first article part was produced. Used to trace the FAI to a specific shop floor location for process validation and traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) ID
description: Unique system-generated identifier for the FMEA record within the Quality Management System.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 6.1

description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_bom. Business justification: FMEAs are performed against specific equipment or asset structures. Quality engineers reference the asset BOM to identify failure modes at the component level — a core requirement of AIAG FMEA methodo
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: FMEA records analyze failure modes for specific quality characteristics (CTQ features). This FK links the risk assessment to the characteristic being analyzed, enabling direct traceability from FMEA s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: FMEAs are conducted for specific products or assemblies. Product engineers and quality teams create FMEAs per product in the hierarchy as part of APQP and design validation processes.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual date on which the recommended action was completed and evidence of implementation was recorded.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization

description: Action Priority classification (High/Medium/Low) per the AIAG-VDA 2019 methodology, providing a structured risk prioritization that complements or replaces the traditional RPN threshold approach.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis

description: Name or role of the person or team responsible for implementing the recommended action.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization

description: Target date by which the recommended action must be completed and verified, used for tracking open action items.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Name or role of the authorized approver (e.g., Quality Manager, Chief Engineer) who formally approved the FMEA for release.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019)

description: Date on which the FMEA document was formally approved and released for use in production or design activities.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019)

description: Product development stage at which this FMEA was created or last updated, aligned with the Advanced Product Quality Planning (APQP) phase gate process.
reference: AIAG APQP Manual (2nd Edition); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Description of existing controls used to detect the failure cause or failure mode before the product reaches the customer or the next process step (e.g., inspection, SPC, testing).
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the ability of current controls to detect the failure cause or failure mode before the item reaches the customer. A rating of 10 indicates no detection capability.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis

description: Root cause or mechanism by which the failure mode could occur, used to identify targeted prevention and detection controls.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4 – Failure Analysis

description: Description of the consequence of the failure mode on the customer, end user, downstream process, or system performance.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4 – Failure Analysis

description: Description of the manner in which the item or process could potentially fail to meet the design intent or process requirement.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 3 – Function Analysis

description: Description of the intended function or purpose of the item or process step being analyzed, forming the basis for failure mode identification.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 2 – Structure Analysis

description: Date on which the FMEA study was formally initiated, used for tracking analysis lead time and APQP milestone compliance.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); AIAG APQP Manual

description: Name of the product, component, subsystem, or process step that is the subject of this FMEA analysis.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1 – Planning and Preparation

description: Engineering part number or process identifier associated with the item being analyzed, as maintained in the PLM or ERP system.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Date of the most recent formal review of the FMEA, required periodically or following engineering changes, quality escapes, or field failures.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.3

description: Model year(s) or product generation(s) to which this FMEA analysis applies, supporting traceability across product lifecycle changes.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); Siemens Teamcenter PLM

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Number
description: Human-readable business reference number for the FMEA document, used for cross-referencing in engineering and quality documentation.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Numeric rating (1–10) estimating the likelihood or frequency with which the failure cause will occur during the design life or process run.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis

description: Name or employee identifier of the quality or engineering professional who authored and prepared the FMEA document.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Description of existing design or process controls that prevent the failure cause or failure mode from occurring (e.g., design standards, process parameters, mistake-proofing / Poka-Yoke).
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis

description: For PFMEA records, the specific manufacturing process step or operation (e.g., welding, assembly, machining) where the failure mode can occur, aligned with the Bill of Process (BoP).
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Proposed corrective or preventive action to reduce severity, occurrence, or detection ratings and lower the overall risk of the failure mode.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Indicates whether the failure mode has potential regulatory or compliance implications (e.g., CE Marking, RoHS, REACH, UL certification requirements).
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; UL Standards

description: Updated Action Priority classification (High/Medium/Low) after mitigation actions have been implemented, confirming whether residual risk is acceptable.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization

description: Updated detection rating after implementation of recommended actions, reflecting improvements in detection controls.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization

description: Updated occurrence rating after implementation of recommended actions, reflecting improvements in prevention controls.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization

description: Recalculated Risk Priority Number (Revised S × Revised O × Revised D) after mitigation actions have been implemented, used to confirm risk reduction effectiveness.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Updated severity rating after implementation of recommended actions, reflecting any design changes that reduce the impact of the failure effect.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6 – Optimization

description: Revision or version identifier of the FMEA document, incremented each time the analysis is formally updated following an engineering change or corrective action.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

description: Risk Priority Number calculated as Severity × Occurrence × Detection (range 1–1000). Used to prioritize failure modes for corrective action. Note: AIAG-VDA 2019 supplements RPN with Action Priority (AP).
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 6.1

description: Indicates whether the failure mode is associated with a safety-critical characteristic that could result in injury, regulatory non-compliance, or product recall. Triggers mandatory escalation and CAPA.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 45001:2018; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Numeric rating (1–10) indicating the seriousness of the failure effect on the customer or downstream process. A rating of 10 represents a safety-critical or regulatory non-compliance failure.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5 – Risk Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 6.1

description: Name of the originating operational system from which this FMEA record was sourced (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP S/4HANA QM), supporting data lineage in the lakehouse.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Current lifecycle status of the FMEA document, governing its applicability and use in production and design activities.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Names or roles of the cross-functional team members who participated in the FMEA study, as required by AIAG-VDA methodology for collaborative risk assessment.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1 – Planning and Preparation

description: Descriptive title of the FMEA study, typically referencing the product, assembly, or process being analyzed.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019)

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Type
description: Classification of the FMEA study: DFMEA (Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) for product design risk, PFMEA (Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) for manufacturing process risk, SFMEA for system-level, or MFMEA for machinery.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); IATF 16949:2016

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Submission ID
description: Unique system-generated identifier for the PPAP submission record within the Quality Management System.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: PPAP submissions require a designated customer approver contact. Quality engineers coordinate approval directly with this person — tracking the contact is essential for submission status and follow-up
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: PPAP approval is often a contractual milestone — the contract specifies PPAP requirements and approval is a condition for releasing production volumes. Linking PPAP to the contract enables contract co
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: PPAP submissions must reference the approved BOM revision — PPAP Element 2 (Design Records) requires the exact BOM. Automotive/industrial PPAP packages are always tied to a specific BOM revision.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: PPAP submissions are made for specific parts/products to gain customer approval. Supplier quality teams submit PPAPs per product; this link is mandatory for automotive and industrial manufacturing.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: PPAP submissions reference the inspection plan as part of the measurement system and capability study documentation. This FK links the PPAP package to the inspection plan used during the production tr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.pricing_agreement. Business justification: PPAP submissions are often scoped to a specific customer pricing/supply agreement (part number, program). Linking ensures the submission is traceable to the commercial contract it supports — required
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.quotation. Business justification: PPAP submissions are prepared during quotation phase for new parts. Customers require PPAP approval before awarding production orders, linking quality validation to commercial quotation.

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: PPAP submissions are made by a specific supplier to qualify a part for production. Procurement and SQE teams manage PPAP approval as a prerequisite before issuing production purchase orders to that su
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Declared annual production volume (units per year) for the part as stated on the Part Submission Warrant (PSW), used to validate process capability studies.

description: Indicates whether the Appearance Approval Report (AAR) element has been completed and included in the PPAP submission package, applicable for appearance-critical parts.

description: Date on which the customer formally approved or conditionally approved the PPAP submission, authorizing production shipments.

description: Formal customer disposition of the PPAP submission: Approved (full production authorized), Conditionally Approved (limited production with open actions), or Rejected (resubmission required).

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a formal CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) must be initiated as a result of this quality notification. Drives CAPA workflow creation in the QMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Description of the immediate containment action taken to prevent further nonconforming product from reaching the customer or next process step (e.g., quarantine, 100% inspection, production hold).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; AIAG PPAP; 8D Problem Solving

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or site where the quality notification originated. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance and geographic quality performance analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Date and time when the quality notification was first created in SAP QM. Marks the start of the notification lifecycle and is used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) response time measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Classification of the defect or quality problem into a standard category (e.g., Dimensional, Surface, Functional, Labeling, Documentation, Process, Material). Used for Pareto analysis and trend reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; AIAG PPAP

description: Detailed narrative description of the quality issue, defect, complaint, or improvement opportunity. Captures the full problem statement as entered by the originator.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Stage in the production or supply chain process at which the quality defect or nonconformance was detected. Supports escape analysis and process improvement initiatives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; AIAG FMEA

description: Descriptive name of the affected material or component as maintained in the SAP material master, providing human-readable context for the notification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Quantity of units, parts, or material identified as nonconforming in the quality notification. Used for scrap rate calculation, PPM (Parts Per Million) reporting, and cost of poor quality analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; AIAG PPAP

description: SAP QM-assigned alphanumeric notification number that uniquely identifies the quality notification in the source system. Used for cross-system referencing and communication with stakeholders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the quality issue originated or was detected. Supports plant-level quality performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Priority level assigned to the notification indicating urgency of resolution. 1 = Very High (Safety/Regulatory), 2 = High (Production Impact), 3 = Medium (Quality Risk), 4 = Low (Improvement). Drives escalation and response time targets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Human-readable label for the priority level (e.g., Very High, High, Medium, Low) to support reporting and user communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: SAP purchase order number associated with the incoming goods or supplier delivery linked to the quality notification, enabling procurement-quality traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; SAP Ariba; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Unit of measure for the nonconforming quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter, L for liter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure for the material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Indicates whether the quality issue documented in this notification must be reported to a regulatory authority (e.g., OSHA, EPA, CE Marking authority, UL). Triggers compliance reporting workflows.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; OSHA; EPA; EU CE Marking; RoHS/REACH; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: SAP user ID or employee identifier of the person who created and submitted the quality notification. Supports accountability tracking and notification originator analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.2

description: Organizational department of the person who reported the quality notification (e.g., Quality Assurance, Production, Procurement, Customer Service). Enables departmental quality reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Target date by which the quality notification must be fully resolved and closed, as defined by SLA (Service Level Agreement) and priority rules. Drives escalation workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Date by which processing of the quality notification must begin, as defined by the notification priority and SLA (Service Level Agreement) rules. Used for scheduling and escalation management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Organizational department responsible for investigating and resolving the quality notification (e.g., Quality Engineering, Manufacturing, Supplier Quality, R&D).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: SAP user ID or employee identifier of the quality engineer or coordinator assigned as the primary owner responsible for processing and closing the notification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Unique serial number of the specific unit or assembly affected by the quality notification, enabling unit-level traceability for serialized products.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM/MM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which the quality notification originated (e.g., SAP_QM, SALESFORCE_CRM for customer complaints, OPCENTER_MES for shop floor defects, MANUAL for paper-based entries). Supports data lineage.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA QM; Salesforce CRM; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Current processing status of the quality notification using SAP system status codes. OSNO = Outstanding (Not Started), NOPR = In Process, NOCO = Completed, NOPT = Partially Completed, CLSD = Closed, DLFL = Deletion Flag. Drives workflow routing and SLA tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Short descriptive title summarizing the quality problem, complaint, or improvement request captured in the notification. Used as the primary display label in dashboards and reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: SAP QM notification type code classifying the nature of the quality event. Q1 = Internal Problem Notification, Q2 = Customer Complaint, Q3 = Supplier Defect Notification, F1 = Maintenance Request, F2 = Activity Report, S1 = Service Notification, S2 = Customer Inquiry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Human-readable description of the notification type (e.g., 'Internal Defect', 'Customer Complaint', 'Supplier Defect Notification') to support reporting and user interfaces.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: SAP vendor master number identifying the supplier responsible for the defective material or component, applicable for supplier defect notifications (Q3 type). Used for supplier quality performance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/QM Module; SAP Ariba; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: SAP work center code identifying the specific production cell, line, or machine where the quality issue was detected or originated. Enables work-center-level defect analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/QM Module; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a quality characteristic (Critical to Quality feature) master record within the Quality Management System.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1 — Control of Production and Service Provision

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.drawing. Business justification: Characteristics are ballooned and traced to specific engineering drawings — each measurable characteristic references the drawing callout. This is standard practice in dimensional inspection and contr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: Quality characteristics (dimensions, tolerances, properties) are derived from product specifications. Quality engineers trace each characteristic back to the engineering spec to ensure inspection crit
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Quality characteristics (dimensions, weights, voltages) are defined with a unit of measure. Referencing the product UOM master ensures standardized units across all quality characteristic definitions.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or employee ID of the quality engineer or quality manager who approved this characteristic definition for use in production inspection. Required for ISO 9001 documented information control.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; SAP QM

description: Date and time when this quality characteristic definition was formally approved for production use. Provides an audit trail for ISO 9001 documented information control and PPAP submission evidence.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: The Acceptable Quality Level (AQL) assigned to this characteristic, representing the maximum percent defective (or defects per hundred units) that is considered acceptable as a process average. Drives the sampling plan stringency for this characteristic.
reference: ISO 2859-1:1999 Sampling Procedures; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Indicates whether the characteristic is measured on a continuous variable scale (e.g., diameter in mm) or evaluated as a pass/fail attribute (e.g., presence of a feature, visual conformance). Drives the appropriate SPC chart type and sampling plan.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870 Control Charts

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that created or last modified this quality characteristic definition. Provides full traceability from characteristic specification to the approved design change.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6; APQP/PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Risk-based classification of the characteristic indicating its impact on safety, regulatory compliance, or product function. Critical characteristics affect safety or regulatory compliance; major characteristics affect product function or customer satisfaction; minor characteristics have limited impact; informational characteristics are tracked for data collection only.
reference: APQP/PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; DFMEA/PFMEA methodology; IATF 16949

description: The minimum acceptable Cp (Process Capability Index) value required for this characteristic, representing the ratio of specification width to process spread. Used alongside Cpk to assess both process spread and centering in capability studies.
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; Six Sigma

description: The minimum acceptable Cpk (Process Capability Index — Centered) value required for this characteristic to demonstrate process capability. Typical thresholds are 1.33 for standard characteristics and 1.67 for safety-critical or CTQ characteristics. Used in PPAP capability studies.
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; Six Sigma

description: Date and time when this quality characteristic master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Indicates whether this characteristic is designated as Critical to Quality (CTQ) — a feature whose performance standard must be met to satisfy the customer. CTQ characteristics receive heightened inspection frequency, SPC monitoring, and PPAP documentation requirements.
reference: Six Sigma DMAIC Methodology; APQP/PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Number of decimal places to which the characteristic measurement should be recorded and displayed. Drives measurement resolution requirements and gauge selection. For example, a diameter measured to 0.001 mm requires 3 decimal places.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Characteristic; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; MSA Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Indicates whether measurement of this characteristic requires destructive testing (i.e., the part cannot be used after measurement). Destructive characteristics require special sampling strategies and cannot be 100% inspected.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; APQP/PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 2859-1 Sampling

description: Reference to the engineering drawing or CAD document from which this quality characteristic specification is derived. Provides traceability to the design record and supports PPAP documentation requirements.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.5 Design Outputs

description: Revision level of the engineering drawing from which this characteristic specification is sourced. Ensures that inspection requirements are aligned with the current approved design revision.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6 Design Changes

description: Type of measurement instrument or gauge required to measure this quality characteristic. Used to link the characteristic to calibrated measurement equipment in the gauge management system and to define MSA (Measurement System Analysis) requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; MSA Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO/IEC 17025 Calibration

description: Logical grouping of related quality characteristics for organizational and reporting purposes (e.g., 'Geometric Tolerances', 'Surface Finish', 'Mechanical Properties', 'Chemical Composition'). Used to structure inspection plans and control plans.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Characteristic Catalog; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Date and time when this quality characteristic master record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail purposes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: The minimum allowable value for the quality characteristic as defined in the engineering specification or customer requirement. Measurements below this limit are non-conforming.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870 Control Charts; Six Sigma

description: The negative tolerance deviation from the nominal value (e.g., -0.05 mm). Together with upper_tolerance, defines the bilateral or unilateral tolerance band around the nominal value as specified on engineering drawings.
reference: ISO 14405:2016 GPS Tolerancing; ISO 2768 General Tolerances; ASME Y14.5 GD&T

description: SAP material number of the part, assembly, or raw material to which this quality characteristic applies. Enables traceability from the characteristic definition to the specific material master record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2 Identification and Traceability

description: Description of the prescribed measurement method or procedure for evaluating this characteristic (e.g., 'Measure at 3 cross-sections using CMM', 'Visual inspection under 10x magnification', 'Tensile test per ASTM E8'). References the applicable test standard or SOP.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ASTM International Standards; ISO/IEC 17025

description: Descriptive name of the quality characteristic (e.g., 'Shaft Outer Diameter', 'Surface Roughness Ra', 'Tensile Strength'). Used in inspection plans, control plans, and operator instructions.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; APQP/PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: The target or ideal value for the quality characteristic as specified in the engineering drawing or product specification. Serves as the reference point for tolerance calculations and SPC centerline.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO 14405 GPS Tolerancing; APQP/PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the quality characteristic as defined in SAP QM master data (e.g., inspection characteristic catalog number). Used to reference the characteristic across inspection plans, control plans, and SPC charts.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Characteristic; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this quality characteristic is applicable. Supports multi-plant operations where the same part may have plant-specific inspection requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Indicates whether this characteristic is subject to regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., RoHS/REACH chemical limits, CE marking dimensional tolerances, UL electrical ratings). Non-conformance may trigger mandatory regulatory reporting.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; CE Marking Directive; UL Standards; EPA Regulations

description: Indicates whether this characteristic is safety-critical, meaning non-conformance could result in personal injury, property damage, or regulatory non-compliance. Safety-critical characteristics require mandatory inspection and cannot be skipped or waived.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO 45001:2018; OSHA Standards; CE Marking Directive; UL Certification

description: Code referencing the applicable sampling procedure or plan for this characteristic (e.g., AQL sampling plan code, skip-lot procedure code). Determines how many samples are drawn from a lot for inspection of this characteristic.
reference: ISO 2859-1 Sampling Procedures for Inspection by Attributes; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9; APQP/PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Abbreviated description of the quality characteristic for use in reports, shop floor displays, and HMI screens where space is limited.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Characteristic; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Indicates whether this characteristic is eligible for skip-lot inspection (reduced inspection frequency based on demonstrated supplier or process quality history). Safety-critical and CTQ characteristics are typically excluded from skip-lot programs.
reference: ISO 2859-3 Skip-Lot Sampling; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this quality characteristic master record originates (e.g., SAP QM inspection characteristic catalog, Siemens Teamcenter PLM, Siemens Opcenter MES). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: The type of SPC control chart used to monitor this characteristic. Variable charts (Xbar-R, Xbar-S, I-MR) are used for continuous measurements; attribute charts (p, np, c, u) are used for pass/fail or count data. CUSUM and EWMA are used for detecting small shifts.
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2:2013 Shewhart Control Charts; ISO 7870-4 CUSUM Charts; Six Sigma

description: Indicates whether Statistical Process Control (SPC) monitoring is activated for this characteristic. When true, measurement results are plotted on control charts and evaluated against control limits to detect process instability.
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2 Shewhart Control Charts; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; Six Sigma

description: Current lifecycle status of the quality characteristic master record. Active characteristics are available for use in inspection plans; inactive are temporarily suspended; obsolete are retired; under_review are pending engineering change approval.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; SAP QM Master Data

description: Classification of the quality characteristic by its physical or functional nature. Dimensional covers length/diameter/angle; functional covers performance attributes; visual covers appearance; material covers composition/hardness; electrical covers voltage/resistance; chemical covers composition/purity.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; APQP/PPAP Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 14638 GPS Masterplan

description: The maximum allowable value for the quality characteristic as defined in the engineering specification or customer requirement. Measurements exceeding this limit are non-conforming.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870 Control Charts; Six Sigma

description: The positive tolerance deviation from the nominal value (e.g., +0.05 mm). Together with lower_tolerance, defines the bilateral or unilateral tolerance band around the nominal value as specified on engineering drawings.
reference: ISO 14405:2016 GPS Tolerancing; ISO 2768 General Tolerances; ASME Y14.5 GD&T

description: Code identifying the production work center or process step where this characteristic is measured. Links the characteristic to the specific manufacturing operation in the routing or Bill of Process (BoP).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: The date from which this quality characteristic definition is effective and may be used in inspection plans. Supports time-phased characteristic management aligned with engineering change notices (ECN/ECO).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6 Design Changes; SAP QM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: The date after which this quality characteristic definition is no longer effective. Used to manage characteristic obsolescence aligned with engineering change orders (ECO) and product end-of-life.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6 Design Changes; SAP QM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the Quality Control Plan record within the enterprise data platform.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Control plans define quality controls for a specific product/part. Manufacturing quality teams maintain control plans per product as a core APQP deliverable and production control document.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Control plans reference inspection plans for the specific inspection characteristics, sampling methods, and AQL levels to be applied during production. This FK links the process control definition to
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.measurement_point. Business justification: Control plans specify where on equipment measurements must be taken. Linking to the asset measurement point ensures control plan instructions align with the physical measurement locations defined in t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: Control plans enforce product specifications — acceptance criteria in the control plan are derived directly from engineering product specs. Quality engineers reference specs daily when authoring and r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: Control plans document quality controls at each routing operation. APQP requirement linking process steps to inspection methods, reaction plans, and control methods.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: Control plans define quality controls at each routing operation step. APQP/PPAP requires control plans to be aligned to specific process operations — quality engineers map each control point to a rout
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or employee ID of the quality authority (e.g., Quality Engineer, Quality Manager) who formally approved this control plan for production use.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual

description: Date and time when the control plan was formally approved, providing an audit trail for quality management system compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual

description: Risk-based classification of the controlled characteristic indicating its impact on safety, regulatory compliance, or fit/function. Critical characteristics typically require 100% inspection or SPC monitoring.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3.3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Type of SPC control chart applied to monitor the characteristic (e.g., X-bar/R, Individuals/MR, p-chart for attribute data). Null or 'none' if SPC is not applied.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: The method or technique used to control or monitor the characteristic (e.g., SPC charting, CMM measurement, go/no-go gauge, visual inspection, Poka-Yoke device).
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual; AIAG MSA Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Minimum acceptable Cpk (Process Capability Index - Centered) threshold required for this characteristic. Typically 1.33 for standard characteristics and 1.67 for critical/safety characteristics per AIAG standards.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Date and time when the control plan record was first created in the quality management system.
Code identifying the customer for whom this control plan was developed, particularly relevant for customer-specific requirements (CSR) in automotive and industrial supply chains.
reference: IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.2.3.1; AIAG PPAP Reference Manual

description: Date on which this revision of the control plan became effective and was authorized for use in production.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual

description: Date on which this control plan revision expires or is scheduled for mandatory review and revalidation, supporting periodic review requirements.
Date and time of the most recent update to the control plan record, supporting change tracking and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity)

description: Specific measurement instrument, gauge, or test equipment used to evaluate the controlled characteristic (e.g., Vernier caliper, torque wrench, CMM, hardness tester).
reference: AIAG MSA Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this control plan to ensure continued adequacy and alignment with current process conditions and engineering specifications.
Engineering revision level of the part drawing or specification that this control plan is aligned to, ensuring traceability between design and process controls.
reference: AIAG PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6

description: Business-assigned unique document number for the control plan, used for cross-referencing in PPAP submissions, APQP deliverables, and quality management systems.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: APQP-defined phase classification of the control plan: Prototype (early development), Pre-Launch (pilot/validation), or Production (full-rate manufacturing). Directly maps to AIAG APQP phase gates.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where this control plan is applied, supporting multi-site quality governance in a multinational manufacturing environment.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1; SAP S/4HANA Plant (MM/PP)

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Submission Level
description: PPAP submission level (1–5) associated with this control plan, defining the extent of documentation required for customer approval per AIAG PPAP Reference Manual.
reference: AIAG PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.4.4

description: Name or employee ID of the quality or process engineer who authored and prepared this control plan.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: The process parameter or input variable being controlled (e.g., temperature, torque, feed rate, pressure) that directly affects the product characteristic.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Document number or identifier of the Process Flow Diagram (PFD) that this control plan is aligned to, maintaining the APQP triad linkage: PFD → PFMEA → Control Plan.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3

description: Name of the manufacturing process or process step covered by this control plan (e.g., Welding, CNC Machining, Assembly, Painting).
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Sequential step number from the Process Flow Diagram (PFD) that this control plan entry corresponds to, enabling direct traceability between the process flow and control plan.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; IATF 16949:2016

description: Identifier or name of the specific production line, cell, or work center where this control plan is applied.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Documented corrective actions or containment steps to be taken when the process or product characteristic goes out of control or exceeds specification limits. Includes operator instructions and escalation path.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949:2016

description: Current revision level or version of the control plan document, incremented upon each approved engineering change or process update.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

description: Frequency or interval at which samples are taken for inspection or measurement (e.g., every 1 hour, every 50 parts, start of shift, per lot, continuous).
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual; ISO 2859-1

description: Number of parts or units to be measured per inspection event, as defined by the AQL sampling plan or SPC subgroup size.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual; ISO 2859-1 (AQL Sampling); ANSI/ASQ Z1.4
