description: Operational system of record from which this control plan record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Siemens Opcenter MES, Siemens Teamcenter PLM), supporting data lineage in the lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Lower Specification Limit (LSL) for the controlled characteristic. Values below this limit indicate a non-conformance requiring NCR initiation.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; ANSI/ASME Y14.5

description: Target or nominal value for the controlled characteristic as specified in the engineering drawing or product specification (e.g., 25.00 mm, 150 Nm).
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; ANSI/ASME Y14.5

description: Upper Specification Limit (USL) for the controlled characteristic. Values exceeding this limit indicate a non-conformance requiring NCR initiation.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; ANSI/ASME Y14.5

description: Current lifecycle status of the control plan, governing whether it is actively used on the shop floor or has been superseded by a newer revision.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual

description: Descriptive title of the control plan, typically referencing the part name, process family, or product line it governs.
reference: AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual 4th Edition

description: Engineering unit of measure for the specification values and measurement results (e.g., mm, Nm, °C, bar, kg, %).
reference: ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; ANSI/ASME Y14.5

business_glossary_term: Work Instruction / Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) Reference
description: Document number or identifier of the associated work instruction or Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that operators follow to execute the controlled process step.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; AIAG APQP/PPAP Reference Manual

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the inspection lot record. Serves as the primary key for all quality inspection events in the Quality Management System (QMS).
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_hierarchy. Business justification: Customer-specific inspection lots are created for goods produced against a customer order. Linking to the customer account enables customer-specific quality plans and traceability of inspection result
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: Every inspection lot is tied to a production batch for traceability. Quality departments use this link to accept or reject entire batches based on inspection results — a core SAP QM workflow.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Inspection lots are executed in accordance with control plan requirements. This FK links the transactional inspection event to the governing control plan that defines the inspection frequency, sample
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Inspection lots are created at goods receipt in SAP QM and equivalent systems. The GR event physically triggers the inspection lot — quality cannot inspect without knowing what was received, when, and
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Inspection lots are triggered for a specific product/material. Production and quality teams link every inspection lot to the product hierarchy to track quality results per product.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: An inspection lot is always governed by an inspection plan that defines the sampling method, AQL level, and characteristics to inspect. The inspection_plan_number and inspection_plan_version string fi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Inspection lots are physically performed at a specific location (warehouse, receiving dock, production floor). Quality planners assign inspection lots to locations for resource scheduling and traceabi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_confirmation. Business justification: Inspection lots are triggered by confirmed customer orders in manufacturing. Quality teams open inspection lots directly against order confirmations to validate goods before shipment — a daily QM/SD i
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Inspection lots are created for production orders. Linking to production_order_cost allows finance and quality to jointly analyze scrap/rework costs against the production order's actual cost, enablin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Incoming goods inspections are triggered directly by purchase orders. Quality teams open inspection lots against specific POs to validate received materials before acceptance — a daily receiving inspe
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing_operation. Business justification: In-process inspection lots are assigned to specific routing operations (e.g., weld inspection at op 30). This links quality gates directly to the production routing step where the check is performed.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Inspection lots are triggered at specific work centers during in-process quality checks. Quality teams use this daily to trace which production station generated the lot and route inspectors according
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Quantity of material within the inspection lot that passed inspection and was accepted for unrestricted use or further processing. Used for yield calculation and stock posting to unrestricted stock.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the inspected material was manufactured or sourced. Required for customs compliance, REACH/RoHS regulatory reporting, and trade documentation.
reference: ISO 3166-1; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; CE Marking

description: Total count of defects or non-conformances identified during inspection of this lot. Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation, SPC charting, and quality trend analysis.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Six Sigma Methodology; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: SAP QM dynamic modification rule applied to this inspection lot, which adjusts the inspection scope (normal, tightened, reduced, skip) based on the supplier or process quality history. Supports risk-based inspection frequency management.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 2859-1 (AQL Switching Rules); IATF 16949 Clause 8.6

description: Actual date on which all inspection activities and results recording were completed for this lot. Used to calculate inspection cycle time and identify bottlenecks in the quality process.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: Overall summarized outcome of all inspection characteristic results for the lot. Indicates whether the lot passed, failed, or was conditionally accepted based on the aggregate of individual characteristic evaluations.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO 2859-1

description: Current inspection severity stage for this lot as determined by the dynamic modification rule. Reflects the quality history-based adjustment to inspection intensity: Normal, Tightened (stricter), Reduced (less frequent), or Skip (no inspection).
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 2859-1 (AQL Switching Rules); ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Planned or actual date on which physical inspection activities commenced for this lot. Used for scheduling, workload planning, and measuring inspection lead time.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: SAP QM inspection type code defining the origin and nature of the inspection lot. Examples: 01 (Goods Receipt from Purchase Order), 04 (Goods Receipt from Production), 05 (Goods Issue), 06 (Delivery to Customer), 08 (Repetitive Manufacturing), 10 (Audit), 89 (Manual). Drives inspection plan selection and process flow.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Human-readable description of the inspection type code, such as 'Goods Receipt from Purchase Order' or 'In-Process Inspection'. Supports reporting and user interface display.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Indicates whether this inspection lot represents a First Article Inspection (FAI) event, typically performed on the first production run of a new or revised part. FAI lots require full dimensional and functional verification per PPAP requirements.
reference: SAP QM Module; AS9102 First Article Inspection; IATF 16949 PPAP; APQP

description: Date and time when the inspection lot was created in SAP QM, either automatically triggered by a business event (e.g., goods receipt, production order confirmation) or manually by a quality user.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the inspection lot as displayed in SAP QM. Used for cross-referencing with production orders, goods receipts, and quality notifications.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Business process that triggered the creation of the inspection lot. Identifies whether the lot originated from a production order, goods receipt, customer delivery, manual creation, or other source. Critical for traceability and root cause analysis.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Total quantity of material submitted for inspection in this lot. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material. Used for AQL sampling plan determination and yield calculations.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 2859-1 (AQL Sampling); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Short text description of the material or component being inspected, as maintained in the SAP material master. Supports readability in quality reports and dashboards.
reference: SAP MM/QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised against this inspection lot when nonconformances are identified. Links the inspection lot to the formal NCR and CAPA workflow for corrective action tracking.
reference: SAP QM Notification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7

description: Quantity of material within the inspection lot that was found to be nonconforming and not accepted for unrestricted use. Drives scrap rate and yield calculations at the lot level.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the inspection lot was created and executed. Enables plant-level quality performance analysis and multi-site benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Quantity of material within the inspection lot that requires rework before it can be accepted for use. Drives rework cost tracking, capacity planning, and quality cost analysis.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7

description: Actual quantity drawn from the inspection lot for physical inspection and testing, as determined by the AQL sampling plan or dynamic modification rule. May differ from the lot quantity.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 2859-1 (AQL Sampling); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Quantity of material within the inspection lot that was scrapped due to nonconformance and cannot be reworked or used. Feeds into scrap rate KPI and COGS impact reporting.
reference: SAP QM/CO Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Lean Manufacturing

description: Current processing status of the inspection lot within the SAP QM workflow. Tracks progression from lot creation through results recording to usage decision and completion. Drives downstream stock posting and release actions.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: SAP storage location within the plant where the inspected material is physically held during the inspection process. Relevant for stock posting and usage decision execution.
reference: SAP MM/WM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Base unit of measure for the inspection lot quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter, L for liter). Aligns with SAP material master unit of measure configuration.
reference: SAP MM Module; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: User ID or name of the quality inspector or quality engineer who made the final usage decision for the inspection lot. Required for accountability, audit trail, and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: SAP QM usage decision code indicating the final disposition of the inspected material (e.g., A=Accept/Unrestricted Use, R=Reject/Scrap, B=Rework, Q=Blocked Stock, S=Sample Retention). Triggers automatic stock posting in SAP MM.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7

description: Human-readable description of the usage decision code, such as 'Accept - Unrestricted Use', 'Reject - Scrap', or 'Conditional Release - Rework Required'. Supports quality reporting and disposition documentation.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Date and time when the usage decision was made and recorded in SAP QM. Critical for measuring inspection cycle time, SLA compliance, and audit trail requirements under ISO 9001.
reference: SAP QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: Name of the external supplier or vendor associated with the inspection lot for incoming goods receipt inspections. Supports supplier quality management reporting without requiring a join to the vendor master.
reference: SAP MM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: SAP vendor/supplier number associated with the inspection lot when the origin is a goods receipt from a purchase order. Enables supplier quality performance tracking, PPM analysis, and vendor scorecard reporting.
reference: SAP MM/QM Module; SAP Ariba; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; IATF 16949 Clause 8.4

description: SAP work center or production cell where the in-process inspection was performed. Enables quality performance analysis at the machine, line, or cell level for SPC and process capability monitoring.
reference: SAP PP/QM Module; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a quality inspection plan record in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan Master Data; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bop. Business justification: Inspection plans are tied to a Bill of Process — quality checks are defined per manufacturing process step. Process engineers and quality teams align inspection points to specific BOP operations daily
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: Inspection plans are built around specific quality characteristics (CTQ features). This FK links the inspection plan master to the characteristic master, enabling direct traceability from the inspecti
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: Inspection plans are built against a specific BOM revision — quality engineers reference the BOM to define which components require inspection. This is a daily quality planning activity in manufacturi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: Inspection plans are developed based on FMEA risk assessments — high RPN characteristics from FMEA drive more stringent inspection requirements. This FK links the inspection plan to the FMEA that info
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Inspection plans are created for specific products in the product hierarchy. Quality engineers reference the product hierarchy daily when setting up inspection plans for manufacturing operations.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Inspection plans may be specific to a product configuration/variant. In configurable product manufacturing, different configurations require different inspection criteria; this link enables configurat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: Inspection plans define quality checks aligned with production routing operations. Quality engineers create inspection plans that mirror the manufacturing process flow for in-process verification.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.task_list. Business justification: Inspection plans reference asset task lists that define the sequence of inspection steps to be performed on equipment. In SAP QM/PM, inspection plans are directly linked to task lists to reuse standar
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or user ID of the quality authority (e.g., Quality Engineer, Quality Manager) who formally approved and released this inspection plan. Required for ISO 9001 document control compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; SAP QM

description: Date and time when the inspection plan was formally approved and released for production use. Provides an auditable approval trail for ISO 9001 document control.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; SAP QM

description: APQP phase during which this inspection plan was developed or is applicable. Tracks the maturity of the quality plan from concept through production launch per AIAG APQP methodology.
reference: AIAG APQP Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3; PPAP

description: Acceptable Quality Level expressed as the maximum percent defective (or defects per hundred units) that is considered satisfactory as a process average. Governs the accept/reject decision for inspection lots per ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 or ISO 2859-1 sampling tables.
reference: ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 2859-1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP QM

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that triggered the creation or revision of this inspection plan. Provides traceability between design changes and quality plan updates.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6; APQP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this inspection plan is applicable. Supports country-specific regulatory requirements and multi-country quality management.
reference: ISO 3166-1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1; EU CE Marking; OSHA

business_glossary_term: Minimum Required Process Capability Index - Centered (Cpk)
description: Minimum acceptable Cpk (Process Capability Index - Centered) threshold that must be achieved for quantitative characteristics in this plan. Typically 1.33 for standard processes and 1.67 for safety-critical characteristics per Six Sigma and AIAG standards.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual; Six Sigma; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; PPAP

description: Date and time when the inspection plan record was initially created in SAP QM. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP QM

description: Customer identifier when this inspection plan is tailored to meet a specific customer's quality requirements or contractual specifications. Supports customer-specific PPAP and SLA compliance.
reference: SAP SD Customer Master; Salesforce CRM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; PPAP

description: Indicates whether any inspection characteristic in this plan requires destructive testing, which renders the sampled unit unusable after inspection. Impacts sample size planning and material cost accounting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: SAP QM dynamic modification rule code that governs automatic tightening, normal, or reduced inspection switching based on cumulative quality history (accept/reject run). Implements skip-lot and reduced inspection per ISO 2859-3.
reference: SAP QM Dynamic Modification; ISO 2859-3; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Indicates whether a First Article Inspection (FAI) must be completed using this plan before serial production is authorized. FAI validates that the manufacturing process can produce parts conforming to all design requirements.
reference: AS9102; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; APQP; PPAP

description: Broad category of inspection methodology applied in this plan, indicating the nature of the quality checks performed (e.g., dimensional measurement, visual examination, functional testing, non-destructive testing).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; IEC 61131

description: Inspection level (I, II, III, or special levels S-1 through S-4) as defined in ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 / ISO 2859-1, determining the relative sample size used for the inspection. Level II is the standard default.
reference: ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 2859-1; SAP QM Sampling Procedure

description: Code identifying the standardized inspection method or test procedure (e.g., CMM measurement, visual gauge, hardness test, tensile test) to be applied for the characteristics in this plan.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Method; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ANSI

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the language in which this inspection plan's descriptions and instructions are maintained. Supports multinational operations with localized shop floor documentation.
reference: ISO 639-1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP QM

description: Date and time of the most recent modification to the inspection plan record. Supports change tracking and ensures the silver layer reflects the latest SAP QM state.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP QM

description: Free-text field containing detailed instructions, notes, or special requirements for executing this inspection plan. May include references to standard operating procedures (SOPs), safety precautions, or customer-specific directives.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP QM Long Text; SOP

description: Human-readable description of the material or part governed by this inspection plan, as maintained in the SAP material master. Supports reporting and plan identification without requiring a material number lookup.
reference: SAP MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Type of measurement or test equipment required to execute the inspection characteristics defined in this plan. Used for calibration scheduling and equipment reservation in Maximo EAM.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025; SAP QM; Maximo EAM

description: Production routing operation number (e.g., 0010, 0020) at which this inspection plan is executed. Ties the quality inspection to a specific step in the Bill of Process (BoP) or production order routing.
reference: SAP PP Routing; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: SAP QM plan group key that logically groups related inspection plans for the same material or process. Multiple plan group counters can exist within a single plan group.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan Group; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Sequential counter within a plan group distinguishing alternative inspection plans (e.g., different sampling levels or customer-specific variants) for the same material.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the inspection plan as assigned in SAP QM (transaction QP01). Used for cross-referencing with shop floor documents and quality records.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Classification of the inspection plan by the stage or trigger in the production and supply chain process. Determines when and where the inspection is executed (e.g., goods receipt from supplier, in-process at work center, final inspection before shipment, First Article Inspection).
reference: SAP QM Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4, 8.5, 8.6; PPAP; FAI

description: SAP organizational unit code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where this inspection plan is applicable. Supports multi-plant and multinational operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: PPAP submission level (1 through 5) required for this inspection plan, defining the extent of documentation and evidence that must be submitted to the customer for part approval. Level 3 is the default full submission.
reference: AIAG PPAP Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the current version of the inspection plan. Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or quality-driven update.
reference: SAP QM / Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; APQP

description: Indicates whether this inspection plan includes characteristics required to verify compliance with RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) and/or REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) regulations for the material.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Fixed or calculated number of units to be drawn from an inspection lot for measurement and evaluation. May be overridden by dynamic sampling rules based on quality history.
reference: ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 2859-1; SAP QM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Unit of measure for the sample size (e.g., EA for each/piece, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure for the material.
reference: SAP MM Unit of Measure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Code referencing the statistical sampling procedure defined in SAP QM, specifying the sampling scheme (e.g., fixed sample, percentage, skip-lot) applied during inspection lot creation.
reference: SAP QM Sampling Procedure; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 2859-1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Indicates whether skip-lot inspection is permitted for this plan, allowing certain lots to bypass full inspection based on a positive quality history. Governed by the dynamic modification rule.
reference: SAP QM Dynamic Modification; ISO 2859-3; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Indicates whether Statistical Process Control (SPC) charting and Cp/Cpk capability analysis are activated for the quantitative characteristics in this inspection plan. When true, measurement results feed SPC control charts.
reference: SAP QM SPC; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Six Sigma; AIAG SPC Manual

description: Current lifecycle status of the inspection plan. Only plans in 'released' or 'active' status are eligible to govern inspection lot execution in SAP QM and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: SAP vendor/supplier code when this inspection plan is specifically applicable to incoming goods from a particular supplier. Enables supplier-specific quality control and PPAP compliance tracking.
reference: SAP MM Vendor Master; SAP Ariba; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; PPAP

description: Indicates whether a formal usage decision (accept, reject, conditional release) must be recorded in SAP QM upon completion of the inspection lot governed by this plan. Mandatory for regulated or safety-critical materials.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Identifier of the SAP work center or Opcenter MES resource where the inspection is physically performed. Used for scheduling inspection capacity and assigning qualified inspectors.
reference: SAP PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Date from which this inspection plan version is effective and may be used to govern inspection lot execution. Part of the plan's validity period in SAP QM.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan Validity; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Date after which this inspection plan version expires and must not be used for new inspection lots. Supports plan lifecycle management and ensures only current-revision plans are applied.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan Validity; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each individual inspection result record captured during inspection lot execution.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 – Release of Products and Services

description: Foreign key linking to quality.quality_characteristic. Business justification: Each inspection result captures a measurement for a specific quality characteristic (CTQ). The characteristic_code and characteristic_name string fields are redundant once the FK to quality_characteri
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.defect_code. Business justification: When an inspection result identifies a defect, it references a standardized defect classification code. The defect_code and defect_description string fields are redundant once the FK to the defect_cod
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the measurement instrument or gauge used to capture this result, enabling traceability to calibration records and Measurement System Analysis (MSA) studies.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.1; AIAG MSA Manual 4th Edition

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Inspection results are child records of an inspection lot — each measured characteristic result belongs to a specific lot execution. The inspection_lot_number string field is redundant once the FK is
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.measurement_point. Business justification: Inspection results captured on production equipment reference the specific measurement point defined on that asset. Quality engineers use this link to correlate inspection outcomes with asset measurem
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.shop_order_operation. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded against the actual shop order operation being executed. Production supervisors and quality engineers use this to correlate defect data with specific manufacturing execu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded in a specific unit of measure. Referencing the product UOM master ensures consistent measurement units across quality results and eliminates unit duplication.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The Acceptable Quality Level (AQL) applied to this inspection, defining the maximum acceptable defect rate (e.g., 0.65, 1.0, 2.5, 4.0). Determines the sampling plan stringency.
reference: ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 2859-1 Sampling Procedures by Attributes; AIAG PPAP

description: Pass/fail conformance result for attribute-type characteristics (e.g., visual inspection, go/no-go gauge). Null for variable characteristics where measured_value is used.
reference: ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 Sampling Procedures for Attributes; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Production batch or lot number of the material being inspected, enabling full batch traceability for quality results and potential recall scenarios.
reference: SAP MM Batch Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820 (where applicable)

description: Classification of the characteristic as variable (measured numeric value), attribute (pass/fail conformance), or count (number of defects). Drives the applicable SPC methodology.
reference: ANSI/ASQ Z1.9 (Variable); ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 (Attribute); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: The quality conformance decision for this individual characteristic result: accepted (within spec), rejected (out of spec), conditional accept (use-as-is disposition), rework required, scrap, or pending review.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP QM Usage Decision

description: Process Capability Index (Cp) for this characteristic, measuring the ratio of the specification width to the process spread (6 sigma). Indicates potential process capability without centering.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 22514-2 Statistical Methods in Process Management

description: Process Capability Index Centered (Cpk) for this characteristic, measuring actual process capability accounting for process centering relative to specification limits. Key Six Sigma metric.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 22514-2; Six Sigma DMAIC Methodology

description: Category of inspection at which this result was captured: incoming goods inspection, in-process (shop floor), final inspection, First Article Inspection (FAI), audit, supplier, or customer return.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition (FAI)

description: Operation or step number within the inspection plan at which this characteristic was measured, aligning to the inspection plan structure in SAP QM.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Production stage at which the inspection was performed, enabling traceability of quality results across the manufacturing value stream.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; Siemens Opcenter MES Quality Module

description: Date and time when the measurement was physically taken and recorded, in ISO 8601 format. Critical for SPC time-series analysis and traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO 8601 Date and Time Format

description: Full name of the quality inspector who performed the measurement. Retained for audit trail and regulatory compliance purposes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Statistical Process Control (SPC) lower control limit for the characteristic, calculated from process data (typically mean - 3 sigma). Triggers SPC alerts when breached.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870 Control Charts

description: The minimum allowable value for the characteristic as defined in the engineering specification or drawing. Used for conformance determination and Cp/Cpk calculation.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ASME Y14.5

description: SAP material number or part number of the product or component being inspected. Enables quality performance analysis by material/SKU.
reference: SAP MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Actual numeric measurement recorded for a variable characteristic during inspection (e.g., 24.987 mm). Null for attribute-type characteristics.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO/IEC 17025 Measurement Traceability

description: Method or technique used to obtain the measurement (e.g., CMM, caliper, micrometer, optical comparator, hardness tester, visual, go/no-go gauge). Supports Measurement System Analysis (MSA).
reference: AIAG MSA Manual 4th Edition; ISO/IEC 17025; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised as a result of this failed inspection result. Null if the result is conforming. Links quality results to the NCR/CAPA workflow.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Non-Conformance Report

description: The engineering design target or nominal value for the characteristic as specified in the drawing or inspection plan.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; ASME Y14.5 Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing (GD&T)

description: Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the inspection was performed. Supports multi-site quality performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Plant Organizational Unit; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Indicates whether this result is a retest measurement taken after a previous failed or inconclusive result for the same characteristic on the same sample.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Retest Functionality

description: Sequential number indicating which retest attempt this result represents (1 = first retest, 2 = second retest, etc.). Value is 0 for the original measurement.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Retest Management

description: Sequential number of this individual sample or specimen within the inspection lot sample set, enabling traceability to specific physical units measured.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP QM Sample Management

description: Number of units or specimens included in the sample for this characteristic measurement, as defined by the sampling plan (AQL-based or fixed).
reference: ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9; ISO 2859 Sampling Procedures

description: Serial number of the specific unit being inspected, where serialized traceability is required (e.g., safety-critical components, high-value assemblies).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; SAP MM Serial Number Management

description: Date and time when the inspector formally signed off on this inspection result, confirming the measurement is complete and the conformance decision is final.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records (where applicable)

description: Identifies the specific SPC rule that was violated (e.g., 'Rule 1: Point beyond 3-sigma', 'Rule 2: 9 consecutive points same side', 'Rule 4: 14 alternating points'). Null if no violation.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition; Western Electric Statistical Quality Control Handbook; Nelson Rules

description: Indicates whether this measurement triggered an SPC rule violation (e.g., Western Electric rules, Nelson rules) such as a point beyond control limits or a run of consecutive points on one side of the mean.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2 Shewhart Control Charts; Western Electric Rules

description: Statistical Process Control (SPC) upper control limit for the characteristic, calculated from process data (typically mean + 3 sigma). Triggers SPC alerts when exceeded.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870 Control Charts

description: The maximum allowable value for the characteristic as defined in the engineering specification or drawing. Used for conformance determination and Cp/Cpk calculation.
reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ASME Y14.5

description: Code of the production work center or inspection station where the measurement was taken, enabling quality performance analysis at the work center level.
reference: SAP PP/QM Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES Work Center

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the SPC chart master record within the Quality Management System.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1 — Control of Production and Service Provision

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_hierarchy. Business justification: In contract manufacturing, SPC charts are maintained per customer to demonstrate process capability compliance. Customers may mandate specific SPC reporting — linking enables customer-specific statist
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.quality_characteristic. Business justification: An SPC chart monitors a specific quality characteristic (CTQ). The characteristic_name and characteristic_type string fields are redundant once the FK to quality_characteristic is established, as thes
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: An SPC chart is defined within the context of a control plan — the control plan specifies the control chart type, sample size, and frequency. The control_plan_reference string field is redundant once
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.measurement_point. Business justification: SPC charts monitor process variation at specific measurement points on equipment. Quality engineers configure SPC charts against asset measurement points to detect equipment-driven variation — standar
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: SPC charts are configured for specific production or storage locations to monitor process variation at that physical point. Quality engineers assign charts to locations to ensure measurements are cont
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: SPC charts monitor process capability at a specific work center. Quality engineers configure and review SPC charts per machine/station — the work center is the primary dimension for statistical proces
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or employee identifier of the quality engineer or quality manager who approved this SPC chart configuration for production use. Required for PPAP documentation and ISO 9001 audit trails.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition

description: Date on which the SPC chart configuration was formally approved for production use by the responsible quality authority.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition

description: Date on which the SPC chart baseline (center line and control limits) was established from the initial process study. Used to track when the chart was last recalibrated.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Number of subgroups collected during the initial baseline study used to establish the center line and control limits. AIAG recommends a minimum of 25 subgroups for a stable baseline.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 5

business_glossary_term: SPC Center Line (Grand Mean / Process Average)
description: Center line value of the primary SPC chart representing the process grand mean (Xbar-bar) or process proportion (p-bar), established during the baseline study phase. Used as the reference for control limit calculations.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 5

description: Human-readable business identifier for the SPC chart, used for cross-referencing in quality documentation, PPAP packages, and audit records.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information; AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition

description: Type of Statistical Process Control (SPC) control chart used to monitor the process characteristic. Xbar-R and Xbar-S are used for variable data with subgroups; I-MR for individual measurements; p, np, c, u charts for attribute data; CUSUM and EWMA for detecting small shifts.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; ISO 7870-2:2013

description: Statistical method used to calculate the control limits for this chart. Standard is 3-sigma (99.73% confidence). Some applications use 2-sigma warning limits or custom multipliers.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 6; ANSI/ASQ B1

description: Minimum acceptable target value for the Cp (Process Capability Index) for this characteristic. Cp measures the spread of the process relative to the specification width. Typical AIAG minimum is 1.33 for new processes.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 22514-1:2014

business_glossary_term: Process Capability Index Centered Target (Cpk Target)
description: Minimum acceptable target value for the Cpk (Centered Process Capability Index) for this characteristic. Cpk accounts for process centering relative to specification limits. Typical AIAG minimum is 1.33 for new processes.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 22514-1:2014

description: Timestamp when the SPC chart master record was first created in the Quality Management System. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 — Control of Documented Information

description: Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and monitored characteristic of the SPC chart for quality documentation and operator guidance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition

description: Identifies the set of run rules applied to detect out-of-control conditions on the SPC chart. Common rule sets include Western Electric (AT&T) rules, Nelson rules, and AIAG rules. Governs alarm logic in Siemens Opcenter MES Quality module.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; Western Electric Statistical Quality Control Handbook; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 7

description: Date from which this SPC chart configuration is effective and enforced in production monitoring. Supports version-controlled chart management aligned with engineering change processes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; AIAG APQP 2nd Edition

description: Date on which this SPC chart configuration expires and must be reviewed or replaced. Supports scheduled revalidation cycles required by quality management systems.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition

description: Type of measurement gauge or instrument used to collect data for this SPC chart, e.g., 'CMM', 'Caliper', 'Vision System', 'Load Cell'. Supports Gauge R&R (Measurement System Analysis) traceability.
reference: AIAG MSA Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5 — Monitoring and Measuring Resources

description: Lower Control Limit for the primary chart, calculated as the process center minus three standard deviations (3-sigma). Measurements below LCL trigger an out-of-control signal. May be zero or null for attribute charts where negative values are not meaningful.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 6; ANSI/ASQ B1

description: Lower engineering specification limit for the monitored characteristic as defined in the product drawing or control plan. Used to calculate process capability indices (Cp, Cpk). Distinct from the statistical LCL.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; AIAG APQP 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

business_glossary_term: SPC Chart Record Last Modified Timestamp
description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SPC chart master record. Supports change tracking and audit compliance under ISO 9001.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 — Control of Documented Information

description: Indicates whether a Measurement System Analysis (MSA) / Gauge R&R study is required for the measurement system used with this SPC chart, as mandated by AIAG PPAP and APQP requirements.
reference: AIAG MSA Reference Manual 4th Edition; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Descriptive name of the SPC chart identifying the monitored process characteristic, e.g., 'Shaft Diameter Xbar-R Chart — Line 3'.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Manufacturing operation or process step number (from the Bill of Process / routing) at which this SPC chart is applied, as defined in SAP S/4HANA PP routing or Siemens Opcenter MES work order operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module — Routing Operations; Siemens Opcenter MES; AIAG APQP 2nd Edition

description: Engineering part number or material number of the product or component whose characteristic is monitored by this SPC chart. Corresponds to the material master in SAP S/4HANA MM and the component in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Material Master; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition

description: Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where this SPC chart is applied. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations and plant-level quality reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure — Plant; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

business_glossary_term: Range / Spread Chart Center Line (R-bar / S-bar)
description: Center line of the range or standard deviation sub-chart, representing the average range (R-bar) or average standard deviation (S-bar) from the baseline study. Null for single-chart types.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 5

business_glossary_term: Range / Spread Chart Lower Control Limit (LCL-R)
description: Lower Control Limit for the range (R) or standard deviation (S) sub-chart. Typically zero for small subgroup sizes (n < 7) in Xbar-R charts. Null for single-chart types.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 6

business_glossary_term: Range / Spread Chart Upper Control Limit (UCL-R)
description: Upper Control Limit for the range (R) or standard deviation (S) sub-chart used in Xbar-R and Xbar-S charts to monitor process variability. Null for single-chart types such as p-chart or c-chart.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 6

description: Defines the condition that triggers recalculation of the SPC chart control limits, such as a scheduled review, an Engineering Change Notice (ECN), a process change event, or automatic recalculation after a defined number of new subgroups.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; AIAG APQP 2nd Edition

description: Narrative explanation for the most recent revision of the SPC chart configuration, e.g., 'Process improvement after Kaizen event', 'Engineering Change Order ECO-2024-0112', 'Annual revalidation'.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG APQP 2nd Edition

description: Defined frequency or interval at which subgroup samples are collected for SPC monitoring, e.g., 'Every 1 hour', 'Every 50 parts', 'Every shift'. Governs the sampling plan in Siemens Opcenter MES Quality module.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Unit of the sampling frequency interval, indicating whether sampling is time-based (minutes, hours, shifts) or production-volume-based (parts, batches).
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013

description: Numeric value of the sampling interval, used in conjunction with sampling_frequency_unit to define the complete sampling schedule, e.g., '2' hours or '100' parts.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013

description: Target sigma level for the monitored process characteristic, expressing the number of standard deviations between the process mean and the nearest specification limit. A Six Sigma target corresponds to 6.0.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; Six Sigma DMAIC Methodology; ISO 13053-1:2011

description: Current operational status of the SPC chart master record. Active charts are enforced in Siemens Opcenter MES for ongoing production monitoring. Suspended charts are temporarily halted pending review.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information Control; Siemens Opcenter MES Quality Module

description: Number of individual measurements collected per subgroup sample for the SPC chart. Determines the statistical basis for control limit calculations. Typically 2–10 for Xbar-R charts; 1 for I-MR charts.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 5

description: Upper Control Limit for the primary (mean or proportion) chart, calculated statistically as the process center plus three standard deviations (3-sigma). Measurements exceeding UCL trigger an out-of-control signal in Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870-2:2013 Clause 6; ANSI/ASQ B1

description: Engineering unit of measurement for the monitored characteristic, e.g., 'mm', 'kg', 'MPa', 'percent', 'count'. Governs how measurement readings are interpreted against control limits.
reference: ISO 80000-1:2022 — Quantities and Units; AIAG SPC Reference Manual

description: Upper engineering specification limit for the monitored characteristic as defined in the product drawing or control plan. Used to calculate process capability indices (Cp, Cpk). Distinct from the statistical UCL.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; AIAG APQP 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Version identifier of the SPC chart configuration, incremented when control limits, sampling parameters, or detection rules are revised. Supports audit trail and change management requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 — Control of Documented Information; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each individual SPC data point record captured during production monitoring.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1 – Monitoring, Measurement, Analysis and Evaluation

description: Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: SPC samples are measurements of specific quality characteristics. While an indirect path exists via spc_sample → spc_chart → characteristic, a direct FK improves query performance for SPC analysis and
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the measurement instrument or gauge used to collect the sample, enabling gauge R&R traceability and calibration status verification.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025; AIAG MSA Manual; SAP S/4HANA QM Equipment

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: SPC samples may be collected during an inspection lot execution. The inspection_lot_number string field is redundant once the FK to inspection_lot is established, enabling traceability from SPC data p
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.measurement_reading. Business justification: SPC samples can be sourced directly from asset measurement readings captured by sensors or condition monitoring systems. Linking eliminates duplicate data entry and enables real-time SPC from asset te
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: SPC samples are collected during specific production shifts. Shift-level stratification is essential for identifying whether process variation is shift-dependent — a standard SPC analysis dimension in
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.spc_chart. Business justification: SPC samples are data points collected for a specific SPC chart. Each sample belongs to a chart that defines the control limits and characteristic being monitored. The chart_type, characteristic_name,
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Production batch or lot number associated with the sampled material, enabling batch-level traceability and quality disposition.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Management

description: Center line value of the control chart (grand mean X-double-bar or mean range R-bar) representing the process average used as the baseline for control limit calculation.
reference: ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Reference to the APQP Control Plan document that governs the SPC monitoring requirements for this characteristic, linking sample data to the formal quality control plan.
reference: AIAG APQP and Control Plan Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; PPAP 4th Edition

description: Process Capability Index (Cp) calculated at the time of sampling, measuring the ratio of specification width to process spread (6-sigma). Indicates potential capability without centering adjustment.
reference: ISO 22514-2 Statistical Methods in Process Management; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; Six Sigma DMAIC; AIAG SPC Manual

description: Centered Process Capability Index (Cpk) calculated at the time of sampling, accounting for process mean offset from the specification midpoint. Key Six Sigma metric for process performance.
reference: ISO 22514-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; Six Sigma DMAIC; AIAG SPC Manual; APQP/PPAP requirements

description: Timestamp when the SPC sample record was created in the system, used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 – Data Integrity

description: Indicates how the measurement was captured: manually entered by operator, automatically collected from a connected gauge, received from MES, SCADA system, or IIoT sensor. Affects data quality confidence.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity; Siemens MindSphere IIoT; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Lower control limit for the monitored statistic calculated from process baseline data. Signals out-of-control condition when the statistic falls below this value.
reference: ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; Western Electric Statistical Quality Control Handbook

description: Lower engineering specification limit for the quality characteristic as defined in the design or customer requirement. Used for Cp/Cpk capability index calculation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; Six Sigma DMAIC

description: SAP material number of the product or component being produced and monitored, enabling quality analysis by material.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: The actual numeric measurement recorded for the monitored quality characteristic at the time of sampling. Core data point for SPC analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; ISO 7870; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Routing operation number within the production order at which the SPC sample was taken, identifying the specific process step being monitored.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Siemens Opcenter MES Routing

description: Indicates whether this sample point triggered any out-of-control signal based on applied Western Electric or Nelson rules. True triggers immediate operator notification and potential process investigation.
reference: Western Electric Statistical Quality Control Handbook; ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the SPC sample was collected, enabling multi-site quality performance comparison.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1; SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure

description: Sequential position of this individual measurement within its subgroup (e.g., 1 of 5). Used to reconstruct subgroup composition for SPC recalculation.
reference: ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Current processing status of the SPC sample record (e.g., COLLECTED upon initial entry, VALIDATED after quality review, REJECTED if measurement error detected, VOIDED if sample invalidated).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; Siemens Opcenter MES Quality

description: Exact date and time when the subgroup sample measurement was collected on the shop floor, used for time-series SPC charting and trend analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9

description: Code referencing the sampling plan frequency rule applied (e.g., EVERY_50_PARTS, HOURLY, EVERY_BATCH), as defined in the inspection plan. Supports audit of sampling compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9; SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Plan

description: Code identifying the production shift during which the SPC sample was collected (e.g., DAY, NIGHT, AFTERNOON). Enables shift-level quality stratification and comparison.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Indicates whether the measured value falls outside the engineering specification limits (USL or LSL), representing a non-conforming measurement regardless of control chart status.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Arithmetic mean of all measured values within the subgroup (X-bar). Plotted on the Xbar control chart to monitor process location.
reference: ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; Montgomery – Statistical Quality Control

description: Identifier grouping individual measurements into a rational subgroup for Xbar-R or Xbar-S chart calculations. Multiple sample records share the same subgroup_id.
reference: ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; ISO 7870-2 Shewhart Control Charts

description: Range (maximum minus minimum) of measured values within the subgroup. Plotted on the R-chart to monitor process variability for small subgroup sizes.
reference: ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Number of individual measurements in the subgroup (n). Determines the appropriate control chart constants (A2, D3, D4) used for control limit calculation.
reference: ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; ISO 7870-2; Montgomery – Statistical Quality Control

description: Sample standard deviation of measured values within the subgroup. Used in Xbar-S charts for larger subgroup sizes as a more efficient estimator of process spread.
reference: ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; Montgomery – Statistical Quality Control

description: Engineering unit of the measured value (e.g., mm, kg, MPa, °C, %). Required for correct interpretation and comparison of SPC data across characteristics.
reference: ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Upper control limit for the monitored statistic (mean, range, or standard deviation) calculated from process baseline data. Signals out-of-control condition when exceeded.
reference: ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; Western Electric Statistical Quality Control Handbook

description: Upper engineering specification limit for the quality characteristic as defined in the design or customer requirement. Used for Cp/Cpk capability index calculation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3; Six Sigma DMAIC

description: Free-text explanation for why a sample record was voided (e.g., gauge malfunction, operator error, environmental disturbance). Populated only when sample_status is VOIDED.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Identifies the specific Western Electric rule that triggered the out-of-control signal (e.g., WE1: one point beyond 3-sigma, WE2: nine consecutive points on one side of center line). NONE if no violation detected.
reference: Western Electric Statistical Quality Control Handbook (1956); ISO 7870-2 Annex B; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Code identifying the specific work center or production cell where the monitored characteristic was measured, sourced from SAP S/4HANA PP/QM.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center

description: Zone classification of the plotted point relative to the control chart center line (Zone C: within 1-sigma, Zone B: 1–2 sigma, Zone A: 2–3 sigma). Used for Western Electric pattern rule evaluation.
reference: Western Electric Statistical Quality Control Handbook; ISO 7870-2; ANSI/ASQ B1-B3

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single usage decision record within the Quality Management System. Serves as the primary key for the usage_decision data product.
reference: SAP QM Module — Usage Decision Object (QA11/QA12)

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: Usage decisions (accept/reject/rework) are made at the batch level after inspection. Quality managers post usage decisions that directly update batch status in production — a fundamental QM-to-batch m
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.quality_capa. Business justification: When a usage decision results in rejection or conditional release, a CAPA is typically initiated. This FK links the formal quality disposition (usage decision) to the corrective action record, providi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Usage decisions (accept, reject, conditional release) are made against a specific goods receipt. The decision directly determines whether received stock is released to inventory or returned to supplie
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A usage decision is formally made at the conclusion of an inspection lot — it is the disposition record for that lot (accept, reject, conditional release). The inspection_lot_number string field is re
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: When a usage decision results in rejection or conditional acceptance, a quarantine hold is placed on the inventory. Quality teams reference the hold record to track disposition status. This link drive
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Quantity of material from the inspection lot that is accepted and posted to unrestricted-use stock following the usage decision. May be less than the total inspection quantity when partial acceptance or split posting is applied.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Stock Posting (Accepted); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Date and time at which the secondary approval for the usage decision was granted. Used for approval cycle time measurement and audit trail documentation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP S/4HANA QM — Approval Workflow

description: Name or employee identifier of the quality manager or authorized approver who provided secondary approval for the usage decision, when requires_approval is true. Supports segregation of duties and audit trail compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3; SAP S/4HANA QM — Approval Workflow; IATF 16949

description: Free-text field for the quality engineer to record additional observations, justifications, or contextual notes related to the usage decision. Supports audit trail documentation and provides qualitative context not captured by structured fields.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision Long Text

description: Free-text description of the specific conditions, restrictions, or requirements that must be met for material released under a conditional usage decision to be used in production or shipped to customers. Applicable only when decision_category is 'conditional_release'.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP S/4HANA QM — Conditional Release; IATF 16949 Clause 8.6.1

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where the usage decision was made. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance reporting (e.g., CE Marking, RoHS/REACH, FDA) and regional quality performance analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS/REACH Directive; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: High-level classification of the usage decision outcome used for reporting and analytics. Maps multiple decision codes to a standardized category for cross-plant and cross-material quality performance analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision

description: Standardized quality decision code assigned at the conclusion of the inspection lot, representing the formal disposition outcome (e.g., A=Accept, R=Reject, C=Conditional Release, S=Scrap). Drives downstream stock posting in SAP QM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision Code (QA11); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: The calendar date on which the formal usage decision was made and recorded in the Quality Management System. Used for cycle time analysis, compliance reporting, and inspection lot closure tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision Date (QA11); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Human-readable description of the usage decision code, providing context for the disposition outcome (e.g., 'Accepted — All characteristics within specification', 'Rejected — Critical defect found').
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Free-text explanation provided when a previously made usage decision is reversed or overturned. Documents the business justification for the reversal, supporting audit trail and corrective action analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision Reversal; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; IATF 16949

description: Current lifecycle status of the usage decision record. Tracks whether the decision is still open (inspection lot not yet closed), under review, formally decided, reversed (decision overturned), or cancelled.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Inspection Lot Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the usage decision was recorded in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and precise cycle time measurement from inspection lot creation to decision closure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

description: Primary defect classification code associated with the usage decision when the outcome is a rejection or conditional release. Drawn from the standardized defect catalog and used for Pareto analysis, FMEA updates, and CAPA initiation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Defect Code Catalog; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; PFMEA; Six Sigma

description: Textual description of the primary defect or nonconformance identified during inspection that influenced the usage decision. Provides qualitative context for the defect code and supports root cause analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Defect Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA Workflow

description: The date on which the inspection lot was originally created in SAP QM. Combined with decision_date, enables calculation of inspection cycle time (time from lot creation to usage decision), a key quality process KPI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Inspection Lot Creation Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: The business process that triggered the creation of the inspection lot subject to this usage decision. Indicates whether the inspection arose from a goods receipt, production order completion, outbound delivery, customer complaint, or other quality event.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Inspection Lot Origin (01=GR, 04=Production, 10=Delivery, etc.); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Total quantity of material submitted for inspection in the inspection lot, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents the full lot size against which the usage decision is applied.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Inspection Lot Quantity; ISO 2859-1 (AQL Sampling)

description: Classification of the inspection stage at which the usage decision is made. Distinguishes between incoming goods inspection, in-process inspection, final inspection, First Article Inspection (FAI), and other inspection categories.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; APQP/PPAP — FAI

description: Short descriptive name of the material or component under inspection, as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable context for the usage decision record without requiring a join to the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master Description

description: The SAP material master number identifying the material or component subject to the inspection lot and usage decision. Enables traceability from quality decision back to the material master and Bill of Materials (BOM).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2 (Traceability)

description: Reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised in association with this usage decision, applicable when the decision results in a rejection or conditional release. Links the usage decision to the formal nonconformance management workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Quality Notification (NCR); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; IATF 16949

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or storage facility where the inspection lot was created and the usage decision was made. Supports multi-plant quality performance reporting and regional compliance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Organizational Structure (Plant); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Numeric quality score (0–100) assigned to the inspection lot at the time of the usage decision, reflecting the overall conformance level of the inspected material. Derived from inspection results and used for supplier quality scorecards and trend analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Quality Score; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; IATF 16949 Clause 9.1.1

description: The unit of measure (UoM) applicable to all quantity fields on the usage decision (inspection_quantity, accepted_quantity, rejected_quantity, scrap_quantity). Examples: EA (each), KG (kilogram), M (meter), L (liter).
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 (Quantities and Units)

description: Indicates whether the material subject to this usage decision is placed under a regulatory hold (e.g., due to RoHS/REACH non-compliance, CE Marking failure, or FDA regulatory action). When true, stock posting is blocked pending regulatory clearance.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; CE Marking; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; ISO 14001:2015

description: Quantity of material from the inspection lot that is rejected and posted to blocked stock or returned to supplier following the usage decision. Drives downstream NCR creation and disposition workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Stock Posting (Rejected); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Indicates whether the usage decision requires a secondary approval from a quality manager or designated authority before it becomes effective. Applicable for high-risk materials, critical components, or decisions involving conditional release.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; IATF 16949 Clause 8.6.1; PPAP — Level 3/4 Approval

description: Name or employee identifier of the quality engineer who made and recorded the formal usage decision. Establishes accountability and supports audit trail requirements under ISO 9001 and regulatory frameworks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision User; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Quantity of material from the inspection lot that is designated for scrapping as part of the usage decision disposition. Used for scrap rate calculation and cost of poor quality (COPQ) reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM/MM — Scrap Posting; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Lean Manufacturing — Scrap Rate

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which the usage decision record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse and enables reconciliation between systems.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse — Silver Layer Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA QM; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: The inventory posting instruction generated by the usage decision, directing SAP MM to transfer stock from quality inspection stock to the appropriate stock category (e.g., unrestricted, blocked, scrap). This is the primary downstream trigger for inventory movement in SAP QM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Usage Decision Stock Posting; SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Movement; Infor WMS

description: Current execution status of the stock posting instruction associated with the usage decision. Indicates whether the inventory transfer triggered by the decision has been successfully posted in SAP MM or is pending/failed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM/MM — Goods Movement Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: SAP storage location within the plant where the inspected stock resides. Used to determine the source location for stock posting instructions triggered by the usage decision (e.g., transfer from quality inspection stock to unrestricted or blocked stock).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/QM — Storage Location; Infor WMS — Inventory Tracking

description: SAP vendor/supplier number associated with the inspected material, applicable when the inspection lot originates from a goods receipt against a purchase order. Enables supplier quality performance tracking and scorecard reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/QM — Vendor Master; SAP Ariba — Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the quality certificate record in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_hierarchy. Business justification: Quality certificates (material certs, conformance certs) are issued to specific customers with shipments. Linking certificate to customer account enables traceability and supports customer audit reque
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Certificates are issued to a specific customer delivery/ship-to address. In manufacturing, certificates accompany physical shipments and must reference the correct destination address for compliance a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
