reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.goods_issue. Business justification: Quality certificates (material certs, conformance certs) are issued at the point of goods issue/shipment. Linking certificate to goods_issue ensures the right certificate accompanies the right deliver
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Certificates of conformance accompany specific deliveries and are matched to the goods receipt document. Receiving inspection teams verify the certificate covers the exact batch/lot received before re
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Quality certificates (material certs, conformance certs) are issued for specific products. Customers and auditors require product-level certificates; quality teams issue and store them per product.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Certificates of Conformance (CoC) and Certificates of Analysis (CoA) are issued based on inspection lot results. This FK links the certificate to the inspection event that validated the product qualit
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Material certificates reference the engineering material specification they certify compliance against. Quality and procurement teams verify that material certs match the approved material spec before
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Quality certificates (material certs, conformance certs) are issued for a specific product variant. Customers and regulatory bodies require certificates tied to the exact variant shipped — this is sta
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: For high-value or regulated serialized products (aerospace, medical), certificates are issued at the individual unit level. The certificate must reference the exact serialized unit to satisfy traceabi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment_item. Business justification: Quality certificates (e.g., material test reports, CoC) are issued per shipment item and must travel with the goods. Logistics teams attach certificates to specific shipment items for customs, custome
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Material and conformance certificates are issued by suppliers and must be tracked against the supplying entity. Procurement requires valid certificates (e.g., ISO, RoHS, material certs) before approvi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.usage_decision. Business justification: Certificates of Conformance are issued following a formal acceptance usage decision. This FK provides direct traceability from the certificate to the quality disposition that authorized its issuance.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Comma-separated list of quality, safety, or regulatory standards met by the certified product (e.g., ISO 9001:2015, IEC 61131, RoHS, REACH, CE, UL, ANSI). Stored as a delimited string for reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; IEC 62443; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; CE Marking Directive

description: Timestamp when the certificate was formally approved by the authorized signatory, marking the transition from draft to approved status.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP QM Certificate Workflow

description: Full name of the quality authority (e.g., Quality Manager, Quality Engineer) who authorized and signed the certificate, as required for legal and regulatory validity.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; EN 10204 Clause 5

description: Job title or role of the authorized signatory (e.g., Quality Manager, Director of Quality Assurance), providing context for the authority level of the certification.
Manufacturing batch or lot number for which the certificate is issued, enabling full traceability from raw material through finished goods.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Indicates whether the product requires CE marking under applicable EU directives, confirming conformity with European health, safety, and environmental protection standards.
reference: EU CE Marking Directives; EU Decision 768/2008/EC

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the issuing plant's location, used for regulatory compliance, CE marking, and export documentation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS/REACH Regulation

description: Timestamp when the quality certificate record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5 – Data Integrity

description: Customer's own part number or drawing number for the certified product, required for customer-specific certificate formats and cross-reference.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; PPAP Customer-Specific Requirements

description: Description of any customer-specific quality certificate requirements (e.g., AIAG, IATF 16949 customer specifics, additional test data, format mandates) that must be met for this certificate.
reference: IATF 16949:2016 Customer-Specific Requirements; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2

description: Reference number of any approved deviation or waiver under which the product was released, indicating conditional conformance and linking to the formal deviation approval record.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Deviation Management

description: Reference path or identifier to the signed certificate document stored in the document management system (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter DMS or SAP DMS), enabling retrieval of the original signed PDF.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management

description: Date after which the certificate is no longer considered valid, applicable for time-limited certifications or shelf-life-controlled products.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Customer-Specific Quality Requirements

description: Date on which the quality certificate was officially issued and signed, as printed on the certificate document.
SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility that produced the goods and issued the quality certificate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/QM Plant Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Full descriptive name of the manufacturing plant issuing the certificate, used for display on printed certificates and customer-facing documents.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Business-assigned unique certificate number used for external reference, customer communication, and traceability. Typically generated by SAP QM or the issuing plant's QMS.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP QM Certificate of Conformance

description: Summary conformance decision for the certified batch or lot: Conforming (all characteristics within specification), Non-Conforming, or Conditionally Conforming (released with deviation).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SAP QM Usage Decision

description: Descriptive name of the part or material covered by the certificate, as defined in the SAP material master or PLM system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Engineering revision level of the part at the time the certificate was issued, ensuring traceability to the correct design baseline.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: SAP production order number under which the certified batch or lot was manufactured, linking the certificate to the manufacturing execution record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Customer's purchase order number referenced on the certificate, providing contractual linkage between the quality document and the commercial transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Order; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3

description: Total quantity of product units or material covered by this certificate.

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the certified product complies with the EU REACH Regulation (EC No 1907/2006) regarding chemical substance registration and restriction.
reference: EU REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3

description: Revision or version identifier of the certificate, incremented when a certificate is amended or reissued to reflect updated test results or corrections.

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the certified product complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3

description: Operational system of record from which this quality certificate record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Siemens Opcenter MES), supporting data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Engineering specification, drawing, or standard number against which the product was tested and certified.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Current lifecycle status of the quality certificate, from initial draft through issuance, potential supersession, or revocation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 – Control of Documented Information

description: Certificate number of the replacement certificate when this certificate has been superseded, enabling version chain traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 – Control of Documented Information

description: Narrative or structured summary of key test results included on the certificate, such as dimensional, mechanical, chemical, or functional test outcomes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; EN 10204 Type 3.1/3.2 Test Reports

description: Classification of the quality certificate: Certificate of Conformance (CoC), Certificate of Analysis (CoA), Material Test Report (MTR), First Article Inspection (FAI) Certificate, Inspection Certificate, Declaration of Conformity, Test Report, or Other.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; EN 10204 Material Test Reports

description: Unit of measure for the certified quantity (e.g., EA, KG, M, L, PC), aligned with SAP base unit of measure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each gauge or measurement instrument master record in the Quality Management System.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5 – Monitoring and Measuring Resources

description: Foreign key linking to asset.class. Business justification: Gauges are classified as assets using the asset classification hierarchy. Asset management teams assign class to gauges for depreciation, inventory, and lifecycle tracking — a standard EAM practice in
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_plan. Business justification: Gauges are physical assets requiring scheduled preventive maintenance. Quality and asset teams assign a maintenance plan to each gauge to ensure it remains fit for measurement use — standard practice
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Gauges are assigned to specific work centers where they're used for inspection. Quality technicians need to locate gauges by work center for production support.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Manufacturer-specified accuracy or maximum permissible error (MPE) of the gauge, expressed in the gauge's unit of measure. Used to assess fitness for purpose against tolerance requirements.
reference: AIAG MSA Manual 4th Edition; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 – Calibration Laboratory Competence

description: Internal fixed-asset tag or barcode label number affixed to the physical gauge, used for asset tracking in the EAM/CMMS system (Maximo) and physical inventory audits.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; IFRS 16 – Asset Identification

description: Physical location where the gauge is assigned and stored when not in use, such as a plant code, building, room, or work center (e.g., 'Plant 01 – Metrology Lab', 'Work Center WC-100').
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; SAP PM Functional Location

description: Reference number of the most recent calibration certificate issued by the calibrating laboratory. Provides traceability to national or international measurement standards.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2

description: Defined frequency of calibration expressed in calendar days, as established by the metrology program. Drives automatic calculation of the next calibration due date.
Accreditation body certificate number for the calibration laboratory (e.g., A2LA, UKAS, DAkkS accreditation number), confirming traceability to national measurement standards.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017; ILAC MRA; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2

description: Name of the internal or external laboratory that performed the most recent calibration. External labs should be accredited to ISO/IEC 17025.

Current calibration validity status of the gauge. 'Calibrated' indicates the gauge is within its valid calibration period; 'overdue' indicates the calibration due date has passed; 'out_of_service' indicates the gauge is removed from use pending repair or calibration.
Indicates whether calibration is performed by an internal metrology lab, an external accredited laboratory, or on-site by a field calibration service.
Financial cost center responsible for the gauge, used for CAPEX/OPEX allocation of calibration costs and depreciation in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Cost Center Accounting; IFRS 16; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where the gauge is physically located. Supports multinational regulatory compliance and regional metrology authority reporting.
Timestamp when the gauge master record was first created in the Quality Management System. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 – Data Integrity

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the purchase cost and any financial values associated with the gauge (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Full descriptive name of the gauge or measurement instrument, including make, model, and key measurement capability (e.g., 'Mitutoyo Digital Caliper 0-150mm').
Indicates whether this gauge serves as a reference or master standard used to calibrate other working gauges within the internal metrology hierarchy.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2 – Measurement Traceability

description: Date on which the most recent calibration of the gauge was performed. Used to calculate calibration status and next due date.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the gauge master record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and data synchronization with source systems.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP QM Change Document

description: Date on which the most recent Measurement System Analysis (MSA) or Gauge Repeatability and Reproducibility (Gauge R&R) study was conducted for this gauge.
reference: AIAG MSA Manual 4th Edition; IATF 16949; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) of the gauge or measurement instrument (e.g., Mitutoyo, Hexagon, Starrett, Fluke).
Upper bound of the gauge's measurement range, expressed in the gauge's unit of measure. Defines the maximum measurable value for capability assessment.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ANSI/ASME B89

description: Lower bound of the gauge's measurement range, expressed in the gauge's unit of measure. Defines the minimum measurable value for capability assessment.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ANSI/ASME B89

description: Manufacturer-assigned model or part number for the gauge, used for procurement, spare parts identification, and technical documentation lookup.
Indicates whether a formal Measurement System Analysis (MSA) — including Gauge R&R study — is required for this gauge per the AIAG MSA manual or PPAP requirements.
reference: AIAG MSA Manual 4th Edition; IATF 16949; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Scheduled date by which the gauge must be recalibrated to remain in a valid calibration status. Derived from last calibration date plus calibration interval.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; ILAC G24

description: Human-readable business identifier for the gauge, typically assigned by the metrology or quality department and used on calibration certificates and inspection records.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; SAP QM Equipment Number convention

description: Defined action to be taken when the gauge is found out of tolerance during calibration. Drives the CAPA or NCR workflow for affected measurements made since the last valid calibration.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ILAC G24; AIAG MSA Manual

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site to which the gauge is assigned. Supports multi-site gauge inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/QM Plant Organization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Original acquisition cost of the gauge in the transaction currency. Used for asset valuation, CAPEX reporting, and cost-benefit analysis of calibration vs. replacement decisions.
reference: IFRS 16; GAAP – Fixed Asset Accounting

description: Date on which the gauge was originally purchased or acquired. Used for asset lifecycle management, depreciation calculation, and warranty tracking.
reference: IFRS 16; GAAP – Fixed Asset Accounting; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Indicates whether a measurement recall investigation is required for inspection results recorded with this gauge since its last valid calibration, triggered when the gauge is found out of tolerance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ILAC G24 – Calibration Intervals

description: Smallest increment of measurement the gauge can display or detect, expressed in the gauge's unit of measure (e.g., 0.001 mm for a digital micrometer). Used in Measurement System Analysis (MSA).
reference: AIAG MSA Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Name or employee ID of the quality or metrology technician responsible for managing the gauge's calibration schedule and custody. Supports accountability under ISO 9001.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; SAP QM Responsible Person

business_glossary_term: Gauge Repeatability and Reproducibility (Gauge R&R) Percentage
description: Result of the most recent Gauge R&R study expressed as a percentage of total process variation or tolerance. Values below 10% are acceptable; 10–30% may be conditionally acceptable; above 30% requires corrective action.
reference: AIAG MSA Manual 4th Edition; IATF 16949; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Operational system of record from which the gauge master record was sourced (e.g., SAP QM, Maximo EAM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework – Data Provenance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Overall lifecycle status of the gauge master record. 'Active' indicates the gauge is available for use in inspection; 'retired' indicates it has been permanently removed from service.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; Maximo EAM Asset Status

description: National or international measurement standard to which the gauge's calibration is traceable (e.g., NIST, PTB, NPL, BIPM). Required for ISO 9001 and ISO/IEC 17025 compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; ISO/IEC 17025:2017; NIST Traceability Policy

description: Classification of the measurement instrument by its measurement principle or physical form, such as CMM (Coordinate Measuring Machine), caliper, micrometer, go/no-go gauge, or torque wrench.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ANSI/ASME B89 Dimensional Metrology Standards

description: Standard unit of measure in which the gauge records measurements (e.g., mm for dimensional gauges, Nm for torque wrenches, °C for thermometers).
reference: ISO 80000 – Quantities and Units; ANSI/ASME B89

description: Date on which the manufacturer's warranty for the gauge expires. Used to determine whether repair or replacement costs are covered under warranty.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; Maximo EAM Warranty Management

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each gauge calibration event record in the Quality Management System.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5 - Monitoring and Measuring Resources

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: External gauge calibration services are procured from certified labs and invoiced via AP. The calibration record must reference the AP invoice to satisfy audit requirements (ISO 9001, IATF 16949) prov
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.calibration_record. Business justification: Gauge calibrations are asset calibration events. Asset management owns the calibration schedule and records; quality references these to validate measurement equipment certification before use.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Unique identifier of the gauge or measurement instrument being calibrated, as registered in the measurement equipment master.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.4

description: Description of any adjustments, repairs, or corrective actions performed on the gauge during the calibration event.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Indicates whether the gauge required physical adjustment or repair during the calibration event to bring it within tolerance.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

description: Condition of the gauge as found at the start of calibration, before any adjustment. Indicates whether the instrument was within tolerance prior to calibration.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3; MSA Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Actual measured value recorded at the start of calibration before any adjustment, used for MSA and drift analysis.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; MSA Reference Manual (AIAG); ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

description: Condition of the gauge after calibration and any adjustments or repairs. Indicates the final state of the instrument upon return to service.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3; MSA Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Actual measured value recorded after calibration and any adjustments, confirming the instrument is within tolerance before return to service.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; MSA Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Human-readable business identifier for the calibration event, used for traceability and audit trail purposes across the QMS.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2 - Measurement Traceability; ISO/IEC 17025:2017

description: Defined recalibration interval in calendar days, used to schedule the next calibration event and manage the calibration program.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.4.10; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

description: Name of the calibration laboratory (internal lab name or external accredited lab) that performed the calibration.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8.2; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

description: Indicates whether the calibration was performed by an internal metrology lab or an accredited external calibration laboratory.
Reference number of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or work instruction used to perform the calibration, ensuring repeatability and compliance.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Overall pass/fail outcome of the calibration event. A 'fail' result triggers gauge withdrawal from service and initiates a CAPA or NCR process.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

description: Reference standard or master gauge used during calibration (e.g., NIST-traceable gauge block set, reference thermometer), ensuring measurement traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.5; NIST

description: Date on which the calibration certificate was issued by the calibration laboratory.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8.2

description: Unique certificate number issued by the calibration laboratory upon completion of calibration, serving as the primary traceability document reference.
Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or remarks recorded by the calibration technician or quality engineer during the calibration event.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the facility where the calibration was performed, supporting multinational traceability and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Code identifying the department or work center responsible for the gauge (e.g., metrology lab, production floor, quality inspection).
Accreditation certificate number of the external calibration laboratory (e.g., A2LA, NVLAP, UKAS accreditation number), confirming traceability to national standards.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.2; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3; NIST Traceability Policy

description: Name of the manufacturer or brand of the gauge or measurement instrument.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.4.3

description: Expanded measurement uncertainty (U) associated with the calibration result, expressed at a 95% confidence level (k=2), as required for MSA and traceability.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; JCGM 100:2008 (GUM); MSA Reference Manual (AIAG)

description: Unit of measure for the as-found and as-left calibration values (e.g., mm, inch, Nm, bar, °C, V, A).
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; SI Units (BIPM)

description: Manufacturer model or part number of the gauge, used for procurement, spare parts, and calibration procedure lookup.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.4.3

description: Reference number of the associated Measurement System Analysis (MSA) study (Gauge R&R, linearity, bias study) linked to this calibration event.
reference: MSA Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO/IEC 17025:2017; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.5.1

description: Scheduled date by which the next calibration of this gauge must be completed to maintain measurement traceability and compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

description: Indicates whether the gauge has been removed from service due to calibration failure, damage, or expiry. Triggers quarantine and prevents use in production measurements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.4.9

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the gauge is assigned and the calibration was performed.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Indicates whether a measurement recall is required for parts or products measured with this gauge during the period it was found out of tolerance, triggering a quality impact assessment.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; AIAG MSA Reference Manual; IATF 16949

description: Name or employee ID of the quality engineer or metrology supervisor who reviewed and approved the calibration record.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.8.2

description: Operational system of record from which this calibration record was sourced (e.g., SAP QM, Maximo EAM), supporting data lineage and audit trail in the lakehouse.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; Data Governance Best Practice

description: Certificate or reference number of the calibration standard used, establishing the metrological traceability chain to national or international measurement standards.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.5; NIST Traceability Policy; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

description: Current calibration status of the gauge. 'Active' means in-service and within calibration period; 'overdue' means past due date; 'withdrawn' means removed from service pending calibration or repair.
Lower tolerance limit for the gauge measurement range, used to determine pass/fail of the calibration result.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

description: Upper tolerance limit for the gauge measurement range, used to determine pass/fail of the calibration result.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; ANSI/NCSL Z540.3

reference: Internal Data Governance Standard

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: account_classification contains a denormalized customer_segment STRING field that references the customer_segment reference table. Normalizing this to a FK eliminates the string duplication and ensure
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The ABC classification assigned to the customer account based on revenue contribution and strategic importance. 'A' represents the highest-value accounts (typically top 20% generating ~80% of revenue), 'B' represents mid-tier accounts, and 'C' represents lower-value accounts. Used in sales prioritization and resource allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD ABC Analysis; Pareto Principle; ISO 9001 Customer Management

description: The username or user ID of the individual who approved the classification assignment. Required for export control classifications (ECCN) and high-risk credit classifications where dual-control approval is mandated.
reference: EAR 15 CFR Part 730; SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Section 8.2

description: The username or user ID of the individual who assigned the classification to the customer account. Supports audit trail requirements and accountability for classification decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Audit Log; ISO 9001 Section 7.5; GDPR Article 5

description: The calendar date on which the classification was formally assigned to the customer account. Used for audit trails, compliance reporting, and tracking classification history.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; EAR 15 CFR; GDPR Article 5 (data accuracy)

description: A free-text or coded reason explaining why the classification was changed or updated. Supports audit trail requirements and provides business context for classification history. Examples: 'Annual ABC review', 'Export license obtained', 'Credit risk reassessment'.
reference: ISO 9001 Section 7.5; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents; EAR 15 CFR

description: The standardized short code representing the classification value within the classification scheme. Used for system-to-system integration, SAP S/4HANA customer classification fields, and export control screening lookups.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; EAR 15 CFR Part 774

description: Human-readable description of the classification value, providing business context for the assigned classification. For example, 'High-value strategic account' for ABC-A, or 'Dual-use item requiring export license' for an ECCN code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Customer Management

description: The ISO 639-1 two-letter language code in which the classification description and value are maintained. Supports multi-language classification management for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA Multi-Language Support

description: A numeric priority rank assigned to the classification record when multiple classifications of the same type exist for a customer account. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to resolve conflicts when multiple classifications apply simultaneously.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Classification; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: The formal scheme, standard, or framework under which the classification value is defined. Examples include 'SAP ABC Analysis', 'Nielsen Global Segment Framework', 'US EAR Export Control', 'EU Dual-Use Regulation', or a company-defined internal scheme.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; EAR 15 CFR; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; ISO 9001

value_regex: ABC|Nielsen|ECCN|EAR99|Industry Sector|Customer Segment|Credit Risk|Revenue Tier|Strategic|Regulatory|Export Control|Tax|Compliance|Custom
description: The category or scheme of classification applied to the customer account. Examples include ABC classification (revenue-based segmentation), Nielsen classification (market research segmentation), ECCN (Export Control Classification Number), EAR99 (Export Administration Regulations catch-all), industry sector, credit risk tier, and strategic account classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Classification; EAR (Export Administration Regulations) 15 CFR Part 774; ISO 9001 Customer Management

description: The specific classification value or code assigned to the customer account under the given classification type. For example, 'A', 'B', or 'C' for ABC classification; an ECCN code such as '3A001' for export control; or a Nielsen segment code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Classification; EAR 15 CFR Part 774; ISO 9001

description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country or jurisdiction in which this classification is applicable. Supports country-specific classification rules, export control by destination, and regional compliance requirements.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EAR 15 CFR; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821

description: The timestamp when the account classification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5; SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: The credit risk classification assigned to the customer account based on financial assessment, payment history, and creditworthiness. Drives credit limit setting, payment terms, and order release decisions in SAP S/4HANA FI/SD.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss; GAAP; ISO 9001

description: The SAP S/4HANA customer account group code that determines the number range, field selection, and partner functions applicable to the customer master record. Examples include KUNA (domestic customers), KUNE (export customers), CPDL (one-time customers).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master (Account Group); SAP Best Practices

description: The most recent date on which the customer account was screened against denied party and sanctions lists. Used to ensure screening is current and to schedule re-screening at required intervals.
reference: EAR 15 CFR Part 764; OFAC Sanctions; EU Regulation 2580/2001

description: The outcome of the most recent denied party and sanctions screening for the customer account. 'clear' indicates no match found; 'match_found' triggers a compliance hold; 'potential_match' requires manual review.
reference: EAR 15 CFR Part 764; OFAC Sanctions; EU Regulation 2580/2001

description: Indicates whether the customer account has been screened against denied party, debarred, and sanctioned entity lists (e.g., US BIS Denied Persons List, OFAC SDN List, EU Consolidated Sanctions List). True = screening has been performed.
reference: EAR 15 CFR Part 764; OFAC Sanctions; EU Regulation 2580/2001; Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS)

description: Indicates whether the customer account has been classified as EAR99 under the US Export Administration Regulations, meaning the associated goods or transactions do not require a specific export license for most destinations. True = EAR99 classification applies.
reference: EAR 15 CFR Part 734; Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS)

description: The Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) assigned to the customer account for export control screening purposes. Identifies whether the customer or their end-use requires an export license under the US Export Administration Regulations (EAR). Applicable for industrial manufacturing customers dealing in dual-use goods.
reference: EAR 15 CFR Part 774; Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS); ANSI

description: The date from which the classification becomes operationally effective for pricing, reporting, and compliance purposes. May differ from the assigned date if the classification is pre-dated or post-dated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001; EAR 15 CFR

description: The date on which the classification expires and is no longer valid. Applicable for time-bound classifications such as export control licenses, credit risk ratings, and contractual tier assignments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; EAR 15 CFR; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether an export license is required for transactions with this customer account based on their export control classification, destination country, and end-use. Drives export compliance screening workflows.
reference: EAR 15 CFR Part 730; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS)

description: The industry sector classification code assigned to the customer account, aligned with standard industry classification systems such as NAICS, SIC, or NACE. Used for market segmentation, reporting, and targeted sales strategies.
reference: NAICS (North American Industry Classification System); NACE Rev. 2 (EU); SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

description: The human-readable name of the industry sector assigned to the customer account. Complements the industry sector code for reporting and analytics purposes.
reference: NAICS; NACE Rev. 2; SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

description: Indicates whether this classification record is the primary or default classification for the given classification type on the customer account. True = this is the primary classification used in pricing and reporting when multiple classifications of the same type exist.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: The timestamp when the account classification record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5; SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: The pricing group code assigned to the customer account in SAP S/4HANA SD, used to determine applicable pricing conditions, discount schedules, and surcharges. Links the customer to a specific pricing strategy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions (VK11/VK12); Internal Pricing Policy

description: Indicates whether the customer account has confirmed compliance with EU REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) and RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) regulations. Relevant for industrial manufacturing customers in the EU market.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; CE Marking

description: The revenue-based tier assigned to the customer account, used for pricing, discount eligibility, and service level differentiation. Platinum and Gold tiers typically receive preferential pricing and dedicated account management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions; Salesforce CRM; Internal Pricing Policy

description: The scheduled date for the next periodic review of the classification. Ensures classifications remain current and accurate, particularly for credit risk, export control, and strategic account tiers.
reference: ISO 9001 Section 9.1; EAR 15 CFR; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: The originating operational system from which the classification record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM, and other systems of record.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM

description: The unique identifier of the classification record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA customer classification key, Salesforce record ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the account classification record. Indicates whether the classification is currently active and in use, pending review, expired, or superseded by a newer classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; ISO 9001 Section 8.2

description: The tax classification assigned to the customer account, determining the applicable tax treatment for sales transactions. Drives VAT/GST determination in SAP S/4HANA SD and ensures compliance with local tax regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Tax Classification; IFRS; GAAP; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

description: The official tax exemption certificate number issued by the relevant tax authority, confirming the customer account's exemption from applicable taxes. Required for compliance and audit purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; IFRS; GAAP; EU VAT Directive; OSHA/EPA (for environmental tax exemptions)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer credit profile record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all credit risk and order-to-cash processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Credit profiles are managed within controlling areas for credit risk monitoring and exposure analysis. Credit management teams track customer credit limits by controlling area for financial control.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the local account currency in which the credit limit and exposure are denominated for this customer (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management

description: Indicates whether the customer account is currently subject to a credit block (True) or not (False). When True, new sales orders for this customer are blocked in SAP S/4HANA SD pending credit review and release by an authorized credit analyst.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Credit Management; ISO 9001 Order Management

description: SAP credit check method code controlling how credit checks are performed for this customer during order-to-cash processing (e.g., A=Static Credit Check, B=Dynamic Credit Check, C=Maximum Document Value, D=No Check). Determines the credit verification logic applied at sales order creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Credit Management Configuration

description: SAP organizational unit (credit control area code) responsible for managing and monitoring the customer's credit. Defines the currency, fiscal year variant, and credit management rules applicable to this customer's credit profile.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; IFRS; GAAP

description: Current total credit exposure for the customer in account currency, comprising open sales orders, open deliveries, open billing documents, and outstanding accounts receivable. Compared against credit limit to determine credit availability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

description: Reason code explaining why the customer account has been placed on credit hold. Used by credit analysts and order management teams to prioritize resolution actions and communicate hold rationale to sales teams.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; ISO 9001 Nonconformance Management

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context or detail for the credit hold reason beyond the structured reason code. Entered by the credit analyst to document specific circumstances such as dispute details or negotiation status.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management

description: Maximum insured amount covered by the trade credit insurance policy for this customer, expressed in account currency. Represents the insurer's approved exposure ceiling and is a key input to the internal credit limit approval process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9; Trade Credit Insurance Standards

description: Reference number of the trade credit insurance policy covering this customer account, if applicable. Credit insurance mitigates the financial risk of customer default and may allow higher credit limits to be extended to insured customers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9 Credit Risk Mitigation

description: Maximum credit amount extended to the customer expressed in the account's local currency. Defines the upper boundary of outstanding receivables permitted before credit block is triggered in SAP S/4HANA SD order processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; GAAP ASC 310

description: Date on which the current credit limit was formally approved by the authorized credit analyst or credit committee. Provides an audit trail for credit limit changes and supports SOX compliance for financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 310

description: Date on which the currently approved credit limit expires and requires renewal or re-approval. After this date, the credit limit may be automatically reduced or the account placed on hold pending review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; ISO 9001

description: Maximum credit amount extended to the customer expressed in the corporate group currency (e.g., USD for a US-headquartered multinational). Enables consolidated credit exposure reporting across all subsidiaries and legal entities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

description: SAP credit risk class code assigned to the customer, representing the internal risk tier (e.g., 001=Very Low Risk, 002=Low Risk, 003=Medium Risk, 004=High Risk, 005=Very High Risk). Drives credit check rules and automatic credit limit proposals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; Basel III Credit Risk Framework

description: Human-readable description of the credit risk class assigned to the customer (e.g., 'Very Low Risk', 'Medium Risk', 'High Risk'). Supports reporting and business user interpretation without requiring code lookups.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management

description: SAP credit segment identifier grouping customers for credit management purposes, enabling separate credit limit management per business unit, sales organization, or geographic region within a single customer account.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management (Credit Segment Configuration)

description: Percentage of the credit limit currently utilized, calculated as (credit_exposure_amount / credit_limit_account_currency) * 100. Used for credit risk monitoring dashboards and automated alert thresholds.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; IFRS 9

description: Average number of days the customer takes to pay invoices, calculated from invoice date to payment receipt date over a rolling period. A key KPI for accounts receivable management and credit risk assessment in the order-to-cash process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Analytics; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310; Order-to-Cash KPI Framework

description: External credit rating assigned by Dun & Bradstreet (D&B) for the customer organization, such as the D&B PAYDEX score or Financial Stress Score. Used as an external benchmark to validate and calibrate internal credit risk assessments.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet PAYDEX; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; Basel III

description: Numeric D&B PAYDEX score (1–100) reflecting the customer's payment performance history based on trade references reported to Dun & Bradstreet. Higher scores indicate better payment behavior. Used alongside internal scoring for credit limit decisions.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet PAYDEX Score Methodology; Basel III Credit Risk

description: Current dunning level (0–4) assigned to the customer in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR, reflecting the escalation stage of the collections process. Level 0 = no dunning; Level 4 = final notice / legal action. Drives automated dunning letter generation and collections escalation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dunning Configuration; IFRS 9

description: Date from which the current credit profile terms (credit limit, risk class, payment terms) became effective. Supports bi-temporal data modeling and historical credit profile analysis in the Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; IFRS 9; Databricks Silver Layer SCD Type 2

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the corporate group reporting currency used to express the group-currency credit limit and exposure (e.g., USD, EUR).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Proprietary internal credit score assigned to the customer based on payment history, financial statements, order behavior, and risk class. Used by credit analysts to make credit limit recommendations and risk classification decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; Basel III Internal Ratings-Based Approach; IFRS 9

description: Date on which the most recent dunning notice was issued to the customer. Used to enforce dunning interval rules and prevent premature re-dunning in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR collections management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dunning Management

description: Date on which the most recent formal credit review was completed for this customer. Used to track review cadence compliance and identify overdue reviews that may require updated credit limit or risk class reassessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; ISO 9001 Periodic Review Requirements

description: Scheduled date for the next formal credit review of this customer account. Drives credit analyst workqueue prioritization and ensures timely reassessment of credit limits and risk classifications per internal credit policy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; ISO 9001

description: Number of days since the oldest unpaid open invoice item was due for this customer. Used in credit risk assessment and dunning level determination — a high value indicates chronic payment delays requiring escalation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Open Item Management; IFRS 9

description: Total value of outstanding invoices that have exceeded their payment due date, expressed in account currency. A primary trigger for credit block activation and dunning process initiation in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dunning Management; IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss; GAAP ASC 310

description: Internal classification of the customer's historical payment behavior based on analysis of invoice payment patterns, average days past due, and frequency of late payments. Informs credit risk class assignment and credit limit decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dunning and Payment History; IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss

description: SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30) assigned to the customer, defining the agreed payment due dates, early payment discount percentages, and baseline date calculation. Directly impacts days sales outstanding (DSO) and credit exposure calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/SD Payment Terms; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP ASC 606

description: Timestamp when the credit profile record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Provides the creation audit trail for compliance, data lineage, and historical analysis in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit profile record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: Frequency at which the customer's credit profile is formally reviewed and updated. Typically determined by the customer's risk class — high-risk customers are reviewed more frequently than low-risk customers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; Basel III Credit Risk Management

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this credit profile record was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for FI/SD-originated records, SALESFORCE_CRM for CRM-originated records). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA; Salesforce CRM

description: Description of any special or negotiated credit terms applicable to this customer that deviate from standard payment terms, such as extended payment windows, consignment arrangements, or letter of credit requirements. Documented for order management and finance reference.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Contract Management; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: Current lifecycle status of the customer credit profile record. ACTIVE indicates a fully operational credit profile; UNDER_REVIEW indicates a formal credit review is in progress; SUSPENDED indicates temporary suspension pending resolution; CLOSED indicates the profile has been deactivated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; ISO 9001

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each partner function assignment record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Indicates the level at which this partner function is assigned — at the customer master level (default for all transactions), sales area level, document header level, or document item level. Determines the scope of applicability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Partner Determination Schema

description: SAP authorization group code controlling which users or roles are permitted to view or modify this partner function assignment. Supports role-based access control and data security governance.

description: Indicates whether this partner function assignment represents an intercompany relationship (i.e., the partner belongs to the same corporate group). Used for intercompany billing and transfer pricing processes.

description: The user ID or username of the person who last modified this partner function assignment in the source system. Supports audit trail and change accountability.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this partner function assignment record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.

description: Free-text notes or comments providing additional context about this partner function assignment, such as special handling instructions, contractual nuances, or operational remarks.

description: The name of the business partner (company or individual) fulfilling this partner function. Used for display on order confirmations, shipping documents, and invoices.

description: The date from which this partner function assignment becomes effective. Used to manage time-bound partner relationships and contractual validity periods.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
