reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.transaction. Business justification: Every goods issue generates or references an inventory transaction for stock movement accounting. Finance and inventory control teams use this link to reconcile order-driven stock movements against in
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The precise date and time at which the goods issue was physically executed and posted in SAP S/4HANA. This timestamp is the definitive event marker for inventory reduction, revenue recognition eligibility, and order-to-cash milestone tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD — Goods Issue Timestamp; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: The production or supplier batch number of the material issued. Critical for batch traceability, quality recall management, and regulatory compliance (e.g., RoHS, REACH). Enables end-to-end lot traceability from production to customer delivery.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Batch Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2; EU RoHS Directive; REACH Regulation

description: The SAP Controlling (CO) cost center to which the goods issue cost is allocated. Used for internal cost accounting, profitability analysis, and COGS reporting. Relevant for non-sales goods issues such as production consumption or internal transfers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Cost Center Accounting; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the issued material was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, export control compliance, and preferential trade agreement eligibility.
reference: ISO 3166-1; WTO Rules of Origin; EU Customs Code; US CBP Regulations; REACH; RoHS

description: The timestamp when this goods issue record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Document; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the goods issue value is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for multinational manufacturing entities.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI — Currency Configuration; IFRS

description: Line item number within the outbound delivery document corresponding to this goods issue line, enabling precise traceability between the delivery and the material document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Outbound Delivery Item

description: The quantity confirmed on the outbound delivery document for this item. Compared against issued_quantity to identify over/under-delivery situations and partial shipment scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Outbound Delivery; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: The physical date on which the goods issue document was created, which may differ from the posting date. Used for document management and audit trail purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Document; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Line item number within the goods issue material document, identifying the specific material line within a multi-line goods issue posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Document Item

description: The material document number generated in SAP S/4HANA upon posting the goods issue. This is the primary business identifier for the goods issue transaction and is referenced in downstream financial and logistics processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Document; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: The export control classification status of the goods issue, indicating whether the issued materials are subject to export licensing requirements or trade embargoes. Critical for regulatory compliance in multinational shipments.
reference: US EAR (Export Administration Regulations); EU Dual-Use Regulation; ITAR; SAP S/4HANA GTS

description: The name or identifier of the person or organizational unit designated as the recipient of the issued goods. Used for internal goods issue scenarios (e.g., production orders, cost centers) and for shipping documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Issue Header; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: Indicates whether the issued material is classified as hazardous under applicable regulations (e.g., OSHA HazCom, EU CLP/GHS, IATA/IMDG dangerous goods). When true, special handling, labeling, and shipping documentation requirements apply.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom; EU CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; REACH; RoHS

description: The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code applicable to this goods issue, defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between seller and buyer. Determines the point at which revenue recognition is triggered under IFRS 15.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP ASC 606

description: The quantity of material physically issued from stock, expressed in the base unit of measure. Negative values represent reversals. This quantity drives inventory reduction and is the basis for revenue recognition eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Movement Quantity; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this goods issue record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Document; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5

description: Short descriptive text for the issued material or finished good, providing human-readable identification for reporting, shipping documents, and customer-facing documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: The SAP material master number identifying the material or finished good being issued. Used for inventory management, BOM traceability, and product costing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2

description: SAP S/4HANA movement type code classifying the nature of the goods issue transaction (e.g., 601 = Goods Issue for Delivery, 261 = Goods Issue for Production Order, 551 = Scrapping). Determines the accounting entries and inventory impact.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Movement Type Configuration; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: The SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing plant or distribution center from which the goods were issued. Determines the organizational unit for inventory valuation, cost accounting, and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD — Plant; ISO 9001 Clause 4.1

description: The accounting and inventory posting date on which the goods issue was recorded in the system. Determines the fiscal period for inventory reduction and revenue recognition eligibility in the order-to-cash process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP ASC 606

description: The SAP Controlling profit center associated with the goods issue, enabling profitability analysis by business segment, product line, or geographic region. Supports management reporting and EBITDA analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Profit Center Accounting; IFRS 8 Operating Segments; GAAP

description: Indicates whether this goods issue record represents a reversal (cancellation) of a previously posted goods issue. When true, the issued_quantity will be negative and the original document reference is captured in reversal_reference_document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Issue Reversal; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: The original goods issue material document number that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag is true, enabling audit trail linkage between the original posting and its cancellation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Document Reversal; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; IFRS 15

description: The SAP transportation route code assigned to the delivery, defining the path from the shipping point to the customer destination. Used for freight cost calculation, transit time estimation, and logistics planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM — Route Determination; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: The unique serial number of the issued item for serialized materials. Enables individual unit traceability from goods issue through customer delivery and after-sales service, supporting warranty management and field service operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD — Serial Number Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2

description: The SAP shipping point from which the goods were dispatched. Represents the organizational unit responsible for outbound shipment processing and determines the shipping execution team and dock assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Shipping Point; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: The Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) identifier for the issued material, used for warehouse management, order fulfillment tracking, and sales reporting. May differ from the material number in multi-SKU scenarios.
reference: Infor WMS — SKU Master; GS1 Standards

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this goods issue record was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for MM/SD postings, INFOR_WMS for warehouse-initiated issues). Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer — Data Lineage; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: SAP special stock indicator identifying whether the issued material belongs to a special stock category such as sales order stock (E), project stock (Q), consignment stock (K), or returnable packaging (W). Affects inventory valuation and ownership.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Special Stock Management

description: Current processing status of the goods issue document. 'posted' indicates the inventory reduction has been recorded; 'reversed' indicates a cancellation has been applied; 'blocked' indicates a hold pending resolution; 'in_transit' indicates goods are en route; 'confirmed' indicates delivery confirmation received.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD — Goods Issue Status; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: The SAP storage location within the plant from which the material was physically picked and issued. Enables precise warehouse-level inventory tracking and supports WMS integration with Infor WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/WM — Storage Location; Infor WMS

description: The base unit of measure in which the issued quantity is expressed (e.g., EA = Each, KG = Kilogram, M = Meter, L = Liter, PC = Piece). Aligned with SAP unit of measure codes and ISO standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; GS1 Standards

description: The specific unloading point or dock at the customer's delivery address where the goods are to be received. Sourced from the customer master or delivery document and used for last-mile logistics coordination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Customer Master Unloading Point; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: The inventory valuation method applied to calculate the goods issue value (e.g., moving average price, standard price, FIFO, LIFO). Determines how COGS is computed and reported in financial statements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Valuation; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: The total inventory value of the issued goods in the document currency, calculated as issued quantity multiplied by the moving average price or standard cost. Represents the COGS impact and triggers revenue recognition eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI — Material Valuation; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory; GAAP ASC 606

description: The SAP Warehouse Management (WM) warehouse number associated with the storage location from which goods were issued. Used for detailed warehouse operations tracking and transfer order management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM — Warehouse Number; Infor WMS

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a specific product configuration captured at the order line item level within SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration (VC).
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration (VC)

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line_item. Business justification: Product configuration options are captured at the order line item level. The string sales_order_line_item_number is replaced by FK order_line_item_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Order configurations are directly tied to product configurations selected by the customer. Manufacturing and order management use this to drive production BOMs and ensure the ordered configuration is
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Configure-to-order manufacturing requires linking customer-specific configurations to engineering-defined product variants. Order fulfillment uses this to determine exact build specifications.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: SAP material number of the configurable base product (e.g., automation system, drive system, switchgear) from which variant characteristics are selected. Corresponds to the configurable material master in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (Material Management); SAP Variant Configuration

description: Reference identifier linking this configuration to the exploded Bill of Materials (BOM) generated by SAP Variant Configuration, used to identify the specific component set required for production of this configured product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-BD (BOM Management); Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management

description: Current status of the BOM explosion process for this configuration, indicating whether the variant-specific component list has been successfully generated, is pending, in progress, or has encountered errors.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Variant Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1

description: Customer-selected regional or industry certification variant for the configured product, determining the applicable regulatory marking and compliance documentation (e.g., CE for EU, UL for North America, ATEX for explosive atmospheres).
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; UL Standards; IECEx Scheme; ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU

description: Identifier of the SAP classification characteristic profile or configuration profile used to define the set of selectable variant characteristics for this product model, enabling traceability to the configuration knowledge base.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration Classification; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Customer-selected color or surface finish variant for the configured product enclosure or housing (e.g., RAL 7035 light grey, RAL 9005 black), applicable for panel-mounted and standalone enclosure products.
reference: RAL Color Standard; SAP Variant Configuration Characteristic

description: Customer-selected industrial communication protocol variant for the configured product, determining the fieldbus or industrial Ethernet interface installed (e.g., PROFINET for Siemens ecosystems, EtherNet/IP for Rockwell environments).
reference: IEC 61158 Industrial Communication Networks; IEC 61131-3; SAP Variant Configuration

description: Customer-selected motor or drive control method variant (e.g., scalar V/f control, sensorless vector, closed-loop vector with encoder, servo control), defining the control algorithm and hardware configuration.
reference: IEC 61800 Adjustable Speed Electrical Power Drive Systems; SAP Variant Configuration

description: Timestamp when the product configuration record was first created in SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration, marking the start of the configuration lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the configuration price uplift and any configuration-specific pricing, aligned with the sales order currency.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that triggered a revision to this configuration, providing traceability between order configurations and engineering change management.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; SAP S/4HANA Engineering Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6

description: Customer-selected enclosure protection class for the configured product, defining the degree of protection against ingress of solid objects and liquids (IP rating per IEC 60529 or NEMA standard).
reference: IEC 60529 Degrees of Protection (IP Code); NEMA 250 Enclosure Types

description: Export control classification code for the configured product variant, determining applicable export licensing requirements. Configurations with advanced communication protocols or safety features may carry different export classifications than the base product.
reference: US EAR (Export Administration Regulations); EU Dual-Use Regulation (EU) 2021/821; NIST SP 800-171

description: Customer-selected mechanical frame size designation for the configured product (e.g., FSA, FSB, FSC for drive systems; IEC frame sizes 63 to 355 for motors), determining physical dimensions and mounting footprint.
reference: IEC 60072 Dimensions and Output Series for Rotating Electrical Machines; SAP Variant Configuration

description: Customer-selected mains supply frequency variant for the configured product (50Hz for European/Asian markets, 60Hz for North American markets, or universal 50/60Hz), determining hardware compatibility.
reference: IEC 60038; SAP Variant Configuration Characteristic

description: Indicates whether the customer has formally approved the final product configuration, required for ETO and ATO orders before production release. Supports PPAP and FAI processes.
reference: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process); FAI (First Article Inspection); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3

description: Indicates whether engineering feasibility of the selected configuration has been confirmed by the engineering team, particularly relevant for ETO orders where custom configurations require technical review before production commitment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; APQP Phase 1 Feasibility; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the product configuration record, used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data extraction in the Silver layer pipeline.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Manufacturing or procurement lead time in calendar days specific to this product configuration, accounting for variant-specific components, special certifications, or ETO engineering effort required.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP Lead Time; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Customer-selected motor power rating characteristic for drive systems and motor-integrated products (e.g., 0.75kW, 7.5kW, 110kW), applicable when the configurable product includes a motor or is a motor drive.
reference: IEC 60034 Rotating Electrical Machines; SAP Variant Configuration Characteristic

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the product configuration as assigned in SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration, used for cross-referencing with production and engineering systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration (VC); Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Customer-selected nominal output current rating for drive systems and power electronics products (e.g., 3.0A, 18.5A, 250A), a key sizing characteristic used for BOM explosion and component selection.
reference: IEC 61800-2; SAP Variant Configuration Characteristic

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility designated to produce this configured product, determined by the variant characteristics and available production capabilities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Plant Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) submission is required for this configuration before production can commence, typically mandated for new configurations or significant variant changes.
reference: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) AIAG Standard; ISO 9001:2015

description: Additional price delta applied to the base product price due to the selected variant characteristics and options (e.g., premium communication protocol, ATEX certification, safety integration), expressed in the order currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing (Variant Pricing); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: High-level product category classifying the configurable product type, used for routing configurations to the appropriate engineering and production teams.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Classification; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Commercial product model or product family name of the configurable product (e.g., SINAMICS G120, SIRIUS 3RV, SENTRON 3VA), as defined in the product catalog and PLM system.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Quantity of units to be produced or assembled with this specific configuration, as ordered by the customer on the associated sales order line item.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Unit of measure for the configured quantity (e.g., EA for each, PC for piece, SET for set, KIT for kit), aligned with SAP base unit of measure for the material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 80000-1

description: Indicates whether the configured product variant complies with EU REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) and RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) directives, based on the selected components and materials.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Timestamp when the product configuration was formally released for production, triggering BOM explosion and production order creation in SAP S/4HANA PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Release; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Customer-selected functional safety integrity level for the configured product, indicating the required safety performance level per IEC 62061 (SIL) or ISO 13849 (Performance Level), applicable for safety-integrated drives and automation systems.
reference: IEC 62061 Functional Safety; ISO 13849 Safety of Machinery; SAP Variant Configuration

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this configuration record was sourced, supporting data lineage and audit traceability in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; Siemens Opcenter MES; DAMA DMBOK Data Lineage

description: Current lifecycle status of the product configuration, indicating whether it is being drafted, under engineering review, validated, released for production, superseded by a newer version, or cancelled.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration (VC); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Date after which this product configuration is no longer valid for new production orders, typically set when superseded by an Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or product discontinuation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration Validity; ECN Process; ISO 9001:2015

description: Date from which this product configuration is considered valid and can be used for production planning and BOM explosion, aligned with engineering change effectivity dates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration Validity; ISO 9001:2015 Change Management

description: Version number of the product configuration, incremented when customer-selected variant characteristics are revised or updated during the order lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration (VC); ISO 9001 Change Control

description: Customer-selected voltage rating characteristic for the configured product (e.g., 400V, 690V, 1.1kV), a key variant characteristic for electrical automation and switchgear products.
reference: IEC 60038 Standard Voltages; SAP Variant Configuration Characteristic

description: SAP work center or production cell designated for manufacturing this configuration, derived from the variant characteristics and routing rules in the Bill of Process (BoP).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each order confirmation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Order Confirmation Object

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Order confirmations in manufacturing trigger AR invoice creation. Finance links confirmations to invoices to validate that every confirmed order is billed and to support order-to-cash process audits.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: Order confirmations may reference the blanket order under which the sales order was placed. The string contract_number is replaced by FK blanket_order_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.credit_check. Business justification: Order confirmations include a credit status that reflects the outcome of the credit check performed before order validation. The credit_status STRING attribute is a denormalized snapshot of the credit
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: An order confirmation is the formal acknowledgment document issued after a sales order line item is validated. The order_confirmation must reference the specific line_item it confirms to support order
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.quotation. Business justification: An order confirmation may reference the originating quotation. The string quotation_number is replaced by FK quotation_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Available to Promise / Capable to Promise (ATP/CTP) Check Status
description: Result of the Available to Promise (ATP) or Capable to Promise (CTP) availability check performed in SAP S/4HANA prior to issuing the confirmation. Indicates whether confirmed delivery dates are backed by ATP stock availability or CTP production capacity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/PP ATP/CTP; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Committed delivery date communicated to the customer in the confirmation, representing the manufacturer's contractual delivery commitment at the header level. Derived from ATP/CTP check results.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Schedule Lines; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3; Incoterms 2020

description: Total confirmed gross value of the order including all taxes and surcharges. Represents the total financial obligation communicated to the customer in the confirmation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: Total confirmed net value of the order as stated in the confirmation document, excluding taxes. Represents the contractual financial commitment. Expressed in the document currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; IFRS 15 (Revenue from Contracts with Customers); GAAP ASC 606

description: Total quantity confirmed by the manufacturer at the header level across all line items. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the order. May differ from ordered quantity if partial confirmation applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3

description: Total tax amount applicable to the confirmed order value, as calculated at the time of confirmation. Includes VAT, GST, or applicable sales tax based on ship-to jurisdiction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Determination; IFRS; GAAP; EU VAT Directive

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the final delivery destination for the confirmed order. Used for export control classification, customs documentation, VAT/GST determination, and REACH/RoHS compliance assessment.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA GTS; EU REACH Regulation; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; CE Marking

description: Timestamp at which the order confirmation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and order-to-cash cycle time analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability and Auditability); ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the confirmed order values are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS; GAAP

description: Date on which the customer formally acknowledged and accepted the order confirmation. Used for contractual binding date determination and SLA compliance measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3; IFRS 15 Contract Inception

description: Status indicating whether the customer has formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the order confirmation. 'Acknowledged' confirms mutual agreement; 'Rejected' triggers order revision workflow; 'No Response' may trigger follow-up SLA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM Service Cloud; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3; SLA Management

description: Calendar date on which the order confirmation was formally issued and sent to the customer. Used for SLA compliance tracking and order-to-cash cycle time reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Signed integer representing the difference in calendar days between the confirmed delivery date and the customer-requested delivery date. Negative values indicate early delivery; positive values indicate late delivery. Used for on-time delivery KPI reporting.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3; APICS CPIM; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Free-text description of any deviations between the original customer order request and the confirmed order terms (e.g., 'Quantity reduced from 500 to 450 due to material shortage; delivery date shifted by 5 days'). Populated only when deviation_flag is TRUE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3; NCR Process

description: Boolean indicator set to TRUE when the confirmed order contains one or more deviations from the original customer order request (e.g., quantity shortfall, delivery date change, price adjustment). Triggers deviation notification workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3 (Review of Requirements)

description: SAP distribution channel code indicating how the confirmed order will reach the customer (e.g., direct sales, wholesale, OEM, e-commerce). Influences pricing, output determination, and revenue reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Organizational Structure

description: Foreign exchange rate applied at the time of confirmation to convert document currency to company code currency. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA exchange rate tables. Required for multi-currency financial consolidation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate; IFRS IAS 21 (Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates); GAAP ASC 830

description: Status of export control compliance review for the confirmed order. Indicates whether the order involves dual-use goods, controlled technology, or restricted end-users requiring export license approval under applicable trade regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GTS (Global Trade Services); EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; US EAR (Export Administration Regulations); ITAR

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code confirmed on the order, defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between manufacturer and customer (e.g., DAP, DDP, FOB, CIF). Critical for logistics, insurance, and export compliance.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD; EU CE Marking; REACH/RoHS Compliance

description: Named place or port associated with the confirmed Incoterms code (e.g., 'Rotterdam' for FOB Rotterdam, 'Customer Warehouse Chicago' for DAP). Required to fully define the delivery obligation.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the order confirmation record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; GDPR Article 5; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; Databricks Delta Lake CDC

description: Channel or medium through which the order confirmation was transmitted to the customer (e.g., email, EDI transaction set 855, customer portal, fax, printed document, or API). Supports communication audit and EDI compliance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Output Determination; ANSI X12 EDI 855 (Purchase Order Acknowledgment)

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the order confirmation document, as generated by SAP S/4HANA SD. Communicated to the customer as the formal reference number on the confirmation document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Order Confirmation; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3 (Customer Communication)

description: Total quantity originally requested by the customer on the sales order. Retained on the confirmation to enable quantity deviation analysis and partial fulfillment tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3

description: SAP payment terms key confirmed on the order (e.g., NT30 for net 30 days, 2/10NT30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Defines the cash discount and due date calculation for accounts receivable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/SD Payment Terms; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility responsible for fulfilling and shipping the confirmed order. Determines production scheduling, inventory sourcing, and shipping point.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/PP Plant Determination; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5 (Production and Service Provision)

description: Unit of measure in which confirmed and ordered quantities are expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter, PC for piece). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure codes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 (Quantities and Units); ANSI

description: Original delivery date requested by the customer on the sales order. Retained on the confirmation to enable deviation analysis between customer request and manufacturer commitment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3

description: SAP sales organization code responsible for the confirmed order, representing the legal entity or regional sales unit. Drives revenue recognition, pricing, and financial reporting allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Organizational Structure; IFRS; GAAP

description: SAP shipping point code from which the confirmed order will be dispatched. Determines the physical loading dock, carrier assignment, and outbound logistics routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Shipping Point Determination; Incoterms 2020; Infor WMS

description: Current lifecycle status of the order confirmation document. 'Issued' indicates the confirmation has been sent; 'Acknowledged' indicates the customer has formally accepted; 'Superseded' indicates a revised confirmation replaced this one; 'Cancelled' indicates the confirmation was voided.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Order Confirmation Lifecycle; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3

description: Confirmation number of the revised confirmation that replaced this record when status is 'Superseded'. Enables version chain traceability for audit and dispute resolution purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 (Document Control)

description: Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the order confirmation was generated and dispatched. Supports audit trail requirements and SLA measurement at sub-day granularity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); ISO 9001 Clause 8.2.3

description: Sequential version number of the order confirmation document. Incremented each time a revised confirmation is issued due to order amendments, deviations, or customer-requested changes. Version 1 is the original confirmation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Change Management; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each order priority level record in the reference classification table.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Priority Configuration

description: SAP plant code(s) for which this priority level configuration applies. Allows plant-specific lead time targets and scheduling weights to reflect differences in production capacity and capability across manufacturing sites.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Plant Configuration; Siemens Opcenter MES - Site Management

description: SAP sales organization code(s) for which this priority level is applicable. Supports multinational operations where priority definitions, lead times, and escalation rules may differ by regional sales organization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Organization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1 - Organizational Context

description: Minimum organizational authority level required to approve assignment or upgrade of an order to this priority level. Enforces governance controls for high-urgency priorities that carry significant cost and resource implications.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 - Organizational Roles; SAP S/4HANA Authorization

description: Indicates whether a formal management approval workflow must be completed before an order can be upgraded to or assigned this priority level. True for high-impact priorities such as Emergency and Critical to prevent unauthorized escalations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; SAP S/4HANA SD - Approval Workflow

description: Broad classification grouping priority levels by their business driver: operational (production-driven urgency), strategic (key account or revenue-driven), regulatory (compliance or safety mandate), or contractual (SLA-bound commitment).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the priority level, used in system integrations with SAP S/4HANA SD, MES scheduling, and WMS pick sequencing (e.g., STD, EXP, CRIT, EMER, STRAT).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Order Priority; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Hexadecimal color code used to visually distinguish priority levels in shop floor Andon boards, MES dashboards, Power BI reports, and order management UI screens for rapid visual identification by operators and planners.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Andon Visualization; Microsoft Power BI - Dashboard Design

description: Percentage surcharge applied to the order net value to recover the incremental operational costs (overtime, expedited freight, premium materials sourcing) associated with fulfilling orders at this priority level.
reference: GAAP - Cost Allocation; IFRS 15 - Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing

description: Date and time when this order priority level record was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail and configuration change management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the base currency in which the max_order_value_threshold and cost_surcharge_percent monetary values are expressed.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Indicates whether the customer must be proactively notified of priority assignment, status changes, or delivery updates for orders at this priority level, supporting SLA transparency and customer satisfaction management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.1 - Customer Communication; Salesforce CRM Service Cloud

description: Indicates whether orders at this priority level require dedicated production resources (machines, operators, cells) to be reserved exclusively for their fulfillment, preventing resource sharing with lower-priority orders.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Resource Scheduling; SAP PP - Capacity Planning

description: Detailed narrative explaining the business conditions, criteria, and use cases that qualify an order for this priority level, including customer impact and operational implications.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2 - Customer Requirements

description: Number of days before the target ship date at which an unshipped order at this priority level triggers an automatic escalation notification to operations management and customer service.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 - Nonconformity and Corrective Action; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Indicates whether orders at this priority level are automatically routed to expedited or premium freight carriers to meet compressed delivery timelines, triggering premium freight cost allocation in the TMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Shipping Condition; TMS Routing Rules; GAAP Cost Allocation

description: Specifies which order fulfillment modes (Make to Order, Make to Stock, Engineer to Order, Assemble to Order, or all modes) this priority level is applicable to, ensuring correct priority assignment during order entry and ATP/CTP checks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Fulfillment Strategy; MRP II

description: Icon identifier or symbol code used in UI systems and dashboards to visually represent the priority level alongside the color code, supporting rapid visual triage on shop floor HMI screens and order management portals.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - HMI Display; SAP S/4HANA Fiori UI

description: Date and time when this order priority level record was most recently updated, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and Silver layer incremental data loading.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: Maximum order net value (in base currency) up to which this priority level can be assigned without additional financial approval. Orders exceeding this threshold require escalated authorization to control premium fulfillment cost exposure.
reference: GAAP - Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA FI - Credit and Authorization Management

description: Human-readable name of the priority level used in UI displays, reports, and customer-facing communications. Standard values include Standard, Expedite, Critical, Emergency, and Strategic Account.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Order Priority; ISO 9001 Customer Focus

description: Qualitative assessment of the potential impact on customer Net Promoter Score (NPS) if orders at this priority level are not fulfilled on time. Used to prioritize customer communication and recovery actions.
reference: Salesforce CRM Service Cloud - NPS Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2 - Customer Satisfaction

description: Indicates whether orders assigned this priority level automatically authorize shop floor overtime to meet the target lead time, enabling MES and workforce scheduling systems to plan extended shifts without separate approval.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; Kronos Workforce Central - Overtime Rules; ISO 45001 Occupational Health

description: Numeric weighting factor applied by the MES and MRP scheduling engine to sequence production jobs for orders at this priority level. Higher values result in earlier scheduling slots and preferential resource allocation.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES - Scheduling Priority; SAP MRP II - Production Scheduling

description: Numeric rank defining the relative urgency ordering of priority levels, where lower values indicate higher urgency (e.g., 1 = Emergency, 2 = Critical, 3 = Expedite, 4 = Strategic Account, 5 = Standard). Used for automated sequencing logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Priority; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Indicates whether this priority level is designated for orders that must be fulfilled to meet regulatory, safety, or government mandate deadlines (e.g., CE Marking compliance shipments, OSHA-mandated safety equipment orders).
reference: ISO 9001:2015; ISO 14001; OSHA; CE Marking; RoHS/REACH

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Breach Notification Hours
description: Number of hours before the committed delivery deadline at which an automated SLA breach warning notification is triggered for orders at this priority level, enabling proactive intervention by customer service and operations teams.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Customer SLA Agreement

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this priority level configuration originates or is mastered, supporting data lineage tracking and reconciliation across SAP S/4HANA, Siemens Opcenter MES, and Salesforce CRM.
reference: Data Governance - Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Lifecycle status of the priority level record indicating whether it is currently available for assignment to orders (active), temporarily disabled (inactive), or retired from use (deprecated).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Configuration Lifecycle

description: Indicates whether this priority level is reserved for orders from designated strategic accounts (key customers with high revenue, long-term contracts, or critical partnership status), enabling preferential treatment in fulfillment sequencing.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Account Classification; SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Priority

description: Indicates whether procurement teams should automatically initiate supplier expediting actions (accelerated PO delivery, premium sourcing) for materials required to fulfill orders at this priority level.
reference: SAP Ariba - Procurement Expediting; SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Expediting

description: Target number of calendar days from order receipt to shipment dispatch for orders assigned this priority level. Used as the baseline commitment for SLA management and ATP/CTP confirmation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Lead Time; Customer SLA

description: Date from which this priority level configuration becomes effective and available for assignment to orders. Supports time-bounded priority configurations for seasonal demand peaks or special business programs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Validity Period; ISO 9001:2015 Document Control

description: Date after which this priority level configuration expires and is no longer available for new order assignments. Null indicates no expiry. Used to retire legacy priority codes without deleting historical records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Validity Period; ISO 9001:2015 Document Control

description: Numeric priority value passed to the WMS (Infor WMS) to sequence warehouse pick tasks for outbound shipments. Lower values indicate higher pick urgency and earlier task assignment to warehouse operatives.
reference: Infor WMS - Pick Priority Configuration; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each delivery schedule line record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for the order_schedule_line entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Schedule Line (VBEP)

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line_item. Business justification: Schedule lines are associated with a specific order line item. The string order_line_number is replaced by FK order_line_item_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Schedule lines define delivery dates and quantities from a specific plant. Production planning and logistics use this to align delivery schedules with plant-level inventory and production capacity for
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Schedule lines represent confirmed delivery quantities and dates tied to available stock. Order scheduling teams reference stock positions to confirm partial delivery quantities across multiple schedu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Available to Promise / Capable to Promise (ATP/CTP) Check Result
description: Result of the ATP (Available to Promise) or CTP (Capable to Promise) availability check performed for this schedule line. Indicates whether the full quantity was confirmed, partially confirmed, or not confirmed by the availability check engine.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD ATP/CTP Module; APICS Supply Chain Standards

description: SAP schedule line category code (ETTYP) that controls the behavior of the schedule line, including relevance for MRP, delivery, goods movement, and billing. Examples: CP (standard with MRP), CN (no MRP), BN (no delivery).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-ETTYP); SAP SD Configuration

description: The ATP/CTP-confirmed date on which the quantity on this schedule line is committed for delivery to the customer. Represents the binding delivery promise date used in fulfillment scheduling and customer communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-EDATU); ISO 8601 Date Format

description: The quantity confirmed by ATP (Available to Promise) or CTP (Capable to Promise) check for delivery on the confirmed delivery date. Represents the binding quantity commitment for this schedule line.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-BMENG); ATP/CTP Confirmation Logic

description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, preferential trade agreements, and import/export compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO Rules of Origin; EU Customs Code; US CBP Regulations

description: The timestamp when this schedule line record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and order processing timeline analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; ISO 8601 Timestamp Format; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the net value of this schedule line is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting in multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: The Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification number for the material on this schedule line. Required for customs clearance, duty calculation, and trade compliance reporting in cross-border shipments.
reference: WCO Harmonized System (HS) Nomenclature; EU Combined Nomenclature; US HTS Schedule B

description: The actual quantity that has been goods-issued and delivered against this schedule line. Used to track fulfillment progress and calculate open/remaining delivery quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-WBMNG); Goods Issue Posting

description: Code indicating whether this schedule line is blocked for delivery processing. A non-empty value prevents the creation of an outbound delivery document. Common blocks include credit holds, export control holds, and customer-requested holds.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-LIFSP); SAP SD Delivery Block Configuration

description: Descriptive reason explaining why the delivery block was applied to this schedule line (e.g., 'Credit limit exceeded', 'Export license pending', 'Customer requested hold'). Supports operational resolution workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; Internal Order Management Policy

description: Indicates whether this schedule line is relevant for outbound delivery document creation. When false, the schedule line is informational only and will not trigger a delivery in the WMS/SD process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-LFREL)

description: Status of export control compliance review for this schedule line. Indicates whether the shipment has been cleared for export, is pending license review, or is blocked due to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR, EU Dual-Use).
reference: US Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ITAR; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; REACH/RoHS Compliance

description: The planned or actual date on which goods are issued from the warehouse/plant for this schedule line delivery. Triggers inventory reduction and initiates the billing process in the order-to-cash workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/WM Module (VBEP-WADAT); ISO 8601 Date Format

description: Current status of goods issue processing for this schedule line. Tracks whether goods have been fully issued, partially issued, or not yet issued from the warehouse, enabling fulfillment progress monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/WM Module; Order-to-Cash Process Standards

description: Indicates whether the material on this schedule line is classified as hazardous goods requiring special handling, packaging, and documentation during transportation. Triggers dangerous goods compliance checks.
reference: IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; ADR (European Agreement on Dangerous Goods); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code applicable to this schedule line delivery, defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between seller and buyer. May differ from the order-level Incoterms for split deliveries.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this schedule line record in the source system. Supports change detection, delta processing in the lakehouse pipeline, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; ISO 8601 Timestamp Format; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: The planned date on which goods for this schedule line are to be loaded onto the transport vehicle at the shipping point. Used for warehouse and dock scheduling coordination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-LDDAT); ISO 8601 Date Format

description: The date by which the material must be available in the warehouse for picking and packing to meet the loading date. Derived from backward scheduling in SAP SD and used to trigger MRP requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-MBDAT); MRP II Scheduling Standards

description: The SAP material number (Stock Keeping Unit) identifying the product or component to be delivered on this schedule line. Aligns with the material master in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 22745 (Industrial Data)

description: Indicates whether this schedule line is relevant for MRP (Material Requirements Planning) processing. When true, the confirmed quantity and date generate a demand requirement in the MRP run to trigger production or procurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MRP Module; MRP II Standards (APICS)

description: The net monetary value of this schedule line, calculated as confirmed quantity multiplied by the net unit price. Used for revenue recognition, billing, and order-to-cash financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP ASC 606

description: Sequential number identifying the delivery schedule line within a sales order line item, as assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD (VBEP-ETENR). Typically a 4-digit counter (e.g., 0001, 0002) representing each distinct delivery split.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-ETENR)

description: The originally requested quantity for this schedule line as entered on the sales order, before ATP/CTP confirmation. Enables comparison between customer request and confirmed commitment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-WMENG)

description: The maximum percentage by which the delivered quantity may exceed the confirmed quantity without requiring a new order or customer approval. Used in goods receipt and delivery processing to handle minor quantity variances.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/MM Module (VBAP-UEBTO); ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Indicates whether a partial delivery is permitted for this schedule line if the full confirmed quantity cannot be shipped on the confirmed delivery date. Drives ATP/CTP split logic and customer service decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBKD-KZTLF); Customer SLA Agreement

description: The manufacturing or distribution plant code responsible for fulfilling this schedule line. Determines the supplying location for MRP planning, inventory allocation, and goods issue processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Module (VBEP-WERKS); ISO 3166 Geographic Standards

description: The unit of measure (UoM) applicable to the confirmed, ordered, and delivered quantities on this schedule line (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 80000 (Quantities and Units); UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20

description: The delivery date originally requested by the customer for this schedule line, prior to ATP/CTP confirmation. Used to measure delivery promise accuracy and customer service level performance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module (VBEP-VDATU); ISO 8601 Date Format

description: The transportation route code assigned to this schedule line, defining the path from shipping point to destination. Determines transit time, carrier selection, and transportation costs in the TMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM Module (VBEP-ROUTE); ISO 3166 Geographic Standards

description: The SAP shipping point (VSTEL) from which the goods for this schedule line will be dispatched. Represents a physical location (warehouse, plant gate, dock) and determines the outbound delivery processing unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/WM Module (VBEP-VSTEL)

description: The specific storage location within the plant from which goods will be picked for this schedule line delivery. Used by the Warehouse Management System (WMS) for inventory allocation and pick order generation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM/MM Module (LGORT); Infor WMS Integration

description: The date by which transportation must be planned and arranged for this schedule line shipment. Used by the Transportation Management System (TMS) to initiate carrier booking and route planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/TM Module (VBEP-TDDAT); ISO 8601 Date Format

description: The maximum percentage by which the delivered quantity may fall short of the confirmed quantity and still be considered a complete delivery. Prevents unnecessary open item management for minor shortfalls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/MM Module (VBAP-UNTTO); ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each order status history record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the order_status_history data product.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry

description: Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: The status_history product is described as the chronological audit trail of all status transitions for a sales order throughout its lifecycle. The primary entity being tracked is the sales order line
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the order_status_history_id of the original status transition that this record reverses. Enables bidirectional traceability between a reversal event and the original transition it corrects. NULL when is_reversal is FALSE.
reference: SAP SD Reversal Processing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Indicates whether this status transition required a formal approval step (e.g., credit manager approval for credit release, export compliance approval). Supports workflow governance and segregation of duties compliance.
reference: SAP SD Workflow; SOX Compliance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Date and time when the approval for this status transition was granted. Used to measure approval cycle time and identify approval bottlenecks in the order-to-cash process.
reference: SAP SD Workflow; SOX Compliance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: System user ID of the approver who authorized this status transition when approval was required. NULL when no approval was required. Supports segregation of duties and audit trail for compliance.
reference: SAP SD Workflow; SOX Compliance; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Available to Promise / Capable to Promise (ATP/CTP) Check Result
description: Result of the Available to Promise (ATP) or Capable to Promise (CTP) availability check performed during this status transition. Captures whether inventory or production capacity was confirmed at the time of the transition.
reference: SAP SD ATP/CTP; MRP II; APICS Supply Chain Standards

description: Categorizes the type of block applied or released during this status transition. Enables analysis of block frequency by type, resolution time, and impact on order-to-cash cycle time.
reference: SAP SD Order Management; Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ISO 9001:2015

description: SAP SD delivery or billing block reason code applied when the status transition results in an order being blocked (e.g., credit block, export control hold, quality hold). NULL when no block is applied. Aligns with SAP SD blocking reason configuration.
reference: SAP SD Delivery Block; SAP SD Billing Block; Export Administration Regulations (EAR)

description: Standardized code identifying the business reason for the order status transition (e.g., CREDIT_HOLD, CUSTOMER_REQUEST, DELIVERY_CONFIRMED, PAYMENT_RECEIVED, SYSTEM_AUTO). Enables root cause analysis and process improvement analytics.
reference: SAP SD Reason Codes; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA Process

description: Human-readable description of the reason for the status transition. Provides additional context beyond the reason code, including free-text notes entered by the user or generated by the system.
reference: SAP SD Change Documents; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: System user ID or service account identifier of the actor who triggered the status transition. May represent a human user (SAP logon ID), an automated process (batch job), or an integration service account. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP SD Change Documents; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Display name of the user or process that triggered the status transition. Complements the user ID for human-readable audit reporting and compliance documentation.
reference: SAP SD Change Documents; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Free-text notes or comments entered by the user or system at the time of the status transition. Provides additional context, exception details, or customer communication notes not captured by structured reason codes.
reference: SAP SD Change Documents; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the sales organization or ship-to location associated with this order status transition. Supports multi-country regulatory compliance, GDPR jurisdiction determination, and regional analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 3; EU RoHS/REACH

description: Timestamp when this order status history record was created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Represents the data ingestion time, which may differ from the status_change_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data lineage and pipeline monitoring.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Outcome of the credit check performed during this status transition, if applicable. Relevant for transitions involving credit status changes (e.g., order release from credit block). Aligns with SAP SD credit management integration.
reference: SAP SD Credit Management; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; Customer Credit Policy

description: Time elapsed in minutes between the previous status transition and this status transition. Enables cycle time analysis for each status stage, bottleneck identification, and order-to-cash process optimization.
reference: Lean Manufacturing Cycle Time Analysis; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; KPI Frameworks

description: Result of the export control compliance screening performed during this status transition. Relevant for orders involving controlled goods, dual-use items, or shipments to restricted countries. Supports regulatory compliance with EAR, ITAR, and EU dual-use regulations.
reference: Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ITAR; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; SAP GTS

description: Indicates whether this status transition represents a reversal or rollback of a previous status change (e.g., un-cancelling an order, reversing a delivery confirmation). TRUE when the transition moves the order backward in its lifecycle. Supports exception and error analysis.
reference: SAP SD Reversal Processing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this order status history record in the Silver Layer. Supports incremental data processing, change data capture, and audit trail completeness verification.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: The order status value assigned as a result of this transition. Captures the 'to' state in the status transition pair. Represents the current status of the order at the point of this event.
reference: SAP SD Order Status Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Indicates whether a customer notification (e.g., order confirmation, shipment notification, cancellation notice) was sent as a result of this status transition. Supports customer communication compliance and SLA tracking.
reference: SAP SD Output Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Customer SLA Agreement

description: Classification of the sales order type at the time of this status transition. Provides context for interpreting the status transition within the appropriate order processing workflow.
reference: SAP SD Order Types; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: SAP plant code associated with the order at the time of this status transition. Enables plant-level analysis of order status progression, fulfillment performance, and SLA compliance across manufacturing sites.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The order status value immediately before this transition occurred. Captures the 'from' state in the status transition pair. NULL for the initial status record when the order is first created.
reference: SAP SD Order Status Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Standardized code identifying the reason an order block or hold was released during this status transition (e.g., CREDIT_APPROVED, EXPORT_CLEARED, QUALITY_PASSED). NULL when the transition does not involve a release action.
reference: SAP SD Release Processing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: SAP sales organization code associated with the order at the time of this status transition. Supports multi-entity reporting and regional order lifecycle analytics across the multinational enterprise.
reference: SAP SD Organizational Structure; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Sequential integer representing the chronological order of this status transition within the lifecycle of a given sales order. Enables reconstruction of the full status progression timeline and identification of the current state.
reference: SAP SD Order Lifecycle; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Indicates whether this status transition occurred after the SLA-defined deadline for reaching this status, constituting an SLA breach. TRUE when the transition is late relative to the SLA target. Supports SLA compliance reporting and customer service analytics.
reference: SLA Agreement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Customer Contract Terms

description: The SLA-defined deadline by which this status transition was expected to occur. Used to calculate SLA compliance and breach duration for order-to-cash process analytics.
reference: SLA Agreement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; IFRS 15

description: Identifies the operational system that originated or triggered the status transition (e.g., SAP SD for order management events, Siemens Opcenter MES for production-driven status changes, Salesforce CRM for customer-initiated changes). Supports IT/OT convergence traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Siemens Opcenter MES; IT/OT Convergence Standards

description: Native transaction or document identifier from the originating system (e.g., SAP change document number, MES work order ID) that caused this status transition. Enables drill-back to the source system for detailed investigation.
reference: SAP SD Change Documents; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Calendar date on which the order status transition occurred. Used for day-level reporting, SLA breach detection, and order lifecycle analytics without requiring timestamp precision.
reference: SAP SD Change Documents; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Exact date and time (with timezone) when the order status transition occurred. Critical for SLA compliance tracking, order-to-cash cycle time analysis, and chronological audit trail reconstruction.
reference: SAP SD Change Documents; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Classifies how the status transition was initiated — whether automatically by system rules (e.g., ATP/CTP check completion), manually by a user, via batch processing, or through system integration. Supports process automation analysis and exception management.
reference: SAP SD Workflow; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Identifier of the SAP workflow or business process instance that triggered or processed this status transition. Enables traceability from the status history record back to the originating workflow for process mining and automation analysis.
reference: SAP Business Workflow; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the blanket order record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement

description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Blanket/framework orders are established through specific sales channels that govern commission eligibility, payment terms, and order entry methods. The distribution_channel STRING attribute is a deno
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.discount_structure. Business justification: Blanket orders negotiate volume-based discount structures upfront. Sales and procurement teams use this link to apply the correct discount automatically to each release order drawn against the blanket
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profitability_segment. Business justification: Blanket orders in manufacturing represent long-term customer commitments tracked against profitability segments for margin analysis. Controlling teams use this link to report contribution margins by s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.rfq_request. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, long-term blanket/framework orders frequently originate from formal RFP/RFQ processes. Linking blanket_order to the originating rfq_request provides end-to-end traceabilit
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.sales_org. Business justification: Blanket orders are created within a specific sales organization context. Sales operations use this to enforce correct pricing, currency, and product availability rules applicable to the sales org mana
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_price_list. Business justification: Blanket orders in manufacturing lock pricing for a period against a specific price list. Contract managers reference this link to enforce agreed pricing across all call-off orders under the blanket.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Business-facing scheduling agreement or blanket order number as assigned in SAP S/4HANA SD. This is the externally communicated reference number used in customer correspondence and order releases.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement (ME31L/VA31)

description: Classification of the blanket order agreement type, distinguishing between scheduling agreements, quantity contracts, value contracts, and framework orders as defined in SAP SD/MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Agreement Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Total quantity of material committed by the customer over the blanket order contract period. Expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for capacity planning and MRP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement Target Quantity; APICS CPIM

description: Reference to the overarching master sales contract or framework agreement under which this blanket order is issued. Enables contract hierarchy traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Contract (VA41); IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the ship-to destination for deliveries under this blanket order. Used for export control, customs compliance, and logistics routing.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS/REACH; Export Control Regulations

description: Timestamp when the blanket order agreement was originally created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Used for audit trail and agreement lifecycle tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 30

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the blanket order values and pricing are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: SAP customer account number (sold-to party) identifying the industrial customer who holds this blanket order agreement. Used for customer master data linkage.

description: Legal or trading name of the industrial customer holding the blanket order. Sourced from SAP customer master and used for reporting and customer-facing documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; GDPR Article 4

description: Percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the net price for this blanket order. Reflects volume commitment or strategic customer pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Condition (K007); IFRS 15

description: SAP division representing the product line or business unit responsible for fulfilling the blanket order. Used for revenue allocation and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Organizational Structure

description: Export control classification status for the blanket order, indicating whether the material requires an export license or is subject to trade embargoes. Critical for multinational compliance.
reference: EAR (Export Administration Regulations); ITAR; EU Dual-Use Regulation; NIST SP 800-171

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery and risk transfer conditions for shipments released under this blanket order.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact location where risk and cost transfer from seller to buyer for releases under this agreement.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the blanket order agreement in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental loads, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 30

description: Date on which the most recent delivery release was issued against this blanket order. Used for consumption tracking and agreement activity monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement

description: Remaining open quantity on the blanket order, calculated as committed quantity minus released quantity. Indicates how much of the customer commitment is yet to be fulfilled.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement Open Quantity

description: SAP payment terms code defining the payment due dates and early payment discount conditions applicable to invoices generated from releases against this blanket order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms; IFRS 9

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility responsible for fulfilling releases against this blanket order. Drives MRP and ATP checks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Plant Configuration

description: The quantity basis for the net unit price (e.g., price per 1 EA, per 100 PC, per 1000 KG). Required to correctly compute line values from quantity and price.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; ISO 15519

description: Reference number of the associated customer pricing agreement or special pricing arrangement that governs the net prices and conditions applied to this blanket order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Agreement; IFRS 15

description: SAP release creation profile controlling the automatic generation of delivery schedule releases (JIT and forecast delivery schedules) based on MRP and planning horizons.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement Release Creation Profile

description: Cumulative quantity released (called off) against the blanket order to date. Represents the sum of all delivery schedule lines confirmed and dispatched.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement Release

description: SAP sales organization responsible for the blanket order agreement. Determines pricing, currency, and legal entity for the order-to-cash process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Organizational Structure

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this blanket order record was sourced. Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the lakehouse.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; GDPR Article 30

description: Current lifecycle status of the blanket order agreement. Drives downstream release processing, replenishment triggers, and order-to-cash workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement Status

description: Maximum percentage by which a delivery release quantity may exceed the scheduled quantity without triggering a goods receipt block. Defined per customer agreement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Delivery Tolerance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Maximum percentage by which a delivery release quantity may fall short of the scheduled quantity and still be accepted as complete by the customer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Delivery Tolerance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Base unit of measure for the committed and released quantities on the blanket order (e.g., EA, KG, PC, M, L). Aligned with SAP base UoM from the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1

description: The date on which the blanket order agreement expires. After this date, no further releases can be processed unless the agreement is extended.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: The date from which the blanket order agreement becomes effective and releases can be made against it. Defines the start of the contract period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each pricing condition record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the pricing_condition data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV/KONP Table Design
