reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.discount_structure. Business justification: Pricing conditions apply discount structures (volume, promotional, customer-specific) to order lines. Order management calculates final prices using defined discount rules and thresholds.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line_item. Business justification: Pricing conditions are applied at the line item level. The polymorphic string document_item_number is replaced by nullable FK order_line_item_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.price_list_item. Business justification: Pricing conditions on orders are derived from product price list items. Pricing and sales teams use this link to apply correct list prices, discounts, and surcharges from the product catalog to order
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.quotation. Business justification: Pricing conditions are also applied to quotations. A nullable FK quotation_id is added (null when condition applies to a sales order). document_number already removed via sales_order_id link.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.quotation_line_item. Business justification: Pricing conditions apply to both sales order line items (already linked via line_item_id) and quotation line items. In industrial manufacturing, detailed pricing conditions (surcharges, discounts, fre
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_price_list. Business justification: Pricing conditions (surcharges, freight, special terms) are anchored to a sales price list in manufacturing ERP. Pricing analysts use this to trace which base list a condition modifies during order pr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.service_contract. Business justification: Pricing conditions for spare parts and service orders in manufacturing are often governed by service contracts that define negotiated rates and discounts. Pricing teams link conditions directly to the
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.tax_code. Business justification: Pricing conditions must apply correct tax codes based on customer location and product taxability. Tax compliance requires accurate tax calculation on every order line.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Code identifying the access sequence used to find the applicable condition record. The access sequence defines the hierarchy of search keys (customer/material, price list, material group) used in pricing determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Access Sequence (T682); SAP Condition Technique

description: Defines the mathematical method used to calculate the condition value: fixed monetary amount, percentage of a base, quantity-based rate, weight-based rate, volume-based rate, formula-driven, or group condition. Directly maps to SAP KONV-KRECH.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Calculation Type (KONV-KRECH); SAP Condition Technique

description: The base amount upon which the condition rate is applied to derive the condition value. For percentage-based conditions, this is the subtotal from the preceding pricing step.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KAWRT; SAP Condition Base Value

description: High-level classification of the pricing condition into business categories: price (base price), discount (reduction), surcharge (addition), freight (logistics cost), tax (statutory levy), rebate, or bonus. Enables aggregated pricing analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Category (KONV-KSCHL); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Identifies the exclusion group to which this condition belongs. Conditions in the same exclusion group are mutually exclusive; only the most favorable or highest-priority condition is applied, preventing double discounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Exclusion (T685A); SAP Condition Exclusion Group

description: Reason code explaining why a pricing condition is inactive or was deactivated. Supports audit trail requirements and pricing dispute resolution in commercial transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KINAK; ISO 9001 Audit Trail; SAP Condition Inactive Reason

description: Indicates how the pricing condition was determined: automatically by the pricing engine, manually entered by a sales representative, derived from a contract, applied from a promotion, or set as an intercompany/transfer price.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KRECH; SAP Condition Origin; IFRS 15

description: The quantity to which the condition rate applies. For per-unit pricing conditions, this is the reference quantity (e.g., price per 100 units). Directly maps to SAP KONV-KPEIN.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KPEIN; SAP Pricing Quantity

description: The rate or percentage used to compute the condition value before application to the base amount. For percentage discounts this is the discount rate; for per-unit surcharges this is the per-unit rate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KBETR; SAP Pricing Rate

description: Business key identifying the pricing condition record as sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD pricing tables (KONV/KONP). Used to trace the condition back to the originating ERP record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV Table; SAP Pricing Condition Record

description: Current lifecycle status of the pricing condition record: active (applied in pricing), inactive (not applied), deleted (removed from document), blocked (temporarily suspended), or statistical (informational only, not included in net value).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KSTATUS; SAP Condition Status

description: SAP SD condition type code identifying the nature of the pricing element (e.g., PR00 for base price, K007 for customer discount, KF00 for freight, MWST for tax). Drives pricing procedure determination logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Type Configuration (T685); SAP Pricing Procedure

description: Human-readable description of the condition type (e.g., 'Price List', 'Customer-Specific Discount', 'Freight Surcharge', 'Value Added Tax'). Supports business reporting and price transparency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Type Configuration (T685)

description: Unit of measure associated with the condition rate (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, PC for piece, TO for metric ton). Relevant for quantity- or weight-based pricing conditions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KMEIN; ISO 80000 Units of Measurement

description: The monetary or percentage value of the pricing condition as applied to the sales document. For fixed-amount conditions this is the absolute value; for percentage conditions this is the computed monetary result.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KWERT; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP ASC 606

description: Reference number of the sales contract or outline agreement from which this pricing condition was derived. Enables traceability of contract-based pricing and supports contract compliance auditing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD VBKD-KDKG1; SAP Contract Pricing; IFRS 15

description: Timestamp when the pricing condition record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and order-to-cash process timeline analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-ERDAT/ERUHR; ISO 9001 Record Control; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the condition value is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing entities.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-WAERS; IFRS 21

description: Customer pricing group classification used in pricing determination. Groups customers with similar pricing entitlements (e.g., key accounts, standard customers, government) to apply consistent pricing policies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KNVV-KONDA; SAP Customer Price Group

description: Indicates whether this condition type is mandatory in the pricing procedure. Mandatory conditions must be present for the document to be processed; missing mandatory conditions trigger pricing errors.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Procedure Configuration; SAP Mandatory Condition

description: Indicates whether this condition is purely statistical (informational) and does not affect the net document value. Statistical conditions are used for reporting and analysis without impacting the price charged to the customer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-KSTAT; SAP Statistical Condition

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the pricing condition record. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and pricing audit trail maintenance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-AEDAT; ISO 9001 Record Control; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether the condition value can be manually overridden by a sales representative. Controls pricing governance and ensures compliance with approved pricing policies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Type Configuration; SAP Manual Pricing Control

description: Material pricing group classification used in pricing determination. Groups products with similar pricing characteristics (e.g., automation components, electrification products, spare parts) for efficient condition record maintenance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD MVKE-KONDM; SAP Material Price Group

description: Identifies the price list category from which this condition was sourced (e.g., wholesale, retail, OEM, distributor, export). Supports differentiated pricing strategies across customer segments in industrial manufacturing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONP-PLTYP; SAP Price List Type

description: The reference date used to determine which pricing condition records are applicable to the sales document. Typically the order creation date or a contractually agreed pricing reference date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD VBKD-PRSDT; SAP Pricing Date

description: Code identifying the pricing procedure (calculation schema) under which this condition is determined. Pricing procedures define the sequence and logic for price determination in SAP SD.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Procedure (T683); SAP Condition Technique

description: Counter within a pricing procedure step used to differentiate multiple conditions at the same step. Supports complex pricing schemas with multiple conditions at identical step positions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Procedure Counter (KONV-ZAEHK)

description: Sequential step number within the pricing procedure at which this condition type is evaluated. Determines the order of price calculation and the base amount for percentage-based conditions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Procedure Step (KONV-STUNR)

description: Reference to the rebate agreement under which this condition was generated. Applicable for rebate accrual conditions that accumulate over a period and are settled at agreement end.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Rebate Agreement (VBOF); IFRS 15 Variable Consideration

description: Defines the dimension on which pricing scales are applied: quantity (volume discount tiers), value (revenue-based tiers), weight, volume, or no scale. Enables tiered pricing models common in industrial manufacturing contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-STFKZ; SAP Scale Basis

description: The quantity threshold at which this pricing scale level applies. Used in conjunction with scale_basis_type to determine the applicable tier for volume-based pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-MENGU; SAP Pricing Scale

description: The monetary value threshold at which this pricing scale level applies. Used for value-based tiered pricing where discounts or rates change based on order value brackets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONV-WRTGU; SAP Pricing Scale Value

description: Tax classification code associated with tax-type conditions (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax). Identifies the applicable tax category for statutory reporting and compliance with regional tax regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Tax Condition (MWST); IFRS; GAAP; EU VAT Directive; REACH/RoHS Compliance

description: Geographic tax jurisdiction code identifying the applicable tax authority for this condition. Critical for multinational operations where tax rates vary by country, state, or region.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD VBPA-TXJCD; OSHA; EPA; EU VAT Directive; GAAP ASC 740

description: The date from which this pricing condition record is valid and applicable to sales transactions. Supports time-bound pricing agreements and seasonal pricing strategies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONP-DATAB; SAP Condition Validity; IFRS 15

description: The date until which this pricing condition record remains valid. After this date the condition expires and is no longer applied in automatic pricing determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD KONP-DATBI; SAP Condition Validity; IFRS 15

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_list. Business justification: Quotations reference price lists to determine base pricing before discounts. Sales teams select appropriate price list based on customer type, region, and contract terms.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.service_contract. Business justification: Quotations for industrial equipment services or parts are frequently issued under an existing service contract that governs pricing, SLAs, and scope. Sales teams reference the service contract on the
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date and time at which the customer formally accepted or rejected the quotation. Used to calculate quotation cycle time and measure sales velocity.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary values on this quotation are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting and FX conversion.

description: SAP customer account number (sold-to party) associated with this quotation. Links the quotation to the customer master record for credit, pricing, and order management purposes.

description: Legal or trading name of the customer or prospect to whom the quotation is addressed. Captured at quotation time to preserve the commercial record even if the customer master is subsequently updated.

description: Number of calendar days from order placement to expected delivery as quoted to the customer. Derived from production scheduling and logistics planning for the specific fulfillment mode.

description: SAP division code representing the product line or business unit responsible for the quoted products. Used for sales area determination and reporting segmentation.

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the transfer of risk, cost responsibility, and delivery obligations between seller and buyer for the quoted shipment.

description: Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact point of risk and cost transfer (e.g., 'FOB Shanghai Port'). Required for logistics and insurance documentation.

description: Calendar date on which the quotation was formally issued and transmitted to the customer or prospect.

description: Total net commercial value of the quotation after all discounts and surcharges but before taxes. Represents the revenue-recognizable amount if the quotation is accepted and converted to a sales order.

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the quotation as assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD. Used in all customer-facing documents, correspondence, and cross-system references.

description: Reference identifier linking this quotation to the originating sales opportunity in Salesforce CRM. Supports opportunity-to-order conversion tracking and win/loss analysis.

description: Agreed payment terms code specifying the due date calculation and any early payment discount conditions (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Sourced from SAP payment terms configuration.

description: Sales representative's estimated probability (0–100%) that this quotation will be accepted and converted to a sales order. Used for pipeline forecasting and revenue planning.

description: Customer-requested delivery date as stated in the RFQ/RFP. Used for Available to Promise (ATP) and Capable to Promise (CTP) checks and delivery lead time commitment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Requested Delivery Date (VDATU); APICS ATP/CTP

description: SAP sales office code identifying the geographic or organizational unit from which the quotation is issued. Supports regional sales performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Sales Office (VKBUR)

description: Name or employee ID of the sales representative responsible for preparing and managing this quotation. Used for commission calculation, workload reporting, and customer relationship management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Sales Employee (PERNR); Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the customer's ship-to destination. Used for export control screening, customs documentation, and logistics routing.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA SD - Ship-To Country; EU Export Control Regulations

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this quotation record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements in the Silver layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Policy; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Current lifecycle status of the quotation. Drives workflow routing, reporting, and order-to-cash pipeline visibility. 'converted' indicates the quotation has been turned into a sales order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Overall Processing Status (GBSTK)

description: Date and time at which the quotation was formally submitted to the customer. Used for response time SLA measurement and sales process analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Change Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Total tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) applicable to the quotation as calculated by the tax determination procedure. Required for statutory invoicing and tax reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Module; IFRS; GAAP; EU VAT Directive

description: Classification of the quotation by commercial structure. Standard quotations cover single transactions; framework quotations establish pricing for repeat orders; blanket quotations cover volume commitments; project quotations support ETO/large capital projects; spot quotations address one-time urgent requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Sales Document Type (AUART)

description: Last calendar date through which the quoted prices, terms, and conditions remain valid for customer acceptance. After this date the quotation status transitions to 'expired'.

description: First calendar date from which the quoted prices, terms, and conditions are commercially binding and valid for customer acceptance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Valid From Date (GUEBG)

description: Sequential version counter incremented each time the quotation is revised and reissued to the customer. Version 1 represents the initial issue; subsequent revisions increment the counter.

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context for the win or loss outcome beyond the structured reason code. Captured by the sales representative for qualitative pipeline analysis.

description: Categorized reason code explaining why the quotation was accepted (won) or rejected (lost). Critical input for sales analytics, pricing strategy, and competitive intelligence reporting.

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each individual line item within a commercial quotation. Serves as the primary key for the quotation_line_item entity.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date and time when this quotation line item record was created in the system. Used for audit trail, SLA tracking, and quotation turnaround time analysis.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the line item pricing is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency quotation management for global operations.

description: Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification number for the quoted product, used for customs declarations, import duty calculation, and trade compliance. Required for cross-border transactions.
reference: WCO Harmonized System (HS) Nomenclature; SAP S/4HANA GTS; EU Customs Code

description: Committed or estimated number of calendar days from order confirmation to delivery for this specific line item. Reflects production lead time, material availability, and logistics scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Scheduling; APICS CPIM

description: Percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the quoted net price for this line item. Reflects customer-specific pricing agreements, volume discounts, or promotional pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing - Condition Type K007; customer.pricing_agreement

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) applicable to the quoted material at the time of quotation. Ensures the correct revision level is quoted and traceable.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; SAP S/4HANA ECM

description: Indicates whether the quoted product is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, packaging, labeling, or transport documentation under applicable regulations.
reference: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HCS); EU REACH/RoHS; IATA/IMDG Dangerous Goods Regulations

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between seller and buyer for this line item. Governs logistics and insurance responsibilities.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD - VBKD-INCO1

description: SAP SD item category code that controls the processing behavior of this line item, including billing relevance, delivery relevance, pricing, and scheduling. Examples: TAN (standard), TAK (consignment), TAP (third-party).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-PSTYV

description: Date and time when this quotation line item record was last updated. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring in the Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - VBAP-AEDAT; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5

description: Total net value of this line item, calculated as quoted net price multiplied by quoted quantity. Represents the commercial value of this specific line in the quotation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-NETWR; IFRS 15

description: Sequential position number of this line item within the parent quotation. Used to order and reference individual items within a multi-line quotation document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-POSNR

description: Standard published catalog or list price per unit of measure for the quoted material, before any discounts or surcharges are applied. Represents the baseline pricing reference.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing - Condition Type PR00; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Short descriptive text for the material or product being quoted on this line item, as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable identification of the quoted item.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - MAKT-MAKTX

description: SAP material master identifier (SKU) for the product, component, or system being quoted on this line. Links to the material master record in SAP S/4HANA MM module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - MARA-MATNR; ISO 22745

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center responsible for fulfilling this line item. Determines production scheduling, inventory sourcing, and shipping origin.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Module - VBAP-WERKS

description: Reference date used to determine applicable pricing conditions, exchange rates, and discount agreements for this line item. Pricing conditions valid on this date are applied.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing - VBAP-PRSDT; IFRS 15

description: Classification grouping for the quoted material, used for pricing, reporting, and sales analysis. Corresponds to the SAP material group or product hierarchy node.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - MARA-MATKL

description: Net unit price offered to the customer on this line item after all applicable discounts, surcharges, and pricing conditions have been applied. This is the contractually binding price per unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing - VBAP-NETPR; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: The quantity of the material or product being quoted on this line item, expressed in the specified unit of measure. Basis for pricing and delivery planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-KWMENG

description: Reason code or description explaining why this specific line item was rejected or removed from the quotation. Used for win/loss analysis and sales process improvement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-ABGRU; Salesforce CRM

description: Customer-requested delivery date for this line item as specified in the RFQ or quotation request. Used as the baseline for ATP/CTP commitment checks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - VBAP-EDATU; APICS ATP/CTP

description: Indicates whether the quoted product complies with the EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Directive, restricting the use of specific hazardous materials in electrical and electronic equipment.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IEC 63000

description: Unit of measure used for sales and pricing purposes on this line item, which may differ from the base unit of measure in the material master (e.g., selling in sets while stocking in pieces).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-VRKME

description: Stock Keeping Unit code representing the specific product variant being quoted, including configuration and packaging distinctions. Used for inventory and fulfillment planning.
reference: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standard; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Current processing status of this quotation line item within the order-to-cash workflow. Tracks the lifecycle from initial creation through approval, conversion to sales order, or rejection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-GBSTA; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

description: SAP storage location within the plant from which the quoted material will be sourced or shipped. Used for inventory reservation and warehouse management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Module - VBAP-LGORT; Infor WMS

description: Secondary sequence number used to identify sub-items or component breakdowns within a parent line item, supporting hierarchical line item structures common in Engineer-to-Order (ETO) and complex system quotations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-UEPOS

description: Percentage surcharge applied to the base price for this line item, covering special handling, expedite fees, hazardous material handling, or custom engineering premiums.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing - Condition Type ZA00

description: Calculated tax amount applicable to this line item based on the tax code and line net value. Supports tax reporting and compliance with local fiscal regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS; GAAP; EU VAT Directive

description: Tax classification code applicable to this line item, determining the applicable VAT, GST, or sales tax rate based on the product type and destination country. Used for tax calculation and compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Module - VBAP-MWSKZ; IFRS; GAAP

description: Condensed summary of the key technical specifications for the product or system being quoted on this line item, including performance parameters, ratings, and compliance certifications relevant to the customer's application.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; IEC 61131; CE Marking; UL Certification

description: Expiration date after which the pricing and terms on this quotation line item are no longer valid. Customer must accept or request renewal before this date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-GUEEN; IFRS 15

description: Date from which the pricing and terms on this quotation line item are valid. Defines the start of the quotation validity window for this specific line.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP-GUEBG; IFRS 15

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each inbound RFQ or RFP record in the order domain. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Inquiry/RFQ Object

description: Foreign key linking to sales.campaign. Business justification: RFQs in industrial manufacturing are frequently triggered by marketing campaigns (trade shows, product launches). Sales ops tracks which campaign sourced the RFQ to measure campaign ROI and pipeline c
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Inbound RFQ/RFP requests arrive through specific sales channels (direct, distributor, portal, EDI). The source_channel STRING attribute is a denormalized channel identifier that is superseded by the F
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: RFQ requests come from specific contacts at customer organizations. Sales teams use this to respond directly to the requesting person and maintain communication history.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_priority. Business justification: RFQ requests carry a priority level for response urgency. The string priority is replaced by FK order_priority_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_specification. Business justification: RFQ requests in industrial manufacturing are issued against a defined product specification. Procurement and engineering jointly use this link to ensure suppliers quote against the correct technical r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: RFQs originate from a specific sales territory in manufacturing. Territory managers review and route RFQs; linking ensures correct team ownership, quota attribution, and regional reporting.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Classification of the end-use application domain for which the requested products or systems are intended. Drives product line routing, engineering specialization assignment, and market segment reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Industry/Segment; SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Document Type

description: Name or identifier of the sales engineer or application engineer responsible for managing the RFQ/RFP response. Drives accountability, workload tracking, and commission attribution.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Owner; SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Representative

description: The sales office or regional sales unit responsible for managing the customer relationship and RFQ response. Used for territory management, revenue attribution, and regional performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Office; Salesforce CRM - Territory

description: The date on which the customer formally awarded the contract or purchase order to the company following the RFQ/RFP evaluation. Marks the transition from pre-sales to order management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Contract Award Date; Salesforce CRM - Close Date Won; IFRS 15 Clause 22

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget indication amount provided by the customer. Supports multi-currency commercial operations across global markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The customer's indicated or estimated budget for the project or procurement as disclosed in the RFQ/RFP. Used for commercial feasibility assessment, pricing strategy, and win probability scoring.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Amount; SAP S/4HANA SD - Target Value; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Names of known competing suppliers or vendors also invited to respond to the RFQ/RFP. Used for competitive intelligence, bid strategy, and win/loss analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Competitors; SAP S/4HANA SD - Competitor

description: Customer-specified regulatory, safety, or certification requirements that the supplied products or systems must meet, such as CE Marking, UL certification, RoHS/REACH compliance, or IEC standards. Drives product compliance validation.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Standards; IEC 61131; ISO 9001:2015

description: Indicates whether the customer requires a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) or confidentiality agreement to be in place before technical details of the RFQ/RFP can be shared internally or with subcontractors.
reference: GDPR Article 28 - Processor Obligations; SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination country where the ordered goods or systems are to be delivered. Drives export compliance checks, trade regulation screening, and logistics planning.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS/REACH Compliance; Export Administration Regulations (EAR)

description: The customer's own internal document or project reference number as provided on the RFQ/RFP submission. Used for cross-referencing with the customer's procurement system and for communication alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Reference; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2

description: Standardized reason code explaining why the RFQ/RFP was declined, either by the company (no-bid decision) or by the customer (lost to competitor). Used for win/loss analysis and continuous improvement.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Loss Reason; SAP S/4HANA SD - Rejection Reason; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 - Nonconformity and Corrective Action

description: The channel through which the customer is engaging for this RFQ, such as direct sales, distributor, OEM partner, or system integrator. Affects pricing tiers, discount structures, and margin reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Distribution Channel; Salesforce CRM - Channel

description: Internal estimate of the total contract value if the RFQ/RFP is won, used for pipeline reporting, revenue forecasting, and resource allocation decisions. Distinct from the customer's budget indication.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Amount; SAP S/4HANA SD - Target Value; IFRS 15

description: Description of the criteria the customer will use to evaluate and select a supplier response, such as price, technical compliance, delivery lead time, quality certifications, or after-sales support. Informs bid strategy and proposal emphasis.
reference: SAP Ariba Sourcing - Evaluation Criteria; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 - External Providers

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to the requested delivery, defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between seller and buyer. Used for logistics planning, insurance, and contract terms.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD - Incoterms

description: The timestamp of the most recent update to the RFQ record, capturing any changes to status, assignments, or commercial data. Used for data freshness tracking, audit compliance, and change detection in the Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Last Changed Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5

description: The date on which the Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) or confidentiality agreement was formally signed between the company and the customer for this RFQ/RFP. Required before sharing proprietary technical information.
reference: GDPR Article 28; SAP Ariba Contract Management; ISO 27001 Information Security

description: The payment terms requested or indicated by the customer in the RFQ/RFP, such as net 30, net 60, or milestone-based payments. Used for credit assessment and commercial proposal structuring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Payment Terms; IFRS 15 - Variable Consideration

description: The name of the customer's project or initiative for which the RFQ/RFP is being issued. Provides business context for the request and is used for opportunity tracking and pipeline reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Opportunity Name; SAP S/4HANA PS - Project Name

description: The total quantity of units, systems, or assemblies requested by the customer in the RFQ/RFP. Used for capacity planning, ATP/CTP checks, and commercial pricing calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Target Quantity; MRP Demand Planning

description: The unit of measure associated with the requested quantity (e.g., EA for each, SET for a system set, KG for weight-based items). Ensures accurate commercial and production planning interpretation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD - Unit of Measure; ISO 80000 International System of Units

description: The precise date and time at which the RFQ/RFP was received and registered in the system. Used for SLA compliance measurement, response time analytics, and audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Creation Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2; GDPR Article 5 - Accountability

description: The date by which the customer requires delivery of the ordered goods or systems. Used for Available-to-Promise (ATP) and Capable-to-Promise (CTP) checks and production scheduling feasibility assessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Requested Delivery Date; ATP/CTP Logic

description: The precise date and time at which the formal quotation or proposal response was submitted to the customer. Used to measure response cycle time and SLA adherence.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Change Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3 - Review of Requirements

description: The deadline by which the customer requires a formal quotation or proposal response. Drives prioritization of sales and engineering resources and triggers escalation workflows when approaching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Valid-To Date; Salesforce CRM - Close Date

description: Flag indicating whether the inbound request is a formal Request for Proposal (RFP) requiring a detailed technical and commercial proposal, as opposed to a standard Request for Quotation (RFQ) focused on pricing. True = RFP; False = RFQ.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Inquiry Type; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Opportunity Type

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier assigned to the inbound RFQ or RFP document, used for cross-system reference and customer communication. Corresponds to the customer-provided document number or internally assigned tracking number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Inquiry Number; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2 - Customer Requirements

description: The SAP sales organization code representing the legal entity or business unit responsible for the sale. Determines pricing procedures, output determination, and revenue booking entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Organization; IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Current lifecycle status of the inbound RFQ/RFP record, tracking progression from initial receipt through evaluation, response, and final disposition. Drives workflow routing and pipeline visibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Inquiry Status; Salesforce CRM - Opportunity Stage; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: The date on which the customer or prospect formally submitted the RFQ or RFP to the company. Used to calculate response lead times and track compliance with response SLAs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Inquiry Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2

description: Free-text summary of the customer's technical specifications, performance requirements, and engineering constraints as stated in the RFQ/RFP document. Provides context for application engineering and product configuration.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Requirements Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2

description: Customer-specified warranty duration, coverage scope, or warranty terms required for the products or systems being quoted. Informs commercial pricing, risk assessment, and after-sales service planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Warranty; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 - Nonconforming Outputs

description: Sales team's estimated probability (0-100%) of winning the RFQ/RFP and converting it to a sales order. Used for weighted pipeline reporting and revenue forecasting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud - Probability; SAP S/4HANA SD - Probability

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each product classification assignment record in the Databricks Silver Layer catalog.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry

description: System-defined code for the characteristic within the classification standard (e.g., eCl@ss property IRDI '0173-1#02-AAB329#007' for Rated Voltage). Enables machine-readable attribute exchange in EDI and punchout catalog integrations.
reference: eCl@ss e.V. IRDI (International Registration Data Identifier); IEC 61360 Common Data Dictionary

description: Data type of the characteristic value as defined by the classification system (e.g., numeric, string, boolean, coded_value, range, date). Enables correct parsing and validation of characteristic values during EDI exchange and catalog integration.
reference: eCl@ss e.V. BASIC/ADVANCED; IEC 61360 Common Data Dictionary; ETIM International

description: Name of the technical characteristic or attribute defined by the classification system for this class (e.g., 'Rated Voltage', 'IP Protection Class', 'Power Rating', 'Rated Current'). Each classification record captures one characteristic key-value pair per the classification system's feature catalog.
reference: eCl@ss e.V. BASIC/ADVANCED; ETIM International Feature Catalog; IEC 61360 Common Data Dictionary

description: Unit of measure associated with the characteristic value (e.g., 'V' for voltage, 'kW' for power, 'A' for current, 'Hz' for frequency). Follows SI units as defined by the classification standard. Null for non-numeric or coded characteristics.
reference: IEC 61360 Common Data Dictionary; SI Units (BIPM); eCl@ss e.V. Unit Catalog

description: The assigned value for the classification characteristic (e.g., '400V' for Rated Voltage, 'IP65' for IP Protection Class, '11kW' for Power Rating). Stored as STRING to accommodate numeric, coded, and free-text values across all classification systems.
reference: eCl@ss e.V.; ETIM International; IEC 60529 (IP Protection Ratings); IEC 60038 (Standard Voltages)

description: The specific class or commodity code assigned to the product within the classification system (e.g., eCl@ss class code '27-37-10-01' for circuit breakers, UNSPSC code '39121000' for power distribution equipment). Used for structured product search and cross-catalog comparison.
reference: eCl@ss e.V.; UNSPSC (United Nations Development Programme); ETIM International; GS1 GPC

description: Human-readable name or label of the assigned classification class (e.g., 'Circuit Breaker', 'Power Distribution Equipment'). Supports product search, catalog display, and reporting without requiring lookup of the class code.
reference: eCl@ss e.V.; UNSPSC (United Nations Development Programme); ETIM International

description: Third-level commodity code within the classification hierarchy (e.g., UNSPSC commodity '391210' for Circuit Breakers). Provides granular product categorization for procurement sourcing, supplier management via SAP Ariba, and spend analytics.
reference: UNSPSC (United Nations Development Programme); SAP Ariba Procurement

description: Confidence percentage (0.00–100.00) associated with the classification assignment, particularly relevant for automated or AI-assisted classifications. Enables quality filtering and prioritization of manual review for low-confidence assignments. Null for fully manual classifications.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management; NIST AI Risk Management Framework

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country for which this classification is applicable when classification_scope is 'country'. Supports country-specific regulatory classification requirements (e.g., CE marking classification in EU countries, UL classification in USA).
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; EU CE Marking Directive; UL (Underwriters Laboratories)

description: Date and time when this classification record was first created in the system. Provides the foundational audit trail entry for the classification assignment lifecycle.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Date on which the product was assigned to this classification class and characteristics were captured. Used for audit trails, regulatory compliance reporting, and tracking classification currency against evolving standards.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management System; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification Module

description: eCl@ss classification code assigned to the product in the format XX-XX-XX-XX (e.g., '27-37-10-01' for Low Voltage Circuit Breakers). eCl@ss is the primary classification standard for industrial automation, electrification, and smart infrastructure products in the manufacturing sector.
reference: eCl@ss e.V.; DIN EN ISO 13584 (PLIB); IEC 61360 Common Data Dictionary

description: Classification code formatted for use in Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) transactions and punchout catalog integrations (e.g., cXML, OCI punchout). May differ from the raw class_code due to EDI partner-specific formatting requirements. Supports SAP Ariba punchout catalog integration.
reference: SAP Ariba Punchout Catalog; cXML Standard (Ariba); OCI (Open Catalog Interface) Standard

description: Date from which this classification assignment is valid and active for use in product search, EDI transactions, and punchout catalog integrations. Supports time-bounded classification management when standards are updated.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry; GS1 GPC Effective Dating

description: Electrotechnical Information Model (ETIM) class code assigned to the product (e.g., 'EC002714' for Circuit Breaker). ETIM is the dominant classification standard for electrotechnical and industrial automation products in European markets. Stored as a dedicated field due to its critical importance in the industrial manufacturing sector.
reference: ETIM International; IEC 61360 Common Data Dictionary; European electrical wholesale trade standard

description: Date on which this classification assignment expires or is superseded. Used when migrating to a new classification system version or when a product is reclassified. Null indicates the classification is currently active with no planned expiry.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry; eCl@ss e.V. Version Migration Guidelines

description: Second-level family code within the classification hierarchy (e.g., UNSPSC family '3912' for Power Distribution and Protection Equipment). Supports mid-level product grouping for procurement category management and spend analysis.
reference: UNSPSC (United Nations Development Programme); eCl@ss e.V.

description: Indicates whether this classification record is the primary/preferred classification for the product within the given classification system. A product may have multiple class assignments within a system (e.g., main class and supplementary class); this flag identifies the authoritative one for search and EDI.
reference: eCl@ss e.V. Classification Best Practices; GS1 GPC Implementation Guide

description: IETF BCP 47 language tag for the language in which class names and characteristic names/values are expressed (e.g., 'en-US', 'de-DE', 'zh-CN'). Supports multilingual classification for global catalog distribution and EDI partner requirements.
reference: IETF BCP 47 Language Tags; ISO 639-1 Language Codes; eCl@ss e.V. Multilingual Support

description: Date and time when this classification record was most recently updated. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, incremental loads to the Silver Layer, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Method by which the product was assigned to this classification. 'manual' indicates a human classifier assigned it; 'automated' indicates rule-based assignment; 'ai_assisted' indicates machine learning-assisted classification; 'inherited' indicates inherited from a parent product; 'imported' indicates bulk-imported from an external catalog.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Process Control; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification Workflow

description: Free-text notes or comments related to this classification assignment. Used to document classification rationale, exceptions, pending reviews, or guidance for future reclassification. Supports knowledge transfer and audit documentation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Indicates whether this classification record is published and active in punchout catalog integrations (e.g., SAP Ariba, Coupa). When true, the classification is available for buyer-side catalog search and procurement workflows.
reference: SAP Ariba Procurement; cXML Punchout Standard; OCI (Open Catalog Interface)

description: Indicates whether this classification assignment has direct regulatory compliance relevance. Values include 'ce_marking' (EU product safety), 'rohs' (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), 'reach' (Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals), 'ul' (Underwriters Laboratories), 'multiple' (more than one regulation applies), or 'none'.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive 2001/95/EC; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; UL (Underwriters Laboratories)

description: Scope of applicability for this classification assignment. 'global' applies across all markets; 'regional' applies to a geographic region; 'country' applies to a specific country market; 'sales_channel' applies to a specific distribution channel (e.g., punchout, EDI); 'internal' is for internal use only.
reference: GS1 GPC Global Implementation; UNSPSC Regional Adoption Guidelines

description: Top-level segment code within the classification hierarchy (e.g., UNSPSC segment '39' for Electrical Systems and Lighting Equipment, eCl@ss segment '27' for Electrical Engineering). Enables high-level product portfolio analysis and spend analytics.
reference: UNSPSC (United Nations Development Programme); eCl@ss e.V.

description: Operational system of record from which this classification record originated. Primarily Siemens Teamcenter PLM (Classification module) or SAP S/4HANA MM (Material Classification). Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the Silver Layer.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification; SAP S/4HANA MM Classification (CL02)

description: Native primary key or record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Teamcenter classification object UID, SAP classification assignment internal number). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA MM Classification

description: Current lifecycle status of the classification assignment. 'active' indicates the classification is in use; 'pending_review' indicates it requires validation against a new standard version; 'superseded' indicates it has been replaced by a newer classification record; 'draft' indicates it is under preparation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Document Control; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Lifecycle Management

description: Name of the industry-standard or internal classification system used for this assignment. Supported systems include eCl@ss (electrotechnical/industrial), UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code), ETIM (Electrotechnical Information Model), GPC (GS1 Global Product Classification), HS (Harmonized System), NAICS, and internal/custom schemas.
reference: eCl@ss e.V.; UNSPSC (United Nations Development Programme); ETIM International; GS1 GPC; WCO Harmonized System

description: Version or release of the classification system applied (e.g., eCl@ss 12.0, UNSPSC v23.0901, ETIM 8.0). Critical for maintaining traceability as classification standards evolve and for EDI/punchout catalog compatibility.
reference: eCl@ss e.V. Release Management; UNSPSC Version Control; ETIM International Release Notes

description: United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC) 8-digit code assigned to the product. Widely used in procurement systems (SAP Ariba) for spend analytics, supplier management, and sourcing category management across global operations.
reference: UNSPSC (United Nations Development Programme / GS1 US); SAP Ariba Procurement

description: Username or employee ID of the person who validated and approved this classification assignment. Required for quality-controlled classification workflows, particularly for regulatory-relevant classifications (CE, RoHS, REACH). Supports ISO 9001 audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Approval Workflow

description: Date and time when the classification assignment was validated and approved. Provides a precise audit trail for compliance reporting and classification governance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the product hierarchy tree. Serves as the primary key for the product_hierarchy entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy (T-code: MM41/MM42)

description: Identifier of the corresponding classification node in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Enables cross-system alignment between the commercial product hierarchy (SAP) and the engineering product structure (PLM).
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification Module

description: Name or employee identifier of the individual who approved the creation or last significant change to this hierarchy node. Supports governance, audit trail, and change management requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Internal Product Portfolio Governance

description: Date and time when this hierarchy node was formally approved by the designated product portfolio governance authority. Distinct from created_timestamp as approval may occur after initial creation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Internal Product Portfolio Governance

description: Name of the reportable business segment (as defined under IFRS 8 / ASC 280) to which this hierarchy node contributes. Used for segment-level P&L reporting and investor disclosures.
reference: IFRS 8 Operating Segments; GAAP ASC 280; SAP S/4HANA CO-PA

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country or market for which this hierarchy node is applicable. Supports regional product portfolio management and country-specific catalog configurations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA Sales Area Configuration

description: Date and time when this product hierarchy node record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change management tracking.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Extended free-text description of the product hierarchy node, providing business context about the product classification, scope, and strategic positioning within the portfolio.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Code identifying the top-level product division to which this hierarchy node belongs (e.g., AUT = Automation Systems, ELC = Electrification Solutions, SMI = Smart Infrastructure). Supports revenue reporting by division.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Division Configuration; Internal Product Portfolio Governance

description: Date from which this hierarchy node becomes valid and available for product assignment and reporting. Supports time-based validity management aligned with SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy date ranges.

description: Date after which this hierarchy node is no longer valid for new product assignments. Null indicates no planned expiry. Supports time-bounded hierarchy management and historical reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; ISO 8601

description: Indicates whether products in this hierarchy node are subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR, EU Dual-Use Regulation). Triggers export license review and trade compliance screening.
reference: US EAR (Export Administration Regulations); EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; ITAR

description: Product hierarchy code used in external-facing systems such as customer portals, distributor catalogs, or EDI transactions. May differ from the internal SAP hierarchy node code.

description: Primary sales and distribution channel strategy for products classified under this hierarchy node. Informs pricing strategy, margin targets, and channel partner management.

description: GS1 Global Product Classification brick code that maps this internal hierarchy node to the globally standardized product classification schema. Enables cross-industry product data exchange and benchmarking.
reference: GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC) Standard

description: Indicates whether products classified under this hierarchy node may contain or involve hazardous materials subject to REACH, RoHS, or OSHA regulations. Triggers additional compliance and documentation requirements.
reference: REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Indicates whether this node is a terminal leaf node in the hierarchy (i.e., it has no child nodes). Leaf nodes represent the lowest level of classification (Product Line) to which SKUs are directly assigned.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy

description: Indicates whether this hierarchy node is designated as a revenue reporting aggregation point in financial and management reporting. Nodes flagged true are used in P&L roll-up and EBITDA reporting by product line.

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the language in which the short_name and long_name are stored. Supports multilingual product hierarchy descriptions for global operations across multiple countries and regions.
reference: ISO 639-1; SAP S/4HANA Language-Dependent Text Tables

description: Date and time when this product hierarchy node record was most recently updated. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 8601; Internal Data Governance Policy; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Numeric depth of this node within the product hierarchy tree. Level 1 = Product Division (e.g., Automation Systems), Level 2 = Product Family, Level 3 = Product Group, Level 4 = Product Line. Supports OLAP drill-down and revenue roll-up reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; ISO 15926

description: Business label for the hierarchy depth of this node. Defines whether the node represents a Product Division, Product Family, Product Group, or Product Line. Enables consistent labeling in reports and catalog navigation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Current stage of the product portfolio lifecycle for this hierarchy node, based on the standard product lifecycle model. Used in portfolio management, investment prioritization, and discontinuation planning.
reference: ISO 15288 System Lifecycle; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Lifecycle Management

description: Full descriptive name of the hierarchy node used in catalog navigation, customer-facing materials, and detailed reporting. Maximum 40 characters per SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy

description: Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this node within the product hierarchy tree. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy key (up to 18 characters). Used as the natural business key for cross-system integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; ANSI X12

description: Name or employee identifier of the product manager accountable for the product classification represented by this hierarchy node. Used for escalation, catalog governance, and stakeholder communication.

description: Internal code identifying the geographic sales or operational region to which this hierarchy node is scoped (e.g., AMER, EMEA, APAC). Supports regional revenue reporting and catalog segmentation.
reference: Internal Geographic Segmentation Standard; SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Organization

description: Comma-separated list of applicable regulatory frameworks and compliance standards for products in this hierarchy node (e.g., CE, UL, RoHS, REACH, IEC 62443). Supports compliance tracking and certification management.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Standards; IEC 62443

description: Name or code of the business unit or product management organization responsible for maintaining and governing this hierarchy node. Supports accountability and change management workflows.
reference: Internal Product Portfolio Governance; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Full concatenated hierarchy path string as stored in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., '00100010001'). Represents the complete ancestry chain from root to this node using fixed-length segment codes. Used for SAP-native hierarchy queries and integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy (Field: PRODH)

description: Abbreviated display name for the hierarchy node, used in space-constrained UI elements, reports, and dashboard labels. Maximum 20 characters per SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy

description: Numeric sequence controlling the display order of sibling nodes within the same parent in catalog navigation, reports, and UI tree views. Lower values appear first.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this hierarchy node was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between SAP S/4HANA and Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 Data Provenance; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Primary key or unique identifier of this hierarchy node as stored in the originating source system (e.g., SAP product hierarchy key PRODH value). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy (Field: PRODH); Internal Data Lineage Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the product hierarchy node. Active nodes are available for product assignment and reporting. Obsolete nodes are retained for historical data integrity. Planned nodes are approved but not yet effective.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Name or code of the corporate strategic initiative or growth program associated with this product hierarchy node (e.g., Digital Factory, Green Energy Transition, Smart Buildings). Supports portfolio-level strategic planning and investment tracking.
reference: Internal Product Portfolio Governance; Corporate Strategy Framework

description: Name of the underlying technology platform or architecture associated with products in this hierarchy node (e.g., TIA Portal, SIMATIC, SENTRON). Supports technology roadmap planning and R&D investment allocation.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; Internal R&D Portfolio Management

business_glossary_term: United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC)
description: UNSPSC code mapping this hierarchy node to the United Nations Standard Products and Services Classification. Used in procurement, sourcing (SAP Ariba), and spend analytics for category management.
reference: UNSPSC v23; SAP Ariba Procurement

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a product substitution or supersession relationship record in the catalog.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Substitution

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: The substitution product defines approved product substitution and supersession relationships between catalog items. source_hierarchy_id references the ORIGINAL catalog item being substituted (the ite
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Defines the business processes in which this substitution relationship is applicable. Allows selective enablement — e.g., a substitution valid for spare parts cross-referencing in service but not for production BOM substitution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD/PP - Material Substitution; Salesforce CRM - Field Service

description: Name or user ID of the authorized approver who validated and activated the substitution record. Supports audit trail and change management governance requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution

description: The date and time when the substitution record was formally approved and authorized for use in business processes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution

description: The date and time when the substitution record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity and Accountability

description: Indicates whether the customer must be notified before or when this substitution is applied to their order. Supports SLA compliance and customer communication workflows in Salesforce CRM Service Cloud.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Cloud; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.1 - Customer Communication

description: Indicates whether the substitution applies in one direction only (source can be replaced by target, but not vice versa) or bidirectionally (either product can substitute for the other). Critical for ATP/CTP fallback logic in order management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Availability Check (ATP); SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution

description: The date from which the substitution relationship becomes valid and can be applied in order management, procurement, and service operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: The date after which the substitution relationship is no longer valid. Null indicates no planned expiry. Used to automatically deactivate time-limited substitutions such as emergency alternates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Indicates whether the substitution involves products subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR). When true, the substitution must be reviewed by the trade compliance team before application in international orders.
reference: U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ITAR; EU Dual-Use Regulation (EC) No 428/2009

description: Describes the degree to which the target product is functionally equivalent to the source product. 'full' means complete drop-in replacement; 'partial' means some functional differences exist; 'form-fit-function' meets dimensional and interface requirements; 'dimensional-only' matches physical dimensions only; 'performance-limited' has reduced performance characteristics.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ANSI/EIA-649 Configuration Management Standard

description: Indicates whether the substitution involves a change in hazardous material content or classification between the source and target products. Triggers mandatory review under REACH, RoHS, and HSE compliance workflows.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HCS)

description: Indicates whether the system is permitted to automatically apply this substitution during order processing (ATP/CTP fallback) without manual intervention. When false, a planner or customer service representative must approve the substitution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Availability Check (ATP); SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution

description: The date and time when the substitution record was most recently updated. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Delta Lake Standards

description: The difference in procurement or production lead time (in calendar days) between the target and source products. Positive values indicate longer lead time for the substitute; negative values indicate shorter. Used in ATP/CTP calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - MRP; SAP S/4HANA SD - Available to Promise (ATP)

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or contextual information relevant to the substitution relationship that is not captured by structured fields.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: Business-facing unique identifier for the substitution relationship, used in order management, service, and procurement communications (e.g., SUB-2024-00123).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management

description: The corresponding substitution or interchangeability record identifier in Siemens Teamcenter PLM, enabling cross-system traceability between the commercial product catalog and the engineering product data management system.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - BOM Management; PDM Interchangeability Records

description: Indicates the general price impact of applying the substitution relative to the source product. Used by order management and sales to communicate cost implications to customers and for margin analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Pricing; SAP S/4HANA CO - Product Costing

description: Numeric priority rank when multiple substitutes exist for a single source product. Lower number indicates higher preference (1 = most preferred). Used by ATP/CTP logic to sequence fallback options during order promising.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Availability Check (ATP/CTP); SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution

description: The quantity conversion ratio between source and target products. A value of 1.0 indicates a 1:1 substitution; a value of 2.0 means 2 units of the target are required to replace 1 unit of the source. Critical for accurate order fulfillment and BOM substitution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution; SAP S/4HANA PP - Bill of Materials (BOM)

description: Standardized reason code explaining why the substitution relationship was established. Supports analytics on substitution drivers and Engineering Change Notice (ECN) traceability.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management (ECN/ECO); SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context for the substitution reason beyond the standardized reason code, such as specific engineering justification or supplier change details.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6 - Control of Changes

description: Indicates whether the substitution was driven by or must satisfy regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., RoHS, REACH, CE Marking). When true, the substitution must be validated against applicable regulatory certifications before activation.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; CE Marking Directive

description: The originating operational system of record from which this substitution record was sourced or created (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.

description: The primary key or unique identifier of the substitution record in the originating source system, enabling bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 - Data Provenance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Lineage Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the substitution record. 'draft' indicates under review, 'pending-approval' awaits authorization, 'active' is approved and in use, 'inactive' is temporarily disabled, 'expired' has passed its end date, 'superseded' has been replaced by a newer substitution record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: SAP material number of the target/replacement product, enabling direct traceability to the ERP material master record for the substitute item.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (MARA)

description: The replacement or substitute product SKU that fulfills the role of the source product. This is the 'to' side of the substitution relationship.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master; GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standard

description: Classifies the nature of the substitution relationship: 'superseded-by' for end-of-life replacements, 'equivalent' for functionally identical items, 'preferred-alternate' for supply continuity alternates, 'cross-reference' for competitor or interchangeable part mapping, 'emergency-alternate' for unplanned supply disruption fallback.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Substitution; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 - Control of Externally Provided Processes

description: The unit of measure applicable to the quantity ratio (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Ensures correct quantity conversion when applying the substitution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master; ISO 80000 - Quantities and Units

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Detailed technical and commercial description of the aftermarket part, including functional purpose, key specifications, and application context. Used in service quotes, field service documentation, and customer portals.

description: Standard procurement or manufacturing lead time in calendar days for the aftermarket part. Used for Available-to-Promise (ATP) calculations, service order scheduling, and safety stock determination.

description: Date on which the aftermarket part is scheduled to be discontinued or withdrawn from the service catalog. Used for end-of-life planning, last-time-buy notifications to customers, and inventory drawdown management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Date on which the aftermarket part became commercially available in the service catalog. Marks the beginning of the active selling period and is used for lifecycle reporting and product availability analytics.

description: SAP material group code classifying the aftermarket part for procurement, spend analytics, and reporting purposes. Aligns with the MM module material group hierarchy.

description: Average operating hours between required replacements for this part, derived from Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) analysis. Used to drive preventive maintenance scheduling, spare parts demand forecasting, and service interval recommendations.
reference: ISO 13306 Maintenance Terminology; IEC 60300-3-11 Reliability; Maximo EAM

description: Minimum quantity of the aftermarket part that must be ordered per transaction in the service channel. Reflects packaging constraints, supplier minimums, or commercial policy for the aftermarket business.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Purchasing Info Record; SAP Ariba

description: Short commercial name or title of the aftermarket part as presented in the service catalog and customer-facing documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: The unique commercial part number assigned to this aftermarket item in the service and spare parts catalog. Used for ordering, quoting, and logistics identification across the aftermarket channel.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 8000

description: Classification of the aftermarket part by its commercial and functional role in the service channel. OEM spare parts are original manufacturer replacements; consumables are regularly depleted items; wear parts have predictable degradation cycles; repair kits bundle components for a specific repair task; exchange units are refurbished assemblies; upgrade kits extend or enhance equipment capability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Type; Maximo EAM Spare Part Classification

description: Indicates whether the aftermarket part complies with EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 regarding chemical substance registration, evaluation, and authorization. Required for EU market access and supply chain chemical transparency.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ECHA Guidance; ISO 14001:2015

description: Unit of measure for the mean time between replacements value. Supports multiple interval bases including operating hours, production cycles, calendar months, years, or distance (km) depending on the equipment type.
