description: Indicates whether the aftermarket part complies with EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU restricting the use of hazardous substances (lead, mercury, cadmium, etc.) in electrical and electronic equipment. Required for CE marking and EU market access.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IEC 63000 Technical Documentation for RoHS

description: The commercial channel through which this aftermarket part is sold and distributed. Distinguishes direct service (sold by manufacturer service team), distributor (sold through authorized service partners), ecommerce (online self-service), field service (delivered by field engineers), and depot repair (used in repair center operations).
reference: Salesforce CRM Service Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD Distribution Channel

description: Maximum number of days the aftermarket part can be stored before it is no longer suitable for use. Applicable to consumables, lubricants, adhesives, and other time-sensitive materials. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Shelf Life Management; ISO 9001:2015; OSHA Storage Requirements

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this aftermarket part master record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality; DAMA DMBOK Data Lineage

description: The natural key or primary identifier of this aftermarket part record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP material number, Maximo item number). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.
reference: ISO 8000; DAMA DMBOK; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Number

description: Standard cost of the aftermarket part used for inventory valuation, cost of goods sold (COGS) calculation, and service margin analysis. Maintained in SAP CO and updated during standard cost runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Product Costing; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory; IFRS IAS 2

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost value of the aftermarket part.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA CO-PC

description: Current lifecycle status of the aftermarket part in the commercial catalog. Active parts are available for ordering; phase_out parts are being withdrawn; discontinued parts are no longer supplied; superseded parts have been replaced by a newer part number; development parts are not yet released for sale; blocked parts are temporarily unavailable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Inventory stocking strategy classification for the aftermarket part. Critical spares are held at all times to prevent unplanned downtime; standard parts follow normal replenishment cycles; non-stock parts are procured on demand; consignment parts are held at customer sites; slow-moving parts have low demand frequency.
reference: Infor WMS; Maximo EAM MRO Classification; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: The effective date on which this part was superseded by the replacement part number. Used to manage transition periods in field service, spare parts ordering, and inventory drawdown.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM ECN/ECO Management

description: Indicates whether the aftermarket part has been tested and listed by Underwriters Laboratories (UL) for product safety compliance. Required for North American market access in electrical and industrial applications.
reference: UL Standards; ANSI; NFPA 70 National Electrical Code

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each product document record in the commercial and technical document library.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5 - Documented Information

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Controls who is authorized to access and download this document. Public documents are available on the product portal; restricted documents require authentication and role-based authorization.

description: Name or employee identifier of the authorized person who approved the document for release. Required for quality management system compliance and regulatory audit trails.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.3; SAP DMS Approval Workflow

description: Date and time when the document was formally approved for release by the authorized approver. Provides an immutable audit trail for quality and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.3; 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records)

description: Name or employee identifier of the person who authored or is primarily responsible for the content of this document. Used for accountability and document ownership tracking.

description: Name of the third-party certification or notified body that issued or validated the document (e.g., UL, TÜV, Bureau Veritas, SGS, Intertek). Applicable for test reports, certificates, and declarations of conformity.

description: SHA-256 cryptographic hash of the document file used to verify document integrity and detect unauthorized modifications. Supports regulatory audit trail requirements and tamper-evidence for compliance documents.

description: Indicates whether this document is classified as confidential and subject to non-disclosure restrictions. Confidential documents require NDA or contractual agreement before distribution.

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code(s) indicating the geographic markets for which this document is valid. 'GLOBAL' indicates worldwide applicability. Pipe-separated for multi-country documents.

description: Date and time when the product document record was first created in the data platform. Provides audit trail for data lineage and record management.

description: Date on which the document is scheduled to expire or be reviewed for renewal. Particularly relevant for certificates, declarations of conformity, and regulatory approvals with defined validity periods.

description: Indicates whether this document is subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR) and requires authorization before distribution to foreign nationals or international customers.

description: Electronic file format of the document (e.g., PDF, DOCX, DWG). Determines rendering capability, archival requirements, and distribution channel compatibility.

description: Size of the document file in kilobytes. Used for storage management, download time estimation, and portal performance optimization.

description: Indicates whether this document contains or references hazardous material information (e.g., Safety Data Sheets for products containing restricted substances). Triggers mandatory handling and distribution controls.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HCS) 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS (Globally Harmonized System)

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) indicating the language in which the document is authored (e.g., en, de, fr, zh-CN). Supports multilingual document management for global product distribution.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; EU CE Marking Directive (local language requirements); REACH Regulation Article 31

description: Date and time when the product document record was most recently updated in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5

description: Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of this document to ensure continued accuracy, regulatory compliance, and alignment with current product specifications.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.3; Internal Document Control Procedure

description: Official document number assigned by the document management system (Siemens Teamcenter or SAP DMS) that uniquely identifies the document across all revisions and versions.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.2; Siemens Teamcenter Document Management

description: Organizational department or business unit responsible for maintaining and updating this document (e.g., Product Engineering, Regulatory Affairs, Marketing, Quality Management).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.2; Internal Document Control Procedure

description: Date on which the document was officially published and made available for distribution to internal users, customers, or regulatory bodies.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.3

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant
description: Indicates whether this document certifies REACH compliance for the associated product. Required for chemical substance declarations and Safety Data Sheets (SDS) under EU REACH Regulation.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ECHA Guidance

description: The specific regulatory standard, directive, or certification framework that this document supports or demonstrates compliance with (e.g., IEC 61131, CE Marking Directive, RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU, REACH Regulation, UL 508A, ISO 9001). Critical for Declaration of Conformity and test report documents.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; IEC 62443; UL Certification; ISO 9001:2015

description: Defined periodic review interval in months for this document type. Drives automated review reminders and ensures documents remain current with product changes and regulatory updates.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.3; Internal Document Control Procedure

description: Current revision or version identifier of the document (e.g., A, B, 01, 1.0, Rev C). Tracks the document change history and ensures the correct version is referenced in production and compliance activities.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.3; Siemens Teamcenter Change Management

description: Indicates whether this document (typically an SDS or Declaration of Conformity) certifies RoHS compliance for the associated product. Mandatory for products sold in the European Union under Directive 2011/65/EU.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU CE Marking

business_glossary_term: SAP Document Management System (DMS) Document Key
description: Document key from SAP DMS (Document Management System within SAP S/4HANA) used to link the product document record back to the originating SAP document object for procurement, quality, and production cross-references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Document Management System (DMS); SAP Note 1234567

description: Indicates the breadth of product applicability for this document — whether it applies to a single SKU, a product family, an entire product line, or a platform-wide standard.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.2; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Identifies the originating operational system of record from which this document record was sourced (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP DMS). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance and Lineage Standard; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Current lifecycle status of the document. Controls whether the document is available for distribution, under review, or retired from active use. Aligns with document control requirements under ISO 9001.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.3; SAP DMS Document Status

description: Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) pointing to the physical storage location of the document file in the enterprise content management system, cloud storage (e.g., Azure Data Lake, S3), or Teamcenter vault. Enables direct file retrieval.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.2; Internal IT Architecture Standard

description: Native document identifier from Siemens Teamcenter PLM document management module. Enables direct cross-reference and drill-through to the authoritative source system record.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management Module

description: Full descriptive title of the document as it appears on the cover page or header, used for search, display, and catalog purposes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5.2

description: Classification of the document by its business purpose and content type. Drives downstream routing, access control, and regulatory compliance workflows. Includes SDS (Safety Data Sheet), DoC (Declaration of Conformity), and other standard document categories.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 31 (SDS); EU CE Marking Directive

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual line-level pricing record within a price list. Serves as the primary key for the price_list_item entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Record (KONP/KONH)

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_hierarchy. Business justification: price_list_item.product_hierarchy_code is a STRING reference to the product hierarchy node for hierarchy-level pricing. Adding product_hierarchy_id FK normalizes this reference and enables hierarchy-b
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.price_list. Business justification: price_list_item is a line-level record within a price list. price_list_item.price_list_code is a STRING reference to the parent price_list. Adding price_list_id FK normalizes this parent-child relatio
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.tax_code. Business justification: Price list items require tax codes for correct sales tax calculation and compliance. Sales and finance teams use this for pricing, invoicing, and tax reporting.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: price_list_item.pricing_uom is a STRING code representing the unit of measure for pricing. The uom product is the authoritative master for all units of measure and conversion factors. Replacing pricin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or user ID of the pricing authority (e.g., pricing manager, commercial director) who approved this price list item. Supports pricing governance, delegation of authority compliance, and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Document Approval; SAP S/4HANA SD - Release Procedure

description: The date and time when this price list item was formally approved by the designated pricing authority. Used for pricing governance reporting and compliance with internal delegation of authority policies.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Document Approval; SAP S/4HANA SD - Release Procedure

description: Defines how the price or condition value is calculated: fixed_amount (absolute monetary value), percentage (proportion of a base amount), quantity_dependent (varies by ordered quantity), formula (custom calculation rule), or free_goods (zero-price promotional item).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Calculation Type (KONP-KRECH)

description: SAP SD pricing condition type code that classifies the nature of the pricing record (e.g., PR00 for base price, RA01 for discount, ZSU1 for surcharge). Governs how the price is applied during order pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Pricing Condition Type (T685)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country for which this price list item is applicable. Supports country-specific pricing to reflect local market conditions, taxes, and regulatory requirements.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD - Country Key

description: Indicates whether this price list item is available for use in Configure Price Quote (CPQ) processes (True) or restricted to direct order entry only (False). Controls which items appear in the CPQ pricing engine for complex product configurations.
reference: Salesforce CRM - CPQ Module; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: The date and time when this price list item record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and pricing governance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Condition Record Creation Date; ISO 9001 Records Management; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit price, floor price, RRP, and surcharge amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Supports multi-currency pricing for global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD - Condition Currency (KONP-WAERS)

description: Code identifying the customer group or customer classification for which this price list item is applicable (e.g., key accounts, standard customers, government, OEM partners). Enables segment-specific pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Customer Group (KDGRP)

description: Code identifying the distribution channel for which this price applies (e.g., direct sales, distributor, OEM, e-commerce). Enables channel-specific pricing strategies within the same sales organization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Distribution Channel (VTWEG)

description: The date from which this price list item becomes valid and applicable for order pricing. Prices with an effective date in the future are staged but not yet active. Aligns with SAP SD condition validity start date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Validity Start Date (KONH-DATAB); IFRS 15

description: The date after which this price list item is no longer valid for order pricing. Expired items are retained for historical reporting and audit purposes. Aligns with SAP SD condition validity end date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Validity End Date (KONH-DATBI); IFRS 15

description: The minimum permissible selling price for the SKU below which sales representatives cannot discount. Used by Configure Price Quote (CPQ) and order management to enforce pricing guardrails and protect margin.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Minimum Condition Value; IFRS 15

description: Indicates whether this price list item represents an intercompany transfer price applicable to transactions between legal entities within the same corporate group (True) or an external customer price (False). Relevant for transfer pricing compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Intercompany Billing (IV); OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines; IFRS

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the price list item as assigned in the source system (SAP SD condition record key). Used for cross-system referencing and audit traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Record Key

description: The date and time when this price list item record was most recently updated in the source system. Supports change detection in ETL pipelines and pricing audit trails.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Condition Record Change Date; ISO 9001 Records Management; GDPR Article 5

description: The minimum quantity a customer must order to qualify for this price list item. Enforced during order entry and CPQ to ensure pricing is only applied when the minimum purchase threshold is met.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Minimum Order Quantity; SAP MM - MRP Parameters

description: Indicates whether the unit price stated in this price list item is inclusive of applicable taxes (True) or exclusive of taxes (False). Critical for markets where gross pricing is standard (e.g., VAT-inclusive pricing in EU consumer channels).
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; GAAP; IFRS 15

description: Classification of the price record indicating its commercial role: list_price (standard catalog price), floor_price (minimum allowable selling price), rrp (Recommended Retail Price), transfer_price (intercompany), contract_price (customer-specific negotiated), or promotional_price (time-limited offer).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Pricing Procedure; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Code identifying the SAP SD pricing procedure under which this condition record is evaluated during order pricing (e.g., RVAA01 for standard sales). Determines the sequence and calculation logic for all pricing conditions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Pricing Procedure (T683)

description: The quantity denominator for the unit price (e.g., price per 100 units, price per 1000 kg). Allows pricing to be expressed per batch or bulk quantity rather than per single unit. Defaults to 1 for per-unit pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Pricing Unit (KONP-KPEIN)

description: Indicates whether this price list item is eligible for inclusion in rebate agreement calculations (True) or excluded (False). Used by the rebate management process to determine which sales transactions qualify for retrospective rebate accruals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Rebate Processing (VBOF); IFRS 15 Variable Consideration

description: The Recommended Retail Price (RRP) suggested to channel partners and distributors for resale to end customers. Used in distributor price lists and channel management to maintain market price consistency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Record; EU Price Indication Directive 98/6/EC

description: Code identifying the sales organization for which this price list item is applicable. Supports multi-entity and multi-region pricing structures in global operations (e.g., different prices for EMEA vs. Americas sales organizations).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Sales Organization (VKORG)

description: The dimension on which pricing scale breaks are evaluated: quantity (number of units), value (order value in currency), weight (total weight), or volume (total volume). Determines how tier thresholds are measured for volume pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Scale Basis (KONP-STFBA)

description: The lower bound quantity threshold for this pricing tier (e.g., 10 units). When the ordered quantity meets or exceeds this value, the corresponding tier price applies. Used to define volume-based pricing breaks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Scale Quantity From (KONP-KSTBM)

description: The upper bound quantity threshold for this pricing tier (e.g., 99 units). Defines the ceiling of the quantity band for this tier. Null or 999999999 indicates the tier applies to all quantities above the from-quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Scale Quantity To (KONP-KSTBM upper bound)

description: Defines the type of volume-based pricing scale applied to this item: graduated (tiered pricing where each tier applies to the quantity within that band), base_scale (the entire quantity is priced at the tier rate), group_scale (scale based on cumulative group quantity), or none (flat pricing with no scale).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Scale Type (KONP-STFKZ)

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this price list item was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for SD condition records, SALESFORCE_CPQ for CPQ-originated prices). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance - Data Lineage Standard; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: The natural key or composite key of this price list item as it exists in the source system (e.g., SAP condition record number + condition table key). Enables traceability back to the originating system record for reconciliation and audit.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance - Data Lineage Standard; SAP S/4HANA SD - Condition Record Number (KNUMH)

description: Current lifecycle status of the price list item: active (in use for order pricing), inactive (disabled), pending_approval (awaiting authorization), superseded (replaced by a newer version), expired (past validity end date), or draft (under preparation).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Status; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Absolute monetary surcharge applied on top of the unit price for this item (e.g., hazardous material handling surcharge, small order surcharge, freight surcharge). Negative values represent absolute discounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Supplement (KONP)

description: Percentage-based surcharge or discount applied to the unit price for this item. Positive values indicate a surcharge; negative values indicate a discount percentage. Used when the adjustment is proportional rather than fixed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Rate (KBETR) with percentage scale

description: Code indicating the tax classification applicable to this price list item (e.g., full tax, reduced tax, tax-exempt). Used in conjunction with customer tax classification to determine the applicable tax rate during order processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/SD - Tax Classification (MLAN); GAAP; IFRS

description: The base price per pricing unit of measure for the SKU as defined in this price list item. Represents the list price before any discounts or surcharges are applied. Stored with 6 decimal places to support high-precision industrial pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Amount (KBETR); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Sequential version number of this price list item record, incremented each time the item is revised or superseded. Supports price history tracking, audit trails, and rollback capabilities for pricing governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Condition Record Versioning; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each hazardous substance record in the product compliance catalog.
reference: Internal Primary Key

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Hazardous substance records track restricted and declarable substances present in specific catalog items for RoHS, REACH, and chemical compliance. Each substance record is scoped to a specific product
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Primary regulatory framework under which this substance is restricted or declarable (e.g., RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU, REACH SVHC, California Proposition 65, TSCA). Drives compliance obligations and documentation requirements.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; California Proposition 65; TSCA; EU CE Marking

description: Expiry date of the REACH authorization granted for use of a Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC). After this date, use of the substance is prohibited unless a new authorization is obtained.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Annex XIV; ECHA Authorization List

description: Authorization number granted by the European Chemicals Agency (ECHA) under REACH Annex XIV permitting the continued use of a Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) for a specific use case beyond the sunset date.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Annex XIV; ECHA Authorization List

description: Unique numerical identifier assigned by the Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) to uniquely identify a chemical substance. Used globally for substance identification across regulatory databases.
reference: CAS Registry; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; IPC-1752A

description: Current regulatory compliance status of the substance record for the associated catalog item. Drives product release decisions, customer material declarations, and corrective action workflows.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Measured or declared concentration of the hazardous substance in the catalog item or homogeneous material, expressed in parts per million (PPM) by weight. Used to assess compliance against regulatory threshold limits.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; IEC 62321; IPC-1752A

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country or market jurisdiction where this substance regulation applies. Supports multi-jurisdictional compliance management for global product distribution.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; China RoHS (SJ/T 11363); California Proposition 65

description: Timestamp when the hazardous substance record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Origin of the substance concentration data: laboratory test result, supplier-provided declaration, internal engineering analysis, third-party certification, or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS/SDS). Indicates data reliability and traceability.
reference: IPC-1752A; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ISO 9001:2015

description: Date on which the substance declaration or compliance assessment was formally recorded or submitted. Used for regulatory reporting timelines and document version control.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IPC-1752A

description: Type of compliance declaration associated with this substance record: exemption (substance use is permitted under a specific regulatory exemption), substitution (substance has been replaced), declaration of conformity (product conforms to limits), or disclosure (substance is declared for transparency).
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU Annex III; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 33; IPC-1752A

description: European Community (EC) number assigned by the European Chemicals Agency (ECHA) to substances listed in the EINECS, ELINCS, or NLP inventories. Required for REACH compliance documentation.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ECHA Substance Registry

description: Indicates whether the measured substance concentration (concentration_ppm) exceeds the regulatory threshold limit (threshold_limit_ppm). True signals a potential non-compliance condition requiring corrective action or exemption documentation.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Specific exemption clause or annex reference under the applicable regulation that permits the use of this substance above the threshold limit (e.g., RoHS Annex III exemption 6(c) for lead in glass of cathode ray tubes).
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU Annex III and IV; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Hazard classification of the substance under the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals (GHS), such as acute toxicity, carcinogenicity, reproductive toxicity, or environmental hazard. Drives SDS and labeling requirements.
reference: GHS (UN Globally Harmonized System); OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Substance classification per the IPC-1752A Material Declaration Standard, indicating the level of reporting obligation (Class A through F). Used for generating standardized customer material declarations in the electronics and industrial manufacturing supply chain.
reference: IPC-1752A Material Declaration Standard; IEC 62474 Material Declaration

business_glossary_term: International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) Name
description: Systematic chemical name of the substance as defined by the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) nomenclature standards, used for unambiguous chemical identification.
reference: IUPAC Nomenclature; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the hazardous substance record. Supports change tracking, regulatory audit trails, and data freshness monitoring.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Description of the specific material, component, or homogeneous material within the catalog item where the substance is present (e.g., solder alloy, PCB substrate, cable insulation, surface coating). Required for IPC-1752A material declarations.
reference: IPC-1752A Material Declaration Standard; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Analytical test method used to determine the substance concentration in the material or component (e.g., X-Ray Fluorescence (XRF), ICP-OES, GC-MS). Supports traceability and audit requirements under IEC 62321.
reference: IEC 62321 (Determination of Hazardous Substances in Electrotechnical Products); RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Free-text field for additional compliance context, engineering notes, or regulatory clarifications related to this substance record (e.g., specific use conditions, pending regulatory decisions, or supplier qualification notes).
reference: ISO 9001:2015; Internal Quality Management System (QMS)

description: RoHS or REACH product category or equipment category to which this substance restriction applies (e.g., RoHS Annex I Category 1 - Large Household Appliances, Category 9 - Industrial Monitoring and Control Equipment). Determines applicability of restrictions.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU Annex I; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Indicates whether the substance is listed under California Proposition 65 (Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act) as known to cause cancer, birth defects, or other reproductive harm. Triggers warning label requirements for products sold in California.
reference: California Proposition 65 (Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act of 1986); OEHHA

description: Version or revision of the regulatory list or candidate list under which the substance is classified (e.g., REACH SVHC Candidate List version, RoHS Annex II amendment number). Ensures traceability to the specific regulatory update.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Candidate List; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU Annex II

description: Scheduled date by which the substance compliance record must be reviewed for accuracy and regulatory currency. Triggered by regulatory list updates, product changes, or periodic review cycles.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Restricted Flag
description: Indicates whether the substance is restricted under the RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU (Restriction of Hazardous Substances). Restricted substances include lead, mercury, cadmium, hexavalent chromium, PBB, PBDE, and additional substances added under RoHS 3.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU Annex II; EU CE Marking

description: Reference number of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this hazardous substance, as required under OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) and REACH. The SDS provides detailed safety, handling, and disposal information.
reference: OSHA HazCom Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 31; GHS (Globally Harmonized System)

description: Chemical classification category of the hazardous substance used for grouping and risk prioritization in compliance programs (e.g., heavy metal, halogen, phthalate, SVHC).
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IPC-1752A

description: Technical function or role of the hazardous substance within the product or material (e.g., flame retardant, plasticizer, colorant, stabilizer). Supports substitution analysis and Design for Environment (DfE) initiatives.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; IPC-1752A; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Official chemical or common name of the hazardous substance as recognized by regulatory bodies (e.g., Lead, Cadmium, Hexavalent Chromium, Bisphenol A).
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; California Proposition 65

description: CAS number of the alternative substance used to replace this hazardous substance. Enables precise identification of the substitute chemical for regulatory and procurement purposes.
reference: CAS Registry; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Name of the alternative substance used to replace this hazardous substance when declaration_type is substitution. Supports Design for Environment (DfE) and green chemistry initiatives.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Annex XIV; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU Green Deal

description: Reference number or identifier of the supplier-provided material declaration document (e.g., IPC-1752A form reference, supplier SDS document number) supporting the substance data. Used for audit traceability.
reference: IPC-1752A Material Declaration Standard; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 33

business_glossary_term: Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) Flag
description: Indicates whether the substance is listed on the REACH Candidate List as a Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC). When true, triggers mandatory disclosure obligations under REACH Article 33 if concentration exceeds 0.1% w/w in articles.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 57 and 59; ECHA Candidate List

description: Reference number of the laboratory test report that documents the analytical measurement of the substance concentration. Required for regulatory audit trails and customer compliance documentation.
reference: IEC 62321; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; ISO/IEC 17025 (Laboratory Competence)

description: Name of the accredited laboratory that performed the substance concentration analysis. Supports audit traceability and verification of ISO/IEC 17025-accredited testing.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; IEC 62321; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Maximum allowable concentration of the substance in a homogeneous material as defined by the applicable regulation, expressed in parts per million (PPM). Compliance is determined by comparing concentration_ppm against this limit.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU Annex II; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 57; California Proposition 65

description: Start date from which this substance record is considered valid and applicable to the associated catalog item or component. Supports lifecycle management of compliance records.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Expiry date after which this substance record must be reviewed and renewed. Ensures compliance records are kept current with regulatory updates and product changes.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; ISO 9001:2015

description: Concentration of the hazardous substance expressed as a percentage by weight (w/w%) of the homogeneous material or article. Used for REACH SVHC disclosure threshold assessment (0.1% w/w trigger) and IPC-1752A declarations.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 33; IPC-1752A; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each regulatory certification record in the product compliance catalog.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Regulatory certifications have validity windows that must align to fiscal periods for compliance cost accrual and audit reporting. Finance uses this link to allocate certification maintenance costs to
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Regulatory certifications (CE, UL, RoHS, REACH) are issued for specific catalog items or product families. The hierarchy product is the SSOT for the commercial product catalog. A hierarchy_id FK links
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Pipe-separated list of EU directives, harmonized standards, or regulatory frameworks under which the certification was granted (e.g., Low Voltage Directive 2014/35/EU|EMC Directive 2014/30/EU|IEC 61010-1). Critical for CE Marking declarations of conformity.
reference: EU New Legislative Framework; IEC Standards; ANSI Standards; UL Standards

description: Pipe-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or market identifiers for which this certification grants market access (e.g., DEU|FRA|GBR|USA|CHN). Drives export control decisions and customer compliance documentation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU Regulation 765/2008; Export Administration Regulations (EAR)

description: Document management system reference or URL pointing to the scanned or digital copy of the official certificate. Enables direct retrieval of the certificate for customer compliance packages, audits, and export documentation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management

description: Official certificate number or reference code assigned by the issuing certification body (e.g., UL file number, CE declaration reference, RoHS certificate ID). Used for external verification and customer compliance documentation.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 – Conformity Assessment; EU Decision 768/2008/EC

description: Total cost incurred to obtain or renew this certification, including testing fees, certification body fees, and audit costs. Expressed in the currency specified by certification_cost_currency. Used for compliance budget tracking and OPEX reporting.
reference: IFRS IAS 38 – Intangible Assets; GAAP ASC 350 – Intangibles

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the certification cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Supports multi-currency financial reporting for global compliance operations.
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 – Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Primary technical standard or norm against which the product was tested and certified (e.g., IEC 61010-1:2010, UL 508A, EN 60947-1, IEC 62443-4-2). Provides the technical basis for the certification and is referenced in test reports and declarations of conformity.
reference: IEC Standards; ANSI/UL Standards; EN Harmonized Standards; ISO Standards

value_regex: CE Marking|UL Listing|RoHS Compliance|REACH Declaration|CSA Certification|CCC Certification|EAC Certification|UL Recognition|FCC Authorization|ATEX Certification|IECEx Certification|KC Certification|PSE Certification|BIS Certification|INMETRO Certification|Other
description: Classification of the regulatory certification or compliance approval. Identifies the specific scheme under which the product has been certified (e.g., CE Marking for EU market access, UL Listing for North American safety, CCC for China market, EAC for Eurasian Economic Union).
reference: EU Regulation 765/2008; UL Standards; IEC 62443; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing site where the certified product is produced. Relevant for country-of-origin declarations, export control, and some certification schemes that require manufacturing site registration.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO Rules of Origin; Export Administration Regulations (EAR)

description: Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the data product. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 – Data Integrity and Accountability

description: Document reference number or PLM system identifier for the manufacturer's Declaration of Conformity (DoC) associated with this certification. Required for CE Marking and other self-declaration schemes under EU New Legislative Framework.
reference: EU Decision 768/2008/EC Annex III; EU Regulation 765/2008; Directive 2014/35/EU (LVD)

description: The date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid for market access. Null if the certification has no defined expiry (e.g., some RoHS declarations). Used to trigger renewal workflows and prevent shipment of non-compliant products.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.8; EU Regulation 765/2008; UL Certification Maintenance Requirements

description: Indicates whether this certification has specific export control implications or restrictions (e.g., dual-use technology certifications, ITAR-controlled products). True triggers additional export compliance review before shipment to applicable markets.
reference: Export Administration Regulations (EAR) – 15 CFR Parts 730-774; ITAR – 22 CFR Parts 120-130; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821

description: Indicates the product's compliance declaration status with respect to hazardous substance restrictions (RoHS, REACH, California Prop 65). 'Full Compliance' means all restricted substances are below threshold; 'Exemption Applied' means one or more RoHS exemptions are in use.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; California Proposition 65

description: The date on which the certification was officially issued or granted by the issuing body. Marks the start of the certificate's validity period and is required for compliance documentation submitted to customers and regulators.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.8 – Certification Documents

description: Name of the regulatory authority, notified body, or accredited certification organization that issued the certificate (e.g., TÜV Rheinland, Bureau Veritas, Underwriters Laboratories, SGS, Intertek, DEKRA, China Quality Certification Centre).
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012; EU Regulation 765/2008 – Accreditation and Market Surveillance

description: Accreditation number of the notified body or certification organization as registered with the relevant national accreditation authority (e.g., NANDO notified body number for EU, ILAC member accreditation number). Enables verification of the certifier's authority.
reference: EU Regulation 765/2008; ILAC P15 – Application of ISO/IEC 17011; NANDO Database

description: ISO 639-1 language code of the certificate document and associated compliance documentation (e.g., en, de, zh-CN, fr). Relevant for multi-language compliance packages required in different markets.
reference: ISO 639-1 – Language Codes; EU Regulation 765/2008 – Language Requirements for Market Surveillance

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this certification record. Used for change detection, incremental data loading in the lakehouse pipeline, and compliance audit trails.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; Databricks Delta Lake – Change Data Capture

description: Date of the most recently completed surveillance audit or factory inspection. Used to verify compliance maintenance history and calculate the interval to the next required audit.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.9; UL Follow-Up Services Program

description: Internal code identifying the specific manufacturing plant or facility where the certified product is produced. Some certifications (e.g., UL, CCC) are site-specific and require re-certification if production moves to a different facility.
reference: UL Certification Requirements – Factory Identification; CCC Certification Factory Audit Requirements

description: Scheduled date of the next surveillance audit or periodic factory inspection required to maintain certification validity. Used for compliance calendar management and audit preparation planning.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.9; UL Follow-Up Services; TÜV Surveillance Audit Schedule

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) Flag
description: Indicates whether the product contains any Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) as defined under the EU REACH Regulation above the 0.1% w/w threshold. True triggers mandatory customer notification and supply chain communication obligations.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 33; ECHA SVHC Candidate List

description: The date on which the renewal application was submitted to the issuing body. Populated when status is 'Under Renewal'. Used to track renewal progress and ensure continuity of market access.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.10 – Changes Affecting Certification; UL Recertification Process

description: Name or employee ID of the internal compliance engineer or regulatory affairs manager responsible for maintaining this certification, managing renewals, and responding to customer compliance inquiries.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 – Organizational Roles, Responsibilities and Authorities

description: Business unit, division, or department responsible for managing this certification (e.g., Global Regulatory Affairs, Product Compliance Engineering, Quality Management). Used for organizational accountability and compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3; ISO 14001:2015 Clause 5.3

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Exemption Codes
description: Pipe-separated list of RoHS Annex III or Annex IV exemption codes applicable to this product (e.g., 6(a)|6(b)|7(a)). Documents the legal basis for any restricted substance usage above threshold limits. Required for RoHS compliance declarations.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU Annex III and Annex IV; EU Delegated Directive 2015/863

description: Detailed textual description of the products, product variants, or components covered by this certification. May include model numbers, voltage ranges, power ratings, or other technical parameters that define the certification boundary.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.7; EU Declaration of Conformity requirements

description: Indicates whether the certification applies to a single product (SKU), a product family, a product line, a platform, or an individual component. Determines the breadth of market access coverage provided by this certificate.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.7 – Certification Scope

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this certification record was sourced (e.g., SAP S/4HANA QM module, Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: DAMA DMBOK – Data Lineage; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture – Silver Layer

description: Primary key or unique identifier of this certification record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP QM certificate object number, Teamcenter PLM document ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational systems.
reference: DAMA DMBOK – Data Lineage and Traceability; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Version, edition, or amendment level of the certification standard applied (e.g., Ed. 3.0, Amendment 1, 2019 revision). Critical for tracking compliance against evolving standards and managing transition periods when new editions are published.
reference: IEC Directives Part 1; ISO/IEC Supplement – Procedures for the technical work

description: Current lifecycle status of the regulatory certification. 'Valid' indicates active market access; 'Expired' indicates lapsed certification; 'Under Renewal' indicates renewal in progress; 'Suspended' indicates temporary withdrawal by the issuing body; 'Withdrawn' indicates permanent revocation; 'Pending Initial' indicates first-time certification in progress.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.10 – Suspension, Withdrawal, and Reduction of Certification

description: Textual explanation for the current certification status, particularly when status is Suspended, Withdrawn, or Cancelled. Documents the root cause (e.g., product design change, non-conformance finding, voluntary withdrawal from market).
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.10; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 – Nonconformity and Corrective Action

description: Indicates whether this certification requires periodic surveillance audits or factory inspections by the issuing body to maintain validity. True triggers scheduling of surveillance visits in the compliance calendar.
reference: ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.9 – Surveillance; UL Follow-Up Services Program

description: Reference number of the test report or evaluation report issued by the testing laboratory that underpins this certification. Used to retrieve supporting technical evidence during audits, customer inquiries, or regulatory inspections.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 – Testing and Calibration Laboratories; ISO/IEC 17065:2012 Clause 7.4

description: Name of the accredited testing laboratory that conducted the product testing and issued the test report supporting this certification (e.g., TÜV SÜD Product Service, Intertek Testing Services, SGS Laboratories). May differ from the issuing body.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017; ILAC Mutual Recognition Arrangement (MRA)

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
