description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this territory assignment record originated, supporting data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Current operational status of the territory assignment record, controlling whether the assignment is in effect for routing, quota, and commission calculations.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Territory Management

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each individual line item within a service contract. Serves as the primary key for the service_contract_line entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Contract Item

description: Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: Service contract lines for recurring maintenance or SLA coverage are commercially executed under blanket orders in manufacturing. Contract management teams link each contract line to the blanket order
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.discount_structure. Business justification: Service contract lines for maintenance agreements carry negotiated discounts defined in the sales discount structure. Contract managers apply these discounts at line level during renewal or upsell, en
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_price_list. Business justification: Service contract lines are priced against a product price list to ensure consistent commercial terms. Contract management and finance teams use this link to validate pricing and apply correct rate str
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Service contract lines define coverage for specific product variants — maintenance contracts, extended warranties, and SLA agreements are scoped to exact product variants. Contract management teams mu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to service.sla_commitment. Business justification: Each contract line defines specific SLA obligations for covered equipment. The sla_tier, response_time_hours, and resolution_time_hours on contract_line are denormalized copies of values defined in sl
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Contractually committed equipment or system availability percentage for the covered asset on this line (e.g., 99.5%). Used for SLA compliance reporting and penalty/bonus calculations.
reference: ISO 9001 Service Level Agreement; IEC 62443 Availability Requirements

description: Frequency at which this contract line is invoiced to the customer (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annually, or one-time). Drives the billing plan and revenue recognition schedule.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Billing Plan; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Type of billing plan applied to this contract line, indicating whether billing is periodic (time-based), milestone-driven, on-demand, prepaid, or postpaid. Determines the invoicing logic in SAP SD.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Billing Plan Type

description: SAP cost center code associated with the delivery of services on this contract line. Used for cost allocation and margin analysis at the service delivery unit level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Cost Center Accounting; GAAP Cost Allocation

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the covered equipment is installed and service is to be delivered. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, tax rules, and service resource allocation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; CE Marking; RoHS/REACH Compliance

description: Date on which service coverage for this specific contract line expires. Used for renewal tracking, SLA enforcement, and billing period management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Item Validity End; ISO 9001 Contract Review

description: Service coverage window for this contract line, defining the hours and days during which the SLA obligations apply (e.g., 8x5 for 8 hours/5 days, 24x7 for round-the-clock coverage).
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Entitlement; ISO 9001 Service Availability

description: Date on which service coverage for this specific contract line becomes effective. May differ from the parent contract start date if coverage for individual assets is staggered.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Item Validity Start; ISO 9001 Contract Review

description: Serial number of the specific physical equipment or asset covered by this contract line. Links the service obligation to a unique installed asset in the field. Corresponds to the installed base serial number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Equipment Serial Number; Maximo EAM - Asset Serial Number

description: Timestamp indicating when this service contract line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and contract administration tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Document Creation; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Percentage discount applied to the unit price for this contract line, reflecting negotiated pricing agreements, volume discounts, or promotional terms. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Discount Condition; IFRS 15 Variable Consideration

description: Defines the escalation tier for service issues related to this contract line, determining the support team level (L1 through L4) that handles escalated cases. Aligns with the service delivery model.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Case Escalation; ISO 9001 Nonconformance Management

description: Reference identifier for the installed base record associated with this contract line. Used when coverage applies to an installed base entry rather than a single serialized asset, supporting multi-unit or fleet coverage scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/PM - Installed Base; Salesforce Service Cloud - Installed Product

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this service contract line record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data loading in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Change Document; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Free-text description of the service scope, covered equipment, or service bundle for this specific contract line item. Provides human-readable context for the contracted service.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Item Description

description: Sequential line item number within the parent service contract, used to identify and order individual line items. Typically increments by 10 (e.g., 10, 20, 30) following SAP SD conventions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Item Number

description: Total monetary value of this contract line, calculated as net price multiplied by quantity. Represents the contracted revenue for this specific line item over the coverage period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Item Net Value; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Unit price after application of the discount percentage for this contract line. Represents the effective per-unit price used for billing and revenue recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Net Price; IFRS 15 Transaction Price

description: Indicates whether financial penalties apply to this contract line if SLA targets (response time, resolution time, availability) are not met. Triggers penalty calculation workflows upon SLA breach.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Contract Terms; ISO 9001 Customer Satisfaction

description: Percentage of the line value that is deducted as a financial penalty per SLA breach event or per period of non-compliance. Only applicable when penalty_clause_applicable is true.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Contract Penalty; IFRS 15 Variable Consideration

description: Number of scheduled preventive maintenance visits included in this contract line per year. Drives field service scheduling and resource planning for the covered asset.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Maintenance Plan; Maximo EAM - Preventive Maintenance

description: Material or product number identifying the service product, spare part bundle, or equipment model covered by this contract line. Aligns with the product catalog in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Number

description: SAP profit center code to which the revenue from this contract line is attributed. Enables contract profitability analysis at the organizational unit level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Profit Center Accounting; IFRS Segment Reporting

description: Number of units, assets, or service instances covered by this contract line. For example, a line covering 5 identical machines would have a quantity of 5. Used in conjunction with unit price to calculate line value.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Item Quantity

description: Indicates whether remote monitoring and diagnostics via IIoT connectivity (e.g., Siemens MindSphere) is included in the scope of this contract line. Enables proactive fault detection and predictive maintenance.
reference: Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform - Asset Monitoring; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity

description: High-level category grouping for the service line, aligning with the company's service portfolio structure (e.g., hardware maintenance, software support, electrification services). Used for revenue reporting and portfolio analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM Service Cloud - Service Category

description: Classification of the service being contracted on this line item, such as preventive maintenance, corrective maintenance, remote monitoring, or full-service coverage. Determines the nature of service obligations and resource requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Maintenance Type; Salesforce Service Cloud - Service Type

description: Indicates whether firmware and software updates for the covered equipment are included in the scope of this contract line. Relevant for PLC, SCADA, HMI, and automation system contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Service Scope; IEC 61131 PLC Programming Standards

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this contract line record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Data Provenance; Enterprise Data Governance

description: Indicates whether spare parts and replacement components are included in the scope of this contract line at no additional charge. Affects cost-to-serve calculations and parts inventory planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Service Scope; Maximo EAM - MRO

description: Current lifecycle status of the service contract line item, indicating whether the line is active, suspended, cancelled, or in another state. Drives billing eligibility and SLA enforcement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Item Status; ISO 9001 Contract Review

description: Tax classification code applied to this contract line for VAT, GST, or sales tax calculation purposes. Determined by the service type, customer tax status, and jurisdiction of service delivery.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module - Tax Code; IFRS; GAAP

description: Unit of measure applicable to the quantity on this contract line (e.g., EA for each unit, HR for hours of service, MON for months of coverage). Follows ISO 80000 and SAP UOM standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Unit of Measure; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Price per unit of service or covered asset for this contract line, expressed in the contract currency. Forms the basis for line value calculation before discounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Price; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each SLA commitment record. Serves as the primary key for the sla_commitment data product.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5 - Production and Service Provision

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code specifying the country to which this SLA commitment applies when geographic_scope is set to 'country'. Supports country-specific regulatory and operational SLA variations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 - Country Codes; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: The product or equipment category to which this SLA commitment applies, such as automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure components, or MRO spare parts. Allows SLA commitments to be scoped to specific product lines within the installed base.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Siemens Opcenter MES Product Classification

description: The type of service request or work order category to which this SLA commitment applies. Enables different SLA targets for different service activities (e.g., emergency breakdown response vs. scheduled preventive maintenance).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Maximo EAM Work Order Classification

description: The time window over which breach counts are accumulated for the purpose of evaluating max_breach_count_before_termination and penalty caps. Defines the rolling or calendar period for breach tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; Salesforce Service Cloud

description: The numeric value at which the SLA is considered breached. For time-based SLAs, this is the maximum allowable duration before a breach is triggered. For rate-based SLAs, this is the minimum acceptable performance level below which a breach is declared.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 - Nonconformity and Corrective Action; Salesforce Service Cloud

description: Categorizes the SLA commitment by its origin and enforceability. Contractual SLAs are legally binding per customer agreements; Operational SLAs are internal performance targets; Regulatory SLAs are mandated by industry or government standards; Internal SLAs govern cross-functional service delivery.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 - Control of Externally Provided Processes

description: Short alphanumeric business code uniquely identifying the SLA commitment for use in contracts, service orders, and system integrations (e.g., SLA-GOLD-RSP-4H).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: Defines the operational coverage window within which the SLA commitment is measured and enforced. 24x7 means the SLA clock runs continuously; Business Hours means only standard working hours count; Extended Hours covers evenings and weekends; Custom requires reference to a defined schedule.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; Salesforce Service Cloud Business Hours

description: The IANA timezone identifier used as the reference timezone for measuring SLA coverage hours and breach calculations (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Singapore). Critical for multinational operations where service delivery spans multiple time zones.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; ISO 8601

description: The date and time when this SLA commitment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to monetary penalty values (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Supports multi-currency operations across global service contracts.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes; IFRS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The customer segment or tier to which this SLA commitment applies. Enables differentiated service levels based on customer value, contract type, or strategic importance. Platinum and Gold tiers typically receive more aggressive response and resolution commitments.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2 - Requirements for Products and Services; Salesforce CRM Customer Segmentation

description: Full narrative description of the SLA commitment, including its business purpose, scope, conditions, and any special terms or exclusions. Provides the human-readable context for the structured SLA parameters.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: The date from which this SLA commitment version becomes active and enforceable. Used to determine which SLA version applies to a given service event based on the event date.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Unit of measure for the escalation_trigger_value, defining how the escalation threshold is quantified (e.g., hours elapsed, percent of target consumed).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Salesforce Service Cloud

description: The numeric threshold at which an escalation action is automatically triggered, typically set before the breach threshold to provide a warning window. For example, escalate at 3 hours for a 4-hour response SLA. Enables proactive intervention before a formal breach occurs.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Escalation Rules

description: Describes conditions under which the SLA commitment is suspended or excluded from measurement, such as force majeure events, customer-caused delays, scheduled maintenance windows, or third-party infrastructure outages. Critical for accurate SLA compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement Management

description: The date on which this SLA commitment version ceases to be active. After this date, the commitment is no longer enforceable unless renewed or superseded by a new version.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; IFRS 15

description: Defines the geographic applicability of this SLA commitment. Global applies uniformly across all regions; Regional applies to a specific geographic region; Country applies to a specific country; Site-Specific applies only to a defined customer installation site.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1 - Understanding the Organization and Its Context

description: Indicates whether this SLA commitment is mandated by a regulatory body or government authority (e.g., OSHA safety response requirements, EPA environmental incident response). Regulatory-mandated SLAs may carry legal penalties beyond contractual penalties.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 45001:2018 - Occupational Health and Safety; EPA Regulations

description: The date and time when this SLA commitment record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Maximum Breach Count Before Termination Right
description: The maximum number of SLA breaches within a defined period before the customer is contractually entitled to terminate the service contract. Provides a quantitative threshold for contract termination rights.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; IFRS 15

description: Defines the business event that starts the SLA clock for measurement purposes. Case Creation starts the timer when the service request is logged; Case Assignment starts when a technician is assigned; Customer Notification starts when the customer is informed; Engineer Dispatch starts when a field engineer is dispatched.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement Management

description: Defines the business event that stops the SLA clock for measurement purposes. Case Resolved stops the timer when the service case is closed; Customer Confirmed stops when the customer acknowledges resolution; System Restored stops when the equipment is returned to operational status.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement Management

description: Unit of measure for the target_metric_value. Defines how the SLA performance target is quantified (e.g., hours for response/resolution time, percent for uptime or first-time fix rate, count for parts availability).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; ANSI/ISO SI Units

description: Descriptive business name for the SLA commitment, such as 'Gold Tier 4-Hour Response SLA' or 'Critical Equipment Uptime Guarantee'. Used for display and reporting purposes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1 - Control of Production and Service Provision

description: The scheduled date for the next formal review of this SLA commitment. Triggers review workflows and ensures SLA definitions are kept current with operational and contractual changes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.3 - Management Review

description: The time period over which the penalty_cap_value is applied and reset. Defines the accumulation window for penalty calculations (e.g., monthly cap resets at the start of each calendar month).
reference: IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: The maximum cumulative penalty amount or percentage that can be applied within a defined period (typically per month or per contract year). Protects against unlimited liability exposure from repeated SLA breaches. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.
reference: IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers; GAAP ASC 606

description: Defines the type of penalty or remedy applicable when this SLA commitment is breached. Service Credit provides a credit against future invoices; Financial Penalty imposes a monetary charge; Contract Termination Right grants the customer the right to exit the contract; None indicates no contractual penalty applies.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Defines how the penalty_value is applied and measured. Percent of Monthly Fee applies the penalty as a percentage of the monthly service fee; Fixed Amount applies a flat monetary penalty; Percent Per Hour Breach applies a compounding penalty for each hour of breach duration.
reference: IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: The numeric value of the penalty applied upon SLA breach. For financial penalties and service credits, this represents the monetary amount or percentage of contract value. Interpreted in conjunction with penalty_unit. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.
reference: IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Priority classification associated with this SLA commitment, indicating the urgency and business impact level for which this commitment applies. Critical typically maps to production-down or safety-critical scenarios; High to significant operational impact; Medium to moderate disruption; Low to minor or cosmetic issues.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Salesforce Service Cloud Priority Management

description: The specific regulatory standard, directive, or legal requirement that mandates this SLA commitment when is_regulatory_mandated is true (e.g., ISO 45001 Clause 8.2, OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147, EU Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC).
reference: ISO 45001:2018; OSHA 29 CFR; EU Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC; IEC 62443

description: Defines how frequently this SLA commitment is scheduled for review and potential revision. Supports continuous improvement processes and ensures SLA targets remain aligned with operational capabilities and customer expectations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.3 - Management Review; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.3 - Continual Improvement

description: Specifies the mode of service delivery to which this SLA commitment applies. On-Site requires a field engineer dispatch; Remote covers telephone, email, or digital support; Hybrid combines remote and on-site; Parts Only covers spare part delivery without labor.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Salesforce Field Service

description: Current lifecycle status of the SLA commitment record. Draft indicates the commitment is being defined; Active indicates it is in use; Deprecated indicates it has been superseded; Retired indicates it is no longer applicable; Under Review indicates it is being assessed for update.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: The numeric performance target defined for this SLA commitment. For example, 4 (hours) for a response time SLA, 99.5 (percent) for an uptime guarantee, or 85 (percent) for a first-time fix rate. Interpreted in conjunction with measurement_unit.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1 - Monitoring, Measurement, Analysis and Evaluation

description: Classifies the nature of the SLA performance commitment. Response Time defines the maximum time to acknowledge a service request; Resolution Time defines the maximum time to fully resolve an issue; Uptime Guarantee defines minimum system availability; First-Time Fix Rate (FTFR) defines the percentage of issues resolved on first visit; Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) defines average repair duration; Parts Availability defines spare part delivery commitment.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Salesforce Service Cloud SLA Management

description: Version identifier for the SLA commitment definition, following semantic versioning conventions (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Enables tracking of changes to SLA terms over time and ensures historical service performance is measured against the correct version.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; ISO 10007 - Configuration Management

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each SLA tracking record. Serves as the primary key for the sla_tracking data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: Employee identifier of the field service technician or support engineer currently assigned to resolve the service request or field service order. Used for individual-level SLA performance tracking.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Resource Assignment; Kronos Workforce Central

description: Foreign key linking to service.dispatch_schedule. Business justification: SLA tracking records monitor compliance for field service orders that are executed via dispatch schedules. Linking sla_tracking.dispatch_schedule_id → dispatch_schedule.dispatch_schedule_id connects t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.measurement_reading. Business justification: SLA tracking for uptime or performance-based contracts relies on actual measurement readings from the asset. Operations teams use sensor readings as objective evidence to validate or dispute SLA compl
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to service.service_request. Business justification: sla_tracking.service_request_number (STRING) is a denormalized reference to service_request.case_number. SLA tracking records monitor real-time SLA compliance for service requests. Adding service_requ
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to service.sla_commitment. Business justification: sla_tracking.sla_agreement_number (STRING) is a denormalized reference to sla_commitment. SLA tracking records monitor compliance against specific SLA commitment definitions. Adding sla_commitment_id
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The actual date and time at which the service issue was fully resolved and the case or field service order was closed. Null if resolution has not yet occurred. Used to determine resolution SLA compliance.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Completion Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: The actual date and time at which the service team provided the initial response or acknowledgement to the customer. Null if the response has not yet been made. Used to determine response SLA compliance.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Completion Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: Name or code of the service team or queue currently responsible for resolving the service request or field service order. Used for workload analysis and team-level SLA performance reporting.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Queue Assignment; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.2

description: The number of minutes by which the SLA target was exceeded at the time of resolution or as of the current timestamp for open breaches. Zero or null for records that have not breached. Used for breach severity analysis and penalty calculations.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Breach Duration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Standardized reason code explaining why the SLA was breached. Used for root cause analysis, CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) workflows, and service improvement initiatives.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Case Closure Code; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA Process

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context for the SLA breach beyond the standardized breach reason code. Captures specific circumstances, contributing factors, and corrective actions taken.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA Process

description: Name or code of the business hours calendar applied to this SLA tracking record. Determines which hours count toward SLA elapsed time (e.g., 24x7, 8x5, regional business hours). Critical for multinational operations with varying time zones.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Business Hours; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the customer site or service location where the service request or field service order applies. Used for regional SLA compliance reporting and regulatory alignment.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 3 (territorial scope)

description: The date and time at which this SLA tracking record was created in the source system. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the penalty amount. Supports multi-currency operations across global service contracts.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 15

description: The escalation tier reached for this SLA tracking record. Level 1 indicates team lead escalation; Level 2 indicates service manager escalation; Level 3 indicates regional director escalation; Executive indicates C-level or key account escalation.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Escalation Rules; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: The date and time at which the SLA tracking record was escalated. Null if no escalation has occurred. Used to measure escalation response time and management intervention effectiveness.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Case Escalation Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Reference identifier of the installed base asset (automation or electrification product) for which the service request or field service order was raised. Enables SLA performance analysis by product line and asset type.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Asset Object; Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform

description: Indicates whether this SLA tracking record has been escalated to a higher support tier, management, or the customer success team due to at-risk or breached status. True when escalation has been triggered.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Case Escalation; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: The date and time at which this SLA tracking record was last updated in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether an at-risk or breach warning notification has been sent to the assigned team, service manager, or customer for this SLA tracking record. Used to prevent duplicate notifications and track proactive communication.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Notification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.4

description: Total accumulated minutes for which the SLA clock was paused for this tracking record. Used to adjust effective SLA elapsed time and ensure fair compliance measurement when delays are customer-attributable.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Pause Duration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Reason for pausing the SLA clock. Used to distinguish customer-attributable delays from service team delays in SLA compliance reporting.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Pause Reason; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The date and time at which the SLA clock was paused, typically due to awaiting customer response, pending parts delivery, or other customer-side dependencies. Null if the SLA clock has not been paused.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Pause; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The financial penalty or service credit amount applicable for the SLA breach, expressed in the contract currency. Populated only when penalty_applicable is true and the breach has been confirmed. Used for financial reporting and customer credit processing.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Service Contract; IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers; GAAP ASC 606

description: Indicates whether a financial penalty or service credit is contractually applicable for a breach of this SLA commitment. Determined by the terms of the governing service contract.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Service Contract Terms; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Priority level assigned to the service request or field service order, which influences the applicable SLA targets. Critical priority cases carry the most stringent SLA commitments.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Case Priority; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this SLA tracking record originated. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit purposes in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud; SAP S/4HANA; NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Data Provenance

description: Internal business region code (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER) for the customer site or service location. Used for regional SLA performance aggregation and management reporting.
reference: Internal Business Hierarchy; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: The committed deadline by which the service issue must be fully resolved and the case or field service order closed. Calculated from the SLA start timestamp plus the contracted resolution time target.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Target Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Specific compliance status for the resolution SLA milestone. Indicates whether the service issue was resolved within the committed resolution target time.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: The contracted SLA target for full resolution, expressed in minutes. Derived from the applicable SLA tier and priority combination. Used as the baseline for resolution compliance measurement.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Entitlement Process; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: The committed deadline by which the service team must provide an initial response or acknowledgement to the customer. Calculated from the SLA start timestamp plus the contracted response time target.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Target Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Specific compliance status for the response SLA milestone. Indicates whether the initial response was provided within the committed response target time.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: The contracted SLA target for initial response, expressed in minutes. Derived from the applicable SLA tier and priority combination. Used as the baseline for response compliance measurement.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Entitlement Process; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Category of service associated with the tracked request or order. Used to segment SLA performance by service type and align with service contract entitlements.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Case Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The exact date and time at which the SLA clock started for this tracking record. Typically corresponds to the moment the service request was created or the field service order was dispatched, after any applicable business hours calendar adjustments.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Milestone Start Time; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Service tier classification that determines the SLA target thresholds applicable to this tracking record. Higher tiers (e.g., Platinum, Gold) carry stricter response and resolution time commitments.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud - Entitlement Process; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Classification of the SLA commitment being tracked. Distinguishes between response SLAs (time to acknowledge), resolution SLAs (time to close), on-site arrival SLAs (field dispatch), and other commitment types.

description: Current real-time SLA compliance status for this tracking record. description: Human-readable, business-facing unique identifier for the service report, used in customer communications, billing, and warranty claim substantiation. Typically formatted as SR-YYYY-NNNNNN.

description: Total cost of all spare parts and materials consumed during the service visit, in the transaction currency. Used for service billing, warranty cost tracking, and COGS reporting.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Cost Accounting; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: Narrative listing of all spare parts replaced during the service visit, including part numbers, descriptions, and quantities. Used for inventory reconciliation, warranty claim substantiation, and billing.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2 - Traceability; SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor WMS

description: Technician's recommendations for follow-up work, preventive maintenance actions, spare parts to be stocked, or equipment upgrades identified during the service visit. Drives future service order creation and proactive maintenance planning.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.3 - Continual Improvement; Maximo EAM Preventive Maintenance

description: Standardized code indicating the type of resolution applied during the service visit. Used for service analytics, spare parts consumption reporting, and billing classification.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Salesforce Field Service; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: Standardized code categorizing the root cause of the equipment failure or service need. Used for CAPA initiation, warranty chargeback decisions, and quality trend analysis.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA Process; PFMEA Methodology

description: Technician's documented observations regarding safety conditions at the customer site or on the equipment, including hazards identified, PPE used, and any safety incidents or near-misses during the service visit.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 - Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; EPA Regulations

description: Current lifecycle status of the service report, from initial draft by the technician through customer signature, internal approval, and final archival. Drives billing eligibility and warranty claim processing.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5 - Production and Service Provision; Salesforce Field Service

description: Precise date and time when the technician formally submitted the completed service report, including timezone offset. Used for SLA resolution time calculation and audit trail.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Salesforce Field Service

description: Professional certification or qualification number of the technician performing the service, demonstrating competency for the specific equipment type. Required for regulatory compliance and warranty validity.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.2 - Competence; UL Certification Requirements

description: Overall outcome of post-service functional testing. A 'pass' result is required for equipment release to operation and warranty validation. 'Fail' or 'conditional_pass' triggers follow-up action.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; IEC 62443 - Industrial Cybersecurity Testing

description: Narrative description of all functional tests, measurements, and performance verifications conducted on the equipment after service completion. Includes pass/fail outcomes and measured values. Required for commissioning sign-off and warranty validation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 - Release of Products and Services; IEC 61131

description: Number of hours spent by the technician traveling to and from the customer site. Used for billing (if billable travel is included in the service contract), cost allocation, and territory optimization.
reference: Salesforce Field Service; SAP S/4HANA SD - Service Billing

description: Classification of the service activity documented in this report. Determines applicable billing rates, warranty eligibility, and regulatory documentation requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Maintenance Order Types

description: Detailed narrative of all work performed by the technician during the service visit, including diagnostic steps, repairs, adjustments, and tests conducted. Serves as the official record of service delivery for warranty substantiation and customer proof of service.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; Salesforce Field Service

description: Unique system-generated identifier for a geographic service territory used in field service dispatch and technician assignment.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory Object

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, service territories are geographically aligned with sales territories so field service and sales reps coordinate on the same accounts. Operations and territory managers us
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Service territories in industrial manufacturing are often aligned to customer segments (e.g., automotive, energy, discrete manufacturing). Territory managers use this link to assign specialized techni
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Current number of active field service technicians assigned to this territory, used for capacity planning, workload balancing, and dispatch optimization.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory Member; Kronos Workforce Central

description: Total geographic area of the service territory measured in square kilometers, used for workload density analysis, technician travel time estimation, and territory rebalancing.
reference: ISO 80000-3; Salesforce Field Service

description: Primary city or metropolitan area associated with the service territory, used for dispatch routing and technician proximity matching.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the service territory, used in dispatch systems, reporting, and routing logic (e.g., EMEA-DE-SOUTH, NA-MW-01).
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory Code; ISO 3166

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country in which this service territory operates (e.g., DEU, USA, CHN).
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3

description: Comma-separated list of days of the week on which field service coverage is provided in this territory (e.g., MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI). Supports SLA scheduling and dispatch availability checks.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Operating Hours; ISO 8601

description: Daily end time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) of standard field service coverage for this territory, used in SLA scheduling and dispatch window enforcement.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Operating Hours; ISO 8601

description: Daily start time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) of standard field service coverage for this territory, used in SLA scheduling and dispatch window enforcement.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Operating Hours; ISO 8601

description: Timestamp when the service territory record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the primary currency used in service billing and cost tracking within this territory (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; GAAP

description: Detailed narrative description of the service territory including geographic scope, special coverage notes, and operational context.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory

description: Numeric priority ranking used by the dispatch engine to sequence territory-based assignments when multiple territories overlap or compete for the same technician resource. Lower values indicate higher priority.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Scheduling Policy

description: Date on which this service territory definition became or becomes operationally effective. Used for territory versioning, historical analysis, and transition planning.
reference: ISO 8601; Salesforce Field Service

description: Date on which this service territory definition expires or is decommissioned. Null indicates an indefinitely active territory. Used for territory lifecycle management and transition planning.
reference: ISO 8601; Salesforce Field Service

description: Numeric level of this territory within the service territory hierarchy (e.g., 1=Global, 2=Region, 3=Country, 4=State, 5=City). Supports hierarchical rollup in analytics and reporting.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Territory Hierarchy

description: Indicates whether this service territory provides round-the-clock, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week field service coverage. Relevant for critical infrastructure and high-priority SLA contracts.
reference: Salesforce Field Service; ISO 9001 - Service Level Requirements

description: IETF BCP 47 language tag representing the primary language used for field service communications, work orders, and customer interactions in this territory (e.g., de-DE, en-US, zh-CN).
reference: IETF BCP 47; ISO 639-1; Salesforce Field Service

description: Date on which the territory boundaries, technician assignments, and coverage parameters were last formally reviewed and validated by the territory manager or service operations team.
reference: ISO 9001 - Continual Improvement; Salesforce Field Service

description: Latitude coordinate of the geographic centroid of the service territory, used for map visualization, proximity calculations, and optimal dispatch routing.
reference: ISO 6709; Salesforce Field Service Geolocation

description: Longitude coordinate of the geographic centroid of the service territory, used for map visualization, proximity calculations, and optimal dispatch routing.
reference: ISO 6709; Salesforce Field Service Geolocation

description: Maximum number of technicians this territory is designed to support based on geographic size, workload projections, and service center capacity. Used for workforce planning and territory expansion decisions.
reference: Salesforce Field Service; Kronos Workforce Central

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the service territory record, used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; Salesforce Field Service

description: Human-readable name of the service territory used in scheduling interfaces, reports, and customer-facing communications (e.g., Germany South, US Midwest Region).
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory Name

description: Ending postal code defining the upper bound of the postal code range covered by this service territory, used in conjunction with postal_code_start for boundary definition.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Territory Boundaries; Universal Postal Union (UPU)

description: Starting postal code defining the lower bound of the postal code range covered by this service territory, used for geographic boundary enforcement in dispatch.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Territory Boundaries; Universal Postal Union (UPU)

description: High-level geographic region grouping for the territory used in global reporting and capacity planning (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER, DACH).
reference: ISO 3166-2; Salesforce Field Service

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the service center assigned to this territory, used for cross-system integration with SAP S/4HANA and Maximo EAM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM; Maximo EAM; Salesforce Field Service

description: Name of the primary service center or depot responsible for dispatching technicians and managing spare parts inventory for this territory.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory; Maximo EAM

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Resolution Time Hours
description: Standard SLA resolution time commitment in hours for completing field service repairs within this territory. Used for SLA compliance reporting and customer contract management.
reference: ISO 9001 - Customer Requirements; Salesforce Field Service SLA; SAP S/4HANA SD

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Response Time Hours
description: Standard SLA response time commitment in hours for initial field service response within this territory. Drives dispatch prioritization and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: ISO 9001 - Customer Requirements; Salesforce Field Service SLA; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this service territory record originated, supporting data lineage and integration traceability in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Data Provenance; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: State, province, or administrative subdivision within the country covered by this service territory (e.g., Bavaria, California, Ontario).
Current operational status of the service territory indicating whether it is actively used for dispatch and scheduling.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory

description: IANA time zone identifier for the service territory (e.g., Europe/Berlin, America/Chicago), used for scheduling, SLA time calculations, and coverage hour enforcement.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; Salesforce Field Service

description: Maximum acceptable one-way travel time in minutes for a technician dispatched from the service center to a customer site within this territory. Used in dispatch optimization and SLA feasibility checks.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Scheduling Optimization; ISO 9001

description: Classification of the territory indicating its role in the field service network. Primary territories are standard assignments; secondary and overflow handle spillover; temporary territories support project-based deployments.
reference: Salesforce Field Service - Service Territory Type

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the field service technician or service engineer record within the service domain. Serves as the primary key for dispatch, work order assignment, and competency tracking.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Service Resource object

description: Foreign key linking to service.service_territory. Business justification: Technicians are assigned to specific service territories for field service dispatch. The service_territory STRING on technician is a denormalized reference to the service_territory master record. Addi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Current number of open or in-progress work orders assigned to the technician. Used by the dispatch scheduling engine to assess workload capacity before assigning new work orders. Updated in near-real-time from the field service management system.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Work Order; Maximo EAM Work Order Management; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: Real-time operational availability of the technician for new dispatch assignments. Distinct from employment status — a technician may be 'active' but currently 'dispatched' on an existing work order. Used by the field service scheduling engine for optimal dispatch.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Service Resource Availability; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: City of the technician's primary base location. Used for geographic dispatch optimization, travel time calculations, and regional reporting.
reference: Salesforce Field Service; ISO 3166-2

description: Name of the technician's primary base location or service depot (e.g., 'Chicago Service Center', 'Frankfurt Depot', 'Singapore Hub'). Represents the physical facility from which the technician is dispatched and to which they return after field assignments.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Service Resource Base Location; Maximo EAM

description: State or province of the technician's primary base location. Used for regional dispatch grouping, labor law compliance determination, and territory management.
reference: ISO 3166-2; Salesforce Field Service

description: SAP cost center code to which the technician's labor costs are allocated. Used for financial controlling, service profitability reporting, and OPEX tracking by service region or product line.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling); IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the technician's primary base of operations. Used for regulatory compliance (work permits, labor law), currency determination for expense reporting, and international dispatch authorization.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR; local labor law compliance

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this technician record was sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when records are updated from multiple upstream systems.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Data Governance Best Practices

description: Expiration date of the technician's electrical safety certification. Dispatch systems must validate this date before assigning electrical work orders. Triggers recertification workflow when within 30 days of expiry.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.333; NFPA 70E; ISO 45001

description: Indicates whether the technician holds a current electrical safety certification required for work on live electrical panels, switchgear, and high-voltage automation equipment. Mandatory for compliance with OSHA 29 CFR 1910.333 and NFPA 70E. Blocks assignment to electrical work orders if false.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.333; NFPA 70E; ISO 45001; IEC 62443

description: Date on which the technician was hired or onboarded into the service organization. Used to calculate tenure, determine eligibility for advanced certifications, and support workforce analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HCM; Kronos Workforce Central; local labor law

description: Indicates whether the technician is certified and authorized to perform new product installation and commissioning at customer sites. Installation work requires completion of product-specific commissioning training and safety certifications.
reference: Salesforce Field Service; ISO 9001 Clause 7.2; IEC 61131; CE Marking Installation Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the technician's standard labor rate (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency service order costing in multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS

description: Pipe-delimited list of ISO 639-1 language codes representing languages the technician can communicate in (e.g., 'en|de|fr|zh'). Used for customer-facing dispatch matching in multinational operations to ensure effective on-site communication.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; Salesforce Field Service; GDPR multilingual compliance

description: Date of the most recent formal review of the technician's skill certifications and competency records. Used to trigger periodic recertification workflows and ensure compliance with ISO 9001 competence management requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.2 Competence; Salesforce Field Service Skills Management

description: Maximum number of work orders the technician is authorized to hold simultaneously, based on role, seniority, and service agreement requirements. Used as a capacity constraint in the dispatch scheduling algorithm.
reference: Salesforce Field Service Scheduling; SLA Management

description: Date by which the technician must complete their next certification renewal or competency assessment. Drives automated recertification reminders and dispatch eligibility checks for regulated work types.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.2; ISO 45001; OSHA Training Requirements

description: Indicates whether the technician is eligible for overtime compensation under applicable labor law and employment contract terms. Used by scheduling to determine availability for after-hours and weekend emergency dispatch.
reference: Kronos Workforce Central; FLSA (Fair Labor Standards Act); EU Working Time Directive; local labor law

description: Pipe-delimited list of product lines the technician is qualified to service (e.g., 'drives|PLCs|HMI|switchgear|motors|robotics'). Derived from completed product-specific training and certification programs. Used by dispatch to match technician competency to the product requiring service.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Skills; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001 Clause 7.2

description: Timestamp when the technician record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and incremental load processing.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the technician record in the Silver Layer. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: Indicates whether the technician is equipped and authorized to provide remote technical support via digital tools (e.g., Siemens MindSphere, remote desktop, AR-assisted support). Enables routing of remote support cases to qualified technicians without physical dispatch.
reference: Salesforce Service Cloud; Siemens MindSphere IIoT Platform; IEC 62443 Remote Access Security

description: Experience and seniority classification of the technician within the service organization. Used for work order complexity routing, mentoring assignments, and labor cost tier determination.
reference: Salesforce Field Service; ISO 9001 Clause 7.2 Competence

description: Indicates whether the technician is authorized to perform work under contracted service agreements (SLAs). Technicians without this flag may only be assigned to time-and-material or warranty work orders, not to SLA-governed service contracts.
reference: Salesforce Field Service; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5 Service Delivery

description: Date on which the technician was activated in the service domain system (Salesforce Field Service). May differ from hire_date if the technician transferred from another department or was onboarded to field service after initial employment.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Service Resource Activation Date

description: Standard shift pattern assigned to the technician, defining their regular working hours for scheduling and dispatch planning. 'on_call' indicates availability outside standard hours for emergency response.
reference: Kronos Workforce Central; SAP S/4HANA HR; EU Working Time Directive

description: Pipe-delimited list of technical skill certifications held by the technician (e.g., 'PLC_programming|drive_commissioning|HMI_configuration|electrical_safety|SCADA_configuration|CNC_maintenance'). Each certification corresponds to a validated competency assessment. Used for skill-based dispatch routing.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Skills; IEC 61131 PLC Programming; ISO 45001 Electrical Safety

description: Standard hourly billing rate for the technician's labor, used for service order costing, customer invoice generation, and profitability analysis. Expressed in the technician's base currency. Classified as confidential business data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP

description: Current employment and service-domain status of the technician. Controls eligibility for dispatch scheduling and work order assignment. 'active' indicates available for deployment; 'on_leave' excludes from scheduling; 'terminated' marks end of service engagement.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Service Resource Status; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: Indicates whether the technician is authorized for international travel assignments. Requires valid passport, work visa eligibility, and completion of international travel safety training. Used to filter eligible technicians for cross-border dispatch.
reference: ISO 45001 Travel Safety; OSHA; Corporate Travel Policy

description: Classification of the technician role within the service organization. Determines the nature of work orders assignable, billing rate category, and required certification level. Commissioning engineers handle initial product startup; warranty technicians focus on in-warranty repair claims.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Service Resource Type; ISO 9001 Clause 7.2 Competence

description: Identifier of the company vehicle assigned to the technician for field service operations. Used for fleet management, GPS tracking integration, and parts/tools inventory carried in the vehicle. Null if the technician operates without a dedicated vehicle assignment.
reference: Salesforce Field Service — Service Vehicle; Maximo EAM Fleet Management

description: Indicates whether the technician is authorized to perform warranty repair and replacement work. Warranty work requires specific product training and authorization to ensure compliance with warranty terms and avoid voiding customer warranty coverage.
reference: Salesforce Field Service; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; SAP S/4HANA SD Warranty Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each spare part record in the aftermarket catalog. Serves as the primary key for the spare_parts_catalog data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: Spare parts catalogs are derived directly from engineering BOMs — service teams reference the BOM to identify which components are serviceable, orderable, and stocked as spares. Parts catalog creation
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Spare parts catalog entries are sourced from specific manufacturing plants. Service operations reference plant-level product data to confirm availability, lead times, and sourcing location for field t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.preferred_supplier_list. Business justification: Spare parts catalogs in service are aligned to procurement's preferred supplier list to ensure parts are sourced from approved vendors. Procurement governance mandates this linkage for cost control an
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Spare parts catalogs are scoped to specific product variants — a motor drive variant has different serviceable parts than another variant. Service teams must know which variant a catalog applies to wh
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.spend_category. Business justification: Spare parts are classified under procurement spend categories for budget tracking and sourcing strategy. Finance and procurement use this link to report MRO spend originating from service operations.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Spare parts catalog must reference the canonical unit of measure from the product domain to ensure consistent quantity handling across ordering, stocking, and invoicing in service operations.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Current commercial availability status of the spare part in the aftermarket catalog. Drives ordering eligibility, customer portal display, and field service planning. 'Phase_out' indicates the part is being discontinued; 'on_request' means it requires special ordering.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Status; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Date from which this spare part record is commercially active and available for ordering in the aftermarket catalog. Used for catalog versioning and price list validity management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Valid From Date; SAP S/4HANA SD - Pricing Condition Validity

description: Date after which this spare part record is no longer commercially active in the aftermarket catalog. Null if the part has no planned expiry. Used for catalog lifecycle management and phase-out planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Valid To Date; SAP S/4HANA SD - Pricing Condition Validity

description: Comma-separated list or free-text description of specific product lines, model numbers, or series within the product family that are compatible with this spare part. Supports cross-reference lookups in field service and customer portals.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Where-Used; SAP S/4HANA MM - Cross-Plant Material Status

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the spare part was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, and trade preference programs.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO Rules of Origin; EU Customs Code; US CBP Regulations

description: Timestamp when the spare part record was first created in the aftermarket catalog system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and catalog change history reporting.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master Creation Date; GDPR Article 5 Accountability

description: Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification code assigned to the spare part for customs and import/export compliance. Used in trade documentation, duty calculation, and cross-border shipment declarations.
reference: WCO Harmonized System (HS) Nomenclature; EU Combined Nomenclature; US HTS Schedule B

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that introduced or last modified this spare part in the catalog. Provides traceability to the product engineering change management process.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management (ECN/ECO); SAP S/4HANA MM - Engineering Change Management

description: Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or equivalent classification code indicating whether the spare part is subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR). Required for multinational shipments of automation and electrification components.
reference: US EAR (Export Administration Regulations) 15 CFR 730-774; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; ITAR 22 CFR 120-130

description: Indicates whether the spare part contains or constitutes a hazardous material requiring special handling, packaging, labeling, and shipping documentation under applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, RoHS, OSHA HazCom, IATA DGR).
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom; EU REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations

description: Date on which the list price for this spare part was last updated in the aftermarket catalog. Supports price audit trails and annual price list revision tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Record; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Standard commercial list price for the spare part as published in the aftermarket catalog. Represents the base price before any customer-specific discounts or contract pricing. Used as the reference price for quotations and service orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Type PR00; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the list price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global aftermarket regions.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or approved manufacturer of the spare part. Used for supplier traceability, approved manufacturer list (AML) compliance, and procurement sourcing.
reference: SAP Ariba - Supplier Master; SAP S/4HANA MM - Manufacturer Part Number; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: The original manufacturer's part number for cross-reference purposes. Enables procurement teams and field engineers to source parts from approved manufacturers and verify interchangeability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Manufacturer Part Number (MPN); SAP Ariba Sourcing; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Minimum quantity of the spare part that must be ordered per transaction, as defined by the supplier or manufacturing constraints. Enforced during order entry in the service portal and ERP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Minimum Order Quantity; SAP Ariba Procurement

description: Timestamp when the spare part record was last updated in the aftermarket catalog system. Used for incremental data loads, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master Change Date; GDPR Article 5 Accountability

description: Business category grouping for the spare part within the aftermarket catalog hierarchy (e.g., Electrical, Mechanical, Hydraulic, Electronic, Pneumatic, Safety). Supports catalog navigation and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Group; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Classification

description: Classification of the spare part by its functional category within the aftermarket catalog. Drives stocking strategy, pricing rules, and service contract coverage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Type; ISO 9001 Clause 8.1

description: The automation system, electrification solution, or smart infrastructure product family for which this spare part is applicable (e.g., SIMATIC S7, SINUMERIK, SINAMICS, SIVACON). Enables field service engineers to filter parts by installed base product family.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Product Structure; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Classification

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the spare part complies with the EU REACH regulation for chemical substance registration, evaluation, and authorization. Required for sale and distribution within the European Union.
reference: EU REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; ECHA Guidance; EU CE Marking

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the spare part complies with the EU RoHS Directive restricting the use of specific hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment. Required for CE Marking and sale in the European Union.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU CE Marking Regulation; IEC 62321

description: Indicates whether this spare part is eligible for coverage under service contracts and maintenance agreements. Drives entitlement checks during field service dispatch and warranty claim processing.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Cloud Entitlement; SAP S/4HANA SD - Service Contract

description: Maximum number of days the spare part can be stored before it is considered expired or unfit for use, measured from the date of manufacture or receipt. Null if the part has no shelf life restriction. Used by warehouse management for FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Shelf Life; Infor WMS - Shelf Life Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this spare part catalog record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Identify

description: Internal standard cost of the spare part used for inventory valuation, margin analysis, and cost of goods sold (COGS) reporting. Maintained in the ERP cost center and updated during standard cost runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Standard Cost Estimate; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory; IFRS IAS 2

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost value (e.g., USD, EUR). Supports multi-currency cost reporting across global manufacturing entities.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21

description: Standard number of calendar days from order placement to delivery for this spare part under normal supply conditions. Used for Available-to-Promise (ATP) calculations and customer delivery commitments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Planned Delivery Time; SAP S/4HANA SD - ATP

description: Prescribed storage requirements for the spare part, including temperature range, humidity limits, light exposure restrictions, and special handling instructions (e.g., 'Store at 15-25°C, <60% RH, away from direct sunlight'). Ensures part integrity during warehousing.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.4 Preservation; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; Infor WMS Storage Management

description: Part number of the newer replacement part that supersedes this part in the aftermarket catalog. Represents the forward link in the supersession chain (old part to new part). Null if this part has not been superseded.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Supersession; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management

description: Part number of the older part that this part replaces in the aftermarket catalog. Represents the backward link in the supersession chain. Null if this part does not supersede a prior part.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Supersession; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management

description: Standard warranty period in months provided for the spare part from the date of installation or delivery. Used to set warranty expiry dates on installed base records and manage warranty claims.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Warranty Management; Maximo EAM - Asset Warranty; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7

description: Gross weight of the spare part in kilograms, including standard packaging. Used for freight cost calculation, shipping documentation, and dangerous goods declarations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Gross Weight; IATA DGR; ISO 80000-4 Mechanics

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each spare parts request record within the aftermarket service domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Service Notification / Reservation

description: Foreign key linking to product.aftermarket_part. Business justification: Field service spare parts requests directly reference aftermarket parts defined in the product domain. Technicians and dispatchers use this link to identify the correct replacement part during repair
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.approved_manufacturer. Business justification: Spare parts must be sourced from engineering-approved manufacturers to maintain product integrity and warranty validity. Service procurement teams are required to reference the Approved Manufacturer L
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.batch. Business justification: A spare parts request may be triggered by a defective production batch requiring rework or repair. Service teams link the request to the originating batch to trace root cause and manage warranty or co
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: Spare parts requests reference specific BOM line items to ensure the exact engineering-specified component is ordered — not a generic substitute. Procurement and service teams use BOM line references
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to service.contract_line. Business justification: Spare parts requests are often fulfilled under a service contract line (spare_parts_included flag on contract_line). The service_contract_number STRING on spare_parts_request is a denormalized referen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to service.dispatch_schedule. Business justification: Spare parts requests are raised to support specific field service dispatch events (parts_required flag on dispatch_schedule confirms this). Linking spare_parts_request.dispatch_schedule_id → dispatch_
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_plan. Business justification: Spare parts requests triggered by service events are fulfilled against an existing fulfillment plan (e.g., a scheduled replenishment or field stocking plan). Logistics and service teams use this link
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Spare parts received for service may require incoming quality inspection. The spare parts request references the inspection lot created at goods receipt to confirm parts passed quality checks before d
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: A spare parts request must specify the delivery location (customer site, warehouse, or service depot). Logistics uses the canonical location record to route and schedule the shipment correctly without
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Industrial service operations require traceability of which lot or batch a spare part came from, especially for warranty claims and quality investigations. Regulatory compliance and recall management
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_item. Business justification: Spare parts requests are raised to fulfill specific maintenance items on a work plan. Procurement and warehouse teams use this link daily to source the exact part specified in the maintenance item, pr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_requisition. Business justification: When a technician requests spare parts not in stock, the service team raises a purchase requisition in procurement. This is a standard fulfillment handoff — service triggers procurement daily for fiel
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
