description: Foreign key linking to order.returns_order. Business justification: When a defective component is returned, a spare parts request is raised to replace it. Service operations link the spare parts request to the originating returns order to track replacement fulfillment
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: High-value or safety-critical spare parts (motors, drives, sensors) are serialized. Service teams must record exactly which serialized unit was installed during a repair for warranty tracking and asse
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to service.spare_parts_catalog. Business justification: Each spare parts request references a specific part from the spare parts catalog. Adding spare_parts_catalog_id FK normalizes the relationship to the authoritative catalog record, enabling retrieval o
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: When a technician requests spare parts for a service job, the system checks live stock position to confirm availability before fulfilling the request. Warehouse and service teams use this link daily t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Spare parts must be picked from a specific warehouse bin or storage location. Dispatch and warehouse staff use this to physically locate and stage parts before a technician departs for a service call.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Spare parts requests are often directed to a preferred or approved supplier already registered in procurement. Service teams reference the supplier master to source critical parts quickly for field re
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Spare parts requests are frequently raised against a specific work center that broke down or needs maintenance. Service teams reference the work center daily to dispatch the right parts and technician
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date on which the spare part was physically delivered to the field service technician or service site, used for SLA performance measurement and on-time delivery KPI reporting.
reference: Infor WMS - Actual Ship Date; SAP S/4HANA SD - Actual Goods Issue Date

description: Approval workflow status for the spare parts request, particularly relevant for high-value or restricted parts requiring managerial authorization before warehouse release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Release Status; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: Date and time when the spare parts request was approved by the authorized approver, used for SLA measurement of approval cycle time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Release Timestamp; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: Name or employee ID of the service manager or authorized approver who approved the spare parts request, supporting audit trail and authorization compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Approved By; ISO 9001 Clause 5.3

description: Position or item number of the spare part within the equipment's Bill of Materials (BOM), enabling structured parts identification and supporting BOM-driven service parts planning.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - BOM Item; SAP S/4HANA MM - BOM Component; engineering.bom_line

description: SAP cost center code to which the spare parts cost is allocated, enabling financial reporting of aftermarket service costs by organizational unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO - Cost Center; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit cost and total cost are expressed, supporting multi-currency operations across global service regions.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the delivery destination, used for export compliance, customs documentation, and cross-border logistics management.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; REACH/RoHS Compliance

description: Serial number of the specific customer equipment unit requiring the spare part, enabling precise asset-level parts consumption tracking and failure pattern analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Equipment Serial Number; Maximo EAM - Serial Number; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Free-text notes entered by warehouse staff or service coordinators regarding fulfillment exceptions, substitutions, partial shipments, or special handling instructions for the spare parts request.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reservation Text; Infor WMS - Order Notes

description: Reference identifier of the customer-installed automation or electrification equipment for which the spare part is being requested, linking the parts demand to the specific asset in the installed base.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Installed Base; SAP S/4HANA PM - Functional Location; Maximo EAM - Asset ID

description: Date confirmed by the warehouse or logistics team as the committed delivery date for the spare part, which may differ from the requested delivery date based on stock availability and shipping lead times.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Confirmed Delivery Date; ATP (Available to Promise)

description: Quantity of the spare part that could not be fulfilled from current stock and has been placed on backorder, requiring procurement or inter-warehouse transfer to satisfy the demand.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Backorder Quantity; Infor WMS - Backorder

description: Actual number of units of the spare part physically issued from the warehouse against this request, which may differ from the quantity requested due to partial availability or substitution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Goods Issue Quantity; Infor WMS - Issued Quantity

description: Number of units of the spare part requested to fulfill the service order or warranty repair, expressed in the part's base unit of measure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reservation Quantity; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: Calendar date on which the spare parts request was formally submitted by the field service technician or service coordinator.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Reservation Date

description: Human-readable business reference number assigned to the spare parts request, used for cross-system tracking and communication with field technicians and warehouse teams.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Reservation Number; Infor WMS - Outbound Order Reference

description: Precise date and time (with timezone) when the spare parts request was created in the system, supporting SLA tracking and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Reservation Creation Timestamp; ISO 8601

description: Classification of the spare parts request by the nature of the service demand, distinguishing between field service, warranty repair, preventive maintenance, emergency breakdown, commissioning, upgrade, and MRO replenishment scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Order Type; Maximo EAM - Work Order Type

description: Date by which the requesting field technician or service coordinator requires the spare part to be delivered to the service site or staging location, used for SLA compliance and logistics planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Requested Delivery Date; Salesforce Field Service - Required By Date

description: Reference number of the outbound shipment or delivery document associated with the physical dispatch of the spare part to the field service location, enabling end-to-end logistics traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Document Number; Infor WMS - Shipment Number

description: Mode of transportation selected for delivering the spare part to the field service location, influencing freight cost, delivery lead time, and SLA compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Shipping Condition; Infor WMS - Carrier Service

description: Specific storage location or bin within the source warehouse from which the spare part is picked, supporting precise inventory management and warehouse execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM - Storage Location; Infor WMS - Bin Location

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which the spare parts request originated, supporting data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Data Provenance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Code identifying the warehouse or storage location from which the spare part is to be issued, enabling multi-warehouse fulfillment routing and inventory depletion tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM - Warehouse Number; Infor WMS - Warehouse Code

description: Current lifecycle status of the spare parts request, tracking progression from initial submission through warehouse fulfillment and delivery to the field service location.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Reservation Status; Infor WMS - Order Status

description: Part number of an approved substitute or alternative spare part used when the originally requested part is unavailable, supporting continuity of service and backorder resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Substitute Material; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Alternate Part

description: Total cost of the spare parts request calculated as unit cost multiplied by quantity issued, representing the actual parts cost charged to the service order or warranty claim.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO - Actual Cost; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Standard or moving average cost per unit of the spare part at the time of the request, used for service cost tracking, warranty cost recovery, and aftermarket profitability analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO - Material Valuation; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Priority classification indicating the operational urgency of the spare parts request, used to drive warehouse picking priority, expedited shipping decisions, and SLA compliance management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - Priority; Maximo EAM - Priority; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: Indicates whether the spare part request is covered under an active warranty agreement, determining whether the cost is charged to the customer or absorbed as a warranty liability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Warranty; asset.asset_warranty; IFRS IAS 37 Provisions

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each parent-child account hierarchy relationship record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for the account_hierarchy data product.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_classification. Business justification: account_hierarchy carries customer_classification and industry_sector_code as denormalized STRING attributes. account_classification is the authoritative multi-dimensional classification record for cu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.sales_area_assignment. Business justification: account_hierarchy stores sales_organization_code as a STRING, which is a denormalized reference to the SAP sales area configuration. Adding sales_area_assignment_id links the corporate hierarchy node
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: account_hierarchy carries a denormalized sla_tier STRING that references the governing SLA tier for the corporate hierarchy. Adding sla_agreement_id as a FK to sla_agreement normalizes this relationsh
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: SAP S/4HANA account group classification code assigned to the child account within this hierarchy, controlling field selection, number ranges, and partner functions. Examples include KUNA (one-time customer), DEBI (domestic customer), EXPO (export customer).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master Account Group

description: Date on which this hierarchy relationship was formally approved by the designated authority. Used for audit trail, compliance reporting, and governance of corporate account structure changes.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SOX Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA Workflow Approval

description: Name or employee identifier of the business authority (e.g., Global Account Director, Credit Manager) who approved the establishment or modification of this hierarchy relationship. Supports governance, audit trail, and segregation of duties requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA Workflow Approval

description: Business identifier of the child (subordinate) account in the hierarchy relationship, sourced from SAP S/4HANA or Salesforce CRM. Represents the subsidiary, division, affiliate, or regional entity reporting up to the parent.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; Salesforce CRM Account Object

description: Maximum credit exposure amount approved for the child account within the context of this hierarchy relationship, expressed in the credit_limit_currency. Used for consolidated credit exposure tracking across subsidiaries and global account credit risk management in SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 7 Financial Instruments Disclosures; Basel III Credit Risk

description: Accounting method applied to consolidate the child account's financials into the parent's financial statements. Determined by ownership percentage and control assessment per IFRS 10 and GAAP ASC 810. Drives credit exposure aggregation and financial reporting roll-up logic.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810; IFRS 28 Investments in Associates

description: Reference number of the master contract or framework agreement governing the commercial relationship between the parent and child accounts within this hierarchy. Links to the contract management system for terms, pricing, and SLA enforcement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Contract Management; Salesforce CRM Contract Object

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the child account's primary registered country. Used for geographic roll-up reporting, regional credit exposure analysis, regulatory compliance (GDPR, CCPA), and multi-country account management.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Timestamp when this account hierarchy relationship record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and incremental processing.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); DAMA DMBOK

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the consolidated credit limit assigned to this hierarchy node. Supports multi-currency credit exposure management across global enterprise accounts.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management

description: The date from which this parent-child hierarchy relationship is valid and active. Used for time-based hierarchy queries, historical roll-up reporting, and compliance with IFRS/GAAP consolidation period requirements.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810; SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy

description: The date on which this parent-child hierarchy relationship ceases to be valid, typically due to corporate restructuring, divestiture, merger, or acquisition. A null value indicates the relationship is currently active with no planned end date.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810; SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy

description: Name or employee identifier of the global account manager responsible for managing the parent-level account relationship. Used for accountability tracking, escalation routing, and global account management reporting in Salesforce CRM.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Owner; SAP S/4HANA SD Partner Functions

description: Business-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this hierarchy relationship, used for cross-system referencing in SAP S/4HANA SD and Salesforce CRM account hierarchy structures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

description: Total number of levels in the hierarchy tree rooted at the ultimate parent account. Indicates the structural complexity of the corporate group. Distinct from hierarchy_level which represents the position of the child; this represents the full tree depth.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

description: Numeric depth level of the child account within the corporate hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents the ultimate parent (global headquarters). Used for roll-up reporting, credit exposure aggregation, and global account management analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

description: Delimited string representing the full ancestry path from the root (ultimate parent) to the current child account, e.g., 'ROOT_CODE/REGION_CODE/COUNTRY_CODE/CHILD_CODE'. Enables efficient tree traversal and ancestor lookups in analytics and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

description: Classifies the purpose or dimension of the hierarchy relationship. 'Corporate' reflects legal ownership structure; 'sales' reflects sales territory or account management grouping; 'credit' reflects credit consolidation grouping; 'reporting' reflects management reporting roll-up; 'legal' reflects legal entity structure; 'operational' reflects operational management grouping.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

description: Indicates whether the parent-child relationship in this record is a direct (immediate) relationship (true) or an indirect/derived relationship representing a non-adjacent ancestor-descendant link in the hierarchy (false). Enables distinction between direct and transitive hierarchy edges for graph traversal and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

description: Indicates whether this hierarchy relationship is the primary (authoritative) hierarchy for the child account when multiple hierarchy types exist (e.g., corporate vs. sales vs. credit). Ensures unambiguous roll-up path selection for consolidated reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this account hierarchy relationship record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for change tracking, incremental data processing, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); DAMA DMBOK

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, business rationale, or special instructions related to this hierarchy relationship. May include details about corporate restructuring events, exceptions to standard hierarchy rules, or account management notes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Object

description: Percentage of ownership or equity stake held by the parent account in the child account entity. Critical for IFRS 10/GAAP ASC 810 consolidation determination (>50% typically triggers full consolidation), credit exposure weighting, and financial risk assessment.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810 Consolidation; IFRS 3 Business Combinations

description: Business identifier of the parent account in the hierarchy relationship, sourced from SAP S/4HANA customer master (SD) or Salesforce CRM. Represents the superior entity (e.g., global headquarters, holding company) in the corporate structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; Salesforce CRM Account Object

description: Internal regional classification code (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER, LATAM) assigned to the child account for geographic segmentation, regional reporting, and global account management analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Area; Salesforce CRM Territory Management

description: Classifies the nature of the corporate relationship between the parent and child accounts. Drives legal, financial, and credit consolidation rules. Examples include subsidiary (majority-owned), division (internal business unit), affiliate (minority-owned), joint venture, branch, or distributor.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810 Consolidation; SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy

description: Reason code explaining why this hierarchy relationship was created, modified, or terminated. Captures corporate events such as mergers, acquisitions, divestitures, spin-offs, or internal reorganizations. Critical for audit trail and financial consolidation history.
reference: IFRS 3 Business Combinations; GAAP ASC 805 Business Combinations; SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy

description: Business identifier of the ultimate parent account at the top of the corporate hierarchy (level 1). Used for consolidated credit exposure tracking, global account management, and enterprise-wide roll-up reporting across all subsidiaries and affiliates.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this hierarchy relationship record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and master data management governance across SAP S/4HANA SD and Salesforce CRM.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry; DAMA DMBOK Data Lineage

description: Native record identifier of this hierarchy relationship in the originating source system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA account hierarchy node ID or Salesforce CRM account relationship ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and operational systems.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry; DAMA DMBOK Data Lineage

description: Current operational status of the parent-child hierarchy relationship. 'Active' indicates the relationship is currently valid; 'inactive' indicates it has been superseded or deactivated; 'pending' indicates awaiting approval; 'terminated' indicates formally ended; 'under_review' indicates a corporate restructuring review is in progress.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Account Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each address record in the customer address repository.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 - Metadata Registry

description: Indicates any access restrictions applicable to this address for deliveries or service visits. Common in industrial manufacturing environments where plant sites require security clearance, scheduled appointments, or certified personnel.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 - General Industry Safety; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: Attention or care-of line specifying the department, role, or individual to whom correspondence or shipments should be directed at this address (e.g., 'Attn: Procurement Department', 'c/o Plant Manager').
reference: USPS Publication 28; Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard

description: Name of the city, municipality, or locality where the address is located. Used for logistics routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and regional reporting.
reference: ISO 19160-4 Addressing; USPS Publication 28

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the country of the address (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN, GBR). Supports multinational customer operations across all geographies.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes

description: County, district, or sub-regional administrative division of the address. Used for local tax jurisdiction determination and regulatory compliance reporting in jurisdictions where county-level data is required.
reference: ISO 19160-4 Addressing; GAAP Tax Jurisdiction Standards

description: Date and time when the address record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity and Accountability

description: Customs or trade region code associated with the address, used for export/import compliance, customs duty calculation, and trade zone classification (e.g., free trade zone, bonded warehouse zone).
reference: World Customs Organization (WCO) Harmonized System; EU Customs Code; US CBP Regulations

description: Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the overall data quality and completeness of the address record, calculated based on validation status, geocoding accuracy, and field completeness. Supports master data governance and data stewardship workflows.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality; ISO 9001 Quality Management; NIST Data Quality Framework

description: Free-text field capturing special delivery instructions for this address, such as dock door numbers, security gate procedures, receiving hours, or hazardous material handling requirements specific to industrial plant sites.
reference: Infor WMS Delivery Configuration; SAP S/4HANA SD Shipping Instructions

description: Date after which this address record is no longer valid for business use. A null value indicates the address is currently active with no planned expiry. Used for historical address retention and compliance record-keeping.
reference: ISO 8601 Date Standard; SAP S/4HANA Validity Period Management; GDPR Data Retention

description: Date from which this address record is valid and active for business use. Supports temporal address management for customers who relocate facilities or open new plant sites.
reference: ISO 8601 Date Standard; SAP S/4HANA Validity Period Management

description: Indicates the precision level of the geocoordinates assigned to the address. Rooftop indicates exact match; range_interpolated and approximate indicate lower precision. Used to assess reliability of location-based analytics.
reference: Google Maps Geocoding API Standards; ISO 19160-4 Addressing

description: Indicates whether this address is the default billing address for invoice delivery. Used by accounts receivable and ERP invoicing processes to route financial documents to the correct address.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Accounts Receivable; GAAP Invoice Addressing Requirements

description: Indicates whether this address is the default shipping/delivery destination for the customer. Used by order management and warehouse management systems to pre-populate delivery addresses on sales orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Shipping Point Determination; Infor WMS Shipping Configuration

description: Indicates whether this address is the primary/default address for the associated customer account and address type. Only one address per customer per address type should be flagged as primary.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; Salesforce CRM Account Address

description: Indicates whether the address has been validated against a postal authority or address verification service. Validated addresses reduce shipping errors and returned mail for industrial shipments.
reference: USPS CASS (Coding Accuracy Support System); Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42

description: ISO 639-1 language code for the preferred communication language at this address (e.g., en, de, zh-CN, fr). Used to localize shipping documents, invoices, and correspondence for multinational customers.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; GDPR Article 12 - Transparent Communication

description: Date and time when the address record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization across ERP and CRM systems, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity and Accountability; ISO 9001 Record Control

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS 84 datum). Enables geocoding, proximity analysis, logistics route optimization, and plant site mapping for multi-site industrial customers.
reference: ISO 6709 - Standard Representation of Geographic Point Locations; WGS 84

description: Primary street address line including building number, street name, and directional indicators. Represents the first line of the physical or mailing address.
reference: USPS Publication 28 - Postal Addressing Standards; ISO 19160-4 Addressing

description: Secondary address line for suite, floor, unit, building name, or additional location detail supplementing the primary street address.
reference: USPS Publication 28 - Postal Addressing Standards; ISO 19160-4 Addressing

description: Tertiary address line used for additional location identifiers such as industrial park name, zone designation, or campus building reference, common in large multi-site industrial facilities.
reference: USPS Publication 28 - Postal Addressing Standards; ISO 19160-4 Addressing

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS 84 datum). Used in conjunction with latitude for geocoding, delivery zone assignment, and spatial analytics.
reference: ISO 6709 - Standard Representation of Geographic Point Locations; WGS 84

description: SAP plant code or equivalent ERP facility identifier associated with this address when the address represents a manufacturing plant or production site. Links the address to production planning and materials management operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Plant Master; ISO 9001 Facility Identification

description: Post Office (PO) Box number for mailing addresses where physical delivery is not applicable. Common for billing and remittance addresses of corporate customers.
reference: USPS Publication 28; Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard

description: Postal or ZIP code for the address. Supports variable formats across countries (e.g., 5-digit US ZIP, alphanumeric UK postcode). Used for shipping rate calculation, tax zone assignment, and geographic analytics.
reference: USPS Publication 28; Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard

description: Business name or descriptive label for the physical site at this address (e.g., 'Chicago Assembly Plant', 'Frankfurt Distribution Center'). Supports multi-site industrial customers with dozens of named plant locations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Plant Master; Salesforce CRM Account Site

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this address record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce CRM, SAP Ariba). Supports data lineage tracking and master data reconciliation across ERP and CRM systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Master Data Governance; Salesforce CRM Data Management

description: The native address identifier from the originating source system (e.g., SAP Business Partner Address GUID, Salesforce Account Address ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability back to the system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BP Address Management; Salesforce CRM Account Object

description: State, province, or administrative region of the address. Stored as ISO 3166-2 subdivision code (e.g., US-CA, DE-BY) to support global multi-country operations and tax jurisdiction mapping.
reference: ISO 3166-2 Country Subdivision Codes

description: Current operational status of the address record. Active addresses are used in order fulfillment and billing. Superseded addresses have been replaced by a newer record. Archived addresses are retained for historical compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; ISO 9001 Record Management

description: Tax jurisdiction identifier assigned to the address for sales tax, VAT, or GST determination. Used by ERP tax engines to calculate applicable tax rates for orders shipped to or billed at this address.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Jurisdiction; GAAP/IFRS Tax Compliance; Vertex Tax Engine

description: IANA time zone identifier for the address location (e.g., America/Chicago, Europe/Berlin). Used for scheduling deliveries, service appointments, and coordinating operations across global plant sites.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database (TZDB); ISO 8601

description: Classifies the functional purpose of the address (e.g., headquarters, billing, shipping/delivery, plant site, registered office). A single customer account may have multiple address types across its global operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Address Category; ISO 19160-4 Addressing

description: Identifies the system or service used to validate the address (e.g., USPS CASS, Loqate, Google Maps, manual review). Supports data quality auditing and validation process governance.
reference: USPS CASS Certification; ISO 9001 Data Quality Management

description: Date and time when the address was last validated against a postal authority or address verification service. Used to schedule re-validation cycles and assess address data freshness.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Value Added Tax (VAT) or GST registration number associated with this address, particularly for EU and international billing addresses. Required for cross-border B2B invoicing and tax compliance in industrial manufacturing.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; IFRS Tax Compliance; SAP S/4HANA FI Tax

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each asset Bill of Materials (BOM) line record in the equipment component hierarchy. Serves as the primary key for the asset_bom entity in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset BOM; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

description: Foreign key linking to product.aftermarket_part. Business justification: Asset BOMs reference aftermarket/spare parts from the product catalog. Maintenance and procurement teams use this link daily to identify correct replacement parts for installed assets during repair or
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Asset BOM components are sourced from or stored at specific warehouse or plant locations. Linking BOM to a logistics location enables inventory and maintenance teams to know exactly where parts are ph
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.product_cost_estimate. Business justification: Asset BOMs define the component structure of manufactured assets and are the basis for product cost estimates. Cost accountants link asset BOMs to cost estimates to calculate standard costs for capita
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.class. Business justification: asset_bom stores spare_part_class as a STRING classification code for BOM components. The class product is the authoritative classification taxonomy for manufacturing assets including spare parts. Add
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Asset BOM components have preferred or approved suppliers. Procurement uses this link to source spare parts during MRP runs and maintenance planning, ensuring the correct supplier is engaged for each
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Approved alternative or interchangeable part number that can substitute for the primary component when the original is unavailable. Supports MRO procurement flexibility and reduces equipment downtime during spare parts shortages.
reference: Maximo EAM - Alternate Items; SAP S/4HANA MM - Cross-Plant Material Status

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that introduced or modified this BOM line. Provides full traceability of component changes back to the formal change management process in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6 Control of Changes

description: Part number or item number of the child component, spare part, or sub-assembly at this BOM line. Corresponds to the material or spare part number in SAP MM or Maximo Item Master used for MRO procurement and inventory management.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset BOM; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (MM60)

description: Functional classification of the component indicating its role in the equipment assembly. Drives MRO stocking strategies, maintenance task material requirements, and spare parts categorization for procurement planning.
reference: Maximo EAM - Item Classification; ISO 14224:2016 Equipment Taxonomy

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility where this asset BOM is applicable. Supports multinational regulatory compliance (e.g., CE Marking in EU, UL in USA) and country-specific spare parts sourcing strategies.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; EU CE Marking; UL Standards

description: Timestamp when this asset BOM line record was first created in the source system (Maximo EAM or SAP PM). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and change history tracking in compliance with ISO 9001 quality management documentation requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Criticality classification of the component based on its impact on equipment availability, safety, and production continuity. Critical components trigger mandatory stocking policies and expedited procurement. Supports FMEA-driven maintenance strategies and TPM programs.
reference: ISO 14224:2016 - Equipment Criticality; IEC 60812 FMEA; Maximo EAM - Criticality

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated unit cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations in a multinational manufacturing enterprise and enables currency conversion for consolidated financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Reference to the engineering drawing or technical document number associated with this component, managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or the document management system. Enables maintenance technicians to access assembly diagrams and installation specifications.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Date from which this BOM line item becomes valid and effective for use in maintenance planning and MRO procurement. Supports date-effective BOM management to handle engineering changes (ECN/ECO) and component supersessions over the asset lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - BOM Validity Date; Maximo EAM - Effective Date; ISO 9001:2015 Change Management

description: Date after which this BOM line item is no longer valid. Used to manage component supersessions, phase-outs, and engineering change orders (ECO). Null value indicates the line is currently valid with no planned expiry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - BOM Validity Date; Maximo EAM - Expiry Date; ISO 9001:2015 Change Management

description: Estimated procurement cost per unit of this component in the transaction currency. Used for MRO budget planning, CAPEX/OPEX spare parts inventory valuation, and maintenance cost estimation. Sourced from SAP MM moving average price or standard cost.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Moving Average Price / Standard Price; IFRS; GAAP - Inventory Valuation

description: Numeric depth level of this component within the BOM tree structure, starting at 1 for the top-level assembly. Enables traversal and explosion of multi-level equipment BOMs for maintenance planning and spare parts analysis.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset BOM; SAP S/4HANA PM - Multi-Level BOM Explosion

description: Physical location or mounting position identifier where the component is installed within the parent assembly (e.g., 'Bearing Housing A', 'Inlet Valve Position 3'). Critical for maintenance technicians to locate and replace components during work order execution.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset BOM Position; SAP S/4HANA PM - Installation Point

description: Company-internal material or part number assigned to the component in the ERP/EAM system (SAP MM material number or Maximo item number). Used for internal procurement, inventory management, and work order material reservations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Number; Maximo EAM - Item Number

description: Indicates whether this component is subject to regulatory control, certification requirements, or compliance obligations (e.g., CE Marking, UL certification, REACH/RoHS compliance, pressure vessel regulations). Triggers mandatory documentation and traceability requirements.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Standards

description: Indicates whether this component is classified as safety-critical, meaning its failure could result in personnel injury, environmental incident, or regulatory non-compliance. Safety-critical components require mandatory inspection intervals and controlled replacement procedures per OSHA and ISO 45001.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; IEC 62443

description: Classification of the BOM line item indicating how the component is managed and procured. Stock items are warehouse-managed spare parts; non-stock items are direct-procured; document items reference technical drawings; text items are descriptive notes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - BOM Item Category (CS01); Maximo EAM - Item Type

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this asset BOM line record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer, change detection, and audit trail requirements for configuration management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Maximo EAM - Change Date; SAP S/4HANA - Last Changed On

description: Standard procurement lead time in calendar days for this component from the preferred supplier. Used in MRO spare parts planning to determine minimum stock levels, reorder points, and emergency procurement timelines to minimize equipment downtime.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Planned Delivery Time; SAP Ariba - Supplier Lead Time; Maximo EAM - Lead Time

description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or component manufacturer. Used for approved vendor list management, warranty validation, and ensuring OEM-specified spare parts are used in maintenance activities to preserve equipment warranties.
reference: Maximo EAM - Manufacturer; SAP S/4HANA MM - Manufacturer Part Number (MPN)

description: Original manufacturer's part number for the component, distinct from the internal material number. Essential for cross-referencing with supplier catalogs, sourcing equivalent parts, and validating OEM compliance during MRO procurement via SAP Ariba.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Manufacturer Part Number (MPN); Maximo EAM - Manufacturer Part Number

description: Business-facing identifier for the equipment BOM structure, typically sourced from Maximo EAM or SAP PM. Groups all component lines belonging to the same asset BOM revision together. Distinct from the engineering product BOM managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset BOM; SAP S/4HANA PM Module (IBIP/IBASE)

description: SAP plant code or facility identifier where this asset BOM is applicable. Supports multi-plant operations in a multinational manufacturing enterprise, enabling plant-specific BOM variants and localized spare parts stocking strategies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA - Plant (Organizational Unit); ISO 55001:2014 Asset Management

description: Sequential numeric position identifier for the component within the BOM structure, following SAP BOM item numbering conventions (e.g., 0010, 0020). Used for ordering and referencing BOM line items in maintenance documentation and work orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - BOM Item Number (CS01); Maximo EAM - BOM Line Sequence

description: Quantity of the child component required per one unit of the parent assembly. Used in MRO spare parts planning, maintenance work order material requirements, and inventory replenishment calculations.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset BOM Quantity; SAP S/4HANA PM - BOM Component Quantity

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant Indicator
description: Indicates whether this component complies with the EU REACH Regulation (EC 1907/2006) for chemical substance registration and restriction. Critical for environmental compliance reporting and supply chain chemical substance management.
reference: REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management; EPA Regulations

description: Recommended minimum on-hand stock quantity for this spare part based on criticality, lead time, and maintenance frequency. Drives MRO inventory stocking policies and reorder point calculations in the Warehouse Management System (WMS).
reference: Maximo EAM - Reorder Point; SAP S/4HANA MM - Safety Stock; Infor WMS - Min/Max Levels

description: Recommended or OEM-specified replacement interval for this component expressed in calendar days. Complements the operating-hours-based interval for time-based preventive maintenance scheduling, particularly for components subject to age-related degradation regardless of usage.
reference: Maximo EAM - PM Schedule; SAP S/4HANA PM - Time-Based Maintenance Plan; ISO 55001:2014

description: Recommended or OEM-specified replacement interval for this component expressed in equipment operating hours. Used to generate preventive maintenance (PM) work orders and schedule planned replacements as part of the TPM program in Maximo EAM.
reference: Maximo EAM - PM Schedule; SAP S/4HANA PM - Maintenance Plan; ISO 55001:2014 Asset Management

description: Revision or version identifier of the asset BOM, incremented when engineering changes (ECN/ECO) are applied to the component structure. Enables tracking of configuration changes over the asset lifecycle.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Compliant Indicator
description: Indicates whether this component complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting the use of hazardous substances such as lead, mercury, cadmium, and hexavalent chromium. Required for CE Marking compliance and environmental regulatory reporting.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU CE Marking; ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this asset BOM line was sourced (e.g., Maximo EAM, SAP S/4HANA PM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when multiple systems contribute BOM data to the Silver layer.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Data Provenance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Current lifecycle status of this BOM line item. Active lines are valid for use in maintenance planning; obsolete lines have been superseded by newer components; draft lines are pending engineering approval. Drives filtering in maintenance planning and MRO procurement workflows.
reference: Maximo EAM - Asset BOM Status; SAP S/4HANA PM - BOM Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Standard unit of measure for the component quantity at this BOM line (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for litre). Aligns with the base unit of measure defined in the material master for accurate MRO procurement and inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Base Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; ANSI X12

description: Defines the intended usage context of this BOM, distinguishing maintenance BOMs (for MRO spare parts and work order planning) from production BOMs (for manufacturing) and engineering BOMs (for design). Ensures the correct BOM is consumed by the appropriate business process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM - BOM Usage (CS01); Maximo EAM - BOM Type

description: Physical weight of the component in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, shipping cost estimation, equipment load calculations, and ergonomic risk assessments for maintenance activities per ISO 45001 occupational health requirements.
reference: ISO 80000-4 Mechanics; ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety

description: Unique surrogate key identifying a product category record in the commercial product catalog taxonomy. Used as the primary key for all category-level joins and references across the Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Category Master

description: The classification identifier from Siemens Teamcenter PLM corresponding to this product category, enabling traceability between the commercial catalog taxonomy and the engineering classification structure.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification Management

description: Foreign key linking to product.warranty_policy. Business justification: product_category.warranty_policy_code is a STRING reference to the default warranty policy applicable to products in this category. Adding warranty_policy_id FK normalizes this relationship and enable
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Pipe-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional designators indicating the geographic markets where this category is commercially active and available for ordering (e.g., 'USA|DEU|CHN|GBR').
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Area

description: The internal business division or strategic business unit responsible for the products within this category (e.g., 'Digital Industries', 'Smart Infrastructure', 'Mobility'). Supports divisional P&L reporting and spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center; SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code

description: Unique alphanumeric business code assigned to the product category, used for catalog navigation, ERP integration, and cross-system referencing. Corresponds to the SAP MM product category key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group (MARA-MATKL); ISO 9001 Clause 8.1 Operational Planning

description: The date and time when this product category record was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change management tracking.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Standard; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); SAP S/4HANA MM Audit Log

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the default pricing currency for products in this category (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'CNY'). Used for catalog pricing display and financial reporting alignment.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The default unit of measure for products in this category (e.g., 'EA' for each, 'KG' for kilogram, 'M' for meter), aligned with SAP S/4HANA base unit of measure. Used for procurement, inventory, and order management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Base Unit of Measure (MEINS); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; ANSI X12

description: Detailed narrative description of the product category, including the types of products it encompasses, intended use cases, and distinguishing characteristics for catalog navigation and spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group Description; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: The date from which this product category becomes valid and active for use in catalog navigation, ordering, and procurement. Supports time-bounded category management and scheduled activations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Validity Period; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: The date after which this product category is no longer valid for new transactions. Used to manage category lifecycle, sunset discontinued groupings, and enforce time-bounded catalog structures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Validity Period; ISO 9001 Clause 8.1 Operational Planning

description: Indicates whether products in this category are subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR, EU Dual-Use Regulation), requiring export licenses or restricted country screening before order fulfillment.
reference: US Export Administration Regulations (EAR); EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; NIST SP 800-171

description: Category code used by external trading partners, distributors, or industry bodies for cross-reference and EDI/B2B integration. Enables mapping between internal taxonomy and partner-specific classification schemes.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI Standards; GS1 Global Data Synchronization Network (GDSN)

description: GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC) brick code assigned to this category for global trade and supply chain interoperability, enabling standardized product data exchange with trading partners and distributors.
reference: GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC) Standard; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Indicates whether products in this category may contain or constitute hazardous materials subject to REACH, RoHS, or OSHA regulations. Triggers additional compliance checks during procurement, manufacturing, and shipping.
reference: REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Numeric depth of this category within the product classification hierarchy. Level 1 represents the top-most division (e.g., 'Automation Systems'), with each subsequent level representing a more granular sub-classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy (PRODH); Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification

description: Full materialized path string representing the category's position in the hierarchy from root to current node (e.g., 'Industrial Automation > Drive Technology > AC Drives'), used for breadcrumb navigation and hierarchical reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification

description: URL pointing to the representative image or icon for this product category, used in digital catalog portals, e-commerce interfaces, and customer-facing product navigation.
reference: Salesforce CRM Commerce Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD Catalog Management

description: The primary industry vertical or market segment this product category is applicable to, used for targeted catalog presentation, sales analytics, and go-to-market alignment (e.g., 'factory_automation', 'building_technologies', 'transportation').
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Organization; Salesforce CRM Industry Classification

description: Indicates whether this category is a terminal (leaf) node in the hierarchy with no child categories. Leaf nodes are the lowest-level categories to which individual SKUs and catalog items are directly assigned.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification

description: Indicates whether products within this category are currently available for customer ordering. A value of True means the category is open for order placement; False means ordering is blocked (e.g., for internal or regulatory categories).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Order Processing; SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing

description: Indicates whether this category is used for procurement spend classification in SAP Ariba, enabling category-level spend analytics, supplier segmentation, and sourcing strategy alignment.
reference: SAP Ariba Category Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4 Control of Externally Provided Processes

description: ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the category name and description are stored in this record (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'zh-CN'). Supports multi-language catalog management for global operations.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM Language-Dependent Texts; GDPR Article 12 (Transparency)

description: The date and time when this product category record was most recently updated, recorded in ISO 8601 format. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Standard; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); SAP S/4HANA MM Change Documents

description: Current product lifecycle stage of the category as a whole, reflecting the commercial maturity of the product group: 'introduction' (newly launched), 'growth', 'maturity', 'decline', 'end_of_life', or 'obsolete'. Supports portfolio management and strategic planning.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Lifecycle Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.1; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Short, human-readable name of the product category as displayed in the commercial catalog and ordering interfaces (e.g., 'Drive Technology', 'Motion Control', 'Power Distribution').
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Category; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification

description: The commercial product line or brand family associated with this category (e.g., 'SIMATIC', 'SINAMICS', 'SENTRON', 'SCALANCE'). Used for brand-level reporting and catalog organization.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Product Line Classification; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group

description: Identifies the primary regulatory or compliance framework applicable to products in this category (e.g., 'ce_marking' for EU market access, 'rohs' for hazardous substance restrictions, 'reach' for chemical registration, 'ul_certification' for North American safety). Drives compliance reporting and certification tracking.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Standards; IEC 62443

description: The SAP S/4HANA Material Group code (field MATKL) mapped to this product category, used for procurement, inventory valuation, and reporting alignment with the ERP system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group (MARA-MATKL); SAP Ariba Procurement

description: Numeric value controlling the display sequence of this category within its parent level in catalog navigation interfaces, portals, and reporting tools. Lower values appear first.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Product Hierarchy Sort; Salesforce CRM Catalog

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this product category record originated (e.g., 'SAP_S4HANA' for MM material group, 'TEAMCENTER_PLM' for PLM classification). Supports data lineage and Silver layer traceability.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 Data Provenance; SAP S/4HANA MM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: The natural or technical key of this category record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP material group code, Teamcenter classification ID). Used for reconciliation, delta processing, and audit traceability in the lakehouse.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 Data Provenance; SAP S/4HANA MM MATKL; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Procurement spend category code used in SAP Ariba for supplier segmentation, sourcing strategy, and spend analytics. Maps this product category to the enterprise procurement taxonomy for category management.
reference: SAP Ariba Category Management; GAAP Cost Classification; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Current lifecycle status of the product category: 'active' (available for ordering and catalog navigation), 'inactive' (temporarily disabled), 'pending' (awaiting approval), 'discontinued' (no longer in use), 'draft' (under creation/review).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group Status; ISO 9001 Clause 8.1

description: Tax classification code assigned to this product category, used to determine applicable VAT, GST, or sales tax rates during order processing and invoicing. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA tax determination logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Determination; IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers; GAAP ASC 606

description: Classifies the category by its primary business purpose: 'commercial' for customer-facing catalog groupings, 'operational' for internal production/procurement use, 'regulatory' for compliance-driven groupings, 'aftermarket' for spare parts and service items, 'internal' for non-saleable items.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group Type; SAP Ariba Category Management

business_glossary_term: United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC)
description: The United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC) mapped to this category, enabling cross-industry spend classification, procurement benchmarking, and supplier management alignment in SAP Ariba.
reference: UNSPSC v23 Classification Standard; SAP Ariba Category Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4 External Providers

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual contact record in the silver layer, serving as the primary key for the contact data product.
reference: Internal Data Architecture Standard

description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: contact stores mailing_city and mailing_state_province as denormalized address fields. The address product is the authoritative repository for all physical and mailing addresses. Adding mailing_addres
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Opt-Out Flag
description: Indicates whether the contact has exercised their CCPA right to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal information. Applicable to contacts who are California residents.
reference: CCPA Section 1798.120; CPRA Amendment; California Attorney General Regulations

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the contact is primarily located or operates. Used for regional segmentation, compliance jurisdiction determination, and territory assignment.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 44 (International Data Transfers)

description: Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and measuring time-to-contact from lead acquisition.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: Native record identifier from Salesforce CRM (Contact object ID, e.g., 003XXXXXXXXXXXXXXX), used for cross-system traceability and lineage back to the source system of record.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Tracks the status of any active data subject rights request (access, deletion, portability, rectification) submitted by this contact under GDPR or CCPA. Supports privacy compliance workflows.
reference: GDPR Articles 15-22 (Data Subject Rights); CCPA Section 1798.100-1798.125

description: Organizational department or functional unit the contact belongs to within their company (e.g., Engineering, Procurement, Operations, Finance, Quality). Supports targeted communication and account mapping.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Primary business email address of the contact, used for commercial communications, order notifications, service updates, and marketing campaigns. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Indicates whether the contact has opted in to receive marketing and commercial email communications. Must be true before any marketing emails are sent. Supports GDPR and CCPA compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 6 and Article 7 (Consent); CCPA Section 1798.120; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Indicates whether the contact has explicitly opted out of all marketing email communications. When true, the contact must be excluded from all commercial email campaigns regardless of opt-in status.
reference: GDPR Article 17 and Article 21 (Right to Object); CCPA Section 1798.120; CAN-SPAM Act; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Given name of the individual contact as recorded in Salesforce CRM. Used for personalized communication and salutation in customer-facing correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Date
description: Date on which the contact granted or last updated their GDPR consent. Required for audit and compliance purposes to demonstrate lawful basis for data processing under GDPR.
reference: GDPR Article 7; GDPR Recital 42; EU Data Protection Board Guidelines

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Status
description: Current GDPR consent status for processing this contact's personal data. Tracks whether consent has been granted, withdrawn, is pending collection, or is not applicable (e.g., for contacts outside GDPR jurisdiction).
reference: GDPR Article 6; GDPR Article 7; GDPR Article 17

description: Indicates whether this contact holds decision-making authority for purchasing, contracting, or technical approvals within their organization. Critical for sales qualification and opportunity management in B2B industrial sales cycles.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Sales Qualification Framework (MEDDIC/SPIN)

description: Indicates whether this contact is identified as a key influencer in the purchasing or technical evaluation process, even if they do not hold final decision-making authority. Supports multi-stakeholder account management strategies.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Sales Engagement Framework

description: Indicates whether this contact is designated as the primary point of contact for the associated customer account. Only one contact per account should be flagged as primary for a given role.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Account Management Policy

description: Official job title or position of the contact within their organization (e.g., Plant Manager, Procurement Manager, Engineering Lead, VP of Operations). Used for segmentation, routing, and persona-based engagement.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Date of the most recent logged activity (call, email, meeting, task) associated with this contact in Salesforce CRM. Used to measure contact engagement recency and identify dormant contacts for re-engagement.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Sales Engagement KPI Framework

description: Date on which a sales or service representative last made direct contact with this individual (distinct from last_activity_date which includes all logged activities). Supports contact cadence management and SLA tracking.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Customer Service SLA Policy

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact record in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental data loads, and audit compliance.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Family name or surname of the individual contact. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for reporting, CRM, and correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Channel or origin through which this contact was first acquired or entered the CRM system. Used for marketing attribution, pipeline analysis, and ROI measurement of demand generation activities.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Marketing Attribution Framework

description: URL of the contact's LinkedIn professional profile. Used by sales and account management teams for social selling, relationship intelligence, and contact verification in B2B industrial markets.
reference: GDPR Article 6 (Lawful Basis for Processing); Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Mobile or cell phone number for the contact, used for urgent communications, field service coordination, and SMS notifications. Distinct from the primary office phone.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response from this contact, on a scale of 0-10. Used to measure individual contact loyalty and satisfaction, and to identify promoters, passives, and detractors within key accounts.
reference: Net Promoter System (Bain & Company); Internal Customer Experience Program

description: Primary business phone number for the contact, typically a direct office line or switchboard extension. Used for sales outreach, service escalations, and account management.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Indicates whether the contact has opted out of phone-based outreach including sales calls and telemarketing. Supports compliance with do-not-call regulations and GDPR consent requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 21; CCPA Section 1798.120; Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA)

description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the contact's preferred language for communications, documentation, and support (e.g., en-US, de-DE, fr-FR, zh-CN).
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; GDPR Article 12 (Transparent Communication)

description: Date from which the current version of the contact record is considered valid in the silver layer. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) type 2 history tracking and point-in-time reporting for analytics and compliance.
reference: Internal Data Architecture Standard; GDPR Article 5 (Storage Limitation)

description: Full name of the contact's direct manager or superior within the customer organization. Used to understand organizational hierarchy and identify escalation paths within the account.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Account Hierarchy Framework

description: Business role classification of the contact within the customer engagement model. Identifies whether the contact is a technical evaluator, commercial decision-maker, executive sponsor, or operational user. Drives sales strategy and communication routing.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; Internal Sales Engagement Framework

description: Formal honorific or title prefix used when addressing the contact in written or verbal communication (e.g., Mr., Dr., Eng.). Supports professional etiquette in B2B industrial contexts.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner identifier corresponding to this contact, enabling cross-system linkage between Salesforce CRM contact records and SAP ERP business partner master data for order management and billing.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Classification of the corrective action taken to resolve the failure, used for maintenance cost analysis, spare parts consumption tracking, and CAPA effectiveness evaluation.

description: The employee ID or username of the person who reported or logged the failure event into the CMMS/EAM system, used for accountability and audit trail purposes.

description: The actual date on which the goods were delivered and goods issue was posted for this line item. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement and customer SLA compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Module (LIKP/LIPS); customer.sla_agreement

business_glossary_term: Available to Promise / Capable to Promise (ATP/CTP) Confirmation Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether the line item quantity and delivery date have been confirmed through an Available to Promise (ATP) or Capable to Promise (CTP) check in SAP S/4HANA. True indicates a firm commitment has been made.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD ATP/CTP Module; APICS Supply Chain Standards

description: Code indicating a billing block applied to this line item, preventing invoice creation until the block is released (e.g., pending approval, dispute resolution). Corresponds to FAKSP in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Billing Module - VBAP.FAKSP; IFRS 15

description: The delivery date confirmed by the system following ATP (Available to Promise) or CTP (Capable to Promise) check, representing the committed delivery date to the customer. May differ from requested delivery date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD ATP/CTP - VBEP.EDATU; APICS ATP/CTP Standards

description: The quantity confirmed by Available to Promise (ATP) or Capable to Promise (CTP) check, representing the quantity the plant can commit to deliver. May be less than ordered quantity in partial confirmation scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - ATP/CTP Confirmation; VBEP.BMENG

description: SAP cost center associated with this line item for cost allocation and controlling purposes, particularly relevant for internal orders and service line items. Supports COGS tracking and profitability analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; IFRS; GAAP - Cost Accounting

description: Timestamp when the order line item was originally created in the source SAP S/4HANA system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and order processing time analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - VBAP.ERDAT / ERZET; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO 9001 Records Management

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the order line item prices and values are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD - VBAP.WAERK

description: The cumulative quantity of the line item that has been physically delivered and goods-issued to the customer. Used to track fulfillment progress and calculate open delivery quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.LFIMG / Delivery Quantity

description: Code indicating a delivery block applied to this line item, preventing delivery processing until the block is released (e.g., credit block, quality hold, export control block). Corresponds to LIFSP in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.LIFSP; Export Control Regulations

description: Percentage discount applied to the gross price for this line item, resulting from customer-specific pricing agreements, volume discounts, or promotional pricing. Used for margin analysis and pricing agreement compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions (KONV); customer.pricing_agreement

description: Gross list price per unit of measure before any discounts or pricing conditions are applied. Represents the catalog or list price for the product. Used for discount analysis and pricing strategy reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions; IFRS 15

description: Line item number of the parent item in a hierarchical order structure, used for sub-items, free goods, or configuration components linked to a main item. Corresponds to UEPOS in SAP VBAP. Null for top-level items.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.UEPOS; BOM-based Order Configuration

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, risk transfer, and cost allocation for this line item. Corresponds to INCO1 in SAP VBAP.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD - VBAP.INCO1; WTO Trade Facilitation

description: SAP SD item category code controlling the processing of the line item, determining billing relevance, delivery relevance, and pricing behavior (e.g., TAN=Standard Item, TAB=Individual Purchase Order, TAD=Service Item).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.PSTYV

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the order line item in the source SAP S/4HANA system. Used for change tracking, delta extraction, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - VBAP.AEDAT; GDPR Article 5; ISO 9001 Records Management

description: Short text description of the material or product being ordered, as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable identification of the ordered item.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - MARA.MAKTX

description: SAP material master number identifying the product, automation system, electrification solution, or smart infrastructure component being ordered. Corresponds to MATNR in SAP VBAP table.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master (MARA.MATNR)

description: Net price per unit of measure for this line item after all applicable discounts and pricing conditions have been applied. Used for revenue recognition and order-to-cash reporting. Corresponds to NETPR in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing (VKOND); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP

description: Total net value of the line item calculated as net price multiplied by ordered quantity. Represents the line item revenue contribution before tax. Corresponds to NETWR in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.NETWR; IFRS 15; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Sequential line item position number within the sales order, identifying the specific item within the order document. Corresponds to POSNR in SAP VBAP table (e.g., 10, 20, 30).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.POSNR

description: The quantity of the material or product requested by the customer on this line item, expressed in the sales unit of measure. Corresponds to KWMENG in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.KWMENG

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center assigned to fulfill this line item. Determines the production plant, storage locations, and shipping point. Corresponds to WERKS in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Module - VBAP.WERKS; ISO 3166 Country Codes

description: The date used to determine applicable pricing conditions, exchange rates, and discounts for this line item. Controls which pricing agreements and condition records are valid for price determination. Corresponds to PRSDT in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing - VBAP.PRSDT; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: SAP product hierarchy code classifying the ordered material within the product portfolio structure (e.g., automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure). Used for sales analysis and reporting by product group.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/MM Module - VBAP.PRODH; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Product Classification

description: SAP profit center assigned to this line item for internal management accounting and profitability analysis. Enables revenue and cost allocation to specific business units or product lines. Corresponds to PRCTR in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - VBAP.PRCTR; IFRS 8 Operating Segments; GAAP

description: Reason code indicating why a line item was rejected or cancelled (e.g., customer cancellation, material unavailability, credit block). Populated only when status is cancelled. Corresponds to ABGRU in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.ABGRU

description: The delivery date requested by the customer for this specific line item. Used as the baseline for ATP/CTP checks and delivery scheduling. Corresponds to VDATU in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.VDATU; APICS Delivery Management

description: Price per single unit of the sales unit of measure, used for unit-level pricing analysis and comparison. Distinct from net_price which may reflect pricing per pricing unit (e.g., per 100 units). Corresponds to KPEIN/KMEIN pricing unit logic in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing - VBAP.KPEIN; IFRS 15
