description: Number of schedule lines (delivery splits) associated with this order line item, indicating how many separate delivery dates or partial shipments have been scheduled. A value greater than 1 indicates split deliveries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Schedule Lines (VBEP) - ETENR

description: SAP shipping point from which the goods for this line item will be dispatched. Determines the physical departure location, shipping conditions, and transportation planning. Corresponds to VSTEL in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Shipping Module - VBAP.VSTEL; Infor WMS

description: Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) identifier used for inventory tracking and warehouse management, representing the specific variant of the product being ordered. May differ from material number for configurable products.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Infor WMS - SKU Management

description: Current processing status of the sales order line item across the order-to-cash lifecycle, from open through delivery and invoicing to completion or cancellation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - VBAP.GBSTA / Overall Processing Status

description: SAP storage location within the assigned plant from which the goods will be picked and shipped for this line item. Used for warehouse management and inventory allocation. Corresponds to LGORT in SAP VBAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Module - VBAP.LGORT; Infor WMS

description: Tax amount applicable to this line item based on the tax classification of the material and customer. Includes VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes depending on the jurisdiction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Module; IFRS; GAAP; EU VAT Directive

description: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element from SAP Project System (PS) linked to this line item, used for Engineer to Order (ETO) and project-based manufacturing scenarios to track revenue and costs against specific projects.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Module - VBAP.PS_PSP_PNR; PMBOK Project Management Standards

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a material lot or production batch record in the inventory domain. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and traceability references.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (MCHA/MCH1); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2 Identification and Traceability

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Lot/batch records are created at goods receipt. Every lot in inventory is traceable to the specific goods receipt that introduced it — critical for quality traceability and supplier accountability in
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hazardous_substance. Business justification: Batches of hazardous materials must reference the product domain's hazardous substance classification for regulatory compliance, safe handling instructions, and transport documentation. Eliminates dup
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Incoming lots are received and inspected against a specific material specification. Quality and receiving teams use this link to pull acceptance criteria, test methods, and certificate of conformance
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.product_cost_estimate. Business justification: Lot/batch inventory is valued using the product cost estimate (standard cost) at time of production. Manufacturing cost accountants link batches to cost estimates to compute production variances and i
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line_item. Business justification: Each lot/batch received into inventory originates from a specific PO line item. This link enables full procurement-to-inventory traceability — essential for audits, warranty claims, and supplier quali
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: lot_batch.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK links each batch to its current storage location for warehouse management.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Lot/batch traceability requires knowing which supplier produced the material. Quality teams use this link to issue supplier corrective actions and perform batch recalls traced back to the originating
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The ABC inventory classification of the material in this batch based on consumption value or criticality. A-class items represent high-value/high-consumption materials requiring tight control; B-class items are medium; C-class items are low-value/low-consumption; D-class items are obsolete or non-moving. Drives cycle counting frequency and safety stock policies.
reference: SAP MM ABC Indicator (ABCIN); Infor WMS ABC Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.4

description: The SAP classification class assigned to this batch, enabling batch-specific characteristic values (e.g., purity percentage, viscosity, tensile strength) to be stored as classification attributes. Supports batch search, selection, and quality characteristic reporting.
reference: SAP MM Batch Classification (KLART 023); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Classification

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier assigned to a material lot or production batch. Corresponds to the SAP MM batch number (CHARG field) and is used for traceability, quality inspection, and FIFO/FEFO picking across the supply chain.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (CHARG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; GS1 GTIN Batch/Lot Number Standard

description: The unit of measure used to express the original batch size at the time of production or receipt, which may differ from the current inventory unit of measure. Supports unit conversion tracking and production yield analysis.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Classification of the batch origin indicating whether it was produced internally, purchased from a supplier, received on consignment, returned from a customer, created through rework, or designated as a quality sample.
reference: SAP MM Batch Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 Control of Externally Provided Processes

description: The date up to which the batch retains its optimal quality characteristics, distinct from the hard expiry date. Used for quality-sensitive materials where degraded-but-not-expired stock may be sold at discount or redirected. Supports FEFO picking and customer SLA compliance.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (MHDRZ); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; GS1 Batch Traceability

description: The document number of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) issued for this batch, confirming that the material meets specified quality standards and test results. Required for regulated industries and customer quality documentation packages.
reference: SAP QM Quality Certificate; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO/IEC 17025 Testing and Calibration Laboratories

description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material in this batch was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, REACH/RoHS regulatory reporting, and customer certificate of origin documentation.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (HERKL); ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; WTO Rules of Origin

description: The date and time when this batch master record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and batch age analysis. Corresponds to SAP batch creation date and time (ERSDA/ERSZT).
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (ERSDA/ERSZT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: The date after which this batch is no longer suitable for use, sale, or consumption. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking strategies, automatic batch blocking, and regulatory compliance for shelf-life-managed materials. Corresponds to SAP batch expiry date (VFDAT).
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (VFDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; EU Regulation 1169/2011 Food Information; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: The date on which this batch was physically received into inventory via a goods receipt posting. For purchased batches, this is the date the material arrived at the plant. Used as the FIFO baseline date for purchased materials and for supplier lead time analysis.
reference: SAP MM Goods Receipt (BUDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3; Infor WMS Receiving

description: Indicates whether the material in this batch is classified as hazardous under applicable regulations (e.g., OSHA HazCom, REACH, RoHS, UN dangerous goods). When true, triggers special handling, storage, and transportation requirements including hazmat documentation and labeling.
reference: SAP MM Hazardous Material (GEFST); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; UN Model Regulations on Transport of Dangerous Goods

description: The SAP QM inspection lot number (PRUEFLOS) associated with the quality inspection triggered for this batch. Links the batch to its quality inspection results, usage decisions, and defect records in the Quality Management system.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot (PRUEFLOS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; Siemens Opcenter MES Quality

description: Current quality inspection status of the batch controlling its availability for use, picking, and shipment. Unrestricted batches are available for all movements; quality_hold and blocked batches are restricted pending inspection or disposition; restricted batches have limited usage; in_inspection batches are undergoing active QM evaluation. Aligned with SAP QM batch status.
reference: SAP QM Batch Status (EINME); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 Release of Products; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Control of Nonconforming Outputs

description: The date and time when this batch master record was most recently updated. Tracks changes to batch attributes such as inspection status, quantity, or storage location for audit compliance and data freshness monitoring.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (AEDAT/AEZST); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: The date on which this batch was manufactured or produced. Used as the baseline for shelf life calculations, FIFO/FEFO picking strategy, and regulatory traceability. Corresponds to SAP batch manufacturing date (HSDAT).
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (HSDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; GS1 Batch/Lot Traceability Standard

description: Classification of the material in this batch according to its stage in the production lifecycle. Distinguishes raw materials, Work in Progress (WIP), finished goods, Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO) stock, semi-finished goods, packaging materials, and spare parts.
reference: SAP MM Material Type (MTART); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; Infor WMS Material Classification

description: The maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this batch. Used in conjunction with minimum storage temperature to define the acceptable temperature range for warehouse compliance monitoring and cold chain management.
reference: SAP MM Batch Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.4; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: The minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this batch. Used to validate warehouse temperature zone compliance, trigger alerts for out-of-condition storage, and support regulatory documentation for temperature-sensitive materials.
reference: SAP MM Batch Classification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.4; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: The total quantity of material in this batch at the time of initial creation (production completion or goods receipt). Used as the denominator for yield calculations, scrap rate analysis, and batch consumption tracking.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; Siemens Opcenter MES Batch Management

description: The inventory picking strategy applied to this batch during warehouse order fulfillment. FIFO (First In First Out) prioritizes oldest batches; FEFO (First Expired First Out) prioritizes soonest-to-expire batches; LIFO (Last In First Out) is used in specific regulatory contexts; FMFO (First Manufactured First Out) is based on manufacturing date; manual allows operator discretion.
reference: SAP WM Picking Strategy; Infor WMS Picking Strategy; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.4

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where this batch was produced or received. Determines the organizational unit for inventory valuation, quality management, and storage location assignment.
reference: SAP MM Organizational Structure (WERKS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: The current on-hand quantity of material in this batch expressed in the base unit of measure. Reflects the net available quantity after all goods movements, consumption, and adjustments. Core field for inventory position reporting and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) calculations.
reference: SAP MM Batch Stock (CLABS/CEINM); Infor WMS Inventory Quantity; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: The number of days remaining before this batch reaches its expiry date, calculated at the time of the last inventory snapshot or transaction. Used for FEFO picking prioritization, customer minimum shelf life checks, and proactive disposition planning.
reference: SAP MM Batch Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; Infor WMS FEFO Strategy

description: Indicates whether the material in this batch contains substances restricted under REACH, RoHS, or other environmental regulations. When true, requires compliance documentation, customer disclosure, and may restrict sale in certain markets or regions.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EPA Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA)

description: The date on which this batch is scheduled for re-inspection or retesting to confirm continued conformance to quality specifications. Used for materials with defined retest intervals (e.g., chemicals, raw materials) to prevent use of out-of-specification stock.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (PRDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ICH Q7 Good Manufacturing Practice

description: The total shelf life of the material in this batch expressed in days, as defined in the material master. Used to calculate expiry dates when manufacturing date is known and to enforce minimum remaining shelf life rules for customer shipments.
reference: SAP MM Material Master (MHDRZ/IPRKZ); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; Infor WMS Shelf Life Management

description: Code identifying the required storage conditions for this batch (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, controlled atmosphere, hazardous). Drives warehouse slotting, temperature zone assignment, and compliance with material safety data sheet (MSDS) requirements.
reference: SAP MM Storage Conditions (LGBKZ); Infor WMS Storage Conditions; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.4; OSHA Hazardous Materials Storage

description: The batch or lot number assigned by the external supplier or vendor for purchased materials. Maintained alongside the internal batch number to enable end-to-end supply chain traceability, supplier quality investigations, and recall management. Corresponds to SAP batch field LICHA.
reference: SAP MM Batch Master (LICHA); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3; GS1 Traceability Standard

description: The base unit of measure in which the batch quantity is expressed (e.g., KG, EA, L, M, PC). Aligns with the SAP material master base unit of measure (MEINS) and is required for all inventory transactions, MRP calculations, and reporting.
reference: SAP MM Material Master (MEINS); ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; UN/CEFACT Unit of Measure Codes

description: The quality management usage decision recorded for this batch following inspection. Determines whether the batch is accepted for unrestricted use, rejected for disposal, conditionally released with restrictions, sent for rework, scrapped, or pending a decision. Corresponds to SAP QM usage decision (VCODE).
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision (VCODE); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 Release of Products; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Control of Nonconforming Outputs

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the batch valuation price is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency inventory valuation for multinational operations.
reference: SAP FI Currency (WAERS); ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories

description: The unit price used to value the inventory in this batch for financial reporting and cost accounting purposes. May reflect standard cost, moving average price, or actual cost depending on the material's valuation method in SAP CO. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.
reference: SAP MM Valuation (VERPR/STPRS); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory

description: The specific warehouse bin or slot identifier within the storage location where this batch is physically stored. Enables precise put-away and picking instructions in the Warehouse Management System (WMS) and supports bin-level inventory accuracy.
reference: SAP WM Storage Bin (LGPLA); Infor WMS Bin Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.4

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each product-plant combination record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master (Plant Data)

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: The product_plant product defines manufacturing and storage plant-level attributes for each catalog item (MRP type, lot sizing, procurement type, standard price, etc.). Each product_plant record belon
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.lifecycle. Business justification: product_plant has a status field and specific_material_status_valid_from date that reflect the plant-level lifecycle/status of the catalog item. Linking product_plant to lifecycle via lifecycle_id ena
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: ABC classification of this material at the plant based on consumption value or strategic importance. A=High value/critical, B=Medium, C=Low value. Used for inventory prioritization and cycle counting. Corresponds to the ABCIN field in SAP MM Plant Data view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; APICS CPIM; Inventory Management Best Practices

description: Indicates whether batch management is activated for this material at this plant, enabling traceability of production batches for quality and regulatory compliance. Corresponds to the XCHPF field in SAP MM Plant Data view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM QM; ISO 9001 Quality Management; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: The timestamp when this product-plant record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001 Quality Management; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the plant-level material valuation prices (standard price, moving average price). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing plants.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; GAAP

description: The fixed order quantity used when the lot sizing procedure is set to Fixed Lot Size (FX). MRP will always propose this quantity regardless of actual requirements. Corresponds to the BSTFE field in SAP MM MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

description: The number of working days required to inspect and process a goods receipt for this material at the plant after delivery. Added to lead time in MRP scheduling. Corresponds to the WEBAZ field in SAP MM MRP 2 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; ISO 9001 Quality Management; GRN Processing Standard

description: Reference number linking this material to its hazardous substance classification record at the plant. Required for materials subject to REACH, RoHS, or OSHA hazardous material regulations.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; EPA

description: The number of working days required to produce this material in-house at the plant. Used by MRP to calculate production order start dates. Corresponds to the DZEIT field in SAP MM MRP 2 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; Siemens Opcenter MES; APICS CPIM

description: The default storage location from which components are issued to production orders at this plant. Corresponds to the LGFSB field in SAP MM MRP 4 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM WM; Infor WMS

description: The timestamp when this product-plant record was last updated in the source system. Used for incremental data loading and change detection in the lakehouse pipeline.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001 Quality Management; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Defines the lot sizing rule used by MRP to calculate order quantities for this material at the plant (e.g., EX=Exact Lot Size, FX=Fixed Lot Size, HB=Replenish to Maximum Stock Level). Corresponds to the DISLS field in SAP MM MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM; MRP II Standard

description: The maximum order quantity that MRP will propose for this material at the plant. Caps production or procurement orders to prevent overproduction or storage capacity breaches. Corresponds to the BSTMA field in SAP MM MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

description: The minimum order quantity that MRP will propose for this material at the plant. Ensures production or procurement orders are not placed below an economically viable threshold. Corresponds to the BSTMI field in SAP MM MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

description: The current weighted moving average cost per base unit of measure for this material at the plant, recalculated with each goods receipt when price control is set to Moving Average Price (V). Corresponds to the VERPR field in SAP MM Accounting view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Three-character code identifying the MRP controller (planner) responsible for planning this material at the plant. Corresponds to the DISPO field in SAP MM MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

description: Defines the MRP procedure used to plan this material at the plant. Common values include PD (MRP), VB (Reorder Point), VM (Automatic Reorder Point), ND (No Planning). Corresponds to the DISMM field in SAP MM MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; MRP II (Manufacturing Resource Planning) Standard; APICS CPIM

description: The number of calendar days required to procure or externally source this material at the plant. Used by MRP to calculate order start dates for externally procured materials. Corresponds to the PLIFZ field in SAP MM MRP 2 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM; Lead Time Standard

description: Defines the inventory valuation method for this material at the plant. S=Standard Price (fixed standard cost), V=Moving Average Price (weighted average). Corresponds to the VPRSV field in SAP MM Accounting view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: The quantity unit to which the standard or moving average price applies (e.g., price per 1 unit, per 100 units, per 1000 units). Corresponds to the PEINH field in SAP MM Accounting view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories

description: Defines whether the material is produced in-house (E), externally procured (F), or both (X) at this plant. Drives MRP planning logic and sourcing decisions. Corresponds to the BESKZ field in SAP MM MRP 2 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

description: Three-character code identifying the production scheduler responsible for managing production orders for this material at the plant. Corresponds to the FEVOR field in SAP MM MRP 4 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Planning; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: The default storage location within the plant where finished goods or semi-finished materials are placed after production. Corresponds to the LGPRO field in SAP MM MRP 4 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM WM; Infor WMS

description: The profit center assigned to this material at the plant for management accounting and profitability analysis. Drives cost allocation and financial reporting by business segment. Corresponds to the PRCTR field in SAP MM Accounting view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Controlling; IFRS 8 Operating Segments; GAAP

description: Two-digit code defining the quality inspection type activated for this material at the plant (e.g., goods receipt inspection, in-process inspection, final inspection). Corresponds to the QMATA field in SAP MM QM view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; ISO 9001 Quality Management; AQL Standard ISO 2859

description: The stock level at which a replenishment order is triggered for this material at the plant when using reorder point planning (MRP type VB). Corresponds to the MINBE field in SAP MM MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

description: The minimum buffer stock quantity maintained at the plant to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. MRP triggers replenishment when stock falls to this level. Corresponds to the EISBE field in SAP MM MRP 2 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Profile code defining when and how serial numbers are assigned to units of this material at the plant (e.g., at goods receipt, goods issue, or delivery). Supports asset traceability and after-sales service. Corresponds to the SERAIL field in SAP MM Plant Data view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM PM; Maximo EAM; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this product-plant record was sourced (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM). Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Data Governance Best Practices

description: The natural composite key from the source system uniquely identifying this product-plant record (typically MATNR + WERKS from SAP MM). Enables reconciliation and delta processing between source and lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Data Governance Best Practices

description: Two-digit code specifying a special procurement scenario such as consignment, subcontracting, stock transfer, or phantom assembly. Complements the standard procurement type. Corresponds to the SOBSL field in SAP MM MRP 2 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

business_glossary_term: Plant-Specific Material Status Valid From Date
description: The date from which the plant-specific material status becomes effective. Enables time-phased status transitions for production planning and procurement control. Corresponds to the MMSTD field in SAP MM Plant Data view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001

description: The standard cost per base unit of measure for this material at the plant, used for inventory valuation when price control is set to Standard Price (S). Corresponds to the STPRS field in SAP MM Accounting view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330; COGS Reporting

description: Current operational status of the material at this plant. Drives whether the material can be procured, produced, or issued at this location. Corresponds to the MMSTA (Plant-Specific Material Status) field in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Two-digit code defining the required storage conditions for this material at the plant (e.g., temperature-controlled, humidity-controlled, hazardous material storage). Corresponds to the LGBKZ field in SAP MM Plant Data/Storage view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Indicator specifying the temperature range or climate class required for storing this material at the plant. Critical for electrification components and chemical materials. Corresponds to the MHDRZ field in SAP MM Plant Data/Storage view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 9001; IEC 60068 Environmental Testing

description: Four-digit code that determines the general ledger accounts updated when inventory movements occur for this material at the plant. Links material master to financial accounting. Corresponds to the BKLAS field in SAP MM Accounting view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards); GAAP

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a customer pricing agreement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Record (KONP/KONH)

description: Foreign key linking to product.price_list. Business justification: Customer-specific pricing agreements reference standard or custom price lists. Order management systems use this to apply correct negotiated pricing when customers place orders.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Customer pricing agreements in manufacturing are negotiated at the product variant level — specific configurations carry distinct pricing. Sales and commercial teams link pricing agreements to exact v
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.sales_org. Business justification: Customer pricing agreements are valid within a specific sales organization that controls pricing authority and currency. Commercial operations teams enforce that pricing agreements are scoped to the c
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: The pricing_agreement table contains a denormalized customer_segment STRING field referencing the customer_segment reference table. Pricing agreements can be segment-level (applying to all accounts in
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.tax_code. Business justification: Pricing agreements reference specific tax codes to calculate correct tax amounts during order processing and invoicing. Sales and billing teams use this for every customer transaction.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Descriptive name or title of the pricing agreement as referenced in commercial negotiations, e.g., 'OEM Partner Annual Volume Discount 2024' or 'Key Account Special Price – Automation Division'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module – Condition Type Description

description: Business-facing unique identifier for the pricing agreement, typically sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD condition record key or CRM contract number. Used for cross-system reference and customer-facing communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module – Condition Record Key

description: Classification of the pricing arrangement. Determines the pricing logic applied in SAP S/4HANA condition records: customer-specific price (fixed net price), volume rebate (retroactive rebate based on cumulative spend), special discount (percentage or absolute deduction), or other commercial pricing constructs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Type (KSCHL); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: The current approval workflow status of the pricing agreement. Tracks whether the agreement has been reviewed and authorized by the required approving authority before activation in SAP S/4HANA condition records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Pricing Approval Workflow; ISO 9001:2015 – Document Approval

description: Name or employee identifier of the individual or role who granted final approval for this pricing agreement. Typically a Sales Director, VP of Sales, or Pricing Manager depending on the discount threshold and customer tier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Pricing Authorization; Salesforce CRM – Approval Process

description: The date and time when the pricing agreement was formally approved by the authorized approver. Used for audit trail, compliance reporting, and SLA tracking of the pricing approval process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Approval Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 – Records Control

description: The organizational authority level required to approve this pricing agreement, determined by the discount depth or deal value. Enforces the pricing governance matrix — e.g., discounts above 20% require VP Sales approval, above 30% require CFO sign-off.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Pricing Authorization Levels; SOX – Internal Controls over Revenue

description: Reference number or identifier of the overarching commercial contract, master service agreement, or framework agreement to which this pricing agreement is linked. Enables traceability between pricing conditions and legal contract documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Sales Contract (VA41); Salesforce CRM – Contract Object

description: The SAP S/4HANA SD condition type code (e.g., PR00 for base price, K007 for customer discount, BO01 for rebate) that this pricing agreement maps to in the condition record. Essential for ERP integration and pricing procedure determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Type (KSCHL)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this pricing agreement is applicable. Supports regional pricing strategies, export compliance, and multi-country reporting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 – Country Codes; SAP S/4HANA – Country Configuration

description: The date and time when the pricing agreement record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA or Salesforce CRM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 – Records Control; GDPR Article 5 – Data Integrity and Accountability

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the agreed price, discount, and rebate values are denominated. Supports multi-currency operations across global customer accounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY).
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI – Currency Configuration

description: The negotiated percentage discount to be applied off the standard list price or base price. Applicable when agreement_type is 'special_discount' or 'volume_rebate'. Expressed as a percentage value (e.g., 12.5 = 12.5%). Null when the agreement is fixed-price based.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Rate (KBETR); IFRS 15 – Variable Consideration

description: The SAP S/4HANA distribution channel through which the products covered by this pricing agreement are sold. Determines the applicable pricing condition table and access sequence. Examples: direct sales, distributor, OEM, export.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Distribution Channel (VTWEG)

description: The SAP S/4HANA division (product line or business unit) to which this pricing agreement applies. Used in condition record key determination and supports divisional P&L reporting for pricing analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Division (SPART)

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between seller and buyer under this pricing agreement. Affects the net price calculation and logistics cost allocation.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD – Incoterms (INCO1)

description: Indicates whether this pricing agreement grants the customer exclusive pricing rights for the covered product or product group within a defined territory or channel. Exclusive agreements restrict the same pricing from being offered to competing customers.
reference: Salesforce CRM – Contract Terms; Competition Law / Antitrust Compliance

description: Indicates whether the pricing agreement terms apply retroactively to orders placed before the agreement was formally activated. Relevant for volume rebate agreements where retroactive settlement is triggered upon reaching the target volume.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Retroactive Rebate Processing (VBOF); IFRS 15 – Variable Consideration

description: The date and time when the pricing agreement record was most recently updated in the source system. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and change data capture (CDC) processing.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 – Records Control; Databricks Delta Lake – CDC / Incremental Processing

description: The minimum quantity the customer must order per transaction or within the agreement period to qualify for the agreed pricing. Expressed in the price_unit. A key commercial condition for volume-based and OEM pricing agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Minimum Order Quantity; SAP MM – MRP Parameters

description: The minimum monetary value of an order or cumulative period spend required to activate or maintain the pricing agreement. Expressed in currency_code. Used for value-based volume rebate thresholds and key account agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Rebate Agreement Minimum Value; IFRS 15 – Performance Obligations

description: The payment terms code associated with this pricing agreement, referencing the standard payment terms defined in SAP S/4HANA FI (e.g., 'NT30' for net 30 days, '2/10NT30' for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Linked to the customer's credit and commercial terms.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI – Payment Terms (ZTERM); GAAP – Revenue Recognition

description: The price list category from which the base price is derived before applying the agreed discount or special price. Determines the reference price point for discount calculation in SAP S/4HANA condition records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Price List Type (PLTYP)

description: The quantity denominator for the agreed price, i.e., the number of units to which the agreed_price applies. For example, a price of $500 per 100 pieces would have price_per_quantity = 100. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA condition record pricing quantity (KPEIN).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Pricing Quantity (KPEIN)

description: The retroactive rebate percentage applied to cumulative purchases once the minimum order quantity or revenue threshold is met within the agreement validity period. Applicable for volume_rebate agreement types. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 3.0 = 3%).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Rebate Agreement (VBOF); IFRS 15 – Variable Consideration

description: Defines the renewal behavior of the pricing agreement upon expiry. 'auto_renew' triggers automatic extension under the same terms; 'manual_renew' requires explicit renegotiation; 'no_renewal' means the agreement terminates at valid_to.
reference: Salesforce CRM – Contract Renewal; SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Record Validity

description: The SAP S/4HANA sales organization code responsible for this pricing agreement. Defines the legal selling entity and regional sales structure under which the agreement is managed. Critical for multi-country and multi-entity operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Sales Organization (VKORG); IFRS – Legal Entity Reporting

description: Defines whether the pricing agreement applies to a single product (SKU), a product group, a material group, a product hierarchy node, or all products. Determines the granularity of condition record creation in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Table / Access Sequence; SAP MM – Material Group

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this pricing agreement record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation between SAP S/4HANA condition records and Salesforce CRM contract objects, and Silver Layer audit requirements.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse – Data Lineage; DAMA DMBOK – Data Provenance

description: Current lifecycle status of the pricing agreement. Controls whether the agreement is eligible to be applied to sales orders in SAP S/4HANA. 'Superseded' indicates a newer version has replaced this agreement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Record Status; ISO 9001:2015 Document Control

description: The agreement_number of the newer pricing agreement that has replaced this one when status is 'superseded'. Provides forward linkage for agreement lineage tracking and ensures sales teams reference the current active agreement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Record Supersession; ISO 9001:2015 – Document Control

description: The agreed annual or period target purchase volume (in price_unit) that the customer commits to purchasing under this pricing agreement. Used for volume rebate tracking and commercial performance monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Rebate Agreement Target Volume; Salesforce CRM – Contract Volume Commitment

description: Sequential version number of the pricing agreement, incremented each time the agreement is renegotiated or amended. Enables version history tracking and audit trail for pricing governance and compliance reviews.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 – Document and Record Control; SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Record Change History

description: The start date from which the pricing agreement becomes effective and eligible to be applied to sales orders. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA condition record validity start date (DATAB). Inclusive boundary.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Validity Period (DATAB); IFRS 15 – Contract Term

description: The end date after which the pricing agreement is no longer effective. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA condition record validity end date (DATBI). Inclusive boundary. Agreements with no end date may use 9999-12-31 as a convention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Condition Validity Period (DATBI); IFRS 15 – Contract Term

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each product configuration record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the product_configuration data product.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality Standards

description: Business identifier for the variant configuration (VC) model as defined in SAP S/4HANA variant configuration or Siemens Teamcenter product configurator. Uniquely identifies the configuration model template from which this configuration is derived.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration (VC); Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Product configurations (ETO/ATO variant rules, option classes, constraint rules) are defined for specific products or product families within the commercial catalog hierarchy. A hierarchy_id FK establ
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.lifecycle. Business justification: product_configuration.lifecycle_status (STRING) is a denormalized lifecycle stage value that duplicates data managed by the lifecycle entity. Linking product_configuration to lifecycle via lifecycle_i
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Pipe-separated list of regulatory certifications or standards applicable to this feature selection (e.g., 'CE|UL|RoHS|REACH|IECEx|ATEX'). Drives compliance documentation requirements in order management and export control processes.
reference: EU CE Marking; UL Certification; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH EC 1907/2006; IECEx; ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU

description: Current approval workflow status of the configuration model or change. PENDING awaits review; APPROVED is authorized for production use; REJECTED has been declined; UNDER_REVIEW is in active review; WITHDRAWN has been recalled by the submitter.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Workflow; SAP S/4HANA

description: Name or employee identifier of the authorized person who approved this configuration model or change record. Required for quality management audit trails and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; ISO 10007 Configuration Management

description: Date and time when this configuration model or feature record was formally approved for use. Provides a precise audit trail timestamp for quality management and regulatory compliance purposes.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; ISO 10007 Configuration Management

description: Pipe-separated list of product family codes or platform identifiers with which this configuration model is compatible. Supports cross-sell, upsell, and system integration recommendations in CPQ and CRM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Plain-language description of the configuration constraint rule for business users and CPQ administrators (e.g., 'High voltage 690V selection requires minimum IP54 enclosure protection rating for safety compliance').
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Formal logical expression or condition defining the configuration constraint rule (e.g., 'IF VOLTAGE = 690V THEN ENCLOSURE_RATING IN (IP54, IP65)'). Encoded in SAP S/4HANA dependency syntax or Teamcenter configurator rule language.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration Dependency Syntax; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Type of configuration constraint rule governing the relationship between feature selections. INCOMPATIBLE prevents two features from being selected together; REQUIRES mandates a dependent selection; EXCLUDES prevents a feature when another is chosen; CONDITIONAL applies rules based on other selections.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration Dependency Rules; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Conditional expression determining when an option class or feature is visible to the user in the CPQ configurator interface. Controls dynamic display logic based on prior selections (e.g., show REDUNDANCY_MODULE option only when POWER_RATING >= 75KW).
reference: SAP CPQ; Salesforce CRM CPQ

description: Date and time when this product configuration record was first created in the source system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and lifecycle tracking.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: Numeric ordering sequence for presenting option classes and feature codes in CPQ configurator interfaces, sales order entry screens, and product catalogs. Lower numbers appear first in the configuration workflow.
reference: SAP CPQ; Salesforce CRM CPQ

description: Date from which this configuration model version, option class, or feature code becomes valid and available for use in CPQ, order entry, and manufacturing execution. Supports date-effective configuration management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration Date Effectivity; ISO 10007

description: Date after which this configuration model version, option class, or feature code is no longer valid for new orders or configurations. Supports end-of-life management and product phase-out planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration Date Effectivity; ISO 10007

description: Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or Harmonized System (HS) code applicable to this feature selection for export licensing and trade compliance purposes. Critical for dual-use technology and controlled items in international sales.
reference: US EAR (Export Administration Regulations); EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; NIST SP 800-171

description: Selectable feature or characteristic value code within an option class (e.g., '400V', 'IP65', 'PROFINET', '75KW'). Represents a specific configurable option that can be selected during CPQ or order entry. Maps to SAP S/4HANA characteristic values.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration; SAP CPQ; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Full descriptive text for the selectable feature code, providing technical and commercial context for the option (e.g., '400V Three-Phase Input', 'IP65 Dust-Tight and Water-Jet Protected Enclosure', 'PROFINET Industrial Ethernet Interface').
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration; IEC 60529 (IP Ratings)

description: Indicates whether this feature selection introduces hazardous materials subject to RoHS, REACH, or transportation hazmat regulations. Triggers additional handling, labeling, and documentation requirements in manufacturing and logistics.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; OSHA HazCom Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; EPA

description: Indicates whether this feature code is the default pre-selected value for its option class when no explicit customer selection is made. Default values streamline CPQ configuration and reduce order entry errors.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration; SAP CPQ

description: Indicates whether a selection must be made for this option class before the configuration can be completed and submitted. Mandatory option classes cannot be left unselected in CPQ or order entry processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration; SAP CPQ

description: Date and time of the most recent update to this product configuration record. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and change detection for downstream consumers.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Additional manufacturing or procurement lead time in calendar days introduced by selecting this feature code. Used in ATP (Available-to-Promise) and CTP (Capable-to-Promise) calculations during order promising.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/SD ATP; APICS CPIM

description: Human-readable name of the variant configuration model, describing the configurable product family or platform (e.g., 'Medium Voltage Drive Series X', 'Smart Panel ATO Model'). Used in CPQ interfaces and sales documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Version number of the configuration model, following semantic versioning conventions. Tracks revisions to the configuration model structure, option classes, and constraint rules over time.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; ISO 10007 Configuration Management

description: Code identifying the option class (characteristic group) within the configuration model, such as VOLTAGE, ENCLOSURE_RATING, COMM_PROTOCOL, MOTOR_SIZE, or COOLING_TYPE. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA characteristic classes in variant configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration (VC); IEC 61131

description: Descriptive name of the option class, providing a human-readable label for the configurable characteristic group (e.g., 'Input Voltage Range', 'Enclosure Protection Rating', 'Communication Protocol Interface').
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the pricing impact value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Supports multi-currency pricing in global CPQ and sales order processes.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Multi-Currency; IFRS

description: Defines how the selection of this feature code affects the product price in CPQ pricing calculations. FIXED_ADDER adds a fixed amount; PERCENTAGE_ADDER applies a percentage uplift; MULTIPLIER scales the base price; BASE_PRICE sets the starting price; NO_IMPACT has no pricing effect.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; SAP CPQ; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Numeric value associated with the pricing impact type for this feature selection (e.g., fixed adder amount in base currency, percentage uplift rate, or multiplier factor). Used in CPQ price calculation engines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions; SAP CPQ; IFRS 15

description: Manufacturing strategy classification for the configurable product. ETO (Engineer-to-Order) requires custom engineering; ATO (Assemble-to-Order) selects from predefined options; MTO (Make-to-Order) builds to customer specification; MTS (Make-to-Stock) is pre-built; CTO (Configure-to-Order) is a hybrid.
reference: APICS Supply Chain Dictionary; SAP S/4HANA PP Module

description: Indicates whether selecting this feature code triggers specific regulatory compliance requirements or certifications (e.g., CE Marking, UL listing, RoHS compliance, REACH declaration). When true, compliance documentation must be verified before order fulfillment.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; UL Standards; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006

description: SAP S/4HANA class number for the variant configuration characteristic class associated with this option class. Provides direct linkage to the SAP VC model for order processing, BOM explosion, and routing selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration (VC); SAP MM/PP

description: Defines how many feature values can be selected within an option class. SINGLE allows exactly one selection; MULTI allows multiple simultaneous selections; EXACTLY_ONE enforces a single mandatory choice; AT_LEAST_ONE requires one or more selections.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Variant Configuration; SAP CPQ

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this configuration record originated (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for variant configuration models, TEAMCENTER for PLM-driven configurations, CPQ for commercial configurator records). Supports data lineage in the lakehouse.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Architecture

description: Reference identifier of the corresponding configuration object in Siemens Teamcenter PLM product configurator. Enables traceability between the commercial CPQ configuration model and the engineering product structure in PLM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 10007 Configuration Management

description: Incremental weight in kilograms added to the product by selecting this feature code. Used for logistics planning, shipping cost estimation, and compliance with transportation weight regulations.
reference: ISO 80000-4 Mechanics; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each price list record in the commercial pricing catalog. Serves as the primary key for all downstream references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing (Condition Type / Price List)

description: Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: product_price_list contains a STRING column named 'category' which is a denormalized reference to the category entity. Replacing this with a category_id FK normalizes the relationship, enabling price
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.currency_exchange_rate. Business justification: Price lists in manufacturing are denominated in specific currencies and must reference the active exchange rate for multi-currency pricing. Finance controls exchange rates centrally; pricing team refe
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Workflow approval status of the price list. Pending Approval indicates the price list is awaiting authorization; Approved means it has been authorized for use; Rejected means it was not approved; Withdrawn means it was recalled before approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Approval Workflow; ISO 9001 Change Control

description: Name or user ID of the authorized individual (e.g., Pricing Manager, VP of Sales) who approved this price list for commercial use. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SAP S/4HANA Authorization

description: The calendar date on which the price list was formally approved by the authorized approver. Used for audit trail, compliance reporting, and pricing governance.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Reference to the parent or base price list from which this price list is derived. Used when a price list is created as a percentage discount or markup from a standard list price, supporting tiered pricing hierarchies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Condition Supplements

description: Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the price list, aligned with the SAP S/4HANA SD pricing condition record key. Used in order processing, quotations, and ERP integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Condition Records

description: SAP S/4HANA SD condition type code (e.g., PR00 for base price, MWST for tax) associated with this price list. Defines the pricing element category within the SD pricing framework.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Type Configuration

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country or market for which this price list is applicable. Supports regional pricing strategies in multinational operations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: The date and time when this price list record was first created in the system. Provides an audit trail for pricing governance and data lineage in the lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001 Record Control; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: SAP S/4HANA SD customer group code identifying the category of customers eligible for this price list (e.g., end customers, distributors, OEM partners, intercompany entities). Drives pricing condition determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Group; IFRS 15 Contract Identification

description: Extended free-text description of the price list, including its intended use, applicable customer segments, and any special conditions or notes for sales operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing

description: Percentage discount applied relative to the base price list when this price list is derived from a parent list. A positive value represents a discount; a negative value represents a surcharge. Null if not applicable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Type Percentage; IFRS 15 Variable Consideration

description: SAP S/4HANA SD distribution channel code indicating the route through which products are sold under this price list (e.g., direct sales, wholesale, e-commerce, OEM, intercompany).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Distribution Channel Configuration

description: SAP S/4HANA SD division code representing the product line or business unit (e.g., Automation Systems, Electrification Solutions, Smart Infrastructure) to which this price list applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Division Configuration

description: The calendar date from which this price list becomes valid and applicable for pricing sales orders, quotations, and contracts. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA SD condition record validity start date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Condition Validity; IFRS 15

description: The calendar date on which this price list ceases to be valid. After this date, the price list can no longer be applied to new sales transactions. A null value indicates an open-ended validity period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Condition Validity; IFRS 15

description: External reference identifier for this price list as used by customers, distributors, or trading partners in their own systems. Facilitates B2B pricing alignment and EDI/API integration with partner systems.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI; SAP S/4HANA SD Partner Functions

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code applicable to this price list, defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer, and cost responsibilities between seller and buyer (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP).
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Terms

description: Indicates whether this price list is the default pricing structure applied when no other specific price list is determined for a sales transaction within the applicable sales area and customer group.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Condition Determination

description: Indicates whether the price list is locked against further modifications. A locked price list cannot be edited until explicitly unlocked by an authorized user, ensuring pricing integrity during active sales periods.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: The date and time when this price list record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, incremental data loading, and audit compliance in the lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001 Record Control; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Minimum total order value (in the price list currency) required for this price list to be applicable to a sales transaction. Orders below this threshold may not qualify for the pricing terms defined in this list.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Condition Scales; IFRS 15 Contract Threshold

description: Descriptive commercial name of the price list (e.g., 'North America Standard List Price 2025', 'EMEA Distributor Price Q1 2025'). Used in sales documents, customer communications, and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about the price list that does not fit into structured fields. Used by pricing analysts and sales operations teams.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing

description: SAP S/4HANA payment terms code associated with this price list, defining the standard payment conditions (e.g., net 30, 2/10 net 30) applicable to sales transactions using this pricing structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms; IFRS 15

description: SAP S/4HANA SD pricing procedure identifier that governs how condition types (discounts, surcharges, taxes, freight) are determined and calculated for sales documents using this price list.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Procedure Configuration

description: Internal regional classification code (e.g., AMER, EMEA, APAC) used to group price lists by geographic sales region for reporting and analytics purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Area; Internal Regional Taxonomy

description: SAP S/4HANA SD sales organization code to which this price list is assigned. Defines the organizational unit responsible for the sale of products and services under this pricing structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Organizational Structure; ISO 9001

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this price list record originates (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, SALESFORCE_CRM). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system integration in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; Data Governance Framework

description: The native identifier of this price list record in the originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA condition record number or Salesforce price book ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Records; Salesforce CRM Price Books

description: Current lifecycle status of the price list. Draft indicates the price list is being prepared; Active means it is currently in use for pricing; Inactive means it has been deactivated; Expired means the validity period has passed; Superseded means it has been replaced by a newer version; Under Review means it is pending approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Tax classification code associated with this price list, indicating whether prices are inclusive or exclusive of taxes (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax). Drives tax determination in SAP S/4HANA SD sales documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; IFRS; GAAP

description: Classification of the price list by commercial purpose. Standard List Price is the published catalog price; Distributor Price applies to channel partners; OEM Price is for original equipment manufacturer agreements; Intercompany Transfer Price governs intra-company transactions; Promotional Price supports time-limited campaigns; Contract Price is negotiated for specific customer agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Condition Types; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Sequential version number of the price list, incremented each time the price list is revised or updated. Enables version tracking and historical comparison of pricing structures over time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; ISO 9001 Document Version Control

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer return order (Return Material Authorization) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing (RE Order Type)

business_glossary_term: Credit Memo Ar Invoice Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Returns orders generate credit memos (AR invoices with negative amounts) to refund customers. Accounts receivable uses this to process customer credits and reconcile payments.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Industrial returns are driven by documented asset failures. Linking the return order to the failure record allows quality and service teams to trace the defective component back to its failure event,
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line_item. Business justification: Returns are typically at the line item level. The string original_line_item_number is replaced by FK order_line_item_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Returned serialized or batch-tracked industrial components must reference the original lot/batch for quality traceability and warranty processing. Quality teams use this to investigate defect patterns
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.goods_issue. Business justification: A customer returns order (RMA) is the reverse logistics counterpart of an original goods issue event. Linking returns_order to the original goods_issue provides full forward-and-reverse traceability f
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Returns orders must reference the exact product configuration being returned for inspection, restocking, or replacement decisions. Returns and quality teams use this to validate the returned item agai
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Returns processing must identify the exact product variant returned to route it to the correct inspection, rework, or scrap disposition. Engineering variant data drives the return evaluation and warra
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: Returned goods in industrial manufacturing often require quality inspection and may be placed on quarantine hold. Quality and returns teams link the returns order to the quarantine hold record to trac
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: Industrial automation equipment returns must reference the specific serialized unit being returned. Service and returns teams use serial number linkage for warranty validation, repair history, and ass
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Returns orders must designate a receiving storage location for returned goods. Reverse logistics teams use this to route returned industrial equipment to the correct inspection or quarantine location.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.warranty. Business justification: Returns in industrial manufacturing are frequently initiated against an active warranty claim. The returns processing team links the return to the specific warranty record daily to validate coverage a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.warranty_policy. Business justification: Returns orders in industrial manufacturing are frequently warranty-driven. Customer service and returns teams reference the product warranty policy to determine eligibility, coverage, and resolution p
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date on which the returned goods were physically received at the return plant and a goods receipt (GR) was posted in SAP S/4HANA MM. Triggers inspection workflow and credit memo eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (MIGO); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Manufacturing batch or lot number of the returned material as recorded in SAP batch management. Critical for traceability, quality investigations, recall management, and CAPA processes in industrial manufacturing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Timestamp when the return order record was created in SAP S/4HANA SD. Used for audit trail, SLA measurement, and return processing cycle time analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Indicates whether a credit memo request has been triggered in SAP S/4HANA FI/SD for this return order. When true, the accounts receivable team processes the credit memo to reverse the original invoice and issue a customer credit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Credit Memo Processing; IFRS 15; GAAP

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the return order transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All monetary values on the return order are expressed in this currency.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS

description: Authorization number provided by the customer (buyer) from their own internal system authorizing the return shipment. Used for cross-referencing with the customer's procurement or ERP system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing

description: Final disposition decision for the returned material following quality inspection or goods receipt assessment. Determines the subsequent inventory movement: unrestricted stock reposting, scrap posting, transfer to refurbishment order, or vendor return.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Usage Decision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA Process

description: SAP distribution channel through which the original sale was made (e.g., direct sales, dealer, OEM). Inherited from the original sales order and used for return analytics segmentation and credit memo routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Organizational Structure

description: Foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the return order value from the transaction currency to the company code currency at the time of return order creation. Used for multi-currency financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Valuation; IFRS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: International Commercial Terms code governing the transfer of risk and responsibility for the return shipment between the customer and the company. Determines who bears freight costs and risk during reverse logistics.
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC); SAP S/4HANA SD; WTO Trade Facilitation

description: SAP QM inspection lot number created for the quality inspection of returned goods. Links the return order to the quality management inspection process and usage decision outcome.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; AQL Standards

description: Indicates whether a quality inspection is required for the returned goods before disposition decision (restock, scrap, repair). When true, an inspection lot is created in SAP QM and the material is placed in quality inspection stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; AQL Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the return order record in SAP S/4HANA SD. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Assessment code describing the physical condition of the returned material upon receipt at the return plant. Determines disposition routing: restock, refurbish, scrap, or warranty repair. Captured during goods receipt or quality inspection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA Process

description: Short description of the returned material or product as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable identification of the returned item for customer communications and inspection documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: SAP material master number identifying the specific product, component, or system being returned. Used for inventory posting, quality inspection routing, and BOM traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Net monetary value of the return order before tax, calculated as return quantity multiplied by the net unit price from the original sales order, less any applicable restocking fee. Forms the basis for credit memo creation in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP

description: Date on which the return order was created in SAP S/4HANA SD. Used as the baseline date for return processing SLA measurement, credit memo aging, and return analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Date requested by the customer or logistics team for pickup or collection of the returned goods from the customer's site. Used for reverse logistics scheduling and carrier coordination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing; Incoterms 2020

description: Fee charged to the customer for processing the return and restocking the material, deducted from the credit memo value. Applicable based on return reason code, material type, and contractual terms. Expressed in the transaction currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; Customer Contract Terms

description: SAP outbound/inbound delivery document number created for the physical return shipment. Used to track the reverse logistics movement from customer to return plant and triggers goods receipt posting upon arrival.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Processing; Infor WMS

description: Business-facing return order document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD during RE order type creation (VA01). This is the externally visible RMA document number used in customer communications and logistics coordination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing (VA01 - RE Order Type)

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility designated to receive and process the returned goods. Determines the inventory posting location, inspection routing, and applicable quality procedures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Configuration; ISO 9001:2015

description: Quantity of the material authorized for return, expressed in the base unit of measure. Drives credit memo value calculation, inventory reposting, and restocking fee computation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing; ISO 9001:2015

description: Standardized reason code classifying why the customer is returning the material. Drives credit memo processing rules, restocking fee applicability, warranty claim routing, and quality management CAPA workflows. Sourced from SAP SD return reason configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA Process

description: Free-text description providing additional detail on the return reason beyond the standardized reason code. Captures customer-provided narrative or internal notes explaining the specific circumstances of the return.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Customer-facing Return Material Authorization number issued to the customer as authorization to ship back goods. This is the number the customer uses on their return shipment and is distinct from the internal SAP return order number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs

description: SAP sales organization code responsible for processing the return order. Determines the applicable pricing, credit memo rules, and revenue recognition treatment for the return transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Organizational Structure; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the returned item, representing the specific sellable configuration including variant, packaging, and unit of measure. Used for inventory reconciliation and warehouse management in Infor WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor WMS; GS1 Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the return order, tracking progression from initial creation through goods receipt, quality inspection, credit memo issuance, and final closure. Drives workflow routing in SAP SD and downstream finance processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns Processing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: SAP storage location within the return plant where returned goods are physically placed upon receipt. Used for inventory management, blocked stock posting, and quality hold management in Infor WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor WMS

description: Tax amount applicable to the return order, calculated based on the tax jurisdiction and applicable tax codes. Reversed upon credit memo creation to correctly adjust the customer's tax liability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Processing; IFRS; GAAP; Local Tax Regulations

description: Unit of measure in which the return quantity is expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, PC for piece). Aligned with SAP base unit of measure configuration and GS1 unit of measure standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; GS1 Unit of Measure Standards; ISO 80000

description: Indicates whether the return is associated with a warranty claim. When true, the return is linked to a warranty case in Salesforce Service Cloud and may trigger a CAPA investigation in the quality management system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Warranty Processing; Salesforce Service Cloud; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the product-to-sales organization assignment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this association entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module — Sales Organization Data (VD51/MM60)

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_hierarchy. Business justification: product_sales_org.product_hierarchy_code is a STRING reference to the product hierarchy node for sales organization assignments. Adding product_hierarchy_id FK normalizes this reference. product_hiera
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: sales_org.sales_unit_of_measure is a STRING code representing the unit of measure used for sales transactions in a specific sales organization. The uom product is the authoritative master for all unit
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
