description: SAP SD account assignment group for the material, used in revenue account determination to map product revenue postings to the correct General Ledger (GL) accounts. Critical for financial reporting and revenue recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI — Revenue Account Determination (MVKE-KTGRM); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP ASC 606

description: SAP authorization group restricting which user roles and organizational units can view or modify the sales organization data for this product. Supports role-based access control for sensitive commercial data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Authorization Concept; GDPR Article 25 — Data Protection by Design; ISO/IEC 27001 Access Control

description: SAP SD availability check group code controlling how Available-to-Promise (ATP) and Capable-to-Promise (CTP) checks are performed for this product in the sales organization. Determines whether stock, planned orders, or production capacity are considered.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/PP — Availability Check (MVKE-MTVFP); SAP ATP/CTP Configuration

description: Indicates whether the product is eligible for cash discount terms (e.g., early payment discounts) within this sales organization assignment. When true, cash discount conditions are applied during invoice processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-SKTOF); IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: SAP SD sales organization code representing the legal selling entity or regional sales unit responsible for the sale of the product. Defines the top-level commercial structure under which the product is authorized for sale.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization (T-code: OVX5); ISO 6523 Organization Identifier

description: SAP SD commission group code used to classify the product for sales commission calculation and incentive compensation management within the sales organization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-PROVG); SAP SD Commission Processing

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market or geography associated with this sales organization assignment. Used for regional reporting, regulatory compliance scoping, and export control checks.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Country Assignment

description: Timestamp when the product-sales organization assignment record was first created in the source system or data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history reporting.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Format; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability

description: SAP material number of a complementary product recommended for cross-selling alongside this product within the sales organization. Supports guided selling and upsell/cross-sell analytics in CRM and order management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 2 (MVKE-ZZXSELL); Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Product Recommendations

description: Default manufacturing or warehouse plant assigned to fulfill orders for this product within the sales organization. Used in Available-to-Promise (ATP) checks and delivery scheduling. Can be overridden at order line level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-DWERK); SAP SD Delivery Plant Determination

description: SAP SD distribution channel code indicating the route through which the product reaches the customer (e.g., direct sales, wholesale, e-commerce, dealer network). Part of the Org/DC/Div assignment key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Distribution Channel (T-code: OVXI); SAP SD Organizational Structure

description: SAP SD division code grouping products into business lines or product families for sales reporting and organizational assignment (e.g., automation systems, electrification, smart infrastructure). Completes the Org/DC/Div assignment key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Division (T-code: OVXA); SAP SD Organizational Structure

description: Indicates whether the product is subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR, EU Dual-Use) when sold through this sales organization. When true, additional export license checks are required before order fulfillment.
reference: US Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations); EU Dual-Use Regulation (428/2009); NIST Export Control Guidelines

description: SAP SD general item category group from the material master basic data, used alongside the sales-org-specific item category group for item category determination in sales documents. Distinguishes standard goods, services, configurable products, and third-party items.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Material Master Basic Data 1 (MARA-MTPOS_MARA); SAP SD Item Category Determination

description: SAP SD item category group assigned to the product for this sales org, controlling how the item behaves in sales order processing (e.g., standard item, configurable item, service item, third-party item). Drives item category determination in order management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-MTPOS); SAP SD Item Category Determination

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the product-sales organization assignment record. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance in the Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Format; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability

description: SAP SD loading group code classifying the product by its physical handling and loading requirements (e.g., crane required, forklift, manual). Used in shipping point and route determination for logistics planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 2 (MVKE-LADGR); SAP SD Shipping Point Determination

description: SAP SD material statistics group controlling whether sales transactions for this product are included in the Logistics Information System (LIS) and sales reporting statistics. Governs data collection for demand planning and sales analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-VERSG); SAP LIS Sales Statistics

description: Minimum quantity of the product that must be ordered in a single sales order line for this sales organization and distribution channel. Enforced during order entry to meet commercial or production efficiency thresholds.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-AUMNG); SAP SD Order Quantity Constraints

description: Minimum monetary value of a sales order line for this product within the sales organization. Orders below this threshold may be rejected or subject to surcharges. Expressed in the sales organization's currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-AUMWE); SAP SD Order Value Constraints

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the minimum order value field, reflecting the transactional currency of the sales organization (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI — Currency Configuration

description: Planned date on which the product will cease to be available for new orders in this sales organization. Used for commercial lifecycle planning, customer notification, and inventory wind-down coordination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Product Lifecycle Management Integration; IEC 62402 Obsolescence Management

description: SAP material number of a reference product whose pricing conditions are used as the basis for pricing this product in the sales organization. Enables pricing inheritance for product variants or successor materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-PMATN); SAP SD Pricing Determination

description: Reason code explaining why the product is blocked for sale in this sales organization. Populated when sales_status is 'blocked' or 'restricted'. Supports root cause analysis and unblocking workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Material Status; ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management; SAP QM Integration

description: Current commercial availability status of the product within the assigned sales organization and channel. Controls whether the product can be included in sales orders. 'Blocked' prevents new orders; 'phasing_out' signals end-of-sale transition; 'pending_activation' indicates awaiting commercial launch approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-VMSTA); SAP SD Material Status

description: Date from which the current sales status is effective for this product-sales org assignment. Used to track status transitions and support time-based commercial availability reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data; ISO 8601 Date Format

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this product-sales org assignment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Material Master; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards

description: Natural key or composite key from the source system uniquely identifying this record (e.g., SAP composite key: MATNR+VKORG+VTWEG+SPART). Enables traceability back to the originating system record for audit and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — MVKE Table Key (MATNR+VKORG+VTWEG+SPART); Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards

description: SAP SD tax classification code for the product within the sales organization, used in tax condition determination to apply the correct VAT, GST, or sales tax rates. Varies by country and product type (e.g., 0=exempt, 1=full tax, 2=reduced rate).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-TAXM1); EU VAT Directive; OECD Tax Guidelines; GAAP/IFRS Tax Accounting

description: SAP SD transportation group code classifying the product by its transportation requirements and constraints (e.g., hazardous goods, oversized, temperature-controlled). Used in route determination and freight cost calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 2 (MVKE-TRAGR); SAP TM Transportation Planning; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations

description: SAP SD rebate group code classifying the product for volume-based rebate agreement processing within the sales organization. Products in the same rebate group are aggregated for rebate accrual and settlement calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data 1 (MVKE-BONUS); SAP SD Rebate Processing

description: Date from which the product is authorized for sale within the sales organization, distribution channel, and division. Defines the start of the commercial validity window for this assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data; ISO 8601 Date Format

description: Date until which the product is authorized for sale within the sales organization, distribution channel, and division. After this date, the product is no longer orderable in this commercial channel. Supports end-of-sale planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Sales Organization Data; ISO 8601 Date Format

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the price list record. Serves as the primary key for the price_list entity in the sales domain lakehouse silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Condition Record (KONP/KONH)

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, sales price lists for configured products are anchored to procurement contract costs. Pricing teams reference active procurement contracts to set floor prices and ensure m
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.currency_exchange_rate. Business justification: Industrial manufacturers sell globally; price lists must reference a validated exchange rate from finance to ensure consistent multi-currency pricing. Finance controls rate governance to prevent reven
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.pricing_agreement. Business justification: Sales price lists for key accounts in manufacturing are derived from or constrained by negotiated pricing agreements. Linking them ensures the price list respects contractual commitments and avoids co
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_price_list. Business justification: Sales price lists are derived from or aligned to product price lists. Sales teams reference the master product price list when creating customer-facing pricing. Pricing operations use this link daily
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Sales price lists in industrial manufacturing are built per product variant (e.g., motor size, voltage rating). Pricing teams reference the exact engineering-defined variant to ensure quotes reflect t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or employee identifier of the individual who formally approved this price list. Supports governance, audit, and compliance requirements for pricing authorization.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: The date on which the price list was formally approved by the authorized pricing authority (e.g., VP of Sales, Regional Pricing Manager). Required for SOX compliance and internal audit trails.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Document Control; SOX Section 302/404

description: Defines the pricing methodology used to establish base prices on this list. List price is the standard published price; transfer price applies to intercompany transactions; cost-plus adds a margin to COGS; market-based aligns to competitive benchmarks; negotiated reflects customer-specific agreements.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Type

description: Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the price list, used for cross-system reference and integration with SAP S/4HANA SD condition records and Salesforce CRM pricing agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Price List (PLTYP); ISO 6422

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country for which this price list is applicable. Supports country-specific pricing strategies for multinational industrial manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD - Country-Specific Pricing

description: The date and time when this price list record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Document Control; SOX Section 302/404; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all prices on this list are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY). Supports multinational pricing operations across all regions.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Defines the customer segment or classification for which this price list is intended. Enables differentiated pricing strategies for key accounts, strategic partners, distributors, OEM customers, government entities, and intercompany transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Customer Price Group (KONDA); Salesforce CRM Account Classification

description: Detailed narrative description of the price list scope, intended audience, applicable product lines, and any special conditions or notes for sales and commercial teams.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Pricing Procedure

description: The sales distribution channel for which this price list is applicable. Differentiates pricing for direct sales, distributor/channel partner sales, OEM partnerships, EPC contractor projects, online commerce, intercompany transfers, and aftermarket service.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Distribution Channel (VTWEG)

description: SAP S/4HANA division code representing the product line or business unit (e.g., Industrial Automation, Electrification Solutions, Smart Infrastructure, Aftermarket Parts) to which this price list applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Division (SPART)

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) applicable to this price list, defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between seller and buyer for industrial equipment shipments.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Incoterms (INCO1)

description: Indicates whether this price list is the default list applied when no other specific price list is determined during sales order processing. Only one price list per sales organization and distribution channel combination should be flagged as default.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Default Price List; Salesforce CRM Standard Price Book

description: Indicates whether prices on this list represent net prices (after all standard discounts have been applied) or gross list prices (before discounts). Critical for correct pricing calculation in SAP S/4HANA condition records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Net Price Indicator; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the primary language in which this price list is published (e.g., EN, DE, FR, ZH, JA). Supports multilingual price list management for global operations.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA - Language-Dependent Texts

description: The date and time when this price list record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, incremental data loading in the lakehouse, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Document Control; SOX Section 302/404; GDPR Article 5

description: The maximum allowable discount percentage that sales representatives are authorized to apply on top of this price list without requiring additional approval. Enforces discount governance and margin protection policies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Discount Condition Type; SOX Section 302/404 - Revenue Controls

description: Minimum order value (in the price list currency) required for this price list to be applicable. Enforces commercial thresholds for industrial automation and electrification solution orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Minimum Order Value; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Descriptive name of the price list, such as 'North America Industrial Automation Standard 2025' or 'EMEA Electrification Solutions Channel Partner List'. Used for display and selection in CRM and ERP systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module - Pricing Procedure

description: Scheduled date for the next formal review of this price list by the pricing governance team. Triggers workflow notifications to pricing managers and commercial directors.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1 - Performance Evaluation; SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Governance

description: Free-text field for additional commercial notes, special conditions, or contextual information about this price list. Used by pricing managers and sales operations to document exceptions, market context, or approval rationale.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: The unit of measure basis on which prices are expressed in this list (e.g., each unit, per 100 units, per kilogram, per meter). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA condition pricing unit (KPEIN) for accurate order pricing.
