When True, the SLA renews under the same terms unless cancelled before the renewal date. Drives automated renewal workflows in SAP SD and Salesforce CRM.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; Salesforce CRM Contract Auto-Renewal

description: The internal business unit or division responsible for fulfilling and managing this SLA. For example, 'Automation Systems', 'Electrification Solutions', or 'Smart Infrastructure'. Enables SLA performance reporting and accountability at the business unit level.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 – Organizational Roles; SAP CO Cost Center Hierarchy

description: Categorizes the SLA by its scope of applicability — whether it applies to a specific customer account, a master contract, a product line, a regional operation, or globally across the enterprise. Supports hierarchical SLA management and reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; SAP SD Service Agreement Configuration

description: The reference number of the master contract or service agreement to which this SLA is linked. Enables traceability between SLA commitments and the underlying contractual documents in SAP SD or Salesforce CRM. A single contract may have multiple SLA records.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; SAP SD Contract Management

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which penalty amounts, cap values, and any financial terms of the SLA are denominated. Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing entities. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY.
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; IFRS 21 – Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Free-text description of the SLA agreement, capturing the scope, context, and any special conditions or exclusions not covered by structured fields. Provides business context for analysts, account managers, and auditors reviewing the SLA record.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement Description

description: The date on which the SLA agreement becomes contractually active and enforceable. Marks the start of the SLA performance measurement period. Used in SAP SD contract management and Salesforce Service Cloud entitlement activation.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Describes the escalation path and procedure to be followed when an SLA is at risk of breach or has been breached. May reference an escalation matrix, named contacts, or a defined process code. Supports Salesforce Service Cloud escalation rule configuration and customer communication workflows.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Escalation Rules

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Escalation Threshold Percentage
description: The percentage of the SLA target value at which an escalation warning is triggered before an actual breach occurs. For example, 80% means an alert is raised when 80% of the allowed response time has elapsed. Enables proactive SLA management and breach prevention.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; Salesforce Service Cloud Milestone Warning Actions

description: The date on which the SLA agreement expires and is no longer enforceable. After this date, the SLA transitions to 'expired' status unless renewed. Used for contract renewal tracking, compliance monitoring, and customer account management.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Governing Law Country
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the SLA agreement in case of disputes. Critical for multinational operations where SLAs may be subject to different legal frameworks. Examples: USA, DEU, GBR, CHN, BRA.
reference: ISO 3166-1 – Country Codes; GDPR Article 3; CCPA; IFRS 15

description: The IETF BCP 47 language code of the primary language in which the SLA agreement is written and communicated. Supports multi-language document management in global operations. Examples: en-US, de-DE, zh-CN, fr-FR, pt-BR.
reference: IETF BCP 47 – Language Tags; ISO 639-1 – Language Codes; GDPR Article 12

description: Defines how often the SLA metric is measured and evaluated for compliance. For example, 'monthly' for delivery performance SLAs, 'per_incident' for support response SLAs, or 'real_time' for uptime SLAs. Drives the cadence of SLA reporting and review cycles.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.1; Salesforce Service Cloud Milestone Configuration

description: Specifies the time window within which SLA performance is measured. Distinguishes between calendar hours (24x7), business hours only, or business days only. Critical for support and field service SLAs where response time commitments differ based on operating hours.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Business Hours Configuration

description: The specific performance metric being measured under this SLA, such as 'First Response Time', 'Order-to-Ship Lead Time', 'Mean Time To Repair (MTTR)', 'On-Time Delivery Rate', or 'Defect Resolution Time'. Defines what is being tracked and enforced.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; KPI (Key Performance Indicator) Framework

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Minimum Threshold Value
description: The minimum acceptable performance level below which penalty clauses are triggered. Distinct from the target value — the target is the goal, while the minimum threshold is the contractual floor. For example, target may be 95% on-time delivery, with a minimum threshold of 90% before penalties apply.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3; IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Descriptive business name of the SLA agreement, such as 'Platinum Delivery SLA – EMEA' or 'Critical Support Response SLA – Tier 1'. Used for identification in dashboards, reports, and customer-facing documents.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Salesforce Service Cloud SLA Object

description: The name of the internal account manager, service manager, or contract owner responsible for managing and enforcing this SLA. Used for accountability, escalation routing, and customer relationship management in Salesforce CRM.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3; Salesforce CRM Account Owner

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalty Cap Value
description: The maximum cumulative financial penalty that can be applied under this SLA agreement, regardless of the number or severity of breaches. Expressed in the agreement currency. Protects both parties from unlimited liability and is a standard clause in enterprise manufacturing contracts.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; GAAP Contract Liability; SAP FI

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalty Clause Flag
description: Indicates whether this SLA agreement includes a contractual penalty clause that is triggered upon SLA breach. When True, the penalty_rate and penalty_type fields define the financial consequences of non-compliance. Critical for financial risk management and contract compliance reporting.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: The rate or amount applied as a financial penalty when the SLA is breached. Interpretation depends on penalty_type: for 'percentage_of_invoice', this is a percentage (e.g., 2.5 = 2.5%); for 'fixed_amount', this is a monetary value in the agreement currency. Used in SAP FI/CO for penalty invoice generation.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; GAAP Revenue Recognition; SAP FI

description: Defines the mechanism by which financial penalties are applied upon SLA breach. 'percentage_of_invoice' applies a percentage deduction to the invoice value; 'fixed_amount' applies a flat fee; 'service_credit' issues a credit note; 'rebate' reduces future billing. Drives penalty calculation logic in SAP FI/CO.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; SAP FI Contract Penalty Configuration

description: The priority tier assigned to this SLA, reflecting the urgency and criticality of the performance commitment. Critical and high-priority SLAs typically have tighter targets, stricter penalties, and escalation paths. Used in Salesforce Service Cloud for case routing and escalation rule configuration.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement Priority

description: The internal business region code to which this SLA applies, such as EMEA, APAC, AMER, or a country-specific region. Used for regional SLA performance reporting, compliance tracking, and regional operations management across the multinational enterprise.
reference: ISO 3166-1 – Country Codes; Internal Regional Hierarchy Standards

description: The date on which the SLA agreement is scheduled for renewal review or automatic renewal. Supports proactive contract management, customer retention workflows, and renewal pipeline reporting in Salesforce CRM.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; Salesforce CRM Contract Renewal

description: The agreed frequency at which SLA performance is formally reviewed with the customer. Drives the scheduling of SLA review meetings, performance reports, and continuous improvement discussions. Supports after-sales and customer success management processes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.3 – Management Review; Salesforce CRM Account Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this SLA agreement record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation between CRM and ERP systems, and Silver Layer data integration governance in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework – Data Provenance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Documents any special contractual conditions, exclusions, or exceptions that modify the standard SLA terms. For example, force majeure clauses, seasonal exclusions, or product-specific carve-outs. Critical for accurate SLA compliance assessment and dispute resolution.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

description: Current lifecycle status of the SLA agreement. Controls whether the SLA is enforceable and actively monitored. Transitions from draft through approval to active, and eventually to expired or terminated upon contract end or early cancellation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement Status

description: The contractually committed performance target for the SLA metric. For example, a target value of 4 with unit 'hours' means the SLA commits to a 4-hour response time. This is the threshold against which actual performance is measured for compliance and penalty assessment.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3; GAAP Contract Liability Recognition

description: The date on which the SLA agreement was early-terminated before its scheduled expiry date. Distinct from expiry_date, which represents the planned end. Populated only when the SLA is terminated early due to contract cancellation, customer churn, or mutual agreement.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; SAP SD Contract Termination

description: The reason code explaining why the SLA agreement was terminated before its scheduled expiry. Used for root cause analysis, customer churn reporting, and contract performance analytics. Populated only when termination_date is set.
reference: IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 – CAPA

description: Classification of the SLA by the nature of the performance commitment. Determines which operational process the SLA governs — e.g., delivery lead time for logistics, technical support response for after-sales, or field service for on-site maintenance. Drives routing and enforcement logic in Salesforce Service Cloud and SAP SD.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement Management

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Unit of Measure
description: The unit in which the SLA metric target value is expressed. For example, 'hours' for response time SLAs, 'percentage' for on-time delivery rate SLAs, 'days' for lead time SLAs, or 'count' for defect occurrence SLAs. Essential for correct interpretation and measurement of the target value.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; ANSI/ISO Measurement Standards

description: The version number of the SLA agreement, incremented each time the SLA terms are formally revised and a new version is issued. Supports SLA change history tracking, audit compliance, and ensures the correct version is enforced at any point in time.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Versioning

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each stock position record in the inventory data product. Serves as the primary key for the stock_position entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Overview; Infor WMS Inventory Balance

description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_bom. Business justification: Stock positions for spare parts and components are directly tied to an asset's BOM. Inventory planners use the asset BOM to determine which parts must be stocked to support that asset — a daily operat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Stock positions must reference a physical logistics location (warehouse, plant, hub). Inventory planners and logistics coordinators use this link to determine where stock is held and plan inbound/outb
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: stock_position.batch_number is a denormalized batch reference. Normalizing to lot_batch_id FK against the lot_batch master enables full batch traceability from the stock position.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.reorder_policy. Business justification: stock_position tracks reorder_point and safety_stock_quantity which are governed by reorder_policy. Adding reorder_policy_id FK links each stock position to its governing replenishment policy, enablin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_list. Business justification: Stock positions reference price lists to determine inventory valuation and available-to-promise pricing. Warehouse systems check price lists when reserving stock for sales orders to calculate order va
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing with regional distribution, stock positions are allocated per sales territory to ensure regional availability commitments are met — territory sales managers check regional
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, stock positions are often ring-fenced or allocated by customer segment (e.g., automotive vs. energy). Demand planning and inventory teams use this to manage segment-specif
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: stock_position.storage_location_code is a denormalized location code. Normalizing to storage_location_id FK against the storage_location master eliminates the redundant code column.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: ABC classification of the material based on consumption value or usage frequency. A-items are high-value/high-usage requiring tight control; B-items are medium; C-items are low-value/low-usage. D may indicate dead stock. Drives cycle counting frequency and replenishment strategy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM ABC Indicator (MARC-ABCIN); APICS CPIM ABC Analysis; Pareto Principle

description: Quantity of the material available to commit to new customer orders or production demands, calculated as unrestricted quantity minus reserved quantity. Supports ATP (Available-to-Promise) and CTP (Capable-to-Promise) checks in order management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD ATP Check (MARC-EISBE); APICS Supply Chain Standards

description: Quantity of the material that has been blocked and is not available for any use, typically due to quality rejection, damage, or regulatory hold. Corresponds to SAP MARD-SPEME (blocked stock).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MARD-SPEME; ISO 9001 Non-Conformance; CAPA Process

description: Quantity of the material held at the company's premises but owned by the supplier (vendor consignment stock). Liability is incurred only upon consumption. Corresponds to SAP special stock type K (vendor consignment).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Consignment Stock (MSKA); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories

description: The difference between the physically counted quantity and the system book quantity at the time of the last physical inventory or cycle count. Positive values indicate surplus; negative values indicate shortage. Used for inventory accuracy KPI reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory Difference (ISEG-MENGE vs ISEG-BUCHM); GAAP ASC 330

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material was manufactured or produced. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, REACH/RoHS regulatory reporting, and trade preference determination.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; WTO Rules of Origin

description: The date on which the most recent goods receipt was posted for this stock position. Used for FIFO valuation, aging analysis, and supplier delivery performance tracking. Corresponds to SAP MSEG-BUDAT (posting date of goods movement).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (MSEG-BUDAT); GRN Process; IFRS IAS 2

description: Flag indicating whether the material is classified as hazardous and subject to special storage, handling, and transportation regulations. Drives compliance with OSHA, EPA, REACH, RoHS, and dangerous goods regulations.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 Hazard Communication; EPA RCRA; EU REACH Regulation; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations

description: Quantity of the material that has been shipped from a source location but has not yet been received at the destination location. Includes stock in transfer orders between plants or from suppliers. Corresponds to SAP stock in transit (MARD-UMLME).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Transfer (MARD-UMLME); Infor WMS Transfer Orders

description: The date on which the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count was performed for this stock position. Used to assess count freshness and schedule next cycle count. Corresponds to SAP MARD-LSOBS (date of last count).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory (MARD-LSOBS); GAAP ASC 330; ISO 9001

description: The date of the most recent inventory movement (goods receipt, goods issue, transfer, or adjustment) for this stock position. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete inventory for write-down analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Document (MSEG-BUDAT); Infor WMS Last Transaction Date

description: The lot-sizing rule applied during MRP planning to determine the replenishment order quantity. Options include exact lot size (order exactly what is needed), fixed lot size, economic order quantity (EOQ), and period-based lot sizing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Lot Size (MARC-DISLS); MRP II; APICS EOQ Model

description: The upper inventory quantity threshold beyond which stock should not be replenished, used to prevent overstock and excess carrying costs. Corresponds to SAP MARC-MABST (maximum stock level).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP (MARC-MABST); Lean Manufacturing Inventory Optimization

description: Defines the planning procedure used for this material's replenishment. Determines whether the material is planned via MRP (demand-driven), consumption-based reorder point, forecast-based planning, or managed manually. Corresponds to SAP MARC-DISMM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Type (MARC-DISMM); MRP II; APICS CPIM

description: Total physical quantity of the material currently present at the storage location, regardless of stock type. Represents the gross inventory count before any reservations or holds are applied. Sourced from SAP MARD-LABST + EINME + SPEME.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Overview (MMBE); Infor WMS On-Hand Balance

description: Identifier for the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the stock position is held. Corresponds to the SAP Plant organizational unit. Enables multi-plant inventory visibility and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant (T001W); ISO 3166-1 Country Codes

description: The exact date and time at which this stock position record was captured or last refreshed from the source systems (SAP S/4HANA MM and Infor WMS). Enables point-in-time inventory reporting and audit trail for the Silver Layer data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Overview Extraction; Infor WMS Inventory Snapshot; IFRS IAS 2

description: Quantity of the material currently held in quality inspection and not yet released for use. Pending QM (Quality Management) inspection lot disposition. Corresponds to SAP MARD-EINME.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Lot (QMEL); ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management

description: The minimum stock level at which a replenishment order should be triggered to avoid stockout. Used in MRP (Material Requirements Planning) reorder point planning. Corresponds to SAP MARC-MINBE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP (MARC-MINBE); APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge; MRP II

description: Quantity of the material that has been reserved for specific production orders, sales orders, or project requirements and is therefore not available for other demands. Sourced from SAP reservation management (RESB).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Reservations (RESB); MRP II Planning

description: The buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty, preventing stockouts. Corresponds to SAP MARC-EISBE (safety stock). A key input to MRP planning runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Safety Stock (MARC-EISBE); APICS CPIM; ISO 22301 Business Continuity

description: The date on which the current batch or stock position expires and can no longer be used. Applicable to batch-managed materials with shelf-life management enabled. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking strategies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Shelf Life Management (MCH1-VFDAT); FDA 21 CFR; ISO 9001 Traceability

description: Flag indicating whether this stock position has been classified as slow-moving based on the absence of inventory movements over a defined threshold period. Triggers review for potential write-down or disposal actions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Slow-Moving Analysis; IFRS IAS 2 Net Realizable Value; GAAP ASC 330

description: Indicates the ownership or special stock category of the inventory position. Distinguishes company-owned stock from consignment stock, returnable packaging, pipeline stock, and customer/project-specific stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Special Stock Indicator (MARD-SOBKZ)

description: Classification of the stock by its usability and availability status. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality inspection stock is pending QM release; blocked stock is not available; in-transit stock is between locations. Drives ATP (Available-to-Promise) calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Type (MARD-LABST/EINME/SPEME); ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Total monetary value of the on-hand stock position, calculated as on-hand quantity multiplied by the valuation price. Expressed in the valuation currency. Used for balance sheet inventory reporting and financial close processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MBEW-SALK3; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: The base unit of measure in which all stock quantities for this material are expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, M for meter). Corresponds to SAP MARA-MEINS (base unit of measure).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MARA-MEINS; ISO 80000 International System of Units (SI); GS1 Unit of Measure Codes

description: Quantity of the material that is freely available for use, sales order fulfillment, or production consumption without any quality or usage restrictions. Corresponds to SAP MARD-LABST (unrestricted-use stock).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MARD-LABST; Infor WMS Available Quantity

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inventory valuation price and stock value are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; GAAP ASC 830

description: The costing method applied to value the inventory position. Standard price uses a fixed planned cost; moving average price recalculates with each goods receipt. FIFO and LIFO are used in specific regulatory contexts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Price Control (MBEW-VPRSV); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: The per-unit price used to value the inventory position, based on the material's valuation method (standard price or moving average price). Corresponds to SAP MBEW-STPRS (standard price) or MBEW-VERPR (moving average price).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Valuation (MBEW); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Sub-classification of a material for split valuation purposes, allowing different batches or procurement types (e.g., domestic vs. imported) to be valued separately within the same material and plant.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Split Valuation (MBEW-BWTAR); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Quantity of the material currently in the production process as Work-in-Progress (WIP), issued to production orders but not yet completed as finished goods. Tracked via Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP production orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order Stock; Siemens Opcenter MES WIP Tracking; IFRS IAS 2

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each storage location record within the warehouse or plant facility. Serves as the primary key for the storage_location data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP WM Storage Bin Master; Infor WMS Location Master

description: ABC inventory classification assigned to the storage location based on the velocity and value of materials typically stored there. Drives slotting optimization — A-class locations are positioned for fastest access, supporting lean and JIT replenishment strategies.
reference: Infor WMS Slotting; SAP S/4HANA MM ABC Indicator; Lean Manufacturing Principles

description: Alphanumeric identifier for the aisle within the warehouse where this storage location is physically situated. Used for navigation, putaway routing, and picking path optimization.
reference: Infor WMS Location Master; SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Coordinate

description: Specific bin or slot identifier within a rack level, representing the most granular physical storage unit. Corresponds to the SAP WM storage bin and Infor WMS bin location.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Bin; Infor WMS Bin Master

description: Reason code explaining why a storage location has been blocked or taken out of service. Required for quality management traceability and corrective action processes (CAPA).
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Blocking Reason; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; CAPA Process

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this storage location is physically situated. Supports multi-country inventory reporting, regulatory compliance, and cross-border logistics management.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 44; IFRS; GAAP

description: Timestamp recording when this storage location master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and master data governance.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: Cycle counting frequency classification assigned to this storage location, determining how often physical inventory counts are performed. A-class locations are counted most frequently. Supports inventory accuracy programs and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Cycle Counting; Infor WMS Cycle Count; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5; GAAP Inventory Valuation

description: Date on which this storage location was or is scheduled to be permanently decommissioned and removed from active warehouse operations. Supports warehouse layout lifecycle management and historical reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Deactivation; Infor WMS Location Lifecycle; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Date from which this storage location record became or becomes operationally active. Supports temporal validity management for location master data and warehouse layout change tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Master Data Validity; Infor WMS Location Activation; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Location identifier used in external or third-party systems such as 3PL warehouse management systems, customer portals, or EDI integrations. Enables cross-system location reconciliation for multi-system environments.
reference: Infor WMS Integration; SAP S/4HANA WM External System Interface; EDI Standards

description: Fire protection classification assigned to the storage location based on the type of materials stored and applicable fire suppression systems. Supports HSE compliance and emergency response planning.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; NFPA 13; ISO 45001 Clause 8.2

description: UN/DOT hazardous materials classification for the storage location, indicating the category of dangerous goods that may be stored. Drives segregation rules, safety signage requirements, and regulatory compliance for hazmat storage zones.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; EPA 40 CFR Part 262; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

description: Internal height dimension of the storage location in millimeters. Critical for tall or oversized SKU putaway validation and rack clearance compliance.
reference: Infor WMS Location Dimensions; SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Dimensions; ISO 80000-3

description: Date on which the most recent physical cycle count was performed for this storage location. Used to schedule upcoming counts, assess inventory accuracy, and support audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Cycle Count; Infor WMS Cycle Count History; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5; GAAP

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this storage location master record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between SAP WM and Infor WMS, and audit trail compliance.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5; SAP S/4HANA Change Document; Infor WMS Audit Trail

description: Internal length dimension of the storage location in millimeters. Used for dimensional compatibility checks during putaway to ensure SKU packaging fits within the bin.
reference: Infor WMS Location Dimensions; SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Dimensions; ISO 80000-3 (Quantities and Units)

description: Vertical level or tier designation within a rack or shelving unit (e.g., floor level, level 1, level 2). Used for ergonomic slotting, weight distribution planning, and picking efficiency.
reference: Infor WMS Location Master; SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Coordinate

description: Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storage location within a warehouse or plant. Used in SAP WM as the storage bin number and in Infor WMS as the location ID for putaway and picking operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Bin; Infor WMS Location Master; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Descriptive human-readable name or label for the storage location, used for display in warehouse management interfaces, picking lists, and operational reports.
reference: Infor WMS Location Master; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Indicates whether the storage location is fixed (assigned to a specific SKU), random (dynamically assigned), dedicated, shared, or virtual. Determines slotting strategy and WMS putaway logic.
reference: Infor WMS Location Type; SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Bin Type

description: Maximum number of pallets or handling units that can be stored simultaneously in this location. Used for pallet-level capacity management and putaway strategy in Infor WMS.
reference: Infor WMS Location Capacity; SAP S/4HANA WM Maximum Quantity

description: Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for this storage location. Enforced for hazardous material storage compliance and product quality preservation.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.4; ISO 14001 Clause 8.1; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Maximum volumetric capacity of the storage location in cubic meters. Used for volumetric putaway checks and space utilization analytics.
reference: Infor WMS Location Capacity; SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Volume; ISO 80000-3

description: Maximum allowable weight in kilograms that the storage location can safely hold. Enforced during putaway to prevent structural overloading and ensure workplace safety compliance.
reference: Infor WMS Location Capacity; SAP S/4HANA WM Maximum Weight; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.22; ISO 45001 Clause 8.1

description: Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for this storage location. Used for environmental compliance monitoring and material storage condition validation.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.4; ISO 14001 Clause 8.1; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Indicates whether multiple lot or batch numbers of the same SKU may be stored together in this location. Critical for pharmaceutical, food, and regulated component traceability and FIFO/FEFO compliance.
reference: Infor WMS Lot Management; SAP S/4HANA WM Batch Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2

business_glossary_term: Mixed SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) Allowed Flag
description: Indicates whether multiple distinct SKUs may be co-located within this storage location simultaneously. When false, the location is restricted to a single SKU to prevent picking errors and inventory commingling.
reference: Infor WMS Location Configuration; SAP S/4HANA WM Mixed Storage Indicator; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: Planned date for the next physical cycle count at this storage location. Drives cycle count scheduling workflows in SAP MM and Infor WMS to maintain inventory accuracy targets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Cycle Count Schedule; Infor WMS Cycle Count Planning; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: Picking strategy assigned to this storage location, determining the sequence and method by which stock is retrieved for outbound orders. Supports FIFO/FEFO compliance for perishable and regulated materials.
reference: Infor WMS Picking Strategy; SAP S/4HANA WM Transfer Order Strategy; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility where this storage location physically resides. Supports multi-plant inventory segregation and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Master; ISO 9001 Clause 7.1

description: Putaway strategy assigned to this storage location, governing how incoming stock is directed to this bin. Supports FIFO, FEFO, and zone-directed strategies aligned with Infor WMS and SAP WM putaway rules.
reference: Infor WMS Putaway Strategy; SAP S/4HANA WM Putaway Strategy; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: Identifier for the rack or shelving unit within the aisle where this storage location is positioned. Supports physical navigation and structural capacity management.
reference: Infor WMS Location Master; SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Coordinate

description: Replenishment strategy governing how this storage location is restocked from reserve or bulk storage. Supports Kanban, min-max, and demand-driven replenishment models aligned with JIT and MRP principles.
reference: Infor WMS Replenishment; SAP S/4HANA WM Replenishment Control; Kanban; JIT Principles

description: Geographic or organizational region designation for the site where this storage location resides (e.g., EMEA, APAC, Americas). Supports regional inventory reporting, supply chain analytics, and organizational hierarchy alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; Infor WMS Facility Hierarchy

description: Current operational status of the storage location. Blocked or inactive locations are excluded from putaway and picking strategies. Supports lifecycle management and WMS operational control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Status; Infor WMS Location Status; ISO 9001 Clause 7.1

description: Code identifying the storage section within a storage type, used to group bins with similar characteristics for putaway strategy optimization in SAP WM and Infor WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Section; Infor WMS Zone

description: Code representing the physical storage type classification within the warehouse structure, such as high-rack, open storage, fixed bin, or automated storage. Corresponds to SAP WM storage type and Infor WMS location type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Type; Infor WMS Location Type

description: Temperature classification of the storage location defining the environmental control requirements. Ensures temperature-sensitive materials (chemicals, adhesives, electronic components) are stored in compliant conditions.
reference: Infor WMS Temperature Zone; ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.4; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Internal width dimension of the storage location in millimeters. Used alongside length and height for volumetric capacity calculations and putaway compatibility validation.
reference: Infor WMS Location Dimensions; SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Dimensions; ISO 80000-3

description: Functional zone classification of the storage location defining its operational purpose within the warehouse. Drives putaway and picking strategies, compliance controls, and inventory segregation rules in Infor WMS and SAP WM.
reference: Infor WMS Zone Configuration; SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Section; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106 (Hazmat)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each unit of measure assignment record for a catalog item. Serves as the primary key for the product_uom entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material UoM Configuration

description: The standardized unit of measure code as defined by ISO 80000 and aligned with SAP S/4HANA internal UoM codes (e.g., EA, KG, L, M, PC, ST, PAL, CTN). Identifies the specific unit used for this UoM assignment.
reference: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; SAP S/4HANA MM - Unit of Measure (T006)

description: The denominator of the conversion factor from this UoM to the base UoM. Together with the numerator, defines the ratio: (numerator / denominator) units of this UoM = 1 base UoM unit. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-UMREN field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Conversion (MARM-UMREN); ISO 80000

description: The pre-computed decimal conversion factor (numerator / denominator) representing how many base UoM units are equivalent to one unit of this UoM. Stored for query performance and reporting convenience. Example: 1 PAL = 48 EA → conversion_factor = 48.000000.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Conversion (MARM); ISO 80000

description: The numerator of the conversion factor from this UoM to the base UoM. Together with the denominator, defines the ratio: (numerator / denominator) units of this UoM = 1 base UoM unit. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-UMREZ field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Conversion (MARM-UMREZ); ISO 80000

description: The date and time when this UoM assignment record was first created in the source system or lakehouse. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: Human-readable description of the unit of measure (e.g., 'Each', 'Kilogram', 'Liter', 'Pallet', 'Carton'). Supports display in user interfaces, reports, and order documents.
reference: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; SAP S/4HANA MM - Unit of Measure Text (T006A)

business_glossary_term: European Article Number (EAN) / Universal Product Code (UPC)
description: The EAN or UPC barcode associated with this UoM packaging level. Used for point-of-sale scanning, warehouse receiving, and inventory tracking. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-EAN11 and GS1 standards.
reference: GS1 EAN/UPC Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM - EAN/UPC (MARM-EAN11)

description: The date from which this UoM assignment and its conversion factors are valid and applicable for transactions. Supports time-bound UoM configurations and change management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master Validity; ISO 9001:2015 Change Management

description: The date after which this UoM assignment is no longer valid for use in transactions. Supports planned UoM transitions and packaging changes without disrupting historical records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master Validity; ISO 9001:2015 Change Management

description: The GS1-standard Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) associated with this specific UoM packaging level (e.g., GTIN-8 for inner pack, GTIN-13 for case, GTIN-14 for pallet). Enables barcode scanning, EDI transactions, and retail/logistics interoperability.
reference: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM - EAN/UPC (MARM-EAN11)

business_glossary_term: Gross Weight per Unit of Measure (UoM) in Kilograms (kg)
description: The gross weight of one unit in this UoM, expressed in kilograms. Used for freight calculation, shipping documentation, and hazardous material compliance. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-BRGEW.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Weight (MARM-BRGEW); IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations

business_glossary_term: Height per Unit of Measure (UoM) in Millimeters (mm)
description: The height dimension of one unit in this UoM, expressed in millimeters. Used for packaging design, storage slot assignment, and transportation planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Dimensions (MARM-HOEHE); ISO 80000-3

business_glossary_term: Is Base Unit of Measure (UoM) Flag
description: Indicates whether this UoM is the base unit of measure for the catalog item. The base UoM is the reference unit to which all other UoM conversions are anchored. Only one UoM per SKU may be the base UoM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master Base Unit of Measure (MARA-MEINS)

business_glossary_term: Is Delivery Unit of Measure (UoM) Flag
description: Indicates whether this UoM is the delivery unit of measure used in outbound deliveries and shipping documents. Supports logistics and warehouse management processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/WM - Delivery Unit of Measure; Infor WMS

business_glossary_term: Is Order Unit of Measure (UoM) Flag
description: Indicates whether this UoM is the order unit of measure used in purchase orders and procurement transactions. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA purchase order unit (BESTELLEINHEIT).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchase Order Unit of Measure (MARA-BSTME)

business_glossary_term: Is Production Unit of Measure (UoM) Flag
description: Indicates whether this UoM is used in production orders and manufacturing execution. Supports Bill of Materials (BOM) and production planning processes in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Production Unit of Measure; Siemens Opcenter MES

business_glossary_term: Is Sales Unit of Measure (UoM) Flag
description: Indicates whether this UoM is the sales unit of measure used in customer orders, quotations, and invoices. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA sales unit (VRKME).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Unit of Measure (MARA-VRKME)

business_glossary_term: Is Storage Unit of Measure (UoM) Flag
description: Indicates whether this UoM is the storage unit of measure used in warehouse and inventory management. Determines how stock is tracked and managed in the warehouse management system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM - Storage Unit of Measure; Infor WMS

description: The ISO 80000-compliant international unit of measure code (e.g., 'KGM' for kilogram, 'LTR' for litre, 'MTR' for metre, 'PCE' for piece). Used for EDI, cross-system interoperability, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; UN/ECE Recommendation 20 - Codes for Units of Measure

description: The date and time when this UoM assignment record was most recently updated in the source system or lakehouse. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

business_glossary_term: Length per Unit of Measure (UoM) in Millimeters (mm)
description: The length dimension of one unit in this UoM, expressed in millimeters. Used for packaging design, storage slot assignment, and transportation planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Dimensions (MARM-LAENG); ISO 80000-3

description: The maximum number of units of this UoM that can be stacked on top of each other during storage or transportation. Used for warehouse slot planning and load safety compliance.
reference: Infor WMS - Storage Constraints; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.176 - Materials Handling

description: The minimum quantity in this UoM that can be ordered in a single purchase or sales transaction. Enforced during order entry to ensure supplier and production efficiency requirements are met.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD - Minimum Order Quantity; ANSI ASC X12 EDI

business_glossary_term: Net Weight per Unit of Measure (UoM) in Kilograms (kg)
description: The net weight of one unit in this UoM, expressed in kilograms, excluding packaging. Used for product content weight declarations, customs documentation, and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Net Weight (MARM-NTGEW); EU Regulation 1169/2011

description: Describes the physical packaging form associated with this UoM (e.g., each, inner pack, case, carton, pallet). Supports warehouse operations, shipping documentation, and customer order fulfillment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Packaging Material; Infor WMS; GS1 Packaging Hierarchy

description: The number of base units contained within one unit of this UoM packaging level (e.g., 12 EA per case, 48 cases per pallet). Supports packaging hierarchy navigation and order quantity validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Conversion; GS1 Packaging Hierarchy

description: The rounding quantity in this UoM to which order quantities are rounded up during Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and procurement. Ensures order quantities align with packaging multiples.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/MRP - Rounding Value (MARC-MTVFP); ISO 80000

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this UoM assignment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability in the lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; Data Governance Best Practices

description: The natural key or composite key from the originating source system that uniquely identifies this UoM assignment record (e.g., SAP MARM key: MATNR + MEINH). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM (MARM); Data Governance Best Practices

description: The current operational status of this UoM assignment for the catalog item. Controls whether the UoM is available for use in transactions. 'active' = in use; 'inactive' = temporarily disabled; 'obsolete' = retired; 'pending_review' = under change review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master Status; ISO 9001:2015 Document Control

description: Classifies the business role of this UoM assignment for the catalog item. Determines how the UoM is used across procurement, production, sales, delivery, and storage processes. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA UoM usage categories.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD/PP - Material UoM Usage; ISO 80000

description: Indicates whether the order unit of measure can be changed at the time of order entry, allowing flexibility in procurement and sales transactions. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA variable order unit configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Variable Order Unit (MARA-VABME)

business_glossary_term: Volume per Unit of Measure (UoM) in Cubic Meters (m³)
description: The volume of one unit in this UoM, expressed in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning, container loading optimization, and freight volume calculations. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA MARM-VOLUM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Volume (MARM-VOLUM); Infor WMS

business_glossary_term: Width per Unit of Measure (UoM) in Millimeters (mm)
description: The width dimension of one unit in this UoM, expressed in millimeters. Used for packaging design, storage slot assignment, and transportation planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material UoM Dimensions (MARM-BREIT); ISO 80000-3

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a warranty policy record in the product catalog.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 - Control of Nonconforming Outputs

description: Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or named regional market groupings (e.g., 'USA|CAN|MEX' or 'GLOBAL') identifying the geographic markets where this warranty policy is valid and enforceable.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; EU Directive 2019/771; GDPR Article 3 - Territorial Scope

description: Defines the scope of catalog items to which this warranty policy applies: all products, a specific product family, product category, individual SKU, or a named product line such as automation systems or electrification solutions.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2; SAP SD Material Determination

description: Name or employee identifier of the authorized person who approved this warranty policy version for publication and use. Required for ISO 9001 documented information control.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2 - Creating and Updating Documented Information

description: Date and time when this warranty policy version was formally approved by the authorized approver. Provides audit trail for ISO 9001 documented information control compliance.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the max_claim_amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Defines what is covered under the warranty: parts only (materials cost), parts and labor (materials plus technician time), full replacement (complete unit swap), on-site service (field technician dispatch), depot repair (return-to-depot), software only, or consumables excluded.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Salesforce Service Cloud SLA/Warranty Object

description: Date and time when this warranty policy record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with ISO 9001 documented information control requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5 - Principles of Data Processing

description: Full narrative description of the warranty policy terms, conditions, and coverage scope as communicated to customers and service teams.

Specifies the contractual mechanism for resolving warranty disputes between the manufacturer and customer: arbitration, mediation, litigation, expert determination, or ombudsman. Relevant for legal and compliance teams.
reference: UCC Article 2; EU Directive 2019/771; ISO 10003 - Guidelines for External Dispute Resolution

description: Total duration of the warranty coverage expressed in months from the warranty start date. Standard industrial automation warranties typically range from 12 to 60 months.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2; EU Directive 2019/771 (minimum 2-year statutory warranty)

description: Date from which this warranty policy version becomes effective and applicable to new product sales or registrations. Used to determine which policy version applies to a given transaction date.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; IFRS 37

description: Narrative description of conditions, events, or damage types that void or exclude warranty coverage (e.g., misuse, unauthorized modifications, improper installation, use outside rated environmental conditions, consumable wear parts, damage from external causes).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; UCC Article 2; EU Directive 2019/771

description: Date after which this warranty policy version is no longer applicable to new sales. Policies with no expiry date remain active until explicitly superseded or retired.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; IFRS 37

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this warranty policy. Critical for multinational legal compliance and dispute resolution.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; UCC Article 2; EU Directive 2019/771; GDPR Article 3

description: Number of months during which labor costs are covered under the warranty. May differ from total duration_months when labor coverage is shorter than parts coverage (e.g., 12 months labor within a 24-month parts warranty).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP SD Warranty Condition Configuration

description: IETF BCP 47 language tag identifying the language in which this warranty policy record is authored (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'fr', 'zh-CN'). Supports multi-language policy management for global markets.
reference: IETF BCP 47 Language Tags; GDPR Article 12 - Transparent Information

description: Date and time when this warranty policy record was most recently updated in the data platform. Supports change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; GDPR Article 5

description: Maximum monetary value of claims that can be processed under this warranty policy over its lifetime. Caps the manufacturer's financial liability exposure per policy instance.
reference: IFRS 37 - Provisions, Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets; GAAP ASC 460 Guarantees

description: Short descriptive name of the warranty policy (e.g., 'Standard 2-Year Parts and Labor', 'Extended 5-Year Performance Guarantee') used in customer-facing documentation and service portals.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2 - Determining Requirements for Products and Services

description: Indicates whether the warranty policy includes on-site field service dispatch at no additional charge to the customer. Drives field service scheduling and cost allocation in Salesforce Field Service and Maximo EAM.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Salesforce Field Service

description: Number of months during which replacement parts are covered under the warranty. May differ from labor_coverage_months in tiered warranty structures.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP SD Warranty Condition Configuration

description: Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the warranty policy, used for cross-system reference in SAP SD, Salesforce Service Cloud, and customer-facing documentation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3 - Review of Requirements for Products and Services

description: Indicates whether scheduled preventive maintenance visits are included within the warranty coverage at no additional cost. Relevant for Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) programs and Maximo EAM maintenance planning.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) Standards; Maximo EAM

description: Indicates whether warranty claims are settled on a pro-rata basis (i.e., the reimbursement value decreases proportionally over the warranty period). Common in battery and consumable component warranties.
reference: IFRS 37; GAAP ASC 460; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Indicates whether the customer must register the product to activate warranty coverage. When true, warranty claims may be rejected if registration was not completed within the required registration window.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Warranty Activation

description: Number of days from the warranty start basis event within which the customer must register the product to activate warranty coverage. Applicable only when registration_required is true.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2; Salesforce Service Cloud

description: Pipe-delimited list of regulatory frameworks or certifications that this warranty policy is designed to satisfy (e.g., 'CE|RoHS|REACH|UL|ISO9001'). Supports compliance reporting and customer documentation requirements.
reference: CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Standards; ISO 9001:2015

description: Indicates whether this warranty policy can be renewed or extended at the end of the coverage period, typically through a service contract or extended warranty purchase.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Salesforce Service Cloud Service Contract

description: Maximum number of hours within which the manufacturer commits to resolve or provide a remedy for a warranty claim. Distinct from response_time_hours which covers acknowledgment only.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SLA (Service Level Agreement) Standards

description: Maximum number of hours within which the manufacturer commits to respond to a warranty claim or service request. Defines the Service Level Agreement (SLA) response commitment embedded in the warranty policy.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SLA (Service Level Agreement) Standards; Salesforce Service Cloud SLA

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this warranty policy record originates (e.g., SAP SD warranty condition, Salesforce Service Cloud warranty object, Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 - Data Provenance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Natural key or identifier of this warranty policy record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP warranty condition number, Salesforce warranty record ID). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 - Data Provenance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Current lifecycle status of the warranty policy: draft (being authored), active (in use for new sales), superseded (replaced by a newer policy version), retired (no longer applicable), or under_review (pending approval for changes).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: Indicates whether the warranty coverage can be transferred to a subsequent owner of the product (e.g., upon resale of industrial equipment). Relevant for asset-intensive industries where equipment changes ownership.
reference: UCC Article 2; EU Directive 2019/771 on Sale of Goods

description: Version identifier of the warranty policy document following semantic versioning (e.g., '1.0', '2.1', '3.0.1'). Enables tracking of policy revisions and ensures correct version is applied to warranty claims.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 - Control of Documented Information

description: Defines the event that triggers the start of the warranty period: ship_date (date product leaves factory), delivery_date (date received by customer), installation_date (date physically installed), commissioning_date (date system is operational), invoice_date, first_use_date, or registration_date.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2; SAP PM Warranty Management; Maximo EAM Warranty Module

description: Classification of the warranty policy type: standard (default manufacturer warranty), extended (purchased additional coverage), performance_guarantee (output/uptime commitment), limited (restricted scope), statutory (legally mandated), or supplier_passthrough (warranty passed from component supplier).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; UCC Article 2 (Uniform Commercial Code Warranty Provisions); EU Directive 2019/771 on Sale of Goods

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual operation step within a maintenance work order. Serves as the primary key for the work_order_operation entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Maximo EAM - Work Order Operation; SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Operation (AFVC)

description: Foreign key linking to product.aftermarket_part. Business justification: Work order operations consume specific spare/aftermarket parts from the product catalog. Maintenance planners and technicians reference this link to confirm part availability and cost before executing
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Every maintenance work order operation incurs labor and material costs that must be allocated to a cost center. Plant controllers settle work order costs to cost allocations at period-end — standard p
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.location. Business justification: Each work order operation is performed at a defined physical location (plant, building, field site). Referencing the logistics location ensures field technicians, parts dispatchers, and planners share
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Work order operations consuming procured spare parts are confirmed against goods receipts. Warehouse and maintenance teams use this link to verify parts availability and confirm material consumption a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_item. Business justification: In SAP PM, work order operations are created from maintenance items defined within a maintenance plan. Linking work_order_operation.maintenance_item_id → maintenance_item.maintenance_item_id provides
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_plan. Business justification: In EAM/CMMS systems (SAP PM), work order operations are generated from maintenance plans during scheduling. Linking work_order_operation.maintenance_plan_id → maintenance_plan.maintenance_plan_id enab
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.production_order_cost. Business justification: Maintenance operations performed on production equipment are often settled against production order costs. Manufacturing finance teams link work order operations to production order cost objects to ca
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to asset.task_list. Business justification: In SAP PM/EAM, work order operations are derived from standardized task list templates. The existing standard_text_key STRING attribute is a loose reference to task text; replacing it with a proper ta
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Cost accounting activity type code used to classify the labor cost of this operation for internal order settlement and cost center allocation (e.g., MAINT_LABOR, INSP_LABOR, OVHL_LABOR).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Activity Type (LSTAR); IFRS 16 - Asset Maintenance Cost Allocation

description: Actual elapsed time recorded for completing this operation. Used for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) analysis, scheduling accuracy assessment, and maintenance performance benchmarking.
reference: Maximo EAM - Actual Duration; SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Actual Duration; ISO 55001 - Asset Performance

description: Date and time when this operation was confirmed as completed by the executing technician or supervisor. Triggers downstream processes such as cost settlement and equipment return to service.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Actual Finish (IEDD/IETD); Maximo EAM - Actual Finish

description: Actual number of labor hours recorded upon confirmation of this operation step. Compared against estimated hours for variance analysis, MTTR tracking, and future planning calibration.
reference: Maximo EAM - Actual Labor Hours; SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Actual Work (ISMNW)

description: Date and time when execution of this operation actually commenced, as recorded by the technician or MES system. Used for schedule adherence analysis and MTTR calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Actual Start (ISDD/ISTD); Maximo EAM - Actual Start

description: Free-text remarks entered by the technician or supervisor at the time of operation confirmation, describing findings, deviations from plan, or conditions observed during execution.
reference: Maximo EAM - Work Log; SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Confirmation Text; ISO 9001 - Record of Work

description: Reference number of the time confirmation document recorded against this operation in the EAM/CMMS system. Links the operation to labor time postings and cost accounting entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Confirmation Number (RUECK); Maximo EAM - Labor Transaction

description: SAP PM control key that governs how the operation is processed — whether it is internally performed, externally procured, or a milestone operation. Determines scheduling, costing, and confirmation behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Control Key (STEUS)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the estimated and actual operation costs are expressed. Supports multi-currency financial reporting in multinational manufacturing environments.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes; IFRS - Multi-Currency Reporting

description: General ledger cost element code to which the labor and resource costs of this operation are posted. Enables OPEX tracking and maintenance cost analysis by operation type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module - Cost Element (KSTAR); GAAP - Maintenance Expense Classification

description: Trade or skill discipline required to perform this operation (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation). Enables multi-craft coordination and ensures qualified technicians are assigned.
reference: Maximo EAM - Craft; SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Qualification Requirements

description: Date and time when this work order operation record was created in the source EAM/CMMS system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: ISO 9001 - Record Control; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity and Accountability

description: Planned cost for executing this operation step, including labor and any directly assigned resources. Used for work order budget control and CAPEX/OPEX planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM/CO Module - Planned Cost; IFRS - Asset Maintenance Cost; GAAP - OPEX

description: Planned elapsed time (wall-clock duration) to complete this operation, distinct from labor hours when multiple technicians work in parallel. Used for scheduling and equipment downtime planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Duration (DAUNO); Maximo EAM - Scheduled Duration

description: Planned number of labor hours required to complete this operation step, as defined during work order planning. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and cost estimation.
reference: Maximo EAM - Planned Labor Hours; SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Work (ARBEI)

description: Purchase order or service order number raised for externally performed operations where a third-party contractor or service provider executes the maintenance task. Links to procurement records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM/MM Module - External Service (BANFN); SAP Ariba - Service Order

description: Indicates whether this operation is a milestone step whose completion triggers automatic progression of the work order status or downstream workflow actions (e.g., safety permit release, quality inspection gate).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Milestone (MLST); Maximo EAM - Milestone Task

description: Indicates whether this operation involves safety-critical activities requiring special permits, lockout/tagout (LOTO) procedures, confined space entry, or other regulatory safety controls.
reference: ISO 45001 - Hazard Identification; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 - Lockout/Tagout; IEC 62443 - Industrial Safety

description: Date and time of the most recent update to this work order operation record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001 - Record Control; SAP S/4HANA - Change Document; Maximo EAM - Change Log

description: Number of technicians or workers required to simultaneously execute this operation. Used for crew sizing, shift planning, and labor cost estimation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Number of Capacities (ANZZL); Maximo EAM - Crew Size
