description: Name or employee ID of the procurement professional responsible for managing this contract, including renewals, compliance monitoring, and supplier performance.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; ISM Procurement Standards

description: SAP payment terms key defining the payment schedule, discount periods, and due date calculation for invoices issued under this contract (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS; GAAP

description: Textual description of the penalty terms, including trigger conditions, calculation method, and maximum liability cap as negotiated with the supplier.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: Indicates whether the contract contains penalty clauses for supplier non-performance, late delivery, or quality failures. Triggers penalty tracking and enforcement workflows.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether the contract covers direct materials (production-related), indirect materials, services, capital expenditure (CAPEX), or maintenance repair and operations (MRO).
reference: SAP Ariba; ISM Procurement Classification

description: SAP purchasing group (buyer group) responsible for day-to-day management and release of purchase orders against this contract.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM

description: SAP purchasing organization responsible for negotiating and managing this contract. Defines the organizational scope and authority for contract execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ANSI/ISM Procurement Standards

description: Indicates whether the contract mandates supplier compliance with the EU REACH regulation (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals).
reference: EU REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; SAP Ariba

description: Cumulative monetary value of purchase orders released against this contract to date. Compared against total contract value to monitor consumption and remaining commitment.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Number of calendar days prior to contract expiry by which a renewal or termination notice must be issued. Used to trigger automated renewal reminders.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: Indicates whether the contract mandates supplier compliance with the EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) directive for all delivered materials.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; SAP Ariba

description: Corresponding SAP S/4HANA MM outline agreement number (scheduling agreement or contract) linked to this procurement contract for purchase order release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Reference to the SAP Ariba sourcing event (RFQ/RFP) that resulted in the award of this contract. Provides traceability from sourcing to contract.
reference: SAP Ariba Sourcing

description: Current lifecycle status of the procurement contract. Drives procurement eligibility, MRP source list activation, and compliance reporting.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: Actual date on which the contract was terminated prior to its scheduled expiry, if applicable. Populated only for contracts with status 'terminated'.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: Reason code explaining why the contract was terminated before its scheduled end date. Used for supplier performance analysis and sourcing strategy review.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; ISO 9001

description: Descriptive title of the procurement contract summarizing the scope of supply or service covered by the agreement.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: Maximum total monetary value committed under this contract over its full validity period, in the contract currency. Used for spend commitment tracking and financial planning.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; IFRS 15; GAAP

description: Classification of the procurement contract by its commercial structure. Determines release mechanism, pricing model, and SAP outline agreement category.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the controlling area record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Controlling Area Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: controlling_area.chart_of_accounts (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the chart_of_accounts master. A controlling area is configured with a specific chart of accounts for cost accounting. Adding
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Indicates whether activity type accounting is activated for this controlling area. When true, cost centers can record and allocate costs based on activity quantities (e.g., machine hours, labor hours), enabling activity-based costing and product cost calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Module - Activity Types; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Default internal cost allocation method configured for this controlling area. Governs how overhead costs are distributed across cost centers, orders, and profit centers. Options include assessment (with secondary cost elements), distribution (with primary cost elements), indirect activity allocation, and template allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Module; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Identifier of the CAPEX budget profile assigned to this controlling area, defining the rules for capital expenditure budgeting, availability control, and tolerance limits for investment orders and WBS elements. Supports CAPEX governance and IFRS IAS 16 compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-IM Module - Investment Management; IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP ASC 360

description: Default controlling version used for plan/actual comparisons within this controlling area. Version '0' is the standard actual version; other versions (e.g., '1', '2') represent planning versions for budget scenarios, forecasts, or what-if analyses.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - CO Version Settings (OKEQ); IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 270

description: Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the controlling area within SAP CO. Typically a 1-4 character uppercase code (e.g., 'CO01', 'AMER'). This is the business key used in all CO transactions and cost accounting documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - OKKS/OKKP Controlling Area Configuration

description: SAP assignment type governing how company codes are linked to the controlling area. Value '1' = controlling area and company codes use the same fiscal year variant and chart of accounts; Value '2' = company codes may have different fiscal year variants (cross-company code controlling).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - OKKP Assignment of Company Codes

description: Type of Profitability Analysis configured for this controlling area. 'costing_based' uses value fields for flexible margin analysis; 'account_based' aligns with G/L accounts for IFRS-compliant segment reporting; 'both' enables parallel CO-PA for comprehensive profitability management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA Module; IFRS IFRS 8 Operating Segments; GAAP ASC 280

description: Name or identifier of the standard hierarchy for cost centers within this controlling area. The standard hierarchy defines the top-level grouping structure for all cost centers, enabling hierarchical cost reporting, allocation cycles, and management reporting roll-ups.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Module - Cost Center Standard Hierarchy (OKEON); IFRS IAS 1

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the primary country of the controlling area. Used for statutory reporting, regulatory compliance, and geographic segmentation in management reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 280

description: Timestamp when the controlling area master record was first created in the source system (SAP CO). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Log; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the controlling area currency used for all internal cost accounting, profitability analysis, and management reporting within this controlling area (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Currency Settings; IFRS IAS 21

description: SAP currency type code defining the role of the controlling area currency in multi-currency accounting. Standard values: 10=Company Code Currency, 20=Controlling Area Currency, 30=Group Currency, 40=Hard Currency, 50=Index-Based Currency, 60=Global Company Currency. Governs how currency translation is applied in CO documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Currency Type Configuration; IFRS IAS 21 Foreign Currency Transactions

description: Extended free-text description of the controlling area, providing additional context about its organizational scope, geographic coverage, or business purpose beyond the short name field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - OKKP Controlling Area Configuration

description: SAP fiscal year variant code (e.g., 'K4' for calendar year, 'V3' for April-March) that defines the fiscal year structure — number of posting periods, special periods, and period-to-calendar-month mapping — applied across all company codes assigned to this controlling area.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module - Fiscal Year Variant (OB29); IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 270

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the corporate group reporting currency used in consolidated financial statements and group-level management reporting (e.g., 'USD' for a US-headquartered multinational). Supports multi-currency consolidation under IFRS and GAAP.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; GAAP ASC 830

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the primary language used for text descriptions, reports, and correspondence within this controlling area (e.g., 'EN' for English, 'DE' for German, 'ZH' for Chinese). Supports multi-language operations in multinational environments.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Language Settings

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the controlling area master record in the source system. Supports change detection, incremental data loading, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Log; GDPR Article 5; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local (functional) currency of the primary legal entity or region associated with this controlling area. Used for statutory reporting and local tax compliance.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: Full descriptive name of the controlling area as configured in SAP CO. Used in reports, dashboards, and management accounting outputs to identify the organizational scope of cost accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - OKKP Controlling Area Configuration

description: Total number of regular posting periods defined for the fiscal year variant of this controlling area (typically 12 for calendar-year companies, 13 for 4-4-5 fiscal calendars). Determines the granularity of period-based cost reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Fiscal Year Variant; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 270

description: Number of special (adjustment) posting periods beyond the regular periods, used for year-end closing adjustments, audit corrections, and statutory adjustments. Typically 1-4 special periods are configured.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Fiscal Year Variant; IFRS IAS 10 Events After Reporting Period; GAAP ASC 855

description: Indicates whether internal order management (CO-OPA) is activated for this controlling area. When true, internal orders can be used to collect and settle costs for specific projects, campaigns, or capital expenditure requests.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OPA Module; IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP ASC 360

description: Primary planning version identifier used for budget and forecast postings within this controlling area. Enables separation of plan data from actuals and supports multi-scenario financial planning (e.g., base plan, revised forecast, stretch target).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - CO Version Settings (OKEQ); IFRS IAS 1

description: Indicates whether Product Cost Controlling (CO-PC) is activated for this controlling area. When true, standard cost estimates, work-in-progress (WIP) calculations, and variance analysis for manufactured products are enabled.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Module; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: Indicates whether Profit Center Accounting (CO-PCA) is activated for this controlling area. When true, all CO postings are simultaneously updated in profit center ledgers, enabling segment-level P&L and balance sheet reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA Module; IFRS IFRS 8 Operating Segments; GAAP ASC 280

description: Name or identifier of the standard hierarchy for profit centers within this controlling area. Defines the organizational structure for profit center accounting, enabling segment-level profitability reporting and internal P&L analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA Module - Profit Center Standard Hierarchy (KCH5N); IFRS IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: Indicates whether Profitability Analysis (CO-PA) is activated for this controlling area. When true, revenues and costs are analyzed by market segments (e.g., customer, product, region) to support EBITDA reporting and contribution margin analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA Module; IFRS IFRS 8; GAAP ASC 280

description: Internal organizational region code (e.g., 'AMER', 'EMEA', 'APAC') to which this controlling area belongs. Supports regional management reporting, cost allocation hierarchies, and geographic profitability analysis.
reference: Internal Organizational Hierarchy; IFRS IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: Default settlement profile for this controlling area, defining the rules for settling costs from internal orders, WBS elements, and production orders to receivers (cost centers, G/L accounts, assets, profitability segments). Critical for period-end closing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Settlement Profile (OKO7); IFRS IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this controlling area master data was sourced (e.g., 'SAP_S4HANA_PROD', 'SAP_S4HANA_EU'). Supports multi-system data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; NIST Cybersecurity Framework - Data Provenance

description: Current operational status of the controlling area. 'active' indicates the area is live and accepting CO postings; 'inactive' indicates it is suspended; 'decommissioned' indicates it has been retired from use.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Controlling Area Lifecycle Management

description: IANA time zone identifier (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/Berlin') for the controlling area, used to correctly interpret period-end closing timestamps, posting cutoff times, and scheduling of CO batch jobs across global operations.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; SAP S/4HANA System Settings

description: Identifier of the transfer price variant configured for this controlling area, governing how intercompany and intra-company goods and service transfers are priced for internal cost accounting and profit center reporting. Supports arm's-length pricing compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Transfer Pricing; IFRS IAS 24 Related Party Disclosures; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the controlling area spans multiple company codes (cross-company) or is assigned to a single company code (single-company). Cross-company controlling areas enable unified cost accounting across legal entities within a multinational group.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - OKKP Controlling Area Assignment Type

description: The date from which this controlling area configuration is effective and active for CO postings. Supports temporal validity tracking and historical configuration management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - OKKP Validity Period; IFRS IAS 1

description: The date until which this controlling area configuration remains effective. A null or maximum date (9999-12-31) indicates the configuration is open-ended and currently active.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - OKKP Validity Period; IFRS IAS 1

description: Default variance key assigned to this controlling area, defining which variance categories (e.g., price variance, quantity variance, efficiency variance) are calculated during production order settlement and product cost variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Module - Variance Calculation; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each cost allocation transaction record within the period-end close cycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Internal Order / Cost Center Allocation

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_classification. Business justification: Cost allocations for warranty, service, and after-sales support in manufacturing are distributed based on customer account classification (e.g., strategic, standard, OEM). Controlling uses this to all
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: cost_allocation.controlling_area (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the controlling_area master. Cost allocations are executed within a controlling area scope. Adding controlling_area_id FK norma
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: cost_allocation uses fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) as separate denormalized fields. Adding fiscal_period_id FK creates a direct relational link to the fiscal_period master for period-end a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Cost allocations in manufacturing are executed at the plant level — overhead, utilities, and labor are allocated to product-plant combinations. Cost accountants run plant-level allocations every perio
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profitability_segment. Business justification: cost_allocation.segment (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the CO-PA profitability segment that receives or originates cost allocations. Internal cost allocation cycles in SAP CO distribute costs
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The monetary amount allocated from the sender to the receiver in the transaction currency. Represents the cost portion distributed during the allocation cycle execution.
reference: IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 420; SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA

description: The allocated amount converted to the company code's local currency. Used for statutory financial reporting and legal entity consolidation.
reference: IFRS IAS 21 Foreign Currency; GAAP ASC 830

description: The allocated amount expressed in the controlling area currency, used for management accounting and cross-company cost analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS IAS 21

description: The numeric quantity of the allocation basis used to determine the receiver's share (e.g., headcount, floor area in m², machine hours, kilowatt-hours). Applicable when allocation method is statistical key figure or activity quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA Statistical Key Figures; IFRS IAS 2

description: The unit of measure for the allocation basis quantity (e.g., EA for headcount, H for hours, M2 for square meters, KWH for kilowatt-hours). Provides context for interpreting the allocation basis quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; ISO 80000 Units of Measurement

description: The business date on which the cost allocation is executed and posted. Determines the period assignment for the allocation transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS IAS 1 Accrual Basis

description: Business document number assigned by the controlling system (e.g., SAP CO) to uniquely identify the allocation posting document within a fiscal period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA (Cost Center Accounting)

description: The specific method used to determine the allocation basis: percentage (fixed split), equivalence numbers (weighted ratios), statistical key figures (e.g., headcount, floor area), fixed amount, activity quantity, or plan values.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; GAAP ASC 420; IFRS IAS 2

description: The percentage share of total sender costs allocated to this specific receiver. Applicable when allocation method is percentage-based. Values between 0 and 100.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IFRS IAS 2

description: Classification of the cost allocation method used: distribution (reallocates primary cost elements), assessment (uses secondary cost elements), reposting (manual correction), overhead calculation (surcharge-based), activity allocation (quantity-based), or template allocation (formula-based).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IFRS IAS 2 Cost Absorption

description: Indicates whether the allocated cost is classified as capital expenditure (CAPEX) or operational expenditure (OPEX). Critical for balance sheet vs. income statement treatment and IFRS IAS 16 compliance.
reference: IFRS IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 1

description: The legal entity company code under which this cost allocation is posted. Enables multi-entity financial reporting and intercompany cost allocation tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 27 Consolidated Financial Statements

description: The timestamp when this cost allocation record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 Accountability; ISO 9001

description: Name of the allocation or assessment cycle as defined in the controlling system (e.g., SAP CO cycle name). Identifies the configured rule set used to distribute costs during period-end close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA Allocation Cycle

description: The foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the company code currency at the time of allocation posting.
reference: IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The fiscal period (accounting period) within the fiscal year in which this allocation is posted. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, with special periods 13-16 for year-end adjustments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 34; GAAP ASC 270

description: The fiscal year in which this cost allocation is recorded. Used for period-end close reporting and annual financial consolidation.
reference: IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 270

description: The functional area classification of the allocation (e.g., production, administration, sales, R&D). Used for cost-of-sales accounting and functional income statement reporting under IFRS.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Functional Classification; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this cost allocation record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading and change data capture in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 9001; NIST SP 800-53

description: Reference to the original allocation document number that this record reverses or corrects. Populated only when reversal_flag is true, enabling audit trail linkage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS IAS 8; SOX Section 404

description: Distinguishes whether this allocation record represents a planned allocation (budget/forecast), an actual posted allocation, or a commitment. Supports plan vs. actual variance analysis in management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP

description: The date on which the allocation document is posted to the general ledger and controlling module. May differ from allocation_date in cases of retroactive adjustments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS IAS 1

description: The cost center receiving the allocated costs. Represents the organizational unit absorbing the shared or overhead costs from the sender.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IFRS IAS 2

description: Work Breakdown Structure element receiving the allocated costs, used for capital project cost absorption and CAPEX tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS (Project System); IFRS IAS 16 CAPEX

description: Indicates whether this allocation record is a reversal of a previously posted allocation. True when the record represents a corrective reversal posting during period-end adjustment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS IAS 8 Accounting Policies

description: The cost element (GL account in controlling) from which costs are being sent. For assessments, this is typically a secondary cost element; for distributions, it is the original primary cost element.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CEL (Cost Element Accounting)

description: The cost element group defining the range of cost elements included in the sender pool for this allocation cycle segment. Used when multiple cost elements are allocated together.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA Cycle Segment

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this allocation record was extracted (e.g., SAP_S4HANA_PROD). Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the lakehouse.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 Data Provenance; SOX Section 404

description: The statistical key figure code used as the allocation tracing factor (e.g., HEADCOUNT, FLOORAREA, MACHHR). Statistical key figures are non-monetary quantities that drive cost distribution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA Statistical Key Figures

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the currency in which the allocation amount is originally expressed.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21

description: The controlling version under which this allocation is recorded (e.g., version 0 for actuals, version 1 for plan). Enables parallel valuation and plan/actual comparisons in management accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM Version Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each currency exchange rate record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: IAS 21 – The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; IFRS Foundation

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: currency_exchange_rate.controlling_area_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the controlling_area master. Exchange rates can be scoped to controlling areas in SAP. Adding controlling_area_id F
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: currency_exchange_rate has denormalized fiscal_period (STRING) and fiscal_year (STRING) attributes that reference the fiscal calendar. Exchange rates are period-specific (monthly average rates, period
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Workflow approval status for the exchange rate record, indicating whether it has been reviewed and authorized by the treasury or finance controller. Supports segregation of duties and internal control requirements under SOX and IFRS governance frameworks.
reference: SOX Section 404; IAS 21; IFRS Foundation; SAP S/4HANA FI Workflow

description: Name or user identifier of the treasury officer or finance controller who approved this exchange rate for use in financial postings. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and internal control frameworks.
reference: SOX Section 404; IAS 21; IFRS Foundation

description: Date and time when the exchange rate record was formally approved for use in financial transactions and consolidation. Part of the audit trail for internal controls and regulatory compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; IAS 21; IFRS Foundation

description: For average-type exchange rates, specifies the mathematical method used to compute the average (e.g., simple average of daily closing rates, weighted average by transaction volume). Required for IAS 21 compliance when using average rates for P&L translation.
reference: IAS 21 paragraph 22; IFRS Foundation; GAAP ASC 830

description: SAP company code identifying the legal entity or organizational unit for which this exchange rate is specifically configured. Some rate types (e.g., budget, intercompany) may be company-code-specific while standard market rates are global.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code; IAS 21; IFRS Foundation

description: Indicates whether this exchange rate is designated for use in group financial consolidation and translation of subsidiary financial statements into the group reporting currency. Supports IAS 21 and IFRS 10 consolidation translation requirements.
reference: IAS 21 paragraphs 38-47; IFRS 10 – Consolidated Financial Statements; SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting

description: Date and time when this exchange rate record was first created in the source system or ingested into the lakehouse. Part of the standard audit trail for financial data governance and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; IAS 21; GDPR Article 5(2) – Accountability; IFRS Foundation

description: Concatenated display representation of the currency pair in standard FX market notation (e.g., USD/EUR, GBP/JPY). Used for reporting, dashboards, and FX analytics. Derived from from_currency_code and to_currency_code but stored for query performance.
reference: ISO 4217; IAS 21; Reuters/Bloomberg FX market conventions

description: The numeric exchange rate expressing how many units of the to-currency are equivalent to one unit of the from-currency. High precision (10 decimal places) is required for currencies with large denomination differences (e.g., JPY/IDR pairs) and to avoid rounding errors in large-value financial transactions.
reference: IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA TCURR.UKURS; ISO 4217; IFRS Foundation

description: ISO 4217 three-letter code of the source currency being converted from (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Represents the transaction or base currency in the exchange rate pair.
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA TCURR.FCURR

description: The reciprocal of the exchange rate (1 / exchange_rate), expressing how many units of the from-currency equal one unit of the to-currency. Stored explicitly to avoid division-by-zero errors and to support bidirectional conversion lookups in financial systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TCURR.FFACT/TFACT ratio logic; IAS 21; IFRS Foundation

description: Indicates whether this exchange rate was derived via triangulation through a third reference currency (e.g., EUR as the pivot currency for non-EUR pairs within the Eurozone). Required for EU regulatory compliance and for auditing cross-rate derivation methodology.
reference: EU Council Regulation (EC) No 1103/97 – Euro Triangulation; IAS 21; ECB

description: Date and time of the most recent update to this exchange rate record, whether in the source system or the lakehouse. Supports change detection, incremental loading, and audit trail requirements for financial data governance.
reference: SOX Section 404; IAS 21; GDPR Article 5(2); IFRS Foundation

description: Indicates whether the exchange rate is expressed as a direct quotation (units of domestic currency per one unit of foreign currency) or indirect quotation (units of foreign currency per one unit of domestic currency). Affects how the rate is applied in currency conversion calculations.
reference: ISO 4217; IAS 21; BIS (Bank for International Settlements) FX conventions

description: Categorizes the rate as mid-market, bank buying, bank selling, official central bank rate, or parallel market rate. Relevant for countries with multiple official exchange rate regimes and for treasury operations distinguishing between buy/sell rates.
reference: IAS 21; BIS; IMF Article IV; Central Bank regulations

description: The specific market date on which the exchange rate was observed or published by the rate source. For spot rates, this is the trade date. For average rates, this may be the last day of the averaging period. Distinct from valid_from_date which governs ERP applicability.
reference: IAS 21; ECB Reference Rates publication date; Reuters; Bloomberg

description: Business natural key uniquely identifying a rate record, composed of from-currency code, to-currency code, rate type, and valid-from date (e.g., USD_EUR_SPOT_2024-01-01). Supports idempotent upserts and deduplication in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI – Exchange Rate Table (TCURR); IAS 21

description: The number of from-currency units used as the base for the exchange rate quotation (e.g., 100 for currencies quoted per 100 units such as JPY). Supports indirect quotation conventions where the rate is expressed per N units rather than per 1 unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TCURR.FFACT; ISO 4217 quotation conventions; IAS 21

description: The number of to-currency units used as the target for the exchange rate quotation. Together with rate_ratio_from, defines the exact quotation convention (e.g., 1 USD = 100 JPY expressed as ratio 1:100). Required for accurate cross-rate calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TCURR.TFACT; ISO 4217 quotation conventions; IAS 21

description: The originating provider or feed from which the exchange rate was obtained. Common sources include ECB (European Central Bank), Reuters, Bloomberg, central bank feeds, IMF, World Bank, internal treasury, or manual entry. Critical for audit trails and rate governance under IAS 21 and group consolidation policies.
reference: IAS 21; ECB Reference Rates; Reuters/Refinitiv; Bloomberg; IMF; IFRS Foundation

description: Specific reference identifier or URL from the rate source (e.g., ECB publication reference, Reuters RIC code, Bloomberg ticker, or central bank bulletin reference). Provides traceability for audit and regulatory compliance purposes.
reference: IAS 21; ECB Statistical Data Warehouse; IFRS Foundation; SOX Section 404

description: The precise date and time at which the exchange rate was captured or published, including timezone offset. Critical for intraday spot rates and for reconciling rates across time zones in a multinational treasury environment.
reference: IAS 21; Reuters/Refinitiv intraday feeds; Bloomberg; IFRS Foundation

description: Classification of the exchange rate indicating its intended use: spot (real-time market rate), average (period average for P&L translation), closing (period-end balance sheet rate), budget (planning rate), historical (original transaction rate), intercompany (internal transfer pricing rate), or hedge (hedging instrument rate). Aligns with IAS 21 translation requirements.
reference: IAS 21 – The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA TCURR.KURST; IFRS 9 – Financial Instruments

description: Short system code identifying the exchange rate type as configured in the ERP (e.g., 'M' for standard translation, 'P' for budget, 'B' for bank buying rate in SAP S/4HANA). Enables direct mapping to source system rate type keys.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI – Exchange Rate Type (TCURV.KURST); IAS 21

description: Indicates whether this exchange rate is designated for use in foreign currency revaluation runs (e.g., month-end balance sheet revaluation of open items and balances). Supports IAS 21 revaluation processes in SAP S/4HANA FI.
reference: IAS 21 paragraphs 23-24; SAP S/4HANA FI Foreign Currency Valuation (FAGL_FC_VAL); IFRS Foundation

description: Identifies the operational system or feed from which this exchange rate record was ingested into the lakehouse (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI, Reuters feed, Bloomberg API, ECB data warehouse, manual treasury upload). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: NIST SP 800-53 – Data Provenance; SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS Foundation

description: The primary key or unique record identifier of this exchange rate as it exists in the originating source system (e.g., SAP TCURR composite key). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TCURR composite key (MANDT/KURST/FCURR/TCURR/GDATU); NIST SP 800-53

description: The bid-ask spread applied to the mid-market rate to derive the buying or selling rate used in actual transactions. Relevant for bank-fed rates and treasury operations where transaction costs are embedded in the rate.
reference: IAS 21; BIS FX Market Conventions; Reuters/Bloomberg

description: Current lifecycle status of the exchange rate record. Active rates are available for transaction processing; superseded rates have been replaced by newer entries; pending_approval rates await treasury or finance controller sign-off before use in official postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate Management; IAS 21; SOX Section 404 – Internal Controls

description: ISO 4217 three-letter code of the target currency being converted to (e.g., EUR, USD, JPY). Represents the reporting or group currency in the exchange rate pair.
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA TCURR.TCURR

description: ISO 4217 code of the intermediate reference currency used in triangulation rate derivation (typically EUR for Eurozone cross-rates). Populated only when is_triangulation_rate is true.
reference: EU Council Regulation (EC) No 1103/97; IAS 21; ECB Reference Rates

description: The calendar date from which this exchange rate is effective and applicable for currency conversion. Used to select the correct rate for a given transaction date. Supports temporal validity management in multi-period financial reporting.
reference: IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA TCURR.GDATU; IFRS Foundation

description: The calendar date until which this exchange rate remains effective. A null or high-date value (e.g., 9999-12-31) indicates the rate is currently active with no defined expiry. Supports SCD Type 2 temporal management in the silver layer.
reference: IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate Management; IFRS Foundation

description: Unique surrogate key identifying a fiscal period record in the financial calendar master. Used as the primary reference for all financial postings, period-end close workflows, and reporting period assignments across legal entities.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 - Presentation of Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 205

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: fiscal_period.controlling_area_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the controlling_area master. Fiscal periods are scoped to controlling areas in SAP. Adding controlling_area_id FK normalizes
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the immediately preceding fiscal period record. Enables period-over-period variance analysis, prior period comparisons in financial statements, and sequential period navigation in reporting tools without requiring complex date calculations.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 - Comparative Information; GAAP ASC 205

description: The accounting standard framework under which this fiscal period is reported. 'IFRS' for International Financial Reporting Standards, 'GAAP' for US Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, 'IFRS_GAAP' for dual-reporting entities, 'LOCAL_GAAP' for local statutory reporting, 'STAT' for statutory-only periods.
reference: IFRS Foundation; FASB GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI Ledger Configuration

description: Flag indicating whether accounts payable postings (vendor invoices, payments, credit memos) are permitted for this fiscal period. Independently controlled from GL postings via the SAP posting period variant by account type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Posting Period; IFRS IAS 37; GAAP ASC 420

description: Flag indicating whether accounts receivable postings (customer invoices, payments, credit memos) are permitted for this fiscal period. Independently controlled from GL postings via the SAP posting period variant by account type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Posting Period; IFRS IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606

description: Flag indicating whether fixed asset accounting postings (acquisitions, retirements, depreciation runs) are permitted for this fiscal period. Asset accounting periods may be closed independently from other sub-ledgers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Posting Period; IFRS IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: Date and time when the period-end close process was fully completed and the period was officially closed for all postings. Used to measure close cycle time and track compliance with internal close calendar SLAs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Period Close; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: Date and time when the period-end close process was formally initiated. Marks the start of the close workflow including sub-ledger reconciliation, accrual posting, and management review activities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Period Close; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: The planned target date by which the fiscal period close should be completed, as defined in the financial close calendar. Used to monitor close cycle performance and identify delays against the internal close SLA.
reference: IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205; Internal Financial Close Calendar

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the legal entity's country of incorporation. Used to apply country-specific fiscal calendar rules, statutory reporting requirements, and tax period definitions.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IFRS IAS 1; Local Statutory Reporting Requirements

description: Date and time when this fiscal period record was created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and SOX compliance documentation of fiscal calendar master data changes.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the functional currency of the legal entity for this fiscal period. Used for multi-currency consolidation and foreign currency translation during period-end close.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; GAAP ASC 830

description: Calendar date on which the fiscal period ends. Defines the upper boundary for financial transactions and journal entries that can be posted to this period. Used in period-end close cutoff enforcement.
reference: IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205; SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period

description: The four-digit fiscal year to which this period belongs. May differ from the calendar year depending on the company's fiscal year-end configuration (e.g., fiscal year 2024 may span April 2023 to March 2024).
reference: IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205; SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant

description: Code identifying the fiscal year variant configuration that defines the fiscal calendar structure (e.g., K4 for calendar year with 4 special periods, V3 for April–March fiscal year). Determines how periods are mapped to calendar months and how special periods are handled.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant (OB29); IFRS IAS 1

description: Flag indicating whether general ledger journal entry postings are currently permitted for this fiscal period. Controlled by the posting period variant and period status. When false, all GL postings to this period are blocked.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period Variant (OB52); IFRS IAS 1

description: The fiscal half-year (1 or 2) to which this period belongs. Used for semi-annual financial reporting and interim statutory filings required in certain jurisdictions.
reference: IFRS IAS 34; GAAP ASC 270

description: Flag indicating whether this period is designated for audit adjustments and prior-period corrections. Adjustment periods allow authorized postings after the standard period close to accommodate external audit findings and statutory corrections.
reference: IFRS IAS 8 - Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors; GAAP ASC 250

description: Flag indicating whether this is a special adjustment period (periods 13–16 in SAP) used exclusively for year-end audit adjustments, closing entries, and statutory corrections. Special periods do not correspond to calendar months and are restricted to authorized finance users.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Special Periods; IFRS IAS 8; GAAP ASC 250

description: Flag indicating whether this period is the final closing period of the fiscal year. Triggers year-end close workflows including balance carryforward, retained earnings posting, and statutory financial statement preparation.
reference: IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205; SAP S/4HANA FI Year-End Closing

description: Date and time when this fiscal period record was last updated. Tracks changes to period status, posting permissions, or calendar definitions. Essential for audit trail and SOX internal controls over financial reporting.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: Code identifying the accounting ledger (leading or non-leading) in SAP S/4HANA to which this fiscal period applies. Supports parallel accounting where multiple ledgers are maintained for different accounting standards (e.g., IFRS ledger '0L', local GAAP ledger 'L1').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Parallel Accounting Ledger; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: Code identifying the legal entity (company code in SAP) for which this fiscal period definition applies. Supports multi-entity and multinational operations where different legal entities may have different fiscal calendars or period statuses.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: Flag indicating whether materials management postings (goods receipts, goods issues, inventory movements) are permitted for this fiscal period. MM period close is managed separately from FI period close in SAP and must be aligned for COGS and inventory valuation accuracy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Posting Period (MMPV/MMRV); IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330

description: Human-readable name of the fiscal period (e.g., 'January 2024', 'Q1 FY2024', 'Period 01 FY2024'). Used in financial reports, dashboards, and period-end close communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Calendar; IFRS IAS 1

description: Sequential number of the period within the fiscal year. Standard periods are 1–12 (monthly) or 1–4 (quarterly). Periods 13–16 are reserved for special year-end adjustment periods used for audit corrections and closing entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 250

description: Abbreviated alphanumeric code for the fiscal period used in financial reports, dashboards, and system interfaces (e.g., 'JAN24', 'Q1FY24', 'P01.2024'). Provides a compact, human-readable period identifier for display in Power BI reports and management dashboards.
reference: Internal Financial Reporting Standards; Microsoft Power BI Reporting

description: Classification of the fiscal period granularity. 'monthly' for standard 12-period calendars, 'quarterly' for 4-period calendars, 'semi_annual' for 2-period calendars, 'annual' for single-period fiscal years, and 'special' for year-end adjustment periods (periods 13–16 in SAP).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: SAP posting period variant code that controls which account types (assets, customers, vendors, general ledger) are open for posting in this period. Maps directly to the SAP FI posting period variant configuration (transaction OB52) and determines posting authorization by account type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period Variant (OB52); IFRS IAS 1

description: The fiscal quarter (1–4) to which this period belongs. Derived from the period number based on the fiscal year variant. Used for quarterly financial reporting, EBITDA reporting, and regulatory filings.
reference: IFRS IAS 34 - Interim Financial Reporting; GAAP ASC 270; SEC Quarterly Reporting

description: Business justification provided when a closed fiscal period is reopened for additional postings. Documents the reason for the exception (e.g., 'External audit adjustment', 'Prior period error correction per IAS 8'). Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.
reference: IFRS IAS 8; GAAP ASC 250; SOX Section 302/404

description: Date and time when a previously closed fiscal period was reopened for additional postings. Populated only when a period has been reopened after closure, typically for audit adjustments or error corrections. Null if the period has never been reopened.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period; IFRS IAS 8; GAAP ASC 250

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the group reporting (presentation) currency used for consolidated financial statements. Enables multi-currency consolidation where local functional currency differs from the group reporting currency.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: Calendar date on which the fiscal period begins. Defines the lower boundary for financial transactions and journal entries that can be posted to this period.
reference: IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205; SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period

description: Current operational status of the fiscal period controlling whether journal entries and financial postings are permitted. 'open' allows posting; 'closed' prevents new postings; 'blocked' temporarily restricts posting; 'in_closing' indicates period-end close is in progress; 'reopened' indicates a previously closed period that has been re-opened for corrections; 'archived' indicates the period is fully closed and archived.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period Variant (OB52); IFRS IAS 8; GAAP ASC 250

description: The tax reporting period number corresponding to this fiscal period, used for VAT/GST returns, withholding tax filings, and other statutory tax submissions. May differ from the financial posting period number in jurisdictions with different tax calendars.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Period; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740; Local Tax Authority Requirements

description: Number of calendar weeks contained within this fiscal period. Relevant for 4-4-5, 4-5-4, or 5-4-4 fiscal calendar configurations used in manufacturing and retail industries where periods are defined in weeks rather than calendar months.
reference: IFRS IAS 1; Internal Fiscal Calendar Configuration; Manufacturing Industry 4-4-5 Calendar

description: Number of business working days within this fiscal period, excluding weekends and public holidays. Used for production planning, labor cost accruals, depreciation calculations, and period-over-period performance normalization in manufacturing operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Factory Calendar; ISO 8601; Internal HR and Production Planning Standards

description: Primary key for goods_receipt

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Goods receipts automatically generate journal entries (inventory debit / GR-IR credit) in finance. This is a core three-way match process used daily in manufacturing accounting.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Goods receipts must specify which storage location the material is being received into. Warehouse and receiving teams use this to direct putaway. Standard in all ERP systems — goods receipt drives sto
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each logistics network location record in the silver layer master data.
reference: Internal Data Architecture Standard

description: Primary street address of the logistics location, including street number and street name. Used for carrier pickup/delivery instructions and customs documentation.
reference: USPS Addressing Standards; Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42

description: Secondary address detail such as suite, building, floor, or unit number for the logistics location.
reference: USPS Addressing Standards; Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42

description: City or municipality in which the logistics location is situated. Used in route planning, carrier zone determination, and regulatory reporting.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42

reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Module; Infor WMS

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the logistics location is physically situated. Drives customs, trade compliance, and incoterms applicability.

description: Timestamp when the logistics location master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and master data governance.

description: Expiration date of the customs bond or bonded warehouse license. Triggers renewal workflows to ensure uninterrupted bonded storage operations and trade compliance.

description: Total number of loading/unloading dock doors available at the logistics location. Used in capacity planning, appointment slot management, and freight order scheduling within TMS and WMS.

description: Date from which this logistics location record is valid and active in the logistics network. Supports temporal validity management for network planning and historical analysis.

description: Date after which this logistics location record is no longer valid or active in the logistics network. Used to manage temporary locations, lease expirations, and network restructuring events.

description: Official FTZ or SEZ designation number assigned by the relevant trade authority for locations within a free trade zone. Used in customs and export control documentation.

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for logistics locations that are air freight terminals or co-located with airports. Used in air freight routing, carrier service selection, and customs documentation.

description: Number of dock doors designated exclusively for inbound freight receiving operations. Supports inbound appointment scheduling and GRN (Goods Receipt Note) throughput planning.

description: Indicates whether the logistics location is a licensed customs bonded warehouse authorized to store imported goods before customs duties are paid. Critical for trade compliance routing and customs declaration management.

description: Indicates whether the logistics location is situated within a designated Free Trade Zone (FTZ) or Special Economic Zone (SEZ). Affects duty deferral, tariff treatment, and export control compliance.

description: Indicates whether the logistics location is certified and equipped to handle, store, or process hazardous materials (HAZMAT) in compliance with applicable regulations. Drives routing logic for hazmat shipments.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119; DOT 49 CFR Parts 171-180; IATA DGR; ADR (European Agreement on Dangerous Goods)

description: Indicates whether the logistics location has temperature-controlled storage or handling capabilities (cold chain, frozen, or climate-controlled). Required for routing temperature-sensitive products.
reference: GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 68/01); WHO Good Distribution Practices; ISO 22000

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the logistics location master record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization across TMS/WMS systems, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS 84 decimal degrees) of the logistics location. Used for geofencing in shipment tracking, distance calculations, and IIoT asset monitoring via Siemens MindSphere.
reference: WGS 84 (World Geodetic System 1984); ISO 6709

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS 84 decimal degrees) of the logistics location. Used in conjunction with latitude for geofencing, route optimization, and real-time tracking event correlation.

description: Maximum total weight in kilograms that the logistics location can handle or store at any given time. Used in freight order feasibility checks and load planning.
reference: Infor WMS; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.22 (Floor Load Limits)

description: Full descriptive name of the logistics location (e.g., 'Chicago Distribution Center', 'Hamburg Port of Entry'). Used in reporting, carrier communications, and shipment documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Module

description: Comma-separated list of days of the week the location is operational (e.g., 'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI'). Used by TMS to validate delivery windows and avoid scheduling shipments on non-operating days.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Factory Calendar; Infor WMS

description: Standard daily closing time of the logistics location in HH:MM (24-hour) format, expressed in the location's local timezone. Defines the latest permissible arrival or departure time for freight operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Scheduling; Infor WMS

description: Standard daily opening time of the logistics location in HH:MM (24-hour) format, expressed in the location's local timezone. Used for appointment scheduling, carrier pickup windows, and TMS cutoff enforcement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Scheduling; Infor WMS

description: Number of dock doors designated exclusively for outbound freight dispatch operations. Supports outbound shipment scheduling and carrier pickup coordination.
reference: Infor WMS Dock Management; SAP S/4HANA SD-WM

description: SAP S/4HANA plant code associated with this logistics location when the location corresponds to a manufacturing plant or distribution center managed in SAP. Enables cross-reference between logistics network master data and SAP MM/PP/WM modules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/WM Module; SAP Plant Organizational Structure

description: ZIP or postal code of the logistics location. Used by TMS for freight zone calculation, carrier rate lookup, and last-mile routing.

description: Internal logistics region or zone code (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER, LATAM) assigned to the location for freight planning, carrier contract scoping, and transport plan organization.
reference: Internal Logistics Network Design Standard; SAP TM Region Configuration

description: State, province, or administrative region of the logistics location. Required for domestic freight zone mapping and tax/regulatory jurisdiction determination.
reference: ISO 3166-2 Subdivision Codes

description: Current operational status of the logistics location. Inactive or decommissioned locations are excluded from TMS route optimization and carrier assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Module

description: Total volumetric storage capacity of the logistics location in cubic meters. Used for inventory planning, warehouse utilization reporting, and network capacity optimization.
reference: Infor WMS; ISO 3166 Warehouse Classification

description: Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius that the location's temperature-controlled storage can maintain. Used alongside temp_min_c to validate cold chain compatibility for inbound and outbound shipments.
reference: GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 68/01); ISO 22000

description: Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius that the location's temperature-controlled storage can maintain. Used to validate suitability for cold chain shipments with specific temperature requirements.
reference: GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 68/01); ISO 22000

description: IANA timezone identifier for the logistics location (e.g., 'America/Chicago', 'Europe/Berlin'). Used to correctly interpret operating hours, appointment windows, and shipment cutoff times in TMS scheduling.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database (TZDB)

description: Classification of the logistics node by its functional role in the supply chain network. Drives routing logic, dock assignment, customs handling, and TMS optimization rules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM/SD Module; Infor WMS; ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security

description: UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations) for the logistics location. Internationally recognized code used in customs declarations, bills of lading, and cross-border trade documentation.
reference: UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations) - UNECE Recommendation 16

description: SAP S/4HANA or Infor WMS warehouse number assigned to this logistics location for warehouse management operations. Links the logistics network node to its WMS configuration for inventory and dock management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Module; Infor WMS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each product cost estimate record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Standard cost estimates are built per product variant (e.g., voltage rating, frame size). Finance must tie each cost estimate to a specific variant to support variant-level profitability analysis and
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to production.production_version. Business justification: Product cost estimates are calculated for specific production versions (BOM/routing combinations) to determine standard costs. Cost accounting uses this for product costing and make-vs-buy decisions.

description: The reference quantity for which the cost estimate is calculated (e.g., 1 unit, 100 units). All cost component values in this record are expressed per this base quantity.

description: Unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Ensures correct interpretation of per-unit cost values in downstream reporting.

description: Alternative BOM number used in the cost estimate when multiple BOM alternatives exist for a material. Relevant for products with multiple production configurations or regional variants.

description: SAP company code representing the legal entity for which the cost estimate is created. Determines the currency, chart of accounts, and statutory reporting context.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all cost component values are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Corresponds to the company code currency for the legal entity.

description: The reference date used to determine prices and rates during the cost estimate calculation (e.g., material prices, activity rates). Controls which price conditions are selected from the system.

description: Unique identifier of the costing run in SAP CO-PC that generated this cost estimate. Used to trace the estimate back to its originating batch costing execution.

description: Current processing status of the cost estimate in SAP CO-PC. Only estimates with status 'released' are used for inventory valuation and standard cost rolls. Errors must be resolved before release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC

description: Classification of the cost estimate by its business purpose. Standard cost estimates feed inventory valuation; simulation estimates support what-if analysis; sales order costing supports margin analysis.

description: SAP costing variant that controls the parameters of the cost estimate, including the valuation strategy, costing type, and date control. Common variants include PPC1 (standard cost estimate) and PPP1 (modified standard cost).

description: Version number of the cost estimate within a costing variant. Allows multiple parallel cost estimates (e.g., plan vs. simulation) to coexist for the same material and period.

description: Timestamp when the cost estimate record was originally created in the source system (SAP CO-PC). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with IFRS and GAAP record-keeping requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC; IFRS; GAAP; ISO 9001

description: Flag indicating whether the cost estimate contains errors or warnings that prevented successful completion. Estimates with errors cannot be released for inventory valuation until resolved.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC

description: Currency exchange rate applied to translate the cost estimate from the company code currency to the group currency at the time of costing. Supports multi-currency consolidation and variance analysis.
reference: IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA CO-PC

description: Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for which this cost estimate applies. Supports monthly cost reporting, period-end COGS calculation, and inventory valuation.

description: Fiscal year for which this cost estimate is created and valid. Used for period-end close, annual standard cost roll, and year-over-year cost variance reporting.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the group reporting currency used in consolidated financial statements (e.g., USD, EUR).
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: Total standard cost translated into the group reporting currency (e.g., USD for a US-headquartered multinational). Supports consolidated COGS and inventory valuation reporting across legal entities.

description: Minimum transaction base amount threshold below which this tax code is not applied. Used for de minimis tax rules and small transaction exemptions in certain jurisdictions.
reference: Local Tax Authority Regulations; SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration

description: Percentage of the input tax that is non-deductible and cannot be reclaimed as input VAT credit (e.g., 50% non-deductible for mixed-use assets). Used in partial VAT recovery calculations and tax return preparation.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC Article 173; SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration

description: Defines the scope of plant-level applicability for this tax code — whether it applies to all manufacturing plants, a specific plant, or all plants within a given country. Supports plant-level tax determination in manufacturing operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI Integration; Manufacturing Tax Compliance Policy

description: Controls how the tax amount is posted in the general ledger: posted to a separate tax GL account, distributed to the expense/revenue line item, or not posted at all. Determines the accounting treatment of tax amounts in financial postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Account Determination; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740

description: Indicates whether this tax code is configured for use in purchasing (procurement) transactions within SAP MM/Ariba. When true, the code is available for assignment on purchase orders, goods receipts, and vendor invoices.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Tax Determination; SAP Ariba Procurement

description: Sub-national region or state code where the tax code applies (e.g., US state code 'CA' for California, 'TX' for Texas). Relevant for US sales tax, Canadian provincial tax, and other sub-national tax regimes.
reference: ISO 3166-2; US Sales Tax Regulations; Canadian GST/HST/PST Framework

description: Categorization of the tax code for statutory tax reporting purposes. Determines which tax return or regulatory report this tax code contributes to (e.g., VAT return box mapping, Intrastat, EC Sales List, withholding tax return). Supports automated tax return preparation.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; Intrastat Regulation (EC) 638/2004; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740

description: Indicates whether this tax code applies the reverse charge mechanism, where the tax liability is shifted from the supplier to the customer (common in B2B cross-border EU transactions and construction services). When true, the buyer self-accounts for VAT.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC Article 194-199; OECD International VAT/GST Guidelines

description: Indicates whether this tax code is configured for use in sales (order-to-cash) transactions within SAP SD. When true, the code is available for assignment on sales orders, billing documents, and customer invoices.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Tax Determination; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this tax code record was sourced (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI, SAP Ariba). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Manufacturing Data Governance Policy; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: The natural key or unique identifier of this tax code record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP tax code + company code composite key). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the system of record.
reference: Manufacturing Data Governance Policy; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Current operational status of the tax code. 'Active' codes are available for use in financial transactions. 'Inactive' codes are blocked from new postings. 'Superseded' codes have been replaced by updated tax codes following rate changes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Name of the tax authority or revenue agency responsible for administering this tax code (e.g., 'Bundeszentralamt für Steuern' for Germany, 'IRS' for USA, 'HMRC' for UK). Used for tax remittance and compliance reporting.
reference: Local Tax Authority Regulations; OECD Tax Administration Framework

description: Defines the basis on which the tax rate is applied: net amount (tax calculated on net invoice value), gross amount (tax calculated on gross including other taxes), tax-on-tax (cascading tax), or fixed amount. Determines the tax calculation logic for financial transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Condition Type; OECD International VAT/GST Guidelines

description: Categorization of the tax code by rate tier or exemption status. Supports VAT compliance reporting, tax return preparation, and cross-border transaction tax determination under EU VAT Directive and local tax laws.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; OECD International VAT/GST Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Description or code of the legal basis for tax exemption when the tax rate is zero or the category is 'exempt' (e.g., 'Intra-community supply Article 138 VAT Directive', 'Export supply', 'Medical exemption'). Required for statutory reporting and audit trail.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; Local Tax Authority Regulations; OECD International VAT/GST Guidelines

description: Grouping classification for tax codes used in tax reporting, analytics, and consolidation (e.g., 'EU_VAT', 'US_SALES_TAX', 'WITHHOLDING', 'EXCISE'). Supports aggregated tax reporting across jurisdictions and tax type consolidation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Group; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740

description: Code identifying the tax calculation procedure or tax schema to which this tax code belongs (e.g., SAP tax procedure TAXD for Germany, TAXUS for USA). Determines the sequence of condition types used in tax calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Procedure Configuration

description: Description of the reason for a tax rate change or new tax code creation (e.g., 'Legislative amendment effective 2024-01-01', 'COVID-19 temporary rate reduction', 'Post-Brexit UK VAT adjustment'). Provides audit trail for tax rate history.
reference: IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740; Local Tax Authority Regulations; ISO 9001 Change Management

description: The applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage (e.g., 19.0000 for 19% German VAT, 7.2500 for 7.25% California sales tax). Used in tax calculation on financial transactions and tax return filings.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; OECD Tax Database; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740

description: Reference code mapping this tax code to a specific box or line on the statutory tax return form (e.g., VAT return box number, sales tax schedule line). Enables automated population of tax returns from transaction data.
reference: Local Tax Authority Filing Requirements; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; GAAP ASC 740

description: Classification of the tax code by tax mechanism. Distinguishes between input VAT (recoverable tax on purchases), output VAT (tax charged on sales), withholding tax (tax deducted at source), sales tax, use tax, excise duty, and other tax types. Critical for VAT return preparation and tax compliance reporting.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Indicates whether this tax code is applicable to purchase transactions (accounts payable / input tax), sales transactions (accounts receivable / output tax), or both directions. Controls tax code availability in procurement and sales processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

description: The date from which this tax code and its associated rate become effective. Supports time-dependent tax rate management, enabling historical tax rate lookups and future rate changes to be pre-configured.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Validity; IFRS IAS 12; Local Tax Authority Regulations

description: The date until which this tax code and its associated rate remain effective. A null or maximum date value (9999-12-31) indicates an open-ended validity. Supports tax rate change management and historical compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Validity; IFRS IAS 12; Local Tax Authority Regulations

description: For withholding tax codes, specifies whether withholding tax is calculated and posted at the time of invoice posting, payment posting, or both. Applicable only when tax_type is 'withholding_tax'. Supports accurate WHT liability tracking and remittance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Extended Withholding Tax; OECD Model Tax Convention; Local WHT Regulations

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Approved Manufacturer List (AML) / Approved Vendor List (AVL) entry in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Architecture Standard

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: AML/AVL entries are qualified for specific BOM component positions. Linking approved_manufacturer to bom_line identifies which component (by BOM position) the manufacturer is approved to supply. This
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.classification. Business justification: Approved manufacturers are classified by product classification (e.g., electronic components, mechanical fasteners, raw materials) to enforce sourcing rules and quality requirements specific to each c
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: Changes to the Approved Manufacturer List (adding, removing, or modifying approved manufacturers) are governed by Engineering Change Notices in industrial manufacturing. Linking approved_manufacturer
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: For raw material and commodity components, AML entries are qualified against a material specification (e.g., a steel grade or alloy spec). Linking approved_manufacturer to material_specification provi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Classification of the manufacturer's role in the supply strategy for this component. Preferred indicates primary source; alternate is secondary; restricted requires engineering approval for use.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; Internal Supply Chain Risk Management Policy

description: Date on which the manufacturer was formally approved for the component following successful qualification testing and review.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1; APQP/PPAP Requirements

description: Date on which the manufacturer approval expires and re-qualification is required. Supports periodic supplier re-evaluation per quality management requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1; SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle Management

description: Current qualification status of the manufacturer for the specified component. Drives procurement eligibility and supply chain decisions.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1; SAP Ariba Supplier Qualification

description: Name or employee ID of the engineering or quality authority who formally approved this manufacturer-component combination on the AML/AVL.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1; Internal Engineering Approval Workflow

description: Date and time when the formal approval was recorded in the system, providing an audit trail for quality management compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Indicates whether the manufacturer has confirmed CE marking compliance for the part, required for sale in the European Economic Area.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; EU Decision 768/2008/EC

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the component is manufactured. Used for trade compliance, tariff classification, and supply chain risk assessment.
reference: ISO 3166-1; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006

description: Date and time when the approved manufacturer record was first created in the system. Provides full audit trail for quality management and data governance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or equivalent classification for the manufacturer's part under applicable export control regulations (EAR/ITAR).
reference: US Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ITAR 22 CFR 120-130; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant
description: Indicates whether the manufacturer's part complies with EU REACH regulation regarding chemical substance registration and restriction.
reference: EU REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; ECHA Guidance

description: Indicates whether the manufacturer's part meets EU RoHS Directive requirements restricting hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IEC 63000:2018

description: Date and time of the most recent update to the approved manufacturer record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data lineage in the Silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Date of the most recent periodic requalification assessment performed for this manufacturer-component combination. Supports ongoing supplier monitoring per ISO 9001.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1; SAP Ariba Supplier Performance Management

description: Standard procurement lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to delivery for this manufacturer's part. Used in MRP/MRP II planning calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS Supply Chain Management Standards

description: Unique short code or identifier assigned to the manufacturer within the enterprise system (e.g., SAP vendor/manufacturer code). Enables cross-system linkage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Manufacturer Part Number (MPN) Profile

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturer's registered business location. Used for geopolitical risk assessment and trade compliance screening.
