reference: ISO 3166-1; Export Administration Regulations (EAR); OFAC Sanctions

description: Document reference number or URL pointing to the manufacturer's official datasheet or technical specification for the approved part.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (D-U-N-S) nine-digit identifier for the manufacturer. Used for global supplier identity verification and risk assessment.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet D-U-N-S Standard; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Legal or trade name of the manufacturer approved to supply the component. Used for procurement qualification and supply chain risk management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Approved Manufacturer; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1

description: Manufacturer's description of the part as stated in their datasheet or catalog. Supports incoming inspection and procurement verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MPN Profile; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3

description: The manufacturer's own part number for the component. Critical for procurement, incoming inspection, and cross-referencing with supplier documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MPN; ANSI/EIA-649 Configuration Management Standard

description: Minimum quantity that must be ordered from this manufacturer per purchase order. Impacts procurement planning and inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record; APICS Standards

description: Free-text field for additional engineering or procurement notes regarding this manufacturer approval, such as special handling instructions, known limitations, or pending actions.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: PPAP submission level required or achieved for this manufacturer-component combination, indicating the depth of documentation provided during qualification.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO/TS 16949

description: Current status of the PPAP submission for this manufacturer-component combination. Determines whether full production release is authorized.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Measured defect rate in Parts Per Million (PPM) for incoming parts from this manufacturer. Key quality KPI for supplier performance monitoring and AQL compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; AIAG PPAP; Six Sigma Quality Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit price of the manufacturer's part (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency global procurement.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Reference number or identifier of the qualification test report, First Article Inspection (FAI) record, or PPAP package that substantiates the manufacturer approval.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; FAI AS9102

description: Numeric quality performance score (0–100) assigned to this manufacturer for the component based on incoming inspection results, PPM defect rates, and audit outcomes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1; SAP Ariba Supplier Performance Management; AIAG Supplier Quality Requirements

description: Interval in months at which this manufacturer-component approval must be periodically requalified and reviewed. Drives quality management scheduling.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1; Internal Supplier Quality Procedure

description: SAP plant code indicating that use of this approved manufacturer is restricted to a specific manufacturing plant. Null if approved globally across all plants.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this AML/AVL entry was sourced (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, SAP Ariba, Siemens Teamcenter PLM).
reference: Internal Data Governance Policy; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Engineering's Approved Manufacturer List (AML) must reference the procurement supplier master. Sourcing and engineering teams use this daily to validate that a component's approved manufacturer exists
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: UL certification file number issued by Underwriters Laboratories for the manufacturer's part, confirming product safety compliance for North American markets.
reference: UL Standards; ANSI/UL Product Safety Certification

description: Negotiated or catalog unit price for the manufacturer's part. Used for cost estimation, BOM costing, and procurement benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record; IFRS 15; GAAP

description: Free-text description of any restrictions on the use of this manufacturer's part, such as limited to specific plants, product lines, or applications. Null if no restrictions apply.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3; Engineering Change Notice (ECN) Process

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a Bill of Process header record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA PP Routing

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: bop.ecn_reference is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized the routing change. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces referential integrity for change management. ecn_reference is redu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: bop.eco_reference is a STRING reference to the ECO that authorized the routing implementation. Replacing with FK eco_id → eco.eco_id enforces referential integrity. eco_reference is redundant once the
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: bop.linked_bom_number is a STRING reference to the BOM this routing is linked to. A BOP defines the manufacturing process for a specific BOM structure. Replacing with FK bom_id → bom.bom_id enforces t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Bills of Process (manufacturing routings) are PLM-managed objects that progress through formal lifecycle states before being released to manufacturing execution systems. Linking bop to plm_lifecycle_s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or employee identifier of the process engineer or engineering authority who formally approved this Bill of Process for production release. Required for ISO 9001 document control and audit trail compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Approval Workflow

description: Date on which this Bill of Process revision was formally approved for production release by the authorized process engineer or engineering authority. Supports ISO 9001 document control and change management audit requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Reference quantity for which the Bill of Process operations and time values are defined (e.g., 1 unit, 100 units). All operation times and resource requirements in the BoP are scaled relative to this base quantity during production order creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Base Quantity; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Unit of measure corresponding to the base quantity for this Bill of Process (e.g., EA for each, PC for piece, KG for kilogram). Aligns with SAP unit of measure configuration and ISO 80000 measurement standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturing plant or facility where this Bill of Process is executed. Used for trade compliance, customs declarations, CE marking, and country-of-origin labeling requirements in multinational operations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking Directive; WTO Rules of Origin

description: System timestamp recording when this Bill of Process record was first created in the source PLM or ERP system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and ISO 9001 document control requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Total elapsed time to complete one full execution of the Bill of Process from first operation start to last operation completion, expressed in seconds. Used for capacity planning, OEE calculation, and production scheduling in Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; ISO 22400 KPI Standards

description: Detailed narrative description of the manufacturing process defined by this BoP, including scope, key process steps, and intended assembly or sub-assembly. Supports operator understanding and process documentation requirements under ISO 9001.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

business_glossary_term: Bill of Process (BoP) Effectivity End Date
description: Date after which this Bill of Process revision is no longer valid for new production orders. Supports date-effective routing expiry in SAP PP and ensures superseded processes are not applied to new manufacturing orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Validity; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Effectivity Management

business_glossary_term: Bill of Process (BoP) Effectivity Start Date
description: Date from which this Bill of Process revision becomes valid for production use. Used in date-effective routing selection in SAP PP and MES scheduling to ensure the correct process version is applied to production orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Validity; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Effectivity Management

description: Environmental impact classification of the manufacturing process, indicating whether it generates waste, emissions, requires chemical handling, or is energy-intensive. Supports ISO 14001 environmental management compliance and EPA reporting obligations.
reference: ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management; EPA Regulations; REACH/RoHS Compliance

description: SAP material number or PLM part number of the finished assembly or sub-assembly produced by this Bill of Process. Provides direct linkage to the product master in SAP MM/PP and Siemens Teamcenter for production planning and scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Flag indicating whether operations within this Bill of Process may be executed in parallel (concurrently) rather than strictly sequentially. Enables parallel scheduling in Siemens Opcenter MES and SAP PP finite scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling

description: Flag indicating whether this Bill of Process is the preferred (primary) routing for the associated finished or semi-finished product. When true, this BoP is selected by default during production order creation in SAP PP unless overridden.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Selection; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the primary language in which this BoP documentation and work instructions are authored (e.g., EN, DE, ZH, FR). Supports multilingual shop floor documentation in global manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Multi-Language Support

description: System timestamp of the most recent modification to this Bill of Process record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and change detection in downstream MES and ERP integrations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake Change Data Capture

description: Total planned manufacturing lead time for the process in calendar days, from order release to completion. Used in SAP MRP/MRP II scheduling, ATP/CTP calculations, and delivery promise management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MRP; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Maximum production quantity for which this Bill of Process is valid. Production orders exceeding this quantity may require splitting or use of an alternate routing. Used in SAP PP lot-size-dependent scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Lot Size; APICS CPIM

description: Minimum production quantity for which this Bill of Process is valid. Production orders below this quantity may require a different routing or process variant. Used in SAP PP lot-size-dependent scheduling and MRP planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Lot Size; APICS CPIM

description: Production strategy governing how this process is triggered: Make to Stock (MTS), Make to Order (MTO), Assemble to Order (ATO), or Engineer to Order (ETO). Determines how the BoP is linked to demand signals in SAP PP/MRP and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planning Strategy; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Descriptive human-readable name for the Bill of Process, summarizing the assembly or sub-assembly manufacturing process it defines (e.g., 'Motor Drive Assembly – Standard Process'). Used in MES scheduling and shop floor documentation.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the Bill of Process, used in PLM, MES, and ERP systems to reference the process structure. Corresponds to the routing number in SAP PP and the BoP document number in Siemens Teamcenter.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA PP Module

description: Four-character SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this Bill of Process is valid and executed. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations and plant-specific routing assignments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Plant Configuration; ISO 9001:2015

description: Classification of the manufacturing process type: discrete (individual units), process (batch/continuous), repetitive (high-volume standard), project (one-off), or continuous. Determines scheduling and execution methodology in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Manufacturing Type; ISO 22400; APICS CPIM

description: Classification of the BoP indicating whether it represents the standard production process, an alternate routing, a rework process, a prototype build, or a pilot run. Drives routing selection logic in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Type; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Product family or product line classification to which the finished assembly produced by this BoP belongs (e.g., 'Drive Systems', 'Switchgear', 'Automation Controllers'). Supports aggregate production planning and portfolio-level reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Product Classification

description: SAP production version identifier linking this BoP to a specific combination of BOM and routing for a finished or semi-finished product. Enables alternative production scenarios (e.g., different work centers or batch sizes) within SAP PP/MRP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Version (C223)

description: Reference to the SAP QM inspection plan or Siemens Opcenter quality plan associated with this Bill of Process. Links the process structure to mandatory quality checkpoints, AQL sampling plans, and SPC control points required by the QMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 AQL

description: Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the version of the Bill of Process (e.g., A, B, 01, 02). Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Order (ECO) affecting the process structure. Aligns with PLM revision management in Siemens Teamcenter.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Safety classification of the manufacturing process defined by this BoP, indicating special handling, access control, or environmental requirements (e.g., hazardous materials, ESD-sensitive components, cleanroom conditions). Supports OSHA and ISO 45001 compliance.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; IEC 62443

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this Bill of Process record originates (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP S/4HANA PP, Siemens Opcenter MES). Supports data lineage and master data governance in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Governance; DAMA DMBOK Data Lineage

description: Lifecycle status of the Bill of Process record, controlling its availability for production use. Only BoPs in 'released' status are eligible for MES execution and SAP PP order creation. Managed through the ECO/ECN change workflow in Siemens Teamcenter.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: The available production time divided by customer demand rate, expressed in seconds. Defines the required pace of production for this process to meet customer demand. A fundamental Lean Manufacturing metric used in line balancing and capacity planning.
reference: Lean Manufacturing Principles; VDI 3633; SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Planning

description: Total number of manufacturing operations (process steps) defined within this Bill of Process. Provides a quick structural indicator of process complexity and is used in MES work order generation and shop floor documentation.
reference: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Routing Operations

description: Category of the primary work center type associated with this Bill of Process, indicating the dominant manufacturing technology or process family. Used for capacity planning aggregation and shop floor routing in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each engineering drawing record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: engineering_drawing.cad_model_reference is a STRING reference to the associated CAD model from which the drawing was derived. Replacing with FK cad_model_id → cad_model.cad_model_id enforces the PLM r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: engineering_drawing.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized the drawing revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for controlled engin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Engineering drawings are PLM-managed controlled documents that progress through formal lifecycle states (In Work, Under Review, Released, Obsolete). Linking drawing to plm_lifecycle_state provides for
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or employee identifier of the authorized approver who released the drawing for production or procurement use. Establishes formal engineering authority and sign-off accountability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; ANSI Y14.100; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management

description: Position of the depicted item within the product structure hierarchy. Indicates whether the drawing represents a top-level assembly, sub-assembly, individual component, or purchased part.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - BOM Management; ISO 10303 STEP

description: Native or exchange file format of the CAD model or drawing file (e.g., Siemens NX, CATIA, STEP, PDF). Determines software compatibility for viewing, editing, and downstream manufacturing use.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management; ISO 10303 STEP Standard for Exchange of Product Data

description: Name or employee identifier of the engineer who performed the technical check/peer review of the drawing. Part of the mandatory drawing approval workflow per quality management standards.
reference: ANSI Y14.100; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; ISO 7200

description: Information security classification of the drawing content. Controls access permissions, distribution rights, and handling requirements for the drawing across internal teams, suppliers, and customers.
reference: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Information Security Management; GDPR Article 32; Company Information Classification Policy

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the engineering site where the drawing was originally created. Relevant for export control compliance, intellectual property jurisdiction, and regulatory certification.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; US EAR; ITAR

description: Calendar date on which the drawing was originally created in the PLM system. Used for drawing age analysis, audit trails, and compliance with document retention policies.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; ISO 7200; SAP DMS

description: Name or employee identifier of the engineer or drafter who created the drawing. Captured in the drawing title block for accountability and traceability per engineering documentation standards.
reference: ANSI Y14.100; ISO 7200; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Export control classification of the drawing and its associated technology under US EAR (Export Administration Regulations) or ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations). Governs distribution of drawings to foreign nationals and international sites.
reference: US Export Administration Regulations (EAR) - 15 CFR 730-774; ITAR 22 CFR 120-130; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821

description: Indicates whether the drawing is a controlled document subject to formal change management, revision control, and distribution tracking per the quality management system. Controlled drawings require ECN authorization for any modification.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management

description: ISO 639-1 language code of the drawing annotations and title block text (e.g., en, de, zh-CN, fr). Supports multilingual drawing management for global manufacturing sites and international customer deliveries.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; ISO 7200 Technical Product Documentation

description: Date on which the drawing record was most recently updated in the PLM system. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and identification of recently revised drawings.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Primary material specification or standard called out on the drawing (e.g., ASTM A36, DIN EN 10025, AL6061-T6). Drives material procurement, supplier qualification, and incoming inspection requirements.
reference: ASTM International; DIN Standards; ISO 6892 Metallic Materials Tensile Testing; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Formal alphanumeric identifier assigned to the engineering drawing per company numbering standards. Used as the primary business key for cross-system reference in Teamcenter PLM and SAP DMS.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management; ANSI Y14.100 Engineering Drawing Practices

description: Date on which the drawing was formally declared obsolete or superseded. Prevents use of outdated drawings in manufacturing and supports document lifecycle management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management

description: Engineering department or design team responsible for creating and owning the drawing (e.g., Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Systems Engineering). Supports ownership accountability and routing.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Standard paper or sheet size designation for the drawing (ISO A-series or ANSI letter sizes). Governs print and reproduction specifications for physical drawing distribution.
reference: ANSI Y14.1; ISO 5457

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility for which the drawing is primarily applicable. Supports plant-specific drawing distribution and manufacturing execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Production Planning; ISO 9001:2015

description: Business product line or product family to which the drawn component or assembly belongs (e.g., automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure). Supports portfolio-level drawing management and reporting.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Product Lifecycle Management

description: Orthographic projection convention used in the drawing (first-angle per ISO/European practice or third-angle per ANSI/US practice). Critical for correct interpretation of multi-view drawings across global manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 128 Technical Drawings - General Principles of Presentation; ANSI Y14.3 Orthographic and Pictorial Views

description: Applicable regulatory compliance certifications or directives that the drawn component or assembly must satisfy (e.g., CE Marking, RoHS, REACH, UL). Drives compliance documentation and design constraint requirements.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Standards; IEC 62443

description: Date on which the drawing was formally approved and released for use in production, procurement, or field operations. Marks the transition from engineering control to manufacturing control.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SAP DMS

description: Current revision identifier of the drawing (e.g., A, B, C or 01, 02). Tracks the engineering change history and ensures the correct version is used in production and procurement.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ANSI Y14.100; ISO 7200

description: Nominal drawing scale expressed as a ratio (e.g., 1:1, 1:2, 2:1, NTS for Not to Scale). Defines the proportional relationship between the drawn representation and the actual object dimensions.
reference: ANSI Y14.2 Line Conventions and Lettering; ISO 5455 Technical Drawings - Scales

description: Total number of sheets comprising the complete engineering drawing package. Used for document completeness verification during review, approval, and distribution.
reference: ANSI Y14.1 Decimal Inch Drawing Sheet Size and Format; ISO 5457 Technical Product Documentation Sheet Sizes

description: Identifies the specific sheet within a multi-sheet drawing set (e.g., sheet 1 of 5). Enables precise reference to individual drawing sheets during manufacturing and inspection.
reference: ANSI Y14.1; ISO 5457

description: Current lifecycle status of the engineering drawing within the PLM workflow. Controls whether the drawing may be used for production, procurement, or field installation activities.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Drawing number of the newer drawing that replaces this drawing when it is obsolete or superseded. Enables forward navigation through the drawing revision history for configuration management.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ANSI Y14.100; ISO 9001:2015

description: Surface finish or surface texture requirement specified on the drawing (e.g., Ra 1.6 µm, 63 µin RMS). Governs machining, grinding, and coating process requirements for manufactured components.
reference: ISO 1302 Indication of Surface Texture; ASME Y14.36 Surface Texture Symbols

description: Official descriptive title of the engineering drawing as it appears in the title block. Identifies the subject component, assembly, or system depicted.
reference: ANSI Y14.100 Engineering Drawing Practices; ISO 7200 Technical Product Documentation

description: Governing dimensional and geometric tolerance standard applied to the drawing. Ensures consistent interpretation of tolerances by manufacturing, quality, and supplier teams.
reference: ASME Y14.5 Dimensioning and Tolerancing; ISO 1101 Geometrical Product Specifications; ISO 2768 General Tolerances

description: Classification of the engineering drawing by its technical purpose. Determines the applicable drafting standards, review workflow, and downstream usage in manufacturing, installation, or maintenance.
reference: ANSI Y14.24 Types and Applications of Engineering Drawings; ISO 10209 Technical Product Documentation

description: Primary dimensional unit used throughout the drawing (millimeters, inches, etc.). Prevents measurement interpretation errors across global manufacturing and supplier facilities.
reference: ANSI Y14.5; ISO 1 Standard Reference Temperature for Geometrical Product Specification

description: Nominal weight of the component or assembly depicted in the drawing, expressed in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, structural analysis, packaging design, and shipping documentation.
reference: ISO 80000-4 Mechanics; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Engineering Change Notice record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the ecn data product.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management Module

description: Actual total cost incurred to implement the engineering change upon completion. Compared against estimated_implementation_cost for variance analysis, budget control, and continuous improvement of change cost estimation accuracy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA; IFRS; GAAP

description: Human-readable description of the primary affected component, assembly, drawing, or process document. Provides context for reviewers and approvers without requiring a PLM system lookup.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - BOM Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Primary part number, assembly number, drawing number, or BOM node identifier that is the subject of the engineering change. Represents the main item being modified. Links to the PLM/PDM product structure in Teamcenter.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - BOM Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: Classification of the primary item being changed: component (individual part), assembly/sub_assembly (multi-part structure), drawing (2D/3D technical drawing), bom (Bill of Materials structure), process (manufacturing process/BoP), document (SOP, specification), software (firmware/embedded code), or tooling (jigs, fixtures, dies).
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Date on which the Engineering Change Notice received final approval from the Engineering Change Board (ECB) or designated approval authority. Marks the transition from review to authorized implementation status.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Name or employee identifier of the final approving authority (e.g., Chief Engineer, Engineering Change Board chairperson) who authorized the ECN. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: The current BOM or drawing revision level that is being superseded by this engineering change. Enables before/after comparison and revision history tracking in Teamcenter PLM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - BOM Management; ANSI/ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

description: The new BOM or drawing revision level that will be established upon implementation of this engineering change. Defines the target state of the product structure post-change.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - BOM Management; ANSI/ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Reference Number
description: Reference identifier of the CAPA record in the Quality Management System that triggered this ECN, when the change is driven by a corrective or preventive action. Provides traceability between quality events and engineering changes per ISO 9001 requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 Nonconformity and Corrective Action; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Business driver or root cause triggering the Engineering Change Notice. CAPA-driven changes link to corrective/preventive actions; obsolescence indicates end-of-life component replacement; regulatory_compliance reflects REACH, RoHS, CE Marking, or other mandates. Critical for change governance and traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; CAPA process per ISO 13485; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU

description: Classification of the engineering change by its primary nature: design (geometry, tolerances, CAD), process (manufacturing method, BoP), material (raw material substitution), regulatory (compliance-driven), software (firmware/embedded), documentation (drawing/SOP update), or safety (hazard mitigation). Drives routing and approval authority.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; APQP/PPAP requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated implementation cost and actual implementation cost fields. Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY, GBP).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: System timestamp recording when the ECN record was first created in Teamcenter, including draft records not yet submitted. Supports full lifecycle audit trail from initial creation through implementation.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity)

description: Indicates whether the engineering change requires formal notification to customers per contractual obligations or regulatory requirements. When true, triggers customer communication workflow in Salesforce CRM and may require customer approval before implementation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; Customer SLA Agreements; Salesforce CRM Service Cloud

description: Detailed narrative describing the full scope, rationale, and technical details of the proposed engineering change. Supports engineering review, impact assessment, and regulatory documentation.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Decision on how to handle existing inventory of the affected component or assembly after the engineering change is implemented. Drives warehouse and inventory management actions in Infor WMS and SAP S/4HANA WM to prevent use of obsolete parts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-WM; Infor WMS; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Control of Nonconforming Outputs

description: Reference number of the Engineering Change Order (ECO) issued to authorize and execute the physical implementation of this ECN. The ECO is the downstream execution document that follows ECN approval in the change management workflow.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Method by which the engineering change becomes effective in production: date_effectivity (change applies from a specific date), serial_number_effectivity (change applies from a specific serial number), lot_effectivity (change applies from a specific production lot), or unit_effectivity (change applies to specific unit numbers). Critical for production control and spare parts management.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Effectivity; APQP

description: The specific value defining when the change becomes effective, interpreted according to effectivity_type. For date_effectivity, this is a date string; for serial_number_effectivity, this is the first serial number; for lot_effectivity, this is the lot number. Enables precise production cutover control.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Effectivity

description: Estimated total cost to implement the engineering change, including engineering labor, tooling modifications, material write-offs, qualification testing, and production line changeover. Used for change prioritization, CAPEX/OPEX planning, and ROI analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA; IFRS; GAAP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Date on which the engineering change was fully implemented and the new revision became effective in production. Used for on-time delivery measurement, change cycle time analytics, and regulatory compliance records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Target date by which the engineering change is planned to be fully implemented in production. Used for production planning, material procurement scheduling in SAP MRP, and milestone tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Full name of the engineer or technical staff member who initiated the Engineering Change Notice. Retained for display in reports and approval documents where the employee ID alone is insufficient for human-readable audit trails.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: System timestamp of the most recent modification to the ECN record in Teamcenter. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for detecting concurrent modification conflicts.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer CDC

description: Human-readable, business-facing unique identifier for the Engineering Change Notice as assigned by Teamcenter Change Management. Used for cross-system referencing, audit trails, and communication with engineering, procurement, and manufacturing teams.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6 Control of Changes

description: Organizational department responsible for the engineering change, such as Product Engineering, R&D, Manufacturing Engineering, or Quality. Used for workload distribution, cost allocation, and departmental KPI reporting.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center

description: SAP S/4HANA plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the engineering change will be implemented. Supports multi-site change management for multinational operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Plant Configuration; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Business priority level assigned to the ECN indicating urgency of implementation. Critical priority typically indicates safety, regulatory, or production-stopping issues requiring immediate action. Drives scheduling of review boards and implementation timelines.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Indicates whether the engineering change affects the product's compliance status with applicable regulations such as RoHS, REACH, CE Marking, UL certification, or IEC standards. When true, triggers compliance re-evaluation and potential re-certification activities.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EU CE Marking; IEC Standards; UL Standards

description: Specific regulatory standard, directive, or compliance requirement driving the engineering change, such as RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, CE Marking Directive, UL certification requirement, or OSHA standard. Populated when change_reason is regulatory_compliance.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EU CE Marking Directives; UL Standards; OSHA Standards

description: Free-text explanation provided by the reviewer or approver when an ECN is rejected. Documents the specific technical, commercial, or compliance reasons for rejection to guide the initiator in revising and resubmitting the change request.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

business_glossary_term: Requires Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) Update
description: Indicates whether the engineering change necessitates an update to the Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) document. Ensures risk assessment documentation remains current and aligned with the changed design.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; APQP

business_glossary_term: Requires Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) Update
description: Indicates whether the engineering change requires an update to the Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) document. Ensures manufacturing process risk documentation is updated to reflect changed process steps or parameters.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; APQP

description: Indicates whether the engineering change requires a Production Part Approval Process submission to the customer or internal quality authority before the changed part can be used in production. Mandatory for automotive and aerospace supply chain compliance.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; APQP

description: Indicates whether the engineering change has a potential impact on product safety, operator safety, or end-user safety. When true, triggers mandatory safety review and may require additional regulatory approvals (CE Marking, UL certification, OSHA compliance review).
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; EU CE Marking Directives; UL Standards; OSHA Standards

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which the ECN record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation in the lakehouse architecture.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Current lifecycle state of the ECN within the Teamcenter Change Management workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through engineering review board approval to physical implementation on the shop floor. Drives downstream actions in SAP S/4HANA MM/PP modules.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6; SAP S/4HANA Change Management

description: Precise date and time when the Engineering Change Notice was formally submitted for review in Teamcenter. Used for change cycle time measurement, SLA compliance tracking, and audit trail.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Short, descriptive title summarizing the nature of the proposed engineering change. Used in dashboards, approval workflows, and search interfaces within Teamcenter and SAP S/4HANA.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.6

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the Bill of Materials header record. Serves as the primary key for the BOM entity across all integrated systems including SAP PP and Siemens Teamcenter.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management

description: Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Engineering BOMs are classified by product category (e.g., automation module, electrification unit) to route them to the correct engineering team and apply category-specific design rules and complianc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: bom.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized the BOM revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces referential integrity for the change management workflow. ecn_number is
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.lifecycle. Business justification: Every engineering BOM is tied to a product lifecycle phase (e.g., prototype, production, obsolete). PLM teams reference this daily to gate BOM releases and control which revisions are active for manuf
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Engineering BOMs are PLM-managed objects that progress through formal lifecycle states (In Work, Under Review, Released, Obsolete, Superseded). Linking engineering_bom to plm_lifecycle_state provides
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: SAP alternative BOM counter (01–99) that distinguishes multiple BOMs for the same material/plant combination, enabling variant or alternative production structures for the same finished good or sub-assembly.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module CS01

description: Name or user ID of the authorized approver who released this BOM revision for production use. Part of the formal BOM approval and release workflow in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and SAP PP, ensuring compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2

description: Date and time when this BOM revision was formally approved and released by the authorized approver. Provides an auditable timestamp for regulatory compliance, quality management system (QMS) traceability, and engineering change management records.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; GDPR Article 5

description: Reference quantity of the parent assembly for which all component quantities in the BOM are specified. Typically 1 unit but may be set to a batch size (e.g., 100 units) for process manufacturing or high-volume production to improve precision in component quantity calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module CS01; ISO 9001:2015

description: Unit of measure corresponding to the base quantity of the parent assembly (e.g., EA for each, PC for piece, KG for kilogram). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure (MEINS) in the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: SAP BOM category (STLTY) classifying the object type to which the BOM is assigned. Material BOMs define product structures; Equipment BOMs support plant maintenance (PM) and asset management; Functional Location BOMs define site-level structures; WBS BOMs support project-based manufacturing (ETO/MTO).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/PM Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: SAP Engineering Change Management (ECM) change number used to control time-phased BOM changes in SAP PP. Enables change-controlled BOM modifications where component additions, deletions, or quantity changes are governed by an approved change record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/ECM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6

description: Total number of direct child components (line items) defined in this BOM header. Provides a quick indicator of BOM complexity and is used for completeness validation during BOM release and change management reviews.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management

description: Indicates whether this BOM is included in product cost calculations (standard cost estimate) in SAP CO-PC (Product Costing). When true, the BOM components and quantities are used to calculate the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and standard cost for the parent material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC Module; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country where the product defined by this BOM is manufactured. Required for customs declarations, CE Marking compliance, RoHS/REACH regulatory reporting, and export control classification.
reference: ISO 3166-1; EU CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006

description: Date and time when this BOM record was initially created in the source system (SAP PP or Siemens Teamcenter). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and lifecycle tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Free-text description of the BOM, providing additional context about the product structure, intended application, or special configuration. Sourced from the BOM header text in SAP PP or the item description in Siemens Teamcenter.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Name or identifier of the engineering team, department, or responsible engineer who owns this BOM. Used for change management routing, approval workflows, and accountability tracking in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3

description: Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or Harmonized System (HS) code assigned to the product defined by this BOM. Required for export license determination, trade compliance, and customs documentation for multinational shipments of automation systems and electrification solutions.
reference: US Export Administration Regulations (EAR); EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; ANSI

description: Indicates whether this BOM is a configurable/variant BOM (SAP variant configuration) that supports multiple product configurations from a single super-BOM. Relevant for Assemble-to-Order (ATO) and Engineer-to-Order (ETO) automation systems and electrification solutions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Variant Configuration; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Indicates whether this BOM represents a phantom assembly — a logical grouping of components that does not physically exist as a stocked sub-assembly. Phantom assemblies are exploded through in MRP and production orders, with their components planned directly at the parent level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; MRP II Planning Standards

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the BOM description and component texts are maintained. Supports multilingual BOM management for global manufacturing operations across multiple countries and regions.
reference: ISO 639-1; SAP S/4HANA Multi-Language Support

description: Date and time of the most recent modification to this BOM record in the source system. Used for change tracking, delta processing in the Databricks Silver layer, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Depth level of this BOM within the overall product structure hierarchy. Level 0 represents the top-level finished good; Level 1 represents direct sub-assemblies; deeper levels represent lower-tier components. Used for multi-level BOM explosion in MRP and costing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015

description: Manufacturing execution strategy associated with this BOM, indicating how the parent assembly is produced relative to customer demand. Drives MRP planning parameters, lead time calculations, and order management behavior in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/SD Module; APICS CPIM Standards; ISO 9001:2015

description: Free-text field for engineering notes, special instructions, or contextual remarks associated with this BOM. May include DFM/DFA considerations, APQP notes, PPAP requirements, or special manufacturing constraints relevant to the product structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the BOM, used in engineering and production documents. Corresponds to the BOM number in SAP PP (CS01/CS02) and Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management

description: Four-character SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or production site for which this BOM is valid. A BOM may have plant-specific variants to reflect local production capabilities, tooling, or supplier substitutions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; ISO 9001:2015

description: Human-readable name of the parent product, finished good, or sub-assembly for which this BOM is defined. Sourced from the material master description in SAP MM or the item name in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: SAP production version identifier linking this BOM to a specific routing/work center combination for a given plant. Enables multiple production methods (e.g., different lines or processes) for the same material, each with its own BOM and routing pairing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Pipe-delimited set of regulatory compliance certifications applicable to the product defined by this BOM. Supports compliance reporting for RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals), CE Marking, and UL (Underwriters Laboratories) certification requirements.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; EU CE Marking Directive; UL Standards

description: Date and time when this BOM was formally released to production, making it available for use in MRP runs, production orders, and manufacturing execution in Siemens Opcenter MES. Distinct from approval timestamp — release may follow approval after additional validation steps.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Engineering revision identifier (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02) indicating the version of the BOM. Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that modifies the product structure.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; ANSI/EIA-649

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this BOM record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the Databricks Silver layer when BOMs are synchronized between SAP S/4HANA PP and Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Current lifecycle status of the BOM record. Controls whether the BOM is available for use in production planning (MRP), manufacturing execution (MES), or procurement. Released BOMs are active for production; Frozen BOMs are locked for costing; Obsolete/Superseded BOMs are retired.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Cross-reference identifier of the corresponding BOM or product structure item in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Enables bidirectional traceability between the SAP PP BOM and the engineering BOM (eBOM) managed in Teamcenter for PLM/PDM alignment.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management

description: Classification of the BOM by its intended use in the product lifecycle. Engineering BOM (eBOM) reflects design intent from PLM/PDM; Manufacturing BOM (mBOM) reflects production structure from MES/ERP; Service BOM supports after-sales and MRO; Sales BOM supports order configuration; Costing BOM supports COGS calculation; Planning BOM supports MRP/MRP II.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015

description: SAP BOM usage indicator that determines in which business context the BOM is applied (e.g., production orders, plant maintenance work orders, sales order processing, product costing). Corresponds to SAP BOM Usage field (STLAN) in CS01.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module CS01; SAP BOM Usage STLAN

description: Date from which this BOM revision becomes effective and valid for use in production planning, MRP runs, and manufacturing execution. Supports time-phased BOM management aligned with Engineering Change Notice (ECN) effectivity dates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Date after which this BOM revision is no longer valid for production planning or manufacturing execution. Used to manage BOM supersession when a new revision is released via ECN/ECO. A null value indicates the BOM is open-ended and currently active.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a material specification record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: material_specification.ecn_reference_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized this material specification revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management trac
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hazardous_substance. Business justification: Material specifications must reference controlled hazardous substances (RoHS, REACH) to enforce compliance during design. Engineering teams use this link to flag restricted materials before BOM approv
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The primary industry or international standard governing the material specification, such as ASTM A36, DIN EN 10025, EN 573-3, or ISO 6892. Defines the normative requirements that the material must meet.
reference: ASTM International; DIN (Deutsches Institut für Normung); EN (European Norm); ISO Standards

description: Name or employee identifier of the engineering or quality authority who formally approved this material specification revision. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance under ISO 9001 document control requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Reference identifier linking this material specification to the corresponding Approved Supplier List (ASL) record in SAP Ariba or SAP S/4HANA MM. Ensures procurement is restricted to qualified and audited suppliers for this material.
reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; SAP S/4HANA MM - Source List; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.1

description: Date and time when the material specification was formally approved by the authorized engineering or quality representative. Provides a precise audit trail for document control and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management

description: The primary elemental or compound material, such as 'Steel', 'Aluminum', 'Copper', 'Polypropylene', or 'Epoxy Resin'. Provides the foundational material identity before alloy or grade qualification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master; IEC 62474

description: Structured text or JSON-serialized representation of the required chemical composition limits (min/max percentages by weight) for key elements such as C, Mn, Si, Cr, Ni, Cu, Fe. Stored as a structured string for Silver layer compatibility.
reference: ASTM International; DIN EN 10025; ISO 6892; IEC 62474 Material Declaration

description: Indicates whether the material contains conflict minerals (tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold — 3TG) sourced from conflict-affected and high-risk areas (CAHRAs). Triggers supply chain due diligence and reporting obligations under EU Conflict Minerals Regulation and SEC Rule 13p-1.
reference: EU Conflict Minerals Regulation (EU) 2017/821; SEC Rule 13p-1 (Dodd-Frank Section 1502)

description: Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes from which sourcing of this material is restricted or prohibited, based on export control regulations, trade compliance, or supply chain risk policies.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Export Administration Regulations (EAR); ITAR; EU Dual-Use Regulation

description: Timestamp when the material specification record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: Nominal density of the material expressed in kilograms per cubic meter (kg/m³). Used in weight calculations for BOM costing, shipping logistics, and structural analysis in CAD/CAM systems.
reference: ISO 1183; ASTM D792; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Detailed narrative description of the material specification including intended application, key characteristics, and any special notes relevant to procurement or manufacturing use.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: The date from which this revision of the material specification is valid and approved for use in procurement and manufacturing. Supports time-bounded specification management and change effectivity control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: The date on which this revision of the material specification expires or is superseded. A null value indicates the specification is currently active with no defined expiry. Used to manage specification lifecycle and prevent use of obsolete standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Minimum required elongation at break expressed as a percentage, indicating the material's ductility. Used in mechanical property verification during incoming inspection and First Article Inspection (FAI).
reference: ASTM E8/E8M; ISO 6892-1

description: Specifies the EN 10204 material test certificate type required for metallic materials: 2.1 (Declaration of Compliance), 2.2 (Test Report), 3.1 (Inspection Certificate by manufacturer), or 3.2 (Inspection Certificate by independent body). Critical for traceability in regulated industries.
reference: EN 10204:2004 Metallic Products - Types of Inspection Documents

description: Specific grade or alloy designation of the material as defined by the applicable standard, such as '6061-T6', 'Grade 304', 'S235JR', or 'A36'. Uniquely identifies the material within its standard family.
reference: ASTM International; EN 10027; ISO 1629

description: Maximum allowable hardness value for the material. Paired with hardness_min to define the acceptable hardness range for incoming inspection and supplier qualification.
reference: ASTM E18; ASTM E10; ISO 6508; ISO 6506

description: Minimum required hardness value for the material. The hardness scale (HRC, HRB, HV, HB) is specified in the hardness_scale attribute. Used in incoming inspection and quality assurance.
reference: ASTM E18; ASTM E10; ISO 6508; ISO 6506

description: The hardness measurement scale used for the hardness_min and hardness_max values (e.g., Rockwell C, Vickers, Brinell). Ensures correct interpretation of hardness requirements during inspection.
reference: ASTM E18; ISO 6508; ISO 6506

description: UN/GHS hazardous material classification code for the material if applicable (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 8 Corrosive). Drives storage, handling, and transportation requirements under OSHA HazCom and EPA regulations.
reference: UN GHS (Globally Harmonized System); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; EPA 40 CFR; IATA DGR

description: Reference to the quality inspection plan in SAP S/4HANA QM module that governs incoming material inspection for this specification. Defines sampling procedures, test methods, and acceptance criteria (AQL) applied at goods receipt.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module - Inspection Plan; ISO 2859-1 (AQL); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Indicates whether the material complies with the EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 regarding Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals. Flags whether the material contains Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) above threshold.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ECHA SVHC Candidate List

description: Indicates whether the material meets the EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Directive 2011/65/EU requirements, restricting the use of lead, mercury, cadmium, hexavalent chromium, PBB, PBDE, and other substances. Mandatory for products sold in the EU market.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IEC 63000

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the material specification record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture - Delta Lake

description: SAP material group code used to classify materials for procurement, reporting, and spend analytics. Aligns with the enterprise commodity taxonomy managed in SAP Ariba for sourcing and supplier management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master (MARA-MATKL); SAP Ariba Procurement

description: High-level classification of the material by physical or chemical category. Drives storage conditions, handling requirements, and applicable regulatory compliance checks (e.g., RoHS for electronic components, REACH for chemicals).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master (MARA-MTART); IEC 62474 Material Declaration

description: Descriptive short name of the material specification, such as 'Low-Carbon Steel Sheet Grade A' or 'Aluminum Alloy 6061-T6 Bar Stock'. Used for display in procurement and engineering systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master

description: Maximum operating temperature in degrees Celsius at which the material maintains its specified mechanical and physical properties. Used in thermal analysis and material selection for electrification and industrial automation components.

Minimum operating temperature in degrees Celsius at which the material maintains its specified mechanical and physical properties. Critical for material selection in automation systems deployed in cold environments.

SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site for which this material specification is applicable. Supports multi-plant operations where material standards may vary by production location or regional regulatory requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Plant (T001W); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Alphanumeric revision identifier indicating the current version of the material specification. Incremented upon each approved Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) affecting the specification.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Change Management; ANSI/ASME Y14.35

description: Reference number or document identifier for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this material, as required by OSHA HazCom Standard (29 CFR 1910.1200) and EU REACH Regulation. Links to the document management system for retrieval.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 31; GHS

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this material specification record was sourced or originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer lakehouse.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Business-facing unique identifier for the material specification, typically assigned by the engineering or quality organization. Used for cross-referencing in procurement, incoming inspection, and supplier qualification documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The standards development organization (SDO) that published the applicable standard for this material specification. Used to filter and group specifications by regional or international standards alignment.
reference: ANSI; ASTM International; ISO; IEC; DIN; EN

description: Lifecycle status of the material specification indicating whether it is actively approved for use in procurement and manufacturing, under engineering review, superseded by a newer revision, or withdrawn from use.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3

description: Maximum allowable surface roughness expressed in micrometers (µm Ra) as required by the material specification. Relevant for sealing surfaces, bearing interfaces, and precision-machined components.
reference: ISO 4287; ASME B46.1

description: Required surface treatment or coating specification for the material, such as zinc plating, anodizing, passivation, powder coating, or hot-dip galvanizing. Defines the surface condition required for corrosion protection or functional performance.
reference: ASTM B633; ISO 1461; EN 12329; SAP S/4HANA MM

business_glossary_term: Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) Flag
description: Indicates whether the material contains one or more Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) as listed on the ECHA Candidate List under REACH. Triggers mandatory disclosure obligations when concentration exceeds 0.1% w/w.
reference: EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ECHA SVHC Candidate List Article 59

description: Unique document identifier in Siemens Teamcenter PLM system for the master specification document (drawing, datasheet, or specification PDF). Enables direct retrieval of the authoritative specification document from the PLM/PDM system.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management (PDM)

description: Heat treatment, temper, or condition designation of the material (e.g., T6, T651, H32, annealed, normalized, quenched and tempered). Defines the mechanical property state resulting from processing.
reference: ASTM B209; EN 515; ISO 2107

description: Minimum required ultimate tensile strength of the material expressed in megapascals (MPa), as specified by the applicable standard. Used in incoming inspection and supplier qualification to verify mechanical property compliance.
reference: ASTM E8/E8M; ISO 6892-1; DIN EN 10002

description: Specifies the type of material test certificate or documentation required from the supplier at delivery. Options include mill certificates (EN 10204 3.1/3.2), certificates of conformance, third-party test reports, or First Article Inspection (FAI) reports.
reference: EN 10204 Material Test Certificates; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4.3; PPAP Requirements

description: The base unit of measure in which the material is procured and inventoried (e.g., KG for kilogram, M for meter, PC for piece, SHT for sheet). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM unit of measure configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master (MARA-MEINS); ISO 80000

description: Minimum required yield strength (0.2% proof stress) of the material expressed in megapascals (MPa). Critical for structural and load-bearing component qualification in automation and electrification systems.
reference: ASTM E8/E8M; ISO 6892-1; DIN EN 10002

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the product specification record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_drawing. Business justification: product_specification.drawing_number is a STRING reference to the engineering drawing that this specification is associated with. Replacing with FK engineering_drawing_id → engineering_drawing.enginee
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: product_specification.ecn_number is a STRING reference to the ECN that authorized this specification revision. Replacing with FK ecn_id → ecn.ecn_id enforces change management traceability for product
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Product specifications often reference specific material specifications (e.g., a housing spec requiring a specific aluminum alloy grade, or an insulation spec referencing a polymer material standard).
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Product specifications are PLM-managed controlled documents that progress through formal lifecycle states (In Work, Under Review, Released, Obsolete). Linking product_specification to plm_lifecycle_st
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Product specifications define the functional, performance, and environmental requirements for a specific product variant (e.g., a 400V/22kW drive variant has different specs than a 690V/45kW variant).
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_regulatory_certification. Business justification: Product specifications must reference the regulatory certifications (CE, UL, IEC) the product is designed to meet. Engineering uses this to validate design requirements against certification scope bef
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.warranty_policy. Business justification: Product specifications encode design-for-reliability targets that must align with the commercial warranty policy. Engineering references warranty policy to set MTBF targets, test durations, and design
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Primary industry or regulatory standard that governs this specification parameter (e.g., 'IEC 61131-2', 'EN 60204-1', 'ISO 9283', 'CE Marking Directive 2006/42/EC', 'RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU'). Supports regulatory traceability and compliance reporting.
reference: IEC 61131; CE Marking Directive; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; UL Standards

description: Formal approval workflow status of the specification record. Approved specifications are authorized for use in design, procurement, and manufacturing. Distinct from lifecycle status; captures the approval gate outcome.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 – Control of Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Management

description: Name or employee identifier of the engineering authority who formally approved the specification. Required for regulatory traceability and design review records under ISO 9001 and PPAP.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) – Level 3 Submission

description: Date and time when the specification was formally approved by the designated engineering authority. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset for global operations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; ISO 8601 – Date and Time Representation

description: Indicates whether this specification parameter contributes to CE Marking conformity assessment for the European market. CE-relevant parameters must be verified and documented in the Technical File.
reference: EU CE Marking Directives (Machinery 2006/42/EC; Low Voltage 2014/35/EU; EMC 2014/30/EU)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country or market for which this specification applies. Relevant for region-specific regulatory requirements (e.g., CE for EU, UL for USA, CCC for China).
reference: ISO 3166-1 – Country Codes; CE Marking; UL; GB Standards (China)

description: Date and time when the specification record was first created in the source system. Provides audit trail for specification lifecycle management and regulatory documentation.
Detailed narrative description of the specification scope, purpose, and applicability. Provides context for design engineers, quality teams, and regulatory reviewers.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; ANSI/IEEE 830 Software Requirements Specification

description: Date from which this specification revision becomes the authoritative design baseline for production, procurement, and quality inspection. Supports time-bounded specification validity management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6; SAP S/4HANA – Material Master Validity

description: Date on which this specification revision expires or is superseded by a newer revision. Null indicates the specification is currently active with no planned expiry.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.6; SAP S/4HANA – Material Master Validity

description: Indicates whether this specification parameter is designated as a Critical Characteristic (CC) or Key Characteristic (KC) requiring enhanced control, SPC monitoring, and mandatory documentation in the Control Plan. Critical characteristics directly impact safety, regulatory compliance, or product function.
reference: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning); AIAG Control Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; DFMEA/PFMEA

description: Indicates whether this specification parameter has direct implications for product safety, operator safety, or end-user safety. Safety-relevant parameters require additional review, documentation, and regulatory approval under CE Marking, UL, and ISO 45001.
reference: CE Marking Directive; UL (Underwriters Laboratories); ISO 45001:2018 – Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA

description: ISO 639-1 language code of the specification document (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'zh-CN', 'fr'). Supports multilingual documentation management for global engineering teams.
reference: ISO 639-1 – Language Codes; GDPR Article 12 – Multilingual Communication

description: Date and time of the most recent update to the specification record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; ISO 8601; Databricks Lakehouse – Delta Lake Change Data Capture

description: Absolute maximum acceptable value for the parameter, representing the hard upper bound. Used in design verification, test planning, and quality inspection to define pass/fail criteria.

Absolute minimum acceptable value for the parameter, representing the hard lower bound beyond which the product fails to meet specification. Distinct from tolerance_lower which is a deviation from nominal.

The target or ideal value for the specification parameter (e.g., '24', '85', '500'). Stored as STRING to accommodate numeric, alphanumeric, and enumerated nominal values across diverse parameter types.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; ANSI/ASME Y14.5 – Dimensioning and Tolerancing

description: Name of the specific technical parameter being specified (e.g., 'Input Voltage', 'Operating Temperature', 'Tensile Strength', 'IP Protection Rating'). Each specification record captures one parameter for traceability granularity.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3; IEC 61131-2 – Programmable Controllers Hardware Requirements

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site for which this specification is applicable. Supports multi-plant, multinational operations where specifications may vary by production site.
reference: SAP S/4HANA – Plant Maintenance (PM); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1 – Operational Planning

description: The product family or product line to which this specification applies (e.g., 'SIMATIC S7 Controllers', 'SINUMERIK CNC Systems', 'SIRIUS Motor Starters'). Enables specification reuse and family-level compliance reporting.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Product Lifecycle Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Indicates whether this specification parameter is subject to REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) regulation, applicable to chemical composition and substance-of-very-high-concern (SVHC) thresholds.
reference: REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; European Chemicals Agency (ECHA)

description: Alphanumeric revision identifier (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02) indicating the version of the specification. Incremented upon each approved engineering change. Aligns with PLM revision management in Siemens Teamcenter.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3
