description: Indicates whether this specification parameter is relevant to RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) compliance, particularly for material composition specifications governing restricted substances such as lead, mercury, cadmium, and hexavalent chromium.
reference: RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU RoHS 3 Directive 2015/863

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this specification record was ingested (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse – Silver Layer Data Lineage; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Engineering discipline category of the specification parameter. Enables domain-specific filtering and routing to the appropriate engineering team for review and verification.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3; IEC 61131; ANSI

description: Formal business identifier for the product specification document, typically assigned by the PLM/PDM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for cross-referencing in engineering change notices (ECN), design reviews, and regulatory submissions.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Classification of the specification by its engineering purpose. Functional specs define what the product must do; interface specs define integration points; material specs define composition; environmental specs define operating conditions; safety and regulatory specs define compliance requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3 – Design and Development Outputs; IEC 61131; CE Marking Directive

description: Current lifecycle status of the specification record. Controls whether the specification is active for design, manufacturing, and procurement use. Released specifications are the authoritative design baseline.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

description: Native item identifier from Siemens Teamcenter PLM system, serving as the source system reference key for the specification document. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver Layer and the PLM system of record.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Product Data Management (PDM)

description: Descriptive title of the product specification document, summarizing the product or component and the nature of the specification (e.g., 'Servo Drive Unit – Functional Performance Specification').
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Document Management

description: Minimum allowable deviation below the nominal value for the specification parameter. Used in SPC and inspection to define the lower specification limit (LSL). Null if not applicable.
reference: ANSI/ASME Y14.5; ISO 286-1 – Limits and Fits; SPC (Statistical Process Control)

description: Maximum allowable deviation above the nominal value for the specification parameter. Used in Statistical Process Control (SPC) and inspection to define the upper specification limit (USL). Null if not applicable (e.g., enumerated parameters).
reference: ANSI/ASME Y14.5; ISO 286-1 – Limits and Fits; SPC (Statistical Process Control)

description: Engineering unit associated with the nominal value and tolerances (e.g., 'V' for volts, '°C' for degrees Celsius, 'MPa' for megapascals, 'mm' for millimeters, 'kg' for kilograms). Follows SI unit conventions.
reference: ISO 80000 – Quantities and Units; NIST SP 811 – Guide for Use of SI Units

description: Method by which compliance with this specification parameter is verified during design validation and production quality inspection (e.g., test, analysis, inspection, demonstration). Aligns with APQP and PPAP verification planning.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.4 – Design and Development Controls; APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning); PPAP (Production Part Approval Process)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each product variant record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality Standards

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.cad_model. Business justification: Each product variant (e.g., a specific voltage/power rating configuration of an automation drive) has an associated master CAD model defining its physical geometry and design. Linking product_variant
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: product_variant.variant_bom_number is a STRING reference to the BOM that defines this product variant's structure. Replacing with FK bom_id → bom.bom_id establishes the authoritative variant-to-BOM tr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Engineering product variants must be positioned within the commercial product hierarchy (platform > family > variant) to ensure correct BOM structuring, cost rollups, and alignment with sales catalog
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.plm_lifecycle_state. Business justification: Product variants are PLM-managed objects that progress through formal lifecycle states (Concept, Development, Released, Obsolete). Linking product_variant to plm_lifecycle_state provides formal lifecy
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_configuration. Business justification: Product variants in engineering are directly derived from commercial product configurations. Engineering uses this link to ensure each variant's BOM and drawings align with the configuration options s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Engineering variants require a canonical unit of measure for quantity specifications in BOMs and drawings. Referencing the shared UOM master prevents inconsistencies between engineering and commercial
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Current status of CE marking certification for this product variant, confirming conformity with applicable EU directives (e.g., Low Voltage Directive, Machinery Directive, EMC Directive). Required for sale in the European Economic Area.
reference: EU CE Marking Directives; EU Low Voltage Directive 2014/35/EU; EU EMC Directive 2014/30/EU

description: Industrial communication protocol supported by this product variant for integration with PLCs, SCADA systems, and IIoT platforms. Determines compatibility with factory automation architectures.
reference: IEC 61158 Industrial Communication Networks; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity

description: Encoded string representing the full combination of selected configuration options (e.g., voltage, power, protocol, enclosure) that define this variant. Used in SAP variant configuration (VC) and order management for ATO/ETO fulfillment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Variant Configuration; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country of manufacture for this product variant. Used for customs declarations, trade compliance, and country-of-origin labeling requirements.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WCO Rules of Origin; US CBP

description: Timestamp when this product variant record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA or Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Format; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO 9001 Records Management

description: Detailed technical and commercial description of the product variant, including key differentiating characteristics such as configuration options, intended application, and performance highlights.
reference: ISO 8000; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Ingress protection (IP) or NEMA enclosure classification of the product variant, indicating resistance to dust, water, and environmental hazards. Critical for application suitability and regulatory compliance.
reference: IEC 60529 Degrees of Protection (IP Code); NEMA 250 Enclosures Standard

description: Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or equivalent classification assigned to this product variant under US Export Administration Regulations (EAR) or EU dual-use regulations. Controls export licensing requirements.
reference: US EAR (Export Administration Regulations); EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; NIST

description: Manufacturing and fulfillment strategy applicable to this variant: Make to Stock (MTS), Make to Order (MTO), Assemble to Order (ATO), or Engineer to Order (ETO). Drives production planning, lead time commitments, and ATP/CTP logic in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/SD; APICS Supply Chain Dictionary

description: Harmonized System (HS) tariff code assigned to this product variant for customs classification in international trade. Used for import/export declarations, duty calculation, and trade compliance reporting.
reference: World Customs Organization (WCO) Harmonized System; US HTS Schedule B

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether this product variant complies with EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 regarding chemical substance registration and restriction. Required for EU market access and supply chain transparency.
reference: EU REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006; ECHA Guidance

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether this product variant complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting the use of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment. Mandatory for EU market access.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IEC 63000 RoHS Technical Documentation

description: ISO 639-1 language code for the language in which the variant name and description are maintained (e.g., en, de, zh-CN). Supports multi-language product catalog management for global operations.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA Multi-Language Support

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this product variant record in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Format; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO 9001 Records Management

description: Published list price per unit for this product variant as maintained in SAP S/4HANA SD pricing conditions. Serves as the baseline for customer quotations, pricing agreements, and revenue reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; IFRS IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the list price is expressed. Supports global pricing management across multiple sales regions.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Comma-separated list of target markets or regions where this product variant is approved for sale and use (e.g., EU, North America, Asia-Pacific). Drives regional catalog availability and regulatory compliance checks.
reference: EU CE Marking Directive; ANSI; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Short commercial name of the product variant used in catalogs, sales orders, and customer-facing materials. Describes the variant in human-readable form.
reference: ISO 8000; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: SAP S/4HANA plant code identifying the primary manufacturing plant responsible for producing this product variant. Drives production planning, MRP, and inventory management at the plant level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Nominal power rating of the product variant expressed in kilowatts (kW). Core technical specification used for product selection, energy compliance reporting, and ISO 50001 energy management.
reference: IEC 60034; ISO 50001 Energy Management; IEC 61131

description: Engineering and commercial classification of the product variant within the product hierarchy (e.g., frequency inverter, circuit breaker, PLC module, electrification panel). Aligns with SAP material group.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 22745 Industrial Data

description: Commercial product line or product family to which this variant belongs (e.g., automation drives, low-voltage switchgear, smart building controllers). Used for portfolio management and sales reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Internal Product Taxonomy

description: Date on which this product variant was formally released from engineering into production and commercial availability, following completion of APQP/PPAP processes and ECO approval.
reference: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning); PPAP (Production Part Approval Process); SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Name or employee identifier of the lead engineer responsible for the design and lifecycle management of this product variant in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Used for accountability and change management workflows.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Current engineering revision level of the product variant as maintained in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Incremented upon approved Engineering Change Orders (ECOs). Critical for BOM and drawing traceability.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ANSI/ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this product variant record originates (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, TEAMCENTER_PLM). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and master data governance.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Standard manufacturing cost per unit for this product variant as calculated in SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) module. Used for inventory valuation, COGS reporting, and profitability analysis. Expressed in the standard cost currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the standard cost is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Current lifecycle status of the product variant as managed in PLM and ERP. Controls whether the variant can be ordered, produced, or procured. Aligns with SAP material status and Teamcenter lifecycle state.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 10303 STEP

description: Primary industry segment for which this product variant is designed and marketed (e.g., factory automation, building automation, transportation). Supports portfolio segmentation and go-to-market strategy.
reference: Internal Product Taxonomy; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: UL certification file number assigned to this product variant by Underwriters Laboratories, confirming compliance with North American safety standards. Required for sale in the US and Canadian markets.
reference: UL Standards; ANSI; NIST Manufacturing Extension Partnership

description: Date from which this product variant is commercially valid and available for ordering, production, and sale. Used for effectivity management in SAP S/4HANA and Teamcenter PLM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 8601 Date Format

description: Date until which this product variant remains commercially valid. After this date, the variant is no longer available for new orders. Supports phase-out planning and end-of-life management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 8601 Date Format

description: Business-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the product variant within the product catalog. Used in order management, BOM references, and customer-facing documentation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM module material number for configurable variants.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Internal engineering variant number as maintained in Siemens Teamcenter PLM, used to cross-reference the variant across PLM and ERP systems. May differ from the commercial variant code.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Classification of the variant by its engineering origin: standard (catalog item), custom (customer-specific modification), engineered (Engineer to Order), configurable (Assemble to Order with option selection), or phantom (planning-only variant).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Nominal voltage rating of the product variant (e.g., 24V DC, 400V AC, 690V AC). Key configuration option differentiating variants within the same base product for automation and electrification solutions.
reference: IEC 60038 Standard Voltages; IEC 61131

description: Gross weight of the product variant in kilograms, including packaging. Used for logistics planning, freight cost calculation, and shipping documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 80000-4 Mechanics

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each tooling and equipment master record in the engineering domain. Serves as the primary key for all downstream references.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5 - Monitoring and Measuring Resources

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
