reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_location. Business justification: Tooling equipment must track its physical storage location for inventory management and deployment planning. Manufacturing engineers use this daily to locate dies, molds, and fixtures when setting up
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Tooling and equipment are assigned to specific manufacturing plants where they are used to produce products. Engineering references the product-plant assignment to ensure tooling designs are scoped to
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Engineering-defined tooling and equipment is sourced from specific suppliers. Linking to the procurement supplier master enables maintenance teams to raise POs for tooling repairs, spare parts, and ca
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Supports multi-currency reporting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Frequency of required calibration expressed in calendar days. Applicable only when calibration_required is true. Drives the calibration schedule in the QMS.
Indicates whether this tooling or equipment item requires periodic calibration per quality management system requirements. Drives calibration scheduling and compliance tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.1 - General; ISO/IEC 17025 Calibration Requirements

description: Date on which the tooling was formally accepted, qualified, and placed into active production service. Marks the start of tool life cycle tracking.
reference: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this tooling is deployed. Supports multinational compliance, export control, and regional regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1; EU CE Marking; RoHS/REACH Compliance

description: Timestamp when the tooling equipment master record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information; GDPR Article 5

description: Cumulative number of production cycles, shots, or strokes consumed against the rated tool life. Used to calculate remaining tool life and trigger preventive maintenance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) Standards

description: Detailed technical description of the tooling or equipment, including its function, design intent, and applicable manufacturing process.
Current engineering design revision level of the tooling item, aligned with the PLM/PDM revision control system. Changes are tracked via ECN/ECO workflows.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 - Documented Information

description: Date on which the most recent calibration was performed. Used to calculate next calibration due date and assess current compliance status.
Date on which the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance was completed on this tooling item.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; Maximo EAM Work Order Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the tooling equipment master record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5

description: Scheduled preventive maintenance interval in calendar days for this tooling item. Drives work order generation in Maximo EAM and SAP PM.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; TPM (Total Productive Maintenance); Maximo EAM

description: Date on which the tooling item was manufactured or first commissioned. Used to calculate tool age, depreciation, and remaining useful life.
reference: IFRS; GAAP ASC 360; ISO 9001:2015

description: Descriptive name of the tooling or equipment item (e.g., 'Progressive Die Stamping Tool', 'CMM Fixture Assembly', 'Injection Mold - Housing Cover').
Scheduled date by which the next calibration must be completed to maintain compliance with quality management system requirements.
Indicates whether the tooling is owned by the manufacturing company, a customer (customer-supplied tooling), a supplier, or leased. Drives asset accounting treatment and liability.
reference: IFRS 16 Leases; GAAP ASC 842; ISO 9001:2015

description: Approval status of the tooling under the Production Part Approval Process (PPAP). Required for automotive and industrial customers before production release.
reference: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) 4th Edition; IATF 16949; ISO 9001:2015

description: Name or employee ID of the engineering owner responsible for the design, qualification, and lifecycle management of this tooling item.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 - Organizational Roles

description: Date on which the tooling was formally retired or scrapped. Populated when status transitions to 'retired' or 'scrapped'. Used for asset lifecycle reporting.
reference: IFRS; GAAP ASC 360; Maximo EAM Asset Lifecycle

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this tooling master record originates (e.g., SAP PM, Siemens Teamcenter, Maximo EAM).
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: The native primary key or equipment number from the originating source system (e.g., SAP PM Equipment Number, Teamcenter Item ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; SAP PM Equipment Master

description: Current operational status of the tooling or equipment. Drives availability for production scheduling and triggers maintenance or procurement workflows.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; Maximo EAM Asset Status

description: Name of the external vendor or toolmaker who manufactured or supplied this tooling item. Used for supplier performance tracking and warranty claims.
reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The toolmaker's or supplier's own reference number for this tooling item. Required for warranty claims, spare part ordering, and supplier communications.
reference: SAP Ariba; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Broader classification grouping tools by their lifecycle stage and investment level. Hard tooling is production-grade; soft tooling is used for prototyping or low-volume runs.
reference: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015

description: Maximum rated tool life expressed in number of production cycles, shots, or strokes before the tool requires refurbishment or replacement. Used for predictive maintenance scheduling.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) Standards

description: Primary material used in the construction of the tooling (e.g., 'H13 Tool Steel', 'P20 Steel', 'Aluminum 7075', 'Carbide', 'Hardened Steel'). Influences tool life and maintenance requirements.
reference: ANSI/ISO 513 - Classification of Hard Cutting Materials; ISO 9001:2015

description: Business-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the tooling or equipment item, used across engineering, production, and maintenance systems. Corresponds to the tool number in SAP PM and Siemens Teamcenter.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; SAP PM Equipment Master

description: Classification of the tooling or equipment by its functional category. Drives maintenance schedules, calibration requirements, and tool life tracking policies.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ANSI/ISO 13399 Cutting Tool Representation

description: Physical weight of the tooling assembly in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, crane/lift requirements, and storage rack load calculations.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; OSHA Material Handling Standards

description: Code identifying the production work center or machine group where this tooling is primarily deployed. Aligns with SAP PP work center and Siemens Opcenter MES resource definitions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each financial transaction record on a fixed asset within the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for the asset_transaction data product.
reference: IFRS 16; IAS 16; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Asset Transaction

description: Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: asset_transaction.gl_account_number is a denormalized STRING reference to the fixed asset GL account in chart_of_accounts. Asset transactions (acquisitions, retirements, depreciation) post to specific
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Asset transactions (CAPEX acquisitions, depreciation runs, retirements) are executed within a controlling area that governs CAPEX budget profiles, depreciation areas, and cost center assignments. Addi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: asset_transaction uses fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) as separate denormalized fields. Adding fiscal_period_id FK creates a direct relational link to the fiscal_period master for period-lev
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to service.installation_record. Business justification: When equipment is installed at a customer or plant site, an asset transaction (capitalization or transfer) is triggered. Finance links the asset transaction to the installation record to establish the
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.tooling_equipment. Business justification: Tooling and equipment in manufacturing are capitalized assets. Finance records acquisition, depreciation, and disposal transactions against specific tooling items. Asset accountants use this link dail
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The total accumulated depreciation charged against the asset in local currency as of the transaction posting date. Used for balance sheet presentation and asset impairment analysis.
reference: IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Accumulated Depreciation

description: The asset transaction amount translated into the group reporting currency for consolidated financial statements. Supports multi-currency consolidation and group-level CAPEX/EBITDA reporting.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI Group Currency; GAAP ASC 810

description: The asset transaction amount translated into the company code local (functional) currency. Used for statutory reporting and local GAAP financial statements of the legal entity.
reference: IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830 Foreign Currency Matters; SAP S/4HANA FI Local Currency

description: The SAP FI-AA asset class code categorizing the fixed asset (e.g., machinery, buildings, vehicles, IT equipment, leasehold improvements). Drives depreciation method, useful life, and account determination for the transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Asset Class; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; ISO 15686 Service Life Planning

description: The date from which the asset transaction takes effect for asset valuation and depreciation calculation purposes. May differ from the posting date; used to determine the start of depreciation for acquisitions or the end date for retirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Asset Value Date; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360-10

description: Classifies the asset transaction as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) or Operational Expenditure (OPEX) for financial reporting, budgeting, and tax purposes. Critical for EBITDA calculation and management reporting.
reference: IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS Conceptual Framework; Internal CAPEX/OPEX Policy

description: SAP company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit for which the asset transaction is posted. Enables multi-entity financial consolidation and statutory reporting across geographies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810

description: The SAP Controlling (CO) cost center to which the asset and its depreciation charges are assigned. Enables cost accounting allocation of depreciation and asset-related costs to organizational units for management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; Management Accounting Standards

description: The date and time when the asset transaction record was created in the source system (SAP FI-AA). Provides the audit creation timestamp for data lineage, SOX compliance, and period-end close verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Creation; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO 9001

description: SAP FI-AA depreciation area code identifying the valuation view under which the transaction is posted (e.g., 01=Book depreciation/IFRS, 02=Tax depreciation, 15=GAAP local, 30=Cost accounting). Enables parallel valuation for multi-GAAP and tax reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Depreciation Area; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS 16

description: The SAP FI-AA depreciation key defining the depreciation method and calculation rules applied to the asset at the time of the transaction (e.g., LINR=Straight-line, DGRV=Declining balance). Relevant for depreciation postings and impairment testing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Depreciation Key; IAS 16; IAS 36; GAAP ASC 360

description: The date of the originating source document (e.g., vendor invoice date, transfer order date, retirement approval date) that triggered the asset transaction. Used for audit trail and document matching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Date; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: The SAP FI accounting document number generated when the asset transaction is posted to the general ledger. Serves as the primary audit trail reference linking the asset transaction to the GL posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Number; IFRS; GAAP; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: The foreign exchange rate applied to translate the transaction currency amount into the local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP exchange rate tables and used for audit and revaluation purposes.
reference: IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate

description: The fiscal period (month 1–12, or special periods 13–16) within the fiscal year in which the asset transaction is posted. Supports period-end close, monthly depreciation runs, and period-level CAPEX/OPEX reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Period; IFRS; GAAP; IAS 16

description: The fiscal year in which the asset transaction is recorded, as determined by the company code fiscal year variant. Used for annual CAPEX reporting, depreciation schedules, and statutory financial statements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year; IFRS; GAAP; IAS 16

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the group consolidation currency in which the group currency amount is expressed (e.g., USD for a US-headquartered multinational).
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS 10; IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI Group Currency

description: Indicates whether the asset transaction involves an intercompany transfer between two legal entities within the same corporate group. True for intercompany asset transfers requiring elimination in consolidated financial statements.
reference: IFRS 10; IAS 27; GAAP ASC 810; SAP S/4HANA FI Intercompany; Transfer Pricing Regulations

description: The date and time when the asset transaction record was last modified in the source system. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and audit trail for change tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Change; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Capture

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code functional (local) currency in which the local currency amount is expressed. Enables statutory reporting in the entity's home currency.
reference: ISO 4217; IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Currency

description: The net book value (carrying amount) of the asset in local currency immediately after the transaction is applied. Provides the closing balance and is used for balance sheet reporting and impairment assessment.
reference: IAS 16; IAS 36; GAAP ASC 360; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Net Book Value

description: The net book value (carrying amount) of the asset in local currency immediately before the transaction is applied. Provides the opening balance for the transaction and is essential for impairment testing and audit compliance.
reference: IAS 16; IAS 36; GAAP ASC 360; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Net Book Value

description: The date on which the asset transaction is posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period and fiscal year assignment for financial reporting and period-end close processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 250; IFRS Conceptual Framework

description: Current processing status of the asset transaction in the financial system. Indicates whether the transaction has been fully posted to the general ledger, reversed, parked for approval, blocked, or cleared.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Status; SOX Section 404; IFRS; GAAP

description: The SAP Controlling profit center associated with the asset transaction, enabling segment-level profitability analysis and EBITDA reporting by business unit or product line.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center Accounting; IFRS 8 Operating Segments; GAAP ASC 280

description: External or upstream reference document number associated with the asset transaction, such as a purchase order number, vendor invoice number, or internal transfer order number. Supports cross-system traceability and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reference Document; SOX Section 404; ISO 9001 Traceability

description: The remaining useful life of the asset in years at the time of the transaction posting. Used for impairment testing, depreciation recalculation, and asset lifecycle reporting.
reference: IAS 36 Impairment of Assets; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA

description: The SAP FI document number of the original transaction that this record reverses, or the document number of the reversal document if this transaction was itself reversed. Enables complete audit trail for corrected postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Reversal; SOX Section 404; IFRS; GAAP

description: Indicates whether this asset transaction is a reversal of a previously posted transaction. True if the transaction was created to reverse an erroneous or incorrect prior posting. Supports audit trail integrity and period-end correction processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Reversal; SOX Section 404; IFRS; GAAP

description: The gross financial amount of the asset transaction posted in the transaction currency. Represents the acquisition cost, retirement proceeds, write-down amount, or depreciation charge depending on the transaction type.
reference: IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS 16; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Amount Field

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the currency in which the asset transaction amount is originally denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency asset accounting for multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency

description: Free-text description or narrative entered at the time of posting the asset transaction, providing additional context about the nature of the financial movement (e.g., 'Acquisition of CNC machine line 3', 'Partial retirement due to component replacement').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Text; ISO 9001 Document Control; SOX Audit Trail

description: Business-level classification of the asset transaction category, grouping SAP transaction type codes into meaningful financial movement categories for CAPEX reporting, impairment testing, and audit compliance.
reference: IAS 16; IAS 36 Impairment of Assets; IFRS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: SAP FI-AA transaction type code identifying the nature of the financial movement on the asset (e.g., 100=External acquisition, 200=Retirement, 300=Transfer, 400=Write-down, 600=Depreciation posting). Drives posting logic and account determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Transaction Type; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: The total expected useful life of the asset in years at the time of the transaction, used to calculate the depreciation charge. May be revised upon impairment testing or asset revaluation.
reference: IAS 16 Paragraph 50; GAAP ASC 360-10; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Useful Life

description: The SAP Project System Work Breakdown Structure element associated with the asset transaction, used for capital project tracking and CAPEX settlement from projects to fixed assets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS WBS Element; IAS 16 Capitalization of Costs; GAAP ASC 360; CAPEX Project Accounting

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each corporate bank account record in the Manufacturing enterprise. Serves as the primary key for the bank_account data product in the finance domain silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Architecture Standard

description: The date on which the bank account was or is scheduled to be closed. Null for active accounts. Used for account lifecycle management and treasury decommissioning processes.
reference: IFRS 7; GAAP ASC 230; Internal Treasury Policy

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the bank account is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Drives multi-currency consolidation and foreign exchange exposure reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; GAAP ASC 830

description: The domestic bank account number assigned by the banking institution to the corporate account. Used for domestic payment processing and cash positioning. May differ from IBAN in non-SEPA regions.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; ISO 13616

description: The date on which the bank account was officially opened with the banking institution. Used for account lifecycle management, audit trails, and regulatory reporting of account tenure.
reference: IFRS 7; GAAP ASC 230; Internal Treasury Policy

description: The designated business purpose for which the bank account is used within Manufacturing treasury operations. Supports segregation of funds, internal controls, and cash flow categorization for CAPEX/OPEX tracking.
reference: IFRS 7; GAAP ASC 230; Internal Treasury Policy

description: Classification of the bank account by its operational purpose. Current accounts are used for day-to-day operations; deposit accounts for term investments; payroll accounts for employee salary disbursements; zero balance accounts for cash concentration; notional pooling for treasury cash pooling structures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR Account Type; IFRS 7

description: Full name of the primary authorized signatory for the bank account, as registered with the banking institution. Required for bank mandate documentation, internal controls, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001 Internal Controls; SOX Section 302/404; Internal Treasury Policy

description: Full name of the secondary authorized signatory for the bank account, as registered with the banking institution. Supports dual-control authorization requirements for high-value payment transactions.
reference: ISO 9001 Internal Controls; SOX Section 302/404; Internal Treasury Policy

description: The name of the specific branch of the banking institution where the account is maintained. Relevant for domestic accounts where branch-level identification is required for payment routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR House Bank Configuration

description: The city where the banking institution or branch is located. Used for bank relationship management and payment routing documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR House Bank Configuration

description: Email address of the primary relationship manager or contact person at the banking institution. Used for treasury operations communication and bank relationship management.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Internal Treasury Policy

description: Name of the primary relationship manager or contact person at the banking institution responsible for this account. Used for bank relationship management and escalation during payment disputes or account issues.
reference: Internal Treasury Policy; Bank Relationship Management

description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the banking institution is domiciled. Used for multi-currency consolidation, regulatory reporting, and cross-border payment compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IFRS 7 Financial Instruments Disclosures

description: The country-specific bank identification key used in SAP S/4HANA to identify the bank (e.g., BLZ in Germany, BSB in Australia, IFSC in India). Complements SWIFT/BIC for domestic payment routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Master Data; Country-Specific Banking Standards

description: The full legal name of the banking institution where the corporate account is held (e.g., Deutsche Bank AG, JPMorgan Chase Bank N.A.). Used for treasury reporting and bank relationship management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR House Bank Configuration

description: The American Bankers Association (ABA) routing transit number for US-domiciled bank accounts. Used for ACH and domestic wire payment routing in the United States.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; ABA Routing Number Standard

description: The electronic bank statement format used for automated reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA FI. MT940 and CAMT.053 are SWIFT/ISO 20022 formats; BAI2 is the US standard; OFX is used for online banking integrations.
reference: SWIFT MT940; ISO 20022 CAMT.053; BAI2 Format Standard; SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Statement

description: The reason code explaining why the bank account has been blocked or restricted from payment processing. Populated only when status is 'blocked' or 'under_review'. Supports internal controls and audit documentation.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO 9001 Internal Controls; Internal Treasury Policy

description: The role of this bank account within the cash pooling structure. Header accounts aggregate balances from participant accounts; participant accounts contribute to or draw from the pool. Not applicable for accounts outside a pooling arrangement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR Cash Pooling; IFRS 7; Internal Treasury Policy

description: The timestamp when the bank account master record was first created in the Manufacturing data platform. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking for SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 9001 Records Management; GDPR Article 5

description: Free-text description of the bank account providing additional context about its purpose, restrictions, or special characteristics. Used for treasury documentation and internal reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR House Bank Account; Internal Treasury Policy

description: Indicates whether electronic banking (e-banking) services are activated for this account, enabling automated bank statement downloads (MT940/CAMT.053) and electronic payment file submission in SAP S/4HANA FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Electronic Bank Statement; SWIFT MT940; ISO 20022 CAMT.053

description: The International Bank Account Number (IBAN) assigned to the corporate bank account, used for cross-border payment processing and SEPA transactions. Mandatory for accounts in SEPA-participating countries.
reference: ISO 13616; SEPA Regulation (EU) 260/2012

description: Indicates whether this bank account is designated for intercompany transactions between Manufacturing legal entities. Intercompany accounts require special handling for consolidation elimination and transfer pricing compliance.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810; Transfer Pricing Guidelines

description: The timestamp of the most recent update to the bank account master record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 9001 Records Management; GDPR Article 5

description: The date of the most recent bank statement reconciliation performed for this account in SAP S/4HANA FI. Supports month-end close processes and audit compliance for accounts payable and receivable.
reference: GAAP ASC 230; IFRS 7; SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Reconciliation

description: The maximum overdraft facility amount authorized by the banking institution for this account, expressed in the account currency. Used for liquidity planning and credit facility monitoring in treasury operations.
reference: IFRS 7; GAAP ASC 470; Internal Treasury Policy

description: Comma-separated list of payment methods authorized for use with this bank account (e.g., ACH, WIRE, CHECK, SEPA_CT, SEPA_DD, SWIFT). Drives payment program configuration in SAP S/4HANA FI-AP and FI-AR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Program; SEPA Regulation (EU) 260/2012; NACHA Rules

description: Indicates whether this bank account is designated exclusively for payroll disbursements. Payroll accounts are subject to additional internal controls, segregation of duties requirements, and labor law compliance.
reference: GAAP ASC 230; Internal Payroll Controls; Kronos Workforce Central Integration

description: The type of signing authority required to authorize transactions on this bank account. Single authority allows one signatory; dual authority requires two signatories; board resolution or power of attorney may be required for specific transaction types.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO 9001 Internal Controls; Internal Treasury Policy

description: The six-digit sort code identifying the bank and branch for UK-domiciled accounts. Used for BACS, CHAPS, and Faster Payments routing in the United Kingdom.
reference: UK Payments Administration; BACS Payment Schemes

description: Current operational status of the bank account. Active accounts are available for payment processing; blocked accounts are temporarily restricted; pending_closure accounts are in the decommissioning process; closed accounts are permanently deactivated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR Account Status; Internal Treasury Policy

business_glossary_term: SWIFT/BIC Code (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication / Bank Identifier Code)
description: The SWIFT/BIC code identifying the banking institution and branch for international wire transfers and correspondent banking. Consists of 8 or 11 alphanumeric characters per ISO 9362 standard.
reference: ISO 9362; SWIFT Standards

description: Primary key for the cash_application association
reference: Internal - M:N Association PK

description: Foreign key linking to the AR invoice record being cleared by the payment
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: Foreign key linking to the payment record that was applied to the invoice
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: The portion of the payment amount applied to this specific invoice in the transaction currency. Belongs to the cash application event, not to the payment (which may cover many invoices) nor to the invoice (which may be partially cleared by multiple payments).
reference: SAP FI-AR open item clearing amount (BSAD.DMBTR)

description: The date on which this specific payment-to-invoice matching event was posted in SAP FI-AR. Represents when the open item was cleared, distinct from the payment execution date or the invoice due date.
reference: SAP FI-AR clearing date (BSAD.AUGDT)

description: SAP accounting document number of the clearing transaction (payment, credit memo, or write-off) that settled this invoice. Supports payment reconciliation and audit trail. [Moved from ar_invoice: With the M:N cash application model in place, the clearing_document_number on ar_invoice becomes redundant and misleading — it can only store one payment reference, but an invoice may be cleared by multiple payments. The full clearing relationship is now captured in the cash_application association table. The clearing_document_number on payment should also be reviewed for the same reason.]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR (BSAD-AUGBL)

description: The early payment cash discount amount applied to this specific invoice within this payment application event. Differs from the invoice-level discount_amount as it reflects the actual discount realized at clearing time for this invoice-payment pair.
reference: SAP FI-AR cash discount taken (BSAD.SKFBT)

description: The customer-provided reference number or line identifier from the remittance advice that links this payment to this specific invoice. Used by AR teams to match incoming payments to open invoices during manual and automated cash application.
Unique surrogate identifier for each payment transaction record in the finance data product. Serves as the primary key for the payment entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Program

description: Foreign key linking to the AP invoice record being cleared or partially cleared by this payment.
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: Internal identifier of the company's house bank account used to execute or receive the payment. References the bank account master data in SAP, enabling bank statement reconciliation and liquidity management. Not a full account number (PCI-sensitive data is masked).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; PCI DSS; IFRS IAS 7

description: Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: payment.gl_account is a denormalized STRING reference to the GL clearing account used for payment postings. Payments clear through specific GL accounts (bank clearing, cash accounts). Adding chart_of_
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: Payments are processed within a controlling area that governs currency settings, cost center assignments, and financial consolidation scope. Adding controlling_area_id FK to payment establishes the CO
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Incoming payments are matched against a customer's credit profile to update outstanding balances and credit utilization. Cash application teams in manufacturing use this to release blocked orders and
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: payment uses fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) as separate denormalized fields. Adding fiscal_period_id FK creates a direct relational link to the fiscal_period master for period-level cash fl
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The gross payment amount in the transaction currency. For outgoing payments, this is the amount disbursed to the payee. For incoming payments, this is the amount received from the payer. Stored with 4 decimal places to support high-precision currencies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: The payment amount translated to the group (consolidation) currency for multinational consolidated financial reporting. Supports EBITDA, COGS, and group-level cash flow reporting under IFRS and GAAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21; IFRS 10; GAAP ASC 830

description: The payment amount converted to the local (company code) currency using the exchange rate at the time of posting. Used for statutory reporting, local GAAP compliance, and legal entity financial statements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: SAP payment block key indicating whether the payment is blocked from processing and the reason (e.g., R = manual block, A = blocked for payment, blank = not blocked). Used for payment approval workflows and dispute management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; SOX Section 302/404

description: SAP business area associated with the payment, representing a distinct operational segment or division within the company code. Supports segment-level financial reporting and internal management accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS 8; GAAP ASC 280

description: The monetary amount of the invoice cleared by this specific payment allocation. For partial payments, this is less than the invoice gross amount. Belongs to the clearing event, not to the payment or invoice alone.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: The date on which the open item (invoice or credit memo) was cleared against this payment in the sub-ledger. Used for days payable outstanding (DPO), days sales outstanding (DSO), and aging analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: The SAP document number of the clearing document that offsets the open item (invoice or credit memo) against this payment. Used for AP/AR sub-ledger reconciliation and open item management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: The SAP company code representing the legal entity that owns and posts the payment. Enables legal entity-level financial reporting, statutory compliance, and multi-entity consolidation across geographies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 810

description: The cost center to which the payment is attributed for internal cost accounting and OPEX/CAPEX tracking. Enables departmental cost allocation, budget variance analysis, and management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 420

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the legal entity or bank account country associated with the payment. Supports country-level regulatory reporting, REACH/RoHS compliance tracking, and multi-jurisdictional tax obligations.
reference: ISO 3166-1; SAP S/4HANA FI; GDPR Article 44; IFRS IAS 21

description: The timestamp when the payment record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA FI). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; SOX Section 302/404; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the transaction currency in which the payment is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Supports multi-currency operations across global legal entities.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: The value date on which the payment is executed and funds are transferred or received. Used for cash flow reporting, bank reconciliation, and period-close activities. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 7; GAAP ASC 230

description: Indicates whether the payment is outgoing (disbursement to a supplier via AP) or incoming (cash receipt from a customer via AR). Drives cash flow classification and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 7; GAAP ASC 230

description: The early payment cash discount amount deducted from the gross invoice amount when payment is made within the discount period. Impacts net payment amount and is tracked for cash discount income/expense reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: The cash discount amount actually captured on this specific clearing event. Reflects whether the early payment discount was applied at the time of this allocation. Belongs to the clearing event, not to the payment or invoice alone.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: The date of the original source document (e.g., check date, bank transfer initiation date) that triggered the payment. Used for document-level audit and aging analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1

description: The financial document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA FI upon posting of the payment transaction. Used for audit trail, clearing, and reconciliation purposes across AP and AR processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: The foreign currency exchange rate applied at the time of payment posting to convert the transaction currency amount to the local currency. Sourced from SAP exchange rate tables. Stored with 6 decimal places for precision.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: The SAP exchange rate type used for currency conversion (e.g., M = standard translation at average rate, B = bank buying rate, G = bank selling rate). Determines which rate table is applied for the conversion.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: The fiscal posting period (1–16, including special periods) within the fiscal year to which the payment is assigned. Supports period-level financial reporting and month-end close reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 34; GAAP ASC 270

description: The fiscal year to which the payment posting belongs, as determined by the company code fiscal year variant in SAP. Used for period-close, statutory reporting, and EBITDA/COGS analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 270

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the group consolidation currency (e.g., USD for a US-headquartered multinational). Used for consolidated financial reporting across all legal entities.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS 10; GAAP ASC 830

description: The SAP house bank key identifying the bank institution through which the payment is processed. Used for bank-level cash position reporting, bank fee analysis, and treasury management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Treasury; IFRS IAS 7

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this payment document has been reversed (cancelled) in the general ledger. A value of True means the payment has been voided and a reversal document exists. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; SOX Section 302/404

description: The timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; SOX Section 302/404; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code's local (functional) currency. Used for local statutory reporting and legal entity financial statements.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21

description: The instrument or mechanism used to execute the payment. Examples include bank transfer, check, ACH (Automated Clearing House), SEPA (Single Euro Payments Area) credit transfer, wire transfer, or direct debit. Sourced from SAP payment method configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; SEPA Regulation (EU) 260/2012; NACHA ACH Rules; IFRS IAS 7

description: Additional qualifier for the payment method, used in SAP to differentiate variants within a payment method (e.g., domestic vs. international wire, standard vs. express ACH). Supports bank-specific routing and format selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Program

description: The accounting date on which the payment document is posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period and year to which the transaction is assigned. May differ from payment_date due to bank processing lags.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: Free-text or structured reference transmitted with the payment to the bank or counterparty (e.g., remittance information, payment advice text, check memo line). Used for bank statement matching and counterparty reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; SEPA Regulation (EU) 260/2012; NACHA ACH Rules

description: External reference number associated with the payment, such as the supplier's invoice number, customer's remittance advice reference, or bank transaction reference. Supports cross-system reconciliation and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1

description: The date on which the payment reversal was posted to the general ledger. Populated only when is_reversed = True. Used for period-close adjustments and audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; SOX Section 302/404

description: The SAP document number of the reversal (cancellation) document created when this payment is voided. Populated only when is_reversed = True. Enables complete audit trail for reversed payment transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; SOX Section 302/404

description: Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction. Tracks progression from proposal through approval, bank processing, GL posting, clearing, and potential reversal or rejection. Critical for cash management and AP/AR reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: Classifies the nature of the payment transaction: AP payment run to suppliers, AR cash receipt from customers, intercompany settlement, employee expense reimbursement, tax remittance, or other. Supports sub-ledger reconciliation and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: The amount of withholding tax (WHT) deducted from the payment at source, as required by local tax regulations. Relevant for cross-border supplier payments and statutory tax reporting obligations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740; OECD Model Tax Convention

description: Unique surrogate key identifying each general ledger account record in the chart of accounts master. Used as the primary key for all downstream joins and references within the finance domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module - SKA1/SKB1 Account Master

description: Indicates which primary financial statement the account feeds into: balance sheet (assets, liabilities, equity), income statement (revenue, expense), cash flow statement, or statistical (non-financial tracking). Supports automated financial statement generation.
reference: IFRS IAS 1; IFRS IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows; GAAP ASC 230

description: The account group classification used to organize GL accounts into logical groupings for number range assignment, field selection, and reporting hierarchy. Corresponds to SAP field KTOKS defining account group.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKA1.KTOKS; IFRS IAS 1

description: The numeric level of this account within the financial reporting hierarchy (e.g., level 1 = major category, level 2 = sub-category, level 3 = detailed account). Supports drill-down financial reporting and rollup aggregations.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 205; Internal Financial Reporting Hierarchy

description: The full descriptive name or long text of the general ledger account providing complete context for the account purpose. Corresponds to SAP field TXT50 (long text).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKAT.TXT50; IFRS IAS 1

description: The short descriptive name of the general ledger account as defined in the chart of accounts. Used in financial reports, trial balances, and ERP display. Corresponds to SAP field TXT20 (short text).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKAT.TXT20; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: The alphanumeric code assigned to the general ledger account within the chart of accounts. This is the primary business identifier used in journal entries, financial statements, and ERP transactions. Corresponds to SAP GL account number (SAKNR).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKA1.SAKNR; IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 205

description: The fundamental financial classification of the account indicating its role in the financial statements: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense. Drives debit/credit behavior and financial statement placement.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 205; SAP S/4HANA FI - SKA1.KTOKS

description: The alternative account number used for local statutory reporting, mapping the group chart of accounts entry to the country-specific statutory chart of accounts. Supports multi-chart configurations for local GAAP versus group IFRS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKB1.ALTKT; IFRS IAS 27; Local GAAP Requirements

business_glossary_term: Balance in Local Currency Only Flag
description: Indicates whether the account balance is managed only in local (company code) currency, suppressing foreign currency balance tracking. Typically set for tax accounts and clearing accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKB1.XSALH; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: Indicates whether this account is blocked from receiving new journal entry postings. Blocked accounts are typically inactive, under review, or retired accounts that should not accept new transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKB1.XSPEB; Internal Financial Controls Policy; SOX Section 404

description: Classifies the account as relating to capital expenditure (CAPEX - investment in long-term assets) or operational expenditure (OPEX - day-to-day operating costs). Critical for CAPEX/OPEX tracking, budgeting, and EBITDA reporting in Manufacturing.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP ASC 360; Internal CAPEX/OPEX Policy; EBITDA Reporting Framework

description: The identifier of the chart of accounts plan to which this account belongs. Manufacturing may maintain multiple charts (e.g., group IFRS chart, local statutory chart per country). Corresponds to SAP field KTOPL.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKA1.KTOPL; IFRS IAS 27 Consolidated Financial Statements

description: Indicates whether this account contributes to the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation. Used to identify and aggregate all manufacturing cost accounts for gross margin and COGS reporting in income statements.
reference: IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330; Internal COGS Reporting Policy

description: The group-level consolidation account number to which this local chart of accounts entry maps during intercompany elimination and group financial consolidation. Enables multi-entity consolidation across Manufacturing legal entities.
reference: IFRS IAS 27 Consolidated Financial Statements; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; SAP S/4HANA FI - SKA1.BILKT

description: The consolidation group or reporting unit to which this account is assigned for group financial reporting. Supports multi-level consolidation hierarchies across Manufacturing's global legal entity structure.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; IFRS IAS 27; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Consolidation

description: Indicates whether this GL account is linked to a primary cost element (direct costs posted from FI to CO), secondary cost element (internal allocations within CO), or not applicable. Critical for COGS, EBITDA, and cost center accounting in SAP Controlling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Cost Element Accounting; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; GAAP ASC 330

description: The cost element type classification used in SAP Controlling to categorize the nature of costs or revenues flowing through this account. Supports COGS analysis, overhead allocation, and EBITDA reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO - Cost Element Master; IFRS IAS 2; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory Costs

description: The date on which this chart of accounts entry was originally created in the system. Provides audit trail for account master data governance and SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKA1.ERDAT; SOX Section 404; Internal Audit Policy

description: The ISO 4217 currency code in which this account is managed. For accounts with a specific currency assignment, postings are restricted to that currency. Blank indicates the account accepts all currencies (company code currency applies).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; GAAP ASC 830

business_glossary_term: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) Line Classification
description: Maps this account to a specific line in the EBITDA bridge or management P&L reporting structure. Enables automated EBITDA calculation and management reporting for Manufacturing's financial performance analysis.
reference: IFRS IAS 1; Internal Management Reporting Framework; EBITDA Reporting Standards; GAAP ASC 205

description: The field status group assigned to this account that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during document entry. Enforces data quality and completeness rules for postings to this account.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKB1.FSTAG; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The specific line item or caption on the published financial statement (e.g., 'Trade Receivables', 'Property Plant and Equipment', 'Cost of Goods Sold') to which this GL account maps. Enables automated financial statement population and IFRS/GAAP disclosure.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 205; IFRS Practice Statement 1

description: The functional area to which this account is assigned for cost-of-sales accounting and segment reporting. Enables income statement presentation by function of expense (e.g., manufacturing, R&D, G&A) as required under IFRS IAS 1.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements; SAP S/4HANA FI - Functional Area; GAAP ASC 280 Segment Reporting

description: The US GAAP ASC topic or classification code assigned to this account for entities reporting under US GAAP. Supports dual-reporting environments where Manufacturing entities must comply with both IFRS and US GAAP.
reference: FASB Accounting Standards Codification (ASC); GAAP ASC 205; SEC Regulation S-X

description: The IFRS standard or taxonomy classification code assigned to this account for group consolidation and statutory reporting under IFRS (e.g., IAS 16 PP&E, IAS 2 Inventories, IFRS 15 Revenue). Supports IFRS taxonomy mapping for XBRL reporting.
reference: IFRS Foundation IFRS Taxonomy; IAS 1; IAS 2; IAS 16; IFRS 15; IFRS 16

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this chart of accounts record. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements for financial master data governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKA1.AEDAT; SOX Section 404; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Audit Policy

description: Indicates whether individual line items posted to this account are stored and displayable for audit and reconciliation purposes. When enabled, all individual postings are retained for drill-down reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Line Item Display; IFRS IAS 1 Disclosure Requirements; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance. Assets and expenses normally carry debit balances; liabilities, equity, and revenue normally carry credit balances. Used for automated variance detection and trial balance validation.
reference: GAAP Double-Entry Bookkeeping Principles; IFRS Conceptual Framework

description: Indicates whether open item management is activated for this account, requiring individual line items to be matched and cleared (e.g., GR/IR clearing accounts, bank clearing accounts). Supports accounts payable/receivable reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Open Item Management; IFRS IAS 39; GAAP ASC 310

business_glossary_term: Posting Without Tax Code Allowed Flag
description: Indicates whether journal entry postings to this account are permitted without a tax code. When false, a tax code is mandatory for all postings, enforcing tax compliance controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; EU VAT Directive; GAAP ASC 740

description: Indicates whether the account is a profit and loss account (income statement) or a balance sheet account. Determines year-end closing behavior — P&L accounts are closed to retained earnings; balance sheet accounts carry forward.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKA1; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: Identifies if this GL account serves as a reconciliation account for a subledger (accounts receivable, accounts payable, fixed assets). Reconciliation accounts cannot be posted to directly; they are updated automatically from subledger transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Reconciliation Account; IFRS IAS 16; IFRS IAS 37; GAAP ASC 310

description: The sort key code that determines which document field is automatically populated in the assignment field of line items posted to this account. Used for sorting and grouping line items in account statements and open item lists.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - SKB1.ZUAWA; IFRS IAS 1

description: The current lifecycle status of the chart of accounts entry. Active accounts are available for posting; inactive accounts are not in use; blocked accounts are temporarily restricted; retired accounts are permanently closed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Account Master; Internal Chart of Accounts Governance Policy

description: Specifies the tax relevance of the account: input tax (deductible VAT on purchases), output tax (VAT on sales), tax account (for posting tax amounts), or not relevant. Controls tax code entry requirements during document posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; IFRS IAS 12 Income Taxes; EU VAT Directive; GAAP ASC 740

description: The date from which this account is valid and available for use in financial postings. Supports time-dependent account management and ensures accounts are only used within their authorized validity period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Account Master; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Financial Controls Policy

description: The date until which this account is valid for financial postings. After this date, the account is automatically blocked for new postings. Supports account lifecycle management and controlled retirement of obsolete accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Account Master; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Financial Controls Policy

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for the journal_entry data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module - BKPF Table

description: Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: journal_entry.gl_account_number is a denormalized STRING reference to the authoritative chart_of_accounts master. Adding chart_of_accounts_id FK normalizes this relationship, enabling JOIN to retrieve
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.controlling_area. Business justification: journal_entry.controlling_area (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the controlling_area master. Adding controlling_area_id FK normalizes this organizational scoping relationship for management acc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: journal_entry uses fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) as separate denormalized fields. Adding fiscal_period_id FK creates a direct relational link to the fiscal_period master, enabling period-s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.tax_code. Business justification: journal_entry.tax_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the tax_code master. Adding tax_code_id FK normalizes this tax reference, enabling proper tax reporting, VAT reconciliation, and complian
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The monetary amount of the journal entry line item translated into the group consolidation currency. Used for consolidated financial reporting and EBITDA/COGS analysis at the enterprise level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Parallel Ledger KGBTR; IFRS 10; GAAP ASC 830

description: The monetary amount of the journal entry line item translated into the company code local (functional) currency. Used for statutory reporting in the country of incorporation. Corresponds to DMBTR in SAP BSEG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BSEG.DMBTR; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: The monetary amount of the journal entry line item expressed in the transaction (document) currency. Positive for debits, negative for credits per SAP convention. Corresponds to WRBTR in SAP BSEG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BSEG.WRBTR; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: Alphanumeric assignment field used for sorting and matching open items in accounts payable and accounts receivable (e.g., payment reference, check number, clearing document). Corresponds to ZUONR in SAP BSEG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BSEG.ZUONR; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: SAP business area code used for cross-company-code segment reporting and internal balance sheet/P&L preparation by business division (e.g., Automation Systems, Electrification Solutions). Corresponds to GSBER in SAP BSEG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BSEG.GSBER; IFRS 8 - Operating Segments; GAAP ASC 280

description: The date on which the open item was cleared (matched/settled) in accounts payable or accounts receivable. Used for aging analysis and cash flow reporting. Corresponds to AUGDT in SAP BSEG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BSEG.AUGDT; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: The document number of the clearing document that settled this open item (e.g., payment document clearing an invoice). Populated when the line item has been cleared/matched. Corresponds to AUGBL in SAP BSEG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BSEG.AUGBL; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: The SAP company code representing the legal entity for which the journal entry is posted. Enables multi-entity financial consolidation and statutory reporting across all Manufacturing legal entities globally.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BKPF.BUKRS; IFRS 10 - Consolidated Financial Statements; GAAP ASC 810

description: The controlling cost center to which the journal entry is assigned for internal cost accounting and management reporting. Enables OPEX tracking by organizational unit (e.g., production plant, department). Corresponds to KOSTL in SAP BSEG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO - BSEG.KOSTL; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 280 - Segment Reporting

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the transaction currency in which the journal entry was originally recorded (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Supports multi-currency operations across all Manufacturing geographies. Corresponds to WAERS in SAP BKPF.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BKPF.WAERS; ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Indicates whether the journal entry line item is a debit (S=Soll/Debit) or credit (H=Haben/Credit) posting. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping integrity. Corresponds to SHKZG in SAP BSEG.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BSEG.SHKZG; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230; Double-Entry Bookkeeping Principles

description: The date of the original source document (e.g., invoice date, goods receipt date) that triggered the journal entry. May differ from posting date. Corresponds to BLDAT in SAP BKPF.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BKPF.BLDAT; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 606

description: Free-text description at the document header level providing context for the journal entry (e.g., 'Monthly depreciation run', 'Accrual reversal Q3'). Corresponds to BKTXT in SAP BKPF.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BKPF.BKTXT; IFRS IAS 1

description: The unique accounting document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA FI upon posting. Identifies the journal entry within a company code and fiscal year. Corresponds to BELNR in SAP BKPF.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BKPF.BELNR; IFRS IAS 1 - Financial Statement Presentation

description: Two-character SAP document type code that classifies the nature of the journal entry (e.g., SA=General Ledger, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, AB=Asset Posting, WA=Goods Issue, RE=Invoice Receipt). Controls number range assignment and account type permissibility. Corresponds to BLART in SAP BKPF.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BKPF.BLART; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 230

description: Human-readable description of the document type for reporting and analytics purposes (e.g., 'General Ledger Document', 'Vendor Invoice', 'Customer Invoice', 'Asset Posting').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - T003T; IFRS IAS 1

description: The exact date and time when the journal entry was created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Supports audit trail requirements and intraday financial monitoring. Derived from CPUDT and CPUTM in SAP BKPF.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - BKPF.CPUDT/CPUTM; IFRS IAS 1; SOX Section 302/404

description: The foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the local currency at the time of posting. Corresponds to KURSF in SAP BKPF.

description: Geographic region code (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER) for which this supplier preference is valid, supporting multinational procurement governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; SAP Ariba Supplier Management

description: Indicates whether the supplier has confirmed compliance with EU RoHS Directive for the designated category, required for electronics and electrical equipment procurement.
reference: EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; IEC 62321; SAP Ariba Supplier Management

description: Documented business justification for sole-source designation, required when is_single_source is true. Captures technical, commercial, or regulatory reasons for limiting to one supplier.
reference: SAP Ariba Sourcing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; Procurement Policy

description: Reference to the SAP Ariba sourcing event (RFQ/RFP) that resulted in this supplier being designated as preferred, providing traceability to the competitive sourcing process.
reference: SAP Ariba Sourcing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Procurement sourcing strategy alignment for this supplier-category combination, indicating whether the supplier is a sole source, one of multiple approved sources, or part of a strategic partnership.
reference: SAP Ariba Sourcing; Procurement Policy; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Target percentage of category spend to be directed to this supplier as part of a multi-source or quota-based sourcing strategy. Supports spend distribution governance.
reference: SAP Ariba Sourcing; Procurement Policy; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Current lifecycle status of the preferred supplier list entry, controlling whether buyers may use this supplier for the designated category.
reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Unique vendor identifier from the supplier master, referencing the approved supplier in SAP S/4HANA or SAP Ariba.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master; SAP Ariba Supplier Management

description: Start date from which this preferred supplier designation is effective and buyers may direct spend to this supplier for the designated category.
reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Expiry date after which this preferred supplier designation is no longer valid and must be renewed or replaced through the qualification and sourcing process.
reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the quota arrangement record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Quota Arrangement (MEQ1/EQUK)

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Quota arrangements split procurement volume across suppliers at the product-plant level. Procurement planners configure quotas per plant to manage multi-source supply strategies — a standard SAP MM op
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Quota arrangements define the split of procurement volume across multiple approved suppliers for a given material. Each quota arrangement line references a specific supplier. The current supplier_code
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supply_agreement. Business justification: Quota arrangements can reference supply agreements (outline agreements) that govern the sourcing split. The table has outline_agreement_number. Adding supply_agreement_id FK formalizes this relationsh
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Cumulative quantity already allocated to this supplier under the quota arrangement (EQUK-ZUGMG). Used by MRP to determine which supplier should receive the next procurement order based on quota ratios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-ZUGMG; MRP II

description: Date on which the quota arrangement was formally approved by the authorized procurement authority. Required for compliance with procurement governance policies.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP S/4HANA MM Release Strategy

description: Name or user ID of the procurement manager or category manager who approved the quota arrangement. Supports audit trail and procurement governance requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP S/4HANA MM Release Strategy

description: Business key identifying the quota arrangement in the source system (SAP MM EQUK table key). Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MEQ1/EQUK

description: Procurement category or material group code classifying the material under the quota arrangement (e.g., raw materials, MRO, packaging). Supports spend analytics and category management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group (T023); SAP Ariba Category Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the supplier sources or manufactures the material. Relevant for trade compliance, REACH/RoHS, and supply chain risk management.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Timestamp when the quota arrangement record was originally created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to pricing and value fields within this quota arrangement. Supports multi-currency procurement in multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Reference to the SAP purchasing info record (EINE/EINA) that defines the price and conditions for this supplier-material combination. Links quota to negotiated pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record (EINE/EINA)

description: Indicates whether this quota line is blocked from being used in automatic source determination. A blocked line is excluded from MRP source selection without being deleted.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MEQ1; Supplier Qualification Management

description: Indicates whether this quota line is designated as a fixed source of supply, bypassing quota ratio calculations and always directing procurement to this supplier. Supports sole-source or preferred-supplier strategies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Source List (EORD); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quota arrangement record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Date on which the quota base quantity and allocated quantity counters were last reset. Quota resets are performed periodically to rebalance supplier allocations and reflect updated volume agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MEQ1 Quota Reset; MRP II

description: Sequential line number within the quota arrangement identifying each supplier's quota item (EQUK-ZEILE). Multiple lines exist per arrangement, one per approved supplier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MEQ1/EQUK Table

description: Short descriptive text for the material subject to the quota arrangement, supporting readability in reports and analytics without requiring a join to the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master (MAKT)

description: Maximum order quantity that can be placed with this supplier in a single procurement order under the quota arrangement. Supports capacity and risk management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-MAXBM; MRP II Lot Sizing

description: Minimum order quantity that must be placed with this supplier under the quota arrangement. Prevents splitting orders below economically viable quantities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-MINBM; MRP II Lot Sizing

description: Free-text field for capturing business rationale, strategic sourcing decisions, risk diversification justifications, or other contextual information related to the quota arrangement.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Line item number within the outline agreement referenced by this quota arrangement line. Required when the agreement contains multiple material lines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement (EKPO)

description: Reference to the SAP scheduling agreement or contract (EKKO) associated with this quota line. Enables procurement to be executed against pre-negotiated framework agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement (EKKO/EKPO)

description: Numeric priority rank assigned to this quota line relative to other suppliers for the same material and plant. Lower values indicate higher priority for source selection when quota ratios are equal.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MEQ1; Supplier Risk Diversification Strategy

description: Indicates the procurement method for this quota line — external procurement from a supplier, subcontracting, consignment, or internal stock transfer. Drives MRP planning logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; MRP II

description: SAP purchasing organization responsible for negotiating and managing the quota arrangement. Determines the procurement authority and supplier contracts applicable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Organization; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Base quantity used as the starting point for quota usage calculation (EQUK-QUOGR). Resets periodically and is used in conjunction with allocated quantity to determine the next source of supply.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-QUOGR

description: Percentage of total procurement volume allocated to this supplier for the material and plant. All active quota lines for a material/plant must sum to 100%. Core field for multi-source supply strategy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-QUOTE; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Number of times this quota line has been selected during automatic source determination (EQUK-QUNUM). Used alongside allocated quantity to track supplier selection frequency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-QUNUM

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this quota arrangement record was extracted. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Type of procurement source assigned to this quota line. Indicates whether the quota directs procurement to a vendor directly, a contract, or a scheduling agreement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-QUTYP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: SAP special procurement key (EQUK-SOBSL) that refines the procurement type, e.g., subcontracting (30), consignment (10), or stock transfer from another plant. Used in MRP source determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-SOBSL; MRP II

description: Current lifecycle status of the quota arrangement line. Drives whether the line is considered during MRP/MRP II source determination and purchase order creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MEQ1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: For stock transfer quota lines, identifies the SAP plant that supplies the material. Applicable when procurement type is internal stock transfer between plants.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-RESWK; Stock Transfer Order

description: Unit of measure for quota base quantity and allocated quantity (e.g., EA, KG, PC, M). Aligns with the material master base unit of measure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-MEINS; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Date from which the quota arrangement line becomes effective and eligible for automatic source determination during MRP runs and purchase order creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-DATAB; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Date on which the quota arrangement line expires and is no longer used for source determination. Supports contract and supplier agreement lifecycle management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM EQUK-DATBI; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each source list record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for the source_list data product.
reference: SAP MM Source List (ME01/EORD) - Internal Primary Key

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.approved_manufacturer. Business justification: The procurement source list (which suppliers can supply a given part) must be constrained to engineering's AML. Only AML-approved manufacturers may appear on the source list — this link enforces that
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plant. Business justification: Source lists in manufacturing are maintained at the product-plant level to define valid supply sources per plant. MRP uses source lists to automatically assign planned orders to approved suppliers for
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.quota_arrangement. Business justification: Source list entries can reference quota arrangements that define the volume split across multiple approved suppliers. The table has quota_arrangement_number. Adding quota_arrangement_id FK formalizes
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supply_agreement. Business justification: Source list entries can reference supply agreements as the approved source of supply for a material. The table has outline_agreement_number. Adding supply_agreement_id FK formalizes this relationship
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Type of supply agreement referenced by this source list entry. Scheduling agreement = delivery schedule-based; Contract = quantity/value contract; Blanket PO = open purchase order; None = spot/info record-based sourcing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Outline Agreement Types (LP/LPA/MK/WK); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Name or user ID of the procurement professional or category manager who approved this source list entry. Supports audit trail and procurement governance requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP S/4HANA MM - Change Documents

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material is manufactured or sourced from. Used for trade compliance, import/export controls, REACH/RoHS compliance, and supply chain risk assessment.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; US Customs and Border Protection

description: Timestamp when this source list record was first created in the source system (SAP MM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.
