reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD Change Documents; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the pricing currency agreed with this supplier for this material. Used for multi-currency procurement and spend analytics in multinational operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Reference to the SAP purchasing info record that holds the negotiated price and conditions for this material-supplier-plant combination. Used when no outline agreement exists.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (EINE/EINA); SAP ME11 Transaction

description: Indicates whether this source of supply is explicitly blocked from being used for procurement. A blocked source cannot be selected for purchase requisitions or purchase orders, even if within the validity period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD-SPERE; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Indicates whether this supplier is designated as the fixed (mandatory) source of supply for the material-plant combination. When true, MRP will exclusively generate purchase requisitions for this supplier, overriding other approved sources.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD-FESTE; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Indicates whether the source list is mandatory for MRP to generate purchase requisitions for this material-plant combination. When true, MRP will only create purchase requisitions for sources listed in the source list.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master MRP2 View (MARC-KORDB); MRP II

description: Indicates whether this supplier is the preferred (but not mandatory) source of supply. When multiple approved sources exist, the preferred source is prioritized during manual and automatic sourcing decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Source List; SAP Ariba Supplier Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this source list record in the source system. Supports change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD Change Documents; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Date when this source list entry was last reviewed and validated by the procurement or category management team. Supports supplier qualification governance and periodic source list maintenance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP Ariba Supplier Management

description: Short descriptive text for the material as maintained in the material master. Provides human-readable context for the source list entry without requiring a join to the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (MAKT)

description: SAP material group (commodity category) classifying the material for spend analytics, category management, and sourcing strategy alignment. Used in procurement reporting and supplier segmentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Group (T023); SAP Ariba Category Management

description: Maximum quantity that can be ordered from this supplier in a single purchase order. Used to enforce supply capacity constraints and split large requirements across multiple sources.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record; Supply Chain Planning

description: Minimum quantity that must be ordered from this supplier in a single purchase order. MRP considers this constraint when generating purchase requisitions for this source.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (EINE-MINBM); SAP Ariba

description: Controls how MRP uses this source list entry when generating purchase requisitions. 0 = Not relevant for MRP (manual sourcing only); 1 = MRP can generate purchase requisitions for this source; 2 = Source is blocked for MRP (MRP must not use this source). Directly maps to SAP EORD-MODUS field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD-MODUS; MRP II (Manufacturing Resource Planning)

description: Free-text field for additional context, sourcing rationale, exceptions, or special instructions related to this source list entry. Used by procurement teams to document sourcing decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Source List Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Business-facing identifier for the source list entry as assigned in SAP MM (EORD table key). Used for cross-referencing with procurement documents and MRP outputs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD Table; SAP ME01 Transaction

description: Unit of measure in which the material is ordered from this supplier (e.g., EA, KG, M, L, PC). May differ from the material's base unit of measure and is specific to this supplier relationship.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD/EINE; ISO 80000 Units of Measurement

description: Line item number within the referenced outline agreement (scheduling agreement or contract) that corresponds to this source list entry. Required when the agreement covers multiple materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Outline Agreement Item (EKPO)

description: Reference to the SAP scheduling agreement or contract (outline agreement) that governs the supply relationship for this source list entry. Links the source list to a binding supply agreement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Outline Agreement (EKKO/EKPO); SAP ME31L/ME31K

description: For internal/inter-plant supply types, identifies the supplying plant (internal manufacturing or distribution center) that will fulfill the demand. Applicable when supply_type is internal_plant or stock_transfer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD-RESWK; Stock Transfer Order (STO) Processing

description: Classifies whether the material is procured as direct material (used in production, part of BOM) or indirect material (MRO, services, overhead). Drives different procurement workflows and spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master; COGS vs OPEX classification; GAAP/IFRS

description: SAP purchasing organization responsible for negotiating and managing the supply agreement with the supplier for this material-plant combination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Organization (T024E)

description: Reference to the SAP quota arrangement that governs the percentage split of procurement volume across multiple approved sources for this material-plant combination. Enables multi-source supply strategies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Quota Arrangement (MEQ1/EQUK/EQUP)

description: Percentage of total procurement volume for this material-plant combination allocated to this supplier source. Used in multi-source supply strategies to distribute demand across approved suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Quota Arrangement (EQUP-QUOTE); Supply Chain Risk Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this source list record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse architecture.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer - Data Lineage; Enterprise Data Architecture Standards

description: SAP special procurement type code indicating non-standard procurement scenarios such as subcontracting (supplier processes company-owned components), consignment (supplier-owned stock), third-party (direct shipment to customer), or pipeline (continuous supply).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Special Procurement Type (MARC-SOBSL); MRP Planning

description: Standard procurement lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt for this material-supplier-plant combination. Used by MRP for scheduling and delivery date calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (EINE-WEBAZ); MRP II Planning

description: Current operational status of the source list entry. Indicates whether the source is currently active and usable for procurement, expired due to validity period lapse, blocked, or pending approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Source List Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Supplier sub-range code identifying a specific product line, manufacturing site, or division within the supplier's organization that is the approved source. Relevant for large suppliers with multiple production facilities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD-LTSNR; Vendor Sub-Range (LFM2)

description: Categorizes the type of supply relationship for this source list entry. External supplier = standard third-party procurement; Internal plant = inter-plant stock transfer; Subcontracting = supplier processes company-owned materials; Consignment = supplier-owned stock at company premises; Stock transfer = intra-company movement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Item Category (EKPO-PSTYP); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Defines whether the source list is mandatory (procurement must use only listed sources) or optional (source list is advisory). Controlled at the material master level but captured here for analytical context.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master MRP2 View (MARC-KORDB)

description: Start date of the validity period during which this source of supply is approved and active. MRP will only consider this source for automatic purchase requisition generation within the validity window.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD-VDATU; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: End date of the validity period for this source of supply. After this date, the source is no longer considered by MRP for automatic purchase requisition generation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EORD-BDATU; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the supply agreement record in the procurement data platform. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement (ME31L/ME31K)

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profitability_segment. Business justification: Supply agreements for direct materials are linked to profitability segments so procurement costs can be traced to product lines or customer segments in margin analysis reporting.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Every supply agreement and blanket purchase agreement is negotiated with a specific supplier. The current supplier_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the supplier master. Adding supplier_id
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Supply agreements define contracted quantities and pricing in a specific unit of measure. Procurement contracts teams reference the product UOM to ensure agreement terms are legally and operationally
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Business-facing document number assigned to the supply agreement, corresponding to the SAP MM Outline Agreement document number (e.g., scheduling agreement or contract number). Used for cross-system reference and supplier communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement (ME31L/ME31K)

description: Classification of the supply agreement by its commercial and operational structure. Scheduling agreements govern delivery schedules; blanket orders allow multiple releases up to a committed value; framework contracts define terms for future individual orders; master supply agreements set overarching terms across multiple categories.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement (ME31L/ME31K); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 External Provision

description: Current status of the internal approval workflow for this supply agreement. Tracks progression through the procurement authorization hierarchy before the agreement becomes active and releases are permitted.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Release Strategy; SAP Ariba Contract Management; GAAP Internal Controls

description: Name or user ID of the procurement authority who granted final approval for this supply agreement. Required for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Release Strategy; GAAP Internal Controls; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Date on which the supply agreement received final internal approval and was authorized for release. Used for procurement cycle time analysis and compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Release Strategy; GAAP Internal Controls

description: Unique contract identifier assigned by SAP Ariba Contract Management for this supply agreement. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the Ariba contract repository and SAP MM Outline Agreement.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: Indicates whether this supply agreement automatically renews upon expiry unless formally terminated by either party within the notice period. Drives contract lifecycle management alerts and renewal workflows.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; IFRS 15

description: Procurement commodity or spend category code (e.g., UNSPSC or internal taxonomy) classifying the goods or services covered by this supply agreement. Enables spend analytics, category management, and strategic sourcing alignment.
reference: UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code); SAP Ariba Category Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the primary country from which goods or services are supplied under this agreement. Used for supply chain risk assessment, import/export compliance, and REACH/RoHS regulatory tracking.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Timestamp when the supply agreement record was first created in the procurement system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary values within this supply agreement are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency procurement operations across global manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Date on which the supply agreement expires and no further releases are permitted without renewal. Corresponds to the validity end date in SAP MM Outline Agreement. Used for contract renewal alerts and compliance monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Validity Period; IFRS 15

description: Date from which the supply agreement becomes legally effective and purchase order releases are permitted. Corresponds to the validity start date in SAP MM Outline Agreement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Validity Period; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and supplier under this supply agreement.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Delivery Terms

description: Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact location where risk and cost transfer between supplier and buyer (e.g., 'Port of Hamburg' for FOB, 'Buyer Warehouse, Detroit' for DDP).
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement

description: Minimum quantity that must be ordered per release or call-off against this supply agreement, as contractually agreed with the supplier. Violations may trigger penalty clauses or supplier non-compliance flags.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Conditions; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the supply agreement record. Used for change detection, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: SAP payment terms key defining the payment conditions negotiated under this supply agreement (e.g., net 30, 2/10 net 30, immediate payment). Drives accounts payable processing and cash flow planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms; GAAP Accounts Payable

description: Textual description of the penalty terms applicable under this supply agreement, including trigger conditions, penalty calculation method, and maximum liability caps. Populated when penalty_clause_flag is true.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Indicates whether this supply agreement contains contractual penalty clauses for supplier non-performance, late delivery, quality failures, or minimum volume shortfalls. When true, penalty terms are documented in the agreement.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility that is the primary recipient of goods or services under this supply agreement. Relevant for scheduling agreements tied to specific production sites.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Configuration

description: Type of pricing mechanism agreed with the supplier. Fixed price locks unit cost for the agreement duration; tiered volume pricing adjusts price based on quantity thresholds; index-linked pricing ties price to a commodity index; cost-plus adds a margin to supplier cost.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Pricing Conditions (ME31L/ME31K); IFRS 15 Variable Consideration

description: Classification of the procurement scope as direct materials (used in production), indirect materials (operational supplies), MRO (Maintenance Repair and Operations), services, or capital expenditure. Drives accounting treatment, approval workflows, and spend reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Type; GAAP/IFRS Cost Classification

description: SAP purchasing group code identifying the buyer or buyer team responsible for day-to-day management of this supply agreement, including release creation and supplier communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Organizational Structure

description: SAP purchasing organization code responsible for negotiating and managing this supply agreement. Determines the organizational unit with procurement authority and defines the scope of the agreement within the enterprise hierarchy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Organizational Structure; GAAP Procurement Controls

description: Cumulative quantity of goods or services released against this supply agreement through purchase order releases or delivery schedule lines. Used to track agreement consumption against committed volume.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Release Documentation

description: Cumulative monetary value of all purchase order releases issued against this supply agreement to date. Compared against total_committed_value to monitor agreement utilization and remaining open commitment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Release Documentation; GAAP Commitment Accounting

description: Number of calendar days prior to agreement expiry by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Used to trigger contract renewal review workflows and supplier negotiation planning.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: SAP MM document number for the Outline Agreement (scheduling agreement or contract) created via transaction ME31L or ME31K. Primary cross-reference key for integration with SAP S/4HANA procurement and finance modules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement (ME31L/ME31K)

description: Reference number of the SAP Ariba sourcing event (RFQ/RFP/auction) that resulted in the award of this supply agreement. Provides traceability from competitive sourcing to contracted agreement for audit and compliance purposes.
reference: SAP Ariba Sourcing; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; GAAP Procurement Controls

description: Current lifecycle status of the supply agreement. Drives procurement workflow, release authorization, and reporting. Active agreements permit purchase order releases; suspended agreements are temporarily blocked; terminated agreements are closed before natural expiry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement; SAP Ariba Contract Management

description: Actual date on which the supply agreement was terminated prior to its natural expiry, if applicable. Populated only for agreements with status 'terminated'. Used for supplier relationship analysis and spend impact assessment.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; IFRS 15

description: Reason code for early termination of the supply agreement. Supports supplier performance analysis, sourcing strategy reviews, and risk management reporting.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Short descriptive title of the supply agreement used for identification in procurement systems, dashboards, and supplier communications. Typically includes supplier name, commodity category, and agreement year.
reference: SAP Ariba Contract Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Total volume of goods or services committed under this supply agreement over its validity period, expressed in the agreement unit of measure. Applicable primarily to scheduling agreements and volume-based contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Target Quantity; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Total monetary value committed under this supply agreement over its validity period, as negotiated with the supplier. For blanket orders, this is the maximum release value. For scheduling agreements, this is the total planned spend. Used for spend commitment reporting and budget planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Target Value; IFRS 15; GAAP

description: Negotiated unit price for the primary material or service covered by this supply agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. For tiered or index-linked agreements, this represents the base or reference price.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Conditions; IFRS 15

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each delivery schedule line released against a supply agreement or blanket Purchase Order (PO). Serves as the primary key for the delivery_schedule data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Scheduling Agreement Delivery Schedule (ME38/ME39)

description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: In drop-ship or direct-to-customer delivery models common in industrial manufacturing, supplier delivery schedules reference the customer's ship-to address directly. Eliminates address duplication and
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.schedule_line. Business justification: Supplier delivery schedules in procurement are directly aligned to customer-facing schedule lines in sales orders. Supply chain coordinators map inbound delivery dates to outbound schedule commitments
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Supplier delivery schedules in industrial manufacturing are structured around customer SLA commitments. Logistics and procurement teams use the SLA agreement to set inbound delivery windows that guara
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Delivery schedule lines are fulfilled by a specific supplier. The current supplier_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference. Adding supplier_id as a FK to the supplier master normalizes the supplier
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supply_agreement. Business justification: Delivery schedules are released against supply agreements (scheduling agreements in SAP). This is a fundamental parent-child relationship in the scheduling agreement lifecycle. agreement_number is ret
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Calendar date on which the supplier actually delivered the material and the Goods Receipt Note (GRN) was posted. Compared against scheduled_delivery_date to calculate on-time delivery performance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Goods Receipt Posting Date (MKPF-BUDAT); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

business_glossary_term: Supply Agreement / Blanket Purchase Order (PO) Number
description: Reference number of the parent supply agreement or blanket Purchase Order (PO) against which this delivery schedule line is released. Links the schedule to its governing contractual instrument.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Number (ME31L/ME32L)

description: Quantity confirmed by the supplier as committed for delivery on the scheduled date. May differ from scheduled quantity due to supplier capacity constraints or partial confirmations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Confirmation (EKET-WEMNG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Timestamp when the delivery schedule line was first created in the source system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Document Creation Timestamp; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Running cumulative total of all quantities actually received against this scheduling agreement line from the start of the agreement period. Enables cumulative quantity reconciliation and supplier delivery performance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Cumulative Received Quantity (EKET-WEMNG cumulative); VDA 4905 / ODETTE

description: Running cumulative total of all scheduled quantities from the beginning of the scheduling agreement period to the current schedule line. Used in automotive and lean manufacturing for cumulative quantity reconciliation with suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Cumulative Quantity (EKET-KUMULNG); VDA 4905 / ODETTE Delivery Schedule Standard

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit price and schedule value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Supports multi-currency procurement operations across global manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI - Currency Configuration

description: Number of calendar days the actual delivery was early (negative value) or late (positive value) relative to the scheduled delivery date. Key metric for supplier delivery performance scorecards.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP S/4HANA MM - Delivery Performance

description: Quantity actually received and posted via Goods Receipt Note (GRN) processing against this schedule line. Used to calculate delivery completeness and schedule adherence.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Goods Receipt (MIGO/EKET-WEMNG); GRN Processing

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the delivery destination, supporting multinational logistics, customs compliance, and cross-border trade documentation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; Incoterms 2020; EU CE Marking / RoHS / REACH Compliance

description: Supplier-provided delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the physical shipment. Used for three-way matching (PO, GRN, invoice) and discrepancy resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Delivery Note (EKBE-XBLNR); Procurement Three-Way Match

description: Reference number of the Goods Receipt Note (GRN) document posted in SAP S/4HANA upon physical receipt of the delivery. Links the schedule line to the inventory posting and financial accrual.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Document (MKPF-MBLNR); GRN Processing

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the transfer of risk, cost responsibility, and delivery obligations between buyer and supplier for this schedule line.
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC - International Chamber of Commerce); SAP S/4HANA MM - Incoterms (EKPO-INCO1)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the actual delivery date fell within the scheduled delivery window (true) or was late/early (false). Populated upon GRN posting and used for supplier on-time delivery (OTD) performance KPI reporting.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP S/4HANA MM - Delivery Performance Monitoring

description: Latest acceptable timestamp for supplier delivery under JIT (Just-In-Time) replenishment rules. Deliveries after this timestamp are considered late and trigger schedule adherence exceptions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - JIT Delivery Schedule (JITL); Lean Manufacturing / JIT Principles

description: Earliest acceptable timestamp for supplier delivery under JIT (Just-In-Time) replenishment rules. Deliveries before this timestamp are considered early and may be rejected or incur storage costs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - JIT Delivery Schedule (JITL); Lean Manufacturing / JIT Principles

description: Unique identifier of the Kanban replenishment signal that triggered this delivery schedule line. Applicable for Kanban-managed materials where pull signals drive supplier delivery requests.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Kanban Control Cycle (PK01); Lean Manufacturing / Kanban Methodology

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the delivery schedule line in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Document Change Timestamp; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5

description: Sequential line item number within the delivery schedule document, distinguishing individual delivery date/quantity combinations within the same schedule release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Line Item

description: Short description of the material or component to be delivered, providing human-readable context for the material number in reports and supplier communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master Description (MAKT)

description: SAP material number or internal part number identifying the specific raw material, component, or indirect material to be delivered. Used for MRP (Material Requirements Planning) reconciliation and inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (MARA/MARC); MRP II

description: Type of MRP (Material Requirements Planning) planning element that generated or is associated with this delivery schedule line, indicating whether it originated from a planned order, purchase requisition, or scheduling agreement release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MRP Element (MD04); MRP II Planning

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center that is the destination for the scheduled delivery. Drives MRP planning and inventory posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Plant (T001W)

description: SAP purchasing group (buyer group) code identifying the individual buyer or team responsible for managing this delivery schedule and supplier relationship.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Group (T024)

description: SAP purchasing organization code responsible for managing the supply agreement and releasing delivery schedules. Represents the organizational unit with procurement authority.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Organization (T024E)

description: Indicates whether this schedule line is a forecast release (planning horizon, non-binding), a JIT (Just-In-Time) release (firm, short-term), an immediate release, or a manually created release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Release Type (EKAB-ABART); JIT / Lean Manufacturing

description: SAP S/4HANA internal 10-digit document number for the scheduling agreement (Outline Agreement) from which this delivery schedule line was released. Enables direct traceability to the source system record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement (EKKO-EBELN)

description: Categorical assessment of supplier delivery adherence for this schedule line: on_time (within window), early (before JIT window start), late (after scheduled date), partial (quantity shortfall), missed (no delivery), or pending (not yet due).
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; Lean Manufacturing / JIT Delivery Performance

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the delivery schedule document, used for cross-system reference and supplier communication. Corresponds to the scheduling agreement release number in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement (SA) Release Number

description: Classification of the delivery schedule indicating the replenishment strategy: JIT (Just-In-Time) for time-critical lean deliveries, forecast for planning horizon releases, firm for committed quantities, or Kanban for pull-based replenishment signals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Category; Lean Manufacturing / JIT Principles

description: Planned calendar date on which the supplier is required to deliver the material to the specified plant or delivery location. Core field for JIT delivery window management and supplier on-time delivery (OTD) performance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Delivery Date (EKET-EINDT); JIT / Lean Manufacturing

description: Specific time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) within the scheduled delivery date by which the supplier must deliver, supporting JIT (Just-In-Time) delivery window precision and dock scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Delivery Time; JIT Delivery Window Management

description: Quantity of material scheduled for delivery on the specified date, expressed in the base unit of measure. Drives MRP (Material Requirements Planning) supply coverage calculations and Kanban replenishment signal sizing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Schedule Quantity (EKET-MENGE); MRP / MRP II

description: Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code used for warehouse and inventory tracking of the scheduled material, aligning with Infor WMS inventory records.
reference: Infor WMS - SKU Master; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Current lifecycle status of the delivery schedule line, tracking progression from initial release through supplier confirmation, shipment, receipt, and closure. Supports schedule adherence monitoring and exception management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 (External Provider Control)

description: SAP storage location within the receiving plant where the delivered material will be physically stored upon Goods Receipt Note (GRN) processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Storage Location (T001L); Infor WMS

description: Maximum percentage by which the supplier may over-deliver against the scheduled quantity without triggering a rejection or return. Defined in the supply agreement and enforced during GRN processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Over-Delivery Tolerance (EKPO-UEBTO); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Maximum percentage by which the supplier may under-deliver against the scheduled quantity and still have the delivery accepted as complete. Defined in the supply agreement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Under-Delivery Tolerance (EKPO-UNTTO); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Base unit of measure for the scheduled, confirmed, and delivered quantities (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter, L for liter, PC for piece). Aligns with SAP material master UoM configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Unit of Measure (T006); ISO 80000 International System of Units

description: Agreed price per unit of measure for the material as specified in the supply agreement or blanket PO. Used for financial accruals, goods receipt valuation, and spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scheduling Agreement Price (EKPO-NETPR); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each demand forecast record in the silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality Standards

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_hierarchy. Business justification: Demand forecasts are often aggregated and disaggregated along customer account hierarchies (e.g., global OEM vs. regional subsidiary). Procurement uses account hierarchy to roll up or drill down forec
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: Demand forecasts are based on blanket orders (framework agreements) with committed volumes. Supply planning uses this to align procurement with contracted customer demand over planning horizons.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Demand forecasts are bucketed by fiscal period to align procurement planning with financial budgeting cycles. Finance uses period-aligned forecasts to project cash outflows and purchase commitments.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_forecast. Business justification: Manufacturing procurement uses sales forecasts to drive MRP demand planning and material requirements. Production planning teams reference sales projections daily to determine raw material purchasing
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Demand forecasts in manufacturing are structured against product hierarchy levels (product family, category, SKU). Demand planners aggregate and disaggregate forecasts through the hierarchy — a core d
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: Demand forecasts are generated at the product variant level to drive variant-specific material requirements. Procurement planners reference product variants daily when building forecasts that feed MRP
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Demand forecasts in industrial manufacturing are driven by customer segment demand signals (e.g., automotive vs. energy). Procurement planners segment forecasts by customer category to align material
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.spend_category. Business justification: Demand forecasts are organized by product/spend category to drive category-level supply planning and MRP inputs. The existing product_category_code (STRING) is a denormalized code; adding spend_catego
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Demand forecasts are created at the material/stock position level to project future inventory needs. Procurement planners reference current stock positions when building forecasts to calculate net pro
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Demand forecasts must be expressed in the product's defined unit of measure to ensure MRP calculations and procurement quantities are consistent. UOM is a mandatory reference on every forecast record.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: ABC inventory classification of the forecasted material based on consumption value: A (high value/high attention), B (medium), C (low value/low attention). Drives differentiated forecasting and safety stock policies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM ABC Analysis; APICS CPIM Inventory Management

description: Name or user ID of the demand planner or S&OP manager who approved and locked the consensus forecast for MRP consumption. Supports audit trail and accountability in the S&OP governance process.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management — Records Control; SAP S/4HANA Workflow

description: Date on which the forecast was formally approved and locked for MRP input. Used for S&OP cycle governance, audit compliance, and measuring planning cycle time.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management; SAP S/4HANA Demand Management

description: Manual quantity adjustment applied to the statistical baseline during the S&OP consensus process. Positive values indicate upward revision; negative values indicate downward revision. Captures commercial intelligence not reflected in historical data.
reference: APICS S&OP Framework; SAP S/4HANA Demand Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or demand origin location. Supports regional demand aggregation, cross-border supply planning, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; EU CE Marking; RoHS/REACH Compliance

description: Timestamp when the demand forecast record was first created in the source system or ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) processing.
reference: ISO 8601 Date/Time; DAMA DMBOK Data Lineage; GDPR Article 5 Accountability

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for any monetary values associated with the forecast (e.g., forecasted spend, standard cost valuation). Supports multi-currency reporting in global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Classification of the demand type: independent (end-customer driven), dependent (derived from parent BOM), spare parts (aftermarket service), promotional (campaign-driven), new product introduction, or phase-out. Drives appropriate forecasting methodology selection.
reference: APICS CPIM Demand Management; SAP S/4HANA Demand Management

description: Statistical or algorithmic method used to generate the baseline forecast. Supports model governance, accuracy benchmarking, and algorithm selection optimization within the S&OP process.
reference: APICS CPIM Forecasting; SAP S/4HANA Demand Management Forecast Models

description: Systematic directional error in the forecast, calculated as the average of (forecast minus actual) over the evaluation period. Positive bias indicates consistent over-forecasting; negative bias indicates under-forecasting. Used to detect and correct systemic forecast errors.
reference: APICS CPIM Forecasting Accuracy; SAP S/4HANA Demand Management

description: End date of the forecast time bucket. Together with forecast_start_date, defines the discrete planning period for which the forecasted quantity applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Planning Horizon; APICS CPIM

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the forecast record, used in S&OP meetings, planning reports, and cross-functional communications. Typically sourced from SAP MRP or APO planning run identifiers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Granularity of the forecast time bucket: weekly (for near-term operational planning), monthly (for mid-term S&OP), or quarterly (for strategic capacity planning and supplier reservation).
reference: APICS S&OP Framework; SAP S/4HANA Demand Management

description: Start date of the forecast time bucket (week, month, or quarter). Combined with forecast_end_date, defines the planning horizon window for this forecast record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Planning Horizon; APICS CPIM

description: Final consensus demand quantity for the forecast period after all adjustments. This is the primary demand signal consumed by MRP planning runs to generate planned orders and purchase requisitions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP; APICS MRP II; ISO 22400 Manufacturing KPIs

description: Estimated procurement spend for the forecasted quantity, calculated using the standard or moving average price. Used for budget planning, spend analytics, and supplier capacity reservation negotiations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA; IFRS; GAAP; SAP Ariba Spend Analytics

description: Indicates whether this forecast record is active and eligible to be consumed as a demand signal in the next MRP planning run. False records are excluded from MRP net requirements calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Demand Management; APICS MRP II

description: Indicates whether this forecast has been shared with the supplier for capacity reservation purposes. Supports collaborative planning (CPFR) and supplier capacity commitment tracking.
reference: VICS CPFR Guidelines; SAP Ariba Supply Chain Collaboration

description: SAP lot-sizing procedure used when converting forecast demand into planned orders or purchase requisitions: exact (lot-for-lot), fixed quantity, economic order quantity (EOQ), period-based, or min/max. Affects procurement cost and inventory levels.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Lot Sizing; APICS MRP II; EOQ Theory

description: Mean Absolute Percentage Error measuring forecast accuracy as a percentage deviation between forecasted and actual demand over the trailing evaluation period. Lower values indicate higher forecast accuracy. Key KPI for S&OP performance management.
reference: APICS CPIM Forecasting Accuracy; ISO 22400-2 Manufacturing KPIs

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the demand forecast record, whether from a consensus adjustment, status change, or MRP parameter update. Enables incremental data processing and change detection in the lakehouse.
reference: ISO 8601 Date/Time; DAMA DMBOK Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: SAP MRP area within the plant that further segments planning for sub-plant locations such as storage locations or subcontractor areas. Enables granular supply planning below plant level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Area Configuration; APICS MRP II Standards

description: SAP MRP type assigned to the material, determining the planning logic applied: standard MRP (deterministic), consumption-based, reorder point, forecast-based, or manual. Directly impacts how the forecast drives procurement signals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Type Configuration; APICS MRP II Standards

description: Number of months into the future this forecast record covers, measured from the forecast creation date. Determines how far out the demand signal extends for supplier capacity reservation and long-lead procurement.
reference: APICS MRP II; SAP S/4HANA Demand Management

description: SAP plant code representing the manufacturing or distribution facility for which the demand forecast is generated. Drives MRP planning at the plant level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Plant Configuration; ISO 3166-1

description: SAP MRP procurement type indicator specifying how the material is sourced: externally purchased, produced in-house, subcontracted, on consignment, or via stock transfer order. Determines which planning elements MRP generates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Procurement Type; APICS MRP II

description: Procurement category or commodity code classifying the forecasted material (e.g., raw materials, electronic components, MRO). Enables category-level demand aggregation for strategic sourcing and spend analytics.
reference: SAP Ariba Category Management; UNSPSC Commodity Classification

description: SAP purchasing organization responsible for procuring the forecasted material. Links demand signals to sourcing and supplier capacity reservation activities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Organization; SAP Ariba Procurement

description: Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered, calculated from the forecast demand rate and supplier lead time. Directly fed into SAP MRP reorder point planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP Reorder Point Planning; APICS MRP II

description: Recommended safety stock level derived from this forecast, accounting for demand variability and supply lead time uncertainty. Used to set reorder points and buffer stock targets in SAP MRP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP Safety Stock; APICS MRP II; ISO 22400

description: The S&OP planning cycle (YYYY-MM) in which this forecast was generated or last reviewed. Links the forecast to the specific monthly S&OP process iteration for governance and audit purposes.
reference: APICS S&OP Framework; Oliver Wight S&OP Best Practices

description: Operational system of record from which this forecast record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP, SAP APO, manual Excel upload). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality; DAMA DMBOK Data Lineage

description: System-generated statistical forecast quantity produced by the forecasting algorithm (e.g., exponential smoothing, moving average) before any manual consensus adjustments. Serves as the baseline for S&OP review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Demand Management; APICS CPIM Forecasting Module

description: Current lifecycle status of the demand forecast record. Locked forecasts are consumed by MRP planning runs. Superseded records are replaced by newer versions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP; APICS CPIM

description: Planned replenishment lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt, as used in MRP net requirements calculation. Drives the timing of procurement signals relative to the forecast period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record; APICS MRP II Lead Time

description: Sequential version number of the forecast for a given material, plant, and period combination. Enables tracking of forecast revisions across S&OP cycles and consensus planning rounds.
reference: APICS S&OP Best Practices; SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Forecast Versioning

description: Classifies the nature of the forecast version: baseline (initial statistical run), statistical (algorithm-generated), consensus (cross-functional agreed), final (locked for MRP input), adjusted (manually overridden), or simulation (what-if scenario).
reference: APICS S&OP Framework; SAP S/4HANA Demand Management

description: XYZ demand variability classification: X (stable/predictable demand), Y (variable demand), Z (irregular/sporadic demand). Combined with ABC classification to determine optimal forecasting strategy and safety stock levels.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM XYZ Analysis; APICS CPIM Demand Variability

business_glossary_term: Material Requirements Planning (MRP) Planned Order ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each MRP/MRP II planned order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: MRP explosion runs against the engineering BOM to generate planned orders. Each planned order must trace back to the BOM revision used during the MRP run — critical for change management when an ECN u
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_forecast. Business justification: MRP systems convert sales forecasts into planned production orders. Manufacturing planners trace planned orders back to originating sales forecasts to validate material requirements against actual cus
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: MRP planned orders are generated against specific product hierarchy nodes in industrial manufacturing. Planners run MRP at hierarchy level to aggregate demand across product families — used daily in p
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: MRP planned orders are generated against specific sales order line items in make-to-order environments. Production planners link planned orders to demand lines to ensure customer-specific production s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.mrp_run. Business justification: MRP planned orders are generated by specific MRP/MRP II planning runs. This is a fundamental parent-child relationship in the MRP data model. mrp_run_date is retained as a date field (not a business k
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.product_cost_estimate. Business justification: MRP planned orders reference product cost estimates to value planned procurement quantities at standard cost. Finance uses this to calculate planned purchase price variances and inventory valuation.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.product_variant. Business justification: MRP planned orders are variant-specific because different product variants have different BOMs and component requirements. This link enables procurement to trace planned orders back to the variant con
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.source_list. Business justification: MRP uses the source list to determine which supplier or contract to assign to a planned order. Planners rely on this link to ensure planned orders are automatically routed to the correct approved supp
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: MRP planned orders are generated based on current stock positions versus demand. The planned order must reference the stock position it is intended to replenish, enabling planners to validate coverage
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: MRP planned orders must reference the product's unit of measure to correctly calculate order quantities. Procurement planners rely on consistent UOM alignment between product master and planned orders
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Indicates whether the MRP run performed a BOM explosion for this planned order to generate dependent requirements for lower-level components. True for production orders; typically false for externally procured materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: SAP company code representing the legal entity or subsidiary for which this planned order is generated. Supports multi-company-code MRP planning in a multinational enterprise context and financial reporting alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS; GAAP

description: The date and time at which the planned order was converted to a purchase requisition or production order. Supports lead time analysis and MRP controller performance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The purchase requisition number or production order number to which this planned order was converted upon firming and release. Populated after conversion; null for unconverted planned orders. Enables traceability from planned to actual procurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-Purchasing; SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant or facility for which this planned order was generated. Supports regional compliance, cross-border procurement regulations, and multinational reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1; REACH; RoHS; CE Marking; GDPR

description: The date and time at which the planned order record was created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA) by the MRP run. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and MRP run frequency analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the planned order value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency procurement planning and spend analytics in a multinational manufacturing environment.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; GAAP

description: The type of demand element that triggered the generation of this planned order by MRP (e.g., sales order, planned independent requirement/forecast, safety stock replenishment, dependent demand from BOM explosion, manual reservation, project requirement, service order).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: SAP MRP exception message code indicating an action required by the MRP controller (e.g., reschedule in, reschedule out, cancel, bring forward, push back). Exception messages highlight planning deviations requiring manual intervention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Descriptive text of the MRP exception message associated with this planned order, providing the MRP controller with actionable guidance on the planning deviation (e.g., 'Reschedule order to earlier date', 'Order quantity exceeds maximum lot size').
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The number of workdays required for goods receipt inspection and posting after physical delivery, as maintained in the SAP material master. Included in MRP lead time calculation to determine the planned start date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the planned order has been firmed (fixed) by the MRP controller to prevent automatic rescheduling or deletion by subsequent MRP runs. Firmed orders are protected from MRP changes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The lot-sizing procedure applied by MRP to calculate the planned order quantity (e.g., EX=exact lot size, FX=fixed lot size, MB=monthly lot size, HB=replenish to maximum stock level). Determines how demand is aggregated into order quantities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The date and time at which the planned order record was last updated in the source system, capturing manual changes by MRP controllers or automatic updates from subsequent MRP runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: MRP area within the plant for which the planned order was generated. Enables sub-plant level planning segmentation such as storage locations or subcontractor areas in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP

description: Code identifying the MRP controller (planner) responsible for reviewing, firming, and converting this planned order. Corresponds to the MRP controller field in the SAP material master MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The date on which the MRP/MRP II planning run was executed that generated or last updated this planned order. Used for audit, traceability, and identifying stale planned orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The MRP procedure used to plan this material (e.g., MRP, MPS, consumption-based planning, reorder point planning). Corresponds to the MRP type field in the SAP material master MRP 1 view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The date on which the planned order should be released or converted to an actionable order (purchase requisition or production order), calculated by subtracting the float before production from the planned start date in SAP MRP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP

description: Classification of the planned order indicating the intended conversion target: purchase requisition (external procurement), production order (in-house manufacturing), transfer order (stock transfer), subcontracting, or consignment. Drives MRP controller firming decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The planned delivery time in calendar days for external procurement of this material, as maintained in the SAP material master or purchasing info record. Used by MRP to calculate planned start date from requirements date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The date by which the planned order must be completed (goods receipt or production completion) to satisfy the dependent or independent demand requirement. Derived from MRP scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the planned order as assigned by the MRP/MRP II run in SAP S/4HANA (MD04/MD06). Used by MRP controllers to reference, review, and firm planned orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Estimated monetary value of the planned order calculated as planned quantity multiplied by the standard or moving average price of the material. Used for spend analytics, budget planning, and procurement commitment reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS; GAAP

description: The quantity of material to be procured or produced as determined by the MRP/MRP II run, expressed in the base unit of measure. Reflects lot-sizing rules, safety stock, and demand requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The date on which procurement or production activities for this planned order are scheduled to begin, as calculated by the MRP/MRP II backward scheduling logic. Used for capacity and supplier lead time planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Indicates whether the material is procured externally (E), produced in-house (F), or both (X), as defined in the SAP material master MRP 2 view. Determines the planned order conversion path.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: SAP purchasing group (buyer group) responsible for the procurement activity associated with this planned order. Used for workload distribution and spend analytics by buyer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-Purchasing; SAP Ariba

description: SAP purchasing organization responsible for converting this planned order into a purchase requisition or purchase order. Relevant for externally procured planned orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-Purchasing; SAP Ariba

description: The date on which the material is required to be available in stock to fulfill the demand element (sales order, production order, forecast, safety stock). Drives MRP scheduling backward to determine planned start date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The minimum stock level maintained as a buffer against demand variability and supply uncertainty, as defined in the SAP material master MRP 2 view. Influences MRP net requirements calculation and planned order quantities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: For stock transfer planned orders, the supplying plant from which the material will be transferred. Relevant for multi-plant and cross-company-code MRP scenarios in multinational manufacturing environments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: SAP special procurement type key indicating non-standard procurement scenarios such as subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer from another plant, or phantom assembly. Influences planned order type and conversion behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP

description: Current lifecycle status of the planned order. 'Created' indicates a system-generated order awaiting review; 'Firmed' indicates the MRP controller has locked the order to prevent MRP rescheduling; 'Converted' indicates the order has been converted to a purchase requisition or production order; 'Cancelled' indicates the order is no longer required; 'Exception' indicates an MRP exception message is pending resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-MRP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: The storage location within the plant where the planned goods receipt will be posted upon conversion and execution of the planned order. Supports warehouse and inventory management integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-WM; Infor WMS

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each MRP or MRP II planning run execution record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: Each MRP run is executed against a specific BOM revision. Traceability to the exact BOM used is required for audit, re-planning after ECNs, and reconciling planned vs. actual material requirements in
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.physical_inventory. Business justification: MRP runs use confirmed physical inventory counts as the authoritative stock baseline. Linking mrp_run to physical_inventory ensures MRP calculations are grounded in verified stock data, not just syste
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: MRP runs in industrial manufacturing are scoped by customer segment to prioritize material requirements for strategic segments (e.g., high-volume automotive). Planners execute segment-specific MRP run
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Maximum number of BOM levels exploded during the MRP run. Indicates the depth of the product structure traversed to derive dependent requirements for sub-assemblies and raw materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; BOM Explosion Logic

description: Indicates whether a capacity requirements planning (CRP) check was executed as part of this MRP II run to validate work center availability against planned production orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP II Capacity Planning

description: Number of work centers identified as overloaded (capacity demand exceeds available capacity) during the MRP II capacity check. Requires production scheduling intervention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP II Capacity Requirements Planning

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the plant location where the MRP run was executed. Supports multi-country reporting and regional supply planning analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes

description: Number of materials with identified supply shortfalls (demand exceeds available supply within the planning horizon) after the MRP run. Critical KPI for supply risk management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; APICS Supply Planning

description: Count of MRP exception messages recommending cancellation of existing planned receipts (purchase orders or production orders) due to excess supply or demand cancellation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP Exception Message Type 20

description: Total count of MRP exception messages generated during the run across all materials. Exception messages flag planning anomalies requiring MRP controller review and action.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP Exception Message Processing

description: Count of MRP exception messages indicating new demand requirements that have been identified and for which new planned orders or purchase requisitions have been created.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP Exception Message Type 25

description: Count of MRP exception messages recommending that existing receipts (purchase orders, production orders) be rescheduled to an earlier date to cover demand shortfalls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP Exception Message Type 10

description: Count of MRP exception messages recommending that existing receipts be rescheduled to a later date due to excess supply or demand postponement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP Exception Message Type 15

description: Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the MRP run was executed. Enables period-over-period comparison of planning run outputs and exception trends.
reference: IFRS; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module

description: Fiscal year in which the MRP planning run was executed. Used for financial period alignment in procurement spend analytics and supply planning reporting.
reference: IFRS; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module

description: The end date of the planning horizon window for this MRP run. Marks the latest date for which planned orders and procurement proposals are generated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module

description: The start date of the planning horizon window for this MRP run. Marks the earliest date for which planned orders and procurement proposals are generated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module

description: SAP user ID or system job name that triggered the MRP planning run. Distinguishes between manually initiated runs by MRP controllers and automated scheduled batch jobs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

description: Starting material number of the material range included in the MRP run when run_scope is 'material_range'. Enables targeted replanning of specific material groups.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; SAP Material Master

description: Ending material number of the material range included in the MRP run when run_scope is 'material_range'. Defines the upper boundary of the material selection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; SAP Material Master

description: SAP MRP area within the plant that scopes the planning run. An MRP area can represent a storage location, subcontractor, or the entire plant, enabling granular supply planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP Area Configuration

description: SAP MRP controller group code responsible for reviewing and acting on the exception messages and planned orders generated by this MRP run.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP Controller Configuration

description: Number of new planned production orders generated by the MRP run. Planned orders represent internal manufacturing proposals that MRP controllers review and convert to production orders.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; APICS MRP Output Standards

description: The business date used as the reference point for the MRP planning run. All requirements, receipts, and planned orders are evaluated relative to this date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; APICS Supply Chain Planning Standards

description: Number of calendar days forward from the planning date that the MRP run covers. Defines the time fence within which planned orders and procurement proposals are generated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; APICS MRP Planning Horizon Guidelines

description: Indicates the planning methodology applied during the run: standard MRP (Material Requirements Planning), MRP II (Manufacturing Resource Planning including capacity), consumption-based, reorder point, or forecast-based planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; APICS MRP/MRP II Standards

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility for which the MRP run was executed. Aligns with the organizational unit in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001 Clause 4.1

description: Number of purchase requisitions automatically generated by the MRP run for externally procured materials. PRs are subsequently converted to purchase orders by the procurement team.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Total elapsed time in seconds for the MRP planning run to complete. Used to monitor system performance, identify degradation trends, and plan batch scheduling windows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module

description: Date and time when the MRP planning run completed in SAP S/4HANA. Combined with run_start_timestamp to calculate run duration for performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Business-facing alphanumeric identifier for the MRP planning run, used for cross-referencing in SAP S/4HANA PP/MM and audit trail documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module

description: Defines the breadth of materials included in the MRP run: a single material, a defined material range, all materials in the MRP area, or the entire plant.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module

description: Date and time when the MRP planning run was initiated in SAP S/4HANA. Used for performance monitoring, audit trail, and scheduling conflict detection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

description: Indicates how the MRP run was initiated: manually by an MRP controller, via a scheduled batch job, triggered by a business event (e.g., large sales order), or via API integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module

description: Classification of the MRP planning run scope: 'regenerative' replans all materials, 'net_change' replans only materials with changes since last run, 'net_change_planning_horizon' limits net change to the planning horizon window.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; MRP Run Types (NEUPL/NETCH/NETPL)

description: Number of delivery schedule lines generated by the MRP run for scheduling agreement-based procurement. Represents firm or forecast delivery commitments to suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Defines the scheduling logic applied during the MRP run: basic date calculation, lead time scheduling using routing data, or capacity planning with work center load leveling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; ISO 9001 Clause 8.1

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which the MRP run data was extracted. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Data Governance Best Practices

description: The native identifier of the MRP run in the source operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA internal run key). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and the system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; Data Lineage Standards

description: Current execution status of the MRP planning run. Supports MRP controller workflow management and exception handling processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; ISO 9001 Clause 8.1

description: Count of distinct material numbers processed during the MRP run. Used to assess run scope, compare against prior runs, and validate completeness of planning coverage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module
